# HARVEST LOG

Detailed per-round / per-cluster harvest notes for every additive round of
the [zsh-more-completions](README.md) corpus. Each bullet captures the
specific upstream sources walked, extraction technique used (clap-derive
`#[arg(...)]` macros, getopt strings, Cobra subcommand trees, case-arm
decoded mutex groups, etc.), and notable per-binary surface quirks caught
during source-direct verification.

For installation, usage, stats, and the general harvested-ecosystem source
families (Nix / Homebrew / Debian / Fedora / GitHub Releases / etc.) see
the main [README.md](README.md).

---

## Harvest entries

Entries are in original README order: older themed-cluster entries (accumulated
before the per-round numbering convention) appear first, followed by the
R-numbered rounds with the newest R-round at the top of that section.

- **R367 — aho source-direct (a complete git implementation in AWK; gawk stdlib getopt.awk library)** (1 file new) — pivots from Kotlin/kotlinx.cli (R366 diktat) to a FIRST AWK CLI library — corpus's **first documented AWK entry** + **first documented gawk stdlib getopt.awk library**.  aho is djanderson's COMPLETE GIT REIMPLEMENTATION IN AWK — a complete subset of git's plumbing+porcelain implemented in GNU AWK including SHA-1 hashing, zlib compression, object database, index, refs, branches, working tree, commit objects, etc.  822 GH stars.  Sister tools: git (in corpus, canonical C implementation), gut (Rust reimpl), libgit2-based tools.  Notably aho is the world's most popular AWK GIT IMPLEMENTATION — proves AWK's Turing-completeness in a way that is often understated.  **MAJOR MILESTONE**: corpus's **first documented AWK entry** + **first documented gawk stdlib getopt.awk library**.  AWK is a FIRST-TIME addition to the cross-language CLI library taxonomy.  Adds gawk stdlib `getopt.awk` (bundled with EVERY gawk installation at `/usr/share/awk/getopt.awk` — a getopt(3)-style reimplementation in AWK by Arnold Robbins, gawk maintainer) to the cross-language CLI taxonomy.
  - `_aho` (1-stem; djanderson/aho aho.awk + getopt.awk; **gawk stdlib getopt.awk library** — `@include "getopt.awk"` brings getopt.awk into scope; **2 top-level flags (`-h/--help` + `--version`) + 7 subcommands (`init` + `add` + `rm` + `config` + `status` + `commit` + `cat-file`) + each subcommand in its OWN .awk module with its own getopt parsing + POSIX getopt(3) SEMANTICS + `getopt::Optind` GLOBAL VARIABLE for current arg index + `getopt::Optarg` GLOBAL VARIABLE for last option's value + `getopt::Optopt` GLOBAL VARIABLE for last option's name + `getopt::Opterr` GLOBAL VARIABLE for error printing control + `getopt::getopt(ARGC, ARGV, shortopts, longopts)` RETURNS next option or -1 + AWK `@namespace "name"` for namespacing (gawk 4.0+) + NO built-in subcommand dispatch + NO auto-generated help + caller writes everything manually**) decoded source-direct from:
    * `aho.awk` line 6 (`@include "getopt.awk"` — AWK `@include` directive (gawk extension) for inline file inclusion; `getopt.awk` is gawk's stdlib at `/usr/share/awk/getopt.awk` (or vendored copy))
    * `aho.awk` lines 33-43 (`function main(    shortopts, longopts, c, command, exitcode) { getopt::Optind = 1; getopt::Opterr = 1; shortopts = "h"; longopts = "version,help"; while ((c = getopt::getopt(ARGC, ARGV, shortopts, longopts)) != -1) { ... } }` — `getopt::Optind = 1` initializes the global cursor; `getopt::Opterr = 1` enables stderr error printing; `shortopts = "h"` declares short options (with colon suffix for value-taking); `longopts = "version,help"` declares long options (with `=` suffix for value-taking); main loop iterates `getopt::getopt(...)` until it returns -1)
    * `aho.awk` lines 45-58 (`if (c == "?") { print_usage(); return 129 } if (getopt::Optopt == "h" || getopt::Optopt == "help") { print_help(); return 0 } if (getopt::Optopt == "version") { print_version(); return 0 }` — `c == "?"` for unknown option; `getopt::Optopt` for matched option name; caller pattern-matches via `if/else if` chain)
    * `aho.awk` lines 60-86 (`command = ARGV[getopt::Optind++]; if (command != "init") { path::assert_in_repo() }; if (command == "init") { exitcode = init::run_command() } else if (command == "add") { exitcode = add::run_command() } else if (command == "rm") { exitcode = rm::run_command() } else if (command == "commit") { exitcode = commit::run_command() } else if (command == "cat-file") { exitcode = catfile::run_command() } else if (command == "status") { exitcode = status::run_command() } else if (command == "config") { exitcode = config::run_command() } else { print "aho: " command " is not an aho command. See 'aho --help'\n" > "/dev/stderr"; print_usage(); exitcode = 1 }` — MANUAL SUBCOMMAND DISPATCH via `if/else if` chain on ARGV[getopt::Optind++]; each subcommand has its OWN `run_command()` function in its own .awk module)
    * `aho.awk` lines 88-103 (`function print_usage() { print "usage: aho [--version] [--help] <command> [<args>]" } function print_help() { print_usage(); print; print "Commands:"; print "  init        Create an empty repo"; ... }` — HAND-WRITTEN help text — getopt.awk does NOT auto-generate help)
  - Critical extraction note: aho uses **gawk stdlib getopt.awk library** at aho.awk:6 — corpus's **first documented AWK entry** + **first documented gawk stdlib getopt.awk library**.  gawk stdlib `getopt.awk` is bundled with EVERY gawk installation at `/usr/share/awk/getopt.awk` — a getopt(3)-style reimplementation in AWK by Arnold Robbins (gawk maintainer himself).  EVERY substantial AWK CLI tool that does argument parsing uses this exact file or a vendored copy of it (since AWK has no native arg parsing — `ARGV` is just the raw array of args, no parsing built into the language).  KEY DIFFERENTIATORS:
    - **POSIX getopt(3) SEMANTICS** — short options string with `:` suffix for value-taking (`"ab:c"` means `-a` boolean, `-b VALUE`, `-c` boolean), long options comma-separated with `=` suffix for value-taking (`"alpha,beta=,gamma"` means `--alpha` boolean, `--beta VALUE`, `--gamma` boolean)
    - **GLOBAL VARIABLES** for parser state — `getopt::Optind` (current arg index), `getopt::Optarg` (last option's value), `getopt::Optopt` (last option's name), `getopt::Opterr` (controls error printing)
    - **`getopt::getopt(ARGC, ARGV, shortopts, longopts)`** RETURNS the next option as a single character (short) or string (long), or `-1` when no more options remain
    - **AWK `@namespace "getopt"`** for namespacing (gawk 4.0+) — `getopt::` prefix avoids name collisions
    - **NO BUILT-IN SUBCOMMAND DISPATCH** — caller writes manual `if`/`else if` chain on `ARGV[getopt::Optind]` after the options loop
    - **NO AUTO-GENERATED HELP** — caller writes help text manually
    - **NO TYPE COERCION** — all values are AWK STRINGS (AWK auto-converts to number when used in arithmetic context)
  Distinct from R272 C POSIX getopt (C language — same SEMANTICS but C syntax with `getopt(3)`/`getopt_long(3)` from `<unistd.h>`), R287 Nim parseopt (low-level char-by-char iteration in Nim), R349 Lua mpeterv/argparse (DECLARATIVE method-chain), R275 Rust clap-derive (struct + macro), R322 JCommander (Java field annotations), R341 yargs (JS fluent builder).  getopt.awk's approach: POSIX getopt(3) SEMANTICS in AWK + GLOBAL CURSOR VARIABLES + AWK `@namespace` for namespacing + caller-driven everything (subcommand dispatch, help text, type coercion) — corpus's **first documented AWK CLI library**.  Extends cross-language CLI taxonomy to ONE HUNDRED AND SIX library variants: Rust (9), Go (9), Python (5), Crystal 4, Haskell (R270), OCaml 4, C (R272), Java 4, Kotlin 2, Scala 3, Dart (R279), Ruby 2, andrey-zherikov/argparse (R363), F# 2 commandlineparser/commandline (R364), Nim 3 iffy/nim-argparse (R365), Kotlin 2 Kotlin/kotlinx.cli (R366), **AWK gawk stdlib getopt.awk** (R367 — NEW).  **MAJOR PATTERN MILESTONE**: 106 cross-language CLI library variants documented.  AWK added to the documented-CLI-language set.
  - Critical extraction note: aho uses **getopt.awk AWK-namespace-for-namespacing CLI pattern** at getopt.awk + aho.awk — `@namespace "getopt"` in getopt.awk + `getopt::Optind` / `getopt::Optarg` / `getopt::Optopt` / `getopt::Opterr` / `getopt::getopt(...)` calls from caller code.  AWK `@namespace` (gawk 4.0+) is gawk's NAMESPACING feature — DECLARES that all subsequent definitions in this file BELONG TO the named namespace; callers reference symbols with `namespace::symbol` syntax.  getopt.awk uses `@namespace "getopt"` to NAMESPACE all its global variables (`Optind`, `Optarg`, `Optopt`, `Opterr`) and functions (`getopt()`) under `getopt::` to AVOID name collisions with caller code.  Caller MUST use `getopt::` prefix to reference these symbols.  Distinct from R272 C POSIX getopt (C uses linker namespaces, `extern int optind, opterr, optopt;` + `extern char *optarg;` are globals in C namespace), R287 Nim parseopt (Nim modules + `import`), R349 Lua argparse (Lua `local` scoping), R341 yargs (JS modules + `require`), R322 JCommander (Java packages + `import`).  getopt.awk's approach: gawk `@namespace` directive for namespacing GLOBAL VARIABLES and FUNCTIONS — corpus's **first documented gawk-@namespace-based namespacing CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: aho uses **getopt.awk POSIX-getopt(3)-semantics-in-AWK CLI pattern** at aho.awk:39 — `shortopts = "h"; longopts = "version,help"; while ((c = getopt::getopt(ARGC, ARGV, shortopts, longopts)) != -1) { ... }`.  getopt.awk implements POSIX `getopt(3)` SEMANTICS in pure AWK:
    - **Short options string** — each character is a short flag; `:` suffix marks the previous flag as value-taking. e.g. `"abc:d"` means `-a` boolean, `-b` boolean, `-c VALUE`, `-d` boolean
    - **Long options string** — comma-separated; `=` suffix marks value-taking. e.g. `"alpha,beta=,gamma"` means `--alpha` boolean, `--beta VALUE`, `--gamma` boolean
    - **Return value** — character for short, string for long, `-1` when no more options, `"?"` for unknown
    - **GLOBAL VARIABLES** — `getopt::Optind` (cursor through ARGV), `getopt::Optarg` (current option's value if value-taking), `getopt::Optopt` (current option's name), `getopt::Opterr` (error printing flag)
    - **Iteration via `while ((c = getopt::getopt(...)) != -1)`** — caller iterates until -1 returned
  Distinct from R272 C getopt (same semantics, C language), R287 Nim parseopt (similar low-level but Nim), R275 clap-derive (DECLARATIVE struct + macro — fundamentally different paradigm), R341 yargs (DECLARATIVE method-chain).  getopt.awk's approach: POSIX getopt(3) SEMANTICS implemented in AWK — corpus's **first documented POSIX-getopt(3)-implemented-in-AWK CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: aho uses **getopt.awk manual-subcommand-dispatch-via-if-else-on-ARGV-Optind CLI pattern** at aho.awk:60-86 — `command = ARGV[getopt::Optind++]; if (command == "init") { exitcode = init::run_command() } else if (command == "add") { exitcode = add::run_command() } else if (...) ...`.  getopt.awk PROVIDES NO BUILT-IN SUBCOMMAND DISPATCH — caller writes a MANUAL `if`/`else if` CHAIN on `ARGV[getopt::Optind]` after the options loop.  Each subcommand has its OWN `run_command()` function in its own .awk module (aho uses gawk's `@namespace` to scope these: `init::`, `add::`, `rm::`, etc.).  Distinct from R275 clap-derive `#[derive(Subcommand)] enum { ... }` (enum-of-variants), R341 yargs `.command(name, desc, builder, handler)` (chained registration), R343 commander.js `program.command(verb).action(fn)` (similar), R357 mitchellh/cli `cli.Commands = map[string]cli.CommandFactory{...}` (Go subcommand map), R346 mainargs MULTIPLE `@main` methods (Scala annotation-driven).  getopt.awk's approach: MANUAL `if`/`else if` DISPATCH on `ARGV[getopt::Optind]` + per-subcommand `run_command()` function in its own .awk module — corpus's **first documented manual-if-else-dispatch-on-ARGV-Optind-with-per-module-run_command CLI pattern**.
  - Hunt-skip notes: skipped `_piawka` (novikovalab/piawka 31 stars VCF population stats — narrow), `_BotWikiAwk` (greencardamom 27 stars — narrow Wikipedia bot framework), `_WaybackMedic` (greencardamom — narrow Wikipedia citation tool), `_epacman` (weynhamz/shell-toolkit — narrow Arch pacman helper).  Probed alternative AWK CLI tools: most AWK scripts use getopt.awk or hand-rolled `for (i=1; i<ARGC; i++)` loops — getopt.awk is THE library.  Considered using gawk itself (`gawk` is in corpus) but gawk is the AWK INTERPRETER, not a CLI tool USING getopt.awk.  Chose aho: 822 GH stars (world's most popular AWK git reimplementation, proves AWK's Turing-completeness in a way that's often understated), 2 top-level flags + 7 subcommands + gawk stdlib `getopt.awk` + POSIX getopt(3) semantics + AWK @namespace + GLOBAL CURSOR VARIABLES + MANUAL DISPATCH via `if/else if` chain + per-subcommand `run_command()` modules, full aho.awk exercising EVERY MAJOR getopt.awk PATTERN.
  - Blacklist update: `aho` added.  Corpus count: 28,789 → 28,790 (+1).

- **R366 — diktat source-direct (strict Kotlin linter; JetBrains kotlinx.cli library)** (1 file new) — pivots from iffy/nim-argparse (R365 slivar) to a SECOND Kotlin CLI library — corpus's **first documented Kotlin/kotlinx.cli library** + **second documented Kotlin CLI library**.  diktat is SaveOurTool's strict Kotlin linter — applies custom rules to detect code smells, style issues, and bugs.  Integrates as standalone CLI + Gradle plugin + Maven plugin.  571 GH stars.  Sister tools: ktlint (Kotlin linter, in corpus), detekt (Kotlin static analyzer, in corpus), checkstyle (Java, in corpus).  **MAJOR MILESTONE**: corpus's **first documented Kotlin/kotlinx.cli library** + **second documented Kotlin CLI library**.  Kotlin ecosystem now has 2 documented CLI libraries (R277 ajalt/clikt + R366 Kotlin/kotlinx.cli) joining the multi-library leaders.  Adds Kotlin/kotlinx.cli (~500 GH stars on Kotlin/kotlinx.cli) — JetBrains' OFFICIAL Kotlin CLI library, designed for KOTLIN MULTIPLATFORM (JVM, Native, JS) projects.  clikt (R277) is JVM-only; kotlinx.cli works across all Kotlin platforms.
  - `_diktat` (1-stem; saveourtool/diktat diktat-cli/src/main/kotlin/com/saveourtool/diktat/cli/DiktatProperties.kt + Kotlin/kotlinx.cli library; **Kotlin/kotlinx.cli library** — `import kotlinx.cli.{ArgParser, ArgType, default, vararg}` brings kotlinx.cli into scope; **10 options + 1 variadic positional + KOTLIN MULTIPLATFORM support + Kotlin `by` DELEGATED PROPERTIES + `ArgParser("name")` parser constructor + `parser.option(type = ArgType.X, fullName = "long", shortName = "s", description = "Y")` typed flag declaration + `parser.argument(type, description).vararg()` for variadic positional + `ArgType.String`/`ArgType.Int`/`ArgType.Boolean`/`ArgType.Choice<EnumType>()`/`ArgType.Choice(choices, toVariant, variantToString)` typed args + `.default(V)` chained default + `.multiple()` chained repeatable + `.vararg()` chained variadic + `parser.parse(args)` parse invocation + `parser.subcommands(Subcommand1, Subcommand2, ...)` for subcommands via Subcommand class inheritance**) decoded source-direct from:
    * `diktat-cli/src/main/kotlin/com/saveourtool/diktat/cli/DiktatProperties.kt` lines 29-32 (`import kotlinx.cli.ArgParser; import kotlinx.cli.ArgType; import kotlinx.cli.default; import kotlinx.cli.vararg` — Kotlin IMPORTS of kotlinx.cli primitives; distributed via Maven Central as `org.jetbrains.kotlinx:kotlinx-cli:VERSION`)
    * `DiktatProperties.kt` `parse` companion (`val parser = ArgParser(DIKTAT); val config: String? by parser.config(); val mode: DiktatMode by parser.diktatMode(); val reporterType: DiktatReporterType? by parser.reporterType(); val output: String? by parser.output(); val groupByFile: Boolean? by parser.groupByFile(); val colorName: String? by parser.colorName(diktatReporterFactory); val logLevel: Level by parser.logLevel(); val patterns: List<String> by parser.argument(type = ArgType.String, description = "A list of files to process by diktat").vararg(); parser.parse(args);` — `ArgParser("name")` constructor + Kotlin `by` DELEGATED PROPERTIES for typed option access)
    * `DiktatProperties.kt` `config()` extension (`private fun ArgParser.config() = option(type = ArgType.String, fullName = "config", shortName = "c", description = "Specify the location of the YAML configuration file. ...")` — REUSABLE OPTION DEFINITIONS via EXTENSION FUNCTIONS on `ArgParser` — caller defines `fun ArgParser.X() = option(...)` and uses `by parser.X()` in the main parse function)
    * `DiktatProperties.kt` `diktatMode()` extension (`private fun ArgParser.diktatMode() = option(type = ArgType.Choice<DiktatMode>(), fullName = "mode", shortName = "m", description = "Mode of `diktat` controls that `diktat` fixes or only finds any deviations from the code style.").default(DiktatMode.CHECK)` — `ArgType.Choice<EnumType>()` REIFIED GENERIC for enum choice + `.default(V)` chained default value)
    * `DiktatProperties.kt` `colorName()` extension (`private fun ArgParser.colorName(diktatReporterFactory: DiktatReporterFactory) = option(type = ArgType.Choice(choices = diktatReporterFactory.colorNamesInPlain.toList(), toVariant = { it }, variantToString = { it }), fullName = "color", shortName = null, description = "Colorize the output.")` — `ArgType.Choice` with RUNTIME CHOICES via `choices = list`, `toVariant = lambda`, `variantToString = lambda`)
    * `DiktatProperties.kt` `addOptionAndShowTextWithExit` (`fun ArgParser.addOptionAndShowTextWithExit(fullName: String, shortName: String?, description: String, args: Array<String>, contentSupplier: () -> String)` — CUSTOM PATTERN for exit-after-print flags like `--version` and `--license`)
  - Critical extraction note: diktat uses **Kotlin/kotlinx.cli library** at DiktatProperties.kt:29 — corpus's **first documented Kotlin/kotlinx.cli library** + **second documented Kotlin CLI library**.  Kotlin/kotlinx.cli (~500 GH stars on Kotlin/kotlinx.cli) is JetBrains' OFFICIAL Kotlin CLI library — distributed via Maven Central as `org.jetbrains.kotlinx:kotlinx-cli:VERSION`.  KEY DIFFERENTIATORS from R277 ajalt/clikt + R276 Java picocli:
    - **KOTLIN MULTIPLATFORM** — works on JVM, Native, AND JS targets (clikt R277 is JVM-only; picocli R276 is Java/JVM with Kotlin support via annotations)
    - **Kotlin `by` DELEGATED PROPERTIES** — `val foo: T by parser.option(...)` uses Kotlin's `by` keyword to wire up the typed accessor; same delegation idiom as clikt but with JetBrains-native types
    - **`ArgParser("name")` constructor** for parser creation
    - **`parser.option(type = T, fullName = "long", shortName = "s", description = "X")`** for FLAG with type-checking via `ArgType.X` GENERIC ARG TYPES
    - **`parser.argument(type, description)`** for POSITIONAL
    - **`ArgType.X` typed args** — `ArgType.String`/`ArgType.Int`/`ArgType.Boolean`/`ArgType.Double`/`ArgType.Choice<EnumType>()` (Kotlin REIFIED GENERIC enum choice) / `ArgType.Choice(choices, toVariant, variantToString)` (runtime choices with conversion lambdas)
    - **`.default(V)` chained method** for default value
    - **`.multiple()` chained** for repeatable
    - **`.vararg()` chained** for variadic positional (catches all remaining)
    - **`parser.parse(args)`** invokes parse — throws on failure
    - **SUBCOMMANDS** via `parser.subcommands(Subcommand1, Subcommand2, ...)` where Subcommand is a CLASS extending `kotlinx.cli.Subcommand`
    - **REUSABLE OPTION DEFINITIONS** via Kotlin EXTENSION FUNCTIONS on ArgParser — `fun ArgParser.X() = option(...)` lets caller define options once and use via `by parser.X()`
  Distinct from R277 Kotlin ajalt/clikt (uses similar `by` delegation but CLIKT-SPECIFIC types like `option().flag()` / `option().required()` — JVM-only), R275 Rust clap-derive (struct + proc-macro), R344 argh (similar Rust), R322 Java JCommander (Java annotations + reflection), R276 picocli (Java annotations — works from Kotlin but uses Java annotations, not Kotlin by-delegation), R359 System.CommandLine (typed Argument<T>/Option<T>).  kotlinx.cli's approach: KOTLIN MULTIPLATFORM + Kotlin `by` DELEGATED PROPERTIES + `ArgType.X` typed args + chained `.default()`/`.multiple()`/`.vararg()` + REUSABLE OPTION DEFINITIONS via EXTENSION FUNCTIONS — corpus's **first documented Kotlin-multiplatform-with-by-delegation-and-ArgType-typed-args CLI pattern**.  Extends cross-language CLI taxonomy to ONE HUNDRED AND FIVE library variants: Rust (9), Go (9), Python (5), Crystal 4, Haskell (R270), OCaml 4, C (R272), Java 4, **Kotlin (2 — clikt R277 + kotlinx.cli Kotlin NOW HAS TWO documented CLI libraries.
  - Critical extraction note: diktat uses **kotlinx.cli reusable-option-definitions-via-extension-functions CLI pattern** at DiktatProperties.kt `companion object` — `private fun ArgParser.config() = option(type = ArgType.String, fullName = "config", shortName = "c", description = "...")` + `private fun ArgParser.diktatMode() = option(...)` + `private fun ArgParser.reporterType() = option(...)` + similar for output/groupByFile/colorName/logLevel.  Kotlin EXTENSION FUNCTIONS on ArgParser let the caller DEFINE OPTIONS as REUSABLE METHODS — each extension returns the option-delegate from `option(...)`.  Main `parse()` then uses `val config: String? by parser.config()` to wire up the typed property.  This pattern allows SHARING OPTION DEFINITIONS across multiple parsers (e.g. between CLI binary and library API) without copy-paste.  Distinct from R277 clikt (uses Kotlin DELEGATED PROPERTIES but with clikt-specific `option().flag()`/`option().required()` builder), R360 Scala scopt (REUSABLE OPTIONS via `using OParserBuilder[C]` implicit parameter), R275 Rust clap-derive (struct field embedding via `#[command(flatten)]`), R346 mainargs (NO sharing — annotations on methods).  kotlinx.cli's approach: REUSABLE OPTION DEFINITIONS via KOTLIN EXTENSION FUNCTIONS — corpus's **first documented Kotlin-extension-functions-on-ArgParser-for-reusable-options CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: diktat uses **kotlinx.cli ArgType.Choice-with-reified-generic-and-runtime-variants CLI pattern** at DiktatProperties.kt — `ArgType.Choice<DiktatMode>()` for ENUM-BASED choices (uses Kotlin REIFIED GENERIC `<T : Enum<T>>` to enumerate all enum values at compile time) + `ArgType.Choice(choices = diktatReporterFactory.colorNamesInPlain.toList(), toVariant = { it }, variantToString = { it })` for RUNTIME choices with conversion lambdas.  Two flavors of `ArgType.Choice`:
    1. `ArgType.Choice<EnumType>()` — REIFIED GENERIC for enum (compile-time-known choices)
    2. `ArgType.Choice(choices = list, toVariant = (String) -> T, variantToString = (T) -> String)` — RUNTIME CHOICES with custom conversion
  Distinct from R275 clap-derive (Rust enum-based choices via `#[derive(ValueEnum)]`), R322 JCommander (`@Parameter(names = ..., converter = ConverterClass)`), R341 yargs (`{choices: ["a", "b"]}` config), R343 commander.js (`.choices(["a", "b"])` method), R333 Mojo argmojo (`choices="a,b,c"` comma-string), R346 mainargs (no choices), R362 cli-matic (Clojure set literal `#{"a" "b"}`).  kotlinx.cli's approach: `ArgType.Choice<EnumType>()` REIFIED GENERIC for enums + `ArgType.Choice(choices, toVariant, variantToString)` for runtime choices — corpus's **first documented reified-generic-enum-choice-with-runtime-conversion-lambda CLI pattern**.

Considered using JetBrains/kotlin itself as canonical kotlinx.cli consumer but the `kotlinc` binary's CLI is mostly hand-rolled.  Chose diktat: 571 GH stars (real production Kotlin linter used in Kotlin CI/CD pipelines), 10 options + 1 variadic positional + KOTLIN MULTIPLATFORM kotlinx.cli + Kotlin `by` delegated properties + ArgType-typed args + `.default()`/`.vararg()` chained + REUSABLE OPTION DEFINITIONS via EXTENSION FUNCTIONS + `ArgType.Choice<EnumType>()` REIFIED GENERIC + `ArgType.Choice(runtime)` with conversion lambdas, full diktat-cli/src/main/kotlin/com/saveourtool/diktat/cli/DiktatProperties.kt exercising EVERY MAJOR kotlinx.cli PATTERN.

- **R365 — slivar source-direct (genetic variant expressions/annotation/filtering in Nim; iffy/nim-argparse library)** (1 file new) — pivots from commandlineparser/commandline (R364 supercluster) to a THIRD Nim CLI library — corpus's **first documented iffy/nim-argparse library** + **third documented Nim CLI library**.  slivar is Brent Pedersen's clinical genetics variant filter — applies JavaScript-style expressions to VCF/BCF variant data files (filter trio/family/group/sample expressions, annotate with gnotate databases, find compound hets, denovo deletions, generate data-driven cutoffs).  Used in clinical genetics + variant analysis pipelines.  274 GH stars.  Sister tools: vcfanno (Brent Pedersen's VCF annotation tool, in corpus), bcftools (samtools project, in corpus), gnomad (population genome AF database).  **MAJOR MILESTONE**: corpus's **first documented iffy/nim-argparse library** + **third documented Nim CLI library**.  Nim ecosystem now has 3 documented CLI libraries (R287 parseopt + R301 cligen + R365 iffy/nim-argparse) joining the multi-library leaders.  Adds iffy/nim-argparse (129 GH stars on iffy/nim-argparse) to the cross-language CLI taxonomy.
  - `_slivar` (1-stem; brentp/slivar src/slivar.nim + iffy/nim-argparse library; **iffy/nim-argparse library** — `import argparse` brings nim-argparse into scope; **6 subcommands (`expr` + `make-gnotate` + `compound-hets` + `tsv` + `duo-del` + `ddc`) + rich expr subcommand with 14 flags + MACRO-DRIVEN BLOCK DSL via `newParser("name"):` + `option("-s", "--long", help="X", default="V", multiple=BOOL)` registration + `flag("-f", "--flag", help="X")` for booleans + `arg("name", help="X")` for positionals + COMPILE-TIME TYPED-RECORD GENERATION + `p.parse(argv)` returns typed result + AUTO-DERIVED FIELD NAMES from long flags (kebab-case → camelCase: `--out-vcf` → `outVcf`) + `command("verb"):` block for nested subcommands (slivar uses MANUAL dispatch on `argv[0]` via OrderedTable instead — interesting alternative)**) decoded source-direct from:
    * `src/slivar.nim` line 3 (`import argparse` — Nim IMPORT of nim-argparse; distributed via nimble package manager as `argparse`)
    * `src/slivar.nim` lines 5-23 (`proc expr_main*(dropfirst:bool=false) = var p = newParser("slivar expr"): option("-v", "--vcf", help="path to VCF/BCF"); option("--region", help="..."); option("-x", "--exclude", help="..."); option("-j", "--js", help="..."); option("-p", "--ped", help="..."); option("-a", "--alias", help="..."); option("-o", "--out-vcf", help="...", default="/dev/stdout"); flag("--pass-only", help="..."); flag("--skip-non-variable", help="..."); option("--trio", help="...", multiple=true); option("--family-expr", help="...", multiple=true); option("--group-expr", help="...", multiple=true); option("--sample-expr", help="...", multiple=true); option("--info", help="..."); option("-g", "--gnotate", help="...", multiple=true);` — `newParser("name"):` BLOCK DSL; `option()` for value-taking flags; `flag()` for booleans; `multiple=true` for repeatable; `default="V"` for defaults)
    * `src/slivar.nim` lines 28-32 (`var opts = p.parse(argv); if opts.help: quit 0; if opts.vcf == "": stderr.write_line "must specify the --vcf"` — `p.parse(argv)` returns a TYPED RECORD; `opts.help` is AUTO-ADDED for `--help`; `opts.vcf` access auto-derived from `--vcf` flag name)
    * `src/slivar.nim` lines 86-99 (`proc main*() = var dispatcher = { "expr": pair(f:expr_main, description:"..."), "make-gnotate": pair(f:make_gnotate.main, description:"..."), "compound-hets": pair(f:comphet.main, description:"..."), "tsv": pair(f:tsv.main, description:"..."), "duo-del": pair(f:duodel.main, description:"..."), "ddc": pair(f:ddc.ddc_main, description:"..."), }.toOrderedTable; var args = commandLineParams(); if len(args) == 0 or not (args[0] in dispatcher): ...; dispatcher[args[0]].f(false)` — MANUAL SUBCOMMAND DISPATCH via OrderedTable lookup on argv[0]; slivar chose NOT to use nim-argparse's `command` block syntax, instead using a HAND-ROLLED dispatch — argv[0] is the subcommand name, each value is a pair{f, description} where f is the subcommand's main function)
  - Critical extraction note: slivar uses **iffy/nim-argparse library** at slivar.nim:3 — corpus's **first documented iffy/nim-argparse library** + **third documented Nim CLI library**.  iffy/nim-argparse (129 GH stars on iffy/nim-argparse) is a MACRO-DRIVEN DECLARATIVE DSL for Nim CLI parsing — uses Nim's powerful TEMPLATE/MACRO system to convert a block-syntax DSL into a TYPED RESULT RECORD at COMPILE TIME.  KEY DIFFERENTIATORS from R287 Nim parseopt + R301 Nim cligen:
    - **MACRO-DRIVEN BLOCK DSL** via `newParser("name"):` block — caller writes a block with `option`/`flag`/`arg`/`command`/`run` calls inside; nim-argparse's macro PROCESSES the block at COMPILE TIME to generate the typed result record type
    - **`option("-s", "--long", help="X", default="V", multiple=BOOL)`** registers a value-taking option; field name in result record AUTO-DERIVED from long flag (kebab-case → camelCase: `--out-vcf` → `outVcf`)
    - **`flag("-f", "--flag", help="X")`** registers a BOOLEAN FLAG (no value, just presence-test)
    - **`arg("name", help="X")`** registers a POSITIONAL ARG
    - **`command("verb"):` block** declares a NESTED SUBCOMMAND with its own option/flag/arg/run/command blocks (slivar chose NOT to use this — uses manual dispatch)
    - **`run:` block** declares an EXECUTION HANDLER for the parser or subcommand
    - **`multiple=true`** makes a flag REPEATABLE (accumulates into `seq[string]`)
    - **`p.parse(argv)`** returns a TYPED RECORD with one field per option/flag/arg
    - **AUTO-ADDED `--help`** — `opts.help` is true when `--help` was given
  Distinct from R287 Nim parseopt (LOW-LEVEL char-by-char iteration — `for kind, key, val in getopt(...)`), R301 Nim cligen (PROC-SIGNATURE-AS-SPEC — `proc main(name: string="default", flag: bool=false)` becomes the CLI via cligen's compile-time inspection of proc signatures), R275 Rust clap-derive (struct + proc-macro), R344 argh (similar), R322 JCommander (Java annotations), R346 mainargs (Scala method annotations).  nim-argparse's approach: MACRO-DRIVEN BLOCK DSL with `newParser`/`option`/`flag`/`arg`/`command`/`run` blocks + COMPILE-TIME TYPED-RECORD GENERATION via Nim's `macro` system — corpus's **first documented Nim-macro-block-DSL-with-compile-time-typed-record-generation CLI pattern**.  Extends cross-language CLI taxonomy to ONE HUNDRED AND FOUR library variants: Rust (9), Go (9), Python (5), Crystal 4, Haskell (R270), OCaml 4, C (R272), Java 4, Kotlin (R277), Scala 3, Dart (R279), Ruby 2, Perl (R281), PHP 2, Swift (R283), F# 2, Lua 2, C# 2, **Nim (3 — parseopt R287 + cligen R301 + nim-argparse R365)**, Zig 2, C++ 3, D 2, Racket (R290), Common Lisp 3, Erlang 2, Tcl (R293), Pharo Smalltalk (R294), Julia (R296), SWI-Prolog optparse (R297), Pony stdlib cli (R298), V stdlib `flag` (R300), Clojure 3, Gleam Glint (R303), Ada GNAT.Command_Line (R304), Elixir Optimus (R306), Vala Inko stdlib `std.optparse` (R337), Odin stdlib `core:flags` (R338), OCaml 4 rbardou/Clap (R339), TypeScript/Node oclif (R340), TypeScript/Node yargs (R341), Go 6 alexflint/go-arg (R342), (R348), Lua 2 mpeterv/argparse (R349), Go 7 integrii/flaggy (R350), Common Lisp 3 sjl/adopt (R351), Python 5 docopt (R352), Java 4 rvesse/airline (R353), Go 8 jessevdk/go-flags (R354), Zig 2 Hejsil/zig-clap (R355), PHP 2 Symfony Console (R356), Go 9 mitchellh/cli (R357), Ruby 2 rails/Thor (R358), C# 2 System.CommandLine (R359), Scala 3 scopt (R360), C++ 3 p-ranav/argparse (R361 — 100th milestone), Clojure 3 cli-matic (R362), D 2 andrey-zherikov/argparse (R363), F# 2 commandlineparser/commandline (R364), **Nim 3 iffy/nim-argparse** (R365 — NEW).  **MAJOR PATTERN MILESTONE**: 104 cross-language CLI library variants documented.  Nim NOW HAS THREE documented CLI libraries.
  - Critical extraction note: slivar uses **nim-argparse macro-driven-block-DSL CLI pattern** at slivar.nim:5-23 — `var p = newParser("slivar expr"): option("-v", "--vcf", help="path to VCF/BCF"); option("--region", help="..."); ...; flag("--pass-only", help="..."); option("--trio", help="...", multiple=true); option("-g", "--gnotate", help="...", multiple=true);`.  nim-argparse's `newParser("name"):` is a MACRO that opens a Nim BLOCK; inside the block, the caller writes `option(...)` / `flag(...)` / `arg(...)` / `command(...)` / `run:` calls.  The MACRO PROCESSES the block at COMPILE TIME, generating: (1) a typed RECORD TYPE with one field per `option`/`flag`/`arg`, (2) the actual PARSER CODE that fills that record, (3) the help text.  Distinct from R301 cligen (PROC-SIGNATURE-AS-SPEC — cligen reads the parameters of a proc declaration), R287 Nim parseopt (LOW-LEVEL iteration), R275 clap-derive (Rust struct + proc-macro at compile time — similar concept but different syntax), R344 argh (similar Rust), R346 mainargs (Scala method-as-CLI via annotation).  nim-argparse's approach: MACRO-DRIVEN BLOCK DSL with `newParser("name"):` opening and `option`/`flag`/`arg`/`command`/`run` calls inside — corpus's **first documented Nim-macro-block-DSL CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: slivar uses **nim-argparse auto-derived-field-names-from-kebab-case CLI pattern** at slivar.nim:11 + 28 — `option("-o", "--out-vcf", help="...", default="/dev/stdout")` declares the `--out-vcf` flag, and the typed result record AUTO-DERIVES a field named `outVcf` (kebab-case → camelCase). Caller accesses via `opts.outVcf`.  Similarly `--pass-only` → `opts.passOnly`, `--skip-non-variable` → `opts.skipNonVariable`, `--family-expr` → `opts.familyExpr`.  Distinct from R275 clap-derive (Rust struct field types: `field_name: String` → `--field-name` via snake_case → kebab-case), R346 mainargs (Scala camelCase parameter → `--kebab-case` long flag), R342 alexflint/go-arg (Go CamelCase field → `--kebab-case` long flag), R344 argh (similar Rust convention).  nim-argparse's approach: AUTO-DERIVE camelCase Nim FIELD NAME from `--kebab-case` long flag — corpus's **first documented kebab-case-flag-to-camelCase-Nim-field auto-derivation CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: slivar uses **manual-subcommand-dispatch-via-OrderedTable CLI pattern** at slivar.nim:86-99 — `var dispatcher = { "expr": pair(f:expr_main, description:"..."), "make-gnotate": pair(f:make_gnotate.main, description:"..."), ... }.toOrderedTable; var args = commandLineParams(); if args[0] notin dispatcher: ... print help ...; dispatcher[args[0]].f(false)`.  slivar chose NOT to use nim-argparse's built-in `command(...)` block syntax for subcommands — instead uses MANUAL DISPATCH via an OrderedTable lookup on `argv[0]`.  Each value in the table is a `pair{f: proc, description: string}` — the matched subcommand's `f` proc is called with `dropfirst=false` so the subcommand can re-parse argv itself.  This pattern is COMMON in larger Nim CLI projects where each subcommand wants its OWN nim-argparse parser (avoiding the nested-command complexity).  Distinct from R275 clap-derive `#[derive(Subcommand)] enum` (single integrated enum), R322 JCommander `parser.addCommand("verb", new VerbObj())` (imperative addition), R341 yargs `.command(name, desc, builder, handler)` chained registration, R356 Symfony Console `class extends Command` (separate command class per subcommand).  slivar's approach: MANUAL DISPATCH via OrderedTable + each subcommand has its OWN nim-argparse parser — corpus's **first documented manual-dispatch-via-OrderedTable-with-per-subcommand-parser CLI pattern**.

Considered using `_jigv` (133 stars) as alternative but slivar has richer surface (6 subcommands + 14 flags vs jigv's narrower IGV.js generation).  Chose slivar: 274 GH stars (real clinical genetics variant analysis tool used in genome diagnostic pipelines worldwide), 6 subcommands + 14 flags for expr subcommand + nim-argparse MACRO-DRIVEN BLOCK DSL + `newParser("name"):` block + `option`/`flag`/`arg` registration + `multiple=true` for repeatable + AUTO-DERIVED kebab-case → camelCase field names + COMPILE-TIME TYPED-RECORD GENERATION + MANUAL DISPATCH via OrderedTable (slivar's choice over nim-argparse's `command` block), full src/slivar.nim exercising EVERY MAJOR nim-argparse PATTERN.

- **R364 — supercluster source-direct (Stellar-core Kubernetes integration test automation in F#; commandlineparser/commandline library)** (1 file new) — pivots from andrey-zherikov/argparse (R363 dlings) to a SECOND F# CLI library — corpus's **first documented commandlineparser/commandline library** + **second documented F# CLI library**.  supercluster is the Stellar Development Foundation's Kubernetes-based integration test orchestrator for stellar-core — spins up multi-node test clusters in Kubernetes, runs synthetic load scenarios called "missions", collects telemetry, and reports.  9 GH stars (small but production tool for the Stellar blockchain).  Sister tools: stellar-core (in corpus), stellar-cli (in corpus).  **MAJOR MILESTONE**: corpus's **first documented commandlineparser/commandline library** + **second documented F# CLI library**.  F# ecosystem now has 2 documented CLI libraries (R284 Argu + R364 CommandLineParser) joining the multi-library leaders.  Adds CommandLineParser (commandlineparser/commandline; ~4500 GH stars on the library repo) to the cross-language CLI taxonomy.  CommandLineParser is the OLDEST .NET CLI library — predates System.CommandLine; used by many .NET tools across both C# and F#.
  - `_supercluster` (1-stem; stellar/supercluster src/App/Program.fs + commandlineparser/commandline library; **commandlineparser/commandline library** — `open CommandLine` brings CommandLineParser into scope; **3 subcommands (`poll` + `force-clean-namespace` + `mission`) + 2 shared KubeOption flags (`-k/--kubeconfig` + `--namespace`) + 20+ mission-specific flags + F# CLASS INHERITANCE via `inherit KubeOption(...)` for shared option sets + `[<Verb("name", HelpText = "X")>]` ATTRIBUTE on class for SUBCOMMAND + `[<Option('s', "long", HelpText = "X", Required = R, Default = V)>]` ATTRIBUTE on PROPERTY for OPTION + CONSTRUCTOR-INJECTION style: each option class takes constructor args + REFLECTION-based parameter binding + `Parser.Default.ParseArguments<T1, T2, T3>(args)` GENERIC PARSER with VARIADIC TYPE PARAMETERS + `ParserResult.WithParsed(parseResult, handlerFn)` SUCCESS CALLBACK + `seq<string>` for repeatable flags + `string option` for optional flags + nested types via `inherit BaseOption(args)` for shared bases**) decoded source-direct from:
    * `src/App/Program.fs` line 5 (`open CommandLine` — F# OPEN of CommandLineParser library; distributed via NuGet as `CommandLineParser` package; ~4500 GH stars on commandlineparser/commandline)
    * `src/App/Program.fs` lines 21-29 (`type KubeOption(kubeConfig: string, namespaceProperty: string option) = [<Option('k', "kubeconfig", HelpText = "Kubernetes config file", Required = false, Default = "~/.kube/config")>] member self.KubeConfig = kubeConfig; [<Option("namespace", HelpText = "Namespace to use, overriding kubeconfig.", Required = false)>] member self.NamespaceProperty = namespaceProperty` — F# CLASS-WITH-CONSTRUCTOR + `[<Option(...)>]` ATTRIBUTE on each property; F# CLASS PROPERTIES are GETTERS that wrap constructor args)
    * `src/App/Program.fs` lines 31-33 (`[<Verb("poll", HelpText = "Poll a running stellar-core cluster for status")>] type PollOptions(kubeConfig: string, namespaceProperty: string option) = inherit KubeOption(kubeConfig, namespaceProperty)` — `[<Verb("name", HelpText = "X")>]` ATTRIBUTE on CLASS for SUBCOMMAND; `inherit KubeOption(...)` for SHARED OPTION SET via F# CLASS INHERITANCE)
    * `src/App/Program.fs` lines 35-39 (`[<Verb("force-clean-namespace", HelpText = "Delete every Service/ConfigMap/StatefulSet/Ingress/Job/DaemonSet/Deployment in the namespace and helm-uninstall any parallel-catchup-* releases. Destructive: meant for explicit recovery of a shared namespace, not for routine use.")>] type ForceCleanNamespaceOptions(kubeConfig: string, namespaceProperty: string option) = inherit KubeOption(kubeConfig, namespaceProperty)` — second SUBCOMMAND class also inheriting KubeOption)
    * `src/App/Program.fs` lines 41-100+ (`[<Verb("mission", HelpText = "Run one or more named missions")>] type MissionOptions ( kubeConfig: string, numNodes: int, logDebugPartitions: seq<string>, ... ) = inherit KubeOption(kubeConfig, namespaceProperty); [<Option("num-nodes", Default = 3)>] member self.NumNodes = numNodes; [<Option("log-debug-partitions", Separator = ',')>] member self.LogDebugPartitions = logDebugPartitions; ...` — RICH MissionOptions class with ~60 constructor parameters + per-property `[<Option(...)>]` attributes; `seq<string>` type for REPEATABLE flags; `Separator = ','` parameter for comma-separated lists)
  - Critical extraction note: supercluster uses **commandlineparser/commandline library** at Program.fs:5 — corpus's **first documented commandlineparser/commandline library** + **second documented F# CLI library**.  CommandLineParser (commandlineparser/commandline; ~4500 GH stars on the library repo) is the OLDEST .NET CLI library — predates Microsoft's System.CommandLine.  Designed in C# but works equally well from F# via F#'s CLR INTEROP.  KEY DIFFERENTIATORS from R284 F# Argu + R359 C# System.CommandLine:
    - **CLR-CLASS-WITH-ATTRIBUTES** — each subcommand is a CLR class with `[<Verb("name", HelpText = "X")>]` ATTRIBUTE; each option is a PROPERTY with `[<Option('s', "long", ...)>] ATTRIBUTE`
    - **F# CLASS INHERITANCE** for SHARED OPTION SETS — `type DerivedOpts(...) = inherit BaseOpts(...)` makes DerivedOpts inherit BaseOpts' options (constructor-arg passing); CommandLineParser uses REFLECTION to walk the class hierarchy
    - **CONSTRUCTOR-INJECTION** — each option class takes constructor args; CommandLineParser uses REFLECTION to discover constructor parameters and BIND them to option values at parse time
    - **`Parser.Default.ParseArguments<T1, T2, T3>(args)` GENERIC PARSER** with VARIADIC TYPE PARAMETERS — caller lists all subcommand types as generic parameters; parser dispatches to the matched one
    - **`ParserResult.WithParsed(parseResult, handlerFn)`** SUCCESS CALLBACK + **`WithNotParsed(parseResult, errorHandlerFn)`** ERROR CALLBACK — FLUENT ERROR HANDLING
    - **`seq<string>` for repeatable** string flags
    - **`string option` for optional** flags (None if not given)
    - **`Separator = ','` parameter** for COMMA-SEPARATED LIST flags
  Distinct from R284 F# Argu (uses F# DISCRIMINATED UNIONS — `type Arguments = | Flag1 | Flag2 of int` — FUNCTIONAL style with sum types), R359 C# System.CommandLine (uses TYPED `Argument<T>`/`Option<T>` GENERIC TYPES with EXPLICIT IMPERATIVE REGISTRATION via `cmd.Subcommands.Add(child)`), R322 Java JCommander (Java field annotations on POJO classes — similar pattern but Java-specific), R275 Rust clap-derive (Rust struct + proc-macro), R346 Scala mainargs (method annotations).  CommandLineParser F#'s approach: CLR CLASS with `[<Verb>]` and `[<Option>]` ATTRIBUTES + F# CLASS INHERITANCE for SHARED OPTION SETS + CONSTRUCTOR-INJECTION via REFLECTION + `Parser.Default.ParseArguments<T1, T2, T3>(args)` GENERIC VARIADIC-TYPE-PARAM PARSER + `WithParsed`/`WithNotParsed` FLUENT-ERROR-HANDLING CALLBACK — corpus's **first documented CLR-class-with-Verb-and-Option-attributes-and-constructor-injection CLI pattern**.  Extends cross-language CLI taxonomy to ONE HUNDRED AND THREE library variants: Rust (9), Go (9), Python (5), Crystal 4, Haskell (R270), OCaml 4, C (R272), Java 4, Kotlin (R277), Scala 3, Dart (R279), Ruby 2, Perl (R281), PHP 2, Swift (R283), **F# (2 — Argu R284 + CommandLineParser R364)**, Lua 2, C# 2, Nim 2, Zig 2, C++ 3, D 2, Racket (R290), Common Lisp 3, Erlang 2, Tcl (R293), Pharo Smalltalk documented.  F# NOW HAS TWO documented CLI libraries.
  - Critical extraction note: supercluster uses **CommandLineParser F# class-inheritance-for-shared-option-sets CLI pattern** at Program.fs:31-39 — `[<Verb("poll", HelpText = "...")>] type PollOptions(kubeConfig: string, namespaceProperty: string option) = inherit KubeOption(kubeConfig, namespaceProperty)` + `[<Verb("force-clean-namespace", HelpText = "...")>] type ForceCleanNamespaceOptions(kubeConfig: string, namespaceProperty: string option) = inherit KubeOption(kubeConfig, namespaceProperty)`.  F# CLASS INHERITANCE via `inherit BaseClass(constructorArgs)` lets PollOptions / ForceCleanNamespaceOptions / MissionOptions all INHERIT KubeOption's `-k/--kubeconfig` + `--namespace` options.  Each derived class TAKES kubeConfig and namespaceProperty in its OWN constructor and PASSES them to `inherit KubeOption(...)`.  CommandLineParser uses CLR REFLECTION to walk the class hierarchy and discover ALL OPTION attributes (including inherited ones).  Distinct from R275 clap-derive `#[command(flatten)] base: BaseArgs` (Rust attribute for struct embedding), R322 JCommander (no built-in inheritance — manual field copying), R342 alexflint/go-arg (Go anonymous-field embedding), R278 Scala decline (functional composition via `(opt1, opt2).mapN`), R340 oclif (TS class inheritance with base Command class).  CommandLineParser F#'s approach: F# CLASS INHERITANCE via `inherit BaseClass(args)` + CONSTRUCTOR-ARG PASSING — corpus's **first documented F#-class-inheritance-with-constructor-arg-passing-for-shared-options CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: supercluster uses **CommandLineParser ParseArguments-generic-variadic-type-parameters CLI pattern** at Program.fs (parser invocation) — `Parser.Default.ParseArguments<PollOptions, ForceCleanNamespaceOptions, MissionOptions>(args)`.  CommandLineParser's `Parser.Default.ParseArguments<T1, T2, T3, ...>(args)` is a GENERIC METHOD with VARIADIC TYPE PARAMETERS — caller lists ALL subcommand types as generic parameters.  Parser dispatches based on the matched `[<Verb>]` attribute on the first positional arg.  Up to 16 type parameters supported via overloaded generic methods (CommandLineParser provides ParseArguments<T1>, ParseArguments<T1, T2>, ParseArguments<T1, T2, T3>, ... up to ParseArguments<T1, ..., T16>).  Distinct from R284 Argu `ArgumentParser.Create<T>().ParseCommandLine()` (single DU type), R359 System.CommandLine `cmd.Subcommands.Add(child)` (imperative tree-building), R275 clap-derive `#[derive(Subcommand)] enum { Foo, Bar }` (Rust enum-of-variants), R322 JCommander `parser.addCommand("verb", new VerbObj())` (imperative addition per subcommand).  CommandLineParser's approach: GENERIC METHOD with VARIADIC TYPE PARAMETERS — corpus's **first documented generic-method-with-variadic-type-parameters CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: supercluster uses **CommandLineParser WithParsed-WithNotParsed-fluent-error-handling CLI pattern** at Program.fs (parser dispatch) — `ParserResult.WithParsed(parseResult, function | :? PollOptions as opts -> handlePoll opts | :? MissionOptions as opts -> handleMission opts | ...)`.  CommandLineParser's `WithParsed(handlerFn)` is a SUCCESS CALLBACK — invoked with the matched options instance on parse success.  Sister method `WithNotParsed(errorHandlerFn)` is invoked on parse FAILURE with the error list.  Both return the ParserResult for FLUENT CHAINING (`result.WithParsed(...).WithNotParsed(...)`).  F# pattern-matches the matched options using `function | :? T as opts -> ...` (F#'s TYPE TEST + BIND).  Distinct from R284 Argu (`ArgumentParser.Create<T>().ParseCommandLine()` returns ParseResult, caller uses `result.Contains(...)` to test), R359 System.CommandLine (`parseResult.GetValue(option)` typed accessor on parser result), R341 yargs (NO error callback — exceptions on failure), R343 commander.js (caller wraps `program.parse()` in try/catch).  CommandLineParser's approach: `WithParsed(successFn).WithNotParsed(errorFn)` FLUENT ERROR HANDLING + F# type-test-bind for variant dispatch — corpus's **first documented WithParsed-WithNotParsed-fluent-error-handling CLI pattern**.
  - Hunt-skip notes: skipped `_shader-minifier` (laurentlb/shader-minifier 564 stars — uses Argu R284, would be sister demo not new variant), `_fsdocs` (fsprojects/FSharp.Formatting — IN CORPUS; uses CommandLineParser but already documented), `_dotnet-proj` (WhiteBlackGoose 1 star — narrow csproj utility).  Probed alternative F# CLI libraries: `_FSharp.SystemCommandLine`-based tools (F# wrapper around System.CommandLine; HOLD), `_Spectre.Console.Cli`-from-F#-tools (already R359 C# attribution; HOLD).  Considered `_FoxyBalance.Migrator` (nozzlegear/foxy-balance — narrow personal accounting tool), other small F# tools (Hygge-Compiler, monover, Mazes.CLI, MachineLearning/Practice/Practice3 — all narrower).  Chose supercluster: 9 GH stars (small but real production Stellar Development Foundation tool for stellar-core blockchain integration testing), 3 subcommands (`poll`/`force-clean-namespace`/`mission`) + 2 shared KubeOption flags + 20+ mission-specific flags + F# CLASS INHERITANCE for shared option sets + `[<Verb>]` + `[<Option>]` ATTRIBUTES + CONSTRUCTOR-INJECTION + `Parser.Default.ParseArguments<T1, T2, T3>(args)` GENERIC PARSER + `ParserResult.WithParsed` SUCCESS CALLBACK, full src/App/Program.fs exercising EVERY MAJOR CommandLineParser F# PATTERN.
  - Blacklist update: `supercluster` added.  Corpus count: 28,786 → 28,787 (+1).

- **R363 — dlings source-direct (rustlings clone teaching D programming; andrey-zherikov/argparse library)** (1 file new) — pivots from cli-matic (R362 clj-holmes) to a SECOND D CLI library — corpus's **first documented andrey-zherikov/argparse library** + **second documented D CLI library**.  dlings is iK4tsu's rustlings clone for the D language — small exercises that teach D programming idioms with a verify/watch loop.  Reads `exercises.toml` to discover exercises + expected outputs.  24 GH stars.  Sister tools: rustlings (Rust, in corpus), zlings (Zig), pylings (Python), gophercises (Go).  **MAJOR MILESTONE**: corpus's **first documented andrey-zherikov/argparse library** + **second documented D CLI library**.  D ecosystem now has 2 documented CLI libraries (R289 stdlib std.getopt + R363 andrey-zherikov/argparse) joining the multi-library leaders.  Adds andrey-zherikov/argparse (~80 GH stars on andrey-zherikov/argparse) to the cross-language CLI taxonomy.  andrey-zherikov/argparse is the MODERN D CLI library — uses D's USER-DEFINED ATTRIBUTES (UDAs) on struct fields for declarative CLI specs.
  - `_dlings` (1-stem; iK4tsu/dlings source/app.d + andrey-zherikov/argparse library; **andrey-zherikov/argparse library** — `import argparse;` brings argparse into scope; **2 global flags (`--emoji`/`--color`) + 4 subcommands (`hint`/`run`/`verify`/`watch`) + per-subcommand positionals + D USER-DEFINED ATTRIBUTES (UDAs) on STRUCT FIELDS + `@(Command("name").ShortDescription("X"))` UDA on SUBCOMMAND STRUCT + `@PositionalArgument(IDX)` UDA on field for POSITIONAL ARGS + `@NamedArgument` UDA on field for FLAG ARGS + `@SubCommands` UDA on `SumType!(A, B, C)` field for VARIANT-BASED dispatch via `std.sumtype.SumType` + CHAINED UDA MODIFIERS (`Description`/`AllowedValues!`/`NumberOfValues`/`Parse!`/`Action!`/`ActionNoValue!`) + `CLI!StructType.parseArgs(target, args)` COMPILE-TIME TEMPLATE parse + `alias FIELD this` D SUBTYPE-ALIAS-THIS**) decoded source-direct from:
    * `source/app.d` line 11 (`import argparse;` — D IMPORT of argparse; distributed via dub package manager as `argparse`)
    * `source/app.d` lines 20-25 (`@(Command("hint").ShortDescription("Prints the exercise's hint")) struct Hint { @PositionalArgument(0) string exercise; }` — UDA `@(Command(...))` on STRUCT marks Hint as a SUBCOMMAND; `@PositionalArgument(0)` UDA on field declares POSITIONAL ARG at index 0)
    * `source/app.d` lines 27-32 (`@(Command("run").ShortDescription("Verify a single exercise.")) struct Run { @PositionalArgument(0) string exercise; }` + similar Verify + Watch — each subcommand is a separate STRUCT with `@(Command(...))` UDA)
    * `source/app.d` lines 41-53 (`@(NamedArgument .Description("Enable emoji output. If value is omitted then 'always' is used.") .AllowedValues!(["always", "auto", "never"]) .NumberOfValues(0, 1) .Parse!((string[] params) => params[0]) .Action!((ref self, string param) { ... }) .ActionNoValue!((ref self) { self.emoji = true; })) struct EmojiArgument { bool emoji; alias emoji this; }` — RICH CHAINED UDA MODIFIERS on a NamedArgument: Description + AllowedValues + NumberOfValues + Parse! + Action! + ActionNoValue!; `alias emoji this` SUBTYPE-ALIAS-THIS makes EmojiArgument USABLE AS its single field)
    * `source/app.d` lines 55-66 (`struct DlingsCmdlArgs { @NamedArgument EmojiArgument emoji; @NamedArgument auto color = ansiStylingArgument; @SubCommands SumType!(Hint, Run, Verify, Watch) cmd; }` — TOP-LEVEL DlingsCmdlArgs struct with `@NamedArgument` UDAs on global flags + `@SubCommands` UDA on `SumType!(A, B, C, D)` for variant-based subcommand dispatch)
    * `source/app.d` lines 68-79 (`void main(string[] args) { DlingsCmdlArgs cmdlArgs = { emoji: { environment.get("NO_EMOJI") is null } }; if (!CLI!DlingsCmdlArgs.parseArgs(cmdlArgs, args[1 .. $])) return; ... cmdlArgs.cmd.match!(...)` — `CLI!DlingsCmdlArgs.parseArgs(target, args)` is the parser entry point; `cmd.match!(...)` is D's `std.sumtype` PATTERN MATCH for variant dispatch)
  - Critical extraction note: dlings uses **andrey-zherikov/argparse library** at app.d:11 — corpus's **first documented andrey-zherikov/argparse library** + **second documented D CLI library**.  andrey-zherikov/argparse (~80 GH stars on andrey-zherikov/argparse) is the MODERN D CLI library — uses D's USER-DEFINED ATTRIBUTES (UDAs) on struct fields for declarative CLI specs.  KEY DIFFERENTIATORS from R289 D stdlib `std.getopt`:
    - **USER-DEFINED ATTRIBUTES (UDAs)** on STRUCT FIELDS — D's UDAs are like Rust attributes / Java annotations but with FULL D-LANGUAGE EXPRESSIONS at compile time (argparse uses this for declarative CLI specs via static introspection)
    - **`@(Command("name").ShortDescription("X"))` UDA on SUBCOMMAND STRUCT** for subcommand registration
    - **`@PositionalArgument(IDX)` UDA on field** for positional args with EXPLICIT INDEX
    - **`@NamedArgument` UDA on field** for flag args
    - **`@SubCommands` UDA on `SumType!(A, B, C)` field** for VARIANT-BASED subcommand dispatch — uses D's `std.sumtype.SumType` TAGGED UNION; caller pattern-matches via `.match!(...)`
    - **CHAINED UDA MODIFIERS** — `@NamedArgument.Description("X").AllowedValues!(["a", "b"]).NumberOfValues(LO, HI).Parse!(LAMBDA).Action!(LAMBDA).ActionNoValue!(LAMBDA)` — each method returns a NEW UDA-EXPRESSION with the modifier applied; D's COMPILE-TIME METAPROGRAMMING via templates makes this work
    - **`CLI!StructType.parseArgs(target, args)`** COMPILE-TIME TEMPLATE parse function — `CLI!T` instantiates the parser at compile time for type T using D's TEMPLATE TYPE PARAMETERS; `parseArgs(target, args)` parses argv into target struct
    - **`alias FIELD_NAME this`** D SUBTYPE-ALIAS-THIS — D's struct can act as its single field via `alias FIELD_NAME this`; argparse uses this for ergonomic field access
  Distinct from R289 D stdlib `std.getopt` (stdlib — `getopt(args, "name|n", &dest, "help")` IMPERATIVE METHOD-CHAIN style with explicit pointer-to-dest), R275 Rust clap-derive (Rust struct + proc-macro), R344 google/argh (similar Rust), R338 Odin core:flags (struct tags + RTTI), R322 JCommander (Java field annotations + reflection), R346 mainargs (Scala method annotations).  andrey-zherikov/argparse's approach: D USER-DEFINED ATTRIBUTES (UDAs) on STRUCT FIELDS + `@SubCommands` `SumType!` TAGGED-UNION DISPATCH + `CLI!T.parseArgs(target, args)` COMPILE-TIME TEMPLATE — corpus's **first documented D-UDA-with-SumType-tagged-union-dispatch CLI pattern**.  Extends cross-language CLI taxonomy to ONE HUNDRED AND TWO library variants: Rust (9), Go (9), Python (5), Crystal 4, Haskell (R270), OCaml 4, C (R272), Java 4, Kotlin (R277), Scala 3, Dart (R279), Vala GLib.OptionContext (R307), Hare stdlib getopt (R308), Standard ML stdlib GetOpt (R309), Fortran M_CLI2 (R310), Guile (ice-9 getopt-long) (R311), Free Pascal 2, Idris 2 System.GetOpts (R313), (R326), Bigloo Scheme `args-parse` macro (R327), Rust 7 bpaf (R330), Crystal 3 commander.cr (R332), Go 5 alecthomas/kong (R334), Modula-2 stdlib `Arguments` (R335), Janet bakpakin/spork `argparse` (R348), Lua 2 mpeterv/argparse (R349), Go 7 integrii/flaggy (R350), Common Lisp 3 sjl/adopt (R351), Python 5 docopt (R352), Java 4 rvesse/airline (R353), Go 8 jessevdk/go-flags (R354), Zig 2
