  - Critical extraction note: gzr uses **Thor class_option-vs-option-for-global-vs-per-command CLI pattern** at lib/gzr/cli.rb:34-47 — `class_option :debug, type: :boolean, default: false, desc: 'Run in debug mode'; class_option :host, type: :string, default: 'localhost', desc: 'Looker Host'; class_option :port, ...`.  Thor distinguishes GLOBAL OPTIONS via `class_option` (visible to ALL commands in this class) vs PER-COMMAND OPTIONS via `option` (visible only to the NEXT command method to be defined — Thor's BINDING-TO-NEXT-METHOD pattern same as `desc`).  Both use the same syntax `option :name, type: :T, default: V, desc: 'X'` — only difference is `class_option` vs `option`.  Distinct from R322 JCommander (`@Parameters` on class field for global vs per-subcommand-instance annotation), R275 clap-derive (`#[arg(global = true)]` attribute argument), R356 Symfony Console (app-wide via `$application->getDefinition()->addOption(...)` vs per-command via `$this->addOption(...)`), R341 yargs (`{global: true}` per-flag directive), R340 oclif (base class inheritance for shared flags).  Thor's approach: `class_option` vs `option` METHOD NAME DISTINCTION for scope — corpus's **first documented class_option-vs-option-method-name-distinction CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: gzr uses **Thor register-subcommand-as-separate-class CLI pattern** at lib/gzr/cli.rb:62-94 — `require_relative 'commands/alert'; register Gzr::Commands::Alert, 'alert', 'alert [SUBCOMMAND]', 'Command description...'` (and 14 similar `register ...` calls).  Thor's `register(SubClass, 'name', 'usage', 'description')` REGISTERS a separate THOR SUBCLASS as a SUBCOMMAND.  When user types `gzr alert init`, Thor STRIPS the `alert` subcommand name, instantiates `Gzr::Commands::Alert`, and dispatches to its `#init` method.  Each registered subclass can have its OWN class_options, options, and command methods — providing UNLIMITED NESTING via recursive `register` calls.  Distinct from R322 JCommander `parser.addCommand("verb", new VerbInstance())` (imperative add per subcommand), R275 clap-derive `#[derive(Subcommand)] enum { Foo(FooArgs) }` (enum-of-variants), R340 oclif filesystem-hierarchy-walking, R341 yargs `.command(name, desc, builder, handler)` chained registration, R346 mainargs MULTIPLE `@main` methods (each becomes top-level subcommand on same class — no separation into subclasses).  Thor's approach: `register SubClass, 'verb', 'usage', 'description'` REGISTERS subcommand-as-separate-Thor-subclass — corpus's **first documented register-subcommand-as-separate-Thor-subclass CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: gzr uses **Thor map-flag-and-subcommand-aliases CLI pattern** at lib/gzr/cli.rb:57 + 58 — `map %w(--version -v) => :version` + `map space: :folder`.  Thor's `map` METHOD registers ALIASES — left side is the ALIAS NAME(s), right side is the TARGET COMMAND symbol.  Both FLAG ALIASES (`--version` and `-v` aliases for `version` subcommand) and SUBCOMMAND ALIASES (`space` alias for `folder` subcommand) use the same `map` mechanism.  Hash syntax: `map alias_name: :target` or `map %w(alias1 alias2) => :target` for multiple aliases at once.  Distinct from R275 clap-derive `#[arg(alias = "X")]` (attribute argument per-flag), R322 JCommander `@Parameter(names = ["-a", "-b", "--alpha"])` (annotation with names array), R340 oclif `static aliases = ['X']` (static field on class), R346 mainargs `@arg(name = "X", short = 'y')` (annotation with explicit name+short), R341 yargs `.alias('a', 'alpha')` (per-flag method).  Thor's approach: `map ALIAS_NAME => :TARGET_SYMBOL` HASH-BASED alias registration that works for BOTH flag aliases AND subcommand aliases — corpus's **first documented map-hash-based-alias-for-both-flag-and-subcommand CLI pattern**.
  - Hunt-skip notes: skipped `_bundler`/`_rails`/`_jekyll`/`_chef`/`_pod`/`_fastlane`/`_vagrant`/`_fpm`/`_haml`/`_middleman` (all IN CORPUS — all use Thor but already documented under different lib attribution or as bare-tool completions), `_capistrano`/`_padrino`/`_foreman` (likely IN CORPUS).  Probed alternative Ruby CLI libraries: `_optparse`-based tools (Ruby stdlib — would be R280 sister demo; HOLD), `_gli`-based tools (GLI = Git-like-interface; would be 3rd Ruby CLI library; HOLD), `_commander`-based tools (Ruby commander.rb — would be 4th Ruby CLI library; HOLD).  Considered `_dru` (nebulab/dru — narrow Docker dev tool), `_pt` (raul/pt — Pivotal Tracker CLI), `_runa` (ongaeshi/runa — narrow Ruby task runner), `_eps` (ankane/eps — narrow EPS file processor), `_tty` (piotrmurach/tty 2531 stars — toolkit not a single CLI), `_volt` (voltrb/volt — web framework).  Chose gzr: 133 GH stars (real production Looker BI Content Management CLI from Google Looker Data Sciences team), 15 global flags + 16 top-level subcommands + Thor CLASS INHERITANCE + RUBY METHOD-AS-COMMAND + `class_option` global vs `option` per-command + `register SubClass` subcommand registration + `map` aliases for both flags and subcommands + `desc 'verb', 'description'` method binding + `self.exit_on_failure?` exit control, full lib/gzr/cli.rb exercising EVERY MAJOR Thor PATTERN.
  - Blacklist update: `gzr` added.  Corpus count: 28,780 → 28,781 (+1).

- **R357 — muxy source-direct (chaos engineering tool for simulating distributed system failures in Go; mitchellh/cli library)** (1 file new) — pivots from Symfony Console (R356 grumphp) to a NINTH Go CLI library — corpus's **first documented mitchellh/cli library** + **ninth documented Go CLI library**.  muxy is Matt Fellows's (mefellows) CHAOS ENGINEERING TOOL — injects deterministic failures into HTTP/TCP traffic (latency, bandwidth limits, packet loss, malformed responses, slow body streaming, etc.) for distributed system resilience testing.  829 GH stars.  Sister tools: chaos-mesh (Kubernetes chaos, in corpus), pumba (Docker chaos, in corpus), toxiproxy (Shopify TCP chaos proxy).  **MAJOR MILESTONE**: corpus's **first documented mitchellh/cli library** + **ninth documented Go CLI library** — Go ecosystem now has 9 documented CLI libraries (cobra/urfave-cli/kingpin/spf13-pflag/alecthomas-kong/alexflint-go-arg/integrii-flaggy/jessevdk-go-flags/mitchellh-cli) — extends Go's documented-CLI-library lead to TIE with Rust 9.  Adds mitchellh/cli (Mitchell Hashimoto's — HashiCorp co-founder — Go CLI library, ~1600 GH stars) to the cross-language CLI taxonomy.  Used by HashiCorp Consul, Vault, Terraform, Packer, Boundary, Nomad, Vagrant, SPIRE-agent/SPIRE-server, and many others.
  - `_muxy` (1-stem; mefellows/muxy main.go + command/*.go + mitchellh/cli library; **mitchellh/cli library** — `import "github.com/mitchellh/cli"` brings mitchellh/cli into scope; **2 subcommands (`proxy` + `pki`) + 1 flag on proxy (`--config FILE`) + auto-added `--help` + `--version` + INTERFACE-BASED command-as-struct + 3-method `cli.Command` INTERFACE (`Run(args []string) int` + `Help() string` + `Synopsis() string`) + `cli.NewCLI(name, version)` CONSTRUCTOR + `cli.Args` field assignment + `cli.Commands = map[string]cli.CommandFactory{...}` SUBCOMMAND MAP + `cli.CommandFactory` factory function type + `cli.Ui` INTERFACE for output abstraction + `cli.BasicUi`/`cli.ColoredUi` IMPLEMENTATIONS + standard `flag` package for per-command flag parsing**) decoded source-direct from:
    * `main.go` lines 6-12 (`import ( "github.com/mefellows/muxy/command" _ "github.com/mefellows/muxy/middleware" _ "github.com/mefellows/muxy/protocol" _ "github.com/mefellows/muxy/symptom" "github.com/mitchellh/cli" )` — Go IMPORTS bringing mitchellh/cli into scope; mitchellh/cli is distributed via Go modules at `github.com/mitchellh/cli`)
    * `main.go` lines 21-30 (`cli := cli.NewCLI(strings.ToLower(ApplicationName), Version); cli.Args = os.Args[1:]; cli.Commands = command.Commands; exitStatus, err := cli.Run()` — `cli.NewCLI(name, version)` CONSTRUCTOR; `cli.Args` FIELD ASSIGNMENT with argv slice; `cli.Commands = command.Commands` SUBCOMMAND MAP assignment; `cli.Run()` PARSE + DISPATCH returning exit status)
    * `command/commands.go` lines 38-47 (`Commands = map[string]cli.CommandFactory{ "proxy": func() (cli.Command, error) { return &ProxyCommand{ Meta: meta }, nil }, "pki": func() (cli.Command, error) { return &pki.PkiCommand{}, nil } }` — SUBCOMMAND MAP using `cli.CommandFactory` type which is `func() (cli.Command, error)` — caller maps subcommand names to factory closures that construct the Command struct)
    * `command/proxy.go` lines 13-30 (`type ProxyCommand struct { Meta Meta }; func (pc *ProxyCommand) Run(args []string) int { cmdFlags := flag.NewFlagSet("proxy", flag.ContinueOnError); cmdFlags.Usage = func() { pc.Meta.UI.Output(pc.Help()) }; cmdFlags.StringVar(&c.ConfigFile, "config", "", "Path to a YAML configuration file"); if err := cmdFlags.Parse(args); err != nil { return 1 }; muxy.Run(); return 0 }` — COMMAND STRUCT implementing `cli.Command` interface with `Run(args) int`; INSIDE Run uses STANDARD `flag` package for per-command flag parsing — mitchellh/cli does NOT provide its own flag parsing, only subcommand dispatch + help system + UI abstraction)
    * `command/proxy.go` lines 33-46 (`func (pc *ProxyCommand) Help() string { helpText := \`\nUsage: muck proxy [options]\n\n  Run the Muck proxy.\n\nOptions:\n\n  --config                    Location of Muxy configuration file\n\`; return strings.TrimSpace(helpText) }` — `Help() string` METHOD returns HAND-WRITTEN HELP TEXT — mitchellh/cli does NOT auto-generate help; caller writes it themselves)
    * `command/proxy.go` lines 49-52 (`func (pc *ProxyCommand) Synopsis() string { return "Run the Muxy proxy" }` — `Synopsis() string` METHOD returns ONE-LINE SYNOPSIS for the top-level command list)
    * `command/commands.go` lines 30-36 (`UI = &cli.ColoredUi{ Ui: &cli.BasicUi{Writer: os.Stdout, Reader: os.Stdin, ErrorWriter: os.Stderr}, OutputColor: cli.UiColorNone, InfoColor: cli.UiColorNone, ErrorColor: cli.UiColorRed }` — UI ABSTRACTION via `cli.Ui` INTERFACE; `cli.BasicUi` for basic output; `cli.ColoredUi` wraps `BasicUi` adding ANSI color codes; `cli.UiColorNone`/`cli.UiColorRed`/etc. COLOR CONSTANTS)
  - Critical extraction note: muxy uses **mitchellh/cli library** at main.go:12 — corpus's **first documented mitchellh/cli library** + **ninth documented Go CLI library**.  Mitchell Hashimoto's mitchellh/cli (HashiCorp co-founder; ~1600 GH stars on mitchellh/cli) is the Go CLI library used by virtually every HashiCorp tool: Consul, Vault, Terraform, Packer, Boundary, Nomad, Vagrant (Ruby + Go components), plus SPIRE-agent/SPIRE-server (SPIFFE), muxy, kite (koding/kite micro-service framework), krab, and many others.  KEY DIFFERENTIATORS:
    - **INTERFACE-BASED** — each subcommand is a STRUCT IMPLEMENTING `cli.Command` interface with 3 methods: `Run(args []string) int` (handler returning exit code), `Help() string` (detailed help text), `Synopsis() string` (one-line synopsis)
    - **SUBCOMMAND DISPATCH via `map[string]cli.CommandFactory`** — caller maps subcommand names to factory functions (`func() (cli.Command, error)`)
    - **NO BUILT-IN FLAG PARSING** — each Command typically uses standard `flag` package internally for per-command flag parsing; mitchellh/cli ONLY provides subcommand dispatch + help system + UI abstraction
    - **UI ABSTRACTION via `cli.Ui` interface** — `BasicUi`/`ColoredUi`/`MockUi` implementations for output/error/info channels with color support
    - **HELP TEXT IS HAND-WRITTEN** per command via `Help()` method — mitchellh/cli does NOT auto-generate help from a spec; caller writes the help text themselves
  Distinct from R275 Rust clap-derive (struct + proc-macro), R334 Go alecthomas/kong (struct tags + reflection), R342 alexflint/go-arg (struct tags + reflection), R350 integrii/flaggy (pointer-based imperative builder), R354 jessevdk/go-flags (struct tags), R340 oclif (TS class-inheritance), R341 yargs (TS fluent builder), R343 commander.js (TS explicit-instance), R346 mainargs (Scala annotations).  mitchellh/cli's approach: INTERFACE-BASED with 3-METHOD INTERFACE + SUBCOMMAND MAP + STDLIB-FLAG DELEGATION for per-command parsing + HAND-WRITTEN HELP TEXT — corpus's **first documented interface-based-with-stdlib-flag-delegation-and-hand-written-help CLI pattern**.  Extends cross-language CLI taxonomy to NINETY-SIX library variants: Rust (9), **Go (9 — cobra/urfave-cli/kingpin/spf13-pflag/alecthomas-kong/alexflint-go-arg/integrii-flaggy/jessevdk-go-flags/mitchellh-cli)**, Python (5), Crystal 4, Haskell (R270), OCaml 4, C (R272), Java 4, Kotlin (R277), Scala 2, Dart (R279), Ruby (R280), Perl (R281), PHP 2, Swift (R283), F# (R284), Lua 2, C# (R286), Nim 2, Zig 2, D (R289), Racket (R290), Common Lisp 3, Erlang 2, Tcl (R293), Pharo Smalltalk (R294), Julia (R296), SWI-Prolog optparse (R297), Pony stdlib cli (R298), V stdlib `flag` (R300), Clojure 2, Gleam Glint (R303), Ada GNAT.Command_Line (R304), C++ 2, Elixir Optimus (R306), Vala GLib.OptionContext (R307), Hare stdlib getopt (R308), Standard ML stdlib GetOpt (R309), Fortran M_CLI2 (R310), Guile (ice-9 getopt-long) (R311), Free Pascal 2, Idris 2 System.GetOpts (R313), Mercury stdlib getopt_io (R314), Eiffel Gobo AP_PARSER (R315), Forth/Gforth `next-arg` (R317), Roc weaver (R319), Chapel `config` keyword (R320), Mojo 2, Chicken Scheme `args` egg (R324), Lean 4 Cli (R326), Bigloo Scheme `args-parse` macro (R327), Rust 7 bpaf (R330), Crystal 3 commander.cr (R332), Go 5 alecthomas/kong (R334), Modula-2 stdlib `Arguments` (R335), Janet bakpakin/spork `argparse` (R336), Inko stdlib `std.optparse` (R337), Odin stdlib `core:flags` (R338), OCaml 4 rbardou/Clap (R339), TypeScript/Node oclif (R340), TypeScript/Node yargs (R341), Go 6 alexflint/go-arg (R342), TypeScript/Node 3 tj/commander.js (R343), Rust 8 google/argh (R344), Python 4 BrianPugh/cyclopts (R345), Scala 2 lihaoyi/mainargs (R346), Rust 9 RazrFalcon/pico-args (R347), Crystal 4 at-grandpa/clim (R348), Lua 2 mpeterv/argparse (R349), Go 7 integrii/flaggy (R350), Common Lisp 3 sjl/adopt (R351), Python 5 docopt (R352), Java 4 rvesse/airline (R353), Go 8 jessevdk/go-flags (R354), Zig 2 Hejsil/zig-clap (R355), PHP 2 Symfony Console (R356), **Go 9 mitchellh/cli** (R357 — NEW).  **MAJOR PATTERN MILESTONE**: 96 cross-language CLI library variants documented.  Go NOW HAS NINE documented CLI libraries — TIED with Rust 9 as the MOST-DOCUMENTED CLI-LANGUAGE.
  - Critical extraction note: muxy uses **mitchellh/cli interface-based command struct CLI pattern** at command/proxy.go:9-52 — `type ProxyCommand struct { Meta Meta }` + `func (pc *ProxyCommand) Run(args []string) int { ... }` + `func (pc *ProxyCommand) Help() string { ... }` + `func (pc *ProxyCommand) Synopsis() string { ... }`.  mitchellh/cli requires each subcommand to IMPLEMENT the `cli.Command` INTERFACE which has 3 METHODS: `Run(args []string) int` (handler returning exit code), `Help() string` (detailed help text), `Synopsis() string` (one-line synopsis).  Caller defines a STRUCT (`ProxyCommand`) and IMPLEMENTS these 3 METHODS on it.  When user types `muxy proxy --config foo.yml`, mitchellh/cli strips the `proxy` subcommand name and calls `proxyCmd.Run([]string{"--config", "foo.yml"})` — the Run method receives ONLY THE REMAINING ARGS.  Distinct from R275 clap-derive (struct + auto-generated parse), R334 kong (struct tag + reflection dispatch), R350 flaggy (pointer-based imperative + `sub.Used` dispatch), R341 yargs (`.command(name, desc, builder, handler)` chained registration), R343 commander.js (similar chained registration).  mitchellh/cli's approach: 3-METHOD INTERFACE (`Run`/`Help`/`Synopsis`) per command struct — corpus's **first documented 3-method-Command-interface CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: muxy uses **mitchellh/cli subcommand-map-with-factory-functions CLI pattern** at command/commands.go:38-47 + main.go:23 — `Commands = map[string]cli.CommandFactory{ "proxy": func() (cli.Command, error) { return &ProxyCommand{Meta: meta}, nil }, "pki": func() (cli.Command, error) { return &pki.PkiCommand{}, nil } }` + `cli.Commands = command.Commands`.  mitchellh/cli uses a `map[string]cli.CommandFactory` for subcommand registration where `cli.CommandFactory` is `func() (cli.Command, error)` — caller maps subcommand NAMES (strings) to FACTORY FUNCTIONS that return constructed Command struct instances.  Factories allow LAZY CONSTRUCTION (only construct the matched subcommand) + DEPENDENCY INJECTION (factories close over external state like `meta`).  Distinct from R334 alecthomas/kong (struct field types AS subcommands via reflection), R275 clap-derive (enum variants as subcommands), R341 yargs (chained `.command(name, desc, builder, handler)` registration), R342 alexflint/go-arg (pointer-to-struct field with `arg:"subcommand:X"` tag), R350 integrii/flaggy (`parent.AttachSubcommand(child, position)` imperative).  mitchellh/cli's approach: SUBCOMMAND MAP `map[string]cli.CommandFactory` with FACTORY FUNCTIONS — corpus's **first documented subcommand-map-with-factory-functions CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: muxy uses **mitchellh/cli stdlib-flag-package-delegation-for-per-command-parsing CLI pattern** at command/proxy.go:14-23 — `cmdFlags := flag.NewFlagSet("proxy", flag.ContinueOnError); cmdFlags.Usage = func() { pc.Meta.UI.Output(pc.Help()) }; cmdFlags.StringVar(&c.ConfigFile, "config", "", "Path to a YAML configuration file"); if err := cmdFlags.Parse(args); err != nil { return 1 }`.  mitchellh/cli does NOT provide its own flag parsing — each Command's `Run(args)` method uses Go's STANDARD `flag` PACKAGE for per-command flag parsing (`flag.NewFlagSet(name, flag.ContinueOnError)` + `cmdFlags.StringVar(&dest, name, default, help)` + `cmdFlags.Parse(args)`).  This is a DELIBERATE DESIGN CHOICE — mitchellh/cli focuses ONLY on subcommand dispatch + help system + UI abstraction, leaving flag parsing to Go's well-established stdlib.  Distinct from R350 flaggy (provides its own flag parsing with POINTER-BASED imperative builder), R354 jessevdk/go-flags (provides its own struct-tag-based parsing), R334 kong (provides its own struct-tag-based parsing), R275 clap-derive (provides its own proc-macro-based parsing), R341 yargs (provides its own fluent builder), R343 commander.js (provides its own builder).  mitchellh/cli's approach: STDLIB `flag` PACKAGE DELEGATION for per-command parsing — corpus's **first documented stdlib-flag-package-delegation CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: muxy uses **mitchellh/cli hand-written-help-text-via-Help-method CLI pattern** at command/proxy.go:34-46 — `func (pc *ProxyCommand) Help() string { helpText := \`\nUsage: muck proxy [options]\n\n  Run the Muck proxy.\n\nOptions:\n\n  --config                    Location of Muxy configuration file\n\`; return strings.TrimSpace(helpText) }`.  mitchellh/cli does NOT AUTO-GENERATE HELP TEXT from a spec — caller writes the HELP TEXT THEMSELVES in the `Help() string` method.  This is similar to docopt R352 (docstring-as-spec, but mitchellh's hand-written help is JUST help, not spec).  Distinct from R275 clap-derive (auto-generates rich help from doc comments + attributes), R344 argh (auto-generates from doc comments), R341 yargs (auto-generates from method calls), R343 commander.js (auto-generates from `.description()` chain), R346 mainargs (auto-generates from `@arg(doc = "...")` annotations).  mitchellh/cli's approach: HAND-WRITTEN HELP TEXT via `Help() string` METHOD — caller has FULL CONTROL over formatting but must MANUALLY KEEP HELP IN SYNC with actual flags — corpus's **first documented hand-written-help-text-via-Help-method CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: muxy uses **mitchellh/cli Ui-interface-abstraction-with-color-wrappers CLI pattern** at command/commands.go:30-36 — `UI = &cli.ColoredUi{ Ui: &cli.BasicUi{Writer: os.Stdout, Reader: os.Stdin, ErrorWriter: os.Stderr}, OutputColor: cli.UiColorNone, InfoColor: cli.UiColorNone, ErrorColor: cli.UiColorRed }`.  mitchellh/cli provides `cli.Ui` INTERFACE for output abstraction with 4 methods (`Ask(query) (response, error)`, `AskSecret(query) (response, error)`, `Output(msg)`, `Info(msg)`, `Error(msg)`, `Warn(msg)`).  Implementations: `BasicUi` (basic stdout/stderr/stdin), `ColoredUi` (wraps BasicUi adding ANSI color), `ConcurrentUi` (thread-safe wrapper), `MockUi` (for testing), `PrefixedUi` (prefix all output).  Color constants: `cli.UiColorNone`, `cli.UiColorRed`, `cli.UiColorGreen`, `cli.UiColorYellow`, `cli.UiColorBlue`, `cli.UiColorMagenta`, `cli.UiColorCyan`.  Distinct from R341 yargs (no built-in UI abstraction; caller uses console.log), R275 clap-derive (no built-in UI abstraction), R340 oclif (`this.log`/`this.warn`/`this.error` methods on Command class), R346 mainargs (no built-in UI abstraction).  mitchellh/cli's approach: `cli.Ui` INTERFACE + COMPOSABLE WRAPPERS (BasicUi/ColoredUi/ConcurrentUi/MockUi/PrefixedUi) — corpus's **first documented Ui-interface-abstraction-with-composable-wrappers CLI pattern**.
  - Hunt-skip notes: skipped `_consul`/`_vault`/`_terraform`/`_packer`/`_boundary`/`_nomad`/`_vagrant` (all HashiCorp tools — IN CORPUS, all use mitchellh/cli but already documented under different lib attribution), `_spire-agent`/`_spire-server` (spiffe/spire — IN CORPUS), `_kite` (koding/kite 3258 stars — uses mitchellh/cli, but kite binary's surface is mostly micro-service framework registration not user-facing CLI; HOLD for future round), `_krab` (ohkrab/krab 21 stars — narrow Postgres migration tool).  Probed alternative Go CLI libraries: `_pborman/getopt`-based tools (would be 10th Go CLI library; HOLD), `_namsral/flag`-based tools (envconfig-style; would be 10th-11th; HOLD), `_DavidGamba/go-getoptions`-based tools (HOLD).  Considered using `_cnsl-migrate` (mkeeler/consul-migrate Consul migration tool — narrow), `_consul-backinator` (myENA/consul-backinator — narrow Consul backup tool), `_archer` (instana/archer — narrow Instana collection tool).  Chose muxy: 829 GH stars (real production chaos engineering tool for distributed system resilience testing), 2 subcommands + 1 flag on proxy + auto-added `--help`/`--version` + INTERFACE-BASED command-as-struct + 3-method `cli.Command` interface + `cli.NewCLI` constructor + `cli.Args` field assignment + `cli.Commands` SUBCOMMAND MAP with `cli.CommandFactory` factory functions + `cli.Ui` interface for UI abstraction + stdlib `flag` package for per-command parsing + HAND-WRITTEN HELP TEXT via `Help() string` method, full mefellows/muxy main.go + command/*.go exercising EVERY MAJOR mitchellh/cli PATTERN.
  - Blacklist update: `muxy` added.  Corpus count: 28,779 → 28,780 (+1).

- **R356 — grumphp source-direct (PHP code-quality task runner with git-hook integration in PHP; Symfony Console library)** (1 file new) — pivots from Hejsil/zig-clap (R355 buzz) to a SECOND PHP CLI library — corpus's **first documented Symfony Console library** + **second documented PHP CLI library**.  grumphp is Phpro's PHP code-quality task runner — registers itself as a git pre-commit hook and runs configured tasks (phpunit, phpstan, php-cs-fixer, phpcs, psalm, infection, jest/eslint via npm, etc.) against staged files before allowing the commit.  4293 GH stars.  Sister tools: husky (in corpus, JS git hooks), pre-commit (in corpus, Python git hooks), overcommit (Ruby git hooks).  **MAJOR MILESTONE**: corpus's **first documented Symfony Console library** + **second documented PHP CLI library**.  PHP ecosystem now has 2 documented CLI libraries (R282 + R356 Symfony Console) joining the multi-library leaders.  Adds Symfony Console (symfony/console — 9820+ GH stars) to the cross-language CLI taxonomy: Symfony Console is THE de-facto PHP CLI framework — used by Composer, Laravel artisan, Drush, WP-CLI, PHPUnit, PHPStan, Psalm, PHP-CS-Fixer, PHPCS, Behat, Deployer, Castor, Grumphp, and ALMOST EVERY major PHP CLI tool.
  - `_grumphp` (1-stem; phpro/grumphp src/Console/*.php + src/Console/Command/*.php + Symfony Console library; **Symfony Console library** — `use Symfony\\Component\\Console\\Application;` + `use Symfony\\Component\\Console\\Command\\Command;` + `use Symfony\\Component\\Console\\Attribute\\AsCommand;` + `use Symfony\\Component\\Console\\Input\\InputOption;` bring Symfony Console into scope; **6 commands (`run` + `configure` + `git:init` + `git:deinit` + `git:pre-commit` + `git:commit-msg` + standard `help`/`list`) + 1 global flag (`-c/--config`) + per-command flags (`--testsuite`/`--tasks` for run; `--force` for configure) + 7 AUTO-ADDED standard flags (`--help/-h` + `--version/-V` + `--verbose/-v/-vv/-vvv` + `--quiet/-q` + `--no-interaction/-n` + `--ansi/--no-ansi`) + PHP 8 `#[AsCommand(name, description)]` ATTRIBUTE for command registration + CLASS-INHERITANCE from `Command` + COLON-SEPARATED subcommand naming (`git:init`/`git:commit-msg`) + `InputOption::VALUE_REQUIRED`/`VALUE_OPTIONAL`/`VALUE_NONE`/`VALUE_IS_ARRAY` MODE CONSTANTS + `$application->getDefinition()->addOption(...)` for app-wide globals**) decoded source-direct from:
    * `bin/grumphp` lines 4-5 (`use GrumPHP\\Configuration\\ContainerFactory;` + `use Symfony\\Component\\Console\\Input\\ArgvInput;` + `use Symfony\\Component\\Console\\Output\\ConsoleOutput;` — PHP `use` statements importing Symfony Console primitives)
    * `src/Console/ApplicationConfigurator.php` lines 16-22 (`public function configure(Application $application): void { $application->setVersion(self::APP_VERSION); $application->setName(self::APP_NAME); $this->registerInputDefinitions($application); }` — APPLICATION CONFIGURATION via `setVersion`/`setName` METHOD CALLS on the Application instance)
    * `src/Console/ApplicationConfigurator.php` lines 24-34 (`private function registerInputDefinitions(Application $application): void { $definition = $application->getDefinition(); $definition->addOption(new InputOption('config', 'c', InputOption::VALUE_REQUIRED, 'Path to config')); }` — APP-WIDE GLOBAL OPTION REGISTRATION via `$application->getDefinition()->addOption(...)` — `InputOption(name, shortcut, mode, description, default)` constructor + `InputOption::VALUE_REQUIRED` MODE CONSTANT)
    * `src/Console/Command/RunCommand.php` lines 14-22 (`use Symfony\\Component\\Console\\Attribute\\AsCommand; use Symfony\\Component\\Console\\Command\\Command; use Symfony\\Component\\Console\\Input\\InputInterface; use Symfony\\Component\\Console\\Input\\InputOption; use Symfony\\Component\\Console\\Output\\OutputInterface; #[AsCommand(name: 'run', description: 'Run configured tasks')] class RunCommand extends Command` — PHP 8 ATTRIBUTE `#[AsCommand(name: 'verb', description: 'X')]` for command registration + CLASS-INHERITANCE from `Command`)
    * `src/Console/Command/RunCommand.php` lines 70-86 (`protected function configure(): void { $this->addOption('testsuite', null, InputOption::VALUE_REQUIRED, 'Specify which testsuite you want to run.', null); $this->addOption('tasks', null, InputOption::VALUE_REQUIRED, 'Specify which tasks you want to run (comma separated). Example --tasks=task1,task2', null); }` — `configure()` METHOD called by Symfony framework to declare command options; `$this->addOption(name, shortcut, mode, description, default)` registers per-command options)
    * `src/Console/Command/RunCommand.php` lines 88-103 (`public function execute(InputInterface $input, OutputInterface $output): int { $taskNames = $input->getOption('tasks') ?? ''; $testsSuite = $input->getOption('testsuite') ?? ''; ... return $results->isFailed() ? self::EXIT_CODE_NOK : self::EXIT_CODE_OK; }` — `execute()` HANDLER METHOD called by Symfony framework when this command is invoked; `$input->getOption('name')` accessor)
    * `src/Console/Command/Git/InitCommand.php` lines 21-22 (`#[AsCommand(name: 'git:init', description: 'Registers the Git hooks')] class InitCommand extends Command` — SUBCOMMAND GROUPING via COLON in command name `git:init` — Symfony Console's CONVENTION for subcommand namespacing)
  - Critical extraction note: grumphp uses **Symfony Console library** at bin/grumphp:5 + src/Console/Command/*.php — corpus's **first documented Symfony Console library** + **second documented PHP CLI library**.  Symfony Console (`symfony/console` on Packagist) is THE de-facto PHP CLI framework — 9820+ GH stars on symfony/console.  Used by virtually every major PHP CLI tool: Composer (PHP package manager), Laravel artisan (Laravel CLI), Drush (Drupal CLI), WP-CLI (WordPress CLI), PHPUnit (test framework), PHPStan (static analyzer), Psalm (static analyzer), PHP-CS-Fixer (code formatter), PHPCS (code sniffer), Behat (BDD framework), Deployer (deployment), Castor (task runner), Grumphp (code quality), Magento CLI, Drupal Console, Symfony itself, and HUNDREDS of others.  Distinct from R282 (the PHP 1 entry — likely Knock-knock or Console-stub or PHP's getopt) — Symfony Console is FUNDAMENTALLY DIFFERENT being CLASS-INHERITANCE-with-attribute-based since PHP 8.  KEY DIFFERENTIATORS:
    - **PHP 8+ `#[AsCommand(name: 'verb', description: 'X')]` ATTRIBUTE** for command registration (since Symfony 5.3 — replaces the older `static $defaultName = 'verb'` static property + `setName('verb')->setDescription('X')` in `configure()`)
    - **CLASS-INHERITANCE** — each command is a subclass of `Command` with `configure()` (declares options/args) + `execute(InputInterface, OutputInterface): int` (handler) methods
    - **COLON-SEPARATED SUBCOMMAND NAMING** — `git:init`, `cache:clear`, `make:controller`, `doctrine:migrations:diff` — colon is the SUBCOMMAND-NAMESPACE SEPARATOR (vs space-separated like `git init` in other CLIs); Symfony Console-specific convention; commands can be nested arbitrarily deep via multiple colons
    - **`InputOption` MODE CONSTANTS** — `InputOption::VALUE_REQUIRED` (flag must have value), `VALUE_OPTIONAL` (flag may have value), `VALUE_NONE` (boolean flag), `VALUE_IS_ARRAY` (combinable with REQUIRED/OPTIONAL for repeatable flags)
    - **AUTO-ADDED GLOBAL FLAGS** — `--help/-h`, `--version/-V`, `--verbose/-v/-vv/-vvv` (verbosity), `--ansi/--no-ansi`, `--no-interaction/-n`, `--quiet/-q` — auto-injected into every command without user action
    - **App-wide GLOBALS** via `$application->getDefinition()->addOption(...)` — global options that apply to all subcommands
    - **STYLE HELPERS** — `SymfonyStyle($input, $output)` provides `$io->success()`, `$io->error()`, `$io->table()`, `$io->progressBar()`, etc. for rich output
    - **HELPERS** — `QuestionHelper`, `ChoiceQuestion`, `ConfirmationQuestion`, `ProgressBar`, `Table` for interactive UI
  Distinct from R340 oclif (TypeScript class-inheritance with static fields), R346 mainargs (Scala method annotations), R322 JCommander (Java field annotations + reflection), R341 yargs (JS fluent builder), R343 commander.js (JS explicit-instance builder).  Symfony Console's approach: CLASS-INHERITANCE + PHP 8 ATTRIBUTE for command + COLON-SEPARATED SUBCOMMAND NAMING + INPUT OPTION MODE CONSTANTS + AUTO-ADDED GLOBAL FLAGS — corpus's **first documented colon-separated-subcommand-naming CLI pattern**.  Extends cross-language CLI taxonomy to NINETY-FIVE library variants: Rust (9), Go (8), Python (5), Crystal 4, Haskell (R270), OCaml 4, C (R272), Java 4, Kotlin (R277), Scala 2, Dart (R279), Ruby (R280), Perl (R281), **PHP (2 — R282 + Symfony Console R356)**, Swift (R283), F# (R284), Lua 2, C# (R286), Nim 2, Zig 2, D (R289), Racket (R290), Common Lisp 3, Erlang 2, Tcl (R293), Pharo Smalltalk (R294), Julia (R296), SWI-Prolog optparse (R297), Pony stdlib cli (R298), V stdlib `flag` (R300), Clojure 2, Gleam Glint (R303), Ada GNAT.Command_Line (R304), C++ 2, Elixir Optimus (R306), Vala GLib.OptionContext (R307), Hare stdlib getopt (R308), Standard ML stdlib GetOpt (R309), Fortran M_CLI2 (R310), Guile (ice-9 getopt-long) (R311), Free Pascal 2, Idris 2 System.GetOpts (R313), Mercury stdlib getopt_io (R314), Eiffel Gobo AP_PARSER (R315), Forth/Gforth `next-arg` (R317), Roc weaver (R319), Chapel `config` keyword (R320), Mojo 2, Chicken Scheme `args` egg (R324), Lean 4 Cli (R326), Bigloo Scheme `args-parse` macro (R327), Rust 7 bpaf (R330), Crystal 3 commander.cr (R332), Go 5 alecthomas/kong (R334), Modula-2 stdlib `Arguments` (R335), Janet bakpakin/spork `argparse` (R336), Inko stdlib `std.optparse` (R337), Odin stdlib `core:flags` (R338), OCaml 4 rbardou/Clap (R339), TypeScript/Node oclif (R340), TypeScript/Node yargs (R341), Go 6 alexflint/go-arg (R342), TypeScript/Node 3 tj/commander.js (R343), Rust 8 google/argh (R344), Python 4 BrianPugh/cyclopts (R345), Scala 2 lihaoyi/mainargs (R346), Rust 9 RazrFalcon/pico-args (R347), Crystal 4 at-grandpa/clim (R348), Lua 2 mpeterv/argparse (R349), Go 7 integrii/flaggy (R350), Common Lisp 3 sjl/adopt (R351), Python 5 docopt (R352), Java 4 rvesse/airline (R353), Go 8 jessevdk/go-flags (R354), Zig 2 Hejsil/zig-clap (R355), **PHP 2 Symfony Console** (R356 — NEW).  **MAJOR PATTERN MILESTONE**: 95 cross-language CLI library variants documented.  PHP NOW HAS TWO documented CLI libraries.
  - Critical extraction note: grumphp uses **Symfony Console PHP-8-attribute-based command registration CLI pattern** at RunCommand.php:22 + InitCommand.php:21 — `#[AsCommand(name: 'run', description: 'Run configured tasks')] class RunCommand extends Command` and `#[AsCommand(name: 'git:init', description: 'Registers the Git hooks')] class InitCommand extends Command`.  Symfony Console's `#[AsCommand(name, description)]` is a PHP 8+ ATTRIBUTE (added in Symfony 5.3) that REGISTERS the command class with the framework — no need to set `$defaultName` static property or call `setName()` in `configure()`.  Framework auto-discovers all `#[AsCommand]`-annotated classes via Composer's PSR-4 autoloader + reflection.  Distinct from R322 JCommander `@Parameters(commandNames = "verb")` (Java annotation on class), R340 oclif `static description = '...'` (TypeScript class static field), R346 mainargs `@main def verb(...)` (Scala annotation on method), R342 alexflint/go-arg `\`arg:"subcommand:X"\`` (Go struct tag on pointer field).  Symfony Console's approach: PHP 8 ATTRIBUTE `#[AsCommand(name, description)]` on COMMAND CLASS — corpus's **first documented PHP-8-attribute-based-command-registration CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: grumphp uses **Symfony Console colon-separated subcommand naming CLI pattern** at Git/InitCommand.php:21 + Git/CommitMsgCommand.php + Git/DeInitCommand.php + Git/PreCommitCommand.php — `#[AsCommand(name: 'git:init', ...)]` + `#[AsCommand(name: 'git:commit-msg', ...)]` + `#[AsCommand(name: 'git:deinit', ...)]` + `#[AsCommand(name: 'git:pre-commit', ...)]`.  Symfony Console uses COLON `:` as the SUBCOMMAND NAMESPACE SEPARATOR — `git:init` is read as "git namespace, init command".  User invokes via `grumphp git:init`.  Arbitrarily deep nesting supported: `doctrine:migrations:diff`, `make:controller`, `cache:clear`.  Distinct from R275 clap-derive (subcommands are SPACE-SEPARATED — `cli verb subverb`), R322 JCommander (space-separated), R340 oclif (FILESYSTEM-HIERARCHY-WALKING `src/commands/verb/subverb.ts` → `cli verb subverb`), R341 yargs (space-separated chain via `.command('verb').command('subverb')`), R343 commander.js (space-separated).  Symfony Console's approach: COLON-SEPARATED SUBCOMMAND NAMING — `verb:subverb:subsubverb` — corpus's **first documented colon-separated-subcommand-naming CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: grumphp uses **Symfony Console InputOption mode constants CLI pattern** at RunCommand.php:73 + 81 + ApplicationConfigurator.php:30 + ConfigureCommand.php:54 — `$this->addOption('testsuite', null, InputOption::VALUE_REQUIRED, 'Specify which testsuite you want to run.', null)` + `$this->addOption('tasks', null, InputOption::VALUE_REQUIRED, '...', null)` + `$definition->addOption(new InputOption('config', 'c', InputOption::VALUE_REQUIRED, 'Path to config'))` + `$this->addOption('force', null, InputOption::VALUE_NONE, '...')`.  Symfony Console's `InputOption::MODE_CONSTANT` MODE CONSTANTS:
    - `VALUE_NONE` (1) — boolean flag (--flag, no value)
    - `VALUE_REQUIRED` (2) — flag must have value (--flag=X)
    - `VALUE_OPTIONAL` (4) — flag may have value (--flag or --flag=X)
    - `VALUE_IS_ARRAY` (8) — flag can be repeated (combinable: `VALUE_REQUIRED | VALUE_IS_ARRAY`)
    - `VALUE_NEGATABLE` (16) — flag has `--no-X` negation (Symfony 5.3+)
  Combine via bitwise OR for hybrid modes (e.g. `VALUE_REQUIRED | VALUE_IS_ARRAY` for repeatable required-value flag).  Distinct from R275 clap-derive `#[arg(num_args = 1..)]` (Rust attribute with arity), R322 JCommander `arity = N` annotation field, R340 oclif `Flags.string({...})` factory, R341 yargs `{string: true, demandOption: true}` config object.  Symfony Console's approach: INTEGER BITMASK MODE CONSTANTS on `InputOption` — corpus's **first documented integer-bitmask-mode-constants CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: grumphp uses **Symfony Console auto-added global flags CLI pattern** (no explicit code; framework auto-injects) — Symfony Console AUTOMATICALLY ADDS the following GLOBAL FLAGS to every command without user action: `--help/-h` (show command help), `--version/-V` (show version), `--verbose/-v|-vv|-vvv` (verbosity levels: normal/verbose/debug), `--quiet/-q` (suppress output), `--no-interaction/-n` (do not ask interactive questions), `--ansi`/`--no-ansi` (force/disable ANSI output).  Caller does NOT need to declare these — framework handles them.  All Symfony Console-based CLIs (Composer, Laravel artisan, Drush, etc.) consequently share THE SAME global flag set, providing a consistent UX across the PHP ecosystem.  Distinct from R275 clap-derive (auto-adds only `--help/-h` and `--version/-V` if `Cli` struct has `#[command(version)]`), R322 JCommander (auto-adds only `--help`), R340 oclif (auto-adds via base class but caller must opt-in via `extends Command`), R341 yargs (auto-adds `--help` via `.help()` method call, opt-in).  Symfony Console's approach: 7-FLAG AUTO-ADDED SET (help/version/verbose/quiet/no-interaction/ansi/no-ansi) injected into every command — corpus's **first documented auto-added-7-flag-standard-set CLI pattern**.
  - Hunt-skip notes: skipped `_composer` (PHP package manager — IN CORPUS, uses Symfony Console), `_artisan` (Laravel CLI — IN CORPUS), `_drush` (Drupal CLI — IN CORPUS), `_wp` (WP-CLI — IN CORPUS), `_phpunit` (test framework — IN CORPUS), `_phpstan` (static analyzer — IN CORPUS), `_psalm` (static analyzer — IN CORPUS), `_phpcs` (code sniffer — IN CORPUS), `_php-cs-fixer` (formatter — IN CORPUS), `_behat` (Behat/Behat — IN CORPUS), `_castor` (jolicode/castor — IN CORPUS), `_deployer` (deployphp/deployer — IN CORPUS).  Probed alternative PHP CLI libraries: `_aura/cli`-based tools (smaller ecosystem; HOLD), `_garden/cli`-based tools (HOLD).  Considered using one of the IN-CORPUS tools to "upgrade" the documentation with Symfony Console attribution but that doesn't add a new completion file.  Chose grumphp: 4293 GH stars (real production PHP code-quality task runner used in PHP CI/CD pipelines worldwide), 6 commands + 1 global flag + per-command flags + 7 auto-added standard flags + PHP 8 `#[AsCommand]` ATTRIBUTE + COLON-SEPARATED subcommand naming (`git:init`/`git:commit-msg`/`git:deinit`/`git:pre-commit`) + `InputOption::MODE_CONSTANT` MODE CONSTANTS + auto-added 7-flag standard set, full src/Console/*.php exercising EVERY MAJOR Symfony Console PATTERN.
  - Blacklist update: `grumphp` added.  Corpus count: 28,778 → 28,779 (+1).

- **R355 — buzz source-direct (small/lightweight statically-typed scripting language compiler in Zig; Hejsil/zig-clap library)** (1 file new) — pivots from jessevdk/go-flags (R354 gostatic) to a SECOND Zig CLI library — corpus's **first documented Hejsil/zig-clap library** + **second documented Zig CLI library**.  buzz is giann's Buzz scripting language compiler in Zig — fits in the niche between Lua and Crystal, with a JIT, REPL, formatter, and WASM build target.  1488 GH stars.  Sister tools: lua (in corpus), crystal (in corpus), zig (in corpus), gleam (in corpus), v (in corpus).  **MAJOR MILESTONE**: corpus's **first documented Hejsil/zig-clap library** + **second documented Zig CLI library**.  Zig ecosystem now has 2 documented CLI libraries (R288 + R355 zig-clap) joining the multi-library leaders.  Adds Hejsil/zig-clap (Jakub Konka's CLI parser for Zig) to the cross-language CLI taxonomy: zig-clap uniquely leverages Zig's `comptime` feature to PARSE THE SPEC AT COMPILE TIME from a MULTI-LINE STRING DSL formatted like a man-page Usage: section.  Distinctive features:
    - `comptime clap.parseParamsComptime(\\\\...)` — Zig's COMPILE-TIME EVALUATION parses the multi-line string DSL at COMPILE TIME, generating type-safe struct fields
    - MULTI-LINE STRING DSL with man-page-like format: `-x, --long DESCRIPTION` for short+long flags, `<TYPE>` for value placeholders with EXPLICIT TYPE (`<usize>`, `<str>`, etc.), `...` for repeatable/variadic, `<str>...` at end for variadic positional
    - `clap.parse(clap.Help, &params, clap.parsers.default, args, .{...})` — parse function with allocator + diagnostic
    - `res.args.<longname>` field access — accessor for parsed values; field name matches the LONG FLAG NAME
    - `res.args.@"line-width"` — Zig's `@"identifier"` ESCAPE SYNTAX for accessing field names with HYPHENS (since `line-width` is not a valid Zig identifier)
    - `clap.usage(...)` + `clap.help(...)` AUTO-GENERATED USAGE+HELP from the params DSL
    - `clap.parsers.default` — built-in parser registry mapping `<usize>`/`<str>`/`<u32>`/etc. to Zig type coercion functions
  Distinct from R288 (the Zig 1 entry — likely manual `process.args()` parsing), R344 google/argh (Rust derive-based proc-macro), R275 clap-derive (Rust derive-based, originally inspired by docopt's DSL approach), R352 docopt (Python — also DOCSTRING-AS-SPEC but RUNTIME parsing not compile-time).  zig-clap's approach: COMPTIME-PARSED MULTI-LINE STRING DSL at compile time — combines docopt's doc-as-spec aesthetics with clap-derive's type-safe struct access.  Extends cross-language CLI library taxonomy variant count to 94.
  - `_buzz` (1-stem; buzz-language/buzz src/main.zig + Hejsil/zig-clap library; **Hejsil/zig-clap library** — `const clap = @import("clap");` brings zig-clap into scope; **7 flags (`-h/--help` + `-t/--test` + `-f/--fmt` + `--line-width <usize>` + `-c/--check` + `-v/--version` + `-L/--library <str>...` repeatable) + variadic positional (`<str>...` for script + args) + COMPTIME-PARSED MULTI-LINE STRING DSL via `comptime clap.parseParamsComptime(...)` + multi-line string format with `-short, --long DESCRIPTION` + `<TYPE>` placeholders + `...` for repeatable/variadic + `clap.parse(clap.Help, &params, clap.parsers.default, args, opts)` parse function + `res.args.<longname>` field access + Zig `@"hyphenated-name"` escape for hyphenated flag names + `clap.usage` / `clap.help` auto-generated output + `clap.Diagnostic{}` diagnostic struct for error reporting**) decoded source-direct from:
    * `src/main.zig` line 3 (`const clap = @import("clap");` — Zig const-import statement; zig-clap is distributed via Zig package manager `zig fetch --save` or in `build.zig.zon`)
    * `src/main.zig` lines 33-43 (`const params = comptime clap.parseParamsComptime( \\\\-h, --help             Show help and exit \\\\-t, --test             Run test blocks in provided script \\\\-f, --fmt              Reformat script, output the result to stdout \\\\--line-width <usize>   Formatter line width, only valid with --fmt (defaults to 80) \\\\-c, --check            Check script for error without running it \\\\-v, --version          Print version and exit \\\\-L, --library <str>... Add search path for external libraries \\\\<str>...               Script to run followed by its eventual arguments \\\\ );` — COMPTIME-EVALUATED `parseParamsComptime` parses the multi-line string DSL at COMPILE TIME; Zig's `comptime` keyword forces evaluation at COMPILE TIME, generating the type-safe params array that the runtime parser uses)
    * `src/main.zig` lines 48-60 (`var diag = clap.Diagnostic{}; var res = clap.parse(clap.Help, &params, clap.parsers.default, init.minimal.args, .{ .allocator = allocator, .diagnostic = &diag }) catch |err| { diag.report(&stderr.interface, err) catch {}; return 1; };` — `clap.parse(HELP_TYPE, &params, PARSERS, args, .{ allocator, diagnostic })` PARSE function; on error catches and reports via diagnostic; uses Zig's `try`/`catch |err|` error handling)
    * `src/main.zig` lines 63-69 (`if (res.args.version == 1) { _repl.printBanner(&stdout.interface, true); return 0; }` — FIELD ACCESS on `res.args` — `version` field is the parsed value for `-v/--version` flag; for boolean flags `== 1` checks presence)
    * `src/main.zig` lines 90-101 (`if (res.args.@"line-width") |line_width| { ... }` — Zig's `@"identifier"` ESCAPE SYNTAX for accessing FIELD NAMES WITH HYPHENS — `line-width` is not a valid Zig identifier (hyphen is the subtraction operator) so `@"line-width"` is the escape syntax; `if (optional) |value| { ... }` is Zig's OPTIONAL UNWRAPPING control flow)
    * `src/main.zig` lines 80-86 (`for (res.args.library) |path| { list.append(allocator, path) catch return 1; }` — REPEATABLE FLAGS land in a SLICE (`[]const u8`) on `res.args`; caller iterates with Zig's `for` loop)
    * `src/main.zig` lines 73-78 (`clap.usage(&stderr.interface, clap.Help, &params) catch return 1;` + `clap.help(&stderr.interface, clap.Help, &params, .{ .description_on_new_line = false, ... }) catch return 1;` — AUTO-GENERATED USAGE and HELP output)
  - Critical extraction note: buzz uses **Hejsil/zig-clap library** at main.zig:3 — corpus's **first documented Hejsil/zig-clap library** + **second documented Zig CLI library**.  Hejsil/zig-clap is Jakub "Hejsil" Konka's CLI parser for Zig (~1000 GH stars).  KEY DIFFERENTIATOR: leverages Zig's `comptime` feature to PARSE THE SPEC AT COMPILE TIME from a MULTI-LINE STRING DSL — combines:
    - **docopt's doc-as-spec aesthetics** — the spec is a human-readable text block similar to docopt's Usage: section, but instead of being parsed at runtime (docopt), it's parsed at COMPILE TIME (zig-clap)
    - **clap-derive's type-safe struct access** — `res.args.<longname>` field access is statically typed; misspelling a flag name produces a COMPILE ERROR
    - **Zig's `<TYPE>` placeholder syntax** — `<usize>`, `<str>`, `<u32>` declare EXPLICIT VALUE TYPES that the parser coerces at runtime via `clap.parsers.default` registry
    - **Repeatable via `...`** — `-L, --library <str>...` makes the flag repeatable (similar to docopt's `...` quantifier)
    - **Variadic positional via `<str>...` at end** — buzz's `<str>...` at end of params declares the script-and-args variadic positional
  Distinct from R288 Zig 1 (likely manual `process.args()` parsing), R344 google/argh (Rust derive-based proc-macro), R275 clap-derive (Rust derive-based — Hejsil cites this as inspiration), R352 docopt Python (DOCSTRING-AS-SPEC RUNTIME parsing — zig-clap is COMPILE-TIME), R338 Odin core:flags (backtick struct tags + RTTI — runtime reflection).  zig-clap's approach: COMPTIME-PARSED MULTI-LINE STRING DSL at COMPILE TIME with TYPED PLACEHOLDERS — corpus's **first documented comptime-parsed-multi-line-string-DSL-at-compile-time CLI pattern**.  Extends cross-language CLI taxonomy to NINETY-FOUR library variants: Rust (9), Go (8), Python (5), Crystal 4, Haskell (R270), OCaml 4, C (R272), Java 4, Kotlin (R277), Scala 2, Dart (R279), Ruby (R280), Perl (R281), PHP (R282), Swift (R283), F# (R284), Lua 2, C# (R286), Nim 2, **Zig (2 — R288 + Hejsil/zig-clap R355)**, D (R289), Racket (R290), Common Lisp 3, Erlang 2, Tcl (R293), Pharo Smalltalk (R294), Julia (R296), SWI-Prolog optparse (R297), Pony stdlib cli (R298), V stdlib `flag` (R300), Clojure 2, Gleam Glint (R303), Ada GNAT.Command_Line (R304), C++ 2, Elixir Optimus (R306), Vala GLib.OptionContext (R307), Hare stdlib getopt (R308), Standard ML stdlib GetOpt (R309), Fortran M_CLI2 (R310), Guile (ice-9 getopt-long) (R311), Free Pascal 2, Idris 2 System.GetOpts (R313), Mercury stdlib getopt_io (R314), Eiffel Gobo AP_PARSER (R315), Forth/Gforth `next-arg` (R317), Roc weaver (R319), Chapel `config` keyword (R320), Mojo 2, Chicken Scheme `args` egg (R324), Lean 4 Cli (R326), Bigloo Scheme `args-parse` macro (R327), Rust 7 bpaf (R330), Crystal 3 commander.cr (R332), Go 5 alecthomas/kong (R334), Modula-2 stdlib `Arguments` (R335), Janet bakpakin/spork `argparse` (R336), Inko stdlib `std.optparse` (R337), Odin stdlib `core:flags` (R338), OCaml 4 rbardou/Clap (R339), TypeScript/Node oclif (R340), TypeScript/Node yargs (R341), Go 6 alexflint/go-arg (R342), TypeScript/Node 3 tj/commander.js (R343), Rust 8 google/argh (R344), Python 4 BrianPugh/cyclopts (R345), Scala 2 lihaoyi/mainargs (R346), Rust 9 RazrFalcon/pico-args (R347), Crystal 4 at-grandpa/clim (R348), Lua 2 mpeterv/argparse (R349), Go 7 integrii/flaggy (R350), Common Lisp 3 sjl/adopt (R351), Python 5 docopt (R352), Java 4 rvesse/airline (R353), Go 8 jessevdk/go-flags (R354), **Zig 2 Hejsil/zig-clap** (R355 — NEW).  **MAJOR PATTERN MILESTONE**: 94 cross-language CLI library variants documented.  Zig NOW HAS TWO documented CLI libraries.
  - Critical extraction note: buzz uses **zig-clap comptime-parsed-multi-line-string-DSL CLI pattern** at main.zig:33-43 — `const params = comptime clap.parseParamsComptime( \\\\-h, --help             Show help and exit \\\\-t, --test             Run test blocks in provided script \\\\-f, --fmt              Reformat script ... \\\\--line-width <usize>   Formatter line width, only valid with --fmt (defaults to 80) \\\\-c, --check            Check script for error without running it \\\\-v, --version          Print version and exit \\\\-L, --library <str>... Add search path for external libraries \\\\<str>...               Script to run followed by its eventual arguments \\\\ );`.  Zig's `comptime` keyword forces evaluation at COMPILE TIME — the entire multi-line string DSL is parsed at compile time, generating a type-safe params array.  The DSL format mimics man-page Usage: sections: `-short, --long DESCRIPTION` for flags with short+long aliases, `<TYPE>` for value placeholders (`<usize>`, `<str>`, `<u32>`, etc.), `...` for repeatable/variadic, `<str>...` at the end for variadic positional.  Distinct from R275 clap-derive (Rust struct + `#[arg(...)]` proc-macro — also compile-time codegen but from struct fields not text DSL), R344 argh (Rust struct + `#[argh(...)]` proc-macro, same as clap-derive), R352 docopt Python (docstring-as-spec but RUNTIME parsing not compile-time — docopt parses the docstring on every program startup), R338 Odin core:flags (struct tags + RTTI at runtime).  zig-clap's approach: COMPTIME-PARSED MULTI-LINE STRING DSL — compile-time docopt-style spec — corpus's **first documented compile-time-text-DSL-parser CLI pattern** (clap-derive is compile-time but from struct fields; docopt is text DSL but runtime; zig-clap is the only one that combines compile-time + text DSL).
  - Critical extraction note: buzz uses **zig-clap @"hyphenated-name"-escape-syntax for field access CLI pattern** at main.zig:90-101 — `if (res.args.@"line-width") |line_width| { ... }`.  In Zig, IDENTIFIERS cannot contain HYPHENS (hyphen is the subtraction operator).  But zig-clap auto-derives field names from long flag names — `--line-width` becomes the field `line-width`.  To access such fields, Zig has the `@"identifier"` ESCAPE SYNTAX that lets ANY STRING become a valid identifier in source code.  So `res.args.@"line-width"` is how the caller accesses the `--line-width` flag's parsed value.  For flag names without hyphens (`--help`, `--version`), regular access works: `res.args.help`, `res.args.version`.  Distinct from R275 clap-derive (Rust converts `field_name` ↔ `--field-name` automatically), R344 argh (same — Rust uses snake_case fields), R338 Odin core:flags (Odin uses snake_case for both fields and flags), R346 mainargs (Scala camelCase ↔ kebab-case auto-conversion), R342 alexflint/go-arg (Go uses CamelCase fields, tags explicitly declare flag name).  zig-clap's approach: PRESERVE FLAG NAME AS-IS (with hyphens) + Zig `@"name"` ESCAPE SYNTAX for access — corpus's **first documented @"escape-syntax"-for-hyphenated-field-access CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: buzz uses **zig-clap <TYPE>-placeholder-with-parser-registry CLI pattern** at main.zig:38 — `--line-width <usize>` (Zig's `usize` machine-word unsigned int) + `-L, --library <str>...` (Zig's `str` string slice).  zig-clap's DSL allows `<TYPE>` PLACEHOLDERS where `TYPE` is a KEY into the PARSER REGISTRY passed to `clap.parse(...)` (defaults to `clap.parsers.default` which has `str`/`u32`/`i32`/`usize`/`f64`/etc.).  Caller can SUPPLY CUSTOM PARSERS via `.{ .allocator = ..., .parsers = my_parsers }` to support custom types (e.g. `<email>` parsed by an email-validation closure).  Distinct from R275 clap-derive (Rust type-driven inference from struct field type), R344 argh (same — Rust uses field type), R338 Odin core:flags (Odin uses field type + struct tags for placeholder name), R343 commander.js `<arg>` (angle-bracket REQUIRED VALUE — no type, just placeholder).  zig-clap's approach: `<TYPE>` PLACEHOLDER WITH PARSER REGISTRY for value-type declaration — corpus's **first documented `<TYPE>`-placeholder-with-pluggable-parser-registry CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: buzz uses **zig-clap diagnostic-struct-for-error-reporting CLI pattern** at main.zig:48-58 — `var diag = clap.Diagnostic{};` + `var res = clap.parse(..., .{ .allocator = ..., .diagnostic = &diag }) catch |err| { diag.report(&stderr.interface, err) catch {}; return 1; };`.  zig-clap's `Diagnostic` struct collects PARSE-TIME ERROR INFORMATION (which flag was problematic, what was expected, where in the input).  Caller passes `&diag` to `clap.parse(...)` via the `diagnostic` field of the options struct.  On parse error, caller calls `diag.report(WRITER, err)` which prints a HELPFUL ERROR MESSAGE referencing the specific flag that caused the failure.  Distinct from R275 clap-derive `clap::Error` (Rust Error type with kind enum), R342 alexflint/go-arg (no diagnostic — single error returned), R346 mainargs `MainsResult` ADT (Scala — no separate diagnostic), R352 docopt `DocoptExit` exception (Python exception with usage).  zig-clap's approach: SEPARATE `Diagnostic` STRUCT + `&diag` PASSED TO PARSE + `diag.report(writer, err)` USER-FRIENDLY REPORTING — corpus's **first documented separate-Diagnostic-struct-for-error-reporting CLI pattern**.
  - Hunt-skip notes: skipped `_glyph` (seatedro/glyph 520 stars ASCII converter — uses zig-clap but simpler 5-flag surface, would be sister demo; HOLD for future round), `_ziggybox` (Midstall/ziggybox 2 stars Busybox port — too narrow), `_wizig` (wizig-org/wizig 1 star — too narrow), `_jmp` (Drust0/jmp — narrow jump tool), `_spacecap` (mgerb/spacecap — narrow screen capture), `_brightness-zig` (jaschiu/brightness-zig — narrow brightness control).  Probed alternative Zig CLI libraries: `_zig-args`-based tools (would be 3rd Zig CLI library — different API style; HOLD), `_yazap`-based tools (would be 4th Zig CLI library; HOLD).  Considered using glyph instead but buzz is a more substantial multi-flag tool with broader appeal (language compiler vs single-purpose ASCII converter).  Chose buzz: 1488 GH stars (real Buzz scripting language compiler with JIT, REPL, formatter, WASM target), 7 flags + variadic positional + COMPTIME-PARSED MULTI-LINE STRING DSL + `<TYPE>` PLACEHOLDERS + `...` REPEATABLE + `@"hyphenated-name"` ESCAPE SYNTAX + `Diagnostic` STRUCT for error reporting, full src/main.zig exercising EVERY MAJOR zig-clap PATTERN.
  - Blacklist update: `buzz` added.  Corpus count: 28,777 → 28,778 (+1).

- **R354 — gostatic source-direct (fast static site generator in Go; jessevdk/go-flags library)** (1 file new) — pivots from rvesse/airline (R353 ontop) to an EIGHTH Go CLI library — corpus's **first documented jessevdk/go-flags library** + **eighth documented Go CLI library**.  gostatic is Alexander Solovyov's (piranha) single-binary static site generator — reads `config` file declaring source/target paths + a chain of processors (markdown, HTML templates, YAML/JSON frontmatter, image processing, etc.), rebuilds the static site, and optionally serves it via HTTP with hot reload on file changes.  443 GH stars.  Sister tools: hugo (Go static gen, in corpus), zola (Rust static gen, in corpus), jekyll (Ruby static gen, in corpus), 11ty (JavaScript static gen, in corpus).  **MAJOR MILESTONE**: corpus's **first documented jessevdk/go-flags library** + **eighth documented Go CLI library** — Go ecosystem now has 8 documented CLI libraries (cobra/urfave-cli/kingpin/spf13-pflag/alecthomas-kong/alexflint-go-arg/integrii-flaggy/jessevdk-go-flags) — extends Go's documented-CLI-library lead.  Adds jessevdk/go-flags (one of the OLDEST Go CLI libraries, since 2012) to the cross-language CLI taxonomy.  Used by Decred daemon (dcrd), Snap (Canonical's package manager — snapcore/snapd), gostatic, Cloud Foundry cred-alert, and many others.  Modeled after GNU getopt with rich struct-tag-based declarative API.  Extends cross-language CLI library taxonomy variant count to 93.
  - `_gostatic` (1-stem; piranha/gostatic gostatic.go; **jessevdk/go-flags library** — `flags "github.com/jessevdk/go-flags"` brings go-flags into scope; **11 flags via `Opts` struct (`--processors`/`--show-config`/`--summary`/`-i/--init [path]`/`-d/--dump FILE`/`-f/--force`/`-w/--watch`/`--no-hotreload`/`-p/--port PORT`/`-v/--verbose`/`-V/--version`) + 1 positional (config file) + STRUCT-TAG-BASED declarative API + MULTIPLE SEPARATE TAGS PER DIRECTIVE (`long:"X" short:"Y" description:"Z" default:"V"`) + Optional value via `*string` POINTER TYPE for `-i/--init [optional-path]` + `flags.NewParser(&opts, flags.PrintErrors|flags.PassDoubleDash|flags.HelpFlag)` PARSER CONSTRUCTION + `argparser.Usage = "..."` USAGE STRING SETTER + `args, err := argparser.Parse()` PARSE RETURNING POSITIONAL ARGS + flag bitmask for PARSER BEHAVIOR**) decoded source-direct from:
    * `gostatic.go` line 13 (`flags "github.com/jessevdk/go-flags"` — Go IMPORT with ALIAS — `flags` is the conventional alias for `go-flags`; library distributed via Go modules at `github.com/jessevdk/go-flags`)
    * `gostatic.go` lines 30-48 (`type Opts struct { ShowProcessors bool \`long:"processors" description:"show page processors"\`; ShowConfig bool \`long:"show-config" description:"print config as JSON"\`; ShowSummary bool \`long:"summary" description:"print all pages on stdout"\`; InitExample *string \`short:"i" long:"init" description:"create example site"\`; DumpPage string \`short:"d" long:"dump" description:"print page metadata as JSON ..."\`; Force bool \`short:"f" long:"force" description:"force building all pages"\`; Watch bool \`short:"w" long:"watch" description:"..."\`; NoHotreload bool \`long:"no-hotreload" description:"disable hot reload during --watch"\`; Port string \`short:"p" long:"port" default:"8000" description:"port to serve on"\`; Verbose bool \`short:"v" long:"verbose" description:"enable verbose output"\`; Version bool \`short:"V" long:"version" description:"show version and exit"\` }` — STRUCT WITH MULTIPLE SEPARATE TAGS per field; each directive (`long`, `short`, `description`, `default`) is a SEPARATE STRUCT TAG)
    * `gostatic.go` line 39 (`InitExample *string \`short:"i" long:"init" description:"create example site"\`` — `*string` POINTER TYPE communicates OPTIONAL-VALUE semantics: caller checks `opts.InitExample != nil` for "flag given without value" vs "flag given with value" disambiguation)
    * `gostatic.go` lines 53-55 (`argparser := flags.NewParser(&opts, flags.PrintErrors|flags.PassDoubleDash|flags.HelpFlag); argparser.Usage = "[OPTIONS] path/to/config\\n\\nBuild a site."; args, err := argparser.Parse()` — `flags.NewParser(&opts, flag_bitmask)` constructs parser binding to `&opts`; bitmask controls behavior: `PrintErrors` (print errors to stderr), `PassDoubleDash` (don't error on `--`), `HelpFlag` (auto-register `-h/--help`), `IgnoreUnknown` (don't error on unknown flags); `argparser.Usage` is FIELD ASSIGNMENT for usage string; `argparser.Parse()` returns `(positional []string, err error)`)
  - Critical extraction note: gostatic uses **jessevdk/go-flags library** at gostatic.go:13 — corpus's **first documented jessevdk/go-flags library** + **eighth documented Go CLI library**.  jessevdk/go-flags (by Jesse van der Kuijl) is ONE OF THE OLDEST Go CLI libraries (since 2012, predating cobra/kong/kingpin).  Used by Decred daemon (dcrd, 773 stars), Snap (Canonical's snapd — installed as `snap` binary on Ubuntu/Debian/Arch, IN CORPUS via SnapD), gostatic, Cloud Foundry cred-alert, and many older Go projects.  Modeled after GNU getopt and Python optparse with a rich struct-tag-based declarative API.  KEY DIFFERENTIATORS: (1) MULTIPLE SEPARATE STRUCT TAGS per directive — `long:"X" short:"Y" description:"Z" default:"V"` — vs single combined tags in kong/go-arg/Odin core:flags; (2) `*POINTER-TYPE` for OPTIONAL VALUE semantics — `*string` means "optional value, distinguishing nil from empty-string"; (3) `flags.NewParser(&opts, BITMASK)` with BEHAVIOR-CONTROL FLAGS bitmask (`PrintErrors`/`PassDoubleDash`/`HelpFlag`/`IgnoreUnknown`/`Default`/`IniDefault`/`AllowBoolValues`); (4) `argparser.Usage = "..."` FIELD ASSIGNMENT (mutable parser); (5) `parser.Parse()` returns `(positional []string, err error)` tuple — caller inspects positional remainder.  Distinct from R334 alecthomas/kong (SINGLE COMBINED `kong:"name='X',short='y'"` tag with key='value' DSL), R342 alexflint/go-arg (SINGLE `arg:"--seeds,separate,env:MGMT_SEEDS"` tag with comma-separated directives), R338 Odin core:flags (SINGLE `args:"name=X,pos=N"` tag), R350 integrii/flaggy (POINTER-BASED imperative builder, no struct tags), R275 Rust clap-derive (proc-macro on struct), R344 Rust google/argh (similar proc-macro).  go-flags' approach: SEPARATE STRUCT TAG PER DIRECTIVE — verbose but each directive is independently identifiable via Go's `reflect.StructTag.Get("long")` etc.  Extends cross-language CLI taxonomy to NINETY-THREE library variants: Rust (9), **Go (8 — cobra/urfave-cli/kingpin/spf13-pflag/alecthomas-kong/alexflint-go-arg/integrii-flaggy/jessevdk-go-flags)**, Python (5), Crystal 4, Haskell (R270), OCaml 4, C (R272), Java 4, Kotlin (R277), Scala 2, Dart (R279), Ruby (R280), Perl (R281), PHP (R282), Swift (R283), F# (R284), Lua 2, C# (R286), Nim 2, Zig (R288), D (R289), Racket (R290), Common Lisp 3, Erlang 2, Tcl (R293), Pharo Smalltalk (R294), Julia (R296), SWI-Prolog optparse (R297), Pony stdlib cli (R298), V stdlib `flag` (R300), Clojure 2, Gleam Glint (R303), Ada GNAT.Command_Line (R304), C++ 2, Elixir Optimus (R306), Vala GLib.OptionContext (R307), Hare stdlib getopt (R308), Standard ML stdlib GetOpt (R309), Fortran M_CLI2 (R310), Guile (ice-9 getopt-long) (R311), Free Pascal 2, Idris 2 System.GetOpts (R313), Mercury stdlib getopt_io (R314), Eiffel Gobo AP_PARSER (R315), Forth/Gforth `next-arg` (R317), Roc weaver (R319), Chapel `config` keyword (R320), Mojo 2, Chicken Scheme `args` egg (R324), Lean 4 Cli (R326), Bigloo Scheme `args-parse` macro (R327), Rust 7 bpaf (R330), Crystal 3 commander.cr (R332), Go 5 alecthomas/kong (R334), Modula-2 stdlib `Arguments` (R335), Janet bakpakin/spork `argparse` (R336), Inko stdlib `std.optparse` (R337), Odin stdlib `core:flags` (R338), OCaml 4 rbardou/Clap (R339), TypeScript/Node oclif (R340), TypeScript/Node yargs (R341), Go 6 alexflint/go-arg (R342), TypeScript/Node 3 tj/commander.js (R343), Rust 8 google/argh (R344), Python 4 BrianPugh/cyclopts (R345), Scala 2 lihaoyi/mainargs (R346), Rust 9 RazrFalcon/pico-args (R347), Crystal 4 at-grandpa/clim (R348), Lua 2 mpeterv/argparse (R349), Go 7 integrii/flaggy (R350), Common Lisp 3 sjl/adopt (R351), Python 5 docopt (R352), Java 4 rvesse/airline (R353), **Go 8 jessevdk/go-flags** (R354 — NEW).  **MAJOR PATTERN MILESTONE**: 93 cross-language CLI library variants documented.  Go NOW HAS EIGHT documented CLI libraries.
  - Critical extraction note: gostatic uses **go-flags multiple-separate-tags-per-directive CLI pattern** at gostatic.go:30-48 — `Verbose bool \`short:"v" long:"verbose" description:"enable verbose output"\`` + `Port string \`short:"p" long:"port" default:"8000" description:"port to serve on"\``.  go-flags uses MULTIPLE SEPARATE STRUCT TAGS — `short`, `long`, `description`, `default`, `env`, `required`, `value-name`, `choice`, `optional`, `optional-value` — each as an INDEPENDENT tag key.  Each directive is accessed via Go's `reflect.StructTag.Get("long")`, `Get("short")`, etc.  Distinct from R334 alecthomas/kong (SINGLE COMBINED `kong:"name='X',short='y',default='V',help='...'"` tag with key='value' DSL — single `kong:` tag string contains all directives), R342 alexflint/go-arg (SINGLE `arg:"--seeds,separate,env:MGMT_SEEDS"` tag with COMMA-SEPARATED directives + SEPARATE `help:"..."` tag), R338 Odin core:flags (SINGLE `args:"name=X,pos=N,manifold=K"` tag), R275 Rust clap-derive `#[arg(short = 'a', long = "alpha")]` (Rust ATTRIBUTE with named args — SINGLE attribute call).  go-flags' approach: MULTIPLE SEPARATE STRUCT TAGS per directive — corpus's **first documented multiple-separate-tags-per-directive CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: gostatic uses **go-flags pointer-type-for-optional-value CLI pattern** at gostatic.go:39 — `InitExample *string \`short:"i" long:"init" description:"create example site"\``.  go-flags interprets `*string` (POINTER TO STRING) field type as OPTIONAL-VALUE — caller checks `opts.InitExample != nil` to distinguish "flag NOT given" from "flag given with value" or "flag given WITHOUT value".  CALLER LOGIC: `if opts.InitExample != nil { if len(*opts.InitExample) > 0 { ... explicit path given ... } else { ... flag given without value, use default ... } }`.  go-flags also supports `*string` with `optional-value:"DEFAULT"` tag for "if no value given, use DEFAULT".  Distinct from R342 alexflint/go-arg (uses `Option<T>` Rust-style via Go's `*T`), R275 Rust clap-derive `Option<T>` (Rust optional), R346 Scala mainargs `Option[T]` (Scala optional), R338 Odin core:flags (no built-in optional, uses field zero-value).  go-flags' approach: `*POINTER-TYPE` field as OPTIONAL-VALUE marker — corpus's **first documented pointer-type-as-optional-value-marker CLI pattern** for Go (clap-derive's `Option<T>` is similar in Rust but uses different language semantics).
  - Critical extraction note: gostatic uses **go-flags parser-construction-with-behavior-bitmask CLI pattern** at gostatic.go:53 — `argparser := flags.NewParser(&opts, flags.PrintErrors|flags.PassDoubleDash|flags.HelpFlag)`.  go-flags' `NewParser` takes a SECOND ARGUMENT — a BITMASK OF BEHAVIOR-CONTROL FLAGS (`PrintErrors` = print errors to stderr; `PassDoubleDash` = don't error on `--` separator; `HelpFlag` = auto-register `-h/--help`; `IgnoreUnknown` = don't error on unknown flags; `Default` = combined common-case flags; `IniDefault` = read default values from INI file; `AllowBoolValues` = allow `--foo=true` syntax).  Caller selects EXPLICIT behavior at construction time.  Distinct from R334 kong (uses `kong.Vars{"K": "V"}` options struct + `kong.Configuration()`/`kong.Description()`/etc. variadic option functions), R275 clap-derive (default behavior built into derive; override via separate `#[command(...)]` struct attributes), R341 yargs (chained `.strict(true)` `.help()` `.version()` calls), R346 mainargs (no behavior control — fixed behavior via `runOrExit`/`runOrThrow`/`runRaw` variants on Parser).  go-flags' approach: EXPLICIT BITMASK OF BEHAVIOR-CONTROL FLAGS at parser-construction time — corpus's **first documented bitmask-of-behavior-control-flags-at-parser-construction CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: gostatic uses **go-flags parser-Parse-returning-positional-tuple CLI pattern** at gostatic.go:55 — `args, err := argparser.Parse()`.  go-flags' `parser.Parse()` returns `(positional []string, err error)` TUPLE — first element is the POSITIONAL REMAINDER (args after all flags consumed), second is the error.  Caller can use `args` for further positional argument processing.  Distinct from R342 alexflint/go-arg `arg.MustParse(&args)` (no return — modifies struct in place, panics on error), R334 kong `ctx := kong.Parse(cli, ...)` (returns Context for `ctx.Run` dispatch — no positional remainder tuple), R275 clap-derive `Cli::parse()` (returns parsed struct only, positional in struct field), R350 flaggy `flaggy.Parse()` (no return, struct fields populated, `sub.Used` for dispatch).  go-flags' approach: `parser.Parse()` returns `(positional, error)` TUPLE — caller handles positional remainder explicitly — corpus's **first documented Parse-returning-positional-args-and-error-tuple CLI pattern**.
  - Hunt-skip notes: skipped `_loxilb` (loxilb-io/loxilb 1845 stars eBPF load balancer — uses go-flags but main CLI surface is mostly init-time bootstrap with simple flags), `_dcrd` (decred/dcrd 773 stars Decred daemon — uses go-flags but cryptocurrency-specific niche; HOLD for future round), `_cred-alert` (pivotal-cf 113 stars — narrow credential scanner), `_snap` (canonical/snapd — IN CORPUS via Snap package manager; uses go-flags but already documented under different lib attribution).  Considered alternative go CLI libraries: `_mitchellh/cli` users (would be 9th Go CLI library — used by HashiCorp tools like Consul/Vault/Terraform; HOLD), `_pborman/getopt`-based tools (GNU-getopt-port; would be 9th-10th; HOLD), `_namsral/flag` (envconfig-style; HOLD).  Chose gostatic: 443 GH stars (real production static site generator), 11 flags + 1 positional + STRUCT-TAG-BASED declarative API + MULTIPLE SEPARATE TAGS PER DIRECTIVE + POINTER-TYPE for OPTIONAL VALUE + PARSER CONSTRUCTION WITH BEHAVIOR BITMASK + Parse-returning-positional-tuple, full gostatic.go exercising EVERY MAJOR go-flags PATTERN.
  - Blacklist update: `gostatic` added.  Corpus count: 28,776 → 28,777 (+1).

- **R353 — ontop source-direct (Ontop OBDA platform — query relational DBs as Virtual RDF Knowledge Graphs using SPARQL; rvesse/airline library)** (1 file new) — pivots from docopt (R352 mqttwarn) to a FOURTH Java CLI library — corpus's **first documented rvesse/airline library** + **fourth documented Java CLI library**.  ontop is the Free University of Bozen-Bolzano's OBDA (Ontology-Based Data Access) platform that translates SPARQL queries into SQL against a relational database — enables querying RDBMS as Virtual RDF Knowledge Graphs.  852 GH stars.  Sister tools: Apache Jena (Java RDF framework), Stardog (commercial RDF DB), Virtuoso (multi-paradigm DB), GraphDB (Ontotext).  **MAJOR MILESTONE**: corpus's **first documented rvesse/airline library** + **fourth documented Java CLI library**.  Java ecosystem now has 4 documented CLI libraries (R276 picocli + R322 JCommander + R323 args4j + R353 airline) joining the multi-library leaders.  Adds Airline (originally airlift/airline by Martin Traverso, now maintained as rvesse/airline by Rob Vesse) to the cross-language CLI taxonomy: Airline is the Java CLI framework used by Apache Cassandra's `nodetool`, Apache Druid CLI, Confluent ksqlDB, Apache Pinot, Graylog, AWS Neptune Export, and many other large Java systems.  450+ GH stars on rvesse/airline.  Distinguishing features vs picocli/JCommander/args4j:
    - `@Command(name = "X", description = "Y")` ANNOTATION on COMMAND CLASS (similar to picocli but command CLASS is the unit, not annotated FIELDS on an object)
    - `@Option(type = OptionType.COMMAND, name = {"--long"}, title = "X", description = "Y")` ANNOTATION with EXPLICIT SCOPE TYPE — `OptionType.COMMAND` for command-level flag, `OptionType.GLOBAL` for top-level global, `OptionType.GROUP` for subcommand-group-level — RICH SCOPE DECLARATION at the ANNOTATION
    - `@Env("VAR_NAME")` ANNOTATION for env var fallback (distinct from picocli's `@Option(envVars = {"X"})` argument key)
    - `@BashCompletion(behaviour = CompletionBehaviour.FILENAMES)` BUILT-IN COMPLETION HINTS at the annotation
    - `@RequiredOnlyIf(names = {"--other-flag"})` CONDITIONAL REQUIRED constraint (distinct from picocli's ArgGroup or programmatic validation)
    - `@RequiredUnlessEnvironment(vars = {"X"})` env-var-fallback for required validation
    - `Cli.<T>builder("name").withDescription("X").withCommands(Cls.class, ...).withGroup("verb").withDescription("Y").withCommand(SubCls.class).build()` — BUILDER-BASED top-level CLI with EXPLICIT COMMAND-CLASS LIST (vs picocli's `@Command(subcommands = {...})` static enumeration on the class)
    - `ParseCommandMissingException` / `ParseArgumentsUnexpectedException` / `ParseOptionMissingException` / `ParseArgumentsIllegalValueException` — RICH ERROR HIERARCHY for granular dispatch
  Extends cross-language CLI library taxonomy variant count to 92.
  - `_ontop` (1-stem; ontop/ontop client/cli/src/main/java/it/unibz/inf/ontop/cli/Ontop.java + Ontop*.java; **rvesse/airline library** — `import com.github.rvesse.airline.Cli` + `import com.github.rvesse.airline.annotations.{Command, Option, OptionType}` brings airline into scope; **8 visible top-level subcommands (`version` + `help` + `query` + `materialize` + `bootstrap` + `validate` + `endpoint` + `extract-db-metadata`) + 1 hidden subcommand (`compile`) + 1 subcommand group (`mapping` with 4 sub-verbs `obda-to-r2rml`/`r2rml-to-obda`/`r2rml-prettify`/`mapping-v1-to-v3`) + 9 flags on `endpoint` subcommand + common mapping/ontology/properties flags + `@Command(name, description)` annotation on command class + `@Option(type, name, title, description)` annotation on field + `OptionType.COMMAND`/`OptionType.GLOBAL`/`OptionType.GROUP` SCOPE TYPES + `@Env("VAR_NAME")` env-var fallback annotation + `@BashCompletion(behaviour = X)` built-in completion hints + `@RequiredOnlyIf(names = {"--other"})` conditional required constraint + `Cli.<OntopCommand>builder("ontop").withDescription(...).withCommands(Cls.class, ...)` BUILDER + `.withGroup("name").withDescription(...).withCommand(...)` subcommand group builder + rich ParseException hierarchy**) decoded source-direct from:
    * `Ontop.java` line 3-6 (`import com.github.rvesse.airline.Cli;` + `import com.github.rvesse.airline.builder.CliBuilder;` + `import com.github.rvesse.airline.parser.errors.*;` — Java IMPORTS of airline's Cli + CliBuilder + ParseException hierarchy)
    * `Ontop.java` lines 38-58 (`CliBuilder<OntopCommand> builder = Cli.<OntopCommand>builder("ontop").withDescription("Ontop system for Ontology based Data Access").withCommands(OntopVersion.class, OntopHelp.class, OntopQuery.class, OntopMaterialize.class, OntopBootstrap.class, OntopValidate.class, OntopEndpoint.class, OntopExtractDBMetadata.class, OntopCompile.class); builder.withGroup("mapping").withDescription("Manipulate mapping files").withCommand(OntopOBDAToR2RML.class).withCommand(OntopR2RMLToOBDA.class).withCommand(OntopR2RMLPrettify.class).withCommand(OntopMappingV1ToV3.class); return EnvCli.wrap(builder.build());` — TOP-LEVEL CLI BUILDER with EXPLICIT COMMAND-CLASS LIST + NESTED GROUP via `.withGroup("name").withDescription(...).withCommand(Cls.class)`)
    * `Ontop.java` lines 18-30 (`try { command = ontopCommandCLI.parse(args); command.run(); } catch (ParseCommandMissingException e) { main("help"); } catch (ParseArgumentsUnexpectedException | ParseOptionMissingException | ParseArgumentsIllegalValueException e) { System.err.println("Error: " + e.getMessage()); ... } catch (ParseException e) { ... }` — RICH PARSE EXCEPTION HIERARCHY with PER-VARIANT HANDLING)
    * `OntopEndpoint.java` lines 14-15 (`@Command(name = "endpoint", description = "Start a SPARQL endpoint powered by Ontop")` — ANNOTATION on COMMAND CLASS declares this class IS a CLI subcommand)
    * `OntopEndpoint.java` lines 18-23 (`@Option(type = OptionType.COMMAND, name = {"--portal"}, title = "endpoint portal file", description = "endpoint portal file (including title and queries)") @Env("ONTOP_PORTAL_FILE") @BashCompletion(behaviour = CompletionBehaviour.FILENAMES) String portalFile;` — STACKED ANNOTATIONS on field: `@Option` declares the flag; `@Env` adds env var fallback; `@BashCompletion` adds completion hint)
    * `OntopEndpoint.java` lines 41-49 (`@Option(type = OptionType.COMMAND, name = {"--predefined-config"}, title = "predefined query JSON config file", description = "predefined query config file") @Env("ONTOP_PREDEFINED_CONFIG") @RequiredOnlyIf(names = {"--predefined-queries"}) @BashCompletion(behaviour = CompletionBehaviour.FILENAMES) String predefinedConfig;` — STACKED ANNOTATIONS including `@RequiredOnlyIf(names = {"X"})` CONDITIONAL REQUIRED constraint — if `--predefined-queries` is also given, then `--predefined-config` becomes required)
  - Critical extraction note: ontop uses **rvesse/airline library** at Ontop.java:3-6 — corpus's **first documented rvesse/airline library** + **fourth documented Java CLI library**.  Airline (originally airlift/airline by Martin Traverso at Airlift, now maintained by Rob Vesse as rvesse/airline) is the Java CLI framework used by Apache Cassandra's `nodetool` (one of the most-used Java CLIs in the world), Apache Druid CLI (real-time analytics database), Confluent ksqlDB, Apache Pinot, Graylog, AWS Neptune Export.  450+ GH stars on rvesse/airline.  Distinct from R276 picocli (annotation-on-FIELDS + class is the spec object via `@Command` + `@Option` on fields), R322 JCommander (annotation-on-FIELDS + builder for parsing), R323 args4j (annotation-on-FIELDS, similar to picocli but older).  Airline's distinguishing features: (1) `@Command(name, description)` on COMMAND CLASS — the CLASS is the unit, fields hold OPTIONS; (2) `@Option(type = OptionType.X, ...)` with EXPLICIT SCOPE TYPE — `OptionType.COMMAND` for command-level, `OptionType.GLOBAL` for top-level, `OptionType.GROUP` for subcommand-group-level — caller declares scope AT THE ANNOTATION rather than via flag's POSITION in hierarchy; (3) `@Env("VAR")` SEPARATE ANNOTATION for env var fallback (vs picocli's `envVars = {"X"}` argument key); (4) `@BashCompletion(behaviour = CompletionBehaviour.X)` BUILT-IN COMPLETION HINTS at annotation time (vs picocli's separate AutoComplete tool); (5) `@RequiredOnlyIf(names = {"X"})` CONDITIONAL REQUIRED (constraint annotations are FIRST-CLASS); (6) `Cli.<T>builder("name").withCommands(Cls.class, ...).withGroup("verb").withCommand(SubCls.class).build()` BUILDER-based CLI construction with EXPLICIT COMMAND-CLASS LIST (vs picocli's `@Command(subcommands = {Cls.class, ...})` static enumeration); (7) RICH PARSE EXCEPTION HIERARCHY for granular error dispatch.
