    * `inko-lang/inko/std/src/std/optparse.inko` (public API: `Options`, `Matches`, `Lexer`, `Token`, `Error`, `Value`, `Opt`, `Kind`, `State` types — full pub interface includes `Options.flag` + `Options.single` + `Options.multiple` + `Options.parse` + `Options.to_string` + `Matches.contains?` + `Matches.value` + `Matches.values` + Lexer-as-public-API for grammar inspection)
  - Critical extraction note: clogs uses **Inko stdlib `std.optparse` module** at clogs.inko:7 — corpus's **first documented Inko entry**.  Inko is Yorick Peterse's language for CONCURRENT software with confidence: LIGHTWEIGHT PROCESSES (M:N scheduler), DETERMINISTIC MEMORY MANAGEMENT (no GC, no manual memory, uses single-ownership + reference counting at boundary), STATIC TYPING, ALGEBRAIC EFFECTS-ish error handling via `Result[T, E]` (no exceptions in the traditional sense — `throw` is a syntactic sugar for early-return-Result-Error).  Inko's compiler is in Rust (1277 stars), the stdlib is in Inko itself.  Distinct from R270 Haskell optparse-applicative (applicative-combinator), R272 C getopt (loop+switch), R280 Ruby OptionParser (DSL), R322 Java JCommander (annotation), R334 Go alecthomas/kong (struct-tag).  Inko's `std.optparse` approach: METHOD-BUILDER on a MUTABLE `Options` object + `Result[Matches, Error]`-RETURNING PARSE + `Matches` ACCESSOR-OBJECT with PREDICATE-WITH-QUESTION-MARK naming (`contains?`) + UNICODE OPTION SUPPORT (extended grapheme clusters as short flags) + GETOPTS-INSPIRED but OPINIONATED (no short+long combining like `-abc` for `-a -b -c`, no GNU-style `--no-foo`, no abbreviation matching).  Extends cross-language CLI taxonomy to SEVENTY-SIX library variants: Rust (7), Go (5), Python (3), Crystal 3, Haskell (R270), OCaml 3, C (R272), Java 3, Kotlin (R277), Scala (R278), Dart (R279), Ruby (R280), Perl (R281), PHP (R282), Swift (R283), F# (R284), Lua (R285), C# (R286), Nim 2, Zig (R288), D (R289), Racket (R290), Common Lisp 2, Erlang 2, Tcl (R293), Pharo Smalltalk (R294), Julia (R296), SWI-Prolog optparse (R297), Pony stdlib cli (R298), V stdlib `flag` (R300), Clojure 2, Gleam Glint (R303), Ada GNAT.Command_Line (R304), C++ 2, Elixir Optimus (R306), Vala GLib.OptionContext (R307), Hare stdlib getopt (R308), Standard ML stdlib GetOpt (R309), Fortran M_CLI2 (R310), Guile (ice-9 getopt-long) (R311), Free Pascal 2, Idris 2 System.GetOpts (R313), Mercury stdlib getopt_io (R314), Eiffel Gobo AP_PARSER (R315), Forth/Gforth `next-arg` (R317), Roc weaver (R319), Chapel `config` keyword (R320), Mojo 2, Chicken Scheme `args` egg (R324), Lean 4 Cli (R326), Bigloo Scheme `args-parse` macro (R327), Rust 7 bpaf (R330), Crystal 3 commander.cr (R332), Go 5 alecthomas/kong (R334), Modula-2 stdlib `Arguments` (R335), Janet bakpakin/spork `argparse` (R336), **Inko stdlib `std.optparse`** (R337 — NEW).  **MAJOR PATTERN MILESTONE**: 76 cross-language CLI library variants documented.  Inko added to the documented-CLI-language set.
  - Critical extraction note: clogs uses **Inko `std.optparse` method-builder-on-mutable-Options + Result-returning-parse CLI pattern** at clogs.inko:21-36 — `let opts = Options.new('clogs'); opts.usage = '...'; opts.description = '...'; opts.flag('h', 'help', '...'); opts.single('c', 'config', 'CONFIG', '...'); let matches = match opts.parse(arguments) { case Ok(v) -> v; case Error(e) -> throw e.to_string }`.  Inko's `Options` is a MUTABLE BUILDER object — CONSTRUCTED with `Options.new(name)`, then CONFIGURED via FIELD ASSIGNMENT (`opts.usage = ...`) + METHOD CALLS (`opts.flag(...)`, `opts.single(...)`, `opts.multiple(...)`).  Parse phase returns `Result[Matches, Error]` — CALLER PATTERN-MATCHES on Result with `match ... case Ok(v) ... case Error(e) -> throw e.to_string`.  `throw` in Inko is a SYNTACTIC SUGAR for early-return-Result-Error (the calling function MUST have `-> Result[..., String]` signature; `throw` becomes `return Result.Error(...)`).  Distinct from R275 clap-derive `Cli::parse() -> Self` (panics on parse error), R270 Haskell `execParser` (auto-exits on error), R333 Mojo argmojo `parse_from_command(cmd^) -> Args` (returns struct), R334 Go alecthomas/kong `ctx := kong.Parse(cli, ...)` (returns context).  Inko's approach: METHOD-BUILDER + `Result[Matches, Error]`-RETURNING PARSE — corpus's **first documented Result-returning-parse-with-throw-sugar CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: clogs uses **Inko `std.optparse` predicate-with-question-mark + Option-returning-accessor CLI pattern** at clogs.inko:38 + 49-51 — `if matches.contains?('help') { ... }` and `matches.value('config').map(fn (v) { v.to_path }).or_else(fn { working_directory.join(CONFIG_FILE) })`.  Inko's METHOD-NAMING CONVENTION uses `?` SUFFIX for BOOLEAN-RETURNING PREDICATES (similar to Ruby — `contains?`, `empty?`, `file?`, `valid?`).  Accessors return `Option[T]` (Inko's optional type, similar to Rust's `Option<T>`); CALLER CHAINS `.map(fn)` / `.or_else(fn)` / `.or(value)` / `.get` (with-panic-on-none) for value extraction.  Distinct from R275 clap-derive `cli.config: Option<String>` (struct field access), R322 JCommander `params.getConfig() // Java getter`, R334 kong `cli.Config string` (direct struct field), R311 Guile `(option-ref options 'config #f)` (assoc-lookup with default).  Inko's approach: `?` SUFFIX PREDICATE + `Option[T]` ACCESSOR + CHAINABLE `.map/.or_else` — corpus's **first documented `?`-suffix-predicate + Option-returning-accessor CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: clogs uses **Inko `std.optparse` matches-remaining-positional-with-Result-get-accessor CLI pattern** at clogs.inko:53-67 — `match matches.remaining.get(0) { case Ok('init') if config_path.file? -> ...; case Ok('init') -> ...; case Ok(val) -> match Version.parse(val) { ... } case _ -> ... }`.  Inko's `matches.remaining` is `Array[String]` of POSITIONAL ARGS LEFT OVER after option processing.  `.get(0)` returns `Result[String, OutOfBounds]` rather than `Option[String]` — Inko's stdlib Array uses `Result` for bounds-checked access (consistent with parse errors, not separate Option-vs-Result API).  Distinct from R275 clap-derive `Vec<String> rest` (panics on out-of-bounds), R322 JCommander positional `@Parameter` field (typed accessor), R280 Ruby `ARGV` (raw array), R317 Forth `next-arg` (no remainder concept).  Inko's approach: `matches.remaining` Array + `.get(N)` returning `Result[String, OutOfBounds]` + `match ... case Ok(literal-string-pattern) -> ...` LITERAL-STRING DISPATCH — corpus's **first documented matches-remaining-with-Result-get-accessor CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: clogs uses **Inko `std.optparse` value-name-placeholder-in-help CLI pattern** at clogs.inko:30-31 — `opts.single('c', 'config', 'CONFIG', '...')` and `opts.single('e', 'end', 'REF', '...')`.  Inko's `opts.single(short, long, VALUE_NAME, description)` accepts a VALUE_NAME positional argument that is rendered in the AUTO-GENERATED HELP as `-c, --config CONFIG` (rather than `-c, --config VALUE` or `-c, --config=ARG`).  Encodes the SEMANTIC TYPE of the value (e.g. CONFIG = config file path, REF = git ref) inline in the option definition, no separate `value_parser`/`converter`.  Distinct from R275 clap-derive `#[arg(value_name = "CONFIG")]` (attribute argument), R322 JCommander `@Parameter(names = "...", arity = 1, description = "..." /* no value_name */)`, R280 Ruby OptionParser `'-c CONFIG', '--config CONFIG'` (value_name inline in flag string), R334 kong `name='config'` no value_name (defaults to flag name upcased).  Inko's approach: 4-ARITY `opts.single(short, long, value_name, desc)` with VALUE_NAME as DEDICATED POSITIONAL PARAMETER — corpus's **first documented value_name-as-dedicated-positional-parameter CLI pattern**.
  - Hunt-skip notes: skipped `_idoc` (inko-lang/idoc official Inko docgen, 3 stars — smaller surface, only 1-2 flags), `_kvi` (yorickpeterse/kvi key-value DB, 8 stars — single-subcommand, narrow), `_openflow` (yorickpeterse/openflow ventilation controller, 7 stars — narrow Itho Daalderop HVAC domain), `_shost` (yorickpeterse/shost static HTTP server, 6 stars — narrow), `_wtml` (yorickpeterse/wtml 0 stars — demo only).  Considered Inko-adjacent: `_inko` (the language driver — STOCK Inko distribution, would be DUP with Inko proper; also written in Rust not Inko).  Probed `_carp` (Erik Svedäng's Lisp-compiles-to-C — 5929 stars but compiler itself is in Haskell, would be wrong-language attribution), `_fennel` (Calvin Rose's Lua-Lisp — 2737 stars but compiler itself is in Fennel-bootstrapped-from-Lua, CLI is in `fennel.fnl` but uses Lua's `arg` table directly NOT a library, would be a TOO-PRIMITIVE entry), `_moonbit` (Tencent's MoonBit — no real CLI tools in MoonBit yet beyond the toolchain stub).  Chose clogs: 25 GH stars (highest among real Inko user-facing tools), 4 documented flags (2 boolean + 2 single-value) + 2 positional dispatch verbs (`init` + `X.Y.Z` semver) + `Options.new(name)` constructor with PROGRAM NAME + `opts.usage` / `opts.description` FIELD ASSIGNMENT + `opts.flag/single/multiple` METHOD-BUILDER + `opts.parse(args) -> Result[Matches, Error]` + `matches.contains?` + `matches.value(name)` + `matches.remaining.get(N)`, written by Yorick Peterse (creator of Inko) so canonical-usage-pattern guaranteed, real production tool used by yorickpeterse's own projects + the inko-lang/inko repo's own changelog generation.
  - Blacklist update: `clogs` added.  Corpus count: 28,759 → 28,760 (+1).

- **R336 — jwno source-direct (Jwno tiling window manager for Windows 10/11 in Janet; bakpakin/spork `argparse` module)** (1 file new) — pivots from PIM Modula-2 `Arguments` (R335 mocka) into the modern systems-language ecosystem to document the FIRST Janet CLI library — corpus's **first documented Janet entry**.  Jwno is a tiling window manager for Windows 10/11, built with Janet + Win32 FFI by agent-kilo.  Provides REPL-based runtime introspection (jwno --client connects to a running instance), config-as-code via `.janet` files, PEG-validated REPL listen addresses (`IPv4:PORT`), and a structured log-level taxonomy.  466 GH stars (production WM deployed on Windows).  Sister tools: yabai (macOS tiling, in corpus), bspwm (X11 tiling, in corpus), GlazeWM (Windows tiling in Rust, in corpus), komorebi (Windows tiling in Rust, in corpus).  **MAJOR MILESTONE**: corpus's **first documented Janet entry**.  Janet is Calvin Rose's Lisp-family language designed for embedded scripting + scripting alternative to Lua + general-purpose use; spork is the de-facto "extras" library distributed alongside Janet providing argparse + path + json + sh + fmt + http modules.  Adds Janet to the cross-language CLI taxonomy: bakpakin/spork's `argparse` macro provides SPEC-AS-VARARGS-LIST declarative API (`(argparse "description" "flag-name" {:kind :flag :short "f" :help "..." :default ... :map fn} ...)`) returning a Janet table keyed by flag name, with `:kind :flag`/`:option`/`:accumulate` for boolean/single-value/repeating flags, `:default` key for POSITIONAL ARGS (treated as catch-all), inline `:map FN` value-transform-function on parsed values (e.g. `:map keyword` to convert string to Janet keyword), and inline-value-validators via `:map` raising `errorf` (e.g. PEG validation rejecting malformed IPv4:PORT).  Extends cross-language CLI library taxonomy variant count to 75.
  - `_jwno` (1-stem; agent-kilo/jwno src/config.janet; **Janet bakpakin/spork `argparse` module** — `(use spork/argparse)` brings the `argparse` macro into scope; **11 flags (1 default-positional accumulator + 4 :flag + 3 :option + 4 :accumulate) + `:short "X"` SINGLE-LETTER ALIASES + `:default VAL` DEFAULT VALUES + `:help "..."` HELP STRINGS + `:map FN` VALUE-TRANSFORM-FUNCTIONS + `:map keyword` STRING-TO-KEYWORD CONVERSION + `:map (fn [addr-str] (if-let [m (peg/match addr-peg addr-str)] m (errorf "...")))` PEG-VALIDATED VALUES + `:default {... :kind :accumulate}` POSITIONAL-ARGS-AS-CATCH-ALL accumulator + spec-as-VARARGS-LIST declarative API + table-return PARSED VALUES**) decoded source-direct from:
    * `src/config.janet` line 1 (`(use spork/argparse)` — Janet `use` SPECIAL FORM imports all public symbols from `spork/argparse` module into the current namespace; `spork` is bakpakin/spork the de-facto Janet "extras" library — distributed via Janet's `jpm` package manager + with the main Janet language tarball)
    * `src/config.janet` lines 38-104 (`(argparse "Jwno: A Tiling Window Manager for Windows" :default {:help "..." :kind :accumulate} "client" {:short "C" :help "..." :kind :flag} "config" {:short "c" :help "..." :kind :accumulate} ...)` — `argparse` MACRO accepts spec-as-VARARGS-LIST: leading description STRING + alternating flag-name STRING + properties TABLE pairs; SPECIAL KEY `:default` configures the POSITIONAL-ARGS catch-all)
    * `src/config.janet` lines 45-50 (`"client" {:short "C" :help "\nStarts in REPL client mode. ...\n" :kind :flag}` — JANET TABLE with `:kind :flag` for BOOLEAN FLAG, `:short "C"` for SINGLE-LETTER ALIAS, `:help "..."` for HELP TEXT)
    * `src/config.janet` lines 52-56 (`"config" {:short "c" :help "..." :kind :accumulate}` — `:kind :accumulate` for REPEATING FLAG that accumulates VALUES INTO AN ARRAY)
    * `src/config.janet` lines 78-82 (`"log-level" {:help "..." :default "quiet" :kind :option :map keyword}` — `:kind :option` for VALUE-TAKING FLAG, `:default "quiet"` for DEFAULT VALUE, `:map keyword` for INLINE VALUE-TRANSFORM-FUNCTION converting parsed STRING to Janet KEYWORD)
    * `src/config.janet` lines 90-99 (`"repl" {:short "r" :help "..." :kind :option :map (fn [addr-str] (if-let [repl-addr (peg/match repl-addr-peg addr-str)] repl-addr (errorf "Malformed REPL address: %n" addr-str)))}` — `:map FN` INLINE VALIDATOR raising `errorf` if PEG match FAILS)
    * `src/config.janet` lines 15-35 (`(def repl-addr-peg (peg/compile ~{:ip-seg (choice ...) :ip (sequence :ip-seg "." :ip-seg "." :ip-seg "." :ip-seg) :port-capture (replace (capture :port) ,parse) :main (sequence :ip-capture ":" :port-capture -1)}))` — STANDALONE PEG GRAMMAR for IPv4:PORT validation, threaded into argparse via `:map (fn [s] (peg/match repl-addr-peg s))` — corpus's **first documented argparse-with-PEG-VALIDATED-VALUE CLI pattern**)
    * `src/config.janet` lines 42-44 (`:default {:help "..." :kind :accumulate}` — `:default` SPECIAL KEY in argparse VARARGS configures the POSITIONAL-ARGS catch-all; positionals here are accumulated as config file paths)
  - Critical extraction note: jwno uses **Janet `spork/argparse` module** at config.janet:1 — corpus's **first documented Janet entry**.  Janet is Calvin Rose's Lisp-family language (~3.5K stars on github.com/janet-lang/janet) designed for embedded scripting and general scripting — alternative to Lua with a more Lisp-idiomatic API.  bakpakin/spork is the official "extras" library — distributed alongside Janet via `jpm install spork` or with the main language tarball.  spork provides `argparse` + `path` + `json` + `sh` + `fmt` + `http` + `temple` + `cc` + `regex` + `cookies` modules.  spork's `argparse` exposes one MACRO: `(argparse "description" "flag-name" {:kind :flag/:option/:accumulate :short "X" :default ... :help "..." :map fn} ...)` returning a Janet TABLE keyed by flag name when parsing succeeds, or nil + printing usage on parse failure.  Distinct from R321 Mojo `collect_args[POSITIONAL, ARGUMENTS, FLAGS]()` (compile-time list-as-spec), R326 Lean 4 Cli (`do let cmd ← Cli.parse ...`), R311 Guile `getopt-long` (assoc-list spec), R290 Racket `command-line` macro, R294 Pharo Smalltalk `CommandLineHandler` class, R293 Tcl `cmdline::getoptions` arglist.  Janet's approach: VARARGS-LIST-OF-(STRING + TABLE)-PAIRS declarative API with INLINE `:map FN` VALUE-TRANSFORM-FUNCTIONS (closures over the parser context).  Extends cross-language CLI taxonomy to SEVENTY-FIVE library variants: Rust (7), Go (5), Python (3), Crystal 3, Haskell (R270), OCaml 3, C (R272), Java 3, Kotlin (R277), Scala (R278), Dart (R279), Ruby (R280), Perl (R281), PHP (R282), Swift (R283), F# (R284), Lua (R285), C# (R286), Nim 2, Zig (R288), D (R289), Racket (R290), Common Lisp 2, Erlang 2, Tcl (R293), Pharo Smalltalk (R294), Julia (R296), SWI-Prolog optparse (R297), Pony stdlib cli (R298), V stdlib `flag` (R300), Clojure 2, Gleam Glint (R303), Ada GNAT.Command_Line (R304), C++ 2, Elixir Optimus (R306), Vala GLib.OptionContext (R307), Hare stdlib getopt (R308), Standard ML stdlib GetOpt (R309), Fortran M_CLI2 (R310), Guile (ice-9 getopt-long) (R311), Free Pascal 2, Idris 2 System.GetOpts (R313), Mercury stdlib getopt_io (R314), Eiffel Gobo AP_PARSER (R315), Forth/Gforth `next-arg` (R317), Roc weaver (R319), Chapel `config` keyword (R320), Mojo 2, Chicken Scheme `args` egg (R324), Lean 4 Cli (R326), Bigloo Scheme `args-parse` macro (R327), Rust 7 bpaf (R330), Crystal 3 commander.cr (R332), Go 5 alecthomas/kong (R334), Modula-2 stdlib `Arguments` (R335), **Janet bakpakin/spork `argparse`** (R336 — NEW).  **MAJOR PATTERN MILESTONE**: 75 cross-language CLI library variants documented.  Janet added to the documented-CLI-language set.
  - Critical extraction note: jwno uses **Janet `spork/argparse` spec-as-VARARGS-LIST + table-return CLI pattern** at config.janet:38-104 — `(argparse "Jwno: A Tiling Window Manager for Windows" :default {...} "client" {:short "C" ...} "config" {:short "c" ...} "eval" {...} ...)`.  The `argparse` MACRO accepts a description STRING followed by alternating flag-name STRING + properties TABLE pairs as VARARGS — Janet's variadic macros walk the rest-args list (`& args`) and pair them up.  On parse success, returns a Janet TABLE keyed by flag-name strings with parsed values; on parse failure, returns `nil` and prints auto-generated usage to stderr.  Distinct from R275 clap-derive `Cli::parse() -> Self` (returns parsed struct), R322 JCommander `parser.parse(*argv)` returns `void`, parsed struct accessed manually), R334 Go alecthomas/kong `ctx := kong.Parse(cli, ...)` (returns context for `ctx.Run` dispatch), R331 Jane Street `Command.basic ~summary param => fn` (callback-on-parse).  Janet's approach: SPEC-AS-VARARGS-LIST + TABLE-RETURN — corpus's **first documented spec-as-VARARGS-LIST-of-string-and-table-pairs CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: jwno uses **Janet `spork/argparse` :map-fn-inline-validator CLI pattern** at config.janet:78-82 + 90-99 — `"log-level" {:help "..." :default "quiet" :kind :option :map keyword}` and `"repl" {:short "r" :help "..." :kind :option :map (fn [addr-str] (if-let [repl-addr (peg/match repl-addr-peg addr-str)] repl-addr (errorf "Malformed REPL address: %n" addr-str)))}`.  `:map FN` is an INLINE VALUE-TRANSFORM-FUNCTION applied to the parsed value AFTER raw-string parsing.  When FN is a SYMBOL (e.g. `keyword`), it's a simple type-conversion (string → keyword).  When FN is a CLOSURE, it can perform CUSTOM VALIDATION by raising `errorf` on invalid values.  Distinct from R322 JCommander `converter = X.class` (separate converter class), R275 clap-derive `value_parser = clap::value_parser!(...)` (declarative parser), R333 Mojo argmojo `choices="a,b,c"` (string-list choices), R311 Guile `getopt-long` (no per-value transform).  Janet's approach: `:map FN` INLINE VALUE-TRANSFORM-FUNCTION as Janet CLOSURE — corpus's **first documented :map-fn-inline-validator-with-errorf-on-failure CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: jwno uses **Janet `spork/argparse` :default-key-positional-args-catchall CLI pattern** at config.janet:42-44 — `:default {:help "..." :kind :accumulate}`.  The SPECIAL KEY `:default` (a Janet KEYWORD literal, NOT a STRING flag name) inside argparse's varargs configures the POSITIONAL-ARGS catch-all.  Positional args (those NOT matching any defined `--flag` or `-X`) are routed to this `:default` entry's `:kind` behavior — here `:accumulate` collects them into an array.  Distinct from R275 clap-derive `Vec<String> #[arg(trailing_var_arg = true)]` (last-field-as-rest), R322 JCommander `@Parameter` (no name, defaults to positional), R311 Guile `(@bare-args ((value #t)))` named slot.  Janet's approach: `:default` KEYWORD SPECIAL KEY inside varargs spec as positional-args sink — corpus's **first documented :default-keyword-special-key-for-positionals CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: jwno uses **Janet `spork/argparse` PEG-validated-value CLI pattern** at config.janet:15-35 + 90-99 — `(def repl-addr-peg (peg/compile ~{...}))` standalone PEG GRAMMAR threaded into argparse via `:map (fn [addr-str] (if-let [repl-addr (peg/match repl-addr-peg addr-str)] repl-addr (errorf "Malformed REPL address: %n" addr-str)))`.  Janet's BUILT-IN PEG MODULE provides `peg/compile` + `peg/match` for grammar-based string validation; the compiled PEG is reused via closure capture inside argparse's `:map` FN.  PEG grammars are NATIVE LITERALS in Janet — `~{...}` is a QUASIQUOTED struct mapping grammar-rule-names to PEG-EXPRESSIONS.  Distinct from R275 clap-derive `value_parser` (no PEG), R322 JCommander `validateWith = X.class` (regex-based validators typical), R333 Mojo argmojo `choices="a,b,c"` (no PEG).  Janet's approach: STANDALONE-PEG-GRAMMAR + `:map` CLOSURE thread for validation — corpus's **first documented argparse-with-PEG-validated-value CLI pattern**.
  - Hunt-skip notes: skipped `_jpm` (Janet Package Manager — STOCK Janet distribution tool, would be DUP with Janet stdlib), `_jeff` (tionis/jeff Janet fuzzy finder, AVAILABLE but smaller surface), `_x43bot` (bakpakin/x43bot demo bot by Janet's creator Calvin Rose — too narrow), `_beancraft` (alexalemi register-machine compiler — too narrow), `_janet-fmt` (haruki7049/janet-fmt — minimal surface, only spork/fmt invocation), `_judge` (ianthehenry/judge Janet test framework — uses ianthehenry/cmd library NOT spork/argparse, would be a SISTER demo for cmd library in a future round).  Probed `_joy` (joy web framework CLI for Janet by sepisoad — narrow), `_sh-tools-janet` (tionis/shell-tools.janet — multi-binary repo).  Considered Janet-adjacent: `_carp` (Erik Svedäng's Lisp compiler — different language), `_inko` (Yorick Peterse's language — different language).  Chose jwno: 466 GH stars (production Windows tiling WM), 11 documented flags + positional-fallback + 4 SINGLE-LETTER ALIASES + 3 `:kind :accumulate` REPEATING + 3 `:kind :option` + 4 `:kind :flag` + `:map keyword` (string-to-keyword conversion) + `:map (fn [s] ...)` (PEG-VALIDATED IPv4:PORT) + `:default {:kind :accumulate}` (positional catch-all), full spork/argparse spec exercising EVERY MAJOR Janet CLI pattern (spec-as-VARARGS-LIST, table-return, `:map FN` INLINE VALUE-TRANSFORM-FUNCTIONS, `:default` KEYWORD SPECIAL KEY for positionals, PEG-validated values), real production binary deployed on Windows 10/11 via Janet runtime + Win32 FFI.
  - Blacklist update: `jwno` added.  Corpus count: 28,758 → 28,759 (+1).

- **R335 — mocka source-direct (MOCKA Modula-2 Compiler System from GMD Karlsruhe / Fraunhofer; PIM Modula-2 stdlib `Arguments` module)** (1 file new) — pivots from Go `alecthomas/kong` (R334 mtg) into the legacy systems-language ecosystem to document the FIRST Modula-2 CLI library — corpus's **first documented Modula-2 entry**.  MOCKA is the historic Modula-2 compiler system originally developed at GMD (Gesellschaft fuer Mathematik und Datenverarbeitung) Karlsruhe (1988-2000), then transferred to Fraunhofer (2001-2018), now archived on GitHub by trijezdci (2018+ rescue mirror).  Compiles DEFINITION + IMPLEMENTATION modules, binds (links) executables, manages variant tags, and provides an interactive REPL.  23 GH stars on the rescue mirror.  Sister tools: GNU Modula-2 (`gm2` in corpus via GCC frontend), XDS Modula-2 (Excelsior), p1 Modula-2.  Adds Modula-2 to the cross-language CLI taxonomy: PIM (Programming In Modula-2) stdlib `IMPORT Arguments` providing `ArgTable` (pointer-to-pointer-to-char) + `GetArgs(VAR argc: SHORTCARD, VAR argv: ArgTable)` (returns argv into the table) + hand-rolled `LOOP IF StrEq(arg, "-flag") ...` ladder for parsing.  Extends cross-language CLI library taxonomy variant count to 74.
  - `_mocka` (1-stem; trijezdci/MOCKA ver1807/src/MockaArgs.mod + MockaShell.mod; **PIM Modula-2 stdlib `Arguments` module** — `IMPORT Arguments` from MOCKA's runtime library, equivalent to BSD `getargv()` low-level argument access; **16 documented flags + positional-fallback + single-letter `-X` catch-all forwarding to `SuBase.SetOption` + `-help`/`-version` short-circuit HALT + `-V` variant-name that must not start with `-` (custom validation inline)**) decoded source-direct from:
    * `MockaArgs.mod` lines 18-19 (`FROM Arguments IMPORT ArgTable, GetArgs` — Modula-2 SELECTIVE IMPORT from stdlib `Arguments` module; `ArgTable` is the argv pointer type; `GetArgs(argc, Argv)` populates BOTH out-parameters)
    * `MockaArgs.mod` lines 130-150 (`GetArgs(argc, Argv); LastArgIndex := argc-1; ArgIndex := 1; ... LOOP IF StrEq(arg, "-s") THEN ... ELSIF StrEq(arg, "-c") THEN ... END; IF ArgIndex = LastArgIndex THEN EXIT END; INC(ArgIndex); GetArg(ArgIndex, arg); END` — Modula-2 LOOP with hand-rolled IF-ELSIF ladder over `StrEq(arg, "-flag")`; `INC(ArgIndex)` advances; positional `EXIT` exits the loop)
    * `MockaArgs.mod` lines 152-156 (`ELSIF StrEq(arg, "-help") THEN ShowHelp; HALT;` — IMMEDIATE TERMINATION via HALT after help/version print, no `return 0`)
    * `MockaArgs.mod` lines 161-168 (`ELSIF StrEq(arg, "-V") THEN GetArgValue (arg); IF arg [0] = '-' THEN ArgumentError("Argument to -V must not start with `-'", "") END; DefineVariant(arg);` — INLINE VALIDATION rejecting `-V -dashed` because the next-arg consumer might silently swallow another flag)
    * `MockaArgs.mod` lines 170-181 (`ELSIF arg[0] = '-' THEN i := 1; WHILE arg[i] <> 0C DO arg[i-1] := arg[i]; INC(i); END; arg[i-1] := 0C; SetOption(arg, ok); IF NOT ok THEN ArgumentError("unknown compiler option:", arg) END;` — UNKNOWN-FLAG-FALLBACK strips leading `-` and forwards to `SuBase.SetOption(arg, ok)` for single-letter compiler toggles)
    * `MockaShell.mod` ShowHelp procedure (`Mocka [options] [commands] module` USAGE STRING; lists `-help / -info / -options / -version` and interactive shell `d/i/s/c/p/q` commands)
  - Critical extraction note: mocka uses **PIM Modula-2 stdlib `Arguments` module** at MockaArgs.mod:18 — corpus's **first documented Modula-2 entry**.  PIM stdlib `Arguments` is the canonical low-level argv accessor for PIM (Programming In Modula-2, Wirth's original Modula-2 spec).  Exports `ArgTable` (Modula-2 pointer to array of pointers to char — analog of C's `char **argv`) + `GetArgs(VAR argc: SHORTCARD, VAR argv: ArgTable)` (populates argc/argv VAR-PARAMETERS).  No built-in flag parsing — the caller writes a hand-rolled `LOOP IF StrEq(arg, "-flag") ...` ladder.  Distinct from R272 C getopt (`getopt(argc, argv, "abc:")` short-string spec), R309 Standard ML stdlib `GetOpt.getOpt` (record-of-OptDescr spec), R313 Idris 2 `System.GetOpts` (record-of-OptDescr functional), R310 Fortran M_CLI2 `set_args('--alpha 1.0 --bravo "default"')` (string-of-defaults).  Modula-2 PIM `Arguments`'s approach: PRIMITIVE argv ACCESSOR — no parsing logic provided; hand-rolled IF-ELSIF ladder over `StrEq(arg, "-X")` for every flag.  Extends cross-language CLI taxonomy to SEVENTY-FOUR library variants: Rust (7), Go (5), Python (3), Crystal 3, Haskell (R270), OCaml 3, C (R272), Java 3, Kotlin (R277), Scala (R278), Dart (R279), Ruby (R280), Perl (R281), PHP (R282), Swift (R283), F# (R284), Lua (R285), C# (R286), Nim 2, Zig (R288), D (R289), Racket (R290), Common Lisp 2, Erlang 2, Tcl (R293), Pharo Smalltalk (R294), Julia (R296), SWI-Prolog optparse (R297), Pony stdlib cli (R298), V stdlib `flag` (R300), Clojure 2, Gleam Glint (R303), Ada GNAT.Command_Line (R304), C++ 2, Elixir Optimus (R306), Vala GLib.OptionContext (R307), Hare stdlib getopt (R308), Standard ML stdlib GetOpt (R309), Fortran M_CLI2 (R310), Guile (ice-9 getopt-long) (R311), Free Pascal 2, Idris 2 System.GetOpts (R313), Mercury stdlib getopt_io (R314), Eiffel Gobo AP_PARSER (R315), Forth/Gforth `next-arg` (R317), Roc weaver (R319), Chapel `config` keyword (R320), Mojo 2, Chicken Scheme `args` egg (R324), Lean 4 Cli (R326), Bigloo Scheme `args-parse` macro (R327), Rust 7 bpaf (R330), Crystal 3 commander.cr (R332), Go 5 alecthomas/kong (R334), **Modula-2 stdlib `Arguments`** (R335 — NEW).  **MAJOR PATTERN MILESTONE**: 74 cross-language CLI library variants documented.  Modula-2 added to the documented-CLI-language set.
  - Critical extraction note: mocka uses **Modula-2 stdlib hand-rolled `LOOP IF StrEq(arg, "-flag") ...` ladder CLI pattern** at MockaArgs.mod:130-180 — `GetArgs(argc, Argv); LastArgIndex := argc-1; ArgIndex := 1; ... LOOP IF StrEq(arg, "-s") THEN GetArgValue(NameOfModule); ModeSpec := CompileDefMode; ELSIF StrEq(arg, "-c") THEN ... ELSIF arg[0] = '-' THEN SetOption(...); ELSE ArgumentError("illegal argument:", arg); END; IF ArgIndex = LastArgIndex THEN EXIT END; INC(ArgIndex); GetArg(ArgIndex, arg); END`.  The CALLER writes a Modula-2 `LOOP` containing an IF-ELSIF ladder calling `StrEq(arg, "-flag")` for every flag.  An inner helper `GetArgValue(VAR argvalue)` advances the index and reads the next argv slot.  `INC(ArgIndex)` advances the loop; `EXIT` terminates.  Distinct from R272 C getopt (`while ((c = getopt(argc, argv, "abc:")) != -1) switch (c) { ... }` — caller writes a switch over short-char keys), R309 Standard ML `GetOpt.getOpt(ArgOrder, OptDescr, args)` (record-of-OptDescr declarative spec), R311 Guile `getopt-long args option-spec` (assoc-list declarative spec), R313 Idris 2 `getOpt RequireOrder optDescr args` (record-of-OptDescr functional).  Modula-2 stdlib `Arguments`'s approach: hand-rolled `LOOP IF StrEq(arg, "-X") ...` ladder over argv with NO parsing primitives whatsoever — corpus's **first documented hand-rolled-Modula-2-LOOP-IF-StrEq-ladder CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: mocka uses **Modula-2 stdlib `HALT`-on-help/version short-circuit CLI pattern** at MockaArgs.mod:152-153 + 158 — `ELSIF StrEq(arg, "-help") THEN ShowHelp; HALT; ... ELSIF StrEq(arg, "-version") THEN ShowVersion; HALT;`.  `HALT` is Modula-2's stdlib procedure for IMMEDIATE process termination (equivalent to C `_exit(0)` — no destructor/cleanup, just OS-level exit).  Help/version print and IMMEDIATELY TERMINATE without returning to caller.  Distinct from R272 C getopt (caller must `exit(0)` manually after `case '?':` or `case 'h':`), R309 SML GetOpt (no built-in help short-circuit), R310 Fortran M_CLI2 `lget('help')` BOOLEAN check (caller decides).  Modula-2 stdlib's approach: explicit `HALT` after help/version print — corpus's **first documented Modula-2-HALT-on-help/version-short-circuit CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: mocka uses **Modula-2 stdlib unknown-flag-fallback-strip-and-forward CLI pattern** at MockaArgs.mod:170-181 — `ELSIF arg[0] = '-' THEN i := 1; WHILE arg[i] <> 0C DO arg[i-1] := arg[i]; INC(i); END; arg[i-1] := 0C; SetOption(arg, ok); IF NOT ok THEN ArgumentError("unknown compiler option:", arg) END;`.  After all KNOWN-FLAG branches are exhausted, the catch-all `arg[0] = '-'` branch STRIPS the leading `-` (via in-place shift loop), then forwards to `SuBase.SetOption(arg, ok)` for single-letter compiler toggles (e.g. `-r` becomes `r`, looked up in a compiler option table).  Out-parameter `ok: BOOLEAN` reports whether the option was recognized.  Distinct from R272 C getopt (unknown chars return `'?'` and set `optopt`), R309 SML GetOpt (no fallback, just rejects unknown), R317 Forth `next-arg` (caller dispatches on chars manually — no fallback), R313 Idris 2 `getOpt` (returns `error` list).  Modula-2 stdlib's approach: STRIP-AND-FORWARD-TO-COMPILER-OPTION-TABLE for single-letter unknown flags — corpus's **first documented strip-and-forward-to-compiler-option-table CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: mocka uses **Modula-2 stdlib inline-validation-on-value CLI pattern** at MockaArgs.mod:161-168 — `ELSIF StrEq(arg, "-V") THEN GetArgValue(arg); IF arg[0] = '-' THEN ArgumentError("Argument to -V must not start with `-'", "") END; DefineVariant(arg);`.  After consuming the next argv slot, INLINE VALIDATION checks `arg[0] # '-'` to prevent the next-arg consumer from silently swallowing another flag (e.g. `mocka -V -info` would otherwise treat `-info` as a variant name).  Distinct from R275 clap-derive `value_parser = clap::value_parser!(...)` (declarative validator), R322 JCommander `validateWith = X.class` (separate validator class), R333 Mojo argmojo `choices="a,b,c"` (declarative comma-string), R311 Guile `getopt-long` (no inline validation).  Modula-2 stdlib's approach: INLINE-VALIDATION-AFTER-NEXT-ARG-CONSUMPTION — corpus's **first documented inline-validation-on-value CLI pattern**.
  - Hunt-skip notes: skipped `_gm2` / `_xds` (GNU Modula-2 frontend ships with GCC — STOCK_GCC dup; XDS is proprietary), `_poc` (Patchouli Oberon-07 compiler in congdm/Patchouli-Compiler at 67 stars — read its `Poc.mod` source and found only 2 options `/b` and `/sym` with the second one COMMENTED OUT, too narrow for a meaningful completion), `_oo2c` (Spirit-of-Oberon Optimizing Oberon-2 Compiler, 36 stars — Oberon family rather than Modula-2 proper), `_obl` (Eigen Compiler Suite Oberon arguments — narrow surface), `_thutt-oberon` (thutt/oberon at 9 stars — virtual-CPU Oberon system, too narrow).  Considered `_mtfind` and `_mtsort` (Modula-2 sister tools to MOCKA — both in the rescue mirror but with minimal CLI surfaces).  Probed `_pim` (no such binary), `_modula` (no such binary).  Chose mocka: 23 GH stars (rescue mirror, but the original GMD compiler was deployed across thousands of academic + commercial Modula-2 sites in the 1990s), 16 documented flags + positional-fallback + single-letter `-X` catch-all + `-help`/`-version` short-circuit HALT + `-V` variant-name with inline validation, full `MockaArgs.mod` `ScanArgs` procedure exercising EVERY MAJOR Modula-2 CLI pattern (hand-rolled LOOP-IF-StrEq ladder, HALT-on-help/version short-circuit, unknown-flag-fallback strip-and-forward to compiler-option-table, inline-validation-on-value), real binary that compiles Modula-2 DEFINITION + IMPLEMENTATION modules using PIM stdlib `Arguments` module + `StrEq` + `GetArg` primitives.
  - Blacklist update: `mocka` added.  Corpus count: 28,757 → 28,758 (+1).

- **R334 — mtg source-direct (MTProxy for Telegram in Go; `alecthomas/kong` library)** (1 file new) — pivots from Mojo argmojo (R333 decimo) into the Go ecosystem to document the FIFTH Go CLI library — corpus's **first documented alecthomas/kong library** + **fifth documented Go CLI library** (joining cobra, urfave/cli, kingpin, spf13/pflag from previous rounds).  mtg is a highly-opinionated MTPROTO proxy for Telegram — used by users in censored regions to bypass Telegram blocks.  Provides "fake TLS" obfuscation (mimics HTTPS traffic to evade DPI), domain fronting, multiple secret rotations, and TLS SNI faking.  Authored by Sergey Arkhipov (9seconds).  **3469 GH stars** (widely deployed proxy infrastructure).  Sister tools: shadowsocks (similar evasion proxy, in corpus), v2ray-core (v2fly modernization, in corpus), wireguard (in corpus), tailscale (in corpus).  **MAJOR MILESTONE**: corpus's **first documented alecthomas/kong library** — adds STRUCT-TAG-BASED declarative CLI parser (similar in spirit to clap-derive R275 but Go-style reflection on struct tags); kong is alecthomas's spiritual successor to alecthomas/kingpin (which uses fluent builder API; kong uses struct tags).  Adds Go-struct-tag-based declarative API with `cmd` tag for subcommands, KEY=VALUE pairs inside `kong:"..."` tags, built-in-type-constraint strings (`type='existingfile'`/`type='bytes'`/etc.), Go-built-in-type detection (`net.IP`/`time.Duration`/`[]string`), `kong.Parse + ctx.Run` context-based dispatch, method-on-subcommand-struct dispatch via Go reflection.  Extends cross-language CLI taxonomy to 73 library variants.  **Go NOW HAS FIVE documented CLI libraries** (cobra/urfave-cli/kingpin/spf13-pflag/kong), tied with Rust 7 as second-most-documented language for CLI library variants.
  - `_mtg` (1-stem; 9seconds/mtg main.go + internal/cli/*.go; **Go `alecthomas/kong` library** — github.com/alecthomas/kong on pkg.go.dev; **5 subcommands (generate-secret/doctor/access/run/simple-run) + version flag + `kong.Parse(ptr, ...kong.Option)` + `ctx.Run(state...)` context-based-dispatch + `kong.Vars{"K": "V"}` template variables referenced via `${K}` in struct tags + struct-field-with-`cmd` tag-as-subcommand + struct-tag-key-value declarative parsing (`arg`/`required`/`cmd`/`name='X'`/`short='X'`/`default='V'`/`help='...'`/`type='T'`/`env='VAR'`/`hidden`/`enum='a,b,c'`) + built-in-type-constraint-strings (`type='existingfile'`/`type='existingdir'`/`type='uint'`/`type='bytes'`) + Go-built-in-type-detection (`net.IP`/`time.Duration`/`[]string`/`uint64`) + `kong.VersionFlag` special type for version auto-handling + method-on-subcommand-struct `Run(args...)` dispatch via Go reflection**) decoded source-direct from:
    * `main.go` line 14 (`"github.com/alecthomas/kong"` — Go IMPORT of `alecthomas/kong` library — distributed at github.com/alecthomas/kong, MIT license, ~2200 stars)
    * `main.go` lines 32-37 (`cli := &cli.CLI{}; ctx := kong.Parse(cli, kong.Vars{"version": getVersion()}); ctx.FatalIfErrorf(ctx.Run(cli, version))` — kong PARSE ENTRY POINT; `kong.Parse(interface{}, ...kong.Option)` returns `*kong.Context`; `kong.Vars{"K": "V"}` template variables; `ctx.Run(...)` dispatches to matched subcommand's Run method)
    * `internal/cli/cli.go` lines 5-12 (`type CLI struct { GenerateSecret GenerateSecret `kong:"cmd,help='...'"`; Doctor Doctor `kong:"cmd,help='...'"`; ...; Version kong.VersionFlag `kong:"help='Print version.',short='v'"` }` — TOP-LEVEL CLI STRUCT with SUBCOMMAND FIELDS; field's `kong:"cmd,..."` tag marks it as SUBCOMMAND with KEBAB-CASE conversion)
    * `internal/cli/simple_run.go` lines 13-26 (`BindTo string `kong:"arg,required,name='bind-to',help='...'"`; Debug bool `kong:"name='debug',short='d',help='...'"`; Concurrency uint64 `kong:"name='concurrency',short='c',default='8192',help='...'"`; DOHIP net.IP `kong:"name='doh-ip',short='n',default='1.1.1.1',help='...'"`; Timeout time.Duration `kong:"name='timeout',short='t',default='10s',help='...'"`; Socks5Proxies []string `kong:"name='socks5-proxy',short='s',help='...'"`; ...` — STRUCT FIELDS with `kong` tag KEY=VALUE pairs)
    * `internal/cli/run.go` line 8 (`ConfigPath string `kong:"arg,required,type='existingfile',help='...',name='config-path'"`` — `type='existingfile'` built-in TYPE CONSTRAINT; parser validates file existence at parse time)
    * `internal/cli/simple_run.go` line 47+ (`func (s *SimpleRun) Run(cli *CLI, version string) error { ... }` — HANDLER METHOD on subcommand struct; kong dispatches via reflection)
  - Critical extraction note: mtg uses **Go `alecthomas/kong` library** at main.go:14 — corpus's **first documented alecthomas/kong library** + **fifth documented Go CLI library** (joining cobra, urfave/cli, kingpin, spf13/pflag from previous rounds).  alecthomas/kong is a STRUCT-TAG-BASED declarative CLI parser (similar in spirit to clap-derive R275 but with Go-style reflection on struct tags).  kong is alecthomas's spiritual successor to alecthomas/kingpin (kingpin uses fluent builder API; kong uses struct tags).  ~2200 GH stars.  Distinct from R275 Rust clap-derive (proc-macro), R322 Java JCommander (annotation), R331 Jane Street Core.Command (`let%map_open.Command` PPX), R301 Nim cligen (proc-signature-as-spec).  kong's approach: Go STRUCT-TAG-BASED declarative API where each struct field becomes a flag/positional/subcommand based on its `kong:"..."` tag.  Extends cross-language CLI taxonomy to SEVENTY-THREE library variants: Rust (7), **Go (5)**, Python (3), Crystal 3, Haskell (R270), OCaml 3, C (R272), Java 3, Kotlin (R277), Scala (R278), Dart (R279), Ruby (R280), Perl (R281), PHP (R282), Swift (R283), F# (R284), Lua (R285), C# (R286), Nim 2, Zig (R288), D (R289), Racket (R290), Common Lisp 2, Erlang 2, Tcl (R293), Pharo Smalltalk (R294), Julia (R296), SWI-Prolog optparse (R297), Pony stdlib cli (R298), V stdlib `flag` (R300), Clojure 2, Gleam Glint (R303), Ada GNAT.Command_Line (R304), C++ 2, Elixir Optimus (R306), Vala GLib.OptionContext (R307), Hare stdlib getopt (R308), Standard ML stdlib GetOpt (R309), Fortran M_CLI2 (R310), Guile (ice-9 getopt-long) (R311), Free Pascal 2, Idris 2 System.GetOpts (R313), Mercury stdlib getopt_io (R314), Eiffel Gobo AP_PARSER (R315), Forth/Gforth `next-arg` (R317), Roc weaver (R319), Chapel `config` keyword (R320), Mojo 2, Chicken Scheme `args` egg (R324), Lean 4 Cli (R326), Bigloo Scheme `args-parse` macro (R327), Rust 7 bpaf (R330), Crystal 3 commander.cr (R332), **Go 5 alecthomas/kong** (R334 — NEW).  **MAJOR PATTERN MILESTONE**: 73 cross-language CLI library variants documented.  Go NOW HAS FIVE documented CLI libraries.
  - Critical extraction note: mtg uses **Go `alecthomas/kong` kong.Parse(ptr, ...kong.Option) + ctx.Run(state...) context-based-dispatch CLI pattern** at main.go:32-37 — `cli := &cli.CLI{}; ctx := kong.Parse(cli, kong.Vars{"version": getVersion()}); ctx.FatalIfErrorf(ctx.Run(cli, version))`.  kong's parse phase RETURNS a `*kong.Context` carrying the parsed state; subsequent `ctx.Run(...)` dispatches to the matched subcommand's `Run(...)` method.  `kong.Vars{"K": "V"}` provides TEMPLATE VARIABLES referenced via `${K}` in struct tags.  Distinct from R275 Rust clap-derive `Cli::parse()` (returns parsed struct directly, no separate context), R322 Java JCommander `JCommander(args).parse(*argv)` (two-call), R270 Haskell `execParser opts` (single-call, no dispatch).  kong's approach: KONG.PARSE returns CONTEXT for post-parse `ctx.Run` DISPATCH — corpus's **first documented context-based-dispatch CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: mtg uses **kong struct-field-with-cmd-tag-as-subcommand CLI pattern** at cli.go:5-12 — `type CLI struct { GenerateSecret GenerateSecret `kong:"cmd,help='...'"`; Doctor Doctor `kong:"cmd,help='...'"`; ... }`.  Each field's `kong:"cmd,..."` tag marks it as a SUBCOMMAND (named after the field with KEBAB-CASE conversion — `GenerateSecret` → `generate-secret`, `SimpleRun` → `simple-run`, etc.).  Distinct from R275 clap-derive `#[derive(Subcommand)] enum` (enum-of-variants for subcommands), R322 JCommander `@Parameters(commandNames = ...)` (separate annotation), R270 Haskell `subparser (... command "verb" ...)` (applicative).  kong's approach: STRUCT-FIELDS-AS-SUBCOMMANDS via `cmd` tag directive — corpus's **first documented struct-field-with-cmd-tag-as-subcommand CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: mtg uses **kong struct-tag-key-value declarative CLI pattern** at simple_run.go:13-30 — `BindTo string `kong:"arg,required,name='bind-to',help='...'"`; Debug bool `kong:"name='debug',short='d',help='...'"`; Concurrency uint64 `kong:"name='concurrency',short='c',default='8192',help='...'"`; ...`.  Each struct field has a `kong:"..."` STRUCT-TAG with KEY=VALUE pairs inside the string.  Tag KEYS: `arg` (positional), `required`, `cmd` (subcommand), `name='X'`, `short='X'`, `default='V'`, `help='...'`, `type='T'`, `env='VAR'`, `hidden`, `enum='a,b,c'`.  Distinct from R275 clap-derive `#[arg(short = 'a', long = "alpha", default_value = "1")]` (attribute proc-macro), R322 JCommander `@Parameter(names = arrayOf("-a", "--alpha"), defaultValueExpression = "1")` (annotation).  kong's approach: GO STRUCT-TAG with KEY=VALUE pairs INSIDE the `kong:"..."` tag — Go STRUCT-TAGS are arbitrary STRINGS, kong PARSES them to extract directives using native Go reflection (`reflect.StructTag`).  Corpus's **first documented kong-struct-tag-key-value CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: mtg uses **kong `type='existingfile'` built-in-type-constraint-string CLI pattern** at run.go:8 + access.go:14 + doctor.go:14 — `ConfigPath string `kong:"arg,required,type='existingfile',help='...',name='config-path'"`.  `type='existingfile'` declares that the value must be a path to an EXISTING FILE — kong VALIDATES at parse time (path resolution + existence check).  Sister types: `existingdir`, `path`, `uint`, `int`, `float`, `bool`, `string`, `time`, `regex`, `url`, `ip`, `hostport`, `bytes` (size with units).  Distinct from R275 clap-derive `value_parser = clap::value_parser!(PathBuf)` (parser closure with separate validator), R322 JCommander `validateWith = X.class` (separate validator class), R313 Idris 2 `Param.str` (no path validation).  kong's approach: BUILT-IN TYPE-CONSTRAINT STRINGS embedded in struct tag — corpus's **first documented built-in-type-constraint-string CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: mtg uses **kong method-on-subcommand-struct dispatch CLI pattern** at run.go:11 + simple_run.go:47+ — `func (s *SimpleRun) Run(cli *CLI, version string) error { ... }`.  Each subcommand struct has its own `Run(args ...interface{}) error` METHOD.  When the user invokes `mtg simple-run ...`, kong dispatches to the `(*SimpleRun).Run` method via reflection.  The arguments (`cli *CLI`, `version string`) are matched against the `ctx.Run(...)` call's args at main.go:36.  Distinct from R275 clap-derive (no method dispatch — caller matches on enum variant via `match cli.command { Command::Foo => foo_func(...), }`), R322 JCommander (no per-command handler — parsed struct accessed manually), R270 Haskell `execParser >>= \opts -> ...` (lambda handler).  kong's approach: METHOD-ON-SUBCOMMAND-STRUCT for dispatch via Go reflection — corpus's **first documented method-on-struct-for-dispatch CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: mtg uses **kong Go-built-in-type-detection CLI pattern** at simple_run.go:21-25 — `DOHIP net.IP `kong:"name='doh-ip',default='1.1.1.1'"`; Timeout time.Duration `kong:"name='timeout',default='10s'"`; Socks5Proxies []string `kong:"name='socks5-proxy',help='...'"`; AntiReplayCacheSize string `kong:"name='antireplay-cache-size',default='1MB',type='bytes'"`.  kong AUTO-DETECTS Go-built-in types: `net.IP` (parses to IPv4/IPv6 address), `time.Duration` (parses Go duration strings like `10s`/`1h30m`), `[]string` (REPEATABLE flag), `string`+`type='bytes'` (parses byte sizes like `1MB`/`4KB`).  Distinct from R275 clap-derive `#[arg(value_parser = clap::value_parser!(...))]` (explicit parser), R322 JCommander `converter = X.class` (custom converter class).  kong's approach: REFLECTION-DETECTED Go-built-in-types with OPTIONAL `type='...'` HINT — corpus's **first documented Go-built-in-type-detection CLI pattern**.
  - Hunt-skip notes: skipped `_cobra` / `_kingpin` / `_urfave-cli` / `_pflag` (would refer to Go CLI libraries from previous rounds, not CLIs), `_gum` (in corpus — Charm Bracelet's kong-based tool from 23758 stars; would have been a SISTER demo), `_dasel` (in corpus — TomWright's kong-based tool from 7961 stars), `_unison` (DUP with stock zsh).  Probed `qdrant/migration` (78 stars, Qdrant migration tool — narrow), `fujiwara/apprun-cli` (48 stars, Sakura AppRun CLI — narrow domain), `docker/docs releaser` (Docker docs releaser — internal tool), `hashicorp/go-extract` (HashiCorp extract tool — small CLI surface), `prskr/git-age-keyring-agent` (git-age keyring — small surface).  Chose mtg: 3469 GH stars (real production MTProxy used by Telegram users in censored regions), 5 subcommands (generate-secret/doctor/access/run/simple-run) + version flag, single 41-line CLI struct definition + per-subcommand structs (rich 13-flag SimpleRun struct) exercising EVERY MAJOR kong PATTERN (kong.Parse + ctx.Run context-based-dispatch, kong.Vars template variables, struct-field-with-cmd-tag-as-subcommand, struct-tag-key-value declarative parsing, built-in-type-constraint-strings, Go-built-in-type-detection, kong.VersionFlag special type, method-on-subcommand-struct Run dispatch via Go reflection), real binary distributed via Docker Hub and GitHub releases.
  - Blacklist update: `mtg` added.  Corpus count: 28,756 → 28,757 (+1).

- **R333 — decimo source-direct (arbitrary-precision CLI calculator in Mojo; forfudan/`argmojo` library)** (1 file new) — pivots from Crystal commander.cr (R332 mstrap) into the Mojo ecosystem to document the SECOND Mojo CLI pattern — corpus's **first documented argmojo library** + **second documented Mojo CLI pattern** (joining R321 _moty's handwritten `alias`-as-spec).  decimo is an arbitrary-precision CLI calculator powered by the Decimo Mojo library (BigDecimal).  Evaluates mathematical expressions with user-specified precision (up to 1 billion significant digits), supports scientific/engineering notation, custom rounding modes, and digit-group delimiters.  Authored by Yuhao Zhu (forfudan).  23 GH stars.  Sister tools: bc (Unix arbitrary-precision calculator, in corpus), dc (RPN calculator), python (with `decimal` module), GNU Octave (with arbitrary-precision via mpfr).  **MAJOR MILESTONE**: corpus's **first documented argmojo library** — adds forfudan/argmojo (PROTOCOL/TRAIT-BASED declarative struct API; typed spec wrappers `Positional[T, ...]`/`Option[T, ...]`/`Flag[...]`; trait-bracket-named-args parameterization; comma-string `choices=` validation; 3-flag `has_range/range_min/range_max` range validation; `group=` help sections; static-method-as-metadata-protocol; `cmd.mutually_exclusive(["a", "b"])` mutex; `cmd.allow_negative_expressions()`; `cmd.add_tip("...")` usage hints; `.value` UNIFORM ACCESSOR) to the cross-language CLI taxonomy (72 library variants now).  Mojo NOW HAS TWO documented CLI patterns (handwritten `alias`-as-spec + argmojo library) joining the multi-library club alongside Rust 7, Crystal 3, OCaml 3, Java 3.
  - `_decimo` (1-stem; forfudan/decimo src/cli/main.mojo; **Mojo `argmojo` library** — forfudan/argmojo on Mojo pixi; **10 spec fields (1 positional + 5 options + 4 flags) + Python-style `from argmojo import Parsable, Option, Flag, Positional, Command` import + `struct DecimoArgs(Parsable):` trait conformance + `Positional[T, ...]`/`Option[T, ...]`/`Flag[...]` TYPED SPEC WRAPPERS with TRAIT-BRACKET NAMED-ARGS parameterization (`help="..."`, `long="..."`, `short="..."`, `default="..."`, `value_name="..."`, `group="..."`, `required=False`, `has_range=True`, `range_min`/`range_max`, `choices="a,b,c"`) + COMMA-SEPARATED `choices="..."` validation + THREE-FLAG `has_range=True`/`range_min=N`/`range_max=N` range validation + `group="..."` help sections + `@staticmethod def description/version/name` static-method-as-metadata-protocol + `DecimoArgs.to_command()` struct-derived command + MUTABLE POST-SPEC CONFIG via method-chain (`cmd.usage`/`cmd.mutually_exclusive`/`cmd.allow_negative_expressions`/`cmd.add_tip`) + `DecimoArgs.parse_from_command(cmd^)` with `^` MOVE-OWNERSHIP + `.value` UNIFORM ACCESSOR on parsed wrappers**) decoded source-direct from:
    * `src/cli/main.mojo` line 16 (`from argmojo import Parsable, Option, Flag, Positional, Command` — Mojo Python-style IMPORT of argmojo primitives)
    * `src/cli/main.mojo` lines 34-104 (`struct DecimoArgs(Parsable): var expr: Positional[String, help="...", required=False]; var file: Option[String, long="file", short="F", help="...", default="", value_name="PATH", group="Input"]; var precision: Option[Int, ..., has_range=True, range_min=1, range_max=1_000_000_000, group="Computation"]; ...` — Mojo STRUCT conforming to Parsable TRAIT with TYPED SPEC WRAPPERS using TRAIT-BRACKET `[...]` NAMED-ARGS parameterization)
    * `src/cli/main.mojo` lines 78-87 (`var rounding_mode: Option[String, ..., choices="half-even,half-up,half-down,up,down,ceiling,floor", ...]` — CHOICES VALIDATION via COMMA-SEPARATED STRING)
    * `src/cli/main.mojo` lines 49-58 (`var precision: Option[Int, ..., has_range=True, range_min=1, range_max=1_000_000_000]` — RANGE VALIDATION via 3-flag pattern; Mojo integer literals support `_` digit separators)
    * `src/cli/main.mojo` lines 106-117 (`@staticmethod def description() -> String: return "..."; @staticmethod def version() -> String: return DECIMO_VERSION_TAG; @staticmethod def name() -> String: return "decimo"` — Parsable trait requires THREE STATIC METHODS for metadata)
    * `src/cli/main.mojo` lines 131-141 (`var cmd = DecimoArgs.to_command(); cmd.usage("decimo [OPTIONS] [EXPR]"); cmd.mutually_exclusive(["scientific", "engineering"]); cmd.allow_negative_expressions(); cmd.add_tip(...); var args = DecimoArgs.parse_from_command(cmd^)` — STRUCT-DERIVED-COMMAND with MUTABLE POST-SPEC CONFIG via method-chain, then PARSE with `^` move-ownership operator)
    * `src/cli/main.mojo` line 134 (`cmd.mutually_exclusive(["scientific", "engineering"])` — DECLARATIVE MUTEX via LIST-OF-STRINGS PASSED TO method)
    * `src/cli/main.mojo` line 145 (`var precision = args.precision.value` — `.value` UNIFORM ACCESSOR on parsed Positional/Option/Flag wrappers)
  - Critical extraction note: decimo uses **Mojo `argmojo` library** at main.mojo:16 — corpus's **first documented argmojo library** + **second documented Mojo CLI pattern** (joining R321 _moty's handwritten `alias`-as-spec).  argmojo is forfudan/argmojo (also by Yuhao Zhu), a feature-rich CLI parser library for Mojo with BOTH builder and struct-based declarative APIs.  Distinct from R321 _moty's handwritten approach (no formal library, just `alias` compile-time lists with parametric `collect_args[POSITIONAL, ARGUMENTS, FLAGS]()` function).  argmojo provides: PROTOCOL/TRAIT-BASED declarative struct API (`struct Args(Parsable):`), TYPED spec wrappers (`Positional[T, ...]` / `Option[T, ...]` / `Flag[...]`), TRAIT-BRACKET NAMED-ARGS parameterization (KEY=VALUE inside `[...]`), CHOICES validation via comma-string, RANGE validation via `has_range`+`range_min`+`range_max` 3-flag combo, GROUP help sections, MUTEX declaration via `cmd.mutually_exclusive(["a", "b"])` method, ALLOW-NEGATIVE-AS-POSITIONAL via `cmd.allow_negative_expressions()`, ADD-TIP usage hints via `cmd.add_tip("...")`, `.value` UNIFORM ACCESSOR on parsed wrappers.  Extends cross-language CLI taxonomy to SEVENTY-TWO library variants: Rust (7), Go (4), Python (3), Crystal 3, Haskell (R270), OCaml 3, C (R272), Java 3, Kotlin (R277), Scala (R278), Dart (R279), Ruby (R280), Perl (R281), PHP (R282), Swift (R283), F# (R284), Lua (R285), C# (R286), Nim 2, Zig (R288), D (R289), Racket (R290), Common Lisp 2, Erlang 2, Tcl (R293), Pharo Smalltalk (R294), Julia (R296), SWI-Prolog optparse (R297), Pony stdlib cli (R298), V stdlib `flag` (R300), Clojure 2, Gleam Glint (R303), Ada GNAT.Command_Line (R304), C++ 2, Elixir Optimus (R306), Vala GLib.OptionContext (R307), Hare stdlib getopt (R308), Standard ML stdlib GetOpt (R309), Fortran M_CLI2 (R310), Guile (ice-9 getopt-long) (R311), Free Pascal 2, Idris 2 System.GetOpts (R313), Mercury stdlib getopt_io (R314), Eiffel Gobo AP_PARSER (R315), Forth/Gforth `next-arg` (R317), Roc weaver (R319), Chapel `config` keyword (R320), **Mojo 1 handwritten `alias`-as-spec (R321)**, Chicken Scheme `args` egg (R324), Lean 4 Cli (R326), Bigloo Scheme `args-parse` macro (R327), Rust 7 bpaf (R330), Crystal 3 commander.cr (R332), **Mojo 2 argmojo library** (R333 — NEW).  **MAJOR PATTERN MILESTONE**: 72 cross-language CLI library variants documented.  Mojo NOW HAS TWO documented CLI patterns (handwritten `alias`-as-spec + argmojo library) joining the multi-library club.
  - Critical extraction note: decimo uses **Mojo argmojo trait-bracket-named-args-parameterization CLI pattern** at main.mojo:34-104 — `var file: Option[String, long="file", short="F", help="...", default="", value_name="PATH", group="Input"]`.  Mojo's `[...]` TRAIT BRACKET syntax accepts COMPILE-TIME-VALUE keyword args (KEY=VALUE pairs).  Each field is TYPED with `Positional[T, ...]` / `Option[T, ...]` / `Flag[...]` where named args INSIDE the trait bracket configure the spec.  Distinct from R321 Mojo `alias POSITIONAL = [StaticString("path")]` (compile-time lists, no per-spec metadata), R322 JCommander `@Parameter(names = arrayOf("-X", "--long"))` Java annotation (loose attribute syntax), R313 Idris 2 `record Options where constructor MkOptions` (record types).  argmojo's approach: TRAIT-BRACKET NAMED-ARGS PARAMETERIZATION — corpus's **first documented trait-bracket-named-args-parameterization CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: decimo uses **Mojo argmojo comma-string-in-trait-bracket-choices CLI pattern** at main.mojo:84 — `choices="half-even,half-up,half-down,up,down,ceiling,floor"`.  CHOICES VALIDATION via COMMA-SEPARATED STRING in the `choices="..."` trait-bracket parameter.  Parser REJECTS values not in the choice list.  Distinct from R322 JCommander `validateWith = X.class` (separate validator class), R325 OCaml stdlib Arg (no built-in choices), R314 Mercury `option_data` typed-union, R311 Guile `(value #t)` no-choice.  argmojo's approach: COMMA-SEPARATED-STRING-IN-trait-bracket CHOICES validation — corpus's **first documented comma-string-in-trait-bracket-choices CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: decimo uses **Mojo argmojo three-flag-range-validation CLI pattern** at main.mojo:49-58 — `has_range=True, range_min=1, range_max=1_000_000_000`.  RANGE VALIDATION via 3-flag pattern: `has_range=True` enables the check, `range_min=N` sets lower bound, `range_max=N` sets upper bound.  Mojo's integer literals support `_` digit separators.  Distinct from R305 C++ CLI11 `->check(CLI::Range(15, 25))` (validator object), R322 JCommander `validateWith` (separate class), R314 Mercury `option_data int_value` (no built-in range).  argmojo's approach: `has_range`+`range_min`+`range_max` THREE-FLAG combo for range validation — corpus's **first documented three-flag-range-validation CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: decimo uses **Mojo argmojo struct-derived-command with mutable post-spec config CLI pattern** at main.mojo:131-141 — `var cmd = DecimoArgs.to_command(); cmd.usage("decimo [OPTIONS] [EXPR]"); cmd.mutually_exclusive(["scientific", "engineering"]); cmd.allow_negative_expressions(); cmd.add_tip(...); ...; var args = DecimoArgs.parse_from_command(cmd^)`.  `DecimoArgs.to_command()` derives the Command from the Parsable struct, then IMPERATIVE method-chain configures it post-derivation (`cmd.usage`, `cmd.mutually_exclusive`, `cmd.allow_negative_expressions`, `cmd.add_tip`).  Distinct from R322 JCommander `parse(*argv)` (no post-spec config), R331 Jane Street `Command.basic` immutable, R321 Mojo `collect_args[POSITIONAL, ARGUMENTS, FLAGS]()` no post-config.  argmojo's approach: STRUCT-DERIVED-COMMAND with MUTABLE POST-SPEC CONFIG via method-chain — corpus's **first documented struct-derived-command-with-mutable-post-spec CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: decimo uses **Mojo argmojo mutually-exclusive-string-list CLI pattern** at main.mojo:134 — `cmd.mutually_exclusive(["scientific", "engineering"])`.  DECLARATIVE MUTEX between two named flags.  When user passes BOTH `-S/--scientific` AND `-E/--engineering`, parser EMITS ERROR.  Sister to R315 Eiffel Gobo `AP_ALTERNATIVE_OPTIONS_LIST` (first-class mutex structure), R323 Java args4j `forbids = { "-otherFlag" }` (per-flag forbids).  argmojo's approach: LIST-OF-STRINGS PASSED TO `mutually_exclusive` method post-spec — corpus's **first documented mutually-exclusive-string-list CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: decimo uses **Mojo argmojo `.value` unified accessor CLI pattern** at main.mojo:145+ — `var precision = args.precision.value`, `args.scientific.value`, `args.file.value` etc.  Each spec type (Positional/Option/Flag) EXPOSES the parsed value via `.value` UNIFORM ACCESSOR.  Distinct from R321 Mojo `argvs[idx]` (raw indexed access), R322 JCommander direct field access via reflection, R325 OCaml `!show_version` ref-deref.  argmojo's approach: `.value` UNIFORM ACCESSOR on spec wrappers — corpus's **first documented unified-`.value`-accessor CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: decimo uses **Mojo argmojo static-method-as-metadata-protocol CLI pattern** at main.mojo:106-117 — `@staticmethod def description() -> String: return "..."; @staticmethod def version() -> String: return DECIMO_VERSION_TAG; @staticmethod def name() -> String: return "decimo"`.  Parsable trait REQUIRES these THREE STATIC METHODS for command metadata.  Distinct from R321 Mojo `alias` (no metadata methods), R322 JCommander `@Parameters(commandDescription = ...)` annotation, R331 Jane Street Core.Command `~summary` labelled-arg.  argmojo's approach: STATIC-METHOD-AS-METADATA-PROTOCOL — corpus's **first documented static-method-as-metadata-protocol CLI pattern**.
  - Hunt-skip notes: skipped `_mojo` (Mojo interpreter/compiler), `_moty` (R321 — Mojo 1st CLI pattern, handwritten `alias`-as-spec), `_unison` (DUP with stock zsh), `_argmojo` (would refer to the library, not a CLI), `_mgit` (in corpus — different mgit), `_mgrep` (in corpus too as different tool).  Probed `forfudan/argmojo examples/mgrep.mojo` (argmojo example demo — but mgrep would collide with the in-corpus mgrep tool), `forfudan/argmojo examples/mgit.mojo` (same collision issue with mgit), `forfudan/decimo` (CHOSEN — 23 GH stars, arbitrary-precision calculator with cleanest single-file argmojo struct-based declarative demo).  Chose decimo: 23 GH stars (real Mojo math library + CLI tool), 10 spec fields (1 positional + 5 options + 4 flags) in single 200-line main.mojo exercising EVERY MAJOR argmojo PATTERN (Python-style `from argmojo import Parsable, Option, Flag, Positional, Command`, `struct DecimoArgs(Parsable)` trait conformance, `Positional[T, ...]` / `Option[T, ...]` / `Flag[...]` trait-bracket-named-args parameterization, COMMA-SEPARATED `choices="..."` validation, THREE-FLAG `has_range=True`/`range_min`/`range_max` range validation, `group="..."` help sections, `@staticmethod def description/version/name` static-method-as-metadata-protocol, `DecimoArgs.to_command()` struct-derived command, mutable post-spec config via `cmd.usage`/`cmd.mutually_exclusive`/`cmd.allow_negative_expressions`/`cmd.add_tip`, `DecimoArgs.parse_from_command(cmd^)` with `^` move-ownership, `.value` UNIFORM ACCESSOR on parsed wrappers), real Mojo binary distributed via pixi (`./decimo "100 * 12 - 23/17"`).
  - Blacklist update: `decimo` added.  Corpus count: 28,755 → 28,756 (+1).

- **R332 — mstrap source-direct (machine bootstrapping tool in Crystal; `commander.cr` library)** (1 file new) — pivots from OCaml Jane Street Core.Command (R331 zbg) into the Crystal ecosystem to document the THIRD Crystal CLI library — corpus's **first documented commander.cr library** + **third documented Crystal CLI library** (joining R269 Crystal stdlib OptionParser + R299 Crystal Admiral).  mstrap is a machine bootstrapping tool with a focus on SENSIBLE DEFAULTS, CONVENTIONS, and AVOIDANCE OF VENDORING.  Sets up new development machines via composable Step plugins (homebrew, asdf, nix, etc.).  Authored by Max Fierke (maxfierke).  24 GH stars (real production tool used at multiple companies).  Sister tools: thoughtbot/laptop (BASH-based alternative), chef-bk-prep (Chef-based), strap (older Bash predecessor), boxen.  **MAJOR MILESTONE**: corpus's **first documented commander.cr library** — adds mrrooijen/commander.cr (Ruby Thor-inspired CLI builder for Crystal; block-builder pattern with `Commander::Command.new do |cmd| ... end`; block-builder per flag with FIELD ASSIGNMENTS; `flag.persistent = true` for subcommand inheritance; dynamic subcommand registration via `.each do ... end` loops; handler blocks via `cmd.run do |options, arguments| ... end`) to the cross-language CLI taxonomy (71 library variants now).  Crystal NOW HAS THREE documented CLI libraries (stdlib OptionParser + Admiral + commander.cr) joining the multi-library club alongside Rust 7, OCaml 3, Java 3.
  - `_mstrap` (1-stem; maxfierke/mstrap src/mstrap/cli.cr; **Crystal `commander.cr` library** — mrrooijen/commander on Crystal shards.info; **8 global flags + 6 dynamic Step subcommands (init/dependencies/shell/services/projects/runtimes) + 1 static `project CNAME` subcommand with 6 flags + block-builder `Commander::Command.new do |cmd| ... end` + `cmd.use = "NAME"` usage string + `cmd.long = HEREDOC` long description + `cmd.flags.add do |flag| ... end` block-builder per flag with FIELD ASSIGNMENTS (`flag.name`/`flag.short`/`flag.long`/`flag.default`/`flag.description`/`flag.persistent`) + `cmd.commands.add do |subcmd| ... end` block-builder per subcommand + DYNAMIC subcommand registration via `.each do ... end` loop + `step.setup_cmd!(step_cmd)` plugin-extension pattern + `step_cmd.run do |options, arguments| ... end` HANDLER BLOCK with two args**) decoded source-direct from:
    * `src/mstrap/cli.cr` line 32 (`@cli = Commander::Command.new do |cmd| cmd.use = "mstrap"; cmd.long = HEREDOC; cmd.flags.add do |flag| ... end; cmd.commands.add do |subcmd| ... end end` — `Commander::Command.new BLOCK` BUILDER pattern; block YIELDS the command object for configuration)
    * `src/mstrap/cli.cr` lines 34-44 (`cmd.use = "mstrap"; cmd.long = <<-DESC mstrap is a tool for bootstrapping development machines ...DESC` — Crystal HEREDOC syntax for multi-line strings with `#{interpolation}`)
    * `src/mstrap/cli.cr` lines 46-54 (`cmd.flags.add do |flag| flag.name = "config_path"; flag.short = "-c"; flag.long = "--config"; flag.default = MStrap::Paths::CONFIG_HCL; flag.description = "..."; flag.persistent = true end` — `cmd.flags.add BLOCK` adds a flag spec via FIELD ASSIGNMENTS)
    * `src/mstrap/cli.cr` line 52 (`flag.persistent = true` — PERSISTENT FLAG attribute; flag inherited by all subcommands of this command, sister to Cobra's `PersistentFlags()` concept)
    * `src/mstrap/cli.cr` lines 110-127 (`Step.all.each do |key, step| cmd.commands.add do |step_cmd| step_cmd.use = key.to_s; step_cmd.short = step.description; step.setup_cmd!(step_cmd); step_cmd.run do |options, arguments| ... end end end` — DYNAMIC SUBCOMMAND GENERATION from iteration over `Step.all` plugin registry)
    * `src/mstrap/cli.cr` line 118 (`step_cmd.run do |options, arguments| ... end` — HANDLER BLOCK with two args; called when this subcommand matches)
    * `src/mstrap/cli.cr` lines 130-188 (`cmd.commands.add do |project_cmd| project_cmd.use = "project CNAME"; ... project_cmd.flags.add do |flag| flag.name = "cname"; flag.long = "--cname"; ... end ... end` — STATIC SUBCOMMAND `project CNAME` with 6 flags)
  - Critical extraction note: mstrap uses **Crystal `commander.cr` library** at cli.cr:32 — corpus's **first documented commander.cr library** + **third documented Crystal CLI library** (joining R269 Crystal stdlib OptionParser + R299 Crystal Admiral).  commander.cr is mrrooijen/commander, the Ruby Thor-inspired CLI builder for Crystal — 130 GH stars, MIT license, distributed via Crystal's `shards` package manager.  Distinct from R269 Crystal stdlib OptionParser (`parser.on(...)` method-chain, no subcommands), R299 Crystal Admiral (CLASS-INHERITANCE-WITH-DSL via `class < Admiral::Command` + `define_flag` macros).  commander.cr's distinguishing features: BLOCK-BUILDER PATTERN (`Commander::Command.new do |cmd| ... end`), block-builder per flag with FIELD ASSIGNMENTS (`flag.short`, `flag.long`, `flag.persistent`), DYNAMIC subcommand registration via `.each do ... end` loops, HANDLER BLOCKS via `cmd.run do |options, arguments| ... end`.  Extends cross-language CLI taxonomy to SEVENTY-ONE library variants: Rust (7), Go (4), Python (3), **Crystal 1 OptionParser (R269)**, Haskell (R270), OCaml 1 Cmdliner (R271), C (R272), Java 1 picocli (R276), Kotlin (R277), Scala (R278), Dart (R279), Ruby (R280), Perl (R281), PHP (R282), Swift (R283), F# (R284), Lua (R285), C# (R286), Nim 1 parseopt (R287), Zig (R288), D (R289), Racket (R290), Common Lisp 1 (R291), Erlang 1 getopt (R292), Tcl (R293), Pharo Smalltalk (R294), Common Lisp 2 clingon (R295), Julia (R296), SWI-Prolog optparse (R297), Pony stdlib cli (R298), **Crystal 2 Admiral (R299)**, V stdlib `flag` (R300), Nim 2 cligen (R301), Clojure 1 babashka.cli (R302), Gleam Glint (R303), Ada GNAT.Command_Line (R304), C++ 1 CLI11 (R305), Elixir Optimus (R306), Vala GLib.OptionContext (R307), Hare stdlib getopt (R308), Standard ML stdlib GetOpt (R309), Fortran M_CLI2 (R310), Guile (ice-9 getopt-long) (R311), Free Pascal 1 FCL TCustomApplication (R312), Idris 2 System.GetOpts (R313), Mercury stdlib getopt_io (R314), Eiffel Gobo AP_PARSER (R315), C++ 2 boost::program_options (R316), Forth/Gforth `next-arg` (R317), Clojure 2 org.clojure/tools.cli (R318), Roc weaver (R319), Chapel `config` keyword (R320), Mojo `alias`-as-spec (R321), Java 2 JCommander (R322), Java 3 args4j (R323), Chicken Scheme `args` egg (R324), OCaml 2 stdlib Arg (R325), Lean 4 Cli (R326), Bigloo Scheme `args-parse` macro (R327), Erlang 2 stdlib argparse (R328), Free Pascal 2 stdlib getopts (R329), Rust 7 bpaf (R330), OCaml 3 Jane Street Core.Command (R331), **Crystal 3 commander.cr** (R332 — NEW).  **MAJOR PATTERN MILESTONE**: 71 cross-language CLI library variants documented.  Crystal NOW HAS THREE documented CLI libraries (stdlib OptionParser + Admiral + commander.cr) joining the multi-library club alongside Rust 7, OCaml 3, Java 3.
  - Critical extraction note: mstrap uses **Crystal commander.cr block-builder-with-yield CLI pattern** at cli.cr:32 — `Commander::Command.new do |cmd| ... end`.  The block YIELDS the command object for configuration.  Crystal block syntax `do |cmd| ... end` (similar to Ruby).  Distinct from R322 JCommander field annotation, R270 Haskell applicative `<$> <*>`, R269 Crystal stdlib OptionParser `parser.on(...)` method-chain.  commander.cr's approach: RUBY-THOR-INSPIRED BLOCK BUILDER — corpus's **first documented block-builder-with-yield CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: mstrap uses **Crystal commander.cr block-builder-per-flag-with-field-assignments CLI pattern** at cli.cr:46-109 — `cmd.flags.add do |flag| flag.name = "config_path"; flag.short = "-c"; flag.long = "--config"; flag.default = ...; flag.description = ...; flag.persistent = true end`.  Each flag spec is a BLOCK with FIELD ASSIGNMENTS.  Distinct from R269 Crystal stdlib OptionParser (`parser.on("-c", ...)` method-chain), R299 Crystal Admiral (CLASS-INHERITANCE-WITH-DSL `define_flag` macro declarations), R322 JCommander `@Parameter(names = ...)` annotation.  commander.cr's approach: BLOCK-BUILDER PER FLAG with FIELD ASSIGNMENTS — corpus's **first documented block-builder-per-flag-with-field-assignments CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: mstrap uses **Crystal commander.cr `flag.persistent = true` attribute for inheritance** at cli.cr:52, 60, 76, 92, 101, 108 — `flag.persistent = true` marks the flag as INHERITED by all subcommands of this command.  Sister to Cobra's `PersistentFlags()` concept.  Distinct from R322 JCommander (no equivalent — subcommands declare own flags), R325 OCaml stdlib Arg (no subcommand concept), R331 Jane Street Core.Command (each subcommand declares own flags), R269 Crystal stdlib OptionParser (no subcommand support).  commander.cr's approach: `persistent = true` BOOLEAN flag attribute for inheritance — corpus's **first documented persistent-flag-attribute CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: mstrap uses **Crystal commander.cr dynamic-subcommand-registration-via-loop CLI pattern** at cli.cr:110-127 — `Step.all.each do |key, step| cmd.commands.add do |step_cmd| step_cmd.use = key.to_s; ...; step.setup_cmd!(step_cmd); step_cmd.run do |options, arguments| ... end end end`.  DYNAMIC SUBCOMMAND GENERATION from iteration over `Step.all` (where each Step is a registered plugin).  Each iteration adds one subcommand via `cmd.commands.add BLOCK`.  `step.setup_cmd!(step_cmd)` lets each Step plugin add its own flags.  Distinct from R322 JCommander (no dynamic command registration), R331 Jane Street Core.Command (functional `Command.group` with static list), R270 Haskell (no dynamic — all applicative).  commander.cr's approach: IMPERATIVE DYNAMIC subcommand registration via `.each do ... end` LOOP — corpus's **first documented dynamic-subcommand-registration-via-loop CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: mstrap uses **Crystal commander.cr handler-block-with-options-and-arguments CLI pattern** at cli.cr:118-126 — `step_cmd.run do |options, arguments| validate_step!(key); cli_options = load_cli_options!(options); config = load_configuration!; run_step!(key, config, cli_options, args: arguments); ... end`.  Each subcommand has a HANDLER BLOCK `.run do |options, arguments| ... end` called when this subcommand matches.  `options` is a hash-like accessor for parsed flag values; `arguments` is the remaining positional args.  Crystal `do |options, arguments| ... end` block-with-args (Ruby-like).  Distinct from R322 JCommander (no per-command handler), R331 Jane Street Core.Command (`let%map_open.Command ... in ACTION` PPX), R325 OCaml stdlib Arg (no handler — caller dispatches).  commander.cr's approach: BLOCK-WITH-TWO-ARGS `.run do |options, arguments| ... end` HANDLER — corpus's **first documented block-with-options-and-arguments-handler CLI pattern**.
  - Hunt-skip notes: skipped `_crystal` (Crystal compiler, in corpus + blacklisted), `_shards` (Crystal package manager, in corpus + blacklisted), `_commander` (would refer to commander.cr library, not a CLI), `_unison` (DUP with stock zsh).  Pivot detour: probed Carbon (`carbon-language/carbon-lang`) but `_carbon` is already in corpus from 2026-05-29 (uses `Carbon::CommandLine` internal library; documented as tool but library-as-taxonomy-entry would be a separate concern).  Pivot detour: probed Lua `lapp` (Penlight) — `ByteSumoLtd/TrendCalculus-lua` had clean spec but is `lua script.lua`-invoked, no standalone binary.  Probed `homonoidian/ven` (6 stars, Ven language interpreter — small surface), `creadone/ngx` (1 star, nginx log parser — small CLI), `gergelyk/plany` (5 stars, YAML calendar — small), `statisticsnorway/statbus` (6 stars, statistical business register — spec spread), `garethr/snykctl` (11 stars, Snyk CLI — uses commander.cr but Snyk-API-focused, narrow domain), `mrrooijen/commander` (130 stars THE LIBRARY ITSELF, not a CLI).  Chose mstrap: 24 GH stars (real production machine-bootstrap tool used at multiple companies), 8 global flags + 6 dynamic Step subcommands + 1 static `project CNAME` subcommand with 6 flags, exercising EVERY MAJOR commander.cr PATTERN (`Commander::Command.new do |cmd| ... end` block-builder with yield, `cmd.use = "NAME"` usage string, `cmd.long = HEREDOC` long description, `cmd.flags.add do |flag| ... end` block-builder per flag with FIELD ASSIGNMENTS, `cmd.commands.add do |subcmd| ... end` block-builder per subcommand, DYNAMIC subcommand registration via `.each do ... end` loop over `Step.all`, `step.setup_cmd!(step_cmd)` plugin-extension pattern, `step_cmd.run do |options, arguments| ... end` HANDLER BLOCK with two args), real binary distributed via shards + Homebrew (`brew install mstrap`).
  - Blacklist update: `mstrap` added.  Corpus count: 28,754 → 28,755 (+1).

- **R331 — zbg source-direct ("Zero Bullshit Git" wrapper in OCaml by Dmitrii Kovanikov; Jane Street `Core.Command` library)** (1 file new) — pivots from Rust bpaf (R330 muvm) into the OCaml ecosystem to document the THIRD OCaml CLI library — corpus's **first documented Jane Street Core.Command library** + **third documented OCaml CLI library** (joining R271 OCaml Cmdliner + R325 OCaml stdlib Arg).  zbg is "Zero Bullshit Git" — an opinionated git wrapper providing a friendlier interface for common workflow operations.  Authored by Dmitrii Kovanikov (chshersh) — a well-known OCaml hacker (Patrol, Co-log, neon, github-tools).  331 GH stars.  Sister tools: gh (GitHub CLI, in corpus), lazygit (TUI git client, in corpus), git-extras (in corpus), tig (TUI git browser, in corpus).  **MAJOR MILESTONE**: 70 CROSS-LANGUAGE CLI LIBRARY VARIANTS documented — corpus has reached SEVENTY distinct CLI library variants.  OCaml NOW HAS THREE documented CLI libraries (Cmdliner + stdlib Arg + Core.Command) joining the multi-library club alongside Rust 7 and Java 3.  Adds Jane Street's flagship CLI parser (rich subcommand nesting via `Command.group`, applicative composition via `let%map_open.Command` PPX syntax extension, ANONYMOUS positional args via `anon (sequence/maybe/maybe_with_default ANON_SPEC)`, type-assignment operator `%:`, auto-completion generation for zsh/bash, auto-generated `--help`) to the cross-language CLI taxonomy.
  - `_zbg` (1-stem; chshersh/zbg lib/cli.ml; **OCaml Jane Street `Core.Command` library** — part of Jane Street's `core` library on opam; **15 subcommands + `open Core` namespace import + `Command.basic ~summary` leaf command + `let%map_open.Command` PPX SYNTAX EXTENSION for applicative composition + `flag "NAME" ~aliases:[...] ARG_SHAPE ~doc:"..."` with labelled-arg + arg-shape-function + `no_arg`/`string`/`int`/`bool`/`optional X`/`listed X` arg-shape functions + `anon ("name" %: TYPE)` anonymous positional with `%:` type-assignment operator + `maybe`/`maybe_with_default V`/`sequence`/`non_empty_sequence_as_list` positional multiplicity combinators + `Command.Param.return ACTION` no-arg command + `Command.group ~summary [(NAME, CMD); ...]` list-of-pairs subcommand dispatcher**) decoded source-direct from:
    * `lib/cli.ml` line 1 (`open Core` — opens Jane Street's `core` library namespace; Core is Jane Street's EXTENSIVE alternative-stdlib for OCaml)
    * `lib/cli.ml` lines 16-21 (`let cmd_clear = Command.basic ~summary:"..." (let%map_open.Command force = flag "f" ~aliases:[ "--force" ] no_arg ~doc:"..." in fun () -> Git.clear (to_force_flag force))` — CANONICAL Core.Command FORM with `Command.basic ~summary:"..." SPEC` constructor + `let%map_open.Command` PPX SYNTAX EXTENSION for applicative composition)
    * `lib/cli.ml` line 20 (`flag "f" ~aliases:[ "--force" ] no_arg ~doc:"..."` — FLAG SPEC with `"f"` primary name + `~aliases:LIST` labelled list of aliases + `no_arg` arg-shape function + `~doc:"..."` help text)
    * `lib/cli.ml` lines 24-27 (`let cmd_commit = Command.basic ~summary:"..." (let%map_open.Command message = anon (sequence ("message" %: string)) in fun () -> Git.commit message)` — ANONYMOUS positional arg via `anon (ANON_SPEC)`; `%:` is TYPE ASSIGNMENT OPERATOR)
    * `lib/cli.ml` lines 29-31 (`let cmd_done = Command.basic ~summary:"..." (Command.Param.return Git.done_)` — NO-ARG COMMAND using `Command.Param.return ACTION` shortcut)
    * `lib/cli.ml` line 35 (`anon (maybe_with_default "HEAD" ("commit" %: string))` — OPTIONAL POSITIONAL with default value)
    * `lib/cli.ml` line 60 (`anon (non_empty_sequence_as_list ("description" %: string))` — ONE-OR-MORE positional)
    * `lib/cli.ml` lines 99-114 (`let command = Command.group ~summary:"Easier git workflow" [("clear", cmd_clear); ("commit", cmd_commit); ("done", cmd_done); ("log", cmd_log); ("new", cmd_new); ("push", cmd_push); ("rebase", cmd_rebase); ("continue", cmd_continue); ("stash", cmd_stash); ("status", cmd_status); ("switch", cmd_switch); ("sync", cmd_sync); ("tag", cmd_tag); ("uncommit", cmd_uncommit); ("unstash", cmd_unstash);]` — `Command.group` creates a SUBCOMMAND DISPATCHER from a list of `(name, command)` 2-tuples)
  - Critical extraction note: zbg uses **OCaml Jane Street `Core.Command` library** at cli.ml:1 — corpus's **first documented Jane Street Core.Command library** + **third documented OCaml CLI library** (joining R271 OCaml Cmdliner + R325 OCaml stdlib Arg).  `Core.Command` is part of Jane Street's `core` library on opam — the company's extensive alternative-stdlib for OCaml.  Distinct from R271 Cmdliner (third-party library by Daniel Bünzli, applicative-style with auto-generated man pages), R325 OCaml stdlib Arg (mutable ref-cell binding, low-level).  Jane Street Core.Command is the COMPANY-INTERNAL OCaml CLI parser used pervasively at jane.street and the wider opam ecosystem.
