Features: rich subcommand nesting via `Command.group`, applicative composition via `let%map_open.Command` PPX syntax extension, ANONYMOUS positional args via `anon (sequence/maybe/maybe_with_default ANON_SPEC)`, type-assignment operator `%:`, auto-completion generation (zsh/bash), auto-generated `--help` output.  Extends cross-language CLI taxonomy to SEVENTY library variants: Rust (7), Go (4), Python (3), Crystal 1 OptionParser (R269), Haskell (R270), **OCaml 1 Cmdliner (R271)**, C (R272), Java 1 picocli (R276), Kotlin (R277), Scala (R278), Dart (R279), Ruby (R280), Perl (R281), PHP (R282), Swift (R283), F# (R284), Lua (R285), C# (R286), Nim 1 parseopt (R287), Zig (R288), D (R289), Racket (R290), Common Lisp 1 (R291), Erlang 1 getopt (R292), Tcl (R293), Pharo Smalltalk (R294), Common Lisp 2 clingon (R295), Julia (R296), SWI-Prolog optparse (R297), Pony stdlib cli (R298), Crystal 2 Admiral (R299), V stdlib `flag` (R300), Nim 2 cligen (R301), Clojure 1 babashka.cli (R302), Gleam Glint (R303), Ada GNAT.Command_Line (R304), C++ 1 CLI11 (R305), Elixir Optimus (R306), Vala GLib.OptionContext (R307), Hare stdlib getopt (R308), Standard ML stdlib GetOpt (R309), Fortran M_CLI2 (R310), Guile (ice-9 getopt-long) (R311), Free Pascal 1 FCL TCustomApplication (R312), Idris 2 System.GetOpts (R313), Mercury stdlib getopt_io (R314), Eiffel Gobo AP_PARSER (R315), C++ 2 boost::program_options (R316), Forth/Gforth `next-arg` (R317), Clojure 2 org.clojure/tools.cli (R318), Roc weaver (R319), Chapel `config` keyword (R320), Mojo `alias`-as-spec (R321), Java 2 JCommander (R322), Java 3 args4j (R323), Chicken Scheme `args` egg (R324), **OCaml 2 stdlib Arg (R325)**, Lean 4 Cli (R326), Bigloo Scheme `args-parse` macro (R327), Erlang 2 stdlib argparse (R328), Free Pascal 2 stdlib getopts (R329), Rust 7 bpaf (R330), **OCaml 3 Jane Street Core.Command** (R331 — NEW).  **MAJOR PATTERN MILESTONE**: 70 CROSS-LANGUAGE CLI LIBRARY VARIANTS documented.  OCaml NOW HAS THREE documented CLI libraries (Cmdliner + stdlib Arg + Core.Command) joining the multi-library club.
  - Critical extraction note: zbg uses **OCaml Jane Street `let%map_open.Command` PPX syntax extension CLI pattern** at cli.ml:16-21+ — `(let%map_open.Command force = flag "f" ~aliases:[ "--force" ] no_arg ~doc:"..." in fun () -> Git.clear (to_force_flag force))`.  `let%map_open` is a PPX SYNTAX EXTENSION (provided by `ppx_let` package) that desugars to APPLICATIVE composition: each `let%map_open.Command X = SPEC` clause binds `X` to the parsed value (extracted applicatively from the SPEC).  Multiple `and X2 = SPEC2` clauses can extend the binding.  Distinct from R270 Haskell optparse-applicative (`<$>` and `<*>` applicative ops explicitly), R322 JCommander annotation-based, R325 OCaml stdlib Arg (mutable ref-cell binding).  Jane Street's approach: PPX SYNTAX EXTENSION for applicative composition — corpus's **first documented let-map-open-PPX-applicative CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: zbg uses **OCaml Jane Street `flag "NAME" ~aliases:[LIST] ARG_SHAPE ~doc:"..."` flag-function CLI pattern** at cli.ml:18+ — `flag "f" ~aliases:["--force"] no_arg ~doc:"..."`.  `flag` is a function returning `Param.t<T>` where T is determined by the ARG-SHAPE FUNCTION.  Arg shapes: `no_arg` (boolean), `string`/`int`/`bool` (typed value), `optional X` (zero-or-one), `listed X` (zero-or-more), `escape` (collect remaining argv).  `~aliases`, `~doc`, `~full_flag_required`, etc. are OCaml LABELLED ARGUMENTS (keyword args).  Distinct from R325 OCaml stdlib `Arg.Set ref_bool` (spec variants), R322 JCommander `@Parameter(names = arrayOf(...))` annotation, R311 Guile `(NAME (single-char #\X) (value #t/#f))` quoted alist.  Jane Street's approach: `flag NAME ~aliases:LIST ARG_SHAPE_FUNC ~doc:"..."` with LABELLED ARGS — corpus's **first documented flag-function-with-arg-shape-function CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: zbg uses **OCaml Jane Street `anon ("NAME" %: TYPE)` `%:` type-assignment-operator CLI pattern** at cli.ml:26+ — `anon (sequence ("message" %: string))`, `anon (maybe_with_default "HEAD" ("commit" %: string))`, `anon ("tag_name" %: string)`.  `%:` is the TYPE ASSIGNMENT OPERATOR binding the positional NAME to its TYPE CONVERTER.  Builtin converters: `string`, `int`, `bool`, `float`, `file` (file path with completion), `date` (Date.t), `time` (Time.t), `host_and_port` (Host_and_port.t).  Custom converters via `Command.Arg_type.create FN`.  Distinct from R325 OCaml stdlib Arg (anon-fun callback for positionals — no type associated), R270 Haskell `argument str (metavar "FILE")`, R272 C POSIX (argv[i] post-parse).  Jane Street's approach: `%:` TYPE-ASSIGNMENT OPERATOR coupling NAME and TYPE in one expression — corpus's **first documented `%:`-type-assignment-operator CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: zbg uses **OCaml Jane Street anonymous-positional combinators `maybe`/`maybe_with_default`/`sequence`/`non_empty_sequence_as_list` CLI pattern** at cli.ml:35, 41, 60, 75 — `anon (maybe_with_default "HEAD" ("commit" %: string))`, `anon (non_empty_sequence_as_list ("description" %: string))`, `anon (sequence ("message" %: string))`, `anon (maybe ("branch" %: string))`.  These are positional MULTIPLICITY COMBINATORS: `maybe` (zero or one — Option), `maybe_with_default V` (zero or one with default V), `sequence` (zero or more — list), `non_empty_sequence_as_list` (one or more — list), `escape` (collect ALL remaining argv as STRING LIST).  Distinct from R330 Rust bpaf `.optional()`/`.many()` (method-chain), R270 Haskell `optional`/`many` (applicative ops).  Jane Street's approach: ANONYMOUS-POSITIONAL COMBINATORS as FUNCTION CALLS — corpus's **first documented function-call-positional-multiplicity-combinator CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: zbg uses **OCaml Jane Street `Command.group ~summary:"..." [(NAME, CMD); ...]` list-of-pairs-subcommand CLI pattern** at cli.ml:99-114 — `Command.group ~summary:"Easier git workflow" [("clear", cmd_clear); ("commit", cmd_commit); ("done", cmd_done); ...]`.  Each command is itself a `Command.basic` or `Command.group` (recursive nesting).  The user dispatches via `zbg SUBCOMMAND ARGS`.  Distinct from R322 JCommander `commands => #{}` (Erlang map), R302 babashka.cli `[:cmds ["sub"]]` (vector tag), R325 OCaml stdlib Arg (no subcommand support — caller routes manually).  Jane Street's approach: `Command.group ~summary [(NAME, CMD); ...]` LIST-OF-PAIRS for subcommands — corpus's **first documented list-of-name-command-pairs subcommand CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: zbg uses **OCaml Jane Street `Command.Param.return ACTION` no-arg-command CLI pattern** at cli.ml:31, 65, 96, 97 — `let cmd_done = Command.basic ~summary:"..." (Command.Param.return Git.done_)`.  `Command.Param.return ACTION` is the IDENTITY applicative — produces a Param that always succeeds and returns ACTION without consuming any args.  Used for subcommands that take NO ARGUMENTS.  Distinct from R325 OCaml stdlib Arg (no direct equivalent — caller checks `Arg.parse` results), R270 Haskell `pure FUNC` applicative return.  Jane Street's approach: `Command.Param.return` AS IDENTITY APPLICATIVE — corpus's **first documented Param.return-no-arg-command CLI pattern**.
  - Hunt-skip notes: skipped `_ocaml` / `_opam` (in corpus + blacklisted), `_cmdliner` (would refer to the library), `_unison` (DUP with stock zsh).  Probed `MinaProtocol/mina` (2116 stars but mina is HUGE — many binaries, spec spread across modules; deferred for separate cluster round), `frenetic-lang/frenetic` (230 stars OpenFlow controller — uses Core.Command but spec is in multiple files), `janestreet/sexp` (50 stars, S-expression swiss-knife by Jane Street — would be canonical SISTER demo but binary `sexp` might collide with other utilities), `mit-plv/koika` (173 stars hardware DSL — binary is `cuttlec`, uses Core.Command but spec is domain-specific), `mukul-rathi/bolt` (605 stars but in corpus), `acieroid/wassail` (60 stars WebAssembly static analyzer — uses Core.Command but smaller surface).  Chose zbg: 331 GH stars (well-known OCaml tool by Dmitrii Kovanikov — chshersh has multiple popular OCaml projects: Patrol, Co-log, neon, github-tools), 15 subcommands in single 115-line cli.ml exercising EVERY MAJOR Jane Street Core.Command PATTERN (`open Core` namespace import, `Command.basic ~summary` leaf command, `let%map_open.Command` PPX SYNTAX EXTENSION for applicative composition, `flag "NAME" ~aliases:[...] ARG_SHAPE ~doc:"..."` flag with labelled args + arg-shape function, `no_arg`/`string`/`int`/`bool` arg-shape functions, `anon ("name" %: TYPE)` anonymous positional with `%:` type-assignment operator, `maybe`/`maybe_with_default V`/`sequence`/`non_empty_sequence_as_list` positional multiplicity combinators, `Command.Param.return ACTION` no-arg command, `Command.group ~summary [(NAME, CMD); ...]` list-of-pairs subcommand dispatcher), real OPAM-distributed binary used by OCaml developers for daily git workflow.
  - Blacklist update: `zbg` added.  Corpus count: 28,753 → 28,754 (+1).

- **R330 — muvm source-direct (Asahi Linux microVM runner in Rust; `bpaf` library — combinator-style applicative parser)** (1 file new) — pivots from Free Pascal stdlib getopts (R329 dw_gdb) into the Rust ecosystem to document the SEVENTH Rust CLI library — corpus's **first documented bpaf library** + **seventh documented Rust CLI library** (joining clap, clap-derive, structopt, argh, gumdrop, lexopt from previous rounds).  muvm is the Asahi Linux microVM runner — runs programs from your system in a microVM using libkrun.  Originally developed for the Asahi Linux project (Linux on Apple Silicon) to run x86_64 binaries via FEX/Box emulators inside a virtualized environment.  Authored by the AsahiLinux project.  878 GH stars.  Sister tools: libkrun (virtualization library it depends on), FEX (x86-on-arm64 emulator), Box86 (x86-on-arm32 emulator), passt (network proxy for unprivileged containers).  **MAJOR MILESTONE**: corpus's **first documented bpaf library** — adds pacak/bpaf (COMBINATOR-STYLE Rust CLI parser with APPLICATIVE-functor design inspired by R270 Haskell optparse-applicative; combinator primitives `long`/`short`/`positional`/`any` composing via method-chain; `.switch()`/`.optional()`/`.many()` multiplicity transformers; `.argument::<T>()` with FromStr-trait conversion; `.parse(closure)` inline custom parser; `construct!` macro with Rust field-init shorthand) to the cross-language CLI taxonomy (69 library variants now).  **Rust REMAINS THE LANGUAGE WITH MOST DOCUMENTED CLI LIBRARIES — now 7**: clap, clap-derive, structopt, argh, gumdrop, lexopt, bpaf.
  - `_muvm` (1-stem; AsahiLinux/muvm crates/muvm/src/cli_options.rs; **Rust `bpaf` library** — pacak/bpaf on crates.io; **16 named options + 2 positionals + combinator primitives `long`/`short`/`positional`/`any` composing via method-chain + `.switch()`/`.optional()`/`.many()` multiplicity transformers + `.argument::<T>(META)` with FromStr-trait conversion + `.parse(closure)` inline custom parser for comma-ranges + KEY=VALUE env + `positional(META)` and `any(META, FILTER)` combinator functions + `construct!(Options { field1, field2, ... })` macro with Rust field-init shorthand + `.to_options()` terminal conversion to `OptionParser<T>`**) decoded source-direct from:
    * `crates/muvm/src/cli_options.rs` line 5 (`use bpaf::{any, construct, long, positional, OptionParser, Parser};` — Rust IMPORT of bpaf's combinator primitives)
    * `crates/muvm/src/cli_options.rs` lines 36-55 (`pub struct Options { pub cpu_list: Vec<Range<u16>>, pub env: Vec<(String, Option<String>)>, pub mem: Option<MiB>, ... pub command: PathBuf, pub command_args: Vec<String>, }` — Rust STRUCT holding all parsed options; each field has TYPED REPRESENTATION (Vec<T>, Option<T>, bool, custom enums))
    * `crates/muvm/src/cli_options.rs` lines 57-78 (`let cpu_list = long("cpu-list").short('c').help("...").argument::<String>("CPU_LIST").parse(|s| {...}).many().map(...);` — DEEP COMBINATOR CHAIN building ONE option spec)
    * `crates/muvm/src/cli_options.rs` lines 18-34 (`#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Default, PartialEq)] pub enum GpuMode { #[default] Drm, Venus, Software, } impl std::str::FromStr for GpuMode { ... }` — Rust CUSTOM ENUM with FromStr impl provides bpaf with the type-conversion logic)
    * `crates/muvm/src/cli_options.rs` line 130 (`let merged_rootfs = long("merged-rootfs").short('m').help("...").switch();` — `.switch()` is the BOOLEAN-FLAG combinator)
    * `crates/muvm/src/cli_options.rs` line 147 (`let gpu_mode = long("gpu-mode").help("...").argument::<GpuMode>("drm|venus|software").optional();` — `.argument::<T>(META)` declares a TYPED ARGUMENT with placeholder; T must implement `FromStr`)
    * `crates/muvm/src/cli_options.rs` lines 192-194 (`let command = positional("COMMAND").help("the command you want to execute in the vm");` — `positional(META)` is the POSITIONAL-ARG combinator)
    * `crates/muvm/src/cli_options.rs` lines 195-198 (`let command_args = any::<String, _, _>("COMMAND_ARGS", |arg| { (!["--help", "-h"].contains(&&*arg)).then_some(arg) }).help("...").many();` — `any::<T, _, _>(META, FILTER)` is the WILDCARD COMBINATOR with caller-provided FILTER function)
    * `crates/muvm/src/cli_options.rs` lines 200-223 (`construct!(Options { cpu_list, env, mem, vram, passt_socket, passt_args, fex_images, merged_rootfs, interactive, tty, privileged, gpu_mode, publish_ports, emulator, init_commands, user_init_commands, command, command_args, }).to_options()` — `construct!` MACRO assembles individual parser values into SINGLE Parser<Options>; `.to_options()` converts to OptionParser<Options>)
  - Critical extraction note: muvm uses **Rust `bpaf` library** at cli_options.rs:5 — corpus's **first documented bpaf library** + **seventh documented Rust CLI library** (joining clap, clap-derive, structopt, argh, gumdrop, lexopt from previous rounds).  bpaf is pacak/bpaf, the COMBINATOR-STYLE Rust CLI parser with APPLICATIVE-functor design (inspired by R270 Haskell optparse-applicative).  Distinct from clap (declarative struct-based via derive macro), structopt (clap-derive predecessor), argh (Google, similar to clap-derive), gumdrop (lighter clap-derive alternative), lexopt (tokenizer-only, parse-it-yourself).  bpaf's distinguishing features: COMBINATOR primitives (`long`, `short`, `positional`, `any`) that COMPOSE via method-chain, `Parser` trait that makes every spec a typed parser, `.parse(closure)` for inline custom parsing, `.switch()` for booleans, `.optional()`/`.many()` for arg multiplicity, `construct!` MACRO using Rust field-init shorthand to assemble the final struct, FromStr-trait-based type conversion.  Extends cross-language CLI taxonomy to SIXTY-NINE library variants: **Rust (6→7)**, Go (4), Python (3), Crystal 1 OptionParser (R269), Haskell (R270), OCaml 1 Cmdliner (R271), C (R272), Java 1 picocli (R276), Kotlin (R277), Scala (R278), Dart (R279), Ruby (R280), Perl (R281), PHP (R282), Swift (R283), F# (R284), Lua (R285), C# (R286), Nim 1 parseopt (R287), Zig (R288), D (R289), Racket (R290), Common Lisp 1 (R291), Erlang 1 getopt (R292), Tcl (R293), Pharo Smalltalk (R294), Common Lisp 2 clingon (R295), Julia (R296), SWI-Prolog optparse (R297), Pony stdlib cli (R298), Crystal 2 Admiral (R299), V stdlib `flag` (R300), Nim 2 cligen (R301), Clojure 1 babashka.cli (R302), Gleam Glint (R303), Ada GNAT.Command_Line (R304), C++ 1 CLI11 (R305), Elixir Optimus (R306), Vala GLib.OptionContext (R307), Hare stdlib getopt (R308), Standard ML stdlib GetOpt (R309), Fortran M_CLI2 (R310), Guile (ice-9 getopt-long) (R311), Free Pascal 1 FCL TCustomApplication (R312), Idris 2 System.GetOpts (R313), Mercury stdlib getopt_io (R314), Eiffel Gobo AP_PARSER (R315), C++ 2 boost::program_options (R316), Forth/Gforth `next-arg` (R317), Clojure 2 org.clojure/tools.cli (R318), Roc weaver (R319), Chapel `config` keyword (R320), Mojo `alias`-as-spec (R321), Java 2 JCommander (R322), Java 3 args4j (R323), Chicken Scheme `args` egg (R324), OCaml 2 stdlib Arg (R325), Lean 4 Cli (R326), Bigloo Scheme `args-parse` macro (R327), Erlang 2 stdlib argparse (R328), Free Pascal 2 stdlib getopts (R329), **Rust 7 bpaf** (R330 — NEW).  **MAJOR PATTERN MILESTONE**: 69 cross-language CLI library variants documented.  Rust REMAINS THE LANGUAGE WITH MOST DOCUMENTED CLI LIBRARIES (now 7: clap, clap-derive, structopt, argh, gumdrop, lexopt, bpaf).
  - Critical extraction note: muvm uses **Rust bpaf combinator-primitives-composing-via-chain CLI pattern** at cli_options.rs:57+ — `long("cpu-list").short('c').help("...").argument::<String>("CPU_LIST").parse(|s| {...}).many().map(...)`.  Each combinator returns a NEW Parser that CHAINS with the previous via method-call dot syntax.  Sister to R303 Gleam Glint's `glint.add(at:, do:)` pipe-builder, R322 JCommander `@Parameter.names` (annotation-based, NOT combinator chain), R270 Haskell optparse-applicative (uses `<*>` applicative ops).  bpaf's approach: METHOD-CHAIN OF COMBINATOR PRIMITIVES per option — corpus's **first documented method-chain-combinators-per-option CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: muvm uses **bpaf `.switch()`/`.optional()`/`.many()` multiplicity combinator CLI pattern** at cli_options.rs:130, 144, 156 — `.switch()` for boolean flags, `.optional()` for `Option<T>` (zero or one), `.many()` for `Vec<T>` (zero or more).  These are TERMINAL combinators that CONVERT the Parser's output type.  Distinct from R270 Haskell optparse-applicative `optional`/`many` (similar but separate functions not methods), R313 Idris 2 `NoArg`/`OptArg`/`ReqArg` constructor variants, R325 OCaml `Arg.Set ref` (set-and-go).  bpaf's approach: METHOD-CHAIN MULTIPLICITY TRANSFORMERS — corpus's **first documented method-chain-multiplicity-transformers CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: muvm uses **bpaf FromStr-trait-typed-argument CLI pattern** at cli_options.rs:18-34 + 147 — `enum GpuMode { Drm, Venus, Software, }; impl FromStr for GpuMode { ... } ; let gpu_mode = long("gpu-mode").argument::<GpuMode>("drm|venus|software").optional();`.  `.argument::<T>(META)` declares a TYPED ARGUMENT with placeholder; `T` must implement `FromStr` and bpaf calls `<T as FromStr>::from_str(argv_value)` to convert.  Distinct from R322 JCommander custom `IStringConverter<T>` (separate converter class), R325 OCaml `Arg.String FN` callback (separate callback function), R311 Guile `(value #t)` (no typed values).  bpaf's approach: STDLIB `FromStr` TRAIT for conversion — UNIFIED with Rust's type system.  Corpus's **first documented FromStr-trait-conversion CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: muvm uses **bpaf `positional()` and `any()` combinator CLI pattern** at cli_options.rs:192-198 — `let command = positional("COMMAND").help("..."); let command_args = any::<String, _, _>("COMMAND_ARGS", |arg| (!["--help", "-h"].contains(&&*arg)).then_some(arg)).help("...").many();`.  `positional(META)` declares a positional arg; `any(META, FILTER)` declares a WILDCARD CAPTURE with caller-provided FILTER function (here used to EXCLUDE `--help`/`-h` so muvm's help isn't shadowed).  Distinct from R322 JCommander bare-`@Parameter`-with-no-names (positional via convention), R323 Java args4j BARE-@Option-with-no-names (similar), R325 OCaml anon-fun callback (for positionals after parse).  bpaf's approach: SEPARATE `positional()` and `any()` COMBINATOR FUNCTIONS — corpus's **first documented `positional()`-and-`any()`-combinator CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: muvm uses **bpaf `construct!` macro with Rust field-init shorthand CLI pattern** at cli_options.rs:200-223 — `construct!(Options { cpu_list, env, mem, vram, passt_socket, passt_args, fex_images, merged_rootfs, interactive, tty, privileged, gpu_mode, publish_ports, emulator, init_commands, user_init_commands, command, command_args, }).to_options()`.  The `construct!` MACRO uses Rust's FIELD-INIT SHORTHAND (`field_name` without explicit `field_name: field_name`) to assemble individual parser values into a SINGLE Parser<Options>.  `.to_options()` converts to OptionParser<Options> which has `.run()`.  Distinct from R322 JCommander reflection (annotation-walking), R325 OCaml `Arg.align [...]` (list of specs), R311 Guile alist return.  bpaf's approach: `construct!` MACRO doing RUST FIELD-INIT SHORTHAND over Parser values — corpus's **first documented construct-macro-with-field-init-shorthand CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: muvm uses **bpaf `.parse(closure)` inline custom parser CLI pattern** at cli_options.rs:65-75, 81-87 — `.argument::<String>("CPU_LIST").parse(|s| { s.split(',').map(|s| s.split_once('-').unwrap_or((s,s))).map(|(start, end)| { ... }).collect::<Result<Vec<_>, _>>() })`.  `.parse(CLOSURE)` takes the argument as a string and runs an INLINE CLOSURE that returns `Result<T, E>`.  Used here to parse comma-separated CPU ranges (`0,5,8-11`).  Distinct from R322 JCommander `converter = X.class` (separate class), R325 OCaml `Arg.String FN` (top-level function reference).  bpaf's approach: INLINE CLOSURE for custom parsing — corpus's **first documented inline-closure-custom-parser CLI pattern**.
  - Hunt-skip notes: skipped `_clap` / `_structopt` / `_argh` / `_gumdrop` / `_lexopt` (would refer to the LIBRARIES, not CLIs), `_biome` (24793 stars but in corpus — Biome uses bpaf, would have been a SISTER demo), `_flox` (3998 stars but in corpus — Flox uses bpaf), `_cauwugo` (in corpus — bpaf author's own example), `_oxfmt` (in corpus — oxc project uses bpaf), `_unison` (DUP with stock zsh).  Probed `vosen/ZLUDA` (14239 stars, CUDA-on-AMD-GPU — but bpaf usage is in xtask helper only, not the primary binary), `dbalsom/martypc` (832 stars, IBM PC emulator — bpaf config but spread across modules), `BitpingApp/bping` (9 stars, ping CLI — small), `AsahiLinux/muvm` (CHOSEN — 878 stars, Asahi Linux microVM runner with clean 16-option bpaf spec).  Chose muvm: 878 GH stars (real Asahi Linux infrastructure tool), 16 named options + 2 positionals in single 230-line cli_options.rs exercising EVERY MAJOR bpaf PATTERN (combinator primitives `long`/`short`/`positional`/`any` composing via method-chain, `.switch()`/`.optional()`/`.many()` multiplicity transformers, `.argument::<T>("META")` with FromStr-trait conversion (`enum GpuMode { Drm, Venus, Software }` + `impl FromStr for GpuMode`), `.parse(closure)` inline custom parser for CPU-range and KEY=VALUE env parsing, `positional()` and `any(META, FILTER)` combinator functions, `construct!` macro with Rust field-init shorthand, `.to_options()` terminal conversion to OptionParser<T>), real binary distributed via cargo + Asahi Linux Fedora repos.
  - Blacklist update: `muvm` added.  Corpus count: 28,752 → 28,753 (+1).

- **R329 — dw_gdb source-direct (debugWIRE-to-GDB bridge in Free Pascal; stdlib `getopts` unit)** (1 file new) — pivots from Erlang stdlib argparse (R328 aerepl) back into the Free Pascal ecosystem to document the SECOND Free Pascal CLI library — corpus's **first documented Free Pascal stdlib `getopts` library** + **second documented Free Pascal CLI library** (joining R312 _pascalcoin_miner FCL `TCustomApplication`).  dw_gdb is a debugWIRE-to-GDB bridge — provides a GDB-RSP (Remote Serial Protocol) server over TCP that translates GDB commands into AVR microcontroller debugWIRE protocol messages, allowing GDB to debug AVR chips via their single-wire DWLINK protocol.  Authored by ccrause.  6 GH stars (specialized embedded-systems tool).  Sister tools: avr-gdb (GDB variant for AVR; in corpus), dwdebug (alternative debugWIRE client), avrdude (AVR downloader/uploader; in corpus), avr-gcc (in corpus).  **MAJOR MILESTONE**: corpus's **first documented Free Pascal stdlib `getopts` library** — adds Free Pascal `getopts` unit (ships with EVERY FPC install since FPC 1.x at `rtl/inc/getopts.pp`; direct PORT of C's GNU getopt_long; procedural API; static-array-of-TOption-records with NULL-TERMINATOR sentinel; `Has_arg` integer enum 0/1/2 for arg-shape; `OptArg` global variable; repeat-until-with-#0-sentinel parse loop) to the cross-language CLI taxonomy (68 library variants now).  Free Pascal expands to 2 documented libraries (FCL TCustomApplication + stdlib getopts) joining the multi-library club.
  - `_dw_gdb` (1-stem; ccrause/debugwire-gdb-bridge dw_gdb.lpr; **Free Pascal stdlib `getopts` unit** — ships with every FPC install since FPC 1.x at `rtl/inc/getopts.pp`; **6 options + `uses getopts` stdlib import + `array [0..N] of TOption` static-array spec + TOption record's 4 fields (`Flag`/`Has_arg`/`Name`/`Value`) + C-getopt_long-port `GetLongOpts(SHORT_SPEC, @opts[0], ID)` parser invocation + sentinel-record NULL TERMINATOR `opts[N].Name := ''; opts[N].Value := #0` + repeat-until-with-#0-sentinel parse loop + case-dispatch-with-OptArg-global + `Has_arg` integer enum 0/1/2 for arg-shape + Pascal `'X'` CHAR literal short flags**) decoded source-direct from:
    * `dw_gdb.lpr` line 4 (`uses ... getopts;` — Free Pascal `uses` directive importing the `getopts` STDLIB UNIT)
    * `dw_gdb.lpr` line 12 (`opts: array [0..6] of TOption;` — Pascal FIXED-SIZE ARRAY OF RECORDS; TOption is the spec record type defined in getopts unit)
    * `dw_gdb.lpr` lines 77-79 (`opts[0].Flag := nil; opts[0].Has_arg := 1; opts[0].Name := 'serialport'; opts[0].Value := 'S';` — TOption RECORD has FOUR FIELDS: Flag/Has_arg/Name/Value; direct port of GNU getopt_long's `struct option` from C)
    * `dw_gdb.lpr` lines 110-113 (`opts[6].Flag := nil; opts[6].Has_arg := 0; opts[6].Name := ''; opts[6].Value := #0;` — NULL TERMINATOR ENTRY; LAST entry MUST have `Name := ''` and `Value := #0`)
    * `dw_gdb.lpr` line 120 (`c := GetLongOpts('S:s:B:b:T:t:Hh?IiVv', @opts[0], ID);` — PARSE INVOCATION; SHORT_SPEC uses POSIX getopt convention `:` after char = required arg; `@opts[0]` is Pascal address-of operator; returns matched short-flag CHAR or `#0` when done)
    * `dw_gdb.lpr` lines 121-160 (`case c of 'S','s': serialPort := OptArg; 'B', 'b': baud := StrToInt(OptArg); ... end;` — CASE DISPATCH on matched short flag; `OptArg` is GLOBAL VARIABLE exported by getopts unit holding current option's argument value)
    * `dw_gdb.lpr` line 119 (`repeat ... c := GetLongOpts(...); ... until c = #0;` — REPEAT-UNTIL LOOP terminating when GetLongOpts returns `#0` Pascal null character)
  - Critical extraction note: dw_gdb uses **Free Pascal stdlib `getopts` unit** at dw_gdb.lpr:4 — corpus's **first documented Free Pascal stdlib `getopts` library** + **second documented Free Pascal CLI library** (joining R312 _pascalcoin_miner FCL `TCustomApplication`).  Free Pascal's `getopts` unit is part of FPC's RTL (Run-Time Library) at `rtl/inc/getopts.pp` — ships with EVERY Free Pascal install since FPC 1.x.  Distinct from R312 FCL `TCustomApplication` (FCL Free Component Library, OOP class with DoRun/CheckOptions methods — HIGH-LEVEL, event-driven), R272 C POSIX getopt (single-char opts only), R325 OCaml stdlib Arg (immutable list of tuple specs).  Pascal `getopts` is the LOW-LEVEL PORT of GNU getopt_long — procedural API, static array of TOption records with NULL TERMINATOR sentinel.  Extends cross-language CLI taxonomy to SIXTY-EIGHT library variants: Rust (6), Go (4), Python (3), Crystal 1 OptionParser (R269), Haskell (R270), OCaml 1 Cmdliner (R271), C (R272), Java 1 picocli (R276), Kotlin (R277), Scala (R278), Dart (R279), Ruby (R280), Perl (R281), PHP (R282), Swift (R283), F# (R284), Lua (R285), C# (R286), Nim 1 parseopt (R287), Zig (R288), D (R289), Racket (R290), Common Lisp 1 (R291), Erlang 1 getopt (R292), Tcl (R293), Pharo Smalltalk (R294), Common Lisp 2 clingon (R295), Julia (R296), SWI-Prolog optparse (R297), Pony stdlib cli (R298), Crystal 2 Admiral (R299), V stdlib `flag` (R300), Nim 2 cligen (R301), Clojure 1 babashka.cli (R302), Gleam Glint (R303), Ada GNAT.Command_Line (R304), C++ 1 CLI11 (R305), Elixir Optimus (R306), Vala GLib.OptionContext (R307), Hare stdlib getopt (R308), Standard ML stdlib GetOpt (R309), Fortran M_CLI2 (R310), Guile (ice-9 getopt-long) (R311), **Free Pascal 1 FCL TCustomApplication (R312)**, Idris 2 System.GetOpts (R313), Mercury stdlib getopt_io (R314), Eiffel Gobo AP_PARSER (R315), C++ 2 boost::program_options (R316), Forth/Gforth `next-arg` (R317), Clojure 2 org.clojure/tools.cli (R318), Roc weaver (R319), Chapel `config` keyword (R320), Mojo `alias`-as-spec (R321), Java 2 JCommander (R322), Java 3 args4j (R323), Chicken Scheme `args` egg (R324), OCaml 2 stdlib Arg (R325), Lean 4 Cli (R326), Bigloo Scheme `args-parse` macro (R327), Erlang 2 stdlib argparse (R328), **Free Pascal 2 stdlib getopts** (R329 — NEW).  **MAJOR PATTERN MILESTONE**: 68 cross-language CLI library variants documented.  Free Pascal expands to 2 documented libraries.
  - Critical extraction note: dw_gdb uses **Free Pascal `array [0..N] of TOption` STATIC ARRAY of records spec CLI pattern** at dw_gdb.lpr:12, 77-113 — `opts: array [0..6] of TOption;` declares a fixed-size array, then each entry's fields are set individually.  TOption RECORD has FOUR FIELDS: `Flag` (pointer-to-int receiver, nil if not used), `Has_arg` (integer arg-shape: 0/1/2), `Name` (long name string), `Value` (Pascal char literal returned by GetLongOpts).  Distinct from R312 Free Pascal FCL `class(TCustomApplication)` (OOP class inheritance), R322 JCommander `@Parameter` annotation, R325 OCaml `[(NAME, Arg.SPEC, HELP); ...]` immutable list.  Pascal getopts's approach: STATICALLY-ALLOCATED ARRAY OF TOption with NULL TERMINATOR — corpus's **first documented static-array-of-records-with-NULL-terminator spec CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: dw_gdb uses **Pascal getopts C-getopt_long-port CLI pattern** at dw_gdb.lpr:120 — `c := GetLongOpts('S:s:B:b:T:t:Hh?IiVv', @opts[0], ID);`.  This is a DIRECT PORT of C's `getopt_long(argc, argv, optstring, longopts, longindex)` — same semantics, different syntax.  The short-opt string uses POSIX getopt convention (`:` after a char = required arg, `::` = optional arg).  Distinct from R272 C POSIX getopt (single-char opts only), R325 OCaml `Arg.parse_argv ~current:(ref 0) argv arg_specs (fun s -> ...) usage` (OCaml-native API), R322 JCommander `parse(*argv)` (Kotlin/Java method call).  Pascal getopts's approach: SAME API AS C GNU getopt_long but with PASCAL TYPE SIGNATURES — corpus's **first documented Pascal-port-of-C-getopt_long CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: dw_gdb uses **Pascal sentinel-record end-marker CLI pattern** at dw_gdb.lpr:110-113 — `opts[6].Flag := nil; opts[6].Has_arg := 0; opts[6].Name := ''; opts[6].Value := #0;`.  The LAST entry in the opts array MUST have `Name := ''` and `Value := #0` — this signals END-OF-OPTIONS to the parser.  `#0` is Pascal's NULL CHARACTER literal.  Distinct from R307 Vala GLib OptionEntry's `{null}` C-struct-NULL terminator (similar pattern, different syntax), R322 JCommander uses annotation count (no terminator needed), R325 OCaml uses list length.  Pascal getopts's approach: SENTINEL-RECORD with EMPTY NAME + `#0` VALUE for end-of-array — corpus's **first documented sentinel-record-end-marker CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: dw_gdb uses **Pascal repeat-until-with-#0-sentinel loop CLI pattern** at dw_gdb.lpr:119, 161 — `repeat c := GetLongOpts(...); case c of ... end; until c = #0;`.  The caller WRITES the parse loop manually, calling `GetLongOpts` repeatedly until it returns `#0` (Pascal null character).  Distinct from R272 C POSIX getopt (`while ((c = getopt(...)) != -1)`), R325 OCaml stdlib Arg (no loop — `Arg.parse_argv` handles everything internal), R322 JCommander single-call.  Pascal getopts's approach: REPEAT-UNTIL LOOP with `#0` SENTINEL — corpus's **first documented repeat-until-with-#0-sentinel CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: dw_gdb uses **Pascal case-dispatch-with-OptArg-global CLI pattern** at dw_gdb.lpr:121-160 — `case c of 'S','s': serialPort := OptArg; 'B', 'b': baud := StrToInt(OptArg); ... end;`.  `OptArg` is a GLOBAL VARIABLE exported by the getopts unit holding the current option's argument value.  The caller writes a CASE STATEMENT to dispatch on the returned char.  Distinct from R272 C POSIX getopt (`switch (c)` on returned int + `optarg` global — SAME PATTERN), R322 JCommander (reflection-based field binding — no dispatch), R325 OCaml Arg.Set FUNCTION thunks (no manual dispatch).  Pascal getopts's approach: CALLER-WRITTEN CASE DISPATCH + `OptArg` global — corpus's **first documented case-dispatch-with-OptArg-global CLI pattern** (Pascal directly ports C getopt's `optarg` global convention).
  - Critical extraction note: dw_gdb uses **Pascal Has_arg integer enum (0/1/2) for arg-shape CLI pattern** at dw_gdb.lpr:78, 91, 103 — `Has_arg := 0` (no arg), `Has_arg := 1` (required arg), `Has_arg := 2` (optional arg).  Same numeric scheme as C's GNU getopt_long (`no_argument`/`required_argument`/`optional_argument` = 0/1/2).  Distinct from R325 OCaml stdlib Arg (variant constructors `Arg.Set`/`Arg.Set_string`/etc.), R313 Idris 2 (`NoArg`/`ReqArg`/`OptArg` constructors), R324 Chicken Scheme `#:none`/`#:required`/`#:optional` keywords.  Pascal getopts's approach: INTEGER ENUM (0/1/2) for arg-shape — direct port of C convention.  Corpus's **first documented integer-enum-arg-shape CLI pattern**.
  - Hunt-skip notes: skipped `_fpc` / `_lazbuild` (FPC compiler and Lazarus build tools in corpus), `_pascalcoin_miner` (R312 — Free Pascal's first CLI library, FCL TCustomApplication; same language but different library now documented), `_chmcmd` (Lazarus help compiler — IN CORPUS as pas2ut sister), `_unison` (DUP with stock zsh).  Probed `doublecmd/doublecmd` (4200 stars but file manager — ucmdlineparams.pas is a small helper, not a primary CLI surface), `Bill-Stewart/JavaInfo` (20 stars, Windows Java detection — small surface), `Bill-Stewart/PathMgr` (31 stars, Windows PATH manager — Windows-specific), `serbod/lazhelp chmcmd` (binary `chmcmd` already in corpus from Lazarus distribution), `jwdietrich21/simthyr` (1 star, thyroid simulator — medical sim, narrow CLI), `ccrause/debugwire-gdb-bridge dw_gdb` (CHOSEN — 6 stars, AVR debugger bridge with clean 6-option getopts demo).  Chose dw_gdb: 6 GH stars (real embedded-systems tool used by AVR developers), 6 options in clean single-file dw_gdb.lpr exercising EVERY MAJOR Free Pascal getopts PATTERN (`uses getopts` stdlib import, `array [0..N] of TOption` static-array spec, TOption record's 4 fields (`Flag`/`Has_arg`/`Name`/`Value`), C-getopt_long-port `GetLongOpts(SHORT_SPEC, @opts[0], ID)` parser invocation, sentinel-record NULL TERMINATOR `opts[N].Name := ''; opts[N].Value := #0`, repeat-until-with-#0-sentinel loop, case-dispatch-with-OptArg-global, Has_arg integer enum 0/1/2 for arg-shape, Pascal `'X'` CHAR literal short flags), real binary distributed via Lazarus + makefile.
  - Blacklist update: `dw_gdb` added.  Corpus count: 28,751 → 28,752 (+1).

- **R328 — aerepl source-direct (Sophia smart-contract REPL on Æternity blockchain in Erlang; stdlib `argparse` module)** (1 file new) — pivots from Bigloo Scheme args-parse (R327 watib) into the Erlang/OTP ecosystem to document Erlang's modern stdlib CLI parser — corpus's **first documented Erlang stdlib `argparse` library** + **second documented Erlang CLI library** (joining R292 _emqtt jcomellas/getopt third-party library).  aerepl is a sophisticated Read-Eval-Print Loop for Sophia — the smart-contract language of the Æternity blockchain.  Allows interactive evaluation of Sophia expressions, function-calls, and stateful contract simulations.  Authored by Radosław Rowicki (radrow) for the Æternity blockchain project.  6 GH stars (specialized blockchain dev tool).  Sister tools: aesophia (the Sophia compiler — escript), aeternity_node (the Æternity blockchain node).  **MAJOR MILESTONE**: corpus's **first documented Erlang stdlib `argparse` library** — adds Erlang stdlib argparse module (introduced in OTP 26, June 2023; map-of-maps spec, type-with-constraint `{integer, [{min, 0}]}` / `{atom, [enum]}`, `{custom, fun X/1}` function-reference custom types, `{Module, Function}` 2-tuple handler dispatch, `argparse:run(Args, Cmd, Opts)` unified parse-and-dispatch entry point) to the cross-language CLI taxonomy (67 library variants now).  Erlang expands to 2 documented libraries (getopt + argparse) joining the multi-library club with Rust 6, Go 4, Python 3, Crystal 2, Common Lisp 2, Nim 2, Clojure 2, C++ 2, Java 3, OCaml 2, and 4 Scheme dialects.
  - `_aerepl` (1-stem; aeternity/aerepl `aerepl` escript; **Erlang stdlib `argparse` module** — introduced in OTP 26 (June 2023); **14 named options + escript shebang (`#!/usr/bin/env escript`) + `%%!`-VM-args-line for Erlang VM config + `-mode(compile).` attribute + `argparse:run(Args, cli(), #{progname => "aerepl"})` unified parse-and-dispatch + `cli/0` returning command spec as Erlang map `#{handler => {Module, Function}, arguments => [#{name, long, short, type, nargs, action, default} ...]}` + map-of-maps spec + Erlang `$a` char-literal short flag + type-with-constraint `{integer, [{min, 0}]}` and `{atom, [sophia,json,fate]}` + `{custom, fun X/1}` function-reference custom type + `?MODULE` preprocessor macro + `{Module, Function}` 2-tuple handler dispatch**) decoded source-direct from:
    * `aerepl` line 1 (`#!/usr/bin/env escript` — ESCRIPT SHEBANG; Erlang's standalone-script execution mode)
    * `aerepl` line 3 (`%%! -smp enable -setcookie aerepl_cookie -mnesia debug verbose` — escript ARGUMENTS LINE; lines starting with `%%!` are passed as ARGS to the Erlang VM)
    * `aerepl` line 7 (`-mode(compile).` — module attribute forcing escript COMPILE mode (vs interpret))
    * `aerepl` line 9 (`main(Args) -> load_paths(), argparse:run(Args, cli(), #{progname => "aerepl"}).` — ENTRY POINT; `argparse:run/3` takes ARGS list + COMMAND SPEC + OPTIONS MAP and handles both parsing AND dispatch)
    * `aerepl` lines 13-32 (`cli() -> #{handler => {?MODULE, aerepl}, arguments => [#{name => accounts, long => "account", short => $a, nargs => 2, action => append, default => [], type => {custom, fun parse_account/1}}, #{name => call_gas, long => "call_gas", type => {integer, [{min, 0}]}}, ...]}` — COMMAND SPEC as Erlang MAP with `handler` 2-tuple and `arguments` LIST OF OPTION SPEC MAPS)
    * `aerepl` line 16-19 (DETAILED OPTION SPEC fields: `name => accounts` (Erlang ATOM key), `long => "account"` (string for --account), `short => $a` (Erlang CHAR LITERAL), `nargs => 2` (consume 2 argv tokens), `action => append` (APPEND to list for repeatable), `default => []`, `type => {custom, fun parse_account/1}` (custom parser function reference))
    * `aerepl` line 27 (`type => {atom, [sophia, json, fate]}` — ATOM-CHOICE TYPE; parser CONVERTS argv string to Erlang atom AND VALIDATES it's in the allowed list)
    * `aerepl` line 19 (`type => {custom, fun parse_account/1}` — CUSTOM TYPE via function reference)
    * `aerepl` line 14 (`handler => {?MODULE, aerepl}` — `{Module, Function}` 2-TUPLE specifying the dispatch handler; `?MODULE` is Erlang preprocessor macro expanding to current module name atom)
  - Critical extraction note: aerepl uses **Erlang stdlib `argparse` module** at aerepl:10 — corpus's **first documented Erlang stdlib `argparse` library** + **second documented Erlang CLI library** (joining R292 _emqtt jcomellas/getopt third-party library).  Erlang stdlib `argparse` was introduced in OTP 26 (June 2023) — a relatively new addition.  Distinct from R292 jcomellas/getopt (third-party `getopt:parse(OptSpecList, Args)` returning `{ok, {[ParsedOpts], [Remaining]}}`, list-of-tuples spec), Apache Commons-CLI Erlang ports.  stdlib argparse adds: NESTED-MAP SPEC, subcommand tree support, type-with-constraint (`{integer, [{min, 0}]}`, `{atom, [enum]}`), `nargs`/`action` fields, `{Module, Function}` handler dispatch, automatic `--help` generation, `argparse:run/3` single-call entry point.  Extends cross-language CLI taxonomy to SIXTY-SEVEN library variants: Rust (6), Go (4), Python (3), Crystal 1 OptionParser (R269), Haskell (R270), OCaml 1 Cmdliner (R271), C (R272), Java 1 picocli (R276), Kotlin (R277), Scala (R278), Dart (R279), Ruby (R280), Perl (R281), PHP (R282), Swift (R283), F# (R284), Lua (R285), C# (R286), Nim 1 parseopt (R287), Zig (R288), D (R289), Racket (R290), Common Lisp 1 (R291), **Erlang 1 getopt (R292)**, Tcl (R293), Pharo Smalltalk (R294), Common Lisp 2 clingon (R295), Julia (R296), SWI-Prolog optparse (R297), Pony stdlib cli (R298), Crystal 2 Admiral (R299), V stdlib `flag` (R300), Nim 2 cligen (R301), Clojure 1 babashka.cli (R302), Gleam Glint (R303), Ada GNAT.Command_Line (R304), C++ 1 CLI11 (R305), Elixir Optimus (R306), Vala GLib.OptionContext (R307), Hare stdlib getopt (R308), Standard ML stdlib GetOpt (R309), Fortran M_CLI2 (R310), Guile (ice-9 getopt-long) (R311), Free Pascal TCustomApplication (R312), Idris 2 System.GetOpts (R313), Mercury stdlib getopt_io (R314), Eiffel Gobo AP_PARSER (R315), C++ 2 boost::program_options (R316), Forth/Gforth `next-arg` (R317), Clojure 2 org.clojure/tools.cli (R318), Roc weaver (R319), Chapel `config` keyword (R320), Mojo `alias`-as-spec (R321), Java 2 JCommander (R322), Java 3 args4j (R323), Chicken Scheme `args` egg (R324), OCaml 2 stdlib Arg (R325), Lean 4 Cli (R326), Bigloo Scheme `args-parse` macro (R327), **Erlang 2 stdlib argparse** (R328 — NEW).  **MAJOR PATTERN MILESTONE**: 67 cross-language CLI library variants documented.  Erlang expands to 2 documented libraries (getopt + argparse) joining the multi-library club.
  - Critical extraction note: aerepl uses **Erlang stdlib `argparse:run(Args, Cmd, Opts)` run-with-dispatch CLI pattern** at aerepl:10 — `argparse:run(Args, cli(), #{progname => "aerepl"})` handles BOTH parsing AND dispatch to the handler function in ONE CALL.  The handler function (specified in the command map as `handler => {Module, Function}`) is called with the parsed args map AFTER successful parsing.  Distinct from R292 Erlang getopt (`getopt:parse/2` returns parsed-result tuple; caller dispatches), R322 JCommander `parse(*argv)` + manual dispatch, R325 OCaml `Arg.parse_argv` (mutates refs).  Erlang stdlib argparse's approach: UNIFIED PARSE-AND-DISPATCH — corpus's **first documented run-with-dispatch argparse CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: aerepl uses **Erlang stdlib argparse map-of-maps spec CLI pattern** at aerepl:13-32 — `#{handler => {?MODULE, aerepl}, arguments => [#{name => accounts, long => "account", short => $a, nargs => 2, action => append, default => [], type => {custom, fun parse_account/1}}, ...]}`.  The COMMAND SPEC is an Erlang MAP `#{handler => H, arguments => L, commands => SUB, help => "..."}` and each OPTION SPEC is also a MAP `#{name => N, long => "L", short => $C, type => T, ...}`.  Distinct from R292 Erlang getopt (list-of-4-tuples spec), R322 JCommander `@Parameter` annotation, R311 Guile `((NAME (single-char #\X) (value #t/#f)))` quoted alist.  Erlang stdlib argparse's approach: NESTED-MAPS SPEC — every command and option is an Erlang MAP.  Corpus's **first documented map-of-maps spec argparse CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: aerepl uses **Erlang stdlib argparse type-with-constraint CLI pattern** at aerepl:20-27 — `type => {integer, [{min, 0}]}` (integer with minimum), `type => {atom, [sophia, json, fate]}` (atom with allowed-values choice list), `type => boolean` (presence-only flag), `type => {custom, fun parse_account/1}` (custom parser function).  The type spec can be a bare atom or a 2-tuple `{TYPE, CONSTRAINTS}`.  Distinct from R322 JCommander reflection-based type detection, R325 OCaml `Arg.Set_int` / `Arg.Set_string` (type-named variants), R324 Chicken `#:required` (keyword arg-shape only).  Erlang stdlib argparse's approach: `{TYPE, [CONSTRAINTS]}` TUPLE for both type AND validation — corpus's **first documented type-with-constraint argparse CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: aerepl uses **Erlang stdlib argparse `{Module, Function}` 2-tuple handler dispatch CLI pattern** at aerepl:14 — `handler => {?MODULE, aerepl}` specifies the dispatch target as a 2-TUPLE.  `?MODULE` is an Erlang PREPROCESSOR macro expanding to the current module name atom; `aerepl` is the function name atom.  argparse calls `apply(Module, Function, [ParsedArgsMap])` after parsing.  Distinct from R311 Guile `(args:make-option ... BODY)` (embedded thunk), R324 Chicken `(args:make-option (h help) #:none HELP BODY)` (embedded body thunk), R322 JCommander main-method (no explicit handler binding).  Erlang stdlib argparse's approach: 2-TUPLE Module-Function-name dispatch — corpus's **first documented 2-tuple-mfa-handler CLI dispatch pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: aerepl uses **Erlang escript `%%!`-VM-args-line CLI pattern** at aerepl:3 — `%%! -smp enable -setcookie aerepl_cookie -mnesia debug verbose`.  Lines starting with `%%!` are passed as ARGS to the Erlang VM when running the escript.  This allows the script to configure SMP, distributed-Erlang cookies, application environments, etc., at the VM level before the escript's `main/1` function runs.  Distinct from R290 Racket / R311 Guile / R324 Chicken shebang-only args.  Corpus's **first documented escript-`%%!`-VM-args-line CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: aerepl uses **Erlang `$a` char-literal short flag** at aerepl:16 — `short => $a` uses Erlang's `$X` CHAR LITERAL syntax.  Sister to R291 CL `#\h`, R298 Pony `'p'`, R300 V `` `n` ``, R308 Hare `'1'`, R311 Guile `#\X`, R315 Eiffel `'V'`, R324 Chicken Scheme `(h help)` (UNQUOTED-SYMBOL pair).  This reaffirms Erlang's `$X` char-literal convention across DIFFERENT CLI libraries: R292 emqtt (jcomellas/getopt third-party library) used `$X`; R328 aerepl (Erlang stdlib argparse) uses the SAME `$X` convention — corpus's **second Erlang library to use `$X` char-literal short flags, demonstrating the convention is LANGUAGE-LEVEL not library-level**.
  - Critical extraction note: aerepl uses **Erlang escript `#!/usr/bin/env escript` shebang + `-mode(compile).` attribute** at aerepl:1, 7 — escript is Erlang's standalone-script execution mode: the script is read by the Erlang VM and either INTERPRETED (default) or COMPILED (`-mode(compile).` attribute).  Distinct from R317 Forth Gforth shebang (`#! /usr/bin/env gforth`), R324 Chicken `#! /usr/bin/env csi -s`, R311 Guile `#! /usr/bin/env guile`.  Erlang's approach: ESCRIPT RUNTIME-COMPILE shebang with explicit mode attribute — corpus's **first documented escript-with-compile-mode-attribute CLI pattern**.
  - Hunt-skip notes: skipped `_erlang` (Erlang language, in corpus + blacklisted), `_escript` (Erlang escript runner, in corpus), `_emqtt` (R292 — already documented as Erlang's first CLI library, jcomellas/getopt), `_edb` (in corpus as Evan's Debugger — different binary than WhatsApp/edb Erlang debugger that ALSO uses argparse:run; naming collision), `_unison` (DUP with stock zsh).  Probed `WhatsApp/edb` (107 stars but binary `edb` collides with Evan's Debugger in corpus), `duncanatt/paterl` (2 stars, Erlang-to-Pat transpiler — niche), `haruki7049/hoblisp.erl` (0 stars, hobby Lisp implementation), `happening-oss/kafcod` (6 stars, Kafka codecs — library not CLI).  Chose aerepl: 6 GH stars (Æternity blockchain Sophia REPL by radrow), 14 named options in single 40-line cli/0 function exercising EVERY MAJOR Erlang stdlib argparse PATTERN (escript shebang + `%%!`-VM-args-line + `-mode(compile)` attribute, `argparse:run(Args, cli(), #{progname => "aerepl"})` run-with-dispatch, map-of-maps spec `#{handler => {Module, Function}, arguments => [#{name, long, short, type, nargs, action, default} ...]}`, type-with-constraint `{integer, [{min, 0}]}` / `{atom, [sophia, json, fate]}`, `{custom, fun parse_account/1}` function-reference custom type, Erlang `$a` char-literal short flag, `?MODULE` preprocessor macro, `{Module, Function}` 2-tuple handler dispatch), real escript binary distributed via Æternity's blockchain release.
  - Blacklist update: `aerepl` added.  Corpus count: 28,750 → 28,751 (+1).

- **R327 — watib source-direct (WebAssembly toolchain in Bigloo Scheme; `args-parse` macro)** (1 file new) — pivots from Lean 4 Cli (R326 doc-gen4) into the Bigloo Scheme compiler ecosystem — corpus's **first documented Bigloo Scheme CLI library** + **fourth documented Scheme dialect CLI library** (joining R290 Racket `racket/cmdline`, R311 Guile `(ice-9 getopt-long)`, R324 Chicken `args` egg).  watib is a WebAssembly TOOLCHAIN written in Bigloo Scheme — validates WAT (WebAssembly Text) files, applies type-based and dataflow-based optimizations, emits WASM binary.  Authored by Aghilas Y. Boussaa (4y8).  2 GH stars (recent project) but cleanest single-file Bigloo args-parse demo.  Sister tools: wabt (WebAssembly Binary Toolkit, in corpus as `_wabt`), wasm-tools (Rust wasm tooling, in corpus), wasm-pack (in corpus), wasmtime (in corpus).  **MAJOR MILESTONE**: corpus's **first documented Bigloo Scheme CLI library** — adds Bigloo's BUILT-IN `args-parse` macro (case-clause-with-embedded-body, quoted-string-list flag names, `?varname` match-case pattern-variable for arg capture, arbitrary-string short flags `-O0`/`-O1`/`-fno-testbr`, `else` clause as positional catch-all, `(module NAME (main NAME))` Bigloo module declaration with main-clause) to the cross-language CLI taxonomy (66 library variants now).  Scheme dialects now FOUR: Racket + Guile + Chicken + Bigloo.
  - `_watib` (1-stem; 4y8/watib watib.scm; **Bigloo Scheme `args-parse` macro** — built into the standard Bigloo distribution since 2.x, not a separate library egg/import; **25+ flags + positional input files + `args-parse args (CLAUSE) ... (else BODY)` macro-expanded case-clause matching + quoted-string-list flag names `("--help" "-h")` + `?VARNAME` match-case pattern-variable for arg capture + arbitrary-string short flags `-O0`/`-O1`/`-fno-testbr`/`-ftestbr` (alphanumeric suffixed, GCC-style) + `(help "...")` inline help inside each clause + `else` clause as positional catch-all (with `else` ALSO bound to current token) + `(module NAME (main NAME))` module-with-main-clause declarative header + `with-access::CLASS o-flags (FIELDS)` OOP destructuring inside CLI body**) decoded source-direct from:
    * `watib.scm` line 5 (`(module watib (main main) (library pthread srfi1) (import ...))` — Bigloo MODULE DECLARATION with `(main main)` designating the entry function)
    * `watib.scm` line 30 (`(define (main argv)` — entry-point function taking ARGV as a list including argv[0])
    * `watib.scm` lines 42-128 (`(args-parse args ((("--help" "-h") (help "Display this help message and quit")) (args-parse-usage #f)) ((("--keep-going" "-k") (help "Continue when encountering an error")) (set! keep-going #t)) (("--stop-after" ?n (help "Stop after encountering N errors")) (set! keep-going (string->number n))) (("-s" (help "Display less verbose error messages")) (set! silent #t)) (("-j" ?n (help "Use multiple threads")) (set! nthreads (string->number n))) ... (else (set! input-files (cons else input-files))))` — Bigloo `args-parse` MACRO with case-clause-with-embedded-body)
    * `watib.scm` line 43 (`((("--help" "-h") (help "Display this help message and quit"))` — FLAG NAMES as QUOTED STRING LIST `("--help" "-h")`)
    * `watib.scm` line 49 (`(("--stop-after" ?n (help "Stop after encountering N errors")) (set! keep-going (string->number n)))` — ARG CAPTURE via `?n` PATTERN VARIABLE from MATCH-CASE)
    * `watib.scm` lines 56-118 (`(("-O0" (help "Disable optimisations")) (with-access::opt-flags o-flags (testbr copyprop uncast unreachable const puredrop peephole) (set! testbr #f) ...))` plus 7 `-f...`/`-fno-...` PAIRS — GCC-STYLE arbitrary-string short flags via STRING MATCHING; `with-access::CLASS OBJECT (FIELDS)` Bigloo OOP destructuring inside CLI body)
    * `watib.scm` line 121 (`(("-o" ?file (help "Output binary format to FILE")) (set! output-file file))` — single-arg option with `?file` pattern capture)
    * `watib.scm` line 123 (`(("--dump-cfg" ?func (help "...")) (set! dump-cfg (string->symbol func)))` — CUSTOM PARSE inside body via `(string->symbol func)`)
    * `watib.scm` line 129 (`(else (set! input-files (cons else input-files))))` — `else` CATCH-ALL clause for POSITIONALS; the `else` symbol is BOUND to the current positional value)
    * `watib.scm` line 173 (`(parse-args (cdr argv))` — INVOKE the parser with argv MINUS argv[0])
  - Critical extraction note: watib uses **Bigloo Scheme `args-parse` macro** at watib.scm:42 — corpus's **first documented Bigloo Scheme CLI library** + **fourth documented Scheme dialect CLI library** (joining R290 Racket `racket/cmdline`, R311 Guile `(ice-9 getopt-long)`, R324 Chicken `args` egg).  Bigloo's `args-parse` is a BUILT-IN MACRO (part of the standard Bigloo distribution since 2.x — not a separate library egg/import).  Distinct from R290 Racket `command-line` (also a macro but uses `#:once-each`/`#:multi`/`#:once-any` section structure), R311 Guile `(getopt-long args SPEC)` (function call with quoted-alist spec, returns alist — no embedded body), R324 Chicken `args:make-option (SHORT LONG) #:KEYWORD HELP BODY` (similar embedded body but uses egg-based spec).  Bigloo's `args-parse` uses MATCH-CASE-style pattern variables (`?n` captures next argv token) and `else` clause for catch-all.  Extends cross-language CLI taxonomy to SIXTY-SIX library variants: Rust (6), Go (4), Python (3), Crystal 1 OptionParser (R269), Haskell (R270), OCaml 1 Cmdliner (R271), C (R272), Java 1 picocli (R276), Kotlin (R277), Scala (R278), Dart (R279), Ruby (R280), Perl (R281), PHP (R282), Swift (R283), F# (R284), Lua (R285), C# (R286), Nim 1 parseopt (R287), Zig (R288), D (R289), **Racket racket/cmdline (R290)**, Common Lisp 1 (R291), Erlang (R292), Tcl (R293), Pharo Smalltalk (R294), Common Lisp 2 clingon (R295), Julia (R296), SWI-Prolog optparse (R297), Pony stdlib cli (R298), Crystal 2 Admiral (R299), V stdlib `flag` (R300), Nim 2 cligen (R301), Clojure 1 babashka.cli (R302), Gleam Glint (R303), Ada GNAT.Command_Line (R304), C++ 1 CLI11 (R305), Elixir Optimus (R306), Vala GLib.OptionContext (R307), Hare stdlib getopt (R308), Standard ML stdlib GetOpt (R309), Fortran M_CLI2 (R310), **Guile (ice-9 getopt-long) (R311)**, Free Pascal TCustomApplication (R312), Idris 2 System.GetOpts (R313), Mercury stdlib getopt_io (R314), Eiffel Gobo AP_PARSER (R315), C++ 2 boost::program_options (R316), Forth/Gforth `next-arg` (R317), Clojure 2 org.clojure/tools.cli (R318), Roc weaver (R319), Chapel `config` keyword (R320), Mojo `alias`-as-spec (R321), Java 2 JCommander (R322), Java 3 args4j (R323), **Chicken Scheme `args` egg (R324)**, OCaml 2 stdlib Arg (R325), Lean 4 Cli (R326), **Bigloo Scheme `args-parse` macro** (R327 — NEW).  **MAJOR PATTERN MILESTONE**: 66 cross-language CLI library variants documented.  Scheme dialects now FOUR: Racket + Guile + Chicken + Bigloo.
  - Critical extraction note: watib uses **Bigloo `args-parse` macro with case-clause-with-embedded-body CLI pattern** at watib.scm:42-129 — `(args-parse args ((CLAUSE) BODY) ... (else BODY))`.  Each clause is a 2-element list: `(PATTERN-MATCH BODY-EXPR)`.  The pattern can be a name-list `("--help" "-h")` or a name+arg-capture `("-j" ?n)`.  Embedded `(help "...")` provides inline help text.  Distinct from R290 Racket `command-line` (s-expr DSL with `#:once-each`/`#:multi`/`#:once-any` section structure), R311 Guile `(getopt-long args SPEC)` (function call with quoted-alist spec, returns alist — NO embedded body), R324 Chicken `args:make-option` (similar embedded body but uses egg-based spec).  Bigloo's approach: MACRO-EXPANDED CASE-CLAUSE MATCHING with EMBEDDED BODY per case — corpus's **first documented case-clause-args-parse-with-embedded-body CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: watib uses **Bigloo `?VARNAME` match-case pattern-variable for arg capture** at watib.scm:49 et seq — `(("--stop-after" ?n (help "...")) (set! keep-going (string->number n)))`.  The `?n` syntax (from Bigloo's MATCH-CASE macros) BINDS the next argv element to the symbol `n` for use in the body.  Distinct from R311 Guile `(value #t)` keyword arg-shape, R313 Idris 2 `NoArg ctor` / `ReqArg ctor "<plc>"` constructor variants, R324 Chicken `#:required` keyword.  Bigloo's approach: `?VARNAME` PATTERN VARIABLE for arg capture — corpus's **first documented match-case-pattern-variable-for-arg-capture CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: watib uses **Bigloo arbitrary-string-as-short-flag CLI pattern** at watib.scm:71-118 — `(("-fno-testbr" ...) BODY) (("-ftestbr" ...) BODY) (("-O0" ...) BODY) (("-O1" ...) BODY)`.  Bigloo's args-parse handles ALPHANUMERIC SUFFIXED short flags via STRING MATCHING (not single-char dispatch).  Distinct from R325 OCaml `Arg.Set` single-char convention, R322 JCommander reflection-based, R272 C POSIX getopt's option-string (single chars only).  Bigloo's approach: STRING-MATCHED short flags allow ARBITRARY ALPHANUMERIC SHORT NAMES like `-O0`/`-O1`/`-fno-testbr`/`-ftestbr` — corpus's **first documented arbitrary-string-as-short-flag CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: watib uses **Bigloo `else` clause as POSITIONAL-CATCH-ALL CLI pattern** at watib.scm:129 — `(else (set! input-files (cons else input-files)))`.  The `else` symbol in the args-parse macro is DOUBLY OVERLOADED: as a CLAUSE KEYWORD (the catch-all) AND as the BOUND VARIABLE holding the current non-matching argv token.  Distinct from R324 Chicken `(operands)` parameter returned after parse, R325 OCaml `(fun s -> mountpoint := s)` anon-fun callback, R270 Haskell returns `[String]` of non-options.  Bigloo's approach: `else` CLAUSE acts as the positional handler with `else` ALSO bound to the current value — corpus's **first documented else-clause-as-positional-handler CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: watib uses **Bigloo `(module NAME (main NAME-OF-ENTRY) (library ...) (import ...))` module-with-main-clause pattern** at watib.scm:5 — `(module watib (main main) (library pthread srfi1) (import (misc_parse "Misc/parse.scm") ...))`.  The `(main main)` clause designates the function `main` as the program ENTRY POINT.  Bigloo's module system is more elaborate than R290 Racket (`(module foo racket ...)` R6RS-style), R311 Guile `(define-module ...)`, R324 Chicken `csi -s` shebang + plain `(define (main args) ...)`.  Corpus's **first documented module-with-main-clause CLI pattern**.
  - Hunt-skip notes: skipped `_bigloo` (Bigloo Scheme compiler, in corpus + blacklisted), `_csi` / `_guile` / `_racket` (other Scheme interpreters in corpus), `_unison` (DUP with stock zsh).  Skipped `_pcc` (Roadsend PHP compiler binary — already in corpus for Portable C Compiler, naming collision), `_php` (Roadsend PHP compat-mode invocation — would shadow real PHP).  Probed `manuel-serrano/hop` (164 stars but `hop` already in corpus), `weyrick/roadsend-php` (108 stars but binary `pcc` collides with Portable C Compiler in corpus — 40+ flags would be a great demo otherwise), `manuel-serrano/bglstone` (0 stars — Bigloo benchmark suite, not user-facing CLI), `samoht/camloo` (11 stars, ML-to-Bigloo compiler — interesting alternative if Bigloo wasn't already covered).  Chose watib: 2 GH stars (recent project) but CLEANEST single-file Bigloo args-parse demo with 25+ flags exercising EVERY MAJOR Bigloo args-parse PATTERN (case-clause-with-embedded-body, quoted-string-list flag names, `?varname` pattern-variable arg capture, arbitrary-string short flags `-O0`/`-O1`/`-fno-testbr`, `else` clause as positional catch-all, `(module NAME (main NAME))` Bigloo module declaration with main-clause, `with-access::CLASS obj (fields)` OOP destructuring inside CLI body, GCC-style `-f...`/`-fno-...` flag pairs for compiler optimizations), real WebAssembly toolchain binary distributed via Bigloo's `bigloo -o watib watib.scm` build.
  - Blacklist update: `watib` added.  Corpus count: 28,749 → 28,750 (+1).

- **R326 — doc-gen4 source-direct (OFFICIAL Lean 4 documentation generator; mhuisi/`Cli` library)** (1 file new) — pivots from OCaml stdlib Arg (R325 gdfuse) into the Lean 4 mathematical-proof-assistant ecosystem — corpus's **first documented Lean 4 CLI library**.  Resolves the deferred Lean detour from R313 _ttc-converter: doc-gen4 is the leanprover org's STANDALONE-BINARY Cli-library example (the "deferred until a standalone-binary Cli-library example surfaces" note).  doc-gen4 is the OFFICIAL Lean 4 documentation generator — produces HTML API docs for Lean 4 projects, used by Lean's mathlib (the LARGEST Lean 4 mathematical library) and many Lean 4 packages.  Authored by the leanprover org.  153 GH stars.  Sister tools: lake (Lean 4 build system, in corpus), lean (compiler, in corpus), mathlib (math library, in corpus), elan (Lean version manager, in corpus).  **MAJOR MILESTONE**: corpus's **first documented Lean 4 CLI library** — adds mhuisi/lean4-cli (Lean-native DSL via `syntax` extension + `[Cli|...]` macro; `name VIA handler;` declaration; sectioned `FLAGS:`/`ARGS:`/`SUBCOMMANDS:` DSL; `...name` prefix for variadic positionals; `Parsed` object with method accessors; `command.validate args` entry-point) to the cross-language CLI taxonomy (65 library variants now).  Lean 4 joins as the FIRST documented dependently-typed PROOF ASSISTANT (distinct from R313 Idris 2 which is a dependently-typed general-purpose language).
  - `_doc-gen4` (1-stem; leanprover/doc-gen4 Main.lean; **Lean 4 `Cli` library** — mhuisi/lean4-cli, distributed via Lake; **5 subcommands (single/genCore/bibPrepass/headerData/fromDb) + `[Cli|...]` syntax-extension DSL macro + `name VIA handler;` command-handler binding + `FLAGS:`/`ARGS:`/`SUBCOMMANDS:` sectioned DSL + `...name : Type` variadic-prefix for variable-arity positionals + `Parsed` object with `p.flag?`/`p.flag!`/`p.positionalArg!`/`p.variableArgsAs!` method accessors + `IO UInt32` handler return type + `command.validate args` single-method entry-point**) decoded source-direct from:
    * `Main.lean` line 1-3 (`import DocGen4` + `import Lean` + `import Cli` — Lean 4 IMPORT directives; `Cli` is mhuisi/lean4-cli, distributed via Lake)
    * `Main.lean` line 5 (`open DocGen4 DocGen4.DB DocGen4.Output Lean Cli` — Lean 4 OPEN directive imports namespaces unqualified into current scope)
    * `Main.lean` lines 8-13 (`def runHeaderDataCmd (p : Parsed) : IO UInt32 := do let buildDir := match p.flag? "build" with | some dir => dir.as! String | none => ".lake/build" headerDataOutput buildDir return 0` — HANDLER FUNCTION signature `(p : Parsed) : IO UInt32`; `p.flag? "build"` returns `Option Flag`; `dir.as! String` is coercion-with-runtime-check accessor)
    * `Main.lean` lines 154-160 (`def singleCmd := `[Cli| single VIA runSingleCmd; "..." FLAGS: b, build : String; "..." ARGS: module : String; "..." db : String; "..." sourceUri : String; "..." ]` — Lean 4 `[Cli|...]` MACRO; sections `FLAGS:` with `short, long : Type; "help"`, `ARGS:` with `name : Type; "help"`)
    * `Main.lean` lines 167-175 (`def bibPrepassCmd := `[Cli| bibPrepass VIA runBibPrepassCmd; "..." FLAGS: n, none; "..." j, json; "..." b, build : String; "..." ARGS: ...source : String; "..." ]` — BOOLEAN flags (no `: Type`: `n, none`, `j, json`) + VARIADIC positional `...source : String`)
    * `Main.lean` lines 196-205 (`def fromDbCmd := `[Cli| fromDb VIA runFromDbCmd; "..." FLAGS: b, build : String; "..." m, manifest : String; "..." ARGS: db : String; "..." ...modules : String; "..." ]` — second variadic example with `...modules : String`)
    * `Main.lean` lines 211-220 (`def docGenCmd : Cmd := `[Cli| "doc-gen4" VIA runDocGenCmd; ["0.1.0"] "A documentation generator for Lean 4." SUBCOMMANDS: singleCmd; genCoreCmd; bibPrepassCmd; headerDataCmd; fromDbCmd ]` — ROOT COMMAND with SUBCOMMANDS block; each subcommand listed by NAME — Lean 4 resolves def names to subcommand objects)
    * `Main.lean` line 224 (`def main (args : List String) : IO UInt32 := docGenCmd.validate args` — ENTRY POINT; `docGenCmd.validate args` parses argv and runs the dispatched handler)
  - Critical extraction note: doc-gen4 uses **Lean 4 `Cli` library** at Main.lean:3 + 154+ — corpus's **first documented Lean 4 CLI library** + corpus's first documented dependently-typed-proof-assistant CLI library.  `Cli` is mhuisi/lean4-cli (formerly leanprover-community/Cli, now maintained by mhuisi).  Distributed via Lake (Lean 4's package manager).  Distinct from R270 Haskell `System.Console.GetOpt`, R313 Idris 2 `System.GetOpts` (Haskell port — sister dependently-typed language but different design), R290 Racket `command-line` macro (s-expression DSL).  Lean 4's `Cli` is a DSL VIA SYNTAX EXTENSION — Lean 4 lets third-party libraries add CUSTOM DSL SYNTAX via the `syntax` extension mechanism; `[Cli|...]` is a MACRO INVOCATION that parses the DSL and produces a `Cmd` value at compile time.  Resolves the deferred Lean detour from R313 _ttc-converter.  Extends cross-language CLI taxonomy to SIXTY-FIVE library variants: Rust (6), Go (4), Python (3), Crystal 1 OptionParser (R269), Haskell (R270), OCaml 1 Cmdliner (R271), C (R272), Java 1 picocli (R276), Kotlin (R277), Scala (R278), Dart (R279), Ruby (R280), Perl (R281), PHP (R282), Swift (R283), F# (R284), Lua (R285), C# (R286), Nim 1 parseopt (R287), Zig (R288), D (R289), Racket (R290), Common Lisp 1 (R291), Erlang (R292), Tcl (R293), Pharo Smalltalk (R294), Common Lisp 2 clingon (R295), Julia (R296), SWI-Prolog optparse (R297), Pony stdlib cli (R298), Crystal 2 Admiral (R299), V stdlib `flag` (R300), Nim 2 cligen (R301), Clojure 1 babashka.cli (R302), Gleam Glint (R303), Ada GNAT.Command_Line (R304), C++ 1 CLI11 (R305), Elixir Optimus (R306), Vala GLib.OptionContext (R307), Hare stdlib getopt (R308), Standard ML stdlib GetOpt (R309), Fortran M_CLI2 (R310), Guile (ice-9 getopt-long) (R311), Free Pascal TCustomApplication (R312), Idris 2 System.GetOpts (R313), Mercury stdlib getopt_io (R314), Eiffel Gobo AP_PARSER (R315), C++ 2 boost::program_options (R316), Forth/Gforth `next-arg` (R317), Clojure 2 org.clojure/tools.cli (R318), Roc weaver (R319), Chapel `config` keyword (R320), Mojo `alias`-as-spec (R321), Java 2 JCommander (R322), Java 3 args4j (R323), Chicken Scheme `args` egg (R324), OCaml 2 stdlib Arg (R325), **Lean 4 Cli** (R326 — NEW).  **MAJOR PATTERN MILESTONE**: 65 cross-language CLI library variants documented.  Lean 4 joins as the FIRST documented dependently-typed PROOF ASSISTANT.
  - Critical extraction note: doc-gen4 uses **Lean 4 `[Cli|...]` syntax-extension DSL macro pattern** at Main.lean:154+ — `def singleCmd := `[Cli| single VIA runSingleCmd; "..." FLAGS: ... ARGS: ... ]` invokes mhuisi/Cli's MACRO which parses the DSL inside the `[Cli|...]` brackets and produces a `Cmd` value at COMPILE TIME.  Lean 4 lets third-party libraries add CUSTOM DSL SYNTAX via the `syntax` extension mechanism — `[CLI|...]` is a MACRO INVOCATION that the Cli library elaborates.  Distinct from R270 Haskell `System.Console.GetOpt` (constructor list), R311 Guile `(getopt-long args SPEC)` (quoted alist — Lisp macro is DIFFERENT mechanism), R290 Racket `command-line` (also macro DSL but s-expression-based).  Lean 4's approach: LEAN-NATIVE DSL via `syntax` extension + macro elaboration — corpus's **first documented syntax-extension-DSL CLI macro pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: doc-gen4 uses **Lean 4 Cli `name VIA handler;` declaration pattern** at Main.lean:154 et seq — `single VIA runSingleCmd;` binds the command NAME `single` to the IO-returning HANDLER FUNCTION `runSingleCmd : Parsed -> IO UInt32`.  Distinct from R322 JCommander `@Parameter` (annotation-based), R311 Guile `(args:make-option (h help) #:none ... BODY)` (embedded body thunk).  Lean 4 Cli's approach: `VIA` KEYWORD binding command name to typed handler — corpus's **first documented `VIA`-keyword command-handler CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: doc-gen4 uses **Lean 4 Cli `FLAGS:` / `ARGS:` / `SUBCOMMANDS:` section DSL** at Main.lean:154+ — the `[Cli|...]` macro parses three optional sections: `FLAGS:` with `short, long : Type; "help"` (or `short, long; "help"` for booleans), `ARGS:` with `name : Type; "help"` (or `...name : Type; "help"` for variadics), `SUBCOMMANDS:` with named subcommand defs.  Distinct from R313 Idris 2 `MkOpt ['?', 'h'] ["help"] (NoArg Help) "Show help"` (single constructor), R310 Fortran M_CLI2 single-string-packed spec.  Lean 4 Cli's approach: SECTIONED-DSL — corpus's **first documented sectioned-DSL-spec CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: doc-gen4 uses **Lean 4 Cli `...name : Type` variadic-prefix pattern** at Main.lean:175, 205 — `...source : String; "..."` and `...modules : String; "..."` mark positionals as VARIABLE-ARITY (one-or-more values).  The `...` prefix is consumed by the `[Cli|...]` macro and elaborated to a `VariableArgsAs` accessor.  Distinct from R311 Guile `(arg ...)` rest-args (Scheme), R313 Idris 2 `Param.str_list({ name: "packages" })` (typed constructor), R310 Fortran M_CLI2 (comma-separated array via single flag).  Lean 4 Cli's approach: `...` PREFIX FOR VARIADIC POSITIONALS — corpus's **first documented `...`-prefix-for-variadic CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: doc-gen4 uses **Lean 4 Cli `Parsed`-object-method-accessor CLI pattern** at Main.lean:8-13 et seq — `(p : Parsed) : IO UInt32` handler signature; `p.flag? "build"` returns `Option Flag` (None if not passed, Some flag if passed); `p.flag! "build"` raises if not passed; `p.positionalArg! "module" |>.as! String` chained access for required positionals; `p.variableArgsAs! String` for variadics returning `Array String`.  Distinct from R322 JCommander reflection-based binding, R311 Guile `(option-ref options 'build "default")` (alist accessor with default), R313 Idris 2 `let (MkResult opts [] [] []) = getOpt ...` (pattern-match destructure).  Lean 4 Cli's approach: `Parsed` OBJECT with method-style accessors — corpus's **first documented Parsed-object-method-accessor CLI pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: doc-gen4 uses **Lean 4 Cli `command.validate args` entry-point** at Main.lean:224 — `def main (args : List String) : IO UInt32 := docGenCmd.validate args`.  `validate` is a method on `Cmd` that parses argv, runs the matched subcommand's handler, and returns the exit code.  No `try/catch` needed at the call site — `validate` handles errors internally.  Distinct from R322 JCommander `parse(*argv)` (two-call), R325 OCaml `Arg.parse_argv` (mutates refs), R313 Idris 2 `getOpt POLICY DESCS ARGS` (returns MkResult).  Lean 4 Cli's approach: SINGLE METHOD CALL on the root Cmd that does everything — corpus's **first documented validate-method-on-Cmd CLI parser pattern**.
  - Hunt-skip notes: skipped `_lean4` / `_lake` / `_lean` / `_elan` (Lean ecosystem in corpus + blacklisted), `_unison` (DUP with stock zsh).  Probed ATS detour: `vmchale/polyglot` (already in corpus as STUB), `metasepi/c2ats` (Haskell-based, ATS only in examples), `githwxi/ATS-Postiats` (compiler internals), `xanadu-lang/xanadu` (compiler).  Probed Lean 4 ecosystem (resolving deferred R313 Lean detour): `leanprover/verso` (339 stars but HANDWRITTEN parser), `argumentcomputer/yatima` (146 stars but `Cli` commented out), `argumentcomputer/ix` (73 stars but `Cli` commented out), `hhu-adam/lean-i18n` (12 stars but `lake exe i18n` target only), `bddicken/languages` (benchmark code, not CLI), `iQua/days` / `towre676-cloud/tau_crystal` / `s-taiga/fpil-ja` (academic examples).  Chose doc-gen4: 153 GH stars, OFFICIAL Lean 4 documentation generator (leanprover org), produces docs for Lean's mathlib (largest formal math library), 5 subcommands (single/genCore/bibPrepass/headerData/fromDb) + global `--help`/`--version`, clean single-file Main.lean exercising EVERY MAJOR Lean 4 Cli PATTERN (`[Cli|...]` syntax-extension macro, `name VIA handler;` declaration, `FLAGS:`/`ARGS:`/`SUBCOMMANDS:` sectioned DSL, `...name : Type` variadic-prefix for positionals, `Parsed` object with method accessors, `command.validate args` entry-point), real binary distributed via Lake + `lake exe doc-gen4` or standalone build at `.lake/build/bin/doc-gen4`.
  - Blacklist update: `doc-gen4` added.  Corpus count: 28,748 → 28,749 (+1).

- **R325 — gdfuse source-direct (Google Drive FUSE filesystem in OCaml; stdlib `Arg` module)** (1 file new) — pivots from Chicken Scheme `args` egg (R324 massmine) back into the OCaml ecosystem to document the SECOND OCaml CLI library — corpus's **first documented OCaml stdlib `Arg` library** + **second documented OCaml CLI library** (joining R271 OCaml Cmdliner — third-party).  google-drive-ocamlfuse is a FUSE filesystem over Google Drive — mounts your Google Drive as a Linux/macOS filesystem.  Authored by Alessandro Strada (astrada).  **5932 GH stars** (one of the most popular OCaml end-user tools).  Sister tools: rclone (multi-cloud FUSE/sync, in corpus), s3fs-fuse (S3 FUSE), gcsfuse (Google Cloud Storage FUSE).  **MAJOR MILESTONE**: corpus's **first documented OCaml stdlib `Arg` library** — adds OCaml `Arg` (in OCaml stdlib since 1.x in 1990s, ref-cell-as-parser-target, single-dash flag default, type-specific `Arg.Set`/`Arg.Set_string`/`Arg.Set_int` variants, `Arg.Unit`/`Arg.String` thunk-on-match variants, anon-fun callback for positionals, `Arg.Help`/`Arg.Bad` exception-based control flow, `Arg.align` spec-list-help-alignment) to the cross-language CLI taxonomy (64 library variants now).  OCaml expands to 2 documented libraries (Cmdliner + Arg) joining the multi-library club with Rust 6, Go 4, Python 3, Crystal 2, Common Lisp 2, Nim 2, Clojure 2, C++ 2, Java 3.
  - `_gdfuse` (1-stem; astrada/google-drive-ocamlfuse src/gdfuseCli.ml; **OCaml stdlib `Arg` module** — ships with every OCaml install since 1.x, lives at `stdlib/arg.ml` in the compiler; **23 single-dash flags + 1 positional mountpoint + `Arg.align [(NAME, SPEC, HELP); ...]` tuple-with-Arg-variant spec list + type-specific `Arg.Set ref_bool`/`Arg.Set_string ref_str`/`Arg.Set_int ref_int` variants + `Arg.Unit (fun () -> ...)` thunk-on-match variant for side effects + `Arg.String (fun s -> ...)` callback-with-arg variant for custom parsing + `ref` mutable cells as parser binding targets + `Arg.parse_argv ~current:(ref 0) argv arg_specs (fun s -> mountpoint := s) usage` parse with ANON-FUN CALLBACK for POSITIONALS + `Arg.Help`/`Arg.Bad` exception-based control flow**) decoded source-direct from:
    * `src/gdfuseCli.ml` (top of file, implicit) (OCaml's stdlib `Arg` module is in the pervasive `Stdlib` namespace; no explicit `open Arg` needed — just use `Arg.foo`)
    * `src/gdfuseCli.ml` lines 21-46 (`let parse_argv argv = let fs_label = ref GdfuseCommon.default_fs_label in let mountpoint = ref "" in let show_version = ref false in let debug = ref false in ...` — OCaml `ref` MUTABLE CELLS for each parsed value; OCaml is immutable-by-default, `ref` is explicit opt-in)
    * `src/gdfuseCli.ml` line 79 (`let arg_specs = Arg.align [...]` — `Arg.align` is a HELPER that takes a list of `(NAME, SPEC, HELP)` tuples and ALIGNS the help text columns)
    * `src/gdfuseCli.ml` lines 80-83 (`[("-version", Arg.Set show_version, " show version and exit."); ("-verbose", Arg.Set Utils.verbose, " enable verbose logging on stdout. Default is false."); ...]` — OCaml `Arg.Set ref_bool` spec constructor; each entry is a 3-tuple `(string * Arg.spec * string)` = NAME + Arg-variant + HELP)
    * `src/gdfuseCli.ml` line 84 (`( "-debug", Arg.Unit (fun () -> debug := true; Utils.verbose := true), " enable debug mode (implies -verbose). Default is false." );` — `Arg.Unit FN` calls a UNIT->UNIT THUNK when option matches; used to set MULTIPLE refs atomically)
    * `src/gdfuseCli.ml` lines 87-89 (`( "-label", Arg.Set_string fs_label, " ..." );` — `Arg.Set_string ref_string` binds next argv element to the ref)
    * `src/gdfuseCli.ml` line 99 (`("-o", Arg.String parse_mount_options, " specify FUSE mount options.");` — `Arg.String FN` calls STRING->UNIT function for custom parsing; here splits comma-separated FUSE options)
    * `src/gdfuseCli.ml` line 144 (`("-port", Arg.Set_int port, " set loopback port. Default is 8080.");` — `Arg.Set_int ref_int` for integer binding)
    * `src/gdfuseCli.ml` lines 146-151 (`try ignore (Arg.parse_argv ~current:(ref 0) argv arg_specs (fun s -> mountpoint := s) usage); ...` — PARSE INVOCATION; `~current:REF` is an INDEX REF into argv allowing resumable parses; `(fun s -> mountpoint := s)` is the ANON-FUN CALLBACK called for EACH POSITIONAL ARG)
    * `src/gdfuseCli.ml` lines 184-186 (`with | Arg.Help message -> Help message | Arg.Bad message -> Error message` — EXCEPTION-based help/error handling; `Arg.Help` raised on `-help`/`--help`, `Arg.Bad` raised on parse errors or via `raise (Arg.Bad "msg")`)
  - Critical extraction note: gdfuse uses **OCaml stdlib `Arg` module** at gdfuseCli.ml:79+ — corpus's **first documented OCaml stdlib `Arg` library** + **second documented OCaml CLI library** (joining R271 OCaml Cmdliner — third-party).  OCaml `Arg` ships in the OCaml stdlib (lives at `stdlib/arg.ml` in the compiler tree) — has existed since OCaml 1.x in the 1990s.  Distinct from R271 OCaml Cmdliner (third-party library by Daniel Bünzli; uses applicative-style combinators, declarative DSL, auto-generated man pages).  OCaml `Arg` is the LOW-LEVEL stdlib option — ref-cell binding, single-dash flags, exception-based error handling, Arg.align for help formatting.  Extends cross-language CLI taxonomy to SIXTY-FOUR library variants: Rust (6), Go (4), Python (3), Crystal 1 OptionParser (R269), Haskell (R270), **OCaml 1 Cmdliner (R271)**, C (R272), Java 1 picocli (R276), Kotlin (R277), Scala (R278), Dart (R279), Ruby (R280), Perl (R281), PHP (R282), Swift (R283), F# (R284), Lua (R285), C# (R286), Nim 1 parseopt (R287), Zig (R288), D (R289), Racket (R290), Common Lisp 1 (R291), Erlang (R292), Tcl (R293), Pharo Smalltalk (R294), Common Lisp 2 clingon (R295), Julia (R296), SWI-Prolog optparse (R297), Pony stdlib cli (R298), Crystal 2 Admiral (R299), V stdlib `flag` (R300), Nim 2 cligen (R301), Clojure 1 babashka.cli (R302), Gleam Glint (R303), Ada GNAT.Command_Line (R304), C++ 1 CLI11 (R305), Elixir Optimus (R306), Vala GLib.OptionContext (R307), Hare stdlib getopt (R308), Standard ML stdlib GetOpt (R309), Fortran M_CLI2 (R310), Guile (ice-9 getopt-long) (R311), Free Pascal TCustomApplication (R312), Idris 2 System.GetOpts (R313), Mercury stdlib getopt_io (R314), Eiffel Gobo AP_PARSER (R315), C++ 2 boost::program_options (R316), Forth/Gforth `next-arg` (R317), Clojure 2 org.clojure/tools.cli (R318), Roc weaver (R319), Chapel `config` keyword (R320), Mojo `alias`-as-spec (R321), Java 2 JCommander (R322), Java 3 args4j (R323), Chicken Scheme `args` egg (R324), **OCaml 2 stdlib Arg** (R325 — NEW).  **MAJOR PATTERN MILESTONE**: 64 cross-language CLI library variants documented.  OCaml expands to 2 documented libraries (Cmdliner + Arg) joining the multi-library club with Rust 6, Go 4, Python 3, Crystal 2, Common Lisp 2, Nim 2, Clojure 2, C++ 2, Java 3.
  - Critical extraction note: gdfuse uses **OCaml `Arg.align [(NAME, Arg.SPEC_VARIANT, HELP); ...]` tuple-with-Arg-variant CLI pattern** at gdfuseCli.ml:79+ — `Arg.align [("-version", Arg.Set show_version, " show version and exit."); ...]`.  Each spec entry is a 3-TUPLE `(string * Arg.spec * string)`: NAME + Arg-variant + HELP.  Distinct from R322 JCommander `@Parameter(names = ...)` (annotation), R311 Guile `((NAME (single-char #\X) (value #t/#f)))` (quoted alist), R270 Haskell `Option ['X'] ["long"] (NoArg ...) "help"` (constructor with 4 args).  OCaml's approach: TUPLE-OF-(NAME, Arg.SPEC_VARIANT, HELP) for each option — corpus's **first documented tuple-with-Arg-variant-spec CLI pattern**.
