Without this, only the top-level `replibyte --version` works; `replibyte dump --version` would fail.  Documents the **`propagate_version` attribute** — corpus's first documented occurrence.  Useful for CLIs where users may forget the version is at the top level.  Standard practice for CLIs with multiple subcommand levels.
  - Hunt-skip notes: cosmwasm (CosmWasm SDK CLI), surrealdb-server (SurrealDB), edgedb-cli, neon-cli (Postgres serverless), tursodb FRESH candidates for future rounds.  llama.cpp / llamacpp probe for next AI-cluster round.
  - Blacklist: added `replibyte`.
  - Corpus 28,686 → 28,687.

- **R260 — dua source-direct rewrite (disk usage analyzer; platform-specific defaults)** (1 file source-direct rewrite) — pivots from performance benchmarking (R259 hyperfine) into the **filesystem-analysis tooling cluster**.  dua (binary from crate `dua-cli`) is the **disk usage analyzer** — `du`-replacement with interactive TUI navigation, parallel traversal, and human-friendly byte formatting.  Sister to dust, ncdu, fclones (all in corpus).  REWRITES the previously URL-cited `_dua` (was 37 lines incomplete, missing `--count-hard-links/-l`, `--log-file`, `completions`/`config` subcommands, all 7 env vars, the 7 ByteFormat values).
  - `_dua` (1-stem; Byron/dua-cli src/options.rs; clap v4 derive; **4 subcommands + 7 global flags + 7-variant ByteFormat ValueEnum + platform-specific defaults + override_usage + ignore_case + systematic env-var prefix convention**) decoded source-direct from:
    * `options.rs` lines 5-14 (ByteFormat ValueEnum: Metric / Binary / Bytes / GB / Gib / MB / Mib)
    * `options.rs` lines 30-36 (dft_format() runtime default fn via `cfg!(target_vendor = "apple")`)
    * `options.rs` lines 40-44 (DEFAULT_THREADS const with `#[cfg(target_os = "macos")]`)
    * `options.rs` lines 47-103 (Args Parser struct with 7 global flags + override_usage)
    * `options.rs` line 93 (cfg_attr-clap-attribute pairing for Linux-only default_values)
    * `options.rs` lines 105-140 (Command Subcommand enum)
    * `options.rs` lines 142-148 (ConfigCommand: single Edit variant)
  - Critical extraction note: dua uses **`override_usage = "dua [FLAGS] [OPTIONS] [SUBCOMMAND] [INPUT]..."`** at options.rs:49 on the top-level Args Parser struct.  This replaces clap's auto-generated usage line in `--help` output with a custom string.  Documents the **`override_usage` attribute** — useful when the auto-generated usage doesn't capture the intended use (e.g. when subcommand is optional and trailing positionals differ from defaults).  Corpus's **first documented `override_usage` attribute**.  Distinct from `next_line_help` / `help_expected` / `max_term_width` which are also Command builder controls (R259 hyperfine).
  - Critical extraction note: dua uses **platform-specific `const DEFAULT_THREADS`** at options.rs:40-44 via `#[cfg(target_os = "macos")]` and `#[cfg(not(target_os = "macos"))]`.  Plus a runtime `dft_format()` fn at options.rs:30-36 returning `Metric` on Apple (via `cfg!(target_vendor = "apple")`) and `Binary` elsewhere.  Documents **platform-specific default values** via TWO methods: (a) compile-time `const` with `#[cfg]` (works for `default_value_t = CONST`); (b) runtime `cfg!(...)` check inside a fn (works for `default_value_t = fn()`).  Corpus's **first documented platform-specific defaults** — distinct from R249 cairo-profiler's `version = generate_version()` (no platform check) and R257 pop-cli's `about = about_build()` (feature check, not platform check).  Documents the **cfg-vs-cfg!** distinction in Rust: `#[cfg(...)]` is compile-time erasure; `cfg!(...)` is a runtime-constant macro.  Useful for cross-platform CLIs where the optimal default differs by OS.
  - Critical extraction note: dua uses **`#[cfg_attr(target_os = "linux", clap(default_values = &["/proc", "/dev", "/sys", "/run"]))]`** at options.rs:93 on `--ignore-dirs/-i` — the `default_values` ARRAY attribute is applied ONLY on Linux.  On macOS / BSD / Windows, there's no default; `--ignore-dirs` starts empty.  Documents the **platform-specific default_values via cfg_attr-clap-attribute pairing** — the cfg_attr applies the `default_values` attribute conditionally.  Distinct from R255 fuel-core's `#[cfg_attr(feature = "X", clap(flatten))]` (feature-gated flatten); R260 dua does PLATFORM-gated default_values.  Corpus's **first documented platform-gated default_values attribute**.  The `/proc`, `/dev`, `/sys`, `/run` paths are Linux-only mount points that almost always should be excluded from disk usage analysis.
  - Critical extraction note: dua uses **`ignore_case = true`** on `--format/-f` at options.rs:65 — the ValueEnum's case-insensitive matching means `--format METRIC` / `--format metric` / `--format Metric` all work.  Documents the **`ignore_case` ValueEnum attribute** — corpus's first documented occurrence.  Distinct from clap's default ValueEnum behavior (case-sensitive).  Useful when CLI users may type values in mixed case.
  - Critical extraction note: dua uses **7 global flags with systematic `DUA_*` env var prefixes** (`--threads/-t` env DUA_THREADS, `--format/-f` env DUA_FORMAT, `--apparent-size/-A` env DUA_APPARENT_SIZE, `--count-hard-links/-l` env DUA_COUNT_HARD_LINKS, `--stay-on-filesystem/-x` env DUA_STAY_ON_FILESYSTEM, `--ignore-dirs/-i` env DUA_IGNORE_DIRS, `--log-file` env DUA_LOG_FILE).  Documents the **systematic env-var prefix convention** for a CLI's configuration surface — every flag has an env var named `DUA_<FIELD>`.  Distinct from R252 saya's `#[clap(long, env)]` shorthand (auto-derived names) — dua uses EXPLICIT `env = "DUA_FOO"` to match the convention even when it matches the field name (defensive consistency).
  - Critical extraction note: dua's `Args` struct has **`Option<Command>` as the subcommand** at options.rs:52 — running `dua` without a subcommand is valid (defaults to launching the TUI / aggregating cwd, depending on build features).  Plus the `input: Vec<PathBuf>` positional is `global = true` at options.rs:97 — the input paths apply to subcommands too, not just bare `dua`.  Documents the **global = true on Vec<> positional** pattern — `dua interactive /home /root` and `dua /home /root` both work because input is global.  Continues R258 bevy's `global = true` on positional documentation.
  - Hunt-skip notes: helix-term / typetura / ru / komodod / tomcat-native FRESH candidates for future rounds.  cargo-prettyplease is a library not a binary.  zed-editor binary is `zed` (already in corpus).
  - Blacklist: `dua` already present.
  - Corpus 28,686 → 28,686 (REWRITE, no new file — R260 is a major source-direct rewrite of the previously URL-cited 37-line completion into a 230+ line source-direct version).

- **R259 — hyperfine source-direct (command-line benchmarking tool; clap BUILDER API)** (1 file new) — pivots from game development (R258 bevy) into the **performance-measurement tooling cluster**.  hyperfine is the **command-line benchmarking tool** from sharkdp (the bat / fd / vivid author).  Runs commands multiple times, measures statistics, supports parameter sweeps + comparisons.  Sister to bat / fd / hexyl / vivid (all in corpus).  **MAJOR MILESTONE**: corpus's first documented **clap v4 BUILDER API** CLI (every prior round R247-R258 used the clap derive API).
  - `_hyperfine` (1-stem; sharkdp/hyperfine src/cli.rs; clap v4 **BUILDER API** with Command::new() + Arg::new() chained methods; **29 flags + builder-method patterns: ArgAction enum, ValueHint enum, num_args(0..=1) + default_missing_value + require_equals, value_names array, conflicts_with_all + overrides_with + hide + hide_default_value**) decoded source-direct from:
    * `cli.rs` lines 18-25 (Command builder: name + version + next_line_help + hide_possible_values + help_expected + max_term_width)
    * `cli.rs` lines 26-37 (positional `command` Arg: Append + required + NonEmptyStringValueParser + ValueHint::CommandString)
    * `cli.rs` lines 38-156 (12 timing-control flags)
    * `cli.rs` lines 155-202 (parameter-scan/-P with 3 value_names + allow_hyphen_values; parameter-step-size/-D requires parameter-scan; parameter-list/-L conflicts with parameter-scan)
    * `cli.rs` lines 203-225 (--shell/-S with overrides_with-self; `-N` separate-flag-as-alias for --shell=none with conflicts_with_all)
    * `cli.rs` lines 226-238 (--ignore-failure/-i with num_args(0..=1) + default_missing_value + require_equals)
    * `cli.rs` lines 239-281 (--style + --sort + --time-unit with value_parser([...]) restricted-string ValueParser)
    * `cli.rs` lines 282-327 (5 export-* flags with ValueHint::FilePath)
    * `cli.rs` lines 391-408 (2 HIDDEN flags via `.hide(true)`)
  - Critical extraction note: hyperfine uses **clap's BUILDER API** (NOT derive!) — every flag is constructed with `Arg::new("name").long("...").short('x').action(...)` chained method calls.  This is the **corpus's first documented clap v4 builder-API CLI** (every prior round R247-R258 used the derive API).  **MAJOR PATTERN MILESTONE**.  Differences vs derive: (a) no struct/enum definitions, (b) imperative chained-method construction, (c) attributes become method calls (`.action(ArgAction::Set)` ≈ `#[arg(action = ArgAction::Set)]`), (d) flag names defined inline by `.long("foo").short('f')`.  Used when CLI has complex builder logic or generated args (less common in modern Rust CLIs but still in production at sharkdp).  Documents the **derive-vs-builder dichotomy** in clap v4 — both APIs are first-class citizens.
  - Critical extraction note: hyperfine uses **`ArgAction` enum** to specify flag behavior — corpus's first documented `ArgAction` usage: `ArgAction::Append` (Vec<String>, repeatable), `ArgAction::Set` (single value, last-wins), `ArgAction::SetTrue` (bool flag, no value).  Equivalent derive form: derive auto-detects from the field type (Vec<T> → Append, T → Set, bool → SetTrue).  Builder form requires explicit `.action(...)`.  Documents the **ArgAction enum** pattern — clap v4's unified flag-behavior model (clap v3 used separate `Arg::multiple` / `Arg::takes_value` flags).
  - Critical extraction note: hyperfine uses **`ValueHint::CommandString`** + **`ValueHint::FilePath`** — `.value_hint(ValueHint::CommandString)` tells clap the value should be auto-completed as a shell command string; `.value_hint(ValueHint::FilePath)` as a file path.  Documents **clap's ValueHint enum** for shell-completion generators (clap_complete reads value_hint to emit appropriate shell completions).  Corpus's **first documented ValueHint usage**.  Other ValueHint variants: AnyPath, DirPath, ExecutablePath, Username, Hostname, Url, EmailAddress, etc.  Important for clap_complete-generated completions but the source completion itself (this file) implements the hint manually via `_files` and `_command_names`.
  - Critical extraction note: hyperfine's `--ignore-failure`/`-i` uses **`num_args(0..=1) + default_missing_value("all-non-zero") + require_equals(true)`** at cli.rs:226-238 — a 3-way parameter behavior: `-i` → uses default_missing_value "all-non-zero"; `-i=all-non-zero` → explicit value; `-i=1,2,3` → comma-separated exit codes; `-i 1,2,3` → REJECTED (require_equals).  Documents the **optional-value flag pattern**: `.num_args(0..=1)` + `.default_missing_value("X")` + `.require_equals(true)`.  Corpus's **first documented default_missing_value + num_args(0..=1) + require_equals triple**.  Useful for "flag with optional value" semantics where the bare flag has a meaningful default but explicit values are also accepted.
  - Critical extraction note: hyperfine's `--parameter-scan`/`-P` takes **THREE value names** `["VAR", "MIN", "MAX"]` at cli.rs:160 — `.value_names([...])` declares a multi-value flag where each value has a distinct display name.  Used as `hyperfine -P threads 1 8 'make -j {threads}'`.  Documents the **multi-value-name flag pattern** — clap v4 builder uses `.value_names([...])`; derive uses `#[arg(num_args = 3, value_names = ["VAR", "MIN", "MAX"])]`.  Distinct from R254 subxt's positional FileOrUrl (which parses a single string as path-or-URL via FromStr).  Corpus's **first documented multi-value-name flag**.
  - Critical extraction note: hyperfine's `-N` flag is an **alias for `--shell=none`** at cli.rs:219-225 — but implemented as a SEPARATE `Arg::new("no-shell")` with `conflicts_with_all(["shell", "debug-mode"])`.  NOT an `.alias()` on `--shell`.  When `-N` is set, the code branches on `matches.get_flag("no-shell")` and treats it as `--shell=none`.  Documents the **separate-flag-as-alias pattern** when the alias has DIFFERENT semantic constraints (here: also conflicts with --debug-mode).  Distinct from R250 sozo's `#[command(alias = "sn")]` (which is true clap-level aliasing).  Useful when an "alias" needs its own conflicts/requires semantics.
  - Critical extraction note: hyperfine's `--shell` uses **`.overrides_with("shell")`** at cli.rs:209 — re-specifying `--shell=A --shell=B` makes B win (last-one-wins).  Without this, clap errors on duplicate flags.  Documents the **overrides_with-self pattern** for "last-flag-wins" semantics.  Corpus's **first documented `overrides_with` attribute**.  Distinct from `conflicts_with` (which forbids both) and `requires` (which mandates pairing).
  - Critical extraction note: hyperfine has **2 HIDDEN flags** via `.hide(true)` at cli.rs:397 + 406: `--min-benchmarking-time` and `--debug-mode`.  These flags work but don't appear in `--help`.  Documents the **hidden-flag pattern** for experimental / internal / unstable flags that are functional but not yet stabilized for public use.  Source comment at cli.rs:391-392 explains: "This option is hidden for now, as it is not yet clear yet if we want to 'stabilize' this".  Corpus's **first documented `.hide(true)` attribute**.  Plus `.hide_default_value(true)` on `--sort` at cli.rs:261 — the default IS `"auto"` (shown via `default_value("auto")`) but the `(default: auto)` text is HIDDEN in --help.  Documents `hide_default_value` distinct from `.hide(true)` (which hides the entire flag).
  - Hunt-skip notes: helix-editor (Helix modal editor; binary name `hx` — check existing).  jujutsu-vcs (Jujutsu VCS) deferred.  sapling-scm / fossil-scm (other VCS) deferred.
  - Blacklist: added `hyperfine`.
  - Corpus 28,685 → 28,686.

- **R258 — bevy source-direct rewrite (Bevy game engine CLI)** (1 file source-direct rewrite) — pivots from blockchain (R254 subxt / R257 pop-cli) into the **game development CLI cluster**.  bevy (binary name from `#[command(name = "bevy")]`) is the **official CLI for the Bevy data-driven Rust game engine**.  Sister to dx (Dioxus CLI; already in corpus), wrangler (Cloudflare; in corpus).  REWRITES the previously URL-cited `_bevy` (was 63 lines incomplete) into a source-direct version (270+ lines) with full 5-subcommand + per-variant feature-gated subcommand-of-subcommand coverage.
  - `_bevy` (1-stem; TheBevyFlock/bevy_cli src/; clap v4 derive; **5 top-level subcommands + visible_alias + per-variant after_help + inline struct variant + global=true positional + conflicts_with_all + allow_hyphen_values**) decoded source-direct from:
    * `bin/main.rs` lines 70-81 (Cli Parser struct: subcommand + global --verbose/-v + runtime after_help() + `next_line_help(false)` + clap_cargo styles)
    * `bin/main.rs` lines 108-132 (Subcommands enum: 5 variants including 2 with `visible_alias` and 4 with runtime `after_help = fn()` calls)
    * `bin/main.rs` lines 134-185 (3 after_help fn-builders)
    * `src/commands/new/args.rs` lines 9-48 (NewArgs)
    * `src/commands/build/args.rs` lines 21-34 + 149-174 (BuildArgs + BuildWebArgs)
    * `src/commands/run/args.rs` lines 19-38 + 137-173 (RunArgs + RunWebArgs)
    * `src/commands/lint/args.rs` lines 10-32 (LintArgs with `global = true` on POSITIONAL) + 95-104 (LintSubcommands: Web/Install/List)
  - Critical extraction note: bevy uses **`visible_alias` (NOT `alias`)** at main.rs:113 + 116 on Build → `b` and Run → `r`.  Documents the **subtle distinction** between `#[command(alias = "X")]` (HIDDEN — works at the CLI but not shown in --help) and `#[command(visible_alias = "X")]` (SHOWN in --help as `build, b` and `run, r`).  Plus their array forms `aliases = [..]` / `visible_aliases = [..]`.  Distinct from R250 sozo + R257 pop-cli which both use plain `alias` (hidden).  Corpus's **first documented `visible_alias` attribute** — completes the **4 clap alias variants taxonomy**: (a) `alias = "X"` (hidden single), (b) `aliases = [..]` (hidden array), (c) `visible_alias = "X"` (shown single), (d) `visible_aliases = [..]` (shown array).  4-pattern taxonomy NOW COMPLETE.
  - Critical extraction note: bevy uses **per-variant `#[command(after_help = fn_name())]`** on 3 of 5 subcommands (Run, Lint, Completions) at main.rs:117, 122, 130.  Each variant has its own fn-builder (`run_after_help`, `lint_after_help`, `completions_after_help`) returning a `String` with examples specific to that subcommand.  Plus a TOP-LEVEL `after_help = after_help()` at main.rs:71 returning a resources list (URLs to Bevy website, repo, CLI docs).  Documents the **per-variant after_help pattern** — distinct from R247 sncast's single `after_long_help` at the top level only.  Allows `--help` for `bevy run` to show different examples than `--help` for `bevy lint`.  Corpus's **first documented per-variant after_help with runtime fn-builders**.
  - Critical extraction note: bevy uses **inline struct variant** for `Completions { shell: clap_complete::Shell }` at main.rs:131 — NO separate `CompletionsArgs` struct, the variant directly takes a single named field.  Documents the **inline-struct-variant pattern** in clap derive — when a subcommand needs ONLY one arg, you can put the field inline in the enum variant without defining a struct.  Distinct from the typical `Subcommand(SubcommandArgs)` tuple-variant pattern (R250 sozo / R256 fuel-core-keygen / R257 pop-cli).  Corpus's **first documented inline-struct-variant** clap derive pattern.  Useful for terse single-arg subcommand definitions.
  - Critical extraction note: bevy uses **`last = true, name = "ARGS"`** at new/args.rs:46, run/args.rs:36, lint/args.rs:30 to mark the trailing-positional `Vec<String>` that captures args AFTER `--`.  E.g. `bevy new myapp -- --foo` passes `--foo` to cargo-generate; `bevy run -- --bar` passes `--bar` to the Bevy app.  Plus lint's `forward_args` is **`#[clap(last = true, name = "ARGS", global = true)]`** — `global = true` on a POSITIONAL!  Most CLIs use `global = true` only on FLAGS.  Documents the **`global = true` on positional args** pattern — means the positional applies to subcommands too.  Corpus's **first documented `global = true` on a positional**.  Useful for "args to forward to underlying tool" patterns where the positional should propagate regardless of subcommand depth.
  - Critical extraction note: bevy uses **`conflicts_with_all = ["revision", "tag"]`** at new/args.rs:32, 36, 40 — a 3-way clean mutex (`--branch`, `--tag`, `--revision` pick one) implemented via the `conflicts_with_all` ARRAY attribute (single attribute per flag declares ALL incompatible siblings).  Distinct from R249 cairo-profiler's repeated per-flag `conflicts_with` (3 attributes for 3-way mutex) and from R204's `#[group(multiple = false)]` (declarative group).  Corpus's **first documented `conflicts_with_all` array attribute** — completes the **3 mutex patterns taxonomy**: (a) per-pair `conflicts_with` (R255 fuel-core single pair), (b) per-flag `conflicts_with_all = [..]` (R258 bevy), (c) `#[group(multiple = false)]` (R250 sozo).  3-pattern taxonomy NOW COMPLETE.
  - Critical extraction note: bevy uses **`allow_hyphen_values = true`** at build/args.rs:163 + run/args.rs:167 on `--wasm-opt Vec<String>` to allow wasm-opt's own dash-prefixed flags to pass through.  Without this, clap would interpret `--O3` as a missing clap flag.  Documents the **`allow_hyphen_values` pattern** — necessary when a flag's VALUES contain leading dashes (e.g. forwarding flags to a downstream tool).  Corpus's **first documented `allow_hyphen_values` attribute**.  Distinct from `last = true` (which captures all trailing positionals AFTER `--`) — `allow_hyphen_values` operates on a SINGLE flag's values.
  - Critical extraction note: bevy uses **`requires = "create_packed_bundle"`** at build/args.rs:168 on `--bundle-dir` — declarative dependency at the FIELD level (BundleDir requires the Bundle flag to be set).  Distinct from R248 cairo-coverage's `requires = "unstable"` (which gates 2 flags behind 1).  Continues the corpus's documentation of clap's `requires=` flag-level dependency attribute.
  - Hunt-skip notes: chain-spec-builder / substrate-node-template / moonbeam (Substrate ecosystem) deferred.  uv (Astral Python) — 8337 lines too large for single round.  tangle (Substrate) deferred.  surrealdb-rs / iceoryx-ext (newer) probe candidates.
  - Blacklist: `bevy` already present.
  - Corpus 28,685 → 28,685 (REWRITE, no new file — R258 is a major source-direct rewrite of the previously URL-cited 63-line completion into a 270+ line source-direct version).

- **R257 — pop-cli source-direct (r0gue Polkadot dapp/parachain CLI)** (2-stem new) — pivots from the Fuel cluster (R255/R256) back to the **Polkadot ecosystem** (extending R254 subxt).  pop-cli (binary `pop`) is the **r0gue Polkadot all-in-one development CLI** — install toolchains, scaffold parachains/pallets/contracts, build/test/deploy/upgrade/verify parachains and ink! smart contracts, fork live chains for local testing, hash data with chain hash algorithms, convert between Polkadot/Ethereum address formats.
  - `_pop-cli` (2-stem: `pop-cli` + `pop`; r0gue-io/pop-cli crates/pop-cli/src/; clap v4 derive; **14 top-level subcommands + 11 single-letter aliases + 4 feature-gated variants + per-variant value_parser restrictions**) decoded source-direct from:
    * `main.rs` lines 115-123 (Cli Parser struct: subcommand + global `--json` flag)
    * `commands/mod.rs` lines 44-92 (Command Subcommand enum: 14 variants; 11 with single-letter aliases; up has 2-array aliases ["u", "deploy"]; 4 feature-gated)
    * `commands/mod.rs` lines 95-115 (about_build() + about_up() runtime cfg-cascading about-text fns)
    * `commands/hash.rs` lines 27-77 (HashArgs + Command enum: 4 hash algorithms with per-algorithm bit-length restrictions)
    * `commands/completion.rs` lines 22-37 (CompletionShell ValueEnum mirroring clap_complete::Shell + 5 variants)
    * `commands/completion.rs` lines 50-60 (CompletionArgs with BOTH positional `shell` AND `--shell` flag with `conflicts_with`)
    * `commands/upgrade.rs` lines 20-31 (UpgradeArgs)
  - Critical extraction note: pop-cli uses **single-letter aliases on 11 of 14 subcommands** for muscle-memory shorthand: install→i, new→n, build→b, call→c, up→u (+ "deploy" alias), upgrade→ug, test→t, hash→h, clean→C (capital), convert→cv, verify→v, fork→f.  Documents **clap's `#[clap(alias = "X")]` and `#[clap(aliases = ["X", "Y"])]` at scale** — one CLI applying alias-shortcut convention systematically across subcommands.  Note: `--C` (capital) avoids collision with `-c` (call's alias).  Plus `up` uses ARRAY-form `#[clap(aliases = ["u", "deploy"])]` for 2 aliases.  Extends the corpus's subcommand-alias documentation (R240+, R250 sozo's 2-depth aliases).  **Most-aliases-per-CLI in the corpus so far** (11 single-letter aliases).
  - Critical extraction note: pop-cli's `Command` enum has **4 different feature-gating membership patterns** for its 14 variants: (a) `#[cfg(any(feature = "chain", feature = "contract"))]` — Install/New/Call/Up; (b) `#[cfg(feature = "chain")]` — Bench/Fork; (c) `#[cfg(feature = "contract")]` — Verify; (d) unconditional — Build/Upgrade/Test/Hash/Clean/Convert/Completion.  Documents the **multi-feature OR-gated variant pattern** — `#[cfg(any(feature = "A", feature = "B"))]` makes the variant present if EITHER feature is on (not requiring both).  Distinct from R250 sozo's single-feature `#[cfg(feature = "walnut")]` and R254 subxt's single-feature field gating.  Pop-cli has **4 different feature-set memberships** for its 14 variants — a production example of conditional CLI surface based on build features.  Corpus's first documented **4-pattern feature-set membership** in one CLI.
  - Critical extraction note: pop-cli uses **runtime `about = about_build()` + `about = about_up()` fns** at commands/mod.rs:58 + 64 — each returns a different `&'static str` based on which features are enabled at compile time via cfg-cascading returns (e.g. `(chain && contract)` → "Build a parachain, chain specification, smart contract or Rust package."; `(chain && !contract)` → "Build a parachain, chain specification or Rust package."; etc.).  Documents the **cfg-cascading about-text fn pattern** — the help message dynamically reflects which features are enabled.  Plus the `about_build()` fn uses `#[cfg(all(...))]` for combinatorial cfg matching (not `#[cfg(any(...))]`).  Distinct from R249 cairo-profiler's runtime `version = generate_version()` and R256 fuel-core-keygen's runtime `default_value = trait::FQN::method()`.  Documents the corpus's **3 runtime-fn-call clap-attribute patterns**: version, default_value, about.  Continues the corpus's runtime-fn-call documentation.
  - Critical extraction note: pop-cli's `hash` sub-subcommand uses **`value_parser = SupportedLengths::new([N,N,..])`** at hash.rs:43-77 — a runtime-constructed ValueParser that restricts the bit-length arg to specific values per hash algorithm: blake2→64/128/256/512, keccak→256/512, sha2→256, twox→64/128/256.  Documents the **per-variant value_parser restriction pattern** — different sibling subcommands accept different value sets for the same-named positional arg (`LENGTH`).  Bonus: TwoX uses BOTH `alias = "xx"` AND `name = "twox"` — the variant Rust name is `TwoX` but the CLI name is `twox` (since `name = "twox"` overrides the auto-rename).  Documents the **simultaneous name + alias override** pattern — rename the canonical CLI name AND add an alias.  Corpus's first documented simultaneous `name=` + `alias=` override.
  - Critical extraction note: pop-cli's `completion` subcommand offers **2 mutually-exclusive ways to specify shell** at completion.rs:50-60: positional `pop completion zsh` AND flag `pop completion --shell zsh`.  The flag form has `conflicts_with = "shell"` (the positional's clap-id).  Documents the **positional-or-flag dual-input pattern** — useful for scripts that prefer explicit `--flag value` form over positional args.  Most CLIs pick one; pop-cli supports both for ergonomic flexibility.  Corpus's first documented positional-or-flag dual-input pattern.
  - Hunt-skip notes: uv (Astral Python package manager) deferred — 8337 lines of CLI definitions; ships its own completions and would require multi-round effort.  zombienet (Substrate multi-chain devnet) deferred.  chain-spec-builder + substrate-node-template (Substrate ecosystem) deferred.
  - Blacklist: added `pop-cli`.
  - Corpus 28,684 → 28,685.

- **R256 — fuel-core-keygen source-direct (Fuel Network keygen utility)** (1 file new) — extends the R255 Fuel Network cluster.  fuel-core-keygen is the **Fuel Network keygen utility** — generates secret/public keypairs for configuring fuel-core (block production keys + libp2p peering keys).  Sister to fuel-core (R255 node) and fuelup (R253 installer).  Continues the **Fuel Network cluster**: fuelup + fuel-core + fuel-core-keygen + forc.
  - `_fuel-core-keygen` (1-stem; FuelLabs/fuel-core bin/keygen/src/main.rs + crates/keygen/src/lib.rs; clap v4 derive; **enum-as-direct-Parser + ValueEnum with CLI-vs-output name discrepancy + runtime default via trait FQN**) decoded source-direct from:
    * `bin/keygen/src/main.rs` lines 19-34 (ParseSecret Args struct: positional `secret` + --pretty/-p + --key-type/-k)
    * `bin/keygen/src/main.rs` lines 37-50 (NewKey Args struct)
    * `bin/keygen/src/main.rs` lines 53-58 (Command enum used as TOP-LEVEL Parser)
    * `crates/keygen/src/lib.rs` lines 27-33 (KeyType ValueEnum with 2 variants: BlockProduction default / Peering)
    * `crates/keygen/src/lib.rs` lines 35-42 (From<KeyType> for &'static str)
  - Critical extraction note: fuel-core-keygen uses **`#[derive(Parser)]` directly on an enum at the TOP level** at main.rs:53-58 — `pub(crate) enum Command { New(NewKey), Parse(ParseSecret) }`.  No outer `struct Cli { #[clap(subcommand)] command: Command }` wrapper.  Documents the **enum-as-Parser shortcut** — when the entire CLI is just "pick a subcommand", you can derive Parser on the enum directly and skip the wrapper struct.  clap derive figures out that an enum-Parser means "this is the subcommand set".  Corpus's **first documented enum-as-direct-Parser pattern** — distinct from the usual `struct Cli { #[clap(subcommand)] command: Enum }` pattern (used in every prior round R247-R255).  Useful when there are NO global args (since global args require a wrapping struct).  Most concise form of multi-subcommand clap derive CLI.
  - Critical extraction note: fuel-core-keygen's `KeyType` ValueEnum has a **DISCREPANCY between CLI value name and JSON output name** for the `Peering` variant: CLI is `--key-type peering` (clap_derive auto-emits kebab-case of the variant ident); JSON is `"p2p"` (via the `From<KeyType> for &'static str` impl at lib.rs:35-42).  So `fuel-core-keygen new --key-type peering` outputs JSON with `"key_type": "p2p"`.  Documents the **CLI-vs-output name discrepancy** in ValueEnum-driven flags.  Subtle gotcha: users may grep for `"peering"` in output expecting to find their flag's effect and come up empty.  Distinct from the usual ValueEnum pattern where the auto-derived CLI name and the Display/Serialize name align.  Possibly intentional (peering is the conceptual term; p2p is the implementation acronym) but worth surfacing.  Corpus's **first documented ValueEnum CLI-vs-output name skew** — distinct from R243-style aliases (which keep all names consistent) and from R249 cairo-profiler's NonZero* types (which have neither CLI nor output names).
  - Critical extraction note: fuel-core-keygen uses **`default_value = <KeyType as std::convert::Into<&'static str>>::into(KeyType::BlockProduction)`** at main.rs:31 + main.rs:47 — runtime evaluation of the default using an explicit trait method call via fully-qualified syntax.  Verbose but type-safe (compile-time checks the trait impl exists).  Documents the **runtime default via trait fully-qualified syntax** — distinct from R249 cairo-profiler's `version = generate_version()` (function call) and from typical `default_value = "block-production"` (string literal).  When the default string needs to derive from the type's serialization logic, this pattern guarantees the default and the ValueEnum stay in sync if the type's serialization changes.  Documents the corpus's **dynamic default-value patterns**: (a) string literal (most common), (b) free function call (R249), (c) trait fully-qualified syntax (R256), (d) const expression (`default_value_t = X` where X is a const).
  - Hunt-skip notes: fuel-core-client (the GraphQL test client; bigger surface) deferred.  zombienet (Substrate multi-chain devnet) deferred.  pop-cli / chain-spec-builder / substrate-node-template (Substrate ecosystem) deferred.
  - Blacklist: added `fuel-core-keygen`.
  - Corpus 28,683 → 28,684.

- **R255 — fuel-core source-direct (Fuel Network node implementation)** (1 file new) — returns to the **Fuel Network cluster** to complete it.  fuel-core is the **Fuel Network node implementation** — the Rust client that participates in Fuel's parallel-execution UTXO blockchain network.  Sister to forc (Fuel contract compiler; in corpus) and fuelup (Fuel toolchain manager; R253).  Completes the **Fuel Network cluster**: fuelup (R253 installer), fuel-core (R255 node), forc (existing compiler).
  - `_fuel-core` (1-stem; FuelLabs/fuel-core bin/fuel-core/src/cli/; clap v4 derive; **5 top-level subcommands + run with 30+ direct flags + 4-deep snapshot tree + cfg_attr-clap-attribute pairing**) decoded source-direct from:
    * `cli.rs` lines 44-68 (Opt + Fuel enum: 5 variants; 3 cfg-gated by `rocksdb`; one with nested `#[clap(subcommand)]`)
    * `cli/run.rs` lines 130-343 (run::Command with 30+ direct flags + 10+ flatten groups; many `#[cfg_attr(feature = "X", clap(flatten))]` feature-gated flattens)
    * `cli/snapshot.rs` lines 32-65 (snapshot::Command + 4-deep subcommand tree)
    * `cli/snapshot.rs` lines 70-86 (Encoding enum with `#[cfg(feature = "parquet")]` on Parquet variant)
    * `cli/snapshot.rs` lines 98-105 (EncodingCommand wrapper enum)
    * `cli/snapshot.rs` line 128 (`#[command(arg_required_else_help = true)]` on Contract)
    * `cli/rollback.rs` lines 21-50 (rollback::Command)
    * `cli/fee_contract.rs` lines 10-20 (fee_contract::Command with positional Address + --output/-o + --force/-f)
    * `cli/archive.rs` lines 7-47 (archive::Command Subcommand: Backup/Restore)
  - Critical extraction note: fuel-core uses **`#[cfg_attr(feature = "X", clap(flatten))]` paired with `#[cfg(feature = "X")]`** at run.rs:291-314 on multiple fields (relayer_args, p2p_args, sync_args, shared_sequencer_args, pre_confirmation_signature_service_args).  This is a **2-layer cfg gating** where: (1) `#[cfg(feature = "X")]` makes the FIELD itself absent when feature is off, AND (2) `#[cfg_attr(feature = "X", clap(flatten))]` conditionally applies the `clap(flatten)` attribute ONLY when feature is on.  Why both?  Pedantic clarity — `cfg_attr` would suffice alone IF the field is conditionally present.  The pair documents intent: "this attribute applies only when this field exists".  Documents the **paired-cfg gating pattern** for clap derive — distinct from R251 torii's struct-level `#[cfg(feature = "server")]` and R254 subxt's single-field gating.  This is **field-level cfg gating + cfg_attr-clap-attribute pairing** at production scale.  Documents the corpus's **most defensive cfg pattern** for cleanly compiling away optional flatten groups.
  - Critical extraction note: fuel-core uses **`rename_all = "kebab-case"`** at cli.rs:49 on the top-level `#[clap(...)]` attribute.  This is clap's **global flag-name renaming policy** — converts every field's snake_case identifier to kebab-case in the CLI (e.g. `service_name` → `--service-name`).  Distinct from per-flag explicit `long = "..."` which fuel-core ALSO uses extensively (redundant in many places — `long = "service-name"` is the same as the auto-derived name).  Documents **clap's rename_all policy** — useful for avoiding manual `long = "..."` on every flag while keeping CLI naming consistent.  4 valid rename_all values: `"camelCase"`, `"kebab-case"`, `"PascalCase"`, `"SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE"`, `"snake_case"`.  Corpus's **first documented `rename_all = "kebab-case"` attribute**.
  - Critical extraction note: fuel-core's `snapshot` subcommand has a **4-deep subcommand tree**: (1) `fuel-core snapshot [--db-path X] [--output-directory Y]`, (2) `everything` | `contract`, (3) `everything` has `Option<EncodingCommand>` = `encoding`, (4) `encoding` has `Encoding` = `json` | `parquet [--group-size N] [--compression-level N]`.  Documents the **wrapper-enum-for-nested-subcommand** pattern: `EncodingCommand` exists ONLY to wrap `Encoding` so clap derive can nest it as a subcommand inside `everything`.  Without the wrapper, clap derive can't nest a bare enum.  Corpus's **first documented clap derive wrapper-enum nesting pattern**.  Plus `#[command(arg_required_else_help = true)]` on Contract at snapshot.rs:128 — when no args provided, show help instead of erroring on missing required arg.  Corpus's **first documented `arg_required_else_help` attribute**.
  - Critical extraction note: fuel-core uses **`conflicts_with = "consensus_key"`** at run.rs:252 on `--consensus-aws-kms` — declarative mutex (the AWS KMS alternative to providing a raw consensus key).  Plus `env = "CONSENSUS_KEY_SECRET"` (explicit rename to a more-descriptive env var name).  Documents the **single-pair conflicts_with mutex** in clap derive — distinct from R249 cairo-profiler's 3-way conflicts_with mutex via repeated attribute and R204's `#[group(multiple=false)]` declarative-mutex group.  3rd documented mutex pattern: (a) `conflicts_with` per-pair, (b) `#[group(multiple=false)]` for N-way, (c) `ArgGroup` (declarative).
  - Critical extraction note: fuel-core uses **`#[allow(clippy::large_enum_variant)]`** on the Fuel enum at cli.rs:56 — same lint-suppression as R252 saya (instead of R250 sozo's Box<XxxArgs> approach).  fuel-core's Fuel enum has 5 variants, all relatively small Args structs, so allow-lint is appropriate.  Documents the corpus's enum-size-handling taxonomy in another production CLI.
  - Hunt-skip notes: fuel-core run has 10+ flatten groups (gas-price, tx-pool, tx-status-manager, graphql, relayer, p2p, sync, rpc, pre-confirmation, shared-sequencer, profiling) each living in its own module — completion includes only the 30+ direct flags; the flatten groups' individual flag enumeration is deferred.  fuel-core-client (the CLI client subcommand binary) deferred.  keygen (fuel-core-keygen) deferred.
  - Blacklist: added `fuel-core`.
  - Corpus 28,682 → 28,683.

- **R254 — subxt source-direct (Substrate/Polkadot client codegen CLI)** (1 file new) — pivots from Fuel (R253) to the **Substrate/Polkadot cluster**.  subxt is the **Substrate/Polkadot client codegen CLI** — generates type-safe Rust client code for any Substrate-based chain (Polkadot, Kusama, Astar, etc.) from on-chain runtime metadata.  Major **Substrate ecosystem tooling** — every Substrate dapp builder uses subxt codegen to emit Rust bindings for their target chain.  Sister tools already in corpus: polkadot, pop.
  - `_subxt` (1-stem; paritytech/subxt cli/src/; clap v4 derive; **7 top-level subcommands + shared FileOrUrl Args struct + 3-deep explore tree + field-level cfg gating**) decoded source-direct from:
    * `main.rs` lines 13-22 (Command Parser enum: 7 top-level subcommands)
    * `utils.rs` lines 21-42 (FileOrUrl shared Args struct flattened into 4 commands; impl FromStr at lines 44-65 enables positional-mode parsing)
    * `commands/metadata.rs` lines 14-41 (Opts: FileOrUrl flatten + 5 fields)
    * `commands/codegen.rs` lines 21-81 (Opts: FileOrUrl flatten + 12 fields incl. 3 custom value_parser validators)
    * `commands/compatibility.rs` lines 17-44 (Opts: 4 direct fields; --nodes accepts Vec<Url>)
    * `commands/diff.rs` lines 25-35 (Opts: TWO POSITIONAL FileOrUrl args + --allow-insecure)
    * `commands/explore/mod.rs` lines 107-138 (3-deep subcommand tree)
    * `commands/explore/pallets/mod.rs` lines 26-30 (PalletSubcommand: Calls/Constants/Storage/Events)
    * `commands/chain_spec/mod.rs` lines 14-38 (Opts with `#[cfg(feature = "chain-spec-pruning")]` on SINGLE FIELD)
  - Critical extraction note: subxt uses **`FileOrUrl` flattened struct** at utils.rs:21-42 — a shared Args struct with `--url <URL>`, `--file <PATH>`, `--version`, `--at-block <HASH>` flags.  Flattened into 4 of 7 commands (metadata, codegen, diff, explore) via `#[command(flatten)] file_or_url: FileOrUrl`.  Documents the **shared-args-struct pattern** for DRY across subcommands — each command gets identical flag names, help text, and validation rules without duplication.  Distinct from R251 torii's option-group flattening (which uses `next_help_heading` groups) — FileOrUrl is a single 4-flag mini-struct shared across commands, not a help-section organizer.  Corpus's **first documented small-shared-Args-struct flatten** pattern.
  - Critical extraction note: subxt's `diff` command takes **TWO POSITIONAL `FileOrUrl` args** at diff.rs:29-31 (`metadata_or_url_1: FileOrUrl, metadata_or_url_2: FileOrUrl`).  Plus `FileOrUrl` has an `impl FromStr` at utils.rs:44-65 that parses a string as either a path-or-stdin OR a URL.  When `FileOrUrl` is a POSITIONAL (not flattened), clap uses FromStr instead of the inner field structure — so `subxt diff foo.scale wss://rpc.polkadot.io` works with 2 bare positionals.  Documents the **dual-mode Args struct** — flattens as multi-flag when nested, parses as single-positional when bare.  This is the **corpus's first documented dual-mode Args struct via impl FromStr**.  Allows the same struct to serve two interface styles within one CLI: form-style (--url=X --file=Y) for explicit-config commands and positional-style (X Y) for shorthand commands.
  - Critical extraction note: subxt's `explore` command has a **3-deep subcommand tree**: `subxt explore [pallet|api] <name> [calls|constants|storage|events]`.  The structure uses `Option<PalletOrRuntimeApi>` (so `subxt explore` alone lists pallets), then `Option<PalletSubcommand>` inside PalletOpts (so `subxt explore pallet System` describes the System pallet without requiring a verb).  Documents the **Option<Subcommand>-as-progressive-discovery pattern** — `Option<X>` for a subcommand makes it OPTIONAL; clap surfaces the next-level help when omitted instead of erroring.  Corpus's **first documented 3-deep clap subcommand tree with Option<> progressive disclosure**.  Used as a CLI-as-interactive-discovery tool — the user types `subxt explore` to see available pallets, then `subxt explore pallet System` to see what's in System, then `subxt explore pallet System calls` to see all calls.  Each level narrows interactively.
  - Critical extraction note: subxt's `chain-spec` command uses **`#[cfg(feature = "chain-spec-pruning")]` on a SINGLE FLAG** at chain_spec/mod.rs:28-30 — only the `--state-root-hash` flag is feature-gated, while the rest of Opts is unconditionally compiled.  Distinct from R201/R250 feature-gated subcommand VARIANTS and R251 torii feature-gated option GROUPS — this is **field-level cfg gating** within a struct.  When the feature is disabled, the entire field is absent (the struct has 3 fields instead of 4).  Documents the **field-level feature-gating pattern** — the most granular cfg unit in clap derive.  Completes the corpus's **feature-gating granularity taxonomy**: (a) crate-level (Cargo features), (b) variant-level (R201/R250 sozo Walnut), (c) struct/group-level (R251 torii server-feature option groups), (d) field-level (R254 subxt chain-spec-pruning), (e) parser-level via requires= (R248 cairo-coverage), (f) runtime body-bail (R225 turbo).  6-layer granularity taxonomy NOW COMPLETE.
  - Critical extraction note: subxt uses **`validate_url_security` runtime validator** at every command's `run()` fn — called AFTER clap parsing, BEFORE business logic, to reject ws:// or http:// URLs unless `--allow-insecure`/`-a` is set.  This is cross-cutting validation (every command that takes a URL repeats the check at its run-fn entry).  Documents the **runtime-cross-cutting-validation pattern** — distinct from clap's `value_parser` (which validates at parse time per-arg).  The runtime check is shared via a free function in utils.rs that every command's run fn calls.  Useful when validation requires combining multiple flag values (e.g. URL + allow-insecure pair) and cannot be expressed at parser-time.
  - Hunt-skip notes: chainspec-builder / substrate-node-template / zombienet (Substrate ecosystem) deferred.  pop-cli (Substrate dapp CLI; check if same as pop) probe.  fuel-core / fuel-core-keygen (Fuel node binaries) for future round.
  - Blacklist: added `subxt`.
  - Corpus 28,681 → 28,682.

- **R253 — fuelup source-direct (Fuel Network toolchain manager)** (1 file new) — pivots away from the Dojo/Starknet cluster (R247-R252) into the **Fuel Network cluster**.  fuelup is the **Fuel Network toolchain manager** — rustup-style installer + version manager for the Fuel blockchain stack (forc compiler, fuel-core node).  Fuel is a parallelized execution UTXO-based blockchain (sister to Solana's parallel-execution model but with Cardano-inspired UTXO storage).  Sister to forc (Fuel's contract compiler; already in corpus).
  - `_fuelup` (1-stem; FuelLabs/fuelup src/commands/ + src/fuelup_cli.rs; clap v4 derive; **9 top-level subcommands + 3 nested sub-subcommand trees + clap_complete::Shell ValueEnum**) decoded source-direct from:
    * `src/fuelup_cli.rs` lines 14-44 (Cli + Commands enum: 9 top-level variants; 3 with nested subcommands)
    * `src/commands/check.rs` lines 6-12 (CheckCommand: 1 flag `--verbose/-v`)
    * `src/commands/completions.rs` lines 7-12 (CompletionsCommand using `clap_complete::Shell` ValueEnum)
    * `src/commands/component.rs` lines 5-25 (ComponentCommand enum: Add/Remove/List)
    * `src/commands/default.rs` lines 5-9 (DefaultCommand with `Default_` variant name + optional positional toolchain)
    * `src/commands/fuelup.rs` lines 5-23 (FuelupCommand enum: Update/Uninstall; renamed to CLI "self" via `#[clap(name = "self", subcommand)]`)
    * `src/commands/toolchain.rs` lines 9-50 (ToolchainCommand enum: Install/New/Uninstall/Export; New uses `name_allowed` custom validator)
    * `src/commands/upgrade.rs` lines 5-9 (UpgradeCommand: 1 flag `--force/-f`)
  - Critical extraction note: fuelup uses **`#[clap(name = "self", subcommand)]`** at fuelup_cli.rs:33 on the `Fuelup(FuelupCommand)` variant.  The Rust enum variant is `Fuelup` (different name from CLI) but the CLI surface is `fuelup self update` / `fuelup self uninstall`.  Documents the **Rust-keyword-avoidance via clap rename pattern** — `self` is a Rust keyword and cannot be used as an enum variant name.  The `#[clap(name = "...")]` attribute lets the CLI name differ from the Rust identifier.  This is the corpus's **first documented `name = "..."` rename used to bypass a Rust keyword conflict** — distinct from R249 cairo-profiler's `#[clap(name = "cairo-profiler")]` which was purely defensive (Rust ident matched CLI name anyway).
  - Critical extraction note: fuelup uses **`Default_` with trailing underscore** at fuelup_cli.rs:31 — the Rust enum variant is `Default_` but clap derive's auto-name strips trailing underscores so the CLI sees `default`.  This dodges the **Rust naming conflict with the `Default` trait** (which is in scope in derive macros).  Documents the **trailing-underscore variant-name pattern** for clap derive — distinct from the `name = "..."` rename (which requires explicit attribute).  Subtle: works because clap derive's name-stripping logic looks at the trailing `_` specifically.  Useful when the CLI name you want IS a Rust reserved-ish identifier (default, type, ref, etc.).  Documents the **2 keyword-avoidance patterns** in clap derive: (a) trailing underscore + auto-strip, (b) explicit `#[clap(name = "...")]` rename.
  - Critical extraction note: fuelup uses **`clap_complete::Shell`** ValueEnum at completions.rs:11 — when used as an `#[arg]` type, clap auto-detects the ValueEnum + emits shell name completions (bash, zsh, fish, elvish, powershell).  Documents the **clap-integration with clap_complete** for shell-completion generators.  Sister to R250 sozo + R251 torii's use of `clap_verbosity_flag::Verbosity` flatten — both integrate community-maintained clap helper crates.  Continues the corpus's **clap-helper-crate ecosystem documentation** (clap_complete, clap_verbosity_flag, clap_mangen, etc.).
  - Critical extraction note: fuelup uses **`name_allowed` custom validator** at toolchain.rs:52-72 for the `toolchain new NAME` arg.  The validator: (1) splits NAME at first `-`; if suffix matches host target triple (e.g. `latest-aarch64-apple-darwin` on M1 Mac), strips it; (2) bails if the (possibly-stripped) prefix is in `RESERVED_TOOLCHAIN_NAMES` (latest / nightly / testnet / mainnet); (3) returns the ORIGINAL (un-stripped) string on success.  This is a **multi-purpose validator** — both transforms (target-triple awareness) AND rejects (reserved-name check).  Documents the **stateful validator with host detection** — clap value_parsers can call into the host OS for context-aware validation.  Distinct from R252 saya's `validate_non_empty` (pure string check) and R248 cairo-coverage's `parse_trace_file` (filesystem check).  5th documented parser-level validation pattern (R231/R247/R248/R252/R253).
  - Critical extraction note: fuelup has **3 clap subcommand-arg patterns** in one CLI: (a) NESTED SUBCOMMAND ENUM marked by `#[clap(subcommand)]`: `Component`, `Toolchain`, `Fuelup`/self; (b) ARG STRUCT: `Check`, `Completions`, `Default_`, `Upgrade`; (c) BARE UNIT VARIANT: `Show`, `Update`.  Documents the **clap subcommand-shape taxonomy** in a single production CLI — a clean reference for understanding how clap derive supports the 3 shapes uniformly.
  - Hunt-skip notes: forc (Fuel contract compiler; already in corpus) confirmed.  fuel-core (Fuel node; check if exists).  fuels (Rust SDK; might not have a binary CLI).  soroban (Stellar) deferred for future round.  risc0 / cargo-risczero (zkVM) — cargo-risczero already in corpus.  aleo (Aleo encrypted blockchain) deferred.
  - Blacklist: added `fuelup`.
  - Corpus 28,680 → 28,681.

- **R252 — saya source-direct (Dojo proving orchestrator; validity rollup)** (1 file new) — completes the Dojo Engine cluster (sozo R250, katana already in corpus, torii R251, saya R252).  saya is the **Dojo proving orchestrator** — produces Cairo execution proofs for Starknet appchain rollups (Dojo "worlds" deployed as L3 sovereign or persistent L2-settled chains).  Major **validity-rollup tooling** — saya integrates with the Atlantic prover service + Celestia data-availability + Piltover settlement contract.
  - `_saya` (1-stem; dojoengine/saya bin/persistent/src/; clap v4 derive; **2 deployment modes (sovereign / persistent L3) + 20 flags for persistent + 11 flags for sovereign**) decoded source-direct from:
    * `main.rs` lines 30-44 (Cli + Subcommands enum: 2 variants `Sovereign(Sovereign)` and `Start(Start)`)
    * `persistent.rs` lines 36-80 (Start Parser struct: 14 direct fields + HintsConfiguration flatten + CelestiaConfiguration flatten)
    * `persistent.rs` lines 82-98 (CelestiaConfiguration: 4 fields with `validate_non_empty` value_parser on namespace)
    * `persistent.rs` lines 100-111 (HintsConfiguration: 3 bool fields with `default_value_t = false`)
    * `sovereign.rs` lines 31-41 (Sovereign nested struct with its own Subcommands enum containing single Start variant)
    * `sovereign.rs` lines 43-77 (sovereign-mode Start struct: 10 direct fields + GenesisOptions flatten)
    * `sovereign.rs` lines 79-86 (validate_non_empty value_parser function)
    * `sovereign.rs` lines 88-95 (GenesisOptions with `long = "genesis.first-block-number"` dotted-prefix CLI flag)
  - Critical extraction note: saya uses **2 deployment modes as top-level subcommands**: `saya sovereign start ...` (Celestia DA only; no settlement layer; standalone proof chain) AND `saya start ...` (persistent L3 mode; settles proofs on a "base layer" Starknet via Piltover; full pipeline including layout bridge).  Note: `sovereign` is a GROUP that ONLY contains `start` — `saya sovereign` alone is incomplete; the user MUST follow with `start`.  Documents the **subcommand-as-group-namespace** pattern where a parent subcommand has only one child but provides organizational grouping in `--help`.  Distinct from a typical 1-deep subcommand structure where every leaf is reachable directly.  Sets up the **future-expansion-hook pattern** — when saya adds more sovereign-mode operations (e.g. status / stop / migrate), they slot under the existing `sovereign` namespace without breaking existing CLI usage.
  - Critical extraction note: saya uses **`#[allow(clippy::large_enum_variant)]`** at main.rs:36 on the Subcommands enum.  Opposite of R250 sozo's `Box<XxxArgs>` pattern — saya just SUPPRESSES the clippy lint instead of wrapping variants in Box.  This is the **corpus's first documented `#[allow(clippy::large_enum_variant)]` lint-suppression alternative to Box wrapping**.  Sozo's 21-variant enum benefited from Box (each variant just 8 bytes); saya's 2-variant enum has fewer values to allocate so the size penalty is tolerable.  Documents the **trade-off**: Box adds indirection (heap allocation + pointer chase) but bounds enum size; allow-lint accepts wasted memory in exchange for stack-only allocation.  For small-variant-count enums (2-3 variants), allow-lint is often the right choice.  Completes the corpus's **enum-size handling taxonomy**: Box-wrap each variant (R250 sozo) vs allow-lint suppression (R252 saya) vs accept-default (silent in most CLIs).
  - Critical extraction note: saya uses **`#[clap(long, env)]` WITHOUT explicit env name** at EVERY env-aware flag.  clap auto-derives the env var name from the field name (e.g. `rollup_rpc` → `ROLLUP_RPC`).  This is the corpus's **first documented `env` clap attribute used WITHOUT a value** — clap derive's auto-derivation rule: take the field name, convert to SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE.  Distinct from R250 sozo's explicit `env = "DOJO_MANIFEST_PATH"` (different name from field) and R251 torii's explicit `env = "DOJO_WORLD_ADDRESS"`.  saya uses the auto-derived names because they happen to match the convention.  The exception is `GENESIS_FIRST_BLOCK_NUMBER` at sovereign.rs:92 which uses explicit `env = "GENESIS_FIRST_BLOCK_NUMBER"` to match the **dotted-prefix CLI flag** `genesis.first-block-number` (the CLI flag and env var conventions diverge: CLI uses dots + dashes, env uses underscores).  Documents the **`#[clap(long, env)]` shorthand** — convenience pattern for matching field names.
  - Critical extraction note: saya uses **`validate_non_empty`** at persistent.rs:96 + sovereign.rs:66 (the value_parser for `celestia_namespace`) defined at sovereign.rs:79-86.  This is a string-trim + empty-check validator returning **`Result<String, String>`** rather than `Result<String, anyhow::Error>` like R248 cairo-coverage.  Documents the **simpler String-error value_parser pattern** — clap accepts any `Fn(&str) -> Result<T, E> where E: Display`, so a plain String error type works fine.  4th documented parser-level validation pattern: (1) R231 ferrishot bpaf guard, (2) R247 sncast NonZero* type-system, (3) R248 cairo-coverage anyhow::ensure! validators, (4) R252 saya plain-String validator.  Completes the **parser-level validation taxonomy**.
  - Critical extraction note: saya's persistent mode has **3 cross-flag implication rules enforced AT RUNTIME** (NOT via clap's `requires` / `conflicts_with`): (1) EITHER `--layout-bridge-program` + `--atlantic-key` OR `--mock-layout-bridge-program-hash` MUST be provided (else bail at persistent.rs:167-169 with "invalid config" error), (2) `--settlement-integrity-address` MUST be provided UNLESS `--mock-layout-bridge-program-hash` is used (bails at persistent.rs:241-244), (3) `--celestia-rpc` + `--celestia-token` must come together (one without the other falls back to Noop DA backend silently at persistent.rs:208-222).  Documents the **runtime cross-flag validation pattern** — saya chose runtime bail over clap's declarative `requires`/`conflicts_with` because the logic is multi-variable (any-of + unless-of combinations) which is awkward to express declaratively.  Distinct from R248 cairo-coverage's `requires = "unstable"` which IS declarative.  Documents when to escape clap's declarative validation: when the validation depends on combinations of multiple flag states, runtime bail is the cleaner choice.
  - Hunt-skip notes: Dojo Engine cluster (sozo R250, katana already in corpus, torii R251, saya R252) now COMPLETE.  Next pivot to other Cairo/Starknet tools or sibling blockchain frameworks (cosmos-sdk simd, anchor for Solana, foundry for Ethereum).
  - Blacklist: added `saya`.
  - Corpus 28,679 → 28,680.

- **R251 — torii source-direct (Dojo World Indexer; 100+ dotted-prefix flags)** (1 file new) — extends the R250 Dojo Engine cluster.  torii is the **Dojo World Indexer** — watches Starknet for world contract events and indexes them into SQLite for GraphQL/gRPC querying.  Sister to sozo (R250) and katana (already in corpus).  Together: sozo manages the world, katana is the devnet, torii indexes the world.  Major **production-scale CLI** — 100+ flags organized into 13 namespaced option groups.
  - `_torii` (1-stem; dojoengine/torii bin/torii/src + crates/cli/src/; clap v4 derive; **100+ flags across 13 dotted-prefix option groups + MergeOptions config-file merging**) decoded source-direct from:
    * `bin/torii/src/cli.rs` lines 9-14 (Cli wraps ToriiArgs via `#[command(flatten)]`)
    * `crates/cli/src/args.rs` lines 16-106 (ToriiArgs Parser struct with 5 direct fields + 13 flattened option groups; 6 are `#[cfg(feature = "server")]`)
    * `crates/cli/src/options.rs` lines 68-1386 (13 option structs each with `#[command(next_help_heading = "...")]` and per-field `long = "<group>.<field>"`)
    * `crates/cli/src/args.rs` lines 140-153 (with_config_file impl: CLI takes precedence over TOML config file)
  - Critical extraction note: torii uses the **dotted-prefix CLI flag naming convention** via explicit `long = "<group>.<field>"` at every flag (e.g. `--indexing.transactions`, `--sql.page_size`, `--http.cors_origins`, `--grpc.max_message_size`).  This is **NOT** clap's default behavior — clap derive normally emits `--<field>` from the field name.  torii manually names every flag with a group prefix to organize 100+ flags into 13 namespaces.  The CLI namespace doesn't always match the struct field name — e.g. ServerOptions struct uses `http.<field>` prefix (NOT `server.<field>`).  Documents the **dotted-prefix flag namespacing pattern** — the corpus's first documented production CLI with >100 flags using namespace-prefixed naming.  Distinct from R244 lefthook + R251 torii which both use dotted prefixes but for different reasons (lefthook for YAML config keys, torii for organizing CLI args by subsystem).
  - Critical extraction note: torii uses the **`MergeOptions` derive macro** at every option struct.  This is a custom proc-macro from dojoengine that generates a `merge(&mut self, other: Option<&Self>)` method to combine the CLI-parsed args with TOML-config-file values.  Combined with `with_config_file` at args.rs:140-153, the precedence is: (1) CLI flag value (if explicitly set), (2) TOML config-file value, (3) struct's Default impl.  This is the **corpus's first documented `MergeOptions` CLI-takes-precedence-over-config-file pattern** — a production approach to "config file with CLI overrides" that's reusable across Rust CLIs.  Distinct from clap's built-in `default_value_t` (struct-defaults only) and from `config::Config` crate (which merges sources but doesn't integrate with clap).
  - Critical extraction note: torii uses **`#[command(next_help_heading = "...")]`** on EVERY option struct (e.g. "Relay options", "Indexing options", "HTTP server options", "Metrics options").  This clap attribute groups the struct's args under a custom heading in `--help` output, organizing 100+ flags by semantic role.  R209/R248 documented `help_heading` on individual args; torii documents the **struct-level next_help_heading** which applies to ALL args defined in the struct.  Documents the **declarative help-section organization** for large CLIs.
  - Critical extraction note: torii has **6 of 13 option groups feature-gated by `#[cfg(feature = "server")]`** at args.rs:77-105 (MetricsOptions, ServerOptions, RelayOptions, GrpcOptions, MessagingOptions, SearchOptions).  When the server feature is disabled, torii becomes a pure indexer with no HTTP/gRPC/relay exposure.  Distinct from sozo (R250)'s feature-gated Walnut VARIANT — torii feature-gates entire option STRUCTS (which means tens of flags appear/disappear atomically).  Documents the **feature-gated option-group pattern** — distinct from feature-gated subcommand variants (R201, R250 Walnut) and from feature-gated individual flags.  Completes the corpus's **feature-gating taxonomy**: (a) variant-level (R201, R250 Walnut), (b) struct/group-level (R251 torii), (c) parser-level via `requires=` (R248 cairo-coverage), (d) runtime body-bail (R225 turbo), (e) majority-feature-gating (oxc cluster).
  - Critical extraction note: torii uses **backward-compat flag aliases** at indexing/options.rs:155-156: `#[arg(long = "indexing.preconfirmed", alias = "indexing.pending")]` AND `#[serde(alias = "pending")]`.  The field was renamed from "pending" to "preconfirmed" (Starknet 0.14.1 terminology change), but both the CLI flag AND the TOML key accept the old name.  Documents the **dual CLI+TOML migration-alias pattern** for renaming user-facing names without breaking existing configs.  Distinct from R244 lefthook deprecation warning (which prints a runtime warning); torii's approach is silent.
  - Critical extraction note: torii's `Default` impl at args.rs:108-138 includes ALL 13 option groups (`#[cfg(feature = "server")]` for the 6 server-gated groups), used by `#[serde(default)]` for partial-TOML parsing.  When the TOML file omits an option group entirely, serde uses the Default::default() for that group rather than failing.  Documents the **partial-config-file with serde defaults pattern** — combine `#[serde(default)]` on the struct + `impl Default` to allow TOML files to specify only the keys that differ from defaults.  This is the corpus's first documented **3-layer config priority** (CLI > TOML > Default) implemented via the clap+serde+MergeOptions trifecta.
  - Hunt-skip notes: saya (dojoengine validity rollup proof producer) deferred for future round.  All Dojo Engine binaries (sozo R250, katana already in corpus, torii R251) covered or scheduled.
  - Blacklist: added `torii`.
  - Corpus 28,678 → 28,679.

- **R250 — sozo source-direct (Dojo Engine Cairo game-world CLI)** (1 file new) — extends the R247/R248/R249 Cairo/Starknet cluster INTO Dojo Engine.  sozo is the **Dojo Engine CLI** — Dojo is the framework for building provable Cairo games + on-chain worlds on Starknet.  Manages the Dojo "world" (the on-chain game state contract): build, declare, deploy, migrate, invoke entrypoints, inspect models (Dojo's ECS storage system).  Sister tool to `katana` (Dojo devnet; already in corpus).  Major **on-chain gaming CLI** — first Dojo Engine tool added to the corpus.
  - `_sozo` (1-stem; dojoengine/dojo bin/sozo/src/; clap v4 derive; **21 top-level subcommands + 15 starknet sub-subs + nested hash/auth/model trees**) decoded source-direct from:
    * `args.rs` lines 15-40 (SozoArgs Parser struct with 5 global flags + ProfileSpec flatten + Verbosity flatten + subcommand)
    * `args.rs` lines 83-98 (ProfileSpec Parser struct with `#[group(multiple = false)]` + 3 profile-selectors: -P/--profile / --release / --dev)
    * `commands/mod.rs` lines 54-100 (Commands enum: 21 variants including 1 feature-gated by `#[cfg(feature = "walnut")]` and 1 with `alias = "sn"` (starknet))
    * `commands/starknet/mod.rs` lines 22-72 (StarknetCommand enum: 15 sub-subcommands; transaction has `alias = "tx"`)
    * `commands/hash.rs` (HashCommand sub: compute / find)
    * `commands/model.rs` (ModelCommand sub: class-hash / contract-address / layout / get / ...)
    * `commands/auth.rs` (AuthCommand sub: grant / revoke with nested AuthKind)
  - Critical extraction note: sozo uses **`Box<XxxArgs>` wrapping every Subcommand variant** at commands/mod.rs:55-100 (e.g. `Auth(Box<AuthArgs>)`, `Bindgen(Box<BindgenArgs>)`, ..., `Starknet(Box<StarknetArgs>)`).  This is **clippy's `large_enum_variant` lint mitigation** — the Commands enum has 21 variants and the largest (e.g. MigrateArgs with nested option flattens) is significantly bigger than the smallest (VersionArgs).  Without Box, the enum allocates space for the largest variant in every value; with Box, each variant is just a pointer (8 bytes).  This is the **corpus's first documented `Box<...>` per-variant enum-size-equalization pattern**.  Documents Rust's idiomatic way to keep large-variant enums memory-efficient.
  - Critical extraction note: sozo uses **`#[command(alias = "sn")]`** on `Starknet(...)` at commands/mod.rs:98 AND **`#[command(alias = "tx")]`** on `Transaction(...)` at starknet/mod.rs:39.  These are subcommand aliases at TWO different nesting depths (top-level + sub-sub-command).  Documents the corpus's clap subcommand-alias pattern at depth — distinct from the `#[arg(alias = "...")]` ARG alias pattern (R240 etc.) which aliases flag names.  This is the **subcommand NAME alias** which lets the user type `sozo sn balance` (= `sozo starknet balance`) or `sozo sn tx 0xabc` (= `sozo starknet transaction 0xabc`).  Extends the R240+ "5 clap alias attribute variants" documentation to **2 subcommand-alias variants at 2 nesting depths**.
  - Critical extraction note: sozo uses **`clap_verbosity_flag::Verbosity`** at args.rs:30 (flattened into SozoArgs).  This is a popular external clap crate that adds `-v`/`--verbose` (count: more = more verbose) and `-q`/`--quiet` (count: more = quieter) with the count mapping to log levels (Error/Warn/Info/Debug/Trace).  sozo's `ui_verbosity()` impl at args.rs:43-55 maps Off → Quiet, Error → Normal, Warn → Verbose, Info → Debug, Debug | Trace → Trace.  Documents **clap-flag external-crate flatten** — integrating a community-maintained common flag set rather than reinventing -v/-q.  3rd-party clap crate documented in corpus (after R210 clap_complete).  Shows how clap derive supports the **flag-providing-crate ecosystem** (clap_verbosity_flag, clap_complete, clap_mangen, etc.).
  - Critical extraction note: sozo uses **`#[group(multiple = false)]`** on ProfileSpec at args.rs:85 — the `-P/--profile`, `--release`, `--dev` flags are mutually exclusive (declarative mutex without `conflicts_with = ...` per-flag).  All 3 flags are tagged `group = "profiles"`; the `multiple = false` constraint means at most one may be specified.  R204 documented this pattern; sozo uses it for profile selection.  Combined with `determine()` impl at args.rs:101-108 (priority order: release > dev > profile > Profile::default()).  Documents the corpus's **declarative-mutex via group(multiple=false) pattern** in production use.
  - Critical extraction note: sozo uses **`global = true` + `env = "..."` on 2 flags**: `--manifest-path` (env: DOJO_MANIFEST_PATH) and `--offline` (env: SOZO_OFFLINE).  Plus all 3 ProfileSpec fields are `global = true` (so `sozo build --release` and `sozo --release build` both work).  R215 documented `global = true`; sozo combines it with `env = "..."` for the **flag/env/global trio** that is the modern Rust CLI standard for "config-source priority": CLI flag > env var > config file > default.  Documents the production pattern for unified config sources.
  - Critical extraction note: sozo uses **runtime `version=generate_version()`** at args.rs:16 instead of a literal version string.  `generate_version()` is in `utils.rs` and computes a version string at runtime (typically combining CARGO_PKG_VERSION + git rev + build date).  Documents **dynamic version pattern** — clap's version attribute accepts a function call (clap evaluates it at parser-build time, not at parse time).  Distinct from `clap::crate_version!()` macro which is a compile-time string literal.  Useful when the version needs to include git SHA / build metadata.
  - Critical extraction note: sozo uses **`#[cfg(feature = "walnut")]`** on the `Walnut` variant at commands/mod.rs:93-95 — the entire subcommand is COMPILED AWAY when the walnut feature is disabled.  R201 documented variant-level cfg gating; sozo uses it for an optional integration (Walnut is a 3rd-party Starknet transaction debugger by walnut.dev).  Documents **feature-gated subcommand variant** — distinct from R248 cairo-coverage's `requires = "..."` (which is parser-level runtime gate, not compile-time) and from R225 turbo body-bail (which is runtime).  Shows the **3 feature-gating layers**: compile-time (cfg), parser-time (requires=), runtime (body-bail).
  - Critical extraction note: sozo's Commands enum has a **manual `Display` impl** at commands/mod.rs:102-128 — used by tracing's `info_span!("Subcommand", name)` at commands/mod.rs:132 to label log spans.  Documents **CLI-subcommand-as-tracing-span pattern** for structured logging in production Rust CLIs.  The Display impl maps each variant to a CamelCase string ("Auth", "Bindgen", "Build", ...) for human-readable span names.
  - Hunt-skip notes: torii / saya (sister dojoengine tools) deferred for future round.  starknet-devnet-rs (Pythonic devnet vs katana the Rust devnet) deferred.  Confirmed dup-free.
  - Blacklist: added `sozo`.
  - Corpus 28,677 → 28,678.

- **R249 — cairo-profiler source-direct (Starknet Cairo profiler; pprof output)** (1 file new) — extends the R247/R248 Cairo/Starknet cluster.  cairo-profiler is the **Starknet Cairo profiler** from software-mansion — sister to cairo-coverage (R248), sncast (R247), snforge.  Consumes .json trace files (typically from snforge) and produces pprof-compatible `.pb.gz` profile files for visualization with `go tool pprof`.
  - `_cairo-profiler` (1-stem; software-mansion/cairo-profiler crates/cairo-profiler/src/; version 0.16.0; clap v4 derive): **2 subcommands (build-profile / view) + 11 BuildProfile fields + 5 ViewProfile fields + default-subcommand-via-flatten + NonZeroUsize + 3-way conflicts_with mutex** decoded source-direct from:
    * `main.rs` lines 19-27 (entry point with default-subcommand fallback)
    * `cli/mod.rs` lines 8-25 (Cli Parser struct with `args_conflicts_with_subcommands = true` + flattened Option<BuildProfile> + Option<Commands>)
    * `cli/build_profile.rs` lines 16-77 (BuildProfile Args struct with 11 fields)
    * `cli/view.rs` lines 8-29 (ViewProfile Args struct with 5 fields)
  - Critical extraction note: cairo-profiler uses the **default-subcommand-via-flatten pattern** at cli/mod.rs:11-17 combined with main.rs:22-26 — the top-level Cli struct `#[clap(flatten)] pub build_profile_args: Option<BuildProfile>` means the BuildProfile args are flattened INTO the parent (so `cairo-profiler trace.json` works as a shortcut for `cairo-profiler build-profile trace.json`).  The main dispatch matches `None => run_build_profile(&cli.build_profile_args.expect("Failed to parse arguments"))` — when no subcommand is given, the parser populates the flattened args and the main fn unwraps and routes to build-profile.  This is the **corpus's first documented `default-subcommand-via-flatten` pattern** — distinct from clap's `default_value_subcommand` (which does not exist) and distinct from the wrapper-shim pattern (R232 etc.) which intercepts argv and re-dispatches.  Documents how clap derive achieves "implicit default subcommand" without runtime argv munging.
  - Critical extraction note: cairo-profiler uses **`args_conflicts_with_subcommands = true`** at cli/mod.rs:9 — 2nd documented use in corpus (R248 cairo-coverage was 1st).  Pairs with the flattened BuildProfile so the parser EITHER accepts the BuildProfile args (top-level) OR a subcommand (View / BuildProfile), never both.  Demonstrates the attribute's intended use case (defending against ambiguity when flattened args overlap with subcommand args).
  - Critical extraction note: BuildProfile uses **3 `requires = "view"` gates** at build_profile.rs:59, 65, 71 — `--sample`, `--limit`, `--hide` REQUIRE `--view` to be set.  These same 3 flags exist on the `view` subcommand WITHOUT the `requires = "view"` attribute, because the subcommand itself implies viewing.  Documents the **shared-flag-set with parser-gated subset** pattern: same flag names with different validation rules depending on whether you're using build-profile-with-view or the standalone view subcommand.
  - Critical extraction note: ViewProfile uses **`conflicts_with = "list_samples"`** at view.rs:15, 23, 27 — `--sample`, `--limit`, `--hide` are mutually-exclusive with `--list-samples`/`-l`.  When listing samples, the other 3 display options have no meaning.  Documents the **3-way conflicts_with mutex group** without using `ArgGroup` (which is the typical declarative approach for mutex groups; R204 documented ArgGroup).
  - Critical extraction note: cairo-profiler uses **`NonZeroUsize`** for `--limit` at build_profile.rs:66 + view.rs:24.  Distinct from R247 sncast's `NonZeroU16` / `NonZeroU8` — `NonZeroUsize` is platform-dependent (typically 64-bit on modern systems).  Both reject 0 at parse time.  3rd documented `NonZero*` type after R247 sncast (NonZeroU8 + NonZeroU16).  Completes the **`std::num::NonZero*` family** documentation in the corpus: NonZeroU8 (R247), NonZeroU16 (R247), NonZeroUsize (R249).
  - Critical extraction note: cairo-profiler uses **`#[clap(name = "cairo-profiler")]`** at cli/mod.rs:10 to override the default binary name.  Useful when the crate name and binary name differ.  This is the **corpus's first documented `#[clap(name = "...")]` parent attribute** — clap derive normally uses `env!("CARGO_BIN_NAME")` but here the crate name `cairo-profiler` matches anyway, so the attribute is defensive/explicit (or for documentation clarity in help output).
  - Hunt-skip notes: confirmed dup-free against /usr/share/zsh + /opt/homebrew/share/zsh + /usr/local/share/zsh.  `cairo-format` deferred (need to probe upstream).  `slither-mutate` (Ethereum) deferred.
  - Blacklist: added `cairo-profiler`.
  - Corpus 28,676 → 28,677.

- **R248 — cairo-coverage source-direct (Starknet Cairo coverage tool)** (1 file new) — extends the R247 Cairo/Starknet cluster.  cairo-coverage is the **Starknet Cairo coverage tool** from software-mansion (same org as scarb, snforge, sncast).  Produces lcov-format coverage reports from snforge test traces.  Continues the corpus's first-class Cairo/Starknet tooling lineage.
  - `_cairo-coverage` (1-stem; software-mansion/cairo-coverage crates/cairo-coverage/src/; version 0.6.1; clap v4 derive): **2 subcommands (clean / run) + 5 RunArgs flags + 2 CleanArgs flags + single-variant ValueEnum + 2 custom validators** decoded source-direct from:
    * `main.rs` lines 11-24 (entry point; `CairoCoverageArgs::parse()`)
    * `args/mod.rs` lines 8-24 (CairoCoverageArgs Parser struct with `args_conflicts_with_subcommands = true` + Command enum: Clean / Run)
    * `args/run.rs` lines 6-31 (RunArgs Parser struct with 6 fields)
    * `args/run.rs` lines 34-38 (IncludedComponent ValueEnum with 1 variant: Macros)
    * `args/run.rs` lines 40-50 (parse_trace_file validator)
    * `args/run.rs` lines 53-60 (parse_project_path validator)
    * `args/clean.rs` lines 5-15 (CleanArgs Parser struct)
  - Critical extraction note: **`args_conflicts_with_subcommands = true`** at args/mod.rs:9 — this attribute marks the parent's args as mutually-exclusive with subcommands.  Useful when the parent accepts args ONLY when no subcommand is given (currently no parent args, but the attribute is set so the marker is reserved for future expansion).  This is the **corpus's first documented `args_conflicts_with_subcommands` clap derive attribute** — distinct from `subcommand_required` (R213/R214) which forces a subcommand, and distinct from `subcommand_negates_reqs` which makes subcommand presence clear required-arg constraints.  Documents the **3-way clap derive attribute family** for parent/child relations: (a) `subcommand_required` (must provide one), (b) `args_conflicts_with_subcommands` (args XOR subcommand), (c) `subcommand_negates_reqs` (subcommand presence relaxes parent's required-args).
  - Critical extraction note: **`requires = "unstable"`** at args/run.rs:17 + 21 gates `--include` and `--no-truncation` behind `--unstable`.  clap surfaces the error "the following required arguments were not provided: --unstable" when either gated flag is used without --unstable.  This is **opt-in feature gating at the parser level** — distinct from the corpus's R225 turbo body-bail (which checks at runtime via `if !ctx.unstable_features { return Err(...) }`), the R201 variant-level gating (which uses `#[cfg(feature = "...")]` to compile variants away), and the R237 dependabot pkg-ecosystem name approach.  Documents the **4th feature-gating pattern** in the corpus: clap's `requires = "X"` attribute marking a runtime gate at the parser level.
  - Critical extraction note: **`#[derive(ValueEnum)]` with a single variant** at args/run.rs:35-38 (`enum IncludedComponent { Macros }`).  This is the corpus's first documented **single-value ValueEnum** — an extension point for future variants without changing the CLI surface.  Distinct from clap's `value_parser = [...]` slice (which is a compile-time fixed array; R209) — the ValueEnum approach allows method implementations on the enum, doc-comments per variant, and `--help` rendering of variant descriptions.  Currently the only value is `macros` (covers inline macros, attribute macros, derive macros).
  - Critical extraction note: **custom `parse_trace_file` + `parse_project_path` validators** via `value_parser = fn` at args/run.rs:9 + 29.  The trace_file validator checks: (a) file exists, (b) is a regular file (not a dir/symlink), (c) has `.json` extension via `matches!(trace_file.extension(), Some("json"))`.  The project_path validator checks: (a) exists, (b) is a directory.  Both use `anyhow::ensure!` for fail-fast semantics with the specific error message surfaced directly to the user.  Documents **clap's `value_parser` attribute pointing to a function returning Result<T,E>** — the parser invokes the function once per arg, surfacing the error from `anyhow::ensure!` as a clap parse error.  Distinct from the corpus's R231 ferrishot `guard()` (bpaf closure-based) and R247 sncast NonZero* types (type-system-based).  This is the **3rd documented parser-level validation pattern**.
  - Critical extraction note: **`num_args = 1..` on the positional `trace_files`** at args/run.rs:9 means "1 or more values" — clap accepts `cairo-coverage run a.json b.json c.json` as 3 values bundled into the Vec<Utf8PathBuf>.  Combined with `required = true`, this means at least 1 trace file is mandatory.  Distinct from `num_args = 0..` (any count) and `num_args = N..=M` (range).  This is **clap's variadic positional pattern**.
  - Hunt-skip notes: `cairo-profiler` (sister tool; deferred for future round to keep this entry focused).  `cairo-format` (the Cairo formatter; check upstream existence).  `slither-mutate` (Ethereum mutation tester; clap-based) deferred.  Confirmed `starkli`, `scarb`, `snforge`, `aiken` all already in corpus.
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist: added `cairo-coverage`.
  - Corpus 28,675 → 28,676.

- **R247 — sncast source-direct rewrite (Starknet Foundry contract CLI)** (1 file source-direct rewrite) — branches off the oxc cluster (R245/R246) into Cairo/Starknet blockchain tooling.  sncast is the **Starknet Foundry contract interaction CLI** — the "Smart Contract Cast" tool from foundry-rs's starknet-foundry suite.  Sister to snforge (the testing framework; already in corpus).  Used to declare, deploy, call, invoke Cairo contracts on Starknet mainnet/sepolia.
  - `_sncast` (1-stem; foundry-rs/starknet-foundry crates/sncast/src/; **REWRITTEN source-direct from R247**): **17 top-level subcommands + 9 global flags + per-subcommand sub-subcommand trees for 6 verbs** decoded source-direct from:
    * `main.rs` lines 67-140 (Cli Parser struct with 9 global flags + Commands subcommand; clap v4 derive)
    * `main.rs` lines 161-213 (Commands enum: 17 top-level variants)
    * `starknet_commands/account/mod.rs` lines 49-56 (Account sub-subcommands: import / create / deploy / delete / list)
    * `starknet_commands/multicall.rs` lines 37-41 (Multicall sub-subcommands: run / new / execute)
    * `starknet_commands/script.rs` lines 24-27 (Script sub-subcommands: init / run)
    * `starknet_commands/get/` dir (7 sub-subcommands: balance / class-hash-at / nonce / spec-version / transaction / tx-receipt / tx-status)
    * `starknet_commands/utils/` dir (5 sub-subcommands: class-hash / contract-address / felt-or-id / selector / serialize)
