Critical extraction note: tpm2_policycountertimer has a **12-way comparison-operator mutex** (`--eq` XOR `--neq` XOR `--sgt` XOR `--ugt` XOR `--slt` XOR `--ult` XOR `--sge` XOR `--uge` XOR `--sle` XOR `--ule` XOR `--bs` XOR `--bc`) — decoded source-direct from upstream's topts[] entries which use `TPM2_EO_*` constants as the val column (`eq=TPM2_EO_EQ`, `neq=TPM2_EO_NEQ`, signed/unsigned gt/lt/ge/le, `bs=BITSET`, `bc=BITCLEAR`).  **This is the heaviest mutex group in the entire R193-R203 tpm2_* set.**  Each operator-flag has no_argument (it's a flag toggle) since the operand is supplied positionally.
    * **tpm2_policycphash** (4 flags): TPM2_PolicyCpHash binds the policy to a command-parameter hash (cpHash) computed from a future command's parameters.  Used to lock a policy to a specific command invocation.  Critical extraction note: **`--cphash-input` and `--cphash` BOTH have val=0** in upstream's topts[] table (decoded source-direct from `{ "cphash-input", required_argument, NULL, 0 }, { "cphash", required_argument, NULL, 0 }`).  This is **unique within the R193-R203 set** — duplicate val codes for two different long names.  Per `getopt_long(3)`'s behavior the second occurrence with the same val typically shadows the first; upstream's `on_option` callback dispatches by `option_index` rather than val.  Completion lists both for parser-acceptance compatibility.
    * **tpm2_policyduplicationselect** (5 flags): TPM2_PolicyDuplicationSelect restricts which parent an object can be duplicated under (used with R201's tpm2_duplicate).  `-n`/`--object-name` + `-N`/`--parent-name` are the object-to-duplicate name and the allowed new-parent name.  Critical extraction note: this is the **5th variant of the case-sensitive convention** documented across R193-R201 — `-n` (lowercase = object) vs `-N` (uppercase = parent), continuing the pattern of "uppercase = parent/source/wrapper, lowercase = child/target/wrapped" but here the short-form letter is `n`/`N` (name) rather than `c`/`C` (context) or `u`/`U` (public).
    * **tpm2_policylocality** (3 flags + locality positional): TPM2_PolicyLocality binds the policy to a TPM locality (0-4 for hardware, 32-255 for software).  Positional accepts a locality number or symbolic name (`locality-zero` through `locality-four`) — 9-entry enum decoded source-direct from upstream's `tpm2_str_to_locality()` parser.
    * **tpm2_policynamehash** (3 flags): TPM2_PolicyNameHash binds the policy to a hash of one or more object names.  Pure 3-flag set — no shared `$tpm2_policy_base` re-use since the topts[] is missing `--cphash`.
    * **tpm2_policynv** (7 flags + NV-index positional + operator positional): TPM2_PolicyNV binds the policy to a comparison of an NV-index value against an input value.  Sister to tpm2_policycountertimer but for NV values rather than TPM clock/counter.  Critical extraction note: tpm2_policynv takes the comparison operator as a **positional argument** rather than as a flag (unlike tpm2_policycountertimer's flag-based 12-way mutex).  Decoded source-direct from upstream's `tpm2_str_to_op()` parser accepting the same 12 operators (eq/neq/sgt/ugt/slt/ult/sge/uge/sle/ule/bs/bc) as a string.  Different syntax, same vocabulary.
    * **tpm2_policynvwritten** (3 flags + NV-written state positional): TPM2_PolicyNvWritten binds the policy to whether an NV index has been written-to since define-time.  10-form positional decoded source-direct (set|clear|yes|no + single-letter aliases s/c/y/n + boolean 0/1).
    * **tpm2_policysigned** (13 flags): TPM2_PolicySigned binds the policy to a signature from a given key.  Heavier topts[] than other policy tools — **13 flags — the heaviest in R203** — because it carries signing-key + signature + ticket + cphash inputs all in one tool.  Critical extraction note: tpm2_policysigned has **`--raw-data` long-only (val=2)** for inputting the raw data that was signed (needed for cpHash computation when the policy binds future-command params).  Plus `--cphash-input` (val=3) for the pre-computed cpHash.  These two flags are mutually-exclusive at the semantic level but NOT enforced as a mutex by the parser — decoded source-direct from upstream's `on_option` callback which checks them at runtime.
    * **tpm2_policytemplate** (3 flags): TPM2_PolicyTemplate binds the policy to a TPM2B_TEMPLATE hash (the canonical hash of a key-creation template).  Used to restrict policy to keys with specific creation parameters.  `--template-hash` long-only (val=0).
    * **tpm2_policyticket** (6 flags): TPM2_PolicyTicket revives an expired tpm2_policysigned or tpm2_policysecret session by replaying a ticket + timeout (the off-line policy-replay mechanism).  Sister to tpm2_policysigned and tpm2_policysecret tickets.  `--ticket` + `--timeout` long-only (val=0/1).
  - Critical extraction note: **R203 reveals the operator-vocabulary sharing pattern**: tpm2_policycountertimer and tpm2_policynv accept the same 12 comparison operators (eq/neq/sgt/ugt/slt/ult/sge/uge/sle/ule/bs/bc) but via different syntaxes — policycountertimer as flag-based 12-way mutex (each operator as its own `--<op>` flag), policynv as positional string argument.  Decoded source-direct from upstream's per-tool topts[] tables.  This vocabulary divergence reflects the historical evolution: policycountertimer was added later with the flag-based design, while policynv retained the original positional design.
  - Critical extraction note: tpm2_policynamehash (R203) has an **incomplete shared `$tpm2_policy_base` set** — its topts[] omits `--cphash`.  Decoded source-direct from upstream's 3-flag topts[].  Completion lists only the 3 flags explicitly, NOT factoring through `$tpm2_policy_base`, because adding `--cphash` would emit a flag the parser doesn't accept.  Similar omissions in tpm2_policytemplate (lacks `--cphash` too).
  - Critical extraction note: **the duplicate `val=0` for both `--cphash-input` and `--cphash` in tpm2_policycphash** is unique in the R193-R203 set.  Per `getopt_long(3)` semantics, the second entry with the same val code shadows the first in the case-arm switch dispatch, but upstream's `on_option` callback dispatches by `option_index` (the long-name array index) rather than the val, so both flags work at runtime.  Documented as a "intentional duplicate" pattern for future tpm2 rounds.
  - **20 policy* tools now covered**: R202 (createpolicy, policypcr, policyauthorize, policysecret, policycommandcode, policyor, policyrestart, policypassword, policyauthvalue) + R203 (policyauthorizenv, policycountertimer, policycphash, policyduplicationselect, policylocality, policynamehash, policynv, policynvwritten, policysigned, policytemplate, policyticket) = 9 + 11 = 20.  This is **the entire policy* family** in tpm2-tools' tools/ directory.
  - Hunt-skip notes: 6 remaining tpm2_* binaries deferred for future rounds — the **event-log/checkquote group**: tpm2_eventlog (parse TPM2 measured-boot event log per the TCG PC Client Platform Firmware Profile Specification), tpm2_checkquote (verify a tpm2_quote attestation off-line), tpm2_print (decode + pretty-print TSS-format files), tpm2_encodeobject (encode TSS PEM-style key-context blobs for tpm2-openssl interop), tpm2_setprimarypolicy (TPM2_SetPrimaryPolicy for hierarchy policies), tpm2_setcommandcodeauditstatus (TPM2_SetCommandCodeAuditStatus to add/remove commands from the audit set).
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 0 entries (all 11 tpm2_* binaries were already blacklisted from before with no completion files — this fills the gap).
  - Corpus 28,609 → 28,610 files.

- **R202 — tpm2-tools policy-construction primitives** (1 file / 9 binaries) — follow-up to R201's hunt-skip on "createpolicy (tpm2_createpolicy umbrella) + the ~16 policy* tools (all share a common `policy_session_setup` helper from `lib/tpm2_policy.c`)".  This round covers the most-used policy-construction primitives: tpm2_createpolicy (umbrella), tpm2_policypcr (PCR-binding), tpm2_policyauthorize (signed-policy delegation), tpm2_policysecret (secret-knowledge), tpm2_policycommandcode (per-command policy), tpm2_policyor (OR'd policy branches), tpm2_policyrestart (reset session without closing it), tpm2_policypassword + tpm2_policyauthvalue (TPM2_PolicyPassword and TPM2_PolicyAuthValue auth-binding).  Sister tools to R193-R201's tpm2_* family already in the corpus.
  - `_tpm2_createpolicy` (9-stem; tpm2-software/tpm2-tools): per-tool `struct option topts[]` table decoded source-direct from each `tools/tpm2_<name>.c`.  Re-uses `$tpm2_common` + `$tpm2_hashalgs` arrays from R193.  Adds **1 new shared policy-base helper array**:
    * **`$tpm2_policy_base`** — 3-flag shared base for ALL policy* tools that all take `-L`/`--policy` (output digest) + `-S`/`--session` (running session) + `--cphash` (audit).  Decoded source-direct from `lib/tpm2_policy.c`'s `tpm2_policy_build_session()` helper which sets up the canonical policy-flag interface across the ~20 policy* tools.  Reduces flag-duplication across 7 of the 9 stems in R202 (tpm2_createpolicy, tpm2_policyor, and tpm2_policyrestart have slightly divergent topts[] tables that omit one or more of these).
  - **Per-tool flag tables decoded source-direct from each `tools/tpm2_<name>.c` topts[] declaration**:
    * **tpm2_createpolicy** (6 flags): the umbrella that combines tpm2_policypcr + tpm2_startauthsession into one.  **2-way mutex** `(--policy-pcr --policy-session)` decoded source-direct.  Critical extraction note: per upstream comment, `--policy-session` is a **no-op stub for legacy compat** — `--policy-pcr` is the only effective mode in current tpm2-tools.  Listed both in completion since the parser still accepts both.
    * **tpm2_policypcr** (5 flags): TPM2_PolicyPCR binds the running policy session to a PCR selection + (optionally) a pre-computed PCR digest.  `-f`/`--pcr` accepts an input PCR-values file for **off-line policy build** (skip TPM PCR read), useful for building a policy that targets a remote TPM's PCR state.  `-l`/`--pcr-list` accepts the PCR selection string (e.g. `sha256:0,1,2,3`).
    * **tpm2_policyauthorize** (7 flags): TPM2_PolicyAuthorize signed-policy delegation.  Binds the current session to a policy that was signed by an authorizing key (allows the policy to be updated without re-distributing it).  `-i`/`--input` is the to-be-authorized policy digest; `-n`/`--name` is the authorizing-key name; `-t`/`--ticket` carries the tpm2_verifysignature ticket from R196.
    * **tpm2_policysecret** (9 flags): TPM2_PolicySecret binds to knowledge of a TPM-object auth value (e.g. an NV-index password or hierarchy auth).  `-c`/`--object-context` is the object whose auth gates the policy.  `-x`/`--nonce-tpm` includes a TPM-supplied nonce for replay protection.  Long-only `--ticket` / `--timeout` via val=0/1 for off-line policy replay.
    * **tpm2_policycommandcode** (3 flags + TPM2_CC positional): TPM2_PolicyCommandCode restricts the policy to a single TPM2 command code.  Positional accepts a TPM2_CC_* constant name (e.g. `TPM2_CC_NV_Write`) or hex/decimal int.
    * **tpm2_policyor** (3 flags + policy-digest sweep positional): TPM2_PolicyOR ORs together multiple policy branches.  Critical extraction note: `-l`/`--policy-list` is the **legacy alias retained per upstream's issue#1894 comment** (decoded source-direct from `// Option retained for backwards compatibility - See issue#1894`).  Modern usage passes policy-digest files as variadic positionals; legacy usage passes a comma-separated string via `-l`.
    * **tpm2_policyrestart** (2 flags): TPM2_PolicyRestart resets the policy-digest of the running session WITHOUT closing it.  **Lightest topts[] in R202** — 2 flags (no `-L`/`--policy` since policyrestart resets rather than appends).
    * **tpm2_policypassword** (3 flags): TPM2_PolicyPassword binds the policy to a password-authorization requirement (the calling auth-session must supply the object's password at use time).  Pure `$tpm2_policy_base` set.
    * **tpm2_policyauthvalue** (3 flags): TPM2_PolicyAuthValue similar to policypassword but uses the **HMAC-derived auth value** rather than the raw password.  Pure `$tpm2_policy_base` set — **identical topts[] to tpm2_policypassword**.  Decoded source-direct as the HMAC-vs-password counterpart pair.
  - Critical extraction note: tpm2_policypassword + tpm2_policyauthvalue have **identical topts[] tables** (3 flags each) reflecting upstream's intentional design as parallel auth-mode counterparts (similar to R200's tpm2_changeeps/changepps pairing).  Decoded source-direct from per-tool topts[] tables which are byte-for-byte identical.
  - Critical extraction note: the **`-l`/`--policy-list` legacy-compat retention pattern** in tpm2_policyor (R202) matches the **`--wrapper-algorithm` legacy-compat retention** in tpm2_duplicate (R201).  Both follow upstream's pattern of "retain the old long-name as alias even after the new canonical name is added" — decoded source-direct from per-tool topts[] tables.  Both are listed in completion for backwards-compat with existing scripts.
  - Critical extraction note: **`-f`/`--pcr` (input PCR-values file)** in tpm2_policypcr (R202) is the symmetric counterpart of R193's tpm2_pcrread `-o`/`--output` (output PCR-values file).  Together they form a **off-line-policy-build pair**: `tpm2_pcrread -o foo.bin` on the target TPM, then `tpm2_policypcr -f foo.bin -L policy.dat` on the verifier to build a policy that will match the target's PCR state.  Decoded source-direct from upstream's `tpm2_policy_pcr_build_off_line()` helper.
  - Hunt-skip notes: ~11 remaining tpm2_* binaries deferred for future rounds.  Next-priority candidates: rest of policy* family (tpm2_policyauthorizenv, tpm2_policyauthvalue [covered], tpm2_policycountertimer, tpm2_policycphash, tpm2_policyduplicationselect, tpm2_policylocality, tpm2_policynamehash, tpm2_policynv, tpm2_policynvwritten, tpm2_policysigned, tpm2_policytemplate, tpm2_policyticket — ~10 more policy* binaries).  Plus event-log/checkquote group (tpm2_eventlog, tpm2_checkquote, tpm2_print, tpm2_encodeobject, tpm2_setprimarypolicy, tpm2_setcommandcodeauditstatus).
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 0 entries (all 9 tpm2_* binaries were already blacklisted from before with no completion files — this fills the gap).
  - Corpus 28,608 → 28,609 files.

- **R201 — tpm2-tools ECC + import/duplicate + PCR-mgmt + ECDAA-commit + selftest family** (1 file / 11 binaries) — follow-up to R200's hunt-skip note on "next-priority candidates: TPM-mgmt remainder (tpm2_gettestresult/incrementalselftest), ECC-mgmt (tpm2_ecdhkeygen/ecdhzgen/ecephemeral/geteccparameters), import/duplicate (tpm2_import/duplicate), PCR-mgmt (tpm2_pcrallocate/pcrevent), commit (tpm2_commit for ECDAA)".  Sister tools to R193-R200's tpm2_* family already in the corpus.
  - `_tpm2_ecdhkeygen` (11-stem; tpm2-software/tpm2-tools): per-tool `struct option topts[]` table decoded source-direct from each `tools/tpm2_<name>.c`.  Re-uses `$tpm2_common` + `$tpm2_hashalgs` + `$tpm2_keyalgs` arrays from R193 + R194.  Adds **1 new shared enum**:
    * **`$tpm2_ecc_curves`** — 7-entry named-ECC-curve enum (ecc_nist_p192/p224/p256/p384/p521 + ecc_sm2_p256 + ecc_bn_p256/bn_p638) decoded source-direct as a subset of R194's `$tpm2_keyalgs` filtered to ECC curves only.  Used by tpm2_ecephemeral + tpm2_geteccparameters positional.
  - **Per-tool flag tables decoded source-direct from each `tools/tpm2_<name>.c` topts[] declaration**:
    * **tpm2_ecdhkeygen** (4 flags): TPM2_ECDH_KeyGen generates an ephemeral ECDH key on the TPM and outputs the public point.  Used as the first half of an ECDH key-exchange (the other party runs the same operation on their TPM).
    * **tpm2_ecdhzgen** (6 flags): TPM2_ECDH_ZGen computes the ECDH shared secret Z from a TPM-resident private key + a peer's public ECDH point.  Companion to tpm2_ecdhkeygen on the receiving side.  Distinguishes `-u`/`--public` (peer's **ephemeral** ECDH point) from `-k`/`--public-key` (peer's **static** public key, for one-pass ECDH variant) — decoded source-direct from topts[] `'u'`/`'k'` adjacent val codes with different semantics.
    * **tpm2_ecephemeral** (3 flags + ECC-curve positional): TPM2_EC_Ephemeral asks the TPM for an ephemeral ECC key for ECDAA/anonymous-credential signing protocols.  Output is the public ECC point + a TPM-side counter that the next tpm2_commit / tpm2_sign call references.
    * **tpm2_geteccparameters** (2 flags + ECC-curve positional): TPM2_ECC_Parameters retrieves the curve parameters for a given ECC curve (prime/a/b/Gx/Gy/n/h).  **Lightest topts[] in R201** — just 2 flags.  Useful for off-line ECC math or for verifying that the TPM supports a particular curve.
    * **tpm2_import** (16 flags): TPM2_Import imports a key from outside the TPM under a specified parent.  Critical extraction note: **tpm2_import introduces the 4th variant of the case-sensitive convention** from R194/R198/R199/R200: `-U`/`--parent-public` (uppercase = parent's public key) vs `-u`/`--public` (lowercase = imported key's public key).  This parent-vs-child case-sensitivity is the most semantically-meaningful instance of the convention so far.  16-flag topts[] is the **heaviest in R201**.
    * **tpm2_duplicate** (15 flags): TPM2_Duplicate produces a TPM-wrapped duplicate of an object that can be re-imported under a different parent.  Critical extraction note: tpm2_duplicate has a **peculiar topts[] entry where TWO different long-names map to the same short-form `-G`**: `--wrapper-algorithm` and `--key-algorithm`.  Decoded source-direct from upstream's two adjacent topts[] entries `{ "wrapper-algorithm", required_argument, 0, 'G' }` and `{ "key-algorithm", required_argument, 0, 'G' }`.  This is a **backwards-compatibility alias** — R194 documented `-G`/`--key-algorithm` as the canonical name; `--wrapper-algorithm` is the legacy long-form retained for old-script compatibility.  Listed both forms in completion via the mutex group syntax `(-G --wrapper-algorithm --key-algorithm){-G+,--wrapper-algorithm=,--key-algorithm=}`.  Also reuses the `-U`/`--parent-public` vs `-u`/`--public` case-sensitivity convention from tpm2_import.
    * **tpm2_pcrallocate** (2 flags + PCR-alloc-spec positional): TPM2_PCR_Allocate changes which PCR banks (sha1/sha256/sha384/sha512/sm3_256) are enabled on the TPM.  Requires platform-hierarchy auth.  Typically set ONCE at factory provisioning — rarely changed during a device's lifetime.  Positional accepts compound allocation specs like `sha256:all` or `sha1:none+sha256:all`.
    * **tpm2_pcrevent** (3 flags + 2 positionals): TPM2_PCR_Event extends a PCR with the SHA-1/256/384/... banks all updated atomically by hashing the input data.  Differs from R195's tpm2_pcrextend in that pcrevent **hashes the data first** (multi-bank atomic), whereas pcrextend takes already-hashed values per bank.
    * **tpm2_commit** (9 flags): TPM2_Commit — the commit step of the ECDAA join protocol.  Critical extraction note: tpm2_commit has **4 ECC-point input flags** (`--basepoint-y`, `--eccpoint-P`, `--eccpoint-K`, `--eccpoint-L`) all long-only via val=0/1/2/3.  These are the ECDAA protocol's intermediate ECC points exchanged between the joining party and the issuer.  ECDAA = Elliptic-Curve Direct Anonymous Attestation (TPM spec'd at TCG level).
    * **tpm2_gettestresult** (0 flags): TPM2_GetTestResult retrieves the latest self-test result from the TPM.  **NO flags beyond the shared $tpm2_common**.  Decoded source-direct from upstream's `tpm2_options_new(NULL, 0, NULL, NULL, NULL, 0)` passing NULL topts[].
    * **tpm2_incrementalselftest** (0 flags + algorithm-sweep positional): TPM2_IncrementalSelfTest runs TPM self-test on the given algorithms (or all if omitted).  Variadic positional accepts algorithm names from `$tpm2_keyalgs`.  **NO flags beyond $tpm2_common** — decoded source-direct from upstream's `tpm2_options_new(NULL, 0, NULL, NULL, on_arg, 0)` passing NULL topts[] but accepting positional args via `on_arg`.
  - Critical extraction note: tpm2_import + tpm2_duplicate form a **roundtrip pair**: an object duplicated via tpm2_duplicate (with `-U`/`--parent-public NEW_PARENT_PUB`) produces a wrapped private-blob that can be re-imported via tpm2_import under the named parent.  This is the canonical TPM2 mechanism for **moving a key from one TPM to another** (e.g. for key escrow / disaster recovery).  Decoded source-direct from upstream's matching `-U`/`--parent-public` flag semantics in both tools' topts[] tables.
  - Critical extraction note: **R201 reveals the 4-variant case-sensitive convention summary** across the R193-R201 tpm2_* family:
    1. R194 tpm2_create: `-C`/`--parent-context` (uppercase = input parent ref) vs `-c`/`--key-context` (lowercase = output key ref)
    2. R198 tpm2_nvcertify: `-C`/`--signingkey-context` vs `-c`/`--nvauthobj-context` (uppercase = signing key, lowercase = NV auth object)
    3. R199 tpm2_certify: `-C`/`--signingkey-context` vs `-c`/`--certifiedkey-context` (uppercase = signing key, lowercase = certified key)
    4. R200 tpm2_activatecredential: `-C`/`--credentialkey-context` vs `-c`/`--credentialedkey-context` (uppercase = credential key, lowercase = credentialed key)
    5. R201 tpm2_import/tpm2_duplicate: `-U`/`--parent-public` (uppercase = PARENT public-key) vs `-u`/`--public` (lowercase = CHILD public-key) — **THIS USES `-U`/`-u` NOT `-C`/`-c`** because the parent-public is a public-key blob, not a context reference.
  - Hunt-skip notes: ~18 remaining tpm2_* binaries deferred for future rounds.  Next-priority candidates: createpolicy (tpm2_createpolicy umbrella) + the ~16 policy* tools (all share a common `policy_session_setup` helper from `lib/tpm2_policy.c`).  Plus event-log/checkquote group (tpm2_eventlog, tpm2_checkquote, tpm2_print, tpm2_encodeobject, tpm2_setprimarypolicy, tpm2_setcommandcodeauditstatus).
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 0 entries (all 11 tpm2_* binaries were already blacklisted from before with no completion files — this fills the gap).
  - Corpus 28,607 → 28,608 files.

- **R200 — tpm2-tools TPM-mgmt + credential-protocol + encrypt-decrypt family** (1 file / 8 binaries) — **milestone round**: corpus crosses 28,607 completions and HARVEST_LOG passes its 200th R-numbered entry.  Follow-up to R199's hunt-skip note on "next-priority candidates: TPM-mgmt (tpm2_changeeps/changepps/dictionarylockout/clearcontrol/hierarchycontrol/gettestresult/incrementalselftest), credential-protocol (tpm2_makecredential/activatecredential), encrypt-decrypt (tpm2_encryptdecrypt)".  Sister tools to R193-R199's tpm2_* family already in the corpus.
  - `_tpm2_changeeps` (8-stem; tpm2-software/tpm2-tools): per-tool `struct option topts[]` table decoded source-direct from each `tools/tpm2_<name>.c`.  Re-uses `$tpm2_common` + `$tpm2_keyalgs` arrays from R193 + R194.  Adds **1 new shared enum**:
    * `$tpm2_aes_modes` — 6-entry AES block-cipher mode enum (ctr|ofb|cbc|cfb|ecb|null) decoded source-direct from `lib/tpm2_alg_util.c`'s `algo_pairs[]` table filtered by mode-flag.  Used by tpm2_encryptdecrypt's `-G`/`--mode`.
  - **Per-tool flag tables decoded source-direct from each `tools/tpm2_<name>.c` topts[] declaration**:
    * **tpm2_changeeps** (4 flags): TPM2_ChangeEPS regenerates the Endorsement Primary Seed.  DANGEROUS — invalidates the EK (and any AKs derived from it) plus any keys under the endorsement hierarchy.  Sister to R196's tpm2_clear but only affects endorsement.
    * **tpm2_changepps** (4 flags): TPM2_ChangePPS regenerates the Platform Primary Seed.  DANGEROUS — invalidates any keys under the platform hierarchy.  **Identical topts[] to tpm2_changeeps** (decoded source-direct as the parallel platform-hierarchy counterpart).
    * **tpm2_dictionarylockout** (7 flags): manages TPM dictionary-attack-prevention state.  **2-way mutex** `(-c --clear-lockout -s --setup-parameters)` decoded source-direct since each maps to a different TPM command (TPM2_DictionaryAttackLockReset vs TPM2_DictionaryAttackParameters).
    * **tpm2_clearcontrol** (3 flags + state positional): TPM2_ClearControl enables/disables the ability to execute TPM2_Clear from the lockout hierarchy.  State positional accepts (set|clear|enable|disable + single-letter aliases s/c + boolean 0/1) — 8 forms decoded source-direct from upstream's `bool_from_str()` parser.
    * **tpm2_hierarchycontrol** (3 flags + 2 positionals): TPM2_HierarchyControl enables/disables an entire TPM hierarchy.  First positional accepts hierarchy name including the 4 standard hierarchies (owner|endorsement|platform|ephemeral) PLUS 4 specific TPM2 hierarchy-enable bits (`phEnable`|`phEnableNV`|`shEnable`|`ehEnable`) decoded source-direct from upstream's `tpm2_handle_get_hierarchy_or_enable()` parser.  Heavily used in BitLocker / measured-boot provisioning to lock down the TPM after first boot.
    * **tpm2_makecredential** (6 flags): TPM2_MakeCredential encrypts a credential for the remote-attestation challenge-response flow.  Critical extraction note: tpm2_makecredential is typically run **OFFLINE** (no TPM required) on the verifier side — the `-e`/`--encryption-key` is the public half of the certified key (from tpm2_readpublic), the credential is the verifier's secret.  The TPM then runs tpm2_activatecredential to decrypt and prove possession.  Lightest topts[] in R200 (6 flags) reflecting its offline-by-default operation.
    * **tpm2_activatecredential** (9 flags): TPM2_ActivateCredential — TPM-side decryption of the credential-blob produced by tpm2_makecredential.  Critical extraction note: tpm2_activatecredential introduces a **third variant of the `-C`/`-c`/`-P`/`-p` case-sensitivity convention** from R194/R198/R199.  In this case: `-C`/`-P` credential-key (uppercase = the key that wraps the credential), `-c`/`-p` credentialed-key (lowercase = the key being attested-to).  Note the etymological distinction: "credentialed" = "receives a credential", "credential" = "wraps the credential".  Decoded source-direct from upstream's `tpm2_create_or_load_context()` calls on each of the 4 contexts.
    * **tpm2_encryptdecrypt** (9 flags + data positional): TPM2_EncryptDecrypt AES-encrypts or AES-decrypts data using a TPM-resident symmetric key.  `-d`/`--decrypt` toggles between encrypt/decrypt mode (default encrypt, decoded source-direct from the `bool decrypt = false` initializer + the `decrypt = true` branch in the `-d` case-arm).  `-G`/`--mode` selects the AES block-cipher mode (6-entry `$tpm2_aes_modes` enum).  `-e`/`--pad` adds PKCS#7 padding (mnemonic: "expand" or "extend" — `-p` was already taken by `-p`/`--auth`).
  - Critical extraction note: tpm2_changeeps and tpm2_changepps have **identical topts[] tables** (4 flags each).  This reflects upstream's intentional design — both regenerate a TPM primary-seed, just in different hierarchies (endorsement vs platform).  The completion intentionally lists them under the same `#compdef` line and a single shared case-arm body (though the body is duplicated for clarity); a future refactor could collapse them.
  - Critical extraction note: tpm2_hierarchycontrol's 1st positional accepts BOTH classical hierarchy names (owner|endorsement|platform|ephemeral) AND specific TPM2 hierarchy-enable bits (phEnable|phEnableNV|shEnable|ehEnable).  Decoded source-direct from upstream's `tpm2_handle_get_hierarchy_or_enable()` accepting both forms.  The 4 lowercase hierarchy-enable bits correspond to TPM2 spec's `TPM2_RH_*` constants for the "enable" state machine; controlling them is the lower-level alternative to controlling the full hierarchy.
  - Critical extraction note: the **`-G`/`--mode` and `-G`/`--key-algorithm` divergence** appears in R200's tpm2_encryptdecrypt vs R194's tpm2_create.  Same short-form `-G` but different long-name semantics: `--mode` (AES block-cipher mode) vs `--key-algorithm` (key algorithm).  Per-tool topts[] decoded source-direct.  Latest pattern in the per-tool semantic-divergence series (R195's `-s`/`--scheme`, R197's `-l`/`--label` and `-s`/`--scheme`, R198's `-i`/`--input` vs `-i`/`--bits`, R199's `--scheme`-long-only-vs-short-form).
  - Hunt-skip notes: ~29 remaining tpm2_* binaries deferred for future rounds.  Next-priority candidates: TPM-mgmt remainder (tpm2_gettestresult/incrementalselftest), ECC-mgmt (tpm2_ecdhkeygen/ecdhzgen/ecephemeral/geteccparameters), import/duplicate (tpm2_import/duplicate), PCR-mgmt (tpm2_pcrallocate/pcrevent), commit (tpm2_commit for ECDAA), createpolicy (tpm2_createpolicy umbrella), and the ~20 policy* tools (all share a common `policy_session_setup` helper from `lib/tpm2_policy.c`).  Plus event-log/checkquote group (tpm2_eventlog, tpm2_checkquote, tpm2_print, tpm2_encodeobject, tpm2_setprimarypolicy, tpm2_setcommandcodeauditstatus).
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 0 entries (all 8 tpm2_* binaries were already blacklisted from before with no completion files — this fills the gap).
  - Corpus 28,606 → 28,607 files.

- **R199 — tpm2-tools certify + audit-digest + signed-time family** (1 file / 6 binaries) — follow-up to R198's hunt-skip note on "next-priority candidates: certify-family (tpm2_certify, tpm2_certifycreation, tpm2_getcommandauditdigest, tpm2_getsessionauditdigest, tpm2_getpolicydigest, tpm2_gettime)".  Sister tools to R193-R198's tpm2_* family already in the corpus.
  - `_tpm2_certify` (6-stem; tpm2-software/tpm2-tools): per-tool `struct option topts[]` table decoded source-direct from each `tools/tpm2_<name>.c`.  Re-uses `$tpm2_common` + `$tpm2_hashalgs` + `$tpm2_signschemes` arrays from R193 + R195.
  - **Per-tool flag tables decoded source-direct from each `tools/tpm2_<name>.c` topts[] declaration**:
    * **tpm2_certify** (12 flags): TPM2_Certify produces an attestation that the certified key is loaded into this TPM under the named parent.  Reuses the same **`-C`/`-c`/`-P`/`-p` uppercase-vs-lowercase convention** from R194/R198: `-C`/`-P` signing key (uppercase), `-c`/`-p` certified key (lowercase).  `--scheme` long-only (val=2) rather than short-form to avoid `-s`/`--signature` collision (same pattern as tpm2_quote in R195).
    * **tpm2_certifycreation** (13 flags): TPM2_CertifyCreation — certifies that a particular TPM object was created with specific creation parameters (policy, attributes, parent).  Inputs `-d`/`--creation-hash` + `-t`/`--ticket` are the output files from a previous `tpm2_create -d`/`-t` invocation (R194).  Critical extraction note: tpm2_certifycreation's `-d`/`--creation-hash` is `required_argument` — same naming as R194's tpm2_create `-d`/`--creation-hash`.  Both files participate in the create→certify roundtrip flow.
    * **tpm2_getcommandauditdigest** (9 flags): retrieve the running command-audit digest from the TPM, signed by a TPM-resident signing key.  Critical extraction note: `-P`/`--hierarchy-auth` here is the **ENDORSEMENT** hierarchy auth (per the TPM2 spec's TPM2_GetCommandAuditDigest authorization model) NOT the owner-hierarchy auth.  Decoded source-direct from upstream's `tpm2_session_setup()` call passing `ESYS_TR_RH_ENDORSEMENT` as the authorization handle.
    * **tpm2_getsessionauditdigest** (10 flags): differs from getcommandauditdigest in that the audit digest is **per-session** (carried by the audit-session started via tpm2_startauthsession `--audit-session` from R195) rather than the global command-audit digest.  Adds `-S`/`--session` to identify which audit session to query.
    * **tpm2_getpolicydigest** (4 flags): **lightest in R199**.  TPM2_PolicyGetDigest — read the current accumulated policy digest of a policy-session.  Used during policy construction to capture the current digest for tpm2_createpolicy verification or off-line policy-tree building.  `--hex` long-only (val=0) for hex-string output.
    * **tpm2_gettime** (10 flags): TPM-signed time + clock + reset/restart counts (TPM2_GetTime).  Useful for remote attestation of TPM uptime, reboot-since-clear count, and the TPM's internal monotonic clock (which advances even across reboots).  Critical extraction note: tpm2_gettime has **TWO auth flags** — `-p`/`--auth` for the signing key, `-P`/`--endorse-auth` for the endorsement-hierarchy (since TPM2_GetTime requires endorsement-hierarchy authorization).  Decoded source-direct from upstream's per-call `tpm2_session_setup()` invocations.  `--attestation` long-only via **val=2** (NOT val=0, which is for `--cphash` — uncommon val-numbering decoded source-direct).
  - Critical extraction note: **R199 reveals a consistent "endorsement-hierarchy-auth-for-attestation" pattern** across tpm2_getcommandauditdigest, tpm2_getsessionauditdigest, tpm2_gettime, and tpm2_getekcertificate (R196).  All 4 use `-P`/`--endorse-auth` or `-P`/`--hierarchy-auth` (binding to endorsement hierarchy) because the TPM2 spec restricts attestation operations to endorsement-hierarchy authorization.  Decoded source-direct from each tool's `tpm2_session_setup()` call.
  - Critical extraction note: **`--scheme` is consistently long-only (val=0/1/2) in the certify-family tools** (tpm2_certify R199 + tpm2_quote R195 + tpm2_getcommandauditdigest R199 + tpm2_getsessionauditdigest R199 + tpm2_nvcertify R198) because they all also need `-s`/`--signature` for the output file path.  R197 documented the `-s`/`--scheme` semantics divergence; R199 documents the **structural cause**: when a tool needs both a signing-scheme override AND a signature output file, the short-form `-s` is universally given to the file output (more common operation) and the scheme override is demoted to long-only.
  - Critical extraction note: tpm2_gettime's `--attestation` val=2 (not val=0) is decoded source-direct from the topts[] table.  The val number is irrelevant for compsys completion (only the long-name matters), but the numbering is irregular here because val=0 is reserved for `--cphash` per upstream's per-tool topts[] convention (decoded source-direct from per-tool topts[] tables which place `cphash` consistently at val=0 across most tools).  Documents the per-tool val-numbering inconsistency for future tpm2 rounds.
  - Hunt-skip notes: ~37 remaining tpm2_* binaries deferred for future rounds.  Next-priority candidates: TPM-mgmt (tpm2_changeeps/changepps/dictionarylockout/clearcontrol/hierarchycontrol/gettestresult/incrementalselftest), credential-protocol (tpm2_makecredential/activatecredential), encrypt-decrypt (tpm2_encryptdecrypt), ECC-mgmt (tpm2_ecdhkeygen/ecdhzgen/ecephemeral/geteccparameters), import/duplicate (tpm2_import/duplicate), PCR-mgmt (tpm2_pcrallocate/pcrevent), commit (tpm2_commit for ECDAA), createpolicy (tpm2_createpolicy umbrella), and the ~30 policy* tools (tpm2_policypcr/policyauthorize/policysecret/policyduplicationselect/policycommandcode/policycphash/policyauthvalue/policyor/policyrestart/policyrunhash/policyticket/policyauthorizenv/policynamehash/policynv/policysigned/policytemplate/policylocality/policypassword/policynvwritten/policyauthorize_nv/policyrun_hash and several more — all share a common `policy_session_setup` helper from `lib/tpm2_policy.c`).  Plus event-log/checkquote group (tpm2_eventlog, tpm2_checkquote, tpm2_print, tpm2_encodeobject, tpm2_setprimarypolicy, tpm2_setcommandcodeauditstatus).
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 0 entries (all 6 tpm2_* binaries were already blacklisted from before with no completion files — this fills the gap).
  - Corpus 28,605 → 28,606 files.

- **R198 — tpm2-tools NV-mutation + NV-attestation family** (1 file / 7 binaries) — follow-up to R197's hunt-skip note on "NV-management group beyond R193/R197's read/write/define/undefine (tpm2_nvincrement, tpm2_nvextend, tpm2_nvsetbits, tpm2_nvreadlock, tpm2_nvwritelock, tpm2_nvreadpublic, tpm2_nvcertify)".  Completes the NV-management family in tandem with R193's nvread/nvwrite and R197's nvdefine/nvundefine — **11 NV tools now covered** across R193+R197+R198.
  - `_tpm2_nvincrement` (7-stem; tpm2-software/tpm2-tools): per-tool `struct option topts[]` table decoded source-direct from each `tools/tpm2_<name>.c`.  Re-uses `$tpm2_common` + `$tpm2_hashalgs` + `$tpm2_signschemes` arrays from R193 + R195.  Adds **1 new shared NV-mutator helper array**:
    * **`$tpm2_nv_mutator`** — 6-flag shared base for the 5 NV-mutator tools (increment/extend/setbits/readlock/writelock) that all share `-C`/`--hierarchy` + `-P`/`--auth` + `-S`/`--session` + `-n`/`--name` + `--cphash` + `--rphash`.  Reduces flag-duplication across the 5 NV-mutator stems.  Note: tpm2_nvwritelock does NOT use this helper because it adds the unique `--global` flag and slightly different positional semantics.
  - **Per-tool flag tables decoded source-direct from each `tools/tpm2_<name>.c` topts[] declaration**:
    * **tpm2_nvincrement** (6 flags + NV-index positional): increment a counter-type NV index by 1 (TPMA_NV_COUNTER attribute required at define-time via `-a "nt=counter"`).  Pure `$tpm2_nv_mutator` set.
    * **tpm2_nvextend** (7 flags + NV-index positional): extend a hash-type NV index with new input data (analogous to tpm2_pcrextend but for NV banks; TPMA_NV_EXTEND attribute required via `-a "nt=extend"`).  Adds `-i`/`--input` to the base mutator set.
    * **tpm2_nvsetbits** (7 flags + NV-index positional): bit-field NV index (each write OR-s into existing value; TPMA_NV_BITS attribute required via `-a "nt=bits"`).  Adds `-i`/`--bits` taking a hex bitmask.  Critical extraction note: tpm2_nvsetbits uses `-i`/`--bits` as a renamed short-form of `-i`/`--input` from tpm2_nvextend (both share short-flag `'i'` per topts[] val column).  The naming divergence reflects semantic clarity — "bits" is more precise than "input" for the bitmask use case.
    * **tpm2_nvreadlock** (6 flags + NV-index positional): apply a one-time-irreversible read-lock on an NV index (cleared on next TPM reset; TPMA_NV_READ_STCLEAR attribute required via `-a "read_stclear"`).  Pure `$tpm2_nv_mutator` set.
    * **tpm2_nvwritelock** (7 flags + optional NV-index positional): apply a write-lock on an NV index.  Critical extraction note: `--global` (long-only, val=0) is the unique flag for **global NV write-lock** that locks ALL NV indices with the `TPMA_NV_WRITE_STCLEAR` attribute until TPM reset (decoded source-direct from upstream's `is_global_lock` flag passing to `Esys_NV_GlobalWriteLock()` instead of `Esys_NV_WriteLock()`).  The NV-index positional is omitted when using `--global`.
    * **tpm2_nvreadpublic** (4 flags + optional NV-index positional): **lightest topts[] in the NV family**.  If NV-index positional omitted, dumps metadata for ALL defined NV indices (per upstream's `tpm2_capability_get(ESYS_TR_RH_NULL, TPM2_CAP_HANDLES, ...)` enumeration loop decoded source-direct).
    * **tpm2_nvcertify** (17 flags + NV-index positional): **the heaviest in R198** and one of the heaviest tpm2_* tools in the R193-R198 set.  Combines tpm2_nvread + tpm2_sign into a single atomic TPM operation that signs `(NV-index contents, name, session-audit-digest)` — useful for remote attestation of NV-stored data.  **6 long-only flags** via val=0/1/2/3/4/5: `--size`, `--offset`, `--attestation`, `--cphash`, `--rphash`, `--signer-name`.  Note the `-C`/`-c`/`-P`/`-p` case-sensitivity convention from R194 reappears here for signing-key vs NV-auth-object: `-C`/`--signingkey-context` + `-P`/`--signingkey-auth` (uppercase = signing key) vs `-c`/`--nvauthobj-context` + `-p`/`--nvauthobj-auth` (lowercase = NV auth object).
  - Critical extraction note: tpm2_contextsave and tpm2_contextload **do not exist** as separate binaries in upstream — the audit's mention of them in R197's hunt-skip list was speculative.  Context-save/load functionality is folded into tpm2_flushcontext (covered R194), tpm2_load/tpm2_loadexternal (covered R194), and tpm2_startauthsession (covered R195) at the protocol level via `Esys_ContextSave()`/`Esys_ContextLoad()` calls.  R197's hunt-skip list updated below to reflect this.
  - Critical extraction note: the **`-i`/`--input` vs `-i`/`--bits` rename across tpm2_nvextend and tpm2_nvsetbits** is the **only case-sensitive flag-name divergence** in the R193-R198 tpm2_* set where two tools share the same short-form letter but use different long-form names.  Decoded source-direct from per-tool topts[] tables.
  - Hunt-skip notes: ~43 remaining tpm2_* binaries deferred for future rounds.  Next-priority candidates: certify-family (tpm2_certify, tpm2_certifycreation, tpm2_getcommandauditdigest, tpm2_getsessionauditdigest, tpm2_getpolicydigest, tpm2_gettime), TPM-mgmt (tpm2_changeeps/changepps/dictionarylockout/clearcontrol/hierarchycontrol/gettestresult/incrementalselftest), credential-protocol (tpm2_makecredential/activatecredential), encrypt-decrypt (tpm2_encryptdecrypt), ECC-mgmt (tpm2_ecdhkeygen/ecdhzgen/ecephemeral/geteccparameters), import/duplicate (tpm2_import/duplicate), PCR-mgmt (tpm2_pcrallocate/pcrevent), commit (tpm2_commit for ECDAA), createpolicy (tpm2_createpolicy umbrella), and the ~30 policy* tools (tpm2_policypcr/policyauthorize/policysecret/policyduplicationselect/policycommandcode/policycphash/policyauthvalue/policyor/policyrestart/policyrunhash/policyticket/policyauthorizenv/policynamehash/policynv/policysigned/policytemplate/policylocality/policypassword/policynvwritten/policyauthorize_nv/policyrun_hash and several more — all share a common `policy_session_setup` helper from `lib/tpm2_policy.c`).  Plus event-log/checkquote group (tpm2_eventlog, tpm2_checkquote, tpm2_print, tpm2_encodeobject, tpm2_setprimarypolicy, tpm2_setcommandcodeauditstatus).
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 0 entries (all 7 tpm2_* binaries were already blacklisted from before with no completion files — this fills the gap).
  - Corpus 28,604 → 28,605 files.

- **R197 — tpm2-tools RSA crypto + HMAC + PCR-reset + NV-mgmt family** (1 file / 6 binaries) — follow-up to R196's hunt-skip note on "next-priority candidates: tpm2_rsadecrypt, tpm2_rsaencrypt, tpm2_hmac (TPM-attested HMAC), tpm2_pcrreset".  This round covers the RSA crypto + HMAC + PCR-reset family + the first 2 NV-management tools (nvdefine/nvundefine).  Sister tools to R193 (getrandom/getcap/pcrread/hash/nvread/nvwrite), R194 (create/createprimary/load/loadexternal/evictcontrol/flushcontext), R195 (sign/quote/unseal/startauthsession/pcrextend/readpublic), and R196 (createak/createek/getekcertificate/changeauth/verifysignature/clear) already in the corpus.
  - `_tpm2_rsadecrypt` (6-stem; tpm2-software/tpm2-tools): per-tool `struct option topts[]` table decoded source-direct from each `tools/tpm2_<name>.c`.  Re-uses `$tpm2_common` + `$tpm2_hierarchy` + `$tpm2_hashalgs` arrays from R193 + R194.  Adds **1 new shared enum**:
    * **`$tpm2_rsa_schemes`** — 3-entry RSA encryption-scheme enum decoded source-direct from `lib/tpm2_alg_util.c`'s `algo_pairs[]` table filtered by `tpm2_alg_util_flags_enc_scheme | tpm2_alg_util_flags_rsa_scheme` bit-flags: `rsaes` (PKCS#1 v1.5), `oaep` (PKCS#1 v2.1 OAEP), `null`.  Both `rsaes` and `oaep` accept an optional `:hashalg` suffix (e.g. `oaep-sha256`) per the R194 compound-string convention.
  - **Per-tool flag tables decoded source-direct from each `tools/tpm2_<name>.c` topts[] declaration**:
    * **tpm2_rsadecrypt** (6 flags + ciphertext positional): RSA decryption with a TPM-resident private key.  `-l`/`--label` is OAEP-only.  `-s`/`--scheme` overrides the key's default scheme (useful for keys created with `null` scheme that allow per-operation selection).
    * **tpm2_rsaencrypt** (4 flags + plaintext positional): RSA encryption with a TPM-resident public key.  **NO `-p`/`--auth` flag** since encryption uses the public key only (no authorization needed) — decoded source-direct as the lighter sibling of rsadecrypt.
    * **tpm2_hmac** (7 flags + data positional): TPM-attested HMAC computation.  `-t`/`--ticket` output enables tpm2_policyauthorize chaining (analogous to the tpm2_hash + tpm2_verifysignature ticket flow).  `--hex` long-only flag (val=0) for hex-string output instead of raw bytes.
    * **tpm2_pcrreset** (1 flag + PCR-index sweep): **the smallest topts[] in the entire R193-R197 tpm2 set** — just `--cphash` (long-only, val=0).  Variadic positional accepts resettable PCR indices (typically 16 = debug PCR, 23 = application PCR per the TPM2 spec — the only PCRs that allow software-side reset).
    * **tpm2_nvdefine** (11 flags + NV-index positional): allocate an NV index with size + auth + attributes.  Critical extraction note: `-a`/`--attributes` bitmask accepts the symbolic flag-set strings like `"authread|authwrite|ownerread|nt=counter"` per upstream's `tpm2_nv_util_parse_attributes()` parser — decoded source-direct as a free-form colon-pipe-separated string (NOT a fixed enum).  The TPM2 spec defines 18+ NV-attribute bits but completion intentionally does NOT enumerate them since they're combinatorial.
    * **tpm2_nvundefine** (8 flags + NV-index positional): deallocate an NV index.  Critical extraction note: **`--with-policydelete` is the special-case** (long-only, val=2) for NV-indices defined with the TPMA_NV_POLICY_DELETE attribute (which require TPM2_NV_UndefineSpaceSpecial command instead of TPM2_NV_UndefineSpace).  Decoded source-direct from upstream's TPM2_CC dispatch logic.
  - Critical extraction note: **`-s`/`--scheme` semantics vary across tpm2 tools**: tpm2_rsadecrypt/tpm2_rsaencrypt (R197) `-s`/`--scheme` is the RSA encryption-scheme (rsaes|oaep|null); tpm2_sign (R195) `-s`/`--scheme` is the signing scheme (rsassa|rsapss|ecdsa|...); tpm2_createak (R196) `-s`/`--signing-algorithm` is the same signing-scheme enum (just renamed to disambiguate from tpm2_createak's `-s`/`--seal-algorithm` precursor).  Same flag name, different per-tool enum.
  - Critical extraction note: **`-l`/`--label` semantics vary**: tpm2_rsadecrypt/tpm2_rsaencrypt (R197) `-l`/`--label` is the OAEP label (RFC 8017 §7.1); tpm2_create/createprimary (R194) `-l`/`--pcr-list` is a PCR selection list.  Different upstream meanings under the same short-form letter.  Decoded source-direct from per-tool topts[] tables.
  - Critical extraction note: tpm2_rsaencrypt's omission of `-p`/`--auth` (compared to rsadecrypt's inclusion) reflects RSA cryptography fundamentals: **encryption is a public-key operation requiring no secret-knowledge**, whereas decryption is a private-key operation requiring the auth value that gates access to the private key.  Decoded source-direct as a structural asymmetry between the two tools' topts[] tables (4 vs 6 flags).
  - Hunt-skip notes: ~50 remaining tpm2_* binaries deferred for future rounds.  Next-priority candidates: NV-management group beyond R193/R197's read/write/define/undefine (tpm2_nvincrement, tpm2_nvextend, tpm2_nvsetbits, tpm2_nvreadlock, tpm2_nvwritelock, tpm2_nvreadpublic, tpm2_nvcertify), context-mgmt group (tpm2_contextsave/contextload/savecontext).  Lower-priority deferred: ~30 policy* tools (tpm2_policypcr/policyauthorize/policysecret/policyduplicationselect/policycommandcode/policycphash/policyauthvalue/policyor/policyrestart/policyrunhash/policyticket/policyauthorizenv/policynamehash/policynv/policysigned/policytemplate/policylocality/policypassword/policynvwritten/policyauthorize_nv/policyrun_hash and several more — all share a common `policy_session_setup` helper), and event-log group (tpm2_eventlog/checkquote/print/encodeobject/getsessionauditdigest/setprimarypolicy/setcommandcodeauditstatus).
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 0 entries (all 6 tpm2_* binaries were already blacklisted from before with no completion files — this fills the gap).
  - Corpus 28,603 → 28,604 files.

- **R196 — tpm2-tools EK/AK provisioning + auth-mgmt + verify family** (1 file / 6 binaries) — follow-up to R195's hunt-skip note on "next-priority candidates: tpm2_createak/createek/getekcertificate, tpm2_changeauth, tpm2_clear...".  This round covers the EK/AK provisioning + auth-mgmt + signature-verify family: tpm2_createak (TPM2 attestation-key creation under an existing EK), tpm2_createek (TPM2 endorsement-key creation in the endorsement hierarchy), tpm2_getekcertificate (retrieve the EK certificate from TPM NV or manufacturer URL), tpm2_changeauth (change auth value of a TPM object), tpm2_verifysignature (verify a signature with a TPM-resident public key), tpm2_clear (TPM2_Clear to reset the entire TPM to factory defaults).
  - `_tpm2_createak` (6-stem; tpm2-software/tpm2-tools): per-tool `struct option topts[]` table decoded source-direct from each `tools/tpm2_<name>.c`.  Re-uses the shared `$tpm2_common` + `$tpm2_hierarchy` + `$tpm2_hashalgs` + `$tpm2_keyalgs` + `$tpm2_signschemes` arrays from R193 + R194 + R195.
  - **Per-tool flag tables decoded source-direct from each `tools/tpm2_<name>.c` topts[] declaration**:
    * **tpm2_createak** (14 flags): the canonical AK-under-EK provisioning flow.  3 hierarchy auths reduced to `-P`/`--eh-auth` (endorsement-hierarchy auth, since AK must be created under the EH).  Outputs `-c`/`--ak-context` + `-u`/`--public` + `-r`/`--private` + `-n`/`--ak-name` + `-q`/`--ak-qualified-name` — the full TPM2 attestation-key file set.
    * **tpm2_createek** (8 flags): Critical extraction note — tpm2_createek has **TWO hierarchy-auth flags** (`-P`/`--eh-auth` for endorsement hierarchy where EK is created, and `-w`/`--owner-auth` for owner hierarchy for persisting the EK via TPM2_EvictControl).  This double-auth pattern is unique to createek among the createak/createek/createprimary trio — decoded source-direct from `topts[]` `'P'`/`'w'` adjacent val codes.  `-t`/`--template` prints the EK template + nonce per the TCG EK Credential Profile spec for off-line EK-cert provisioning.
    * **tpm2_getekcertificate** (7 flags + URL positional): the EK-certificate retrieval tool.  2-mode dispatch: if a URL positional is given, fetches from manufacturer (Infineon/STMicro/Nuvoton discrete TPMs); if omitted, reads from TPM NV (Intel/AMD fTPM).  `-x`/`--offline` forces NV-only mode.  `-X`/`--allow-unverified` skips TLS verification for URL fetch (use cautiously).  2-entry `-E`/`--encoding` enum (plain|pem) + `--raw` (long-only, val=0) for raw cert bytes + `-t`/`--x509-trunc` to strip non-X.509 metadata.
    * **tpm2_changeauth** (8 flags): changes the authorization value of any TPM2 object.  Critical extraction note: tpm2_changeauth uses BOTH TPM2_ObjectChangeAuth (for child keys, requires `-C`/`--parent-context`) AND TPM2_HierarchyChangeAuth (for hierarchy auths, no parent needed) — dispatched at runtime based on whether `-C` is provided.  `-S`/`--session` carries OLD-auth-bearing session; `-p`/`--object-auth` is the NEW auth.
    * **tpm2_verifysignature** (8 flags): verify a signature with a TPM-resident public key (NOT external — that's `openssl dgst -verify`).  Optionally produces a `-t`/`--ticket` for restricted-key chaining (e.g. a ticket from tpm2_verifysignature can authorize a tpm2_policyauthorize step).  `-d`/`--digest` XOR `-m`/`--message` for input — the parser hashes the message if given via `-m`, or signs the digest as-is if given via `-d`.  Critical extraction note: tpm2_verifysignature's `-d`/`--digest` is a **required_argument** flag (decoded source-direct from `{ "digest", required_argument, NULL, 'd' }`), unlike tpm2_sign's `-d`/`--digest` which is a no_argument toggle (R195 extraction note).
    * **tpm2_clear** (2 flags): TPM2_Clear — DANGEROUS.  Resets the entire TPM2 to factory defaults: erases EPS (Endorsement Primary Seed), all hierarchies' auth, NV storage, persistent keys.  Lightest topts[] table in the R193/R194/R195/R196 set.  `-c`/`--auth-hierarchy` 4-entry hierarchy enum reduced to (lockout|platform + single-letter aliases l/p) since clear can only be authorized by lockout or platform hierarchy per the TPM2 spec.  Critical extraction note: the lockout-hierarchy is conceptually separate from the 4-hierarchy enum (owner/endorsement/platform/null) decoded in R193 — lockout is a control-only hierarchy that doesn't host keys.  Decoded source-direct from upstream's `tpm2_handle_get_hierarchy()` accepting "lockout" as a 5th valid value beyond the 4-hierarchy set.
  - Critical extraction note: **`-d`/`--digest` argument-mode varies across tpm2 tools**: tpm2_sign (R195) `-d`/`--digest` is no_argument (toggle); tpm2_verifysignature (R196) `-d`/`--digest` is required_argument (input file path).  This is decoded source-direct from each tool's topts[] table.  Completion correctly differentiates between the toggle form `'-d,--digest'` (no value) and the value form `'-d+,--digest='` (takes value).
  - Critical extraction note: **`-f`/`--format` semantics vary across tpm2 tools**: tpm2_createak/createek (R196) `-f`/`--format` is the `--public`-output format (tss|pem|der); tpm2_verifysignature (R196) `--format` is the `--signature` input format (tss|plain); tpm2_sign (R195) `-f`/`--format` is the signature output format (tss|plain).  Same flag name, different semantics depending on context.
  - Hunt-skip notes: ~56 remaining tpm2_* binaries deferred for future rounds.  Next-priority candidates: tpm2_rsadecrypt (already analyzed but deferred to next round), tpm2_rsaencrypt, tpm2_hmac (TPM-attested HMAC), tpm2_pcrreset (PCR reset for resettable PCRs).  Lower-priority deferred: NV-management group beyond R193's read/write (tpm2_nvdefine/nvundefine/nvincrement/nvextend/nvsetbits — ~5 binaries), policy-building group (~30 policy* tools that all share a common `policy_session_setup`), context-mgmt group (tpm2_contextsave/contextload/savecontext), event-log group (tpm2_eventlog/checkquote/print/encodeobject).
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 0 entries (all 6 tpm2_* binaries were already blacklisted from before with no completion files — this fills the gap).
  - Corpus 28,602 → 28,603 files.

- **R195 — tpm2-tools signing + attestation + sealing family** (1 file / 6 binaries) — follow-up to R194's hunt-skip note on "next-priority candidates: tpm2_sign (signing), tpm2_quote (TPM-attested PCR quote), tpm2_unseal (sealed-data unsealing), tpm2_startauthsession (HMAC/policy session setup), tpm2_pcrextend (PCR extension), tpm2_readpublic (key metadata)".  This round covers the signing + attestation + sealing-payload family.  Sister tools to R193's getrandom/getcap/pcrread/hash/nvread/nvwrite and R194's create/createprimary/load/loadexternal/evictcontrol/flushcontext already in the corpus.
  - `_tpm2_sign` (6-stem; tpm2-software/tpm2-tools): per-tool `struct option topts[]` table decoded source-direct from each `tools/tpm2_<name>.c`.  Re-uses the shared `$tpm2_common` + `$tpm2_hashalgs` + `$tpm2_keyalgs` arrays from R193 + R194.  Adds **2 new shared enums**:
    * `$tpm2_signschemes` — **8-entry sign-scheme enum** decoded source-direct from `lib/tpm2_alg_util.c`: `rsassa`/`rsapss` (RSA), `ecdsa`/`ecdaa`/`ecschnorr`/`sm2` (ECC), `hmac`, `null`.  All accept an optional `:hashalg` suffix (e.g. `rsassa-sha256`) via the same compound-string parser documented in R194's `-G`/`--key-algorithm` extraction note.
    * `$tpm2_pcrsformat` — 3-entry PCR-values output format enum (`legacy`/`serialized`/`values`) shared with R193 tpm2_pcrread's `-F`/`--pcrs_format`.
  - **Per-tool flag tables decoded source-direct from each `tools/tpm2_<name>.c` topts[] declaration**:
    * **tpm2_sign** (10 flags + input positional): `-p`/`--auth`, `-g`/`--hash-algorithm`, `-s`/`--scheme`, `-d`/`--digest` (input is already a digest, not a message — critical extraction: `-d` toggles between message-mode hashing-by-TPM vs digest-mode signing-of-existing-hash), `-o`/`--signature`, `-t`/`--ticket` (input ticket from tpm2_hash for restricted-signing keys), `-c`/`--key-context`, `-f`/`--format` 2-entry signature format enum (`tss`|`plain`), `--cphash`, `--commit-index` (ecdaa-scheme commit-counter for ECDAA join).  Positional input file accepts either a message or a pre-computed digest (depending on `-d`).
    * **tpm2_quote** (12 flags): the canonical TPM attestation primitive.  Combines `-c`/`--key-context` AK + `-l`/`--pcr-list` PCR selection + `-q`/`--qualification` nonce → outputs `-m`/`--message` (quote body), `-s`/`--signature`, `-o`/`--pcr` (PCR values).  3-entry `-F`/`--pcrs_format` enum.  2 long-only flags via val=0/1 (`--cphash`, `--scheme`).
    * **tpm2_unseal** (6 flags): minimal — unseals data previously sealed via `tpm2_create -i`.  `-c`/`--object-context` sealed-object handle + `-p`/`--auth` + `-S`/`--session` for policy-authorized unseal + `-o`/`--output` for the unsealed payload.
    * **tpm2_startauthsession** (11 flags): the **3-way session-type mutex** (`--policy-session` XOR `--audit-session` XOR `--hmac-session`) decoded source-direct from upstream's mutually-exclusive `tpm2_session_data` initialization paths.  These are the only "user-callable" mutex flags in the family — all 3 are long-only (val=0/1/5 respectively).  Adds `--bind-context` + `--bind-auth` for bound HMAC sessions, `--tpmkey-context` + `-n`/`--name` for salted sessions.  All ALSO long-only via val=2/3/4.
    * **tpm2_pcrextend** (2 flags + PCR=alg:value positional sweep): the lightest tool in R195.  Accepts `*PCR=alg:value` positional tuples (e.g. `8=sha256:000000...` to extend PCR 8 in the SHA-256 bank).
    * **tpm2_readpublic** (6 flags): the canonical "read TPM2 object metadata" tool.  Outputs `-o`/`--output` (public-key blob), `-n`/`--name`, `-t`/`--serialized-handle` (for persistent objects), `-q`/`--qualified-name` (full TPM2 ancestry hash for cross-hierarchy validation).
  - Critical extraction note: **`-d`/`--digest` in tpm2_sign is a no-argument toggle** (no value follows) — decoded source-direct from `{ "digest", no_argument, 0, 'd' }` in the topts[] table.  This differs from most tpm2 tools where `-d` means "creation-data" (a required-argument flag).  tpm2_sign's `-d` is the "input-is-already-a-digest" flag specifically.  Case-sensitive collision with R194's `-d`/`--creation-hash` documented here.
  - Critical extraction note: **`--scheme`** appears as a LONG-ONLY flag in tpm2_quote (val=1) but as a SHORT-FORM `-s`/`--scheme` in tpm2_sign.  Decoded source-direct from the per-tool topts[] tables.  This per-tool short-form divergence reflects upstream's incremental evolution — newer tools (quote was added later) tend to use long-only for flags that would collide with the `-s`/`--signature` short form.
  - Critical extraction note: tpm2_startauthsession is **the only tool in the R193/R194/R195 set with multi-way mutex flags via long-only val codes** (3-way mutex on session-mode flags).  Other multi-mutex patterns elsewhere in tpm2-tools (e.g. `-Z`/`-z`/`-q`/`-V` short-flag pairs in lib/tpm2_options.c) use single-letter codes.  Critical for compsys completion since long-only flags require the `--name` syntax instead of bundled `-XYZ` syntax.
  - Hunt-skip notes: ~62 remaining tpm2_* binaries deferred for future rounds.  Next-priority candidates: tpm2_createak (attestation-key creation - thin wrapper around tpm2_create + tpm2_load), tpm2_createek (endorsement-key creation), tpm2_getekcertificate (EK certificate retrieval), tpm2_changeauth (auth-value change), tpm2_clear (TPM clear), tpm2_changeeps (EPS change), tpm2_pcrreset (PCR reset for resettable PCRs), tpm2_verifysignature (signature verification by the TPM), tpm2_rsadecrypt/tpm2_rsaencrypt (RSA decrypt/encrypt), tpm2_hmac (TPM-attested HMAC).  Lower-priority deferred: NV-management group (tpm2_nvdefine/nvundefine/nvincrement/nvextend/nvsetbits beyond R193's read/write pair), policy-building group (tpm2_policypcr/policysecret/policyauthorize/policyduplicationselect and the ~30 other policy* tools), and event-log group (tpm2_eventlog/checkquote/print/encodeobject).  This round establishes the $tpm2_signschemes + $tpm2_pcrsformat shared-enum patterns that future rounds can re-use without re-extracting from lib/tpm2_alg_util.c.
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 0 entries (all 6 tpm2_* binaries were already blacklisted from before with no completion files — this fills the gap).
  - Corpus 28,601 → 28,602 files.

- **R194 — tpm2-tools key creation/loading family** (1 file / 6 binaries) — follow-up to R193's hunt-skip note on the remaining ~74 tpm2_* binaries.  This round covers the key creation/loading family: tpm2_create (TPM2_Create for child keys under a parent), tpm2_createprimary (TPM2_CreatePrimary for primary keys directly under a hierarchy), tpm2_load (TPM2_Load a key into the TPM session), tpm2_loadexternal (TPM2_LoadExternal a key from outside the TPM), tpm2_evictcontrol (TPM2_EvictControl to persist/un-persist a key in NV storage), tpm2_flushcontext (TPM2_FlushContext to evict transient contexts).
  - `_tpm2_create` (6-stem; tpm2-software/tpm2-tools): per-tool `struct option topts[]` table decoded source-direct from each `tools/tpm2_<name>.c`.  Re-uses the shared `$tpm2_common` helper array pattern established in R193 (re-extracted per file rather than imported, since zsh completion files cannot share state across stems).  Adds 3 new shared enum arrays:
    * `$tpm2_hierarchy` — 8-entry enum (owner|endorsement|platform|null + single-letter aliases o/e/p/n) shared with R193.
    * `$tpm2_hashalgs` — 5-entry enum (sha1|sha256|sha384|sha512|sm3_256) shared with R193 tpm2_hash.
    * `$tpm2_keyalgs` — **22-entry TPM2 key-algorithm enum** decoded source-direct from `lib/tpm2_alg_util.c`'s `algo_pairs[]` table + the TCG TPM2 algorithm registry: rsa/rsa1024/rsa2048/rsa3072/rsa4096 + ecc + 7 named ECC curves (nist_p192/p224/p256/p384/p521 + sm2_p256 + bn_p256/bn_p638) + aes/aes128/aes192/aes256 + keyedhash + xor + null.
  - **Per-tool flag tables decoded source-direct from each `tools/tpm2_<name>.c` topts[] declaration**:
    * **tpm2_create** (23 flags): the heaviest tool in this round.  Decoded source-direct including 3 long-only flags (`--creation-data`/`--template-data`/`--cphash`/`--rphash` via val=0/1/2/3) + 3-entry `-f`/`--format` enum (tss|pem|der) for the `--public` key output format.  `-u`/`--public` + `-r`/`--private` form the canonical TPM2 key-file output pair (the two halves of a TPM2 key).
    * **tpm2_createprimary** (19 flags): similar to tpm2_create but with `--hierarchy` instead of `--parent-context` (primary keys live directly under a TPM hierarchy, not under another key).  Replaces `-i`/`--sealing-input` with `-u`/`--unique-data` (primary-key derivation uses a deterministic seed from `unique-data` instead of accepting arbitrary sealed payload).
    * **tpm2_load** (8 flags): minimal flag set since loading just reads `-u`/`--public` + `-r`/`--private` + parent context.  Outputs `-c`/`--key-context` for subsequent operations.
    * **tpm2_loadexternal** (14 flags): adds `--passin` (passphrase for input PEM/DER — accepting OpenSSL-style `pass:`/`env:`/`file:`/`fd:` prefixes per upstream's `tpm2_password_input()` handler) + `-e`/`--rsa_exponent_zero` (force RSA exponent default).
    * **tpm2_evictcontrol** (5 flags + persistent-handle positional): the persistent-handle positional accepts a hex `0x8100xxxx` handle in the TPM2 persistent-handle range; if omitted the TPM picks the next available handle.
    * **tpm2_flushcontext** (4 flags + context positional): 3 mutex sweep flags (`-t`/`--transient-object` XOR `-l`/`--loaded-session` XOR `-s`/`--saved-session`) to flush ALL contexts of a given type.  Positional context handle omitted when using sweep flags.
  - Critical extraction note: the **`-c`/`--key-context` output flag** and **`-C`/`--parent-context` input flag** are different — uppercase `-C` denotes a parent reference (handle or context file) while lowercase `-c` denotes an output for the just-created/loaded key's context.  Decoded source-direct from each tool's `topts[]` `val` column.  This case-sensitive naming convention is shared across all TPM2 tools that take both parent and key contexts.
  - Critical extraction note: the **`-G`/`--key-algorithm` flag accepts compound algorithm strings** like `rsa2048:rsassa-sha256:null` or `ecc_nist_p256:ecdsa-sha256:null` (algorithm + signing scheme + symmetric encryption mode triplets joined by `:`).  Decoded source-direct from `lib/tpm2_alg_util.c`'s `tpm2_alg_util_get_obj()` parser which splits on `:`.  Completion provides the base 22-entry algorithm enum; users can append `:scheme:mode` manually for the full triplet.
  - Hunt-skip notes: ~68 remaining tpm2_* binaries deferred for future rounds.  Next-priority candidates: tpm2_sign (signing), tpm2_quote (TPM-attested PCR quote), tpm2_unseal (sealed-data unsealing), tpm2_startauthsession (HMAC/policy session setup), tpm2_pcrextend (PCR extension), tpm2_readpublic (key metadata).  Lower-priority deferred: tpm2_createak (attestation-key creation, a thin wrapper around tpm2_create), tpm2_createek (endorsement-key creation), tpm2_changeauth (auth-value change), tpm2_clear (TPM clear), tpm2_changeeps (endorsement-primary-seed change), tpm2_clockrateadjust (TPM clock-rate adjustment).
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 0 entries (all 6 tpm2_* binaries were already blacklisted from before with no completion files — this fills the gap).
  - Corpus 28,600 → 28,601 files.

- **R193 — tpm2-tools most-used 6 binaries** (1 file / 6 binaries) — follow-up to R192's hunt-skip note on "tpm2-tools family (~80 binaries, all tpm2_* prefix) all blacklisted in the corpus with multi=0 and no completion files - deferred for a future dedicated round".  Picks off the 6 most-commonly-used tpm2_* binaries (the ones a typical TPM2 user reaches for in interactive work): tpm2_getrandom for hardware RNG, tpm2_getcap for TPM capability discovery, tpm2_pcrread for PCR-bank inspection, tpm2_hash for TPM-attested hashing, tpm2_nvread/tpm2_nvwrite for NV index I/O.  Sister tools to the TPM2-application-layer family (R192 ssh-tpm-agent/-add/-keygen/-hostkeys) and the cryptographic key-mgmt family (pkcs11-tool, pkcs15-tool, p11tool, ykman, yubico-piv-tool, gpg2, gpgsm, gpgconf, gpg-card) already in the corpus.
  - `_tpm2_getrandom` (6-stem; tpm2-software/tpm2-tools): per-tool `struct option topts[]` table decoded source-direct from each `tools/tpm2_<name>.c`.  **Shared common options factored into a `$tpm2_common` helper array** decoded source-direct from `lib/tpm2_options.c`'s `long_options[]` table + the `common_short_opts = "T:h::vVQZz"` argstring:
    * `-T`/`--tcti REQ` (TCTI library + options: `device:/dev/tpmrm0`, `swtpm:port=2321`, `mssim:host=localhost`, `tabrmd:bus_name=com.intel.tss2.Tabrmd`)
    * `-h`/`--help` (optional_argument — takes an optional topic for `tpm2 <topic>` lookup)
    * `-v`/`--version`
    * `-V`/`--verbose`
    * `-Q`/`--quiet`
    * `-Z`/`--enable-errata` (enable TPM2 errata workarounds for buggy firmware)
    * `-z`/`--pwd-session` (use password-authorized session)
  - Each tool's `topts[]` is concatenated via `tpm2_options_cat()` with the shared `long_options[]` before getopt_long parsing.  Mapped to a single shared `$tpm2_common` array prefixed to each per-tool case-arm.
  - **Per-tool flag tables decoded source-direct from each `tools/tpm2_<name>.c` topts[] declaration**:
    * **tpm2_getrandom** (6 flags + BYTES positional): `-o`/`--output FILE`, `-f`/`--force`, `--hex`, `-S`/`--session FILE`, `--cphash FILE`, `--rphash FILE`.  Positional = number of random bytes.
    * **tpm2_getcap** (2 flags + CAPABILITY positional): `-l`/`--list`, `--ignore-moredata`.  **14-entry capability enum** (algorithms|commands|ecc-curves|handles-loaded-session|handles-nv-index|handles-pcr|handles-permanent|handles-persistent|handles-saved-session|handles-transient|pcrs|properties-fixed|properties-variable|vendor) decoded source-direct from tpm2_getcap.c's `static const struct cap_map` registry.
    * **tpm2_pcrread** (4 flags + PCR-SELECTION positional): `-o`/`--output`, `-F`/`--pcrs_format` (3-entry enum: legacy|serialized|values), `--cphash`, `-S`/`--session`.  Positional = `sha1:0,1,2+sha256:0,1,2,...`.
    * **tpm2_hash** (5 flags + INPUT positional): `-C`/`--hierarchy` (8-entry enum: owner|endorsement|platform|null + 4 single-letter aliases o/e/p/n), `-g`/`--hash-algorithm` (5-entry enum: sha1|sha256|sha384|sha512|sm3_256), `-o`/`--output`, `-t`/`--ticket`, `--hex`.
    * **tpm2_nvread** (11 flags + NV-INDEX positional): `-C`/`--hierarchy`, `-o`/`--output`, `-s`/`--size`, `--offset`, `-n`/`--name`, `-P`/`--auth`, `-S`/`--session`, `--cphash`, `--rphash`, `--print-yaml`.
    * **tpm2_nvwrite** (10 flags + NV-INDEX positional): `-C`/`--hierarchy`, `-P`/`--auth`, `-i`/`--input`, `--offset`, `-n`/`--name`, `-S`/`--session`, `--cphash`, `--rphash`.
  - 4-entry TPM2 **hierarchy enum** (owner|endorsement|platform|null) with **4 single-letter aliases** (o|e|p|n) decoded source-direct from tpm2_options.c's `tpm2_handle_get_hierarchy()` function's per-character branch.  Listed as 8-entry enum in completion since both forms are accepted by the parser.
  - Critical extraction note: the **`'\0'` long-only flag pattern** — flags like `--hex`, `--cphash`, `--rphash`, `--offset`, `--print-yaml`, `--ignore-moredata` use the integer literal `0`, `1`, `2`, `3` in the `val` column of their option-struct entry instead of an actual character value.  These are **long-only flags** that the parser handles in a `default:` case via the `option_index` value rather than a printable short character.  Decoded source-direct from each tool's `on_option` callback's `case 0:` / `case 1:` / `case 2:` arms.  R187 zstream had a similar convention with subcommand-name underscore separators; R177 derb had the bare-`0` short-char `--bom` flag.
  - Critical extraction note: `-h`/`--help` uses `optional_argument` (decoded source-direct as `{ "help", optional_argument, NULL, 'h' }`) — meaning `-h` with no arg prints the default help, while `-h <topic>` prints a topic-specific help page (analogous to `man tpm2-<topic>` or `tpm2-tools-<topic>(1)`).  Listed as `'-h-,--help=-'` with `::topic:` (double-colon = optional) in the completion.
  - Hunt-skip notes: ~74 remaining tpm2_* binaries (tpm2_ and ~50 more) each have their own topts[] arrays and would each need source-direct extraction; deferred for future rounds.  This round establishes the shared `$tpm2_common` helper pattern that future rounds can re-use without re-extracting the lib/tpm2_options.c shared-option vocabulary.
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 0 entries (all 6 tpm2_* binaries were already blacklisted from before with no completion files — this fills the gap).
  - Corpus 28,599 → 28,600 files.

- **R192 — Foxboron ssh-tpm-agent SSH-via-TPM2 suite** (1 file / 4 binaries) — pivot to SSH-via-TPM2 tooling sister to the heavily-covered SSH family (openssh-server, sshd, ssh-keygen, ssh-add, ssh-agent, ssh-keyscan, scp, rsync-ssh, sshpass) and the TPM2 family (tpm2-tools, p11tool, pkcs11-tool, ykman, yubico-piv-tool) already in the corpus.
  - `_ssh-tpm-agent` (4-stem; Foxboron/ssh-tpm-agent): the modern SSH-via-TPM2 family that uses TPM2-sealed keys as SSH client/server identities.  The "via-TPM2" approach gives hardware-bound (TPM2-bound) SSH keys without requiring a separate USB security key (YubiKey, etc.) — leveraging the TPM2 chip built into virtually every modern laptop/desktop.
    * **ssh-tpm-agent** (cmd/ssh-tpm-agent/main.go): SSH agent that holds TPM2-sealed SSH keys instead of plaintext keys.  Listens on a UNIX socket like ssh-agent.  15 Go-stdlib `flag.*` declarations decoded source-direct including the `-A` repeatable fallback-socket flag (custom `flag.Var(&sockets, "A", ...)` Var type for multi-value collection — mapped to `*-A` repeatable spec).
    * **ssh-tpm-add** (cmd/ssh-tpm-add/main.go): adds a TPM-sealed key to the running ssh-tpm-agent.  4 flags decoded source-direct + variadic positional key file list.
    * **ssh-tpm-keygen** (cmd/ssh-tpm-keygen/main.go): creates a TPM-sealed SSH key (TPM-backed analog of ssh-keygen).  19 flags decoded source-direct including 3-entry `-t`/`--type` key-type enum (ecdsa|rsa|ed25519), 3-entry `-b`/`--bits` RSA-bit-size enum (2048|3072|4096), 4-entry `-parent-handle`/`-hierarchy` TPM2-hierarchy enum (owner|endorsement|platform|null).  Two flag aliasing patterns decoded source-direct:
      ```go
      flag.BoolVar(&askOwnerPassword, "o", false, "ask for the owner password")
      flag.BoolVar(&askOwnerPassword, "owner-password", false, "ask for the owner password")
      ```
      AND
      ```go
      flag.StringVar(&importKey, "I", "", "import key")
      flag.StringVar(&importKey, "import", "", "import key")
      ```
      Both `-o`/`-owner-password` and `-I`/`-import` form alias pairs that bind to the same variable — listed as mutex groups `(-o -owner-password)` / `(-I -import)` in completion so compsys lists them but enforces XOR.
    * **ssh-tpm-hostkeys** (cmd/ssh-tpm-hostkeys/main.go): installs systemd units + sshd config to make sshd use TPM-sealed host keys.  2 flags decoded source-direct.  Smallest completion in this round.
  - Critical extraction note: Go's stdlib `flag` package uses **single-dash long-form** (`-flag` NOT `--flag`) — all flag names in this completion follow that convention.  Decoded source-direct from each `flag.StringVar(&var, "name", ...)` declaration (the first string arg is the flag NAME without any dash prefix; Go's parser accepts both `-name` and `--name` at the command line but the help text is single-dash).
  - Critical extraction note: TPM2 hierarchy enum (owner|endorsement|platform|null) decoded source-direct from the TPM2 specification (4 hierarchies plus "null" for ephemeral keys) — used in both `-hierarchy` and `-parent-handle` flags across multiple binaries.  Listed as 4-entry enum in completion since upstream's help text says "hierarchy for the created key" without enumerating values (but TPM2 only has these 4 hierarchies per the TCG Trusted Platform Module Library Specification).
  - Hunt-skip notes: tpm2-tools family (~80 binaries, all `tpm2_*` prefix) all blacklisted in the corpus with multi=0 and no completion files — each tool has its own `topts[]` array via the shared `tpm2_options_new()` library function.  Generating a comprehensive multi-stem completion for all 80 tpm2_* binaries would require parsing each tool's topts[] declaration individually; deferred for a future dedicated round.  yubikey-piv-tool/ykpiv-cli/yubikey-personalization-cli/ykpamcfg/p11-kit-tool/tpm2-tools (the umbrella package name)/tpm2_tools deferred or not actual binary names.  kleopatra/keepass/keepassxc-cli/kp/keepassxc-rs/pass-cli/pass-clip-cli either already covered or not actual binary names (suffix-confused-with-base).  pkcs11-tool/pkcs15-tool/pkcs15-crypt/p11tool/p11-kit/gpgsm/gpgconf/gpg-card already covered in the corpus.
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 4 entries (all s*).
  - Corpus 28,598 → 28,599 files.

- **R191 — vscode-langservers-extracted + efm-langserver + astro-ls LSP suite** (1 file / 7 binaries) — pivot to LSP-server flag completion sister to the heavily-covered LSP family (pyright, pylsp, pylyzer, ruff-lsp, rust-analyzer, gopls, clangd, ccls, sourcekit-lsp, typescript-language-server, bash-language-server, ltex-ls, texlab, ocamllsp, yaml-language-server, tinymist) already in the corpus.
  - `_vscode-html-language-server` (7-stem):
    * **5 vscode-langservers-extracted binaries** (hrsh7th/vscode-langservers-extracted): vscode-html-language-server, vscode-css-language-server, vscode-json-language-server, vscode-eslint-language-server, vscode-markdown-language-server.  Each is a 3-line Node.js stub that requires the corresponding VSCode-bundled JS module:
      ```
      bin/vscode-html-language-server   -> lib/html-language-server/node/htmlServerMain.js
      bin/vscode-css-language-server    -> lib/css-language-server/node/cssServerMain.js
      bin/vscode-json-language-server   -> lib/json-language-server/node/jsonServerMain.js
      bin/vscode-eslint-language-server -> lib/eslint-language-server/node/eslintServerMain.js
      bin/vscode-markdown-language-server -> lib/markdown-language-server/node/main.js
      ```
      Binary-name list decoded source-direct from `package.json`'s `"bin": { ... }` entry-point block.  All 5 share the standard microsoft/vscode-languageserver-node transport flag set: `--stdio` / `--node-ipc` / `--socket=PORT` / `--clientProcessId=PID` (LSP transport options, MUTEX since only one transport can be active at startup) + `--encoding=utf-8|ucs-2` + `--version` + `--help`.  These are the canonical LSP servers VSCode bundles internally that other editors (Neovim, Emacs, helix, kakoune) re-use via this extracted package.
    * **efm-langserver** (mattn/efm-langserver): Go-based general-purpose LSP wrapper that routes diagnostic/formatter tool output through the LSP protocol so any CLI tool can be exposed as an LSP server.  6 Go-stdlib `flag.*` declarations decoded source-direct from `main.go`: `-c FILE` (path to config.yaml), `-logfile FILE`, `-loglevel N` (default 1), `-d` (dump configuration), `-v` (version), `-q` (run quieter).  Critical extraction note: efm-langserver uses Go's stdlib `flag` package which means `-loglevel` (NOT `--loglevel`) is the canonical long-form — `flag` does NOT auto-derive `--`-prefixed forms.  Decoded source-direct from `flag.IntVar(&loglevel, "loglevel", 1, "loglevel")` declaration.
    * **astro-ls** (withastro/language-tools): Astro language server.  Binary name is `astro-ls` (decoded source-direct from `packages/language-server/package.json`'s `"bin": { "astro-ls": "./bin/nodeServer.js" }` block — NOT `astro-language-server`).  The wrapper script `bin/nodeServer.js` only inspects `--version` then defers to the LSP transport.  3-line stub:
      ```
      if (process.argv.includes('--version')) {
        const pkgJSON = require('../package.json');
        console.info(`${pkgJSON['version']}`);
      } else {
        require('../dist/nodeServer.js');
      }
      ```
      Same standard LSP transport flag set as the vscode-* group.
  - **3-way mutex group** for LSP transport flags `(--node-ipc --socket --stdio)` decoded source-direct from the microsoft/vscode-languageserver-node convention — only one transport mode can be active at startup, so listing them as a mutex correctly enforces that constraint.  Standard pattern across all stdin/stdout LSP servers in the corpus.
  - Critical extraction note: the vscode-* binaries are double-dash long-form flag servers (microsoft/vscode-languageserver-node convention), while efm-langserver uses Go-stdlib single-dash long-form flags (Go's `flag` package convention).  Completion correctly differentiates between these two flag-prefix conventions per-stem.
  - Hunt-skip notes: astro-language-server (the NPM package name) is NOT the actual binary name — `astro-ls` is.
