Decoded source-direct from upstream's package.json.  svelte-language-server / angular-language-server / vue-language-server / solidity-lsp / nimlsp / nim-lsp / idris-lsp / idris2-lsp / elixir-lsp / erlang-lsp / pwsh-lsp / powershell-lsp / deno-lsp / markdown-lsp / json-language-server / schema-language-server / ocaml-lsp / coc-lsp deferred — each is a separate upstream with its own argv parsing; each would need its own source-direct extraction round.  vue-language-server actually does have multi=1 in audit (covered).  ocaml-lsp is the package name for `ocamllsp` (already covered with multi=2).  vscode-html/css/json/markdown-language-server were all in multi=1 audit despite NOT having explicit `#compdef` entries — these are GitHub-Actions-installed shims that some plugins generate dynamically; this round provides the proper static completion.  rust-lsp / go-lsp / golangci-lsp / clangd-cli / vale-ls / cquery / iwyu / iwyu-tool / iwyu_tool / include-cleaner / clang-tools-extra / pyflakes-cli / pylinx / rustup-component-history / ra (rust-analyzer alias) / tsserver / tsls are not actual binary names (suffix-confused-with-base / package-name-not-binary / alias).
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 7 entries (1 a*, 1 e*, 5 v*).
  - Corpus 28,597 → 28,598 files.

- **R190 — variar/klogg + nickbnf/glogg log explorers** (1 file / 2 binaries) — pivot to Qt-based GUI log file viewers sister to the heavily-covered log-analysis family (lnav, multitail, logwatch + wtmpdb + tail + less + dnstop + atop + bottom + btop + logcheck + journalctl already in the corpus).  klogg is the actively-maintained successor to glogg, both fast Qt-based log file viewers / log explorers for large (gigabyte-scale) log files with regex filtering, multi-file split-view, follow-tail mode, search history.
  - `_klogg` (2-stem; variar/klogg + nickbnf/glogg):
    * klogg (variar/klogg): 9 QCommandLineOptions decoded source-direct from `src/app/cli.h` `CliParameters` constructor's QCommandLineParser + QCommandLineOption declarations.  klogg has **two modes (GUI vs console) gated on the `bool console` constructor arg**:
      - GUI build registers: multi-instance, load-session, new-session, log-to-file, follow-file, window-width, window-height options.
      - Console build registers: pattern option only.
      - Completion lists ALL options regardless since the parser silently ignores unknown options across builds (decoded source-direct from the `if (!console) { ... } else { ... }` switch).
    * glogg (nickbnf/glogg): 7 user-facing flags decoded source-direct from `src/main.cpp` boost::program_options `desc.add_options()(...)` chain.  Critical extraction: `-d`/`--debug` is **repeatable for verbosity escalation** per the upstream help text "include multiple times for more verbosity e.g. -dddd" — decoded source-direct.  Mapped to `*-d/--debug` repeatable spec in completion.  glogg uses an unusual `boost::program_options::command_line_style` recipe `(((unix_style ^ allow_guessing) | allow_long_disguise) ^ allow_sticky)` which enables stacked-short-flag behavior — decoded source-direct from the command_line_style XOR/OR/XOR chain.
  - 6-flag shared common surface between klogg and glogg: `-h`/`--help`, version (`--version` for klogg / `-v`/`--version` for glogg — critical 1-flag divergence: glogg's `-v` short form is taken by klogg's verbose-pattern context), `-m`/`--multi`, `-s`/`--load-session`, `-n`/`--new-session`, `-l`/`--log`, `-d`/`--debug`.
  - 3-flag klogg-only additions: `-f`/`--follow` (tail-like follow), `-e`/`--pattern` (console-mode regex), `--window-width`/`--window-height` (initial-window-size pair).
  - Critical extraction note: klogg's version flag is the bare `--version` (no `-v` short form per QCommandLineParser's addVersionOption()'s default which only registers `-v` if not already taken) whereas glogg explicitly registers `-v`/`--version` pair via boost::program_options.  Decoded source-direct from each upstream's parser configuration.
  - Hunt-skip notes: sapling SCM is already covered in the corpus via `more_src/_sl` + `more_src/_sapling` (both compdef'ing `sl` per the Sapling SCM's `sl` binary name).  This caused the audit's `sapling blk=1 more=1 multi=0` confusion — `more=1` means a file named _sapling exists but it compdef's `sl` not `sapling`.  `loadwatch`/`tcpdump-rs`/`pgcli-rs`/`pgrep-rs`/`tre-grep`/`tree-rs`/`eza-rs`/`darktable-rs`/`htop-rs`/`dust-rs`/`dust-cli`/`fdupes-rs`/`jdupes-rs`/`tldr-rs`/`tldr-go`/`nudu`/`xhs`/`httpie-rs` are not commonly-packaged tools / suffix-confused-with-base.  `claude-cli`/`aider-cli`/`cursor-cli`/`mcp-server`/`llm-cli`/`shell-gpt`/`goose-cli`/`together-cli`/`stability-cli`/`vapi-cli`/`fireworks-cli`/`groqcli`/`grokai`/`groqai`/`anthropic-cli`/`openai-cli`/`replicate-cli`/`openrouter-cli`/`phind`/`phind-cli`/`quillbot`/`pikabolt`/`ghcp`/`cohere-cli`/`litellm-cli`/`copilot`/`gh-copilot` etc. either already covered or not actual binary names.  `mcp-inspector`/`mcp`/`mcp-cli` (Model Context Protocol family) are covered transitively via the `mcp` umbrella.  jujutsu (jj VCS) already covered.
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 2 entries (both g*/k*).
  - Corpus 28,596 → 28,597 files.

- **R189 — Dave Coffin dcraw canonical RAW image decoder** (1 file / 1 binary) — pivot to RAW camera image decoding sister to the heavily-covered RAW + photo processing family (rawtherapee/rawtherapee-cli, darktable/darktable-cli, digikam/digikam-cli, enfuse, enblend, exiftool, exiftran, exif, exiv2 already in the corpus).  dcraw is the canonical RAW-camera-image decoder by Dave Coffin (dcoffin@cybercom.net) that underlies libraw, RawTherapee, darktable, dcraw.js, and countless other RAW processing pipelines.  Supports 600+ camera models.  Filling in a previously-blacklisted-but-no-completion gap.
  - `_dcraw` (1-stem; ncruces/dcraw mirror of Dave Coffin's dcraw.c): the canonical dcraw.  37 short flags decoded source-direct from main()'s case-arm `switch(opt)` block + the `puts(_("-X ..."))` documented-usage block in the help-print branch.
  - **5 enum autocompletes** decoded source-direct from the documented [0-N] ranges in each `puts` line:
    * **`-H` highlight mode (10 entries)**: 0=clip, 1=unclip, 2=blend, 3-9=rebuild-N (incrementally aggressive highlight reconstruction)
    * **`-t` flip image (4 entries)**: 0=none, 3=180°, 5=90°CCW, 6=90°CW
    * **`-o` output colorspace (7 entries)**: 0=raw, 1=sRGB, 2=Adobe, 3=Wide, 4=ProPhoto, 5=XYZ, 6=ACES (or pass file path for output ICC profile)
    * **`-q` interpolation quality (4 entries)**: 0=bilinear, 1=VNG, 2=PPG, 3=AHD (the AHD demosaicing algorithm is the default in most modern RAW pipelines)
  - **`+M`/`-M` sign-prefix XOR pair** decoded source-direct from `case 'M': use_camera_matrix = 3 * (opm == '+');` — the `+M` form enables embedded camera color matrix use; `-M` disables it.  Listed as `(+M)-M` / `(-M)+M` mutex group so compsys enforces the XOR.  This is a rare convention shared with `mt rewind` / `mt eod` from R148 and with shells' `+/-flag` conventions for set/unset.
  - **Document mode stacking decoded source-direct from**:
    ```c
    case 'E':  document_mode++;
    case 'D':  document_mode++;
    case 'd':  document_mode++;
    ```
    (the C switch *intentionally* falls through — `-E` increments by 3, `-D` by 2, `-d` by 1).  These are listed as 3 independent toggles in completion since users typically pick exactly one rather than stacking them.
  - **Multi-value flag specs** decoded source-direct from the numeric-arg consumption table `sp = "nbrkStqmHACg"` + arg-count string `"114111111422"` (each character is a single-digit count of numeric args the flag consumes — `n=1`, `b=1`, `r=4`, `k=1`, `S=1`, `t=1`, `q=1`, `m=1`, `H=1`, `A=4`, `C=2`, `g=2`):
    * `-r r g b g` (4 floats — custom white balance)
    * `-A x y w h` (4 ints — grey-box for WB averaging)
    * `-C r b` (2 floats — chromatic-aberration correction red/blue scale)
    * `-g p ts` (2 floats — custom gamma curve power + toe-slope)
  - Mapped to multi-colon completion specs (`:r:g:b:g:`, `:x:y:w:h:`, etc.).
  - **22-extension RAW file glob** `*.(arw|ARW|cr2|CR2|cr3|CR3|crw|CRW|dng|DNG|nef|NEF|raf|RAF|raw|RAW|rw2|RW2|orf|ORF|pef|PEF|srw|SRW|x3f|X3F|mrw|MRW|3fr|3FR|fff|FFF|iiq|IIQ|kdc|KDC|nrw|NRW)` for positional arg completion — covers all major camera manufacturers' RAW formats: Sony ARW, Canon CR2/CR3/CRW, Adobe DNG, Nikon NEF/NRW, Fuji RAF, Panasonic RW2, Olympus ORF, Pentax PEF, Samsung SRW, Sigma X3F, Minolta MRW, Hasselblad 3FR/FFF, PhaseOne IIQ, Kodak KDC.
  - Critical extraction note: dcraw uses a **hand-rolled arg-parser** (NOT getopt) decoded source-direct from the `for (arg=1; (((opm = argv[arg][0]) - 2) | 2) == '+'; ) { opt = argv[arg++][1]; ... switch(opt) { ... } }` loop.  The `((c - 2) | 2) == '+'` test accepts both `+` (0x2B) and `-` (0x2D) as flag prefix bytes - decoded source-direct.  This explains why `+M`/`-M` both work as flags (other case-arms ignore the sign, but `case 'M':` reads `opm` to determine direction).
  - Critical extraction note: dcraw also accepts **stdin RAW input** via `-I` flag (`case 'I': read_from_stdin = 1; break;`) — useful for piping `cat raw.cr2 | dcraw -I -c > out.ppm` or similar.  Decoded source-direct from case-arm but NOT documented in the help-print block (so it's an undocumented-but-real flag).  Added to completion regardless since the parser accepts it.
  - Hunt-skip notes: dcraw_emu (the libraw companion that mimics dcraw's CLI exactly) — covered transitively via dcraw flag vocabulary, deferred since libraw users typically use libraw's higher-level APIs.  `libraw`/`libraw-cli` are not actual binary names.  `luminance-hdr`/`luminance` (Luminance HDR) ships a GUI primarily; CLI surface is minimal (deferred).  `darktable-rs`/`xnview`/`qimgv` are not commonly-packaged tools.  `snaphu`/`isce`/`isce2`/`pyrate`/`mintpy`/`gmtsar`/`gmtinsar`/`dem3`/`dem-tool` are scientific InSAR/satellite processing tools — `gmt` already covered; others deferred for a dedicated round.  `rawtherapee-cli`/`darktable-cli`/`digikam-cli`/`luminance-cli`/`exiv2-cli`/`mirage-cli`/`mintpy-cli` are package-name variants of already-covered tools (suffix-confused-with-base).
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 0 entries (dcraw was already blacklisted from before with no completion file — this fills the gap).
  - Corpus 28,595 → 28,596 files.

- **R188 — Draisberghof usb_modeswitch cellular-modem USB-mode-switcher** (1 file / 1 binary) — pivot to USB-device-mode-switching tooling sister to the heavily-covered USB family (usbview, lsusb, usbhid-dump, usb-creator, libusb-cli) and the embedded-serial family (dfu-util/dfu-prefix/dfu-suffix/dfu-tool/dfu-programmer, flashrom, pyocd, esptool, picotool, stm32flash, st-info/st-flash/st-util/st-trace, sigrok-cli, urjtag, gtkterm, kermit, picocom, microcom) already in the corpus.
  - `_usb_modeswitch` (1-stem; Danw33/usb-modeswitch mirror of http://www.draisberghof.de/usb_modeswitch/): the canonical USB-device-mode-switching tool for Linux.  Used to switch USB devices from CD-ROM emulation mode (also called "Zero CD" / "ZeroCD" for the auto-install Windows-driver trick used by cellular modem vendors) into their normal modem/MBIM/NCM/serial mode so they can be used by the host OS.
  - 43 long_options[] entries decoded source-direct from `usb_modeswitch.c`'s `static struct option long_options[] = { ... };` table + getopt_long argstring `"hejWQDndKHJSOBEGTNALZFRItv:p:V:P:C:m:M:2:3:w:r:c:i:u:a:s:f:b:g:"`.
  - **12 vendor-specific quirk modes** decoded source-direct as a logical "trigger mode" group (the Mode flags additively contribute to ModeMap via `ModeMap = ModeMap + X_MODE` so multiple are technically allowed but conceptually disjoint):
    * `-H`/`--huawei-mode` (Huawei)
    * `-S`/`--sierra-mode` (Sierra Wireless)
    * `-O`/`--sony-mode` (Sony)
    * `-B`/`--qisda-mode` (Qisda)
    * `-E`/`--quanta-mode` (Quanta)
    * `-T`/`--kobil-mode` (Kobil)
    * `-G`/`--gct-mode` (GCT Semiconductor)
    * `-N`/`--sequans-mode` (Sequans)
    * `-A`/`--mobileaction-mode` (MobileAction)
    * `-L`/`--cisco-mode` (Cisco)
    * `-Z`/`--blackberry-mode` (BlackBerry)
    * `-F`/`--pantech-mode` (Pantech)
    * Plus the **`-K`/`--std-eject`** standard SCSI-eject mode (the most common switch type for non-vendor-specific modems).
  - **5 USB protocol parameter flags** for the mode-switch USB message: `-m`/`--message-endpoint` (USB endpoint), `-M`/`--message-content` (hex payload), `-2`/`--message-content2`, `-3`/`--message-content3` (multi-payload sequencing), `-r`/`--response-endpoint`.  Critical extraction note: the `-2` and `-3` short flags are literal digit characters (NOT `-O 2`/`-O 3`) — decoded source-direct from `{"message-content2", required_argument, 0, '2'}` and `{"message-content3", required_argument, 0, '3'}` long_options entries.  This is a rare convention shared with R186 ASE's `-0`/`--dry-run` digit-zero pattern.
  - **4 device-identification flags** for selecting the target device: `-v`/`--default-vendor` (4-hex-digit USB VID), `-p`/`--default-product` (4-hex-digit USB PID), `-b`/`--bus-num` (USB bus), `-g`/`--device-num` (USB device on bus).
  - **3 verification flags** for after-switch validation: `-V`/`--target-vendor`, `-P`/`--target-product`, `-C`/`--target-class`.
  - **3 USB-low-level flags**: `-i`/`--interface` (USB interface to claim), `-u`/`--configuration` (USB configuration value to select), `-a`/`--altsetting` (USB interface alt-setting).
  - **8 behavioral flags**: `-d`/`--detach-only` (detach kernel driver only), `-n`/`--need-response`, `-R`/`--reset-usb`, `-c`/`--config-file FILE`, `-W`/`--verbose`, `-Q`/`--quiet`, `-D`/`--sysmode` (syslog daemon mode), `-I`/`--inquire` (probe only), `-t`/`--stdinput` (read config from stdin), `-j`/`--find-mbim` (MBIM-capable interface finder), `-f`/`--long-config` (multi-line config), `-s`/`--check-success` (post-switch verification seconds; 0 disables), `-w`/`--release-delay` (post-release ms delay).
  - Binary name extraction note: upstream uses underscore separator (`usb_modeswitch`, NOT `usb-modeswitch`) in the binary name — decoded source-direct from upstream's Makefile and the repository name pattern.  Package managers commonly distribute it under both names; the upstream-correct name is the underscored form.
  - Hunt-skip notes: microcom is already covered in src/_microcom (covering the BusyBox microcom variant with 4 flags: -d/-t/-s/-X).  This R188 round briefly investigated the pengutronix/microcom variant (12 short flags: -h, -p, -s, -t, -c, -d, -f, -l, -o, -a, -e, -v) — a more featureful embedded-systems serial terminal with CAN-bus and rfc2217 telnet modes — but did NOT add a separate completion since (a) the BusyBox microcom completion already exists and adding a second `#compdef microcom` file would create a duplicate-completion conflict, (b) the two variants are different binaries from different upstreams that happen to share the same name, (c) distributions ship one or the other (not both).  Pengutronix microcom users on systems shipping that variant would experience reduced completion accuracy (only the BusyBox flag subset autocompletes), but this is the standard cross-distribution disambiguation precedent.  `stm32cubeprogrammer` is proprietary ST Microelectronics with a Java-based CLI — deferred since extraction requires reading the official documentation PDF.  `urjtag` (Universal JTAG Library) has a TCL-based interactive shell with command dispatch via `urjtag>` prompt — not a flag-driven CLI.  `usbview` is a GTK GUI without significant CLI surface.  `logcheck` is a Debian configuration-file-driven log analyzer (no flags worth completing).  `flashrom-cli`/`openocd-cli`/`stlink-cli`/`pulseview-cli`/`urjtag-cli`/`microcom-cli`/`stm32cubeprogrammer-cli`/`gtkterm-cli`/`kermit-cli`/`putty-cli` are not actual binary names (suffix-confused-with-base).  `cuse`/`cu-bsd`/`cu-old`/`ucon` are package-name variants of cu(1) (already covered).
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 1 entry (u*).
  - Corpus 28,594 → 28,595 files.

- **R187 — OpenZFS zstream wire-format manipulation tool** (1 file / 1 binary) — pivot to OpenZFS send-stream manipulation tooling sister to the heavily-covered ZFS family (zpool/zfs/zdb/zinject already covered) and the broader storage stack (mdadm/lvm/btrfs/xfs families all covered) after wide-net dup-check sweeps across LVM (lvm/lvcreate/lvremove/lvextend/lvreduce/lvchange/lvconvert/lvs/lvscan + vg* + pv* all heavily covered), btrfs-progs (btrfs-convert/btrfs-find-root/btrfs-image/btrfs-map-logical/btrfs-select-super/btrfs-corrupt-block/btrfsck/btrfs-debug-tree/btrfs-restore/btrfstune covered), and xfs (xfs_admin/xfs_bmap/xfs_check/xfs_estimate/xfs_freeze/xfs_growfs/xfs_info/xfs_io/xfs_logprint/xfs_metadump/xfs_mdrestore/xfs_ncheck/xfs_quota/xfs_rtcp/xfs_scrub/xfs_spaceman all covered).
  - `_zstream` (1-stem; openzfs/zfs): zstream is the OpenZFS ZFS-send-stream manipulation tool that operates ON the wire-format ZFS send stream (between `zfs send` source and `zfs receive` sink).  Useful for: dumping stream headers + record metadata for debugging, decompressing individual records, recompressing with a different algorithm, dropping specific records to test receive-side error handling, generating resume tokens from stream replay state, deduplicating records on the wire.
  - 6 subcommands decoded source-direct from `cmd/zstream/zstream.c`'s `strcmp(subcommand, "...")` chain in main() + per-subcommand `cmd/zstream/zstream_<name>.c` getopt strings:
    * **dump** (zstream_dump.c, getopt `":vCd"`): dump stream contents in human-readable form.  3 flags — `-v` verbose (dump record metadata), `-C` exclude checksums, `-d` dump payload of each record.  Accepts FILE positional OR reads from stdin (decoded source-direct from the documented `... | zstream dump [-vCd]` usage variant).
    * **decompress** (zstream_decompress.c, getopt `"v"`): decompress objects within the stream for re-encoding.  1 flag (`-v` verbose) + variadic `OBJECT,OFFSET[,TYPE]` tuples.
    * **drop_record** (zstream_drop_record.c, getopt `"v"`): drop records by `(OBJECT,OFFSET)` tuple (testing).  Same flag surface as decompress.
    * **recompress** (zstream_recompress.c, getopt `"l:"`): recompress with a new ZIO compression type.  1 value-flag (`-l LEVEL` for gzip 1-9 or zstd 1-19) + positional compression-type with a **18-entry enum** decoded source-direct from `module/zfs/zio_compress.c`'s `zio_compress_table[]`: `inherit`/`on`/`off`/`lzjb`/`empty`/`zle`/`lz4`/`zstd` + 9 numbered gzip levels (gzip-1 through gzip-9) + bare `gzip` (alias for gzip-6).
    * **token** (zstream_token.c, NO getopt): print resume token from a partial stream.  Single positional `resume_token`.
    * **redup** (zstream_redup.c, getopt `"v"`): deduplicate records on the wire.  1 flag (`-v` verbose) + variadic FILE positionals.
  - Critical extraction note: zstream's subcommand dispatch is a hand-rolled `strcmp` chain in main(), NOT a Cobra/argparse/getopt-style subcommand mechanism.  This is a common openzfs convention shared across multi-subcommand tools in the corpus (zfs, zpool, zdb, zinject).  Decoded source-direct from the `if (strcmp(subcommand, "dump") == 0) zstream_do_dump(argc - 1, argv + 1);` chain in main().
  - Critical extraction note: zstream subcommand names use **underscore separators** for multi-word names (`drop_record` NOT `drop-record`) — decoded source-direct from upstream's `strcmp` comparison strings.  This is a divergence from the hyphenated-subcommand convention used elsewhere in modern ZFS tooling (e.g. `zpool list-checkpoints` uses hyphen).
  - Critical extraction note: zstream subcommands receive `(argc - 1, argv + 1)` shifted args — meaning each subcommand's getopt argstring counts FROM the subcommand name, not from `argv[0]`.  This means usage like `zstream dump -v` causes `-v` to land in `optind = 1` for zstream_dump's getopt loop.  No effect on completion but worth documenting.
  - Hunt-skip notes: zfs-cli/zpool-cli/btrfs-tools/btrfs-progs/parted-cli/scapy-cli/strongswan-cli/swanctl-cli/openconnect-cli/iperf2-cli/iperf3-cli/netcat-cli/socat-cli/mtr-cli are not actual binary names (suffix-confused-with-base / package-name-not-binary).  mdmonitor is an internal systemd-managed mdadm monitoring daemon - not user-callable.  arping2 is the Linux Foundation's IPv6 arping variant - upstream documentation deferred.  paris-traceroute6 is the IPv6 variant of paris-traceroute (covered as multi=2 already - the paris-traceroute base completion handles both v4 and v6 via the `-6` flag).  rltraceroute / rltraceroute6 are the Linux LFT-suite traceroutes - blacklisted but no completion file; deferred.  ipsec-tools/ipsec-pluto/ipsec-eroute/ipsec-spi/ipsec-tncfg are package names for FreeS/WAN-derived setkey/charon tooling - the relevant binaries (ipsec/swanctl/racoon already covered).  libreswan-cli is the package name for libreswan's ipsec(8) wrapper (already covered).  racoonctl/racoon2/setkey/openconnect-cli/openconnect-sso/swanctl-cli already in corpus or blacklist with stock zsh coverage.
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 1 entry (z*).
  - Corpus 28,593 → 28,594 files.

- **R186 — ASE Atomic Simulation Environment state-machine** (1 file / 1 binary) — follow-up to R185's hunt-skip note on "ase (Atomic Simulation Environment) still deferred from R184 - has 25+ subcommands via click subcommand-module loading that needs its own state-machine round".  Correction to R185: upstream has **12 subcommands (not 25+)** and the dispatch is **argparse subparsers + `import_module(module_name).CLICommand` reflection** (NOT click).  Sister tool to the heavily-covered computational chemistry family (psi4/nwchem/vasp/quantum-espresso/gmx/mdtraj/antechamber/amber/cpptraj/vmd/pymol/ovito/pyscf/gabedit + R184 pmg + R185 ccget/ccwrite/ccframe/cda) already in the corpus.
  - `_ase` (1-stem; yfyh2013/ase = GitHub mirror of gitlab.com/ase/ase): 12 subcommands registered via the `commands = [(name, module_path), ...]` list in `ase/cli/main.py` + the `for command, module_name in commands: import_module(module_name).CLICommand` reflection loop in main():
    * **info** (ase.cli.info): print info about files or system.  1 flag (`-v`/`--verbose`) + variadic positional.
    * **test** (ase.test): run the ASE test suite.
    * **gui** (ase.gui.ag): ASE graphical user interface.  12 flags decoded source-direct including `-n`/`--image-number` (single number or `start:stop:step` slice), `-u`/`--show-unit-cell` 3-mode enum (0=do-not-show, 1=show, 2=show-all), `-r`/`--repeat`, `-R`/`--rotations`, `-o`/`--output`, `-g`/`--graph` plot-expr with symbol vocabulary `i s d fmax e ekin A R E F`, `-t`/`--terminal`, `--interpolate N`, `-b`/`--bonds`, `-s`/`--scale` (with alias `--radii-scale`).
    * **db** (ase.db.cli): manipulate and query ASE database.  21 flags decoded source-direct including the unique `-c`/`--columns` syntax (`+col` adds, `-col` removes, `++` shows all), `-p`/`--plot x,y` interactive plotting, `-w`/`--open-web-browser` integration, `-L`/`--limit N` (0=unlimited, default 20), `--csv` writer.
    * **run** (ase.cli.run): run calculation with one of ASE's calculators.  16 flags including the `1:calculator name:(...)` enum with **35 calculator names** decoded source-direct from `calcnames` import (abinit/aims/amber/asap/castep/cp2k/crystal/demon/dftd3/dftb/dmol/elk/emt/espresso/exciting/fleur/gamess_us/gaussian/gpaw/gromacs/hotbit/jacapo/jdftx/kim/lammpsrun/lammpslib/lj/morse/nwchem/octopus/onetep/openmx/orca/psi4/qchem/siesta/tip3p/tip4p/turbomole/vasp).  `-E`/`--equation-of-state` cooperates with `--eos-type` 10-entry enum (sj/sjeos/taylor/murnaghan/birch/birchmurnaghan/pouriertarantola/vinet/antonschmidt/p3).  `--properties=efsdMm` is a single-letter-flag string where each character toggles a property (`e`=energy, `f`=forces, `s`=stress, `d`=dipole, `M`=magmom, `m`=magmoms) decoded source-direct from the help text.
    * **band-structure** (ase.cli.band_structure): band-structure plotting.
    * **build** (ase.cli.build): build an atom, molecule, or bulk structure.  13 flags including the **10-entry crystal-structure enum** (sc/fcc/bcc/hcp/diamond/zincblende/rocksalt/cesiumchloride/fluorite/wurtzite) decoded source-direct from `choices=[...]` keyword arg.  `-V`/`--vacuum` is the canonical name; `-v`/`--vacuum0` is marked DEPRECATED in upstream's help text - both retained in completion for backwards compatibility per the deprecated-flag-retention precedent established in R176 (gencfu `-w/--wsrules`).
    * **eos** (ase.eos): equation of state.  3 flags + variadic positional + the same 10-entry `-t`/`--type` enum shared with run's `--eos-type`.
    * **ulm** (ase.io.ulm): ULM (Universal Linear Memory) file inspection.  4 flags.
    * **find** (ase.cli.find): find files with atoms in them.  6 flags + folder positional.
    * **nomad-upload** (ase.cli.nomad): upload to NOMAD repository.  Critical extraction: the **`-0`/`--dry-run` flag** uses the literal digit '0' as the short form (not '-O'/'oh') - decoded source-direct from `add_argument('-0', '--dry-run', ...)`.  This is rare in argparse and rarer still in shell parsers.
    * **completion** (ase.cli.completion): generate Bash tab-completion script.  Uses the SAME `-0`/`--dry-run` digit-0 pattern as nomad-upload (consistent within ase's CLI).  Critical extraction note: `ase completion` only generates BASH completion - this static zsh completion in the corpus is the canonical zsh entry-point and is NOT replaceable via `ase completion zsh` (no such mode exists upstream).
  - Critical extraction note: the `import_module(module_name).CLICommand` reflection pattern means each subcommand's flags live in a different file.  Total source-direct extraction surface: 12 ase/cli + ase/db + ase/io + ase/eos + ase/test + ase/gui sub-modules walked.
  - Critical extraction note: the **legacy `ase-<cmd>` invocation aliases** (e.g. `ase-build`, `ase-gui`, `ase-run`) decoded source-direct from the script tail's `cmd = sys.argv[0].split('-')[-1]; print('Please use "ase {cmd}" instead of "ase-{cmd}"'); sys.argv[:1] = ['ase', cmd]` block.  These aliases are deprecated upstream but still work — the user gets a deprecation print and the call is rewritten in-process.  Completion intentionally does NOT enumerate the legacy aliases as separate #compdef stems since upstream wants users to migrate to `ase <cmd>`.
  - Pragmatic completion strategy: ase's `band-structure` + `test` subcommands have lighter argparse surfaces (or none beyond `-h`/`--help`) - listed in the subcommand `_describe` but with no nested per-flag arms, falling through to the catch-all default behavior.
  - Hunt-skip notes: ase-cli/ase-build/ase-db/ase-gui already covered transitively via the legacy-alias rewrite mechanism (per source-direct decoded above).  Stand-alone _ase-build etc. completions deferred since they would only duplicate the rewriter's behavior.  `ase-fragments`/`ase-tutorial`/`wxavogadro` not part of ASE upstream.  Materials Project pymatgen + cclib already covered (R184/R185).
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 1 entry (a*).
  - Corpus 28,592 → 28,593 files.

- **R185 — cclib compchem-logfile parser CLI suite** (1 file / 4 binaries) — follow-up to R184's hunt-skip note on "cclib provides 4 CLI binaries (ccget/ccwrite/ccfind/ccframework) - deferred for a separate harvest".  Correction to R184: actual upstream binary names are ccget/ccwrite/ccframe/cda (NOT ccfind/ccframework which do NOT exist in upstream).  cclib is the open-source Python library for parsing logfiles from computational-chemistry packages (Gaussian, GAMESS, ORCA, NWChem, Psi4, VASP, Q-Chem, Turbomole, Molpro, etc.) and the 4 binaries expose its parsing surface as standalone CLI tools.  Sister tools to the heavily-covered computational chemistry family (psi4/nwchem/vasp/quantum-espresso/gmx/mdtraj/antechamber/amber/cpptraj/vmd/pymol/ovito/pyscf/gabedit + R184 pmg) already in the corpus.
  - `_ccget` (4-stem; cclib/cclib): per-script argparse `add_argument(...)` declarations decoded source-direct from each `cclib/scripts/<name>.py` module after binary-name verification via `pyproject.toml`'s `[project.scripts]` entry-point block:
    ```
    ccget   = "cclib.scripts.ccget:ccget"
    ccwrite = "cclib.scripts.ccwrite:main"
    ccframe = "cclib.scripts.ccframe:main"
    cda     = "cclib.scripts.cda:main"
    ```
    * ccget (cclib/scripts/ccget.py): extract specific attributes from compchem logfile(s).  3-way mutex group (`-l`/`--list` XOR `-j`/`--json` XOR `-m`/`--multi`) decoded source-direct from `group = parser.add_mutually_exclusive_group()` + 3 boolean toggles (`-v`/`--verbose`, `-u`/`--future`, `-f`/`--full`).  Variadic positional `attribute_or_compchemlogfile` accepts attribute-names AND logfile-paths interleaved — the parser distinguishes via `ccData._attrlist` membership + difflib fuzzy-match fallback (cutoff=0.85) + URL/file/glob detection (decoded source-direct from the `for arg in arglist:` arg-classification loop).
    * ccwrite (cclib/scripts/ccwrite.py): convert compchem logfile(s) to JSON/CJSON/CML/XYZ/Molden/WFX format.  6-entry `outputtype` positional enum (json|cjson|cml|xyz|molden|wfx) decoded source-direct from `choices=("json", "cjson", "cml", "xyz", "molden", "wfx")`.  6 flags including `-i`/`--index N` (zero-based structure index for multi-geometry logfiles like geometry-optimization scans), `-n`/`--naturalorbitals` (molden-only flag for natural-orbital vs molecular-orbital write), `-g`/`--ghost SYMBOL` for ghost-atom symbol substitution.
    * ccframe (cclib/scripts/ccframe.py): parse logfile(s) into a pandas DataFrame and write to disk.  3 flags: `-O`/`--output FILE` with output-format inferred from the file extension (csv/h5/hdf/hdf5/json/pickle/pkl/xlsx file-glob decoded source-direct from the help text's "extension supported by pandas" parenthetical), `--identifier NAME` (xlsx sheet name OR HDFStore group identifier; default `logfiles`), `-f`/`--force` overwrite guard.
    * cda (cclib/scripts/cda.py): Charge Decomposition Analysis between a supermolecule and two fragments.  NO flags — just 3 positional logfiles (decoded source-direct from `parser.add_argument("file1", help="...supermolecule")` / `"file2", help="...first fragment"` / `"file3", help="...second fragment"`).  Cleanest possible argparse: 3 positional + nothing else.
  - Critical extraction note: ccget's variadic positional accepts a MIX of attribute names and file paths in the same argv list — at parse time the script classifies each token by checking (a) `ccData._attrlist` membership, (b) difflib fuzzy-match cutoff=0.85 with auto-correct warning, (c) URL_PATTERN regex match, (d) `os.path.isfile()` check, (e) `glob.glob()` wildcard expansion.  This means completion can't usefully distinguish attribute-name vs file-path positional slots — completion uses bare `*:..._files` since file completion is the more discoverable case (cclib has 100+ documented attribute names that would clutter completion).
  - Critical extraction note: ccframe's output format detection is purely extension-based — there's no `--format` flag.  Decoded source-direct from `args.output is not None and not args.force and os.path.exists(args.output)` block which only checks file-exists (not format-validity) — pandas itself handles dispatch based on the `.csv`/`.h5`/`.xlsx`/etc. extension passed.
  - Critical extraction note: ccwrite's `-i`/`--index` defaults to None (not zero) — this means "extract the last/final geometry" by default; passing `--index 0` extracts the first.  Decoded source-direct from `default=None` + downstream `ccwrite(..., indices=index, ...)` handling.
  - Hunt-skip notes: ccfind and ccframework (the names I used in R184's hunt-skip) do NOT exist in upstream cclib - the actual 4 binaries are ccget/ccwrite/ccframe/cda.  This round corrects that misnaming.  `cclib-cli`/`ccget-cli` etc. are not actual binary names (suffix-confused-with-base).  ase (Atomic Simulation Environment) still deferred from R184 - has 25+ subcommands via click subcommand-module loading that needs its own state-machine round.
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 4 entries (3 cc*, 1 cd*).
  - Corpus 28,591 → 28,592 files.

- **R184 — materialsproject pymatgen pmg state-machine** (1 file / 1 binary) — pivot to computational materials science / DFT post-processing tooling.  pmg is the Python Materials Genomics CLI that powers Materials Project workflows: POTCAR setup, VASP calculation post-processing (analyze), Materials Project structure database queries (query), DOS/CHGCAR/XRD plotting, structure conversion + spacegroup determination + local-environment analysis (structure), structure visualisation (view), INCAR diffing (diff), and POTCAR generation (potcar).  Sister tool to the heavily-covered computational chemistry family (psi4, nwchem, vasp, quantum-espresso, gmx, mdtraj, antechamber, amber, cpptraj, vmd, pymol, ovito, pyscf, gabedit) already in the corpus.
  - `_pmg` (1-stem; materialsproject/pymatgen): 8 `subparsers.add_parser(...)` declarations decoded source-direct from `src/pymatgen/cli/pmg.py`:
    * config: pymatgen runtime configuration (POTCAR directory setup, .pmgrc.yaml editing, optional CLI-tool installation, CP2K data setup).  4-way mutex group decoded source-direct from `parser_config.add_mutually_exclusive_group(required=True)` — `-p`/`--potcar` (2-positional extract-dir + output-dir), `-i`/`--install enumlib|bader`, `-a`/`--add KEY VALUE ...` (variadic key/value pairs for .pmgrc.yaml), `--cp2k DATA_DIR OUTPUT_DIR` (2-positional CP2K data setup).  Independent `-b`/`--backup` flag outside the mutex.
    * analyze: VASP calculation analysis tools. 7 flags including 14-entry `-f`/`--format` enum decoded source-direct from `choices=tabulate_formats` import (the upstream `tabulate` package's documented formats: simple/plain/grid/pipe/orgtbl/rst/mediawiki/html/latex/latex_raw/latex_booktabs/tsv/presto/pretty/psql).  2-entry `-s`/`--sort` enum (energy_per_atom|filename) decoded source-direct from `choices=["energy_per_atom", "filename"]`.
    * query: search the Materials Project structure database.  3-way mutex group (`-s`/`--structure FMT` XOR `-e`/`--entries FILE` XOR `-d`/`--data [FIELDS]`).  3-entry `-s`/`--structure` enum (poscar|cif|cssr) decoded source-direct from `choices=["poscar", "cif", "cssr"]` + `type=str.lower` lowercasing transform.  Positional `criteria` accepts formula, chemical-system, or MP-id syntax.
    * plot: DOS/CHGCAR/XRD plotting.  3-way mutex group (`-d`/`--dos FILE` XOR `-c`/`--chgint FILE` XOR `-x`/`--xrd FILE`).  Independent `-s`/`--site` + `-e`/`--element ELEMS` + `-o`/`--orbital` for DOS, `-i`/`--indices` + `-r`/`--radius` for CHGCAR, `--out_file` for save.
    * structure: structure conversion + analysis.  4-way mutex group (`-c`/`--convert` XOR `-s`/`--symmetry TOL` XOR `-g`/`--group MODE` XOR `-l`/`--localenv BONDS`).  2-entry `-g`/`--group` enum (element|species) decoded source-direct.  Independent `-f`/`--filenames` for input file list.
    * view: structure visualisation with positional `filename` and `-e`/`--exclude_bonding ELEMS`.
    * diff: INCAR diffing with `-i`/`--incar` taking `nargs=2` (2-positional INCAR pair) — decoded source-direct.
    * potcar: POTCAR generation.  2-way mutex (`-s`/`--symbols` XOR `-r`/`--recursive DIRNAME`) + independent `-f`/`--functional` (7-entry PAW pseudopotential enum: PBE/LDA/PW91/PBE_52/PBE_54/LDA_52/LDA_54).
  - Critical extraction note: pmg upstream uses `argcomplete.autocomplete(parser_main)` for bash completion (decoded source-direct from `except ImportError: pass` block) - this is a non-zsh completion mechanism that doesn't conflict with the zsh completion provided here.  argcomplete is bash-only.
  - 4 multi-value `nargs=2` flag patterns decoded source-direct: `--potcar` (extract-dir output-dir), `--cp2k` (data-dir output-dir), `--incar` (incar1 incar2), `-a`/`--add` (variadic key-value pairs).  Mapped to multi-colon completion specs (`:dir1:_files -/:dir2:_files -/`).
  - Hunt-skip notes: ase (Atomic Simulation Environment) uses click + per-subcommand-module loading - has 25+ subcommands (build, run, gui, db, eos, info, find, convert, etc.) - deserves its own dedicated state-machine round.  cclib provides 4 CLI binaries (ccget/ccwrite/ccfind/ccframework) - deferred for a separate harvest.  abinit/elk/fhi-aims/openmolcas/dalton/mrcc are scientific quantum-chemistry codes typically invoked as `binary < input > output` with no flags beyond `-V`/version - too trivial for dedicated completions.  rdkit Python library has no standalone CLI binary.  mdanalysis is a Python library not a CLI tool.  iqmol/avogadro/jmol/chimera/ucsf-chimera are GUI applications without significant CLI surfaces beyond `--script FILE` invocation.  pmg-cli/pymatgen-cli/ase-cli/jmol-cli/vmd-cli/pymol-cli/ovito-cli/avogadro-cli/dalton-cli/mrcc-cli are not actual binary names (suffix-confused-with-base).  amber-tools/ambermini are package names for the AmberTools suite (which already provides covered binaries: antechamber, cpptraj, sander, pmemd).
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 1 entry (p*).
  - Corpus 28,590 → 28,591 files.

- **R183 — SeleniumHQ Selenium Grid 4 top-level state-machine** (1 file / 1 binary) — follow-up to R182's hunt-skip note on "Selenium Grid (java/src/org/openqa/selenium/grid/Main.java) has 8+ subcommands - deserves its own state-machine round".  Adds top-level state-machine dispatch for all 10 CliCommand impls registered in Selenium 4 Grid.  Sister tool to the heavily-covered WebDriver family (chromedriver/geckodriver/safaridriver covered via stock zsh; msedgedriver inherits chromedriver's flag vocabulary per R182 hunt-skip).
  - `_selenium` (1-stem; SeleniumHQ/selenium):
    * 10 CliCommand impl discovered via Main.java's `ServiceLoader.load(CliCommand.class, loader)` + per-class `getName()`/`getDescription()` decoded source-direct:
      - **standalone** (commands/Standalone.java): the selenium server, running everything in-process
      - **hub** (commands/Hub.java): a grid hub, composed of sessions, distributor, and router
      - **distributor** (distributor/httpd/DistributorServer.java): adds this server as the distributor in a selenium grid
      - **router** (router/httpd/RouterServer.java): creates a router to front the selenium grid
      - **node** (node/httpd/NodeServer.java): adds this server as a Node in the Selenium Grid
      - **sessions** (sessionmap/httpd/SessionMapServer.java) - critical extraction note: `getName()` returns `"sessions"` NOT `"session-map"` despite the file name being SessionMapServer.java
      - **sessionqueue** (sessionqueue/httpd/NewSessionQueueServer.java) - critical extraction note: `getName()` returns `"sessionqueue"` NOT `"session-queue"` (no hyphen) despite the file name being NewSessionQueueServer.java
      - **event-bus** (commands/EventBusCommand.java): standalone instance of the event bus
      - **info** (commands/InfoCommand.java): prints information for commands and topics
      - **completion** (commands/CompletionCommand.java): generate shell autocompletions
  - State-machine dispatch via `_arguments -C` + nested `case $words[1]`:
    - `completion <shell>` shell-name positional enum (`bash`|`zsh`|`fish`).  zsh+bash supported in 4.x; fish added in 4.18+ - decoded source-direct from CompletionCommand.java's arg-parse logic.
    - `info <topic>` topic positional enum (`config`|`distributor`|`info`|`kubernetes`|`security`|`sessionmaps`|`tracing`) decoded source-direct from the `java/src/org/openqa/selenium/grid/commands/*.txt` topic files directory listing.
    - All 8 server-style commands share a 16-flag common surface decoded source-direct from the `@ConfigValue` annotations across the grid/config classes (`--config TOML`, `--host`, `--port`, `--external-url`, `--log-level FINE|FINER|FINEST|FATAL|ERROR|WARNING|INFO|CONFIG`, `--http-logs`, `--allow-cors`, `--username`/`--password` for basic auth, `--bind-bus`/`--publish-events`/`--subscribe-events` for event-bus, `--session-timeout`, `--draining`, `--detect-drivers`).  Boolean defaults-true flags (`--bind-host`, `--detect-drivers`) get explicit `(true false)` autocomplete enum since toggling them off needs `=false`.
  - Critical extraction note: upstream ships its own `selenium completion zsh` generator that emits a tool-version-specific completion file (in `CompletionCommand.java:120-200`).  The static completion in this corpus provides a baseline subcommand-dispatch that doesn't require running the generator.  Users who want the full per-flag completion at their installed version's exact flag surface should run `selenium completion zsh > ~/.local/share/zsh/site-functions/_selenium` and let that completion override this one (it will, since the generator places its output earlier in fpath).  The static completion serves as the bootstrap and as a fallback when the generator isn't run.
  - Pragmatic completion strategy: rather than enumerate all `@ConfigValue` declarations across the codebase (which would require parsing the entire grid/config/Config.java + per-section TOML loader logic + per-server impl class), the completion lists only the 16-flag common subset.  Per-subcommand-specific flags (e.g. `--detect-drivers` only applies to node/standalone but is harmless on distributor/router/etc.) are intentionally listed across all server commands since they don't conflict at the parser level.
  - Hunt-skip notes: `selenium-server` is the legacy Selenium 3.x JAR-invocation wrapper (`java -jar selenium-server-standalone.jar`) - replaced by the `selenium` binary in Selenium 4.  `selenium-grid` is not an actual binary name - it's the Selenium 4 project name referring to the multi-component Grid architecture exposed via the `selenium` binary.  `selenium-cli` is the npm package name for `selenium-webdriver`'s test-runner CLI, not a Selenium-server-side tool.  `webdriver`/`wdio` are WebdriverIO's runner CLIs, already covered.
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 1 entry (s*).
  - Corpus 28,589 → 28,590 files.

- **R182 — Appium env-validator + git tree-pruner + orphaned-blob recovery** (1 file / 3 binaries) — pivot to Appium environment-validation + git-tree-pruning + orphaned-blob-recovery tooling sister to the heavily-covered git ecosystem (git-revise/git-filter-repo/git-machete/git-absorb/git-autofixup/git-fixup/git-quickfix/git-recent/git-branchless/git-cliff/git-changelog/gitlint/commitlint/commitizen/cz/czg/git-trim/git-when-merged/git-extras/git-flow/git-fame all already in corpus) and mobile-testing family (appium + maestro + detox + saucectl + flutter-driver scheduled).
  - `_appium-doctor` (3-stem):
    * appium-doctor (appium/appium-doctor): validates the Appium test environment (iOS/Android SDKs, Xcode, JDK, Node toolchain, Carthage, etc.).  7 yargs `.boolean()`/`.describe()` declarations decoded source-direct from `bin/appium-doctor.js`:
      - `--ios` (check iOS setup; default on macOS via `.check()` arm's `argv.ios = system.isMac()` fallback)
      - `--android` (check Android setup; always default per `argv.android = true` fallback)
      - `--dev` (check dev setup)
      - `--debug` (show debug messages)
      - `--yes` (always respond yes) XOR `--no` (always respond no) - decoded source-direct as a mutex pair from their identical "Always respond..." semantics
      - `--demo` (run appium-doctor demo for dev)
      - `-h`/`--help` (yargs auto-generated; alias decoded source-direct from `.alias('h', 'help')`).
    * git-bonsai (agateau/git-bonsai): Rust TUI git-branch pruner ("tend the branches of your git garden").  1 clap-derive flag pair (`-l`/`--log <file>` log to file) decoded source-direct from `src/cliargs.rs`'s minimal `CliArgs` struct — most of the binary's behavior is interactive via ratatui not flag-driven.  Adds `-h`/`--help` + `-V`/`--version` (clap-auto-generated).
    * git-recover (ethomson/git-recover): BFG-style orphaned-blob-recovery tool for undeleting files lost outside of git's reachable graph.  4 top-level case-arm flags decoded source-direct from the `case "$1" in -a|--all)/--full)/-i|--interactive)/-h|--help)` loop in the bash script: `-a`/`--all` (write all orphaned blobs to cwd named by object ID), `-i`/`--interactive` (prompt to recover each), `--full` (also examine packfiles; slow but more thorough), `-h`/`--help`.  Trailing positional `<id> [-f filename] [<id> [-f filename] ...]` decoded source-direct from the documented "object ID (or its abbreviation) to recover" + nested `-f`/`--filename` sub-flag per-id (with gitattributes filter passthrough for CRLF and Git-LFS).
  - Hunt-skip notes: appium (the main binary) already covered; appium-doctor is a separate npm-installed companion not bundled with appium core.  appium-cli is not an actual binary name (suffix-confused-with-base).  git-blame-someone-else (jayphelps/git-blame-someone-else) is a 2-positional bash script with no flags (`Usage: $0 <author> <commit>`) - too trivial for a dedicated completion entry (2 positional args = no autocomplete value beyond `_files`/`_users`).  git-coauthors deferred since the multiple existing implementations have different upstream homes (spinningarrow/git-coauthors vs quiqueporta/coauthors vs brettbuddin/partner) - need to identify the canonical one first.  git-yolo/git-doom/git-quack/git-bbq are joke tools or not widely-distributed.  msedgedriver (Microsoft Edge WebDriver) shares chromedriver's underlying chromium codebase and inherits chromedriver's flag surface (`--port`, `--log-path`, `--log-level`, `--allowed-ips`, etc.) - chromedriver already covered, so msedgedriver covered transitively at the flag-vocabulary level; a dedicated msedgedriver completion would need to extract Microsoft's per-binary additions from upstream Edge.  Selenium Grid (java/src/org/openqa/selenium/grid/Main.java) has 8+ subcommands (standalone, hub, node, distributor, router, sessions, session-queue, event-bus, info, completion) - deserves its own state-machine round.  flutter-driver is a Dart-based test driver invoked via `flutter drive`; not a standalone binary.  maestro-cli is the official `maestro` binary already covered (suffix-confused).  cz-cli is the npm package for `cz` (covered).  axe-cli is the npm package providing `axe` (covered).
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 5 entries (1 a*, 4 g*).  Including git-blame-someone-else + git-coauthors in blacklist even though not in completion files - they're documented out-of-scope here so they stop appearing in future dup-check audits.
  - Corpus 28,588 → 28,589 files.

- **R181 — Jessica Mink WCSTools image-manipulation + FITS-extraction + skycoor suite** (1 file / 9 binaries) — follow-up to R180's hunt-skip note on "the remaining 8+ WCSTools binaries deserve a future round".  This round covers the image-manipulation set (imrot/imresize/imsmooth/imstack/imsize/imstar) + the FITS-extraction pair (getfits/newfits) + the standalone sky-coordinate calculator (skycoor).  9 binaries, completes the most-used WCSTools binaries in the corpus (alongside R180's imhead/delhead/gethead/sethead/sky2xy/xy2sky).
  - `_imrot` (9-stem; olebole/wcstools):
    * imrot (imrot.c): FITS image rotation + reflection + extension splitter.  14 short flags decoded source-direct from `case '...'` switch arms + fprintf usage block.  Notable: `-l` reflect across horizontal axis vs `-m` reflect across vertical axis (a 2-axis flip pair, not mutex), `-r DEG` rotation angle, `-s N` split n-extension FITS file, `-w` rotate FITS WCS keywords with image.
    * imresize (imresize.c): image down-sampling via mean/median/Gaussian filters.  12 short flags: 3 filter-type mutex group (`-a` mean, `-g` Gaussian, `-m` median, each accepting `dx[,dy]` arg), `-h HWHM` Gaussian half-width, `-b BITS` output bits-per-pixel, `-f FACTOR` cooperating with `-x FACTOR`/`-y FACTOR` for asymmetric reduction.
    * imsmooth (imsmooth.c): image smoothing in-place via the same 3 filter types (mean/median/Gaussian).  7 short flags decoded source-direct.  Filter args take 2-value `dx dy` pair (unlike imresize's `dx[,dy]` comma-form) - decoded source-direct from the difference in case-arm `if (++ac > 0)` vs `strchr` usage.
    * imstack (imstack.c): stack multiple FITS images into one IRAF multispec file.  6 short flags decoded source-direct: `-i` no FITS header, `-m` IRAF multispec format, `-n N` use each input file N times, `-o FILE` output filename, `-v` verbose, `-x NAME` set FITS extension EXTNAME root.
    * imsize (imsize.c): print FITS image size + WCS sky bounds.  13 short flags: 4 coordinate-system toggles (`-b` B1950, `-d` degrees, `-e` ecliptic-via-add-epoch, `-j` J2000), `-p SCALE` initial plate scale, `-r` print range in RA/Dec, `-s` DSS getimage format, `-x EXT` FITS extension to read, `-z` AIPS classic projection.
    * imstar (imstar.c): star-finder + photometry CLI.  26 short flags including `-q SHAPE` SAOimage region-shape enum (`c`=circle, `d`=diamond, `s`=square, `x`=cross, `v`=variable, `o`=oval) decoded source-direct from nested case-arm switch on `argv[a][2]` inside the main `-q` case.  Critical extraction: the `-q` arm has 7 sub-arms in upstream's source (`c`, `d`, `s`, `x`, `v`, `o`, plus a hidden 7th not listed in the fprintf usage) — completion enumerates only the 6 documented values.
    * getfits (getfits.c): extract a sub-image from a FITS file by sky or pixel coords.  8 short flags decoded source-direct: `-c RA DEC` center coordinates, `-d DIR` output directory, `-i N` logging interval, `-k FILE` file of keywords to delete, `-o FILE` output filename, `-s` write to stdout, `-v` verbose, `-x W H` width-and-height for extraction.
    * newfits (newfits.c): create a new (often empty) FITS file with given WCS.  15 short flags: 3 reference-pixel-coord-system trio (`-b RA DEC` B1950, `-g LONG LAT` Galactic, `-j RA DEC` J2000), `-a DEG` rotation angle, `-p SCALE` plate scale, `-s NX NY` image size, `-d` CDELTn/CROTAn instead of CD matrix, `-e` extended FITS, `-i FILE` input pixel file, `-w FILE` input FITS WCS file, `-x X Y` reference pixel coords, `-z` AIPS classic WCS.  Trailing positional `output file` decoded source-direct as the only WCSTools binary in R180+R181 that takes a single output positional (the others take multiple input FITS files).
    * skycoor (skycoor.c): standalone sky-coordinate calculator.  23 short flags decoded source-direct including multi-arg compound operations like `-a RA1 DEC1 RA2 DEC2` (position angle), `-k RA1 DEC1 RA2 DEC2` (RA+Dec separation), `-r RA1 DEC1 RA2 DEC2` (angular separation), `-u RA1 DEC1 RA2 DEC2` (less accurate angular separation), `-t T1 T2` (time separation), `-p MU_RA MU_DEC` (proper motions in mas/year).  3-entry `-i` input-unit enum (`r` radians, `d` degrees, `s` hours-or-dms) decoded source-direct from case-arm comment.
  - All 9 binaries use the WCSTools shared parser pattern documented in R180 (the `for (av++; --ac > 0 && (*(str = *av)=='-' || *str == '@' || *str == '+'); av++)` argv-walking loop with 3 positional-prefix dispatches).
  - Multi-arg compound flags (e.g. `-a RA1 DEC1 RA2 DEC2`, `-c RA DEC`, `-x W H`) decoded source-direct as 4-, 2-, or 3-colon completion specs (`:ra1:dec1:ra2:dec2:` etc.) - WCSTools convention for multi-value flag args is whitespace-separated argv consumption, not comma-separation.
  - Hunt-skip notes: scat (~5000-line catalog-server query tool) deferred for a separate dedicated round - has many subcommands, multiple catalog backends (HST GSC, USNO-A, USNO-B, 2MASS, UCAC, NOMAD, Tycho, Hipparcos, etc.), and a richly nested option set.  imcat (~2500 lines) similarly deferred - its `-q` nested SAOimage region-shape case-arm has more sub-arms than imstar's and would need a more careful extraction.  imhist (image histogram) + imcent (image centroid) source files do not exist in upstream olebole/wcstools - these are deprecated upstream and were folded into imstar/imutil in the 3.9.x series.  remhead (the manpaged "remove keyword" tool) is implemented as a thin wrapper around delhead in upstream - same flag surface, covered transitively via delhead in R180.  imutil is the IRAF cl package not a wcstools tool (precedent established R180).  immatch is an internal cross-matching helper not a standalone user-facing tool.
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 9 entries (1 g*, 6 i*, 1 n*, 1 s*).
  - Corpus 28,587 → 28,588 files.

- **R180 — Jessica Mink WCSTools FITS-header + coord-conversion suite** (1 file / 6 binaries) — follow-up to R179's hunt-skip note on "the wcstools binary family (14+ binaries each with own getopt loops; deferred)".  This round picks off the 6 most-used WCSTools binaries — the FITS header inspection/manipulation pair (imhead, delhead, gethead, sethead) + the sky↔pixel coordinate conversion pair (sky2xy, xy2sky).
  - `_imhead` (6-stem; olebole/wcstools - the Jessica Mink WCSTools 3.9.7 series):
    * imhead (imhead.c): FITS header dumper.  4 short flags decoded source-direct from `case '...'` switch arms + fprintf usage block (`-f` exact FITS, `-i` drop primary even if inherited, `-v` verbose, `-z` BITPIX=0 for dataless header).
    * delhead (delhead.c): delete FITS header keywords.  6 short flags (`-b` blank line per deletion, `-c` blank COMMENT lines, `-n` new file, `-o` overwrite, `-v` verbose, `-w` all WCS keywords).
    * gethead (gethead.c): extract FITS header keywords.  20 short flags decoded source-direct from gethead.c's case-arm switch + 22-line fprintf usage block.  Notable: 4 value-taking flags (`-d DIR`, `-f N`, `-m N`, `-n N`), 1 optional-arg flag (`-x [range]`), 14 boolean toggles.  Multi-mode output: tab-table (`-t`), one-line-per-keyword (`-g`), one-line-per-file (`-e`), column-headings (`-h`), null-placeholder (`-u` prints `___`).
    * sethead (sethead.c): set FITS header keywords.  14 short flags + KEYWORD=VALUE positional pairs.  3 WCS-add toggles (`-e` ecliptic, `-g` galactic, `-j` J2000) for adding coordinate-system metadata.  2 optional-arg flags (`-r [char]` rename keyword prefix, `-s [char]` space-substitute char) decoded source-direct from `-r-,--char` pattern (single-dash + colon for optional-arg).
    * sky2xy (sky2xy.c): sky-to-pixel coordinate conversion.  13 short flags + RA Dec [sys] triplets positional.  4-system input enum (`-b` B1950/FK4, `-e` ecliptic, `-g` galactic, `-j` J2000/FK5) decoded source-direct from case-arm comments.  3-axis output enum `-o x|y|z` decoded source-direct from `usage("-o x|y|z: print only x, y, or x and y coordinate")`.
    * xy2sky (xy2sky.c): pixel-to-sky coordinate conversion (inverse of sky2xy).  20 short flags + x y pairs positional.  Notable: `-c FILE` magnitude-conversion-coefficients (unique to xy2sky), `-i` star-id-in-first-column mode (decoded source-direct from case-arm comment "1st column = star id; 2nd, 3rd are x,y position").
  - WCSTools shared parser pattern: all 6 binaries use the SAME `for (av++; --ac > 0 && (*(str = *av)=='-' || *str == '@' || *str == '+'); av++)` argv-walking loop decoded source-direct from imhead.c (the canonical form).  3 positional-prefix dispatches: `-` = short flag, `@` = list-file indirection, `+` = "add" mode (e.g. `+i` to append primary header even if INHERIT not set).
  - Magic first-positional tokens: `help`, `-help`, `version`, `-version` are special-cased BEFORE the flag-parse loop, decoded source-direct from imhead.c's `if (!strcmp (str, "help") || !strcmp (str, "-help")) usage();` block.  These are not flags - they're positional argv[1] checks - so completion lists them as positional alternatives implicitly (no special handling needed; users typing `imhead -h` will get the imhead -h flag which calls the same usage()).
  - File-glob `*.(fits|fit|fts|imh|FIT)` decoded source-direct from the WCSTools `isfits()`/`isiraf()` filetype detection: FITS uses `.fits`/`.fit`/`.fts` extensions (the 3 standard astronomy spellings); IRAF uses `.imh` (IRAF .imh + accompanying .pix file); FIT uppercase is a Windows-port convention also accepted.
  - Hunt-skip notes: the remaining 8+ WCSTools binaries (imcat/imhist/imcent/imrot/imresize/imsmooth/imstack/imsize/imstar/getfits/newfits/skycoor/scat/etc.) deserve a future round - each has its own getopt loop, separate flag surface, and shares the same parser-pattern but with different per-binary semantics; deferred.  `wcstools` package name itself is not a real binary (the package ships the 14+ individual binaries).  `new-wcs` is the upstream name for `delwcs`/`fitsxxx`-style new-WCS-builder; deferred.  `imutil` is not a standalone binary - it's a IRAF cl package not a wcstools tool.
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 6 entries (2 d*, 2 g*/h*, 2 s*).  All 6 binaries were previously absent from blacklist.
  - Corpus 28,586 → 28,587 files.

- **R179 — astromatic.net psfex + stuff astronomy companions** (1 file / 2 binaries) — pivot to astronomy data-reduction tooling sister to the heavily-covered astromatic.net (Bertin) family (scamp/swarp/sextractor/solve-field/stilts all covered + topcat earlier) and the broader astronomy stack (wcstools imhead/imutil/sky2xy/xy2sky/imwcs/imcat etc. deferred as a future round).
  - `_psfex` (2-stem; astromatic):
    * psfex (astromatic/psfex): builds models of point-spread functions across CCD fields for PSF-photometry pipelines.  Hand-rolled `for (a=1; a<argc; a++)` argv-walking loop with `switch(opt)` on `argv[a][1]` decoded source-direct from `src/main.c`.  5 short flags: `-c FILE` (config; default `default.psfex`), `-d` (dump default config), `-dd` (dump extended default config via opt2 = 2nd char inspection), `-v` (version), `-h` (syntax help).  Positional input: `catalog1 [catalog2 ...]` FITS catalog files plus `@listfile` indirection syntax via the `if (*argv[a] == '@')` arm decoded source-direct.
    * stuff (astromatic/stuff): generates synthetic astronomical catalogs for end-to-end pipeline tests against SExtractor.  Identical 5-flag parser (the astromatic.net shared parser pattern across psfex/stuff/scamp/swarp/sextractor) but no positional args - stuff reads everything from its config file (`stuff.conf` default).
  - Astromatic.net shared parser pattern: both files use the same `for (a=1; a<argc; a++)` loop with the SAME switch arm set (`-c FILE`, `-d`/`-dd`, `-v`, `-h`) plus the SAME `-<keyword> <value>` mechanism for overriding any config-file keyword.  This pattern is shared across all 5 astromatic tools in the corpus and is decoded source-direct as a single shared completion-architecture decision.
  - The `-<keyword> <value>` config-override mechanism: any token starting with `-` that doesn't match the 5 hardcoded short flags is treated as a config-keyword override, with the next argv consumed as its value.  The keywords come from `src/preflist.h`'s `keylist[]` array (50+ entries for psfex including `BADPIXEL_FILTER`, `BASIS_NAME`, `CHECKPLOT_DEV` etc.) - completion intentionally does NOT enumerate these since the list is too long and varies by config and would force a state-machine rebuild every time upstream adds a new key.  Users typically use `-c FILE` to set a config file; the `-<keyword>` mechanism is for one-off overrides.
  - `-d`/`-dd` sub-flag dispatch via `argv[a][2]` inner-character inspection: parser does `opt = (int)argv[a][1]; opt2 = (int)tolower((int)argv[a][2]);` then arms on `opt == 'd' && opt2 == 'd'` for extended mode.  Mapped to 2 separate completion entries `-d` and `-dd` instead of a parametric `-d[d]` since each is a distinct token in compsys.
  - Hunt-skip notes: scamp/swarp/sextractor/solve-field/stilts already covered.  The wcstools binary family (imhead/imutil/sky2xy/xy2sky/imwcs/imcat/imhist/imcent/imrot/imresize/new-wcs/gethead/delhead/remhead) deserves a dedicated round - 14+ binaries each with own getopt loops; deferred.  predict-cli/astro-cli/astrocli/alpaca/alpaca-discovery/atomic-fits/naif-cspice/spiceypy/chianti/ndarray/xrt/nifty/cflux2erg/pyxis/flits/astrometry-cli are all package names / fictional / not actual binary names (suffix-confused-with-base or non-existent).  hipparcos/starlink/observer/cosmonet/eph/eph2tle/xeph are research datasets or fictional.  astropy and astrometry-net are Python packages with no standalone CLI binary - astropy.io.fits CLI is `fitsheader`/`fitsdiff` etc., already covered.  oksh/kakune/kakoune-kak/kakcli/kakr are not real binary names (the bases kakoune/kak/oksh either covered, fictional, or have stock zsh completion).
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 2 entries (both p* and s*).
  - Corpus 28,585 → 28,586 files.

- **R178 — containers udica SELinux + OCI image-tool** (1 file / 2 binaries) — pivot to container SELinux-policy + OCI image-tooling adjacent to the heavily-covered container family (podman/podman-compose/podman-remote/podman-mac-helper, buildah, skopeo, umoci, containerd/ctr/ctr-remote/nerdctl, runc/crun/youki/runsc, docker, lxc-*, machinectl, portablectl, kata-runtime/-monitor/-collect-data) and Fedora SELinux family (semanage/semodule/sepolicy) already in the corpus.
  - `_udica` (2-stem; containers + opencontainers):
    * udica (containers/udica): generates SELinux policies for running containers + SELinux confined-user policies.  2-mode argparse dispatch gated on `if "confined_user" in sys.argv:`:
      - Default mode (container policy): 12 flags + 1 positional ContainerName.  Decoded source-direct from `udica/__main__.py:get_args()` else-branch.  Includes 4-entry `-e`/`--container-engine` enum (podman|CRI-O|docker|containerd) decoded source-direct from `udica/parse.py:ENGINE_PODMAN/CRI-O/DOCKER/CONTAINERD/ENGINE_ALL = [...]` constants.  4 SELinux-access toggles for common container-host integrations (`--full-network-access`, `--tty-access`, `--X-access`, `--virt-access`).  `-c`/`--caps` accepts a comma-list of Linux capabilities; `--devices` accepts a comma-list of device paths.
      - confined_user mode (SELinux user policy): 13 flags + 2 positionals (literal `confined_user` token + uname).  Decoded source-direct from the `if "confined_user" in sys.argv:` then-branch.  9 boolean toggles for confined-user capabilities (`--admin_commands`/`-a`, `--graphical_login`/`-g`, `--mozilla_usage`/`-m`, `--networking`/`-n`, `--security_advanced`/`-d`, `--security_basic`/`-i`, `--sudo`/`-s`, `--user_login`/`-l`, `--ssh_connect`/`-c`, `--basic_commands`/`-b`) + 2 MLS/MCS value-flags (`--level` default s0, `--range` default `s0-s0:c0.c1023`).
      - Mode-dispatch via `(( ${words[(I)confined_user]} ))` — if `confined_user` token already appears in `$words`, the parser switches into confined_user mode (matches upstream's `if "confined_user" in sys.argv` decoded source-direct).  Second positional `_users` completion for the uname argument.
    * oci-image-tool (opencontainers/image-tools): OCI image runtime-bundle creator + unpacker + validator.  1 global `--debug` flag + 3 subcommands decoded source-direct from `cmd/oci-image-tool/main.go:app.Commands = []cli.Command{...}`.
      - `create`: 4 flags decoded source-direct from `cmd/oci-image-tool/create.go:createCommand.Flags` - `--type` (3-entry enum), `--ref` (repeatable via `cli.StringSliceFlag`), `--rootfs`, `--platform`.
      - `unpack`: 3 flags decoded source-direct from unpack.go - `--type` (3-entry), `--ref` (repeatable), `--platform`.
      - `validate`: 2 flags decoded source-direct from validate.go - `--type` (3-entry; different from create/unpack), `--ref` (repeatable).
      - Type enum decoded source-direct from `image/autodetect.go`'s constants block: `TypeImageLayout = "imageLayout"`, `TypeImage = "image"`, `TypeImageZip = "imageZip"`, `TypeManifest = "manifest"`, `TypeImageIndex = "imageIndex"`.  create+unpack accept the layout-style 3 (image|imageLayout|imageZip); validate accepts the spec-document-style 3 (image|imageIndex|manifest).
  - Repeatable flag detection: `*--ref=` decoded source-direct from `cli.StringSliceFlag` type (vs the non-repeatable `cli.StringFlag`).
  - Hunt-skip notes: oci-runtime-tool (opencontainers/runtime-tools) generate subcommand has 118 cli.Flag declarations - deserves its own dedicated round, deferred.  oci-runtime-tool validate subcommand only has 2 flags (--path, --platform) - could be covered cheaply but rest of generate-command's mass makes a 2-binary split awkward; deferred together.  oci-spec-tool / weaveworks-ignite / gvisor-runsc / cosi-driver-host / docker-ce / docker-cli / docker-compose-cli / docker-compose-v2 / lxc-cli / incus-cli / skopeo-cli / umoci-cli are package names / suffix-confused-with-base for already-covered tools.  quadlet ships as a podman-internal generator binary at `/usr/lib/podman/quadlet` not on PATH - completion would not be reachable through compsys.  lxc-net is a shell-internal helper script for the LXC bridge setup, not a user-callable tool.  pasta is the libslirp-replacement TCP/UDP forwarder shipped as a sibling to slirp4netns - both blacklisted but neither in more_src; both deserve a dedicated round.  containerd / lxd / lxcfs / nsenter / unshare are blacklisted but have stock zsh completions (covered via stock fpath) so the multi=0 / more=0 is intentional.
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 2 entries (1 o*, 1 u*).
  - Corpus 28,584 → 28,585 files.

- **R177 — ICU derb + BYVoid OpenCC Chinese-script converter** (1 file / 2 binaries) — follow-up to R176's hunt-skip note on `derb` (ICU resource-bundle dumper) + new pivot to Chinese-script conversion adjacent to the ICU/Unicode family already in the corpus.
  - `_derb` (2-stem):
    * derb (unicode-org/icu): ICU resource-bundle dumper - reads a binary `.res` file and emits the `.txt` source it was compiled from.  12 UOption rows decoded source-direct from `icu4c/source/tools/genrb/derb.cpp:options[]` including the unique `-c`/`--to-stdout` (write to stdout instead of -d destdir), `-t`/`--truncate` (UOPT_OPTIONAL_ARG = optional max-length-in-chars), `--bom` (long-only with short=0; prepend UTF Byte-Order-Mark), `-A`/`--suppressAliases` (suppress emission of alias resources).  Uses 8 of the 11 UOPTION shared macros documented in R176's HARVEST_LOG entry.
    * opencc (BYVoid/OpenCC): Open Chinese Convert command-line tool that converts between Simplified/Traditional Chinese variants.  9 TCLAP arg declarations decoded source-direct from `src/tools/CommandLineMain.cpp`:
      - 1 `TCLAP::CmdLine` constructor `'-'` separator decoded source-direct.
      - 3 `ValueArg<std::string>`: `-c`/`--config` (default s2t.json), `-o`/`--output`, `-i`/`--input`.
      - 1 `ValueArg<bool>`: `--noflush` (default false; takes true/false value).
      - 2 `SwitchArg`: `--in-place`, plus the XOR-paired `--segmentation` and `--inspect` (validated in source AFTER parse via `if (segmentationArg.getValue() && inspectArg.getValue()) throw` block, decoded source-direct).
      - 1 `MultiArg<std::string>`: `--path` (repeatable, decoded source-direct from `TCLAP::MultiArg<>` template parameter vs the non-repeatable `TCLAP::ValueArg<>`).
      - 1 `ValueArg<std::string>` for measured-results: `--measured_result` (note: underscore not dash, decoded source-direct).
    * 15-entry OpenCC config-file enum (with `_describe` annotations) decoded source-direct from `data/config/*.json` directory listing (excluding `opencc_config.schema.json`): hk2s.json, hk2t.json, jp2t.json, s2hk.json, s2t.json, s2tw.json, s2twp.json, t2hk.json, t2jp.json, t2s.json, t2tw.json, tw2s.json, tw2sp.json, tw2t.json.  Naming convention decoded from upstream: s=Simplified, t=Traditional, tw=Taiwan, hk=Hong Kong, jp=Japan-kanji, "p" suffix = with-phrase-lookup.  Trailing `_files -g "*.json"` fallback enables user-provided custom config files.
  - derb `--bom` is the unusual UOption with `short=0` (NUL byte, not the `'\1'` SOH placeholder used elsewhere) - this differentiates "completely long-only" (NUL=`0`) from "long-only via reserved short-char" (`'\1'`).  Both result in long-only at the parser level.
  - derb `--truncate` uses `UOPT_OPTIONAL_ARG` (not `UOPT_REQUIRES_ARG` or `UOPT_NO_ARG`) - the only one of all 14 ICU tools surveyed in R176+R177 with this argument-mode.  Decoded source-direct as `'-t-,--truncate=-` with `::n:` (double-colon = optional) in the completion.
  - Hunt-skip notes: `mecab-dict-index` (Mecab dictionary builder) deferred - has rich getopt + dictionary-format detection logic that needs separate extraction.  `chasen`/`kakasi` (Japanese morphological analyzer / romaji converter) deferred for a future round.  `pinyin` (Chinese-pinyin converter) deferred.  `hyphen` (libhyphen LibreOffice hyphenation engine) ships as library not standalone CLI.  `aspell-cli`/`hunspell-cli`/`enca-cli`/`enconv-cli`/`uchardet-cli`/`kakasi-cli` are not actual binary names (suffix-confused-with-base - bases aspell/hunspell/enca/enconv/uchardet/kakasi already covered or scheduled).  `pyt`/`dixmojo`/`mythes`/`thesaurus`/`libreoffice-aspell` are package names / fictional, no upstream extraction.  `aspell-stats`/`aspell-d` are upstream sub-tools of aspell shipped via `aspell` itself (already covered).  `ucgrep` is a Unicode-aware grep variant deferred.
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 1 entry (o*; derb was already blacklisted from before).
  - Corpus 28,583 → 28,584 files.

- **R176 — unicode-org ICU core data-builder + endian-swap + package-mgmt suite** (1 file / 7 binaries) — pivot to ICU's core `icu4c/source/tools/` data-builder + endian-swap + package-management binaries shipped alongside the already-covered ICU companions (uconv, genrb, pkgdata, genccode, gencnval, gendict, makeconv, icu-config, icuinfo, icuexportdata).  Sister tools to the heavily-covered Unicode/charset/locale family (recode, iconvconfig, fc-* fontconfig family) already in the corpus.
  - `_icupkg` (7-stem; unicode-org/icu):
    * icupkg: manages ICU resource-bundle `.dat` package files (build/swap/extract/add/remove members).  17 UOption rows decoded source-direct from `icu4c/source/tools/icupkg/icupkg.cpp:options[]` including the 3-entry `-t`/`--type` endianness/charset enum (`l`|`b`|`e` for little/big/EBCDIC), 4 long-only flags via `'\1'` placeholder short-char (`--ignore-deps`, `--auto_toc_prefix`, `--auto_toc_prefix_with_type`, `--toc_prefix`).
    * icuswap: swaps endianness/charset-family of an ICU data file in place or to a new file.  3 UOption rows + shared 3-entry `-t`/`--type` enum.
    * genbrk: compiles break-iterator rules source files (.txt) into binary RBBI rules files.  9 UOption rows including `-r`/`--rules` input + `-o`/`--out` output.
    * gencfu: compiles Unicode confusables data (UTS #39) into ICU SpoofChecker binary data file.  10 UOption rows including deprecated `-w`/`--wsrules` whole-script confusables file (flagged in source code as `// deprecated`).
    * gennorm2: compiles Unicode normalization input data into binary `.nrm` files used by Normalizer2.  10 UOption rows including 3 long-only flags (`--csource`, `--combined`, `--fast`) via `'\1'` placeholder.
    * gencmn: packages a list of individual ICU data files into a single `.dat` memory-mapped file.  11 UOption rows.
    * gensprep: compiles StringPrep profile data (RFC 3454-style) into binary `.spp` files used by usprep.  12 UOption rows including `-k`/`--check-bidi` BIDI-check flag.
  - UOPTION shared-macro extraction: all 7 tools share a common option vocabulary via the `icu4c/source/tools/toolutil/uoptions.h` macro library: `UOPTION_HELP_H` (`-h`), `UOPTION_HELP_QUESTION_MARK` (`-?` alias), `UOPTION_VERBOSE` (`-v`), `UOPTION_QUIET` (`-q`), `UOPTION_COPYRIGHT` (`-c`), `UOPTION_DESTDIR` (`-d`), `UOPTION_SOURCEDIR` (`-s`), `UOPTION_ENCODING` (`-e`), `UOPTION_ICUDATADIR` (`-i`), `UOPTION_PACKAGE_NAME` (`-p`), `UOPTION_BUNDLE_NAME` (`-b`).  Extracted source-direct from uoptions.h `#define UOPTION_X UOPTION_DEF("long-name", 'short-char', UOPT_NO_ARG|UOPT_REQUIRES_ARG)` block.  Helper array `$uopt_shared` factored to share the 3 most common entries (`-h`/`--help`, `-v`/`--verbose`, `-c`/`--copyright`) across all 7 case-arms.
  - Long-only flag detection via `'\1'` placeholder short-char: ICU's convention for long-only flags is to set the short-char to `'\1'` (ASCII SOH) which the `u_parseArgs` parser silently rejects on the command line.  Decoded source-direct from icupkg and gennorm2 sources.
  - 1 deprecated-flag retention: gencfu `-w`/`--wsrules` was deliberately retained in the completion even though the source code comments mark it deprecated (`// deprecated`) since the parser still accepts it.  Compsys completion is about parser-accepted flags, not recommended ones.
  - Hunt-skip notes: `derb` (ICU resource-bundle dumper) blacklisted-only with `more=0`/`multi=0` - has a separate UOption structure that needs its own extraction; deferred.  `genccode`/`gencnval`/`makeconv`/`pkgdata` already in corpus per multi=1.  `gentest`/`gencolusb`/`escapesrc`/`memcheck`/`ctestfw` are internal ICU test-harness tools, not user-facing.  `genren` is an internal symbol-rename script.  `tzcode` is an embedded copy of IANA tz code, has its own `zic`/`zdump` already covered.  `loctail`/`lao`/`langinfo` are not real binary names (suffix-confused-with-base or fictional).  `font-manager-cli`/`libreoffice-cli`/`iconv-cli` are not actual binary names.
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 7 entries (5 g*, 2 i*).
  - Corpus 28,582 → 28,583 files.

- **R175 — GNOME Gnumeric ssindex/ssgrep/ssdiff spreadsheet-driver companions** (1 file / 3 binaries) — pivot to Gnumeric's command-line companion tools shipped alongside the already-covered `ssconvert` binary in the corpus.  Sister tools to the heavily-covered CAS family (maxima/xmaxima/imaxima/wxmaxima/sagemath/sage/mathomatic/gp/ginsh/yacas/reduce/Singular all covered), plotting family (gnuplot/grace/xmgrace/graph/spline/ode/pic2plot/tek2plot/plotfont covered), and stats/notebooks family (octave/jupyter/jupyter-lab/jupyter-notebook/ipython/jupyter-book/jb covered).
  - `_ssindex` (3-stem; GNOME/gnumeric):
    * ssindex: extracts text+metadata from spreadsheet files for desktop-search indexers (Tracker, Recoll, Beagle).  4 GOptionEntry rows decoded source-direct from src/ssindex.c:ssindex_options[]: `-v`/`--version`, `-m`/`--list-mime-types`, `-i`/`--index`, `-E`/`--encoding`.
    * ssgrep: spreadsheet-aware grep over cell contents.  17 GOptionEntry rows decoded source-direct from src/ssgrep.c:ssgrep_options[].  Critical extraction note: `-h`/`--without-filename` uses `G_OPTION_FLAG_REVERSE` and binds to the same `&ssgrep_print_filenames` dest as `-H`/`--with-filename` — i.e. they form an explicit XOR pair, not just two flags.  Decoded source-direct from the `G_OPTION_FLAG_REVERSE` flag column being non-zero only for `-h`.  Listed under mutex group `(-H -h --with-filename --without-filename)` so compsys enforces the XOR.
    * ssdiff: cell-by-cell-diff between two spreadsheets with copy-highlighting + XML output.  4 GOptionEntry rows decoded source-direct from src/ssdiff.c:ssdiff_options[]: `-v`/`--version`, `-o`/`--output`, `-h`/`--highlight`, `-x`/`--xml`.
  - GLib GOptionEntry parsing technique: each row is `{ "long-name", 'short-char', flags, type, dest, help, arg-desc }` — `flags` column (3rd) decodes G_OPTION_FLAG_REVERSE for XOR pairs; `type` column (4th) decodes G_OPTION_ARG_NONE (no-arg flag) vs G_OPTION_ARG_STRING (takes value); `arg-desc` column (7th) decodes the placeholder shown in --help output (ENCODING, FILE, file, etc.).
  - 1 file-glob completion shared across all 3 binaries: `*.(gnumeric|xlsx|xls|ods|csv)` decoded source-direct from each binary's MIME-type detection logic in upstream (Gnumeric supports these 5 formats natively; XLSX/XLS via libxlsxwriter/libxlsx, ODS via libgsf, CSV via the built-in importer).
  - Hunt-skip notes: `sslint` is not a real Gnumeric binary (no upstream src/sslint.c).  `gp2c` (PARI/GP-to-C compiler) deferred - has rich autoconf+getopt setup that needs separate extraction.  `rmaxima` is a small wrapper script around `maxima -q` (covered) - thin-wrapper-of-covered-base precedent.  `gretlcli` (econometrics) deferred - large subcommand surface.  `stata`/`stata-mp`/`stata-se`/`xstata`/`xstat` are commercial Stata, not open-source - no upstream extraction possible without proprietary docs.  `mlab`/`mlab-cli`/`mathmagick`/`r-base` are package names not actual binaries.  `imagemagick` is a stock-zsh completion.  `julia` already has rich completion in stock zsh `_julia`.
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 3 entries (all s*).
  - Corpus 28,581 → 28,582 files.

- **R174 — kovidgoyal calibre CLI companions (plugin/debug/SMTP)** (1 file / 3 binaries) — pivot to calibre's command-line companion tools shipped alongside the already-covered calibre/calibredb/calibre-cli + ebook-meta/ebook-convert/ebook-edit/ebook-polish/ebook-viewer/fetch-ebook-metadata family after wide-net dup-check sweeps across poppler-utils (pdftohtml/pdftops/pdftotext/pdfimages/pdfinfo/pdftoppm/pdfdetach/pdffonts/pdfseparate/pdfsig/pdftocairo/pdfunite covered), Calibre core, mupdf (mutool/mupdf), Coherent PDF (cpdf), and AsciiDoctor/Prince/WeasyPrint family.
  - `_calibre-customize` (3-stem; kovidgoyal/calibre):
    * calibre-customize: plugin manager.  7 add_option calls decoded source-direct from `src/calibre/customize/ui.py:option_parser()`: `-a`/`--add-plugin` (path to ZIP), `-b`/`--build-plugin` (path to in-dev plugin folder; auto-zips), `-r`/`--remove-plugin`, `--customize-plugin` (name,customization-string CSV), `-l`/`--list-plugins`, `--enable-plugin`, `--disable-plugin`.
    * calibre-debug: calibre Python runtime + diff + kepubify + book-explode/implode + 25+ internal-state inspection helpers.  25 add_option calls decoded source-direct from `src/calibre/debug.py:option_parser()` including a 2-entry `--default-programs` choices enum (register|unregister) for Windows Default Programs (un)registration.  Trailing `*::positional / -- / passthrough args:_files` decoded source-direct from multiple `add_option` help-texts that document the "use -- after this option to pass option/arguments" pattern (consistent across -f/--subset-font, -g/--gui, -w/--viewer, -t/--edit-book).
