- **Subdomain enum + port-scan deception R158 — 2 files / 2 binaries** (`_sublist3r` 1-stem + `_portspoof` 1-stem). Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`. Pivot to offensive-security + defensive-deception tooling after wide-net dup-check sweeps across DNS/network tools (heavily covered: dig/drill/kdig/dog/dnsperf/dnsx/dnstwist/dnsrecon/dnsenum/massdns for DNS; nmap/zmap/masscan/rustscan for port-scan; ipcalc/ipv6calc/sipcalc/subnetcalc for IP). Surface extracted source-direct via `gh api`. **File `_sublist3r` (1-stem)** from [`aboul3la/Sublist3r/sublist3r.py`](https://github.com/aboul3la/Sublist3r/blob/master/sublist3r.py) argparse `parser.add_argument(...)` declarations. Passive subdomain enumeration tool for penetration testing / bug-bounty workflows; sister to the heavily-covered recon family (`amass`/`subfinder`/`assetfinder`/`httpx`/`katana`/`waybackurls`/`subjack` from R144) + the DNS family (`dnsx`/`dnstwist`/`dnsperf`/`dnsrecon`/`dnsenum`/`massdns`). **8 short+long flag pairs** decoded source-direct: `-d`/`--domain` (required; positional-style target), `-b`/`--bruteforce` (enable subbrute module), `-p`/`--ports PORT-LIST` (TCP port scan of found subdomains), `-v`/`--verbose` (realtime display), `-t`/`--threads N` (default 30), `-e`/`--engines` (comma-separated list with **9-entry autocomplete enum** of supported engines: `baidu`/`yahoo`/`google`/`bing`/`ask`/`netcraft`/`dnsdumpster`/`virustotal`/`threatcrowd`/`ssl`/`passivedns` decoded source-direct from upstream's documented engine list), `-o`/`--output FILE`, `-n`/`--no-color`. **File `_portspoof` (1-stem)** from [`drk1wi/portspoof/src/Configuration.cpp`](https://github.com/drk1wi/portspoof/blob/master/src/Configuration.cpp) `Configuration::usage` function body + the getopt argstring `"l:i:p:s:c:f:n:t:T:dvh12D"` decoded by reading the `case 'X':` arms in `Configuration::parse_arguments`. **Critical role-reversal catch**: portspoof is a **defensive deception tool** despite being adjacent to nmap/masscan/zmap in tool-cluster space — it returns valid service banners for ALL closed TCP ports so a port scanner sees 65535 open ports with random services instead of the real ones (used as a defensive measure on bastion hosts). Sister tool to the offensive-side family (`nmap`/`masscan`/`zmap`/`rustscan`/`subjack`/`subfinder`). **15 flags** decoded source-direct: `-i IP` bind IP, `-p PORT`, `-s SIGNATURES-FILE` (Portspoof service signature regex file), `-c CONFIG`, `-l LOG` (port-scanning alerts), 2 FUZZER_MODE flags `-f FUZZ-PAYLOAD-FILE` + `-n WRAPPING-SIGNATURES-FILE`, **2-way mutex `-1` xor `-2`** decoded from upstream Usage block: `-1` (FUZZER_MODE: generate fuzzing payloads internally) xor `-2` (switch to simple reply mode — does NOT work for Nmap detection per upstream's bang-warned help text), `-t MIN-HOLD-SEC` (default 0) + `-T MAX-HOLD-SEC` (default 120) per-port-connection hold-time bracket, `-D` daemonize, `-d` disable syslog, `-v` verbose, `-h` help. **Hunt-skip log this round**: `udppinger` (`facebookarchive/UdpPinger` upstream is ARCHIVED 2025-11-17; archived-with-no-CLI-flag-surface precedent), `dnstwisted`/`dns-recon`/`dnsdumpster`/`mass-dns`/`nslookup-ng`/`dnsmasq-server`/`dog-cli`/`udppinger`/`arping-cli` (suffix variants of already-covered base binaries OR need canonical URL discovery), `adns`/`adnsfind` (parent library — library-not-binary precedent for `adns` itself; `adnshost` and `adnsheloex` are already covered; `adnsfind` needs canonical URL discovery), the broader carryforward queue (`forgejo-runner`, `gitea/act_runner`, `cuelang`, `proton`, `trpc`, `spiceai`, `zng`, `astro-cli`, AleoNet, `metamath-knife`, `metamath`, `esmtp`, `lbdb`, `nmh`, `barrier`, `peid`, `pestudio`, `elf-cli`, `nccmp`, `speedtest-go`, bioinformatics tail, batctl subcommand-internal flags, warg, faustlive, dush/dirstat/puffin-cli). **Blacklist additions (2)**: 1 entry in `p*` (portspoof) + 1 in `s*` (sublist3r). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 163rd consecutive clean round.
- **Cuts + tinysearch + simdjson tools R157 — 3 files / 4 binaries** (`_cuts` 1-stem + `_tinysearch` 1-stem + `_json2json` 2-stem). Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`. Pivot to text/JSON utilities + static-search after wide-net dup-check sweeps across modern Rust replacements + observability tooling (heavily covered: choose/hck/csview/csvlens/sttr/ouch/ugrep/typos/ast-grep/kondo/lnav/htop/btm/bottom/procs/bmon/nload/vnstat/mtr/trippy/starship/hyperfine). Surface extracted source-direct via `gh api`. **File `_cuts` (1-stem)** from [`arielf/cuts`](https://github.com/arielf/cuts) Perl script `sub usage` heredoc + `Getopt::Std` `our $opt_* / use vars qw(...)` declarations. **Critical script-not-binary catch from upstream shebang**: `#!/usr/bin/perl -w` verbatim — the `cuts` binary is a **single Perl script** (NOT a compiled binary), preserved in completion header. "Unix `cut` (and `paste`) on steroids" per upstream README; sister to the standard `cut`/`paste` and the heavily-covered Rust replacements `choose`/`hck`/`csview`/`csvlens` in the corpus. **9 short flags** decoded source-direct: `-v` verbose, `-0` toggle to 1-based indexing (mnemonic from upstream comment: "-0 means 'disable 0-based'"), `-c` skip personal `~/.cuts.pl` config; **3-way alias mutex `-d`/`-t`/`-s`** for input column delimiter (Perl regex; all 3 target the same `$ICS` variable per upstream `our $ICS = qr/.../;`); **3-way alias mutex `-D`/`-T`/`-S`** for output column delimiter (literal string; all 3 target the same `$OCS` variable); variadic positional for column-specs and files supporting upstream's flexible `filename:colno | filename | colno | -` syntax. **File `_tinysearch` (1-stem)** from [`tinysearch/tinysearch/src/bin/tinysearch.rs`](https://github.com/tinysearch/tinysearch/blob/master/src/bin/tinysearch.rs) `#[derive(FromArgs, Clone)] struct Opt { ... }`. **Critical clap-vs-argh catch**: tinysearch uses Google's **argh derive macro** (NOT clap-derive — different `#[argh(option, short = 'X', long = "long", default = "...")]` attribute syntax than clap's `#[arg(short, long)]`). Tiny static-website search engine (the binary that builds the WASM index); sister to the heavily-covered static-site-generator family (hugo/eleventy/gridsome/zola/jekyll-build). **4-mode `-m`/`--mode` enum decoded source-direct from upstream's struct-level doc-comment** verbatim: "**search** - runs search engine on generated storage data, **storage** - generates storage data for posts, **crate** - creates a Rust crate with storage data, **wasm** - creates a crate and generates a loadable js/wasm script." 8 flags + 1 positional including `--release` production-ready output, `-S`/`--search-term` (only used in search mode), `-N`/`--num-searches` default 5, `-p`/`--path` default `./wasm_output`, `--crate-path` (wasm-mode only; tempdir if omitted), `-e`/`--engine-version` (TOML table definition for the generated Cargo.toml). **File `_json2json` (2-stem `#compdef json2json jsonstats`)** from [`simdjson/simdjson/tools`](https://github.com/simdjson/simdjson/tree/master/tools) `cxxopts::Options(...).add_options()(...)` chains. **Critical binary-discovery technique caught from upstream `tools/CMakeLists.txt`**: `foreach(tool IN ITEMS json2json jsonstats jsonpointer minify) add_executable("${tool}" "${tool}.cpp")` — the 4 simdjson example tools share an upstream binary-name registration foreach-loop; the 2 binaries `jsonpointer` + `minify` from this same loop are already covered in the corpus (verified via pre-write dup-check showing `multi=1` for both), so only `json2json` and `jsonstats` remain fresh. Sister tools to the heavily-covered JSON CLIs (jq/yq/jaq/gron/gomplate/dasel). **json2json** (re-emit a JSON file via round-trip parse+serialize using the simdjson parser; 4 flags including `-z`/`--ondemand` to use the On-Demand front-end and `-d`/`--rawdump` to dump the raw parser-tape contents). **jsonstats** (print stats: string/integer/object/array counts, byte counts, escape-character counts; 2 flags `-h`/`--help` + `-f`/`--file` plus positional alternative for file). Both binaries support positional file argument as an alternative to `-f`/`--file` per upstream `options.parse_positional({"file"});` declaration. **Hunt-skip log this round**: `tre`/`diskonaut` (initially flagged as fresh from audit but pre-write dup-check showed `multi=1`/`more=1` for both — already covered), `dush`/`dirstat`/`puffin-cli`/`uutils-coreutils`/`uutils` (need canonical URL discovery), `nu-cli`/`zellij-cli`/`watchexec-cli`/`glow-cli`/`mtr-cli`/`mtr-rs`/`hyperfine-cli`/`shellcheck-cli`/`shellfmt`/`shellfmt-cli`/`pomonostore`/`exa`/`lsd-cli`/`lscolors`/`shfmt`/`starship-cli`/`trippy-cli`/`procs-cli`/`bottom`/`scalene-cli`/`twine-cli` (suffix variants of already-covered base binaries OR need canonical URL discovery), `vmprof`/`pyrasite`/`pyrasite-shell` (R156 carryforward), `airbyte`/`kestra-cli`/`orchest` (R156 carryforward), the broader carryforward queue (`forgejo-runner`, `gitea/act_runner`, `cuelang`, `proton`, `trpc`, `spiceai`, `zng`, `astro-cli`, AleoNet, `metamath-knife`, `metamath`, `esmtp`, `lbdb`, `nmh`, `barrier`, `peid`, `pestudio`, `elf-cli`, `nccmp`, `speedtest-go`, bioinformatics tail, batctl subcommand-internal flags, warg, faustlive). **Blacklist additions (4)**: 1 entry in `c*` (cuts) + 2 in `j*` (json2json, jsonstats) + 1 in `t*` (tinysearch). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 162nd consecutive clean round.
- **PyPA auditwheel + macOS delocate sweep R156 — 2 files / 8 binaries** (`_auditwheel` 1-stem + `_delocate-addplat` 7-stem). Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`. Pivot to Python-wheel-distribution tooling after wide-net dup-check sweeps across data orchestration (airflow/prefect/dagster/dbt/temporal/mage/flyte/pachctl/meltano/windmill heavily covered) and Python packaging (hatch/tox/flit/twine/cibuildwheel/maturin/pyinstaller covered). Surface extracted source-direct via `gh api`. **File `_auditwheel` (1-stem)** from [`pypa/auditwheel/src/auditwheel/main.py`](https://github.com/pypa/auditwheel/blob/main/src/auditwheel/main.py) argparse + 3 sub_parsers registered via `main_show.configure_parser(sub_parsers)` / `main_repair.configure_parser(sub_parsers)` / `main_lddtree.configure_subparser(sub_parsers)` + each subcommand's `main_*.py` `add_argument(...)` chain. PyPA's tool for auditing and repairing Linux Python wheels for manylinux/musllinux compatibility; sister to the heavily-covered Python-packaging family. **3-subcommand state-dispatch tree**: `show` (display deps + required platform tag for a wheel; 1 flag `--disable-isa-ext-check` + 1 positional), `repair` (the heavy lifter; 11 flags including `--plat TAG`, `--only-plat`, `-L`/`--lib-sdir` for bundled-libs subdir default `.libs`, `-w`/`--wheel-dir` default `wheelhouse`, `--no-update-tags`, `--strip` symbols, `-z`/`--zip-compression-level` enum `0-9`, repeatable `*--exclude GLOB` for soname filtering; variadic positional wheels), `lddtree` (show lddtree of a `.so`; `--ld-paths` colon-separated extra paths + 1 positional). Root flag `*-v`/`--verbose` is **additive (stackable)** per upstream `action="count"` semantics. **File `_delocate-addplat` (7-stem `#compdef delocate-addplat delocate-fuse delocate-listdeps delocate-merge delocate-patch delocate-path delocate-wheel`)** from [`matthew-brett/delocate/pyproject.toml`](https://github.com/matthew-brett/delocate/blob/master/pyproject.toml) `[project.scripts]` (7 entries, each mapped to `delocate.cmd.delocate_<name>:main` entry point) + per-tool argparse declarations in `delocate/cmd/delocate_*.py`. The **macOS counterpart to auditwheel for Linux** — bundles dependent `.dylib` shared libraries into a Python wheel so the wheel becomes self-contained. Sister to auditwheel (also fresh this round) and the broader cibuildwheel/maturin Python-wheel-distribution family. **All 7 binaries decoded source-direct, each with its own per-tool flag surface**: **delocate-wheel** (the main delocator that rewrites a wheel to bundle external dylib dependencies + patches install names to use `@loader_path`; 5 flags including `--require-archs` enum (`x86_64`/`arm64`/`universal2`), `-k`/`--check-archs`, `--require-target-macos-version`). **delocate-listdeps** (list external dylib dependencies of a wheel; 2 short+long flag pairs `-a`/`--all` + `-d`/`--depending` to also print depending-library mappings). **delocate-addplat** (add a macOS platform tag to a wheel name; 7 flags including `--dual-arch-type` enum (`intel`/`universal2`/`universal-fat3`), repeatable `*--plat-tag`, `--rm-orig`, `--skip-errors`). **delocate-fuse** (fuse two wheels typically x86_64 + arm64 into a single universal2 wheel by combining dylibs; 2 positionals only, no flags). **delocate-merge** (merge dylib deps from a second wheel into a first wheel; 1 flag + 2 positionals). **delocate-patch** (apply unified diff to wheel contents in place; 1 flag + 2 positionals where 2nd positional autocompletes `*.patch`/`*.diff`). **delocate-path** (delocate a filesystem-directory tree of dylibs — NOT a wheel — so they reference `@loader_path`-relative paths; `-L`/`--lib-path` for bundled-dylibs subdir + variadic directory positional). **Hunt-skip log this round**: `pyrasite`/`pyrasite-shell` (Python process attach + injection — pyrasite project unmaintained since 2014; deferred), `fil-profiler` (Python memory profiler; the canonical upstream `pythonspeed/filprofiler` was rebranded `memray` which is already heavily covered; deferred per renamed-tool precedent), `vmprof` (Python sampling profiler; older PyPy-era tool with thin CLI; deferred), `austin-cli`/`scalene-cli`/`twine-cli`/`dbt-cli`/`dbt-core`/`tox-uv`/`tox-conda`/`pdm-build`/`pdm-backend`/`rye-build`/`maturin-py`/`maturin-import-hook`/`setuptools-rust`/`nuitka-run`/`cython-build`/`pyinstaller-bin` (suffix variants of already-covered base binaries OR need canonical URL discovery), `airbyte`/`kestra-cli`/`mage-cli`/`prefect-cli`/`airflow-cli`/`orchest`/`flyte-cli`/`estuary-cli`/`fivetran-cli`/`argo-workflows-cli`/`opendal-cli` (orchestration -cli suffix variants of already-covered base binaries OR upstream CLI not located via `gh api`), `delocate-merge`/`delocate-fuse` differ minimally from the surface enumerated above but the full positional semantics (which wheel goes first) is preserved verbatim per upstream argparse `help=` text, the broader carryforward queue (`forgejo-runner`, `gitea/act_runner`, `cuelang`, `proton`, `trpc`, `spiceai`, `zng`, `astro-cli`, AleoNet, `metamath-knife`, `metamath`, `esmtp`, `lbdb`, `nmh`, `barrier`, `peid`, `pestudio`, `elf-cli`, `nccmp`, `speedtest-go`, bioinformatics tail, batctl subcommand-internal flags, warg, faustlive). **Blacklist additions (8)**: 1 entry in `a*` (auditwheel) + 7 in `d*` (delocate-addplat, delocate-fuse, delocate-listdeps, delocate-merge, delocate-patch, delocate-path, delocate-wheel). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 161st consecutive clean round.
- **HDL-checker + MAVLink-REST sweep R155 — 2 files / 2 binaries** (`_hdl_checker` 1-stem + `_mavlink2rest` 1-stem). Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`. Pivot to HDL-tools + drone/MAVLink tooling after wide-net dup-check sweeps across FPGA/EDA (yosys/nextpnr/verilator/iverilog/vvp/gtkwave/ghdl/cocotb all heavily covered), Scala (scala-cli/scalafmt/scala3 covered), and robotics/ROS (ros/ros2/rqt covered). Surface extracted source-direct via `gh api`. **File `_hdl_checker` (1-stem)** from [`suoto/hdl_checker`](https://github.com/suoto/hdl_checker) setup.py `entry_points.console_scripts` (`hdl_checker = hdl_checker.server:main`) + [`hdl_checker/server.py`](https://github.com/suoto/hdl_checker/blob/master/hdl_checker/server.py) argparse declarations. **Critical PyPI-vs-binary name discrimination caught**: the installed binary uses **underscore** (`hdl_checker`) NOT hyphen (`hdl-checker`) per upstream entry-point name — PyPI package name vs. binary name disambiguation (PyPI hyphenates `hdl-checker` but the entry-point declares `hdl_checker` so the installed binary keeps the underscore). HDL (VHDL/Verilog/SystemVerilog) code-checker daemon that wraps multiple synthesis tools (GHDL, Riviera-Pro, ModelSim, Vivado xvhdl, msim) behind a common HTTP + LSP interface; sister to the heavily-covered yosys/nextpnr/verilator/iverilog/vvp/gtkwave/ghdl HDL family. **9 flags** decoded source-direct: `--host`/`--port` (HTTP server bind), `--lsp` (run as LSP over stdio instead of HTTP), `--attach-to-pid PID` (attach + exit-on-parent-exit pattern; `_pids` autocomplete), `--log-level` enum (`DEBUG`/`INFO`/`WARNING`/`ERROR`/`CRITICAL`), `--log-stream`/`--stdout`/`--stderr` (file-redirection trio), `-V`/`--version`. **File `_mavlink2rest` (1-stem)** from [`mavlink/mavlink2rest`](https://github.com/mavlink/mavlink2rest) [`src/cli.rs`](https://github.com/mavlink/mavlink2rest/blob/master/src/cli.rs) `clap::App::new(env!("CARGO_PKG_NAME"))` + `.arg(clap::Arg::with_name("X").short("Y").long("LONG")...)` chain. **clap-library-version note caught**: this binary uses **clap-builder v2 API** (`clap::App::new(...)` + `clap::Arg::with_name(...)`) NOT clap-derive — different from the typical modern clap-derive idiom seen in most other recent Rust CLIs in the corpus. REST API + WebSocket bridge to a MAVLink autopilot (PX4 / ArduPilot etc.); sister tool to the broader drone/MAVLink ecosystem. **8 flags** decoded source-direct: `-c`/`--connect` (MAVLink connection string with default `udpin:0.0.0.0:14550` — note the colon-separated `TYPE:IP/SERIAL:PORT/BAUD` format escaped in completion as `TYPE\:IP/SERIAL\:PORT/BAUD` to survive zsh's `:` parsing), `-s`/`--server` (REST server `IP:PORT` default `0.0.0.0:8088`), `--mavlink` enum (`1`/`2` MAVLink protocol version; default `2`), `--system-id` (MAVLink system ID 1-255; default 255), `--component-id` (default 0), `--default-api-version` enum (`1` — single-entry but kept as enum for future-proofing per upstream `.possible_values(&["1"])` declaration), `-v`/`--verbose`, `--send-initial-heartbeats` (burst initial heartbeats spaced 0.1s apart to wake up PX4-like autopilots per upstream help text). **Hunt-skip log this round**: `hdl-checker` (with hyphen — same binary as `hdl_checker` but the underscore form is the actual binary per upstream entry-point), `xrun` (Cadence Xcelium proprietary; not on GitHub), `quartus`/`quartus-cli`/`vivado`/`vivado-cli`/`vivado-tcl`/`xilinx-cli`/`xilinx-vivado`/`lattice-radiant` (proprietary EDA tools; deferred — generally distributed as offline installer tarballs, no canonical GitHub source for flag extraction), `yosys-show` (a Python wrapper script in the Yosys upstream that calls `yosys -p "show ..."` — minimal CLI surface; deferred), `dotty`/`dotty-cli`/`scala3-cli`/`scalafmt-cli`/`scalac`/`scalac-cli`/`scaladoc`/`scaladoc-cli` (suffix variants of already-covered Scala binaries OR need canonical URL discovery), `rosrun`/`roslaunch`/`rostopic`/`rosnode`/`rosbag`/`rosservice` (ROS classic commands — complex Python tools that deserve a dedicated round each; deferred), `gz`/`ign-gazebo`/`isaac-sim`/`isaac-cli`/`isaaclab`/`webots-cli`/`bullet-tools`/`urdf-tools`/`urdf-cli`/`urdf2model`/`ros2-cli` (robotics tools — need canonical URL discovery), `dronekit`/`dronesim`/`mavlink-router`/`px4-cli`/`ardupilot`/`mavutil`/`mavp2p`/`vehicleinfod`/`sphinx-mavlink` (additional drone/MAVLink ecosystem — needs per-tool drill; deferred), the broader carryforward queue (`forgejo-runner`, `gitea/act_runner`, `cuelang`, `proton`, `trpc`, `spiceai`, `zng`, `astro-cli`, AleoNet, `metamath-knife`, `metamath`, `esmtp`, `lbdb`, `nmh`, `barrier`, `peid`, `pestudio`, `elf-cli`, `nccmp`, `speedtest-go`, bioinformatics tail, batctl subcommand-internal flags, warg, faustlive). **Blacklist additions (2)**: 1 entry in `h*` (hdl_checker) + 1 in `m*` (mavlink2rest). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 160th consecutive clean round.
- **Ham-radio tail R154 — 2 files / 3 binaries** (`_rigctld` 1-stem + `_chirpc` 2-stem). Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`. Pivot to amateur-radio tooling after wide-net dup-check sweeps across the ham-radio + emulator ecosystems (heavily covered: wsjtx/fldigi/flrig/rigctl/direwolf/gpredict for ham; mame/mednafen/scummvm/retroarch/dolphin-emu/dolphin-tool/ppsspp/pcsx2/nestopia/rpcs3/yuzu/ryujinx for emulators). Surface extracted source-direct via `gh api`. **File `_rigctld` (1-stem)** from [`Hamlib/Hamlib/tests/rigctld.c`](https://github.com/Hamlib/Hamlib/blob/master/tests/rigctld.c) `static struct option long_options[]` table + `SHORT_OPTIONS` argstring `"m:r:p:d:P:D:s:S:c:T:t:C:W:w:x:lLuovhVZRA:b"`. **Upstream maintenance contract preserved verbatim in completion header**: "keep up to date SHORT_OPTIONS, usage()'s output and man page. thanks." (a self-imposed source-comment by Hamlib maintainers that signals these 3 surfaces must stay aligned). TCP network daemon for controlling ham radios via the Hamlib backend library; sister daemon to the already-covered `rigctl` (local-only client equivalent). **26 long options** decoded source-direct from the `long_options[]` table aligned with the `SHORT_OPTIONS` argstring's `:` arg-required markers, with rich enum autocomplete: `-P`/`--ptt-type` enum (`RIG`/`DTR`/`RTS`/`PARALLEL`/`CM108`/`GPIO`/`GPION`/`NONE`); `-D`/`--dcd-type` enum (`RIG`/`DSR`/`CTS`/`CD`/`PARALLEL`/`CM108`/`GPIO`/`GPION`/`NONE`); `-s`/`--serial-speed` baud enum (`1200`/`2400`/`4800`/`9600`/`19200`/`38400`/`57600`/`115200`); `-x`/`--uplink` satellite-mode enum (`0`/`1`/`2`); plus the standard server-side flags `-T LISTEN_ADDR`/`-t PORT` (default 4532), `-A PASSWORD` connecting-client auth, `-b`/`--bind-all` (override default localhost-only bind), `-R`/`--rigctld-idle` (allow startup without a rig — for testing/CI). **File `_chirpc` (2-stem `#compdef chirpc experttune`)** from [`kk7ds/chirp/setup.py`](https://github.com/kk7ds/chirp/blob/main/setup.py) `entry_points.console_scripts` (3 entries: `chirp=chirp.wxui:chirpmain`, `chirpc=chirp.cli.main:main`, `experttune=chirp.cli.experttune:main`) + per-CLI argparse declarations. **Critical entry-point-vs-GUI catch**: `chirp` itself is a **wxPython GUI launcher** (per `chirp.wxui:chirpmain` entry-point name with `wxui` module suffix) — intentionally omitted from the `#compdef` line since it has minimal CLI surface (just launches the GUI). The 2 CLI binaries not previously covered. CHIRP is the standard open-source ham-radio programming toolkit; sister to the heavily-covered ham radio family. **chirpc** (CLI for programming memory channels into a connected ham radio via serial or MMAP file image; 30+ flags including 4 download/upload MMAP variants `--mmap FILE`/`--download-mmap FILE`/`--upload-mmap FILE`, 6 list operations `--list-radios`/`--list-settings`/`--list-mem`/`--list-special-mem`/`--raw`/`--get-mem`, and **18 memory-set flags** including `--set-mem-mode` enum (`FM`/`AM`/`NFM`/`WFM`/`USB`/`LSB`/`CW`/`DV`/`DN` — common ham modulation modes), `--set-mem-dup` duplex direction (`+`/`-`/`off`/`split`), `--set-mem-tenc` transmit tone encoder type (`Tone`/`DTCS`/`Cross`), `--set-mem-dtcspol` DTCS polarity (`NN`/`NR`/`RN`/`RR`); variadic positional for memory location numbers). **experttune** (auto-tune an SPE Expert linear amplifier via CAT control; 4 flags + 2 positionals: `--bands` comma-list of ham bands like `40,20,15`, `--call CALLSIGN` for CW ID after each tune step, `--next` strategy enum (`interactive`/`auto`), `--debug`; positionals are radio model + CAT serial port; the **band enum** values `160`/`80`/`40`/`30`/`20`/`17`/`15`/`12`/`10`/`6` US ham bands decoded source-direct from upstream `BANDS = {...}` dictionary at the top of experttune.py with band-edge frequency limits embedded in upstream comment "only the frequencies from the chart that fit into the US band edges"). **Hunt-skip log this round**: `chirp` (the bare command — wxPython GUI launcher per upstream `chirp.wxui:chirpmain` entry-point; GUI-app-with-no-CLI-surface precedent), `duckstation`/`mesen`/`xemu`/`cemu`/`pj64`/`project64` (game emulators — mostly Qt-based GUI launchers with minimal CLI surface; deferred since CLI flag-set is typically just `--rom`/`--no-gui`/`-fs`, not worth a dedicated round each), `hamlib` itself (the C library — library-not-binary precedent), `chirp-cli` (suffix variant; the actual CLI binary is `chirpc` per upstream pyproject.toml entry-point), `wsjt`/`adsbexchange`/`adsb-feed`/`mednafen-cli`/`scummvm-cli`/`retroarch-cli` (suffix variants of already-covered base binaries OR need canonical URL discovery), the broader carryforward queue (`forgejo-runner`, `gitea/act_runner`, `cuelang`, `proton`, `trpc`, `spiceai`, `zng`, `astro-cli`, AleoNet, `metamath-knife`, `metamath`, `esmtp`, `lbdb`, `nmh`, `barrier`, `peid`, `pestudio`, `elf-cli`, `nccmp`, `speedtest-go`, bioinformatics tail, batctl subcommand-internal flags, warg, faustlive). **Blacklist additions (3)**: 1 entry in `c*` (chirpc) + 1 in `e*` (experttune) + 1 in `r*` (rigctld). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 159th consecutive clean round.
- **PyTorch LLM tooling + InternLM LMDeploy R153 — 3 files / 3 binaries** (`_tune` 1-stem + `_torchx` 1-stem + `_lmdeploy` 1-stem). Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`. Pivot to LLM training/deployment tooling after wide-net dup-check sweeps across the LLM/AI runtime ecosystem (heavily covered: ollama/cog/bentoml/torchserve/torchrun/openllm/litellm/sglang/mistralrs/instructor/llama-stack/agno/vllm/wandb/mlflow). Surface extracted source-direct via `gh api`. **File `_tune` (1-stem)** from [`pytorch/torchtune/torchtune/_cli/tune.py`](https://github.com/pytorch/torchtune/blob/main/torchtune/_cli/tune.py) argparse `TuneCLIParser` + 6 subcommand classes registered via `<Class>.create(subparsers)` (Cat, Copy, Download, List, Run, Validate). **Critical binary-name discovery caught from upstream `pyproject.toml`**: `[project.scripts] tune = "torchtune._cli.tune:main"` — the installed binary is **`tune`** (NOT `torchtune` — that's the Python module name; deferred per package-name-vs-binary-name precedent). Meta's official PyTorch LLM fine-tuning library CLI. **6 subcommand state-dispatch tree** decoded source-direct: `download` (HF Hub model downloader with `--source huggingface|kaggle` + `--hf-token` + `--output-dir` + space-separated `--ignore-patterns`), `list` (built-in recipes/configs), `cp` (copy built-in to local; `-n`/`--no-clobber` + `--make-parents`), `run` (run recipe; variadic recipe-args forwarded), `validate` (config file pre-flight check; single positional `.yaml`/`.yml`), `cat` (print recipe/config; `--show-defaults`). **File `_torchx` (1-stem)** from [`pytorch/torchx/torchx/cli/main.py`](https://github.com/pytorch/torchx/blob/main/torchx/cli/main.py) argparse subcommand registry (top-level imports 10 `Cmd<Name>()` classes from `torchx/cli/cmd_*.py`, each implementing `add_arguments(self, subparser)` for its per-subcommand flag set). Per upstream pyproject.toml `[project.scripts] torchx = "torchx.cli.main:main"`. PyTorch job launcher: unified CLI for running PyTorch jobs across local processes, Kubernetes, Slurm, AWS Batch, Ray, and other schedulers; sister tool to torchrun/torchserve already in the corpus + `tune` (also fresh this round). **11-entry subcommand enum** decoded from upstream's `cmd_*.py` imports (`run`/`describe`/`list`/`status`/`log`/`cancel`/`delete`/`configure`/`runopts`/`tracker`/`builtins`). **7-entry scheduler enum** decoded from upstream scheduler-registry common usage (`local_cwd`/`local_docker`/`kubernetes`/`slurm`/`aws_batch`/`ray`/`gcp_batch`). Per-subcommand state dispatch: `run` (rich flag set including `-s SCHEDULER`/`-cfg KEY=VAL,...`/`--dryrun`/`--wait`/`--log`/`--tee_logs`/`--workspace DIR` + variadic component spec), `describe`/`status`/`log`/`cancel`/`delete` (all take a single `<scheduler>://session/app-id` job URI positional), `list` (`-s SCHEDULER`), `configure`/`runopts` (single scheduler positional). **File `_lmdeploy` (1-stem)** from [`InternLM/lmdeploy/lmdeploy/cli/entrypoint.py`](https://github.com/InternLM/lmdeploy/blob/main/lmdeploy/cli/entrypoint.py) (per upstream `setup.py` `entry_points={'console_scripts': ['lmdeploy = lmdeploy.cli:run']}`) + **3 sub-CLI classes** decoded from `def run()`: `CLI` ([`lmdeploy/cli/cli.py`](https://github.com/InternLM/lmdeploy/blob/main/lmdeploy/cli/cli.py) — top-level `chat` / `check_env`), `SubCliServe` ([`lmdeploy/cli/serve.py`](https://github.com/InternLM/lmdeploy/blob/main/lmdeploy/cli/serve.py) — `serve api_server` / `serve proxy`), `SubCliLite` ([`lmdeploy/cli/lite.py`](https://github.com/InternLM/lmdeploy/blob/main/lmdeploy/cli/lite.py) — `lite auto_awq` / `lite auto_gptq` / `lite calibrate` / `lite smooth_quant`). OpenMMLab's LMDeploy: unified API for converting, compressing, and deploying large language models; sister to vllm/llama-server/llama-cli/llamafile/wandb/mlflow already heavily covered. **4 top-level + 2-level nested subcommand tree** decoded source-direct. The `chat` subcommand carries a rich 12-flag set including `--backend` enum (`turbomind`/`pytorch`), `--model-format` enum (`hf`/`llama`/`awq`/`gptq`), `--quant-policy` enum (`0`/`4`/`8` — KV cache quantization mode), `--dtype` enum (`auto`/`float16`/`bfloat16`/`float32`), `--tp` tensor parallel size, `--enable-prefix-caching`, `--trust-remote-code`. Used `_arguments -C` + state-handler dispatch with 1 top-level state + 1 nested state for `serve`/`lite` sub-subcommands. **Hunt-skip log this round**: `torchtune` (the bare module name; the actual installed binary is `tune` per upstream pyproject.toml `[project.scripts]` mapping — package-name-vs-binary-name precedent), `torchtitan` (no explicit CLI entry point; primarily a Python library import rather than a console-script), `torchx`-subcommand-detail (each `cmd_*.py` carries 5-15 per-subcommand flags that would need separate per-cmd drilling for full per-flag coverage; the current completion surfaces only the most-common `run` flags), `lmdeploy serve api_server`/`lmdeploy serve proxy`/`lmdeploy lite *` per-subcommand flags (each has its own `ArgumentHelper.*(parser)` helper-chain in upstream — deferred for completeness round), `mlserver`/`kserve`/`seldon-server`/`torchtitan`/`vllm-server`/`vllm-cli`/`openllm-cli`/`openllm-serve`/`tensorflowjs-cli`/`onnxruntime-server`/`onnxruntime-quantize`/`triton-cli`/`tensorflow-cli`/`litdata`/`lmstudio`/`llamaindex`/`llama-stack-server`/`llama_stack` (server-side OR underscore variants OR need canonical URL discovery — deferred), the broader carryforward queue (`forgejo-runner`, `gitea/act_runner`, `cuelang`, `proton`, `trpc`, `spiceai`, `zng`, `astro-cli`, AleoNet, `metamath-knife`, `metamath`, `esmtp`, `lbdb`, `nmh`, `barrier`, `peid`, `pestudio`, `elf-cli`, `nccmp`, `speedtest-go`, bioinformatics tail, batctl subcommand-internal flags, warg, faustlive). **Blacklist additions (3)**: 1 entry in `l*` (lmdeploy) + 2 in `t*` (torchx, tune). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 158th consecutive clean round.
- **OTel-viewer + tetragon/tracee bench/operator R152 — 3 files / 4 binaries** (`_otel-desktop-viewer` 1-stem + `_tetragon-bench` 1-stem + `_tracee-bench` 2-stem). Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`. Pivot to observability auxiliary/bench tooling after wide-net dup-check sweeps across the heavily-covered observability core (otel-cli/otelcol/otelcol-contrib/jaeger/zipkin/beyla/falco/falco-driver-loader/falcoctl/tetragon/tetra/tracee/traceectl). Surface extracted source-direct via `gh api`. **File `_otel-desktop-viewer` (1-stem)** from [`CtrlSpice/otel-desktop-viewer/main.go`](https://github.com/CtrlSpice/otel-desktop-viewer/blob/main/main.go) cobra `rootCmd.Flags().<TypeVar>(...)` declarations. The binary is built per Makefile target `go build -o otel-desktop-viewer` at the repo root (NOT under a `cmd/` subdirectory). **Upstream-source self-deprecating comment preserved verbatim in completion header**: "I have edited the code generated by `go.opentelemetry.io/collector/cmd/builder`. Pray I don't edit it any further. Program otel-desktop-viewer is an OpenTelemetry Collector binary." Local-development OTel Collector distribution that ships a DuckDB-backed exporter + web UI for visualizing OpenTelemetry traces/metrics/logs locally. **6 flags** decoded source-direct: `--http` (OTLP/HTTP listen port default 4318), `--grpc` (OTLP/gRPC listen port default 4317), `--browser-port` (UI port default 8000), `--host` (default `localhost`; use `::` or `0.0.0.0` for all interfaces per upstream help text), `--db FILE` (DuckDB file path; omit for in-memory mode per upstream `Omitting this flag opens DuckDB in in-memory mode, with no data persisted to disk`), `--open-browser=true|false` (default true). **File `_tetragon-bench` (1-stem)** from [`cilium/tetragon/cmd/tetragon-bench/bench.go`](https://github.com/cilium/tetragon/blob/main/cmd/tetragon-bench/bench.go) Go stdlib `flag.Int/.Bool/.String(...)` declarations in `func init()`. **Build-tag caught from source-file header**: `//go:build !windows` — this binary is Linux/macOS only, preserved in completion header comment. Performance/throughput benchmark harness for the Tetragon eBPF security observability runtime; sister tool to the already-covered `tetragon` daemon and `tetra` CLI from cilium/tetragon. **10 flags** decoded source-direct: `-rb-size` (perf ring buffer size default 65535), `-debug`, `-json-encode` (also measure encoding overhead), `-baseline` (run without tetragon), `-print`/`-store` (different output paths for events), `-trace BENCH` (with autocomplete enum derived from upstream `bench.TraceBenchSupported()` output: `none`/`tcp`/`connect`/`listen`/`socket-accept`/`syscall`/`write`/`open`), `-crd FILE`, `-csv FILE`, `-name LABEL`. **File `_tracee-bench` (2-stem `#compdef tracee-bench tracee-operator`)** from [`aquasecurity/tracee/cmd/tracee-bench/main.go`](https://github.com/aquasecurity/tracee/blob/main/cmd/tracee-bench/main.go) urfave/cli `app := cli.App{Name: "tracee-bench", Usage: ..., Flags: []cli.Flag{...}}` + [`cmd/tracee-operator/main.go`](https://github.com/aquasecurity/tracee/blob/main/cmd/tracee-operator/main.go) Go stdlib `flag.StringVar/BoolVar(...)` declarations in `parseConfig()`. The 2 tracee auxiliary binaries not previously covered (`tracee` + `traceectl` from the same upstream `cmd/` tree are already in the corpus). **Critical version-evolution catch**: the legacy `tracee-rules` and `tracee-ebpf` binaries from older tracee versions have been **consolidated into the single `tracee` binary in current upstream** — confirmed by 404 on `cmd/tracee-rules/` and `cmd/tracee-ebpf/` lookups; deferred per consolidated-no-longer-exists precedent. **tracee-bench** (Prometheus-based performance probe — uses urfave/cli v2 NOT cobra; 3 flags): `--prometheus URL` (default `http://localhost:9090`), `--period SEC`, `--output` enum (`pretty`/`json`). **tracee-operator** (Kubernetes operator for the Tracee DaemonSet using `sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime`; manages the `tracee.aquasec.com/v1beta1` Policy CRD; uses Go stdlib flag + zap logging options; 10 flags including `--metrics-bind-address` default `:8080`, `--health-probe-bind-address` default `:8081`, `--tracee-namespace`/`--tracee-name`/`--configmap-name`, `--leader-elect`, plus 4 zap-logger flags `--zap-devel`/`--zap-encoder` enum (`json`/`console`)/`--zap-log-level` enum (`debug`/`info`/`error`)/`--zap-stacktrace-level` enum (`info`/`error`/`panic`) — added by upstream `cfg.LoggingOpts.BindFlags(flag.CommandLine)` controller-runtime convention). **Hunt-skip log this round**: `tracee-rules`/`tracee-ebpf` (consolidated into `tracee` per upstream cmd/ tree inspection; consolidated-no-longer-exists precedent), `tetragon-tester`/`tetragon-vmtests-run`/`dump-syscalls-info`/`ksyms`/`tetragon-metrics-docs` (additional cilium/tetragon `cmd/` binaries — most are internal/testing utilities or doc generators; deferred), `tracee-operator` zap-logger flags from `zap.Options.BindFlags()` — the upstream controller-runtime convention auto-registers a larger set of `--zap-*` flags than the 4 most-common ones surfaced here (deferred for completeness round), `otel`/`otelcontribcol`/`opentelemetry-collector`/`otel-collector-contrib`/`collector-cli`/`hubble-relay`/`pixie`/`px` (suffix variants of already-covered binaries OR need canonical URL discovery), the broader carryforward queue (`forgejo-runner`, `gitea/act_runner`, `cuelang`, `proton`, `trpc`, `spiceai`, `zng`, `astro-cli`, AleoNet, `metamath-knife`, `metamath`, `esmtp`, `lbdb`, `nmh`, `barrier`, `peid`, `pestudio`, `elf-cli`, `nccmp`, `speedtest-go`, bioinformatics tail, batctl subcommand-internal flags, warg, faustlive). **Blacklist additions (4)**: 1 entry in `o*` (otel-desktop-viewer) + 1 in `te*` (tetragon-bench) + 2 in `tr*` (tracee-bench, tracee-operator). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 157th consecutive clean round.
- **Grame Faust audio-wrapper tail R151 — 1 file / 4 binaries** (`_faust2alqt` 4-stem `#compdef faust2alqt faust2alsa faust2caqt faust2jaqt`). Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`. Pivot to audio-synthesis tooling after wide-net dup-check sweeps across DSP/synthesis (heavily covered: csound/sclang/scide/sonic-pi/pdsend/pdreceive/faust/supercollider/wavpack/lame/oggenc/oggdec/vorbiscomment/eyeD3/fdkaac/opusenc/aubio* family). Surface extracted source-direct from upstream [`grame-cncm/faust/tools/faust2appls/`](https://github.com/grame-cncm/faust/tree/master-dev/tools/faust2appls) bash scripts + shared `usage.sh` option-documentation helper. **Critical naming-vs-implementation discovery caught**: all 4 `faust2*` tools are **bash scripts** (NOT compiled binaries) that wrap the canonical `faust` Faust DSP compiler with audio-backend + GUI-toolkit combinations decoded source-direct from the per-script `ARCHFILE=$FAUSTARCH/<backend>-<gui>.cpp` assignment: **`faust2alsa = ALSA + GTK` (Linux)**, **`faust2alqt = ALSA + Qt` (Linux)**, **`faust2caqt = CoreAudio + Qt` (macOS)**, **`faust2jaqt = JACK + Qt` (cross-platform)**. Reusable helper `_faust2_shared` (8 options inherited from `usage.sh`'s built-in `option` doc helper) covers all 4 binaries' common surface: `-help`/`-h`, `-httpd` (HTTP control), `-osc` (OSC control), `-midi` (MIDI control), `-qrcode` (QR-code generation), `-soundfile` (bundle resources for DSP using the `soundfile` primitive), `-nvoices NUM` (polyphonic-DSP voice count), `-effect FILE|auto` (effect DSP file or `auto`). **Per-script extensions decoded source-direct from each script's `elif [ $p = "-X" ]` argv-walking chain**: **faust2alsa** + **faust2alqt** (shared options only — no per-script extensions). **faust2caqt** (4 macOS-specific extensions: `-opt LEVEL` LLVM optimization level enum `0`/`1`/`2`/`3`/`s`/`z`, `-nodeploy` skip self-contained app generation via `macdeployqt`, repeatable `*-I DIR` Faust include path, `-resample MODE` sample-rate conversion mode enum `0`/`1`/`2`/`3`). **faust2jaqt** (2 cross-platform-specific extensions: `-deploy` FORCE self-contained app generation — opposite of caqt's `-nodeploy` since jaqt's default is no-deploy; `-resample MODE` same sample-rate enum). **Hunt-skip log this round**: `faustlive` (GUI app primarily — investigated upstream `grame-cncm/faustlive/src/MainStructure/` directory structure but main.cpp not present at the expected path; GUI-app-with-no-CLI-surface precedent; deferred), `nyquist`/`nyquist-cli` (Lisp-based audio DSL with REPL surface rather than argv flags; deferred for separate audit), `csoundpost`/`csb`/`csound-cli` (suffix variants of already-covered `csound`; need canonical URL discovery), `sox-bsd`/`sox-cli`/`sox-info` (suffix variants of already-covered `sox`; need canonical URL discovery), `wavpackexe`/`mp3check`/`neroaacenc` (need canonical upstream URL discovery), `faust2alsaconsole`/`faust2caqtios`/`faust2jaqtchain` (additional faust2* variants in upstream `tools/faust2appls/` — could be added in a deeper round but each needs careful per-script flag audit to avoid over-claiming shared options that don't actually apply; deferred), the broader carryforward queue (`forgejo-runner`, `gitea/act_runner`, `cuelang`, `proton`, `trpc`, `spiceai`, `zng`, `astro-cli`, AleoNet, `metamath-knife`, `metamath`, `esmtp`, `lbdb`, `nmh`, `barrier`, `peid`, `pestudio`, `elf-cli`, `nccmp`, `speedtest-go`, bioinformatics tail, batctl subcommand-internal flags, warg). **Blacklist additions (4)**: 4 entries in `f*` (faust2alqt, faust2alsa, faust2caqt, faust2jaqt). - **Kubernetes-debug + Wasm-AOT sweep R150 — 3 files / 3 binaries** **(150-round milestone)** (`_kubedog` 1-stem + `_ksniff` 1-stem + `_wasmedgec` 1-stem). Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`. Pivot to Kubernetes-debug + Wasm-AOT tooling after wide-net dup-check sweeps across modern infrastructure (`cilium`/`hubble`/`stern`/`kail`/`promtool`/`grafana-cli`/`tempo`/`tempo-cli`/`mimirtool`/`wac`/`wkg`/`iox`/`quickwit`/`wasmedge` heavily covered). Surface extracted source-direct via `gh api`. **File `_kubedog` (1-stem)** from [`werf/kubedog/cmd/kubedog/main.go`](https://github.com/werf/kubedog/blob/main/cmd/kubedog/main.go) cobra `rootCmd` + `rootCmd.PersistentFlags()` declarations + nested `followCmd.AddCommand(...)` / `trackCmd.AddCommand(...)` calls under `rolloutCmd.AddCommand(trackCmd)`. Werf's Kubernetes resource-readiness tracker — watches K8s resources and emits log streams + status indicators until rollout/readiness is reached; used internally by Werf and as a standalone CLI. **7 persistent root flags** decoded source-direct: `-n`/`--namespace` (default `default`), `-t`/`--timeout` (default `-1`; 0 means wait forever), `--logs-since` (Go-duration or special tokens `all`/`now`), `--kube-context` (env `KUBEDOG_KUBE_CONTEXT`), `--kube-config` (env `KUBEDOG_KUBE_CONFIG`/`KUBECONFIG`), `--kube-config-base64` (env `KUBEDOG_KUBE_CONFIG_BASE64`), `--output-prefix`. **3-level cobra subcommand tree** decoded source-direct: top-level `multitrack` / `follow` / `rollout` / `version`; `follow` takes a resource-kind enum (`job`/`deployment`/`statefulset`/`daemonset`/`pod`); `rollout` has nested `track` subcommand which takes the same 5-resource-kind enum. Uses `_arguments -C` + nested state-handler dispatch with 3 distinct states (`subcmd` → `rollout-sub` → `trackkind`). Reusable `_kubedog_persistent` helper for the 7 persistent flags + `_kubedog_kinds` helper for the 5 resource kinds shared by `follow` and `rollout track`. **File `_ksniff` (1-stem)** from [`eldadru/ksniff/pkg/cmd/sniff.go`](https://github.com/eldadru/ksniff/blob/master/pkg/cmd/sniff.go) `cmd.Flags().<TypeVarP>(...)` declarations (cobra+pflag via k8s.io/cli-runtime). **Crucial anti-dup catch in flight**: ksniff ships as both `ksniff` (legacy direct invocation) AND `kubectl-sniff` (kubectl-plugin convention; invoked as `kubectl sniff`) per upstream `cmd/kubectl-sniff.go` entrypoint name. The pre-write dup-check showed `kubectl-sniff` is **already covered** elsewhere in the corpus with `multi=2`, so I removed `kubectl-sniff` from the `#compdef` line before commit and updated the header comment to document the rationale per anti-dup policy. **13 flags + 1 positional** decoded source-direct: `-n`/`--namespace`, `-i`/`--interface` (default `any`; enum autocomplete `any`/`eth0`/`lo`/`cni0`/`weave`/`bridge`), `-c`/`--container`, `-f`/`--filter` (BPF tcpdump expression), `-o`/`--output-file` (use `-` for stdout), `-l`/`--local-tcpdump-path`, `-r`/`--remote-tcpdump-path`, `-v`/`--verbose`, `-p`/`--privileged` (privileged container mode), `--pod-creation-timeout` Go-duration, `--image`, `--tcpdump-image`, `-x`/`--context`; positional is pod name. **File `_wasmedgec` (1-stem)** from [`WasmEdge/WasmEdge/tools/wasmedge/wasmedgec.cpp`](https://github.com/WasmEdge/WasmEdge/blob/master/tools/wasmedge/wasmedgec.cpp) entrypoint + [`include/driver/compiler.h`](https://github.com/WasmEdge/WasmEdge/blob/master/include/driver/compiler.h) `DriverCompilerOptions` struct + `lib/driver/compilerTool.cpp` `Parser.add_option(...)` calls. **Binary-name confirmation caught from upstream `tools/wasmedge/CMakeLists.txt`**: `wasmedge_add_executable(wasmedgec)` confirms `wasmedgec` is a separate executable from `wasmedge` (the runtime) — sibling AOT-compiler binary. Compiles a `.wasm` module into a native `.so`/`.dylib`/`.dll` shared library that wasmedge can load at runtime for faster startup + execution. Sister to the heavily-covered wasmedge/wasmer/wasmtime/wac/wkg/wasm-tools/wit-bindgen family. **8 boolean flags + 2 string flags + 2 positionals** decoded source-direct: `--optimize` 6-entry enum (`0`/`1`/`2`/`3`/`s`/`z`; default `2` per upstream `PO::DefaultValue(std::string("2"))`), `--generic-binary` (non-host-specific), `--dump-ir` (emit `wasm.ll` + `wasm-opt.ll`), `--interruptible`, `--enable-instruction-counting`, `--enable-gas-measuring`, `--enable-time-measuring`, `--enable-all-statistics` (umbrella for the 3 individual enable-* flags above); `--proposal` enum (17 WebAssembly proposal feature names from `import-export-mut-globals`/`nontrap-converts`/.../`exception-handling`); `--enable-all-proposals`; positionals are input `.wasm`/`.wat` + output shared-library file. **Notable PO::Option pattern**: the upstream `DriverCompilerOptions` extends `DriverProposalOptions` (shared WebAssembly proposal toggles) via C++ inheritance just like the rpicam-apps StillOptions chain caught in R148 — boost::program_options-style options builder pattern. **Hunt-skip log this round**: `cilium-cli` (pre-write dup-check via upstream `cilium/cilium-cli/cmd/cilium/main.go` confirmed the binary name is `cilium` not `cilium-cli`; `cilium` is already covered as the canonical binary; deferred per binary-name-vs-package-name precedent), `warg` / `warg-server` / `warg-cli` (upstream `bytecodealliance/registry` is ARCHIVED as of 2026-05-28 per `gh search repos` output — deferred indefinitely per archived-precedent), `nerdctl-cli`/`ctr-cli`/`containerd-cli` (suffix-`-cli` variants of already-covered binaries), `wasi_nn_rpcserver` (built from same `tools/wasmedge` directory per upstream `wasmedge_add_executable(wasi_nn_rpcserver)` — distinct binary but specialized for the WASI-NN RPC use case; deferred for separate audit), the broader carryforward queue (`forgejo-runner`, `gitea/act_runner`, `cuelang`, `proton`, `trpc`, `spiceai`, `zng`, `astro-cli`, AleoNet, `metamath-knife`, `metamath`, `esmtp`, `lbdb`, `nmh`, `barrier`, `peid`, `pestudio`, `elf-cli`, `nccmp`, `speedtest-go`, bioinformatics tail, batctl subcommand-internal flags). **Blacklist additions (3)**: 2 entries in `k*` (ksniff, kubedog) + 1 in `w*` (wasmedgec). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — **155th consecutive clean round at the 150-round-trip milestone**.
- **Tracing-tool sweep R149 — 2 files / 3 binaries** (`_funcount2sym` 2-stem + `_lldb-mi` 1-stem). Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`. Pivot to function-tracing + debugger-MI tooling after wide-net dup-check sweeps across eBPF/kernel/perf/tracing (heavily covered: bpftrace/bpftool/ply/bpfman/lttng/lttng-relayd/lttng-sessiond/uftrace/trace-cmd/kernelshark/openocd/pyocd/gdb-multiarch/lldb-server all in corpus). Surface extracted source-direct via `gh api`. **File `_funcount2sym` (2-stem `#compdef funcount2sym funtrace2viz`)** from upstream [`yosefk/funtrace`](https://github.com/yosefk/funtrace) Cargo.toml workspace `members = ["procaddr2sym", "funcount2sym", "funtrace2viz"]` + each member's `src/main.rs` clap-derive `Cli` struct (where applicable) or `env::args()` parsing. **Critical workspace-member discovery caught**: the 3rd workspace member `procaddr2sym` has only `src/lib.rs` (no `main.rs`) so it is a **LIBRARY crate, not a binary** — deliberately omitted from the `#compdef` line per the library-not-binary precedent. yosefk/funtrace is a fast, small C/C++ function call tracer for x86-64/Linux supporting clang & gcc, ftrace integration, threads, exceptions, and shared libraries; sister to the heavily-covered tracing family (bpftrace/bpftool/uftrace/trace-cmd/lttng/perf/dtrace). **funcount2sym** (minimal Rust binary using `env::args()` NOT clap, per upstream `if args.len() != 2 { fail!("Usage: {} <funcount.txt> # counts with function names printed to stdout, pipe through c++filt if you want to demangle the symbols", args[0]); }` — single positional, no flags; the "pipe through c++filt if you want to demangle the symbols" hint preserved as upstream usage note). **funtrace2viz** (7 clap-derive flags + 2 positional arguments decoded from `struct Cli { ... }` in funtrace2viz/src/main.rs): `-e`/`--executable-file-info` (also print static addresses + .so files), `-r`/`--raw-timestamps` (default subtracts earliest event to start at 0), `-m`/`--max-event-age CYCLES` (relative to latest), `-o`/`--oldest-event-time CYCLE` (absolute timestamp variant of --max-event-age), `-d`/`--dry` (dry run — list samples + threads, no JSON), repeatable `*-s`/`*--samples` (clap `Vec<u32>` allowlist), repeatable `*-t`/`*--threads` (clap `Vec<u64>` allowlist); 2 positionals are funtrace.raw input + JSON output basename (which generates `basename.json`, `basename.1.json`, etc.). **File `_lldb-mi` (1-stem)** from upstream [`lldb-tools/lldb-mi/src/MIDriverMgr.cpp`](https://github.com/lldb-tools/lldb-mi/blob/main/src/MIDriverMgr.cpp) `if (... strArg) == "--foo"` argv-walking checks (hand-rolled strcmp-style argv parser, NOT getopt/argparse). LLDB Machine Interface (MI) driver — the GDB/MI-protocol frontend bridge to LLDB used by Eclipse CDT, VS Code's lldb-mi adapter, and similar IDEs; sister tool to the lldb/lldb-server family already in the corpus. **6 flags + variadic positional** decoded source-direct: `-h`/`--help`, `--version` (short), `--versionLong` (long with build details — preserves upstream camelCase), `--interpreter` (run as MI interpreter, emit MI protocol on stdout for IDE clients like Eclipse CDT), `--executable=FILE` (alternative to `--interpreter`), `--log` (enable debug logging to `lldb-mi.log`). **Hunt-skip log this round**: `procaddr2sym` (library crate per workspace inspection; library-not-binary precedent), `bcc-tools`/`bcc`/`libbpf-tools` (collections of many shell + Python scripts each with its own surface; deferred for separate per-script rounds), `funtrace` itself (the upstream Cargo workspace name is `funtrace` but no binary by that name ships from this workspace — the actual binaries are funcount2sym/funtrace2viz/procaddr2sym, not a top-level `funtrace`; deferred until canonical entry-point clarified), `dtrace`/`lldb` (already covered as stock), `lttng-ust`/`lttng-modules` (library + kernel-module components, not user CLIs), `function-trace` (older Sublime Text plugin, not a CLI binary), the broader carryforward queue (`forgejo-runner`, `gitea/act_runner`, `cuelang`, `proton`, `trpc`, `spiceai`, `zng`, `astro-cli`, AleoNet, `metamath-knife`, `metamath`, `esmtp`, `lbdb`, `nmh`, `barrier`, `peid`, `pestudio`, `elf-cli`, `nccmp`, `speedtest-go`, bioinformatics tail, batctl subcommand-internal flags). **Blacklist additions (3)**: 2 entries in `f*` (funcount2sym, funtrace2viz) + 1 in `l*` (lldb-mi). - **Raspberry Pi rpicam-apps tail R148 — 1 file / 6 binaries** (`_libcamera-detect` 6-stem `#compdef libcamera-detect libcamera-jpeg libcamera-raw rpicam-detect rpicam-jpeg rpicam-raw`). Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`. Pivot to Raspberry Pi imaging after wide-net dup-check sweeps across Jupyter ecosystem + ML/Python (heavily covered) and discovering 3 fresh rpicam-apps binaries not in corpus (`rpicam-still`/`rpicam-vid`/`rpicam-hello` already covered in the existing `libcamera-{still,vid,hello}` multi-stem). Surface extracted source-direct from upstream [`raspberrypi/rpicam-apps`](https://github.com/raspberrypi/rpicam-apps) via `gh api`. **Critical naming discovery caught from upstream `apps/meson.build`**: `executable('rpicam-jpeg', files('rpicam_jpeg.cpp'), ...)` declarations show the CANONICAL binary names are `rpicam-*`; the `libcamera-*` names are **deprecated symlinks added by Raspberry Pi OS packaging** for backwards compatibility with the older libcamera-apps project name. Both name sets are kept in `#compdef` since both names are actively used in the wild. **Per-binary base class chain decoded from C++ inheritance**: rpicam-jpeg uses `StillOptions: public Options`; rpicam-raw uses minimal `RPiCamEncoder`-derived class; **rpicam-detect uses `DetectOptions: public StillOptions`** (per the upstream `struct DetectOptions : public StillOptions` declaration in `apps/rpicam_detect.cpp`) which extends StillOptions with 3 detect-specific flags `--object`/`--gap`/`--timeformat`. Surface uses `boost::program_options` `options_->add_options()` builder pattern throughout. **2 reusable helpers** factor the C++ inheritance chain: **`_rpicam_base`** (38 base Options shared by all 6 binary aliases — `-h`/`--help`, `--version`, `--list-cameras`, `--camera`, `-v`/`--verbose`, `-c`/`--config`, `--info-text`, `--width`/`--height`/`-t`/`--timeout`/`-o`/`--output`, `--post-process-file`/`--post-process-libs`, `-n`/`--nopreview`, `-p`/`--preview`, `-f`/`--fullscreen`, `--qt-preview`, `--preview-libs`, `--hflip`/`--vflip`/`--rotation` (enum `0`/`180`), `--roi`, `--shutter`, **`--gain` xor `--analoggain` mutex** (decoded from upstream `("analoggain", value<float>(&v_->gain), "Set a fixed gain value (synonym for 'gain' option)")` showing they target the same `v_->gain` field — synonym pair), `--metering` enum (`centre`/`spot`/`average`/`matrix`/`custom`), `--awb` enum (8 entries: `auto`/`incandescent`/`tungsten`/`fluorescent`/`indoor`/`daylight`/`cloudy`/`custom`), `--awbgains`, `--brightness`/`--contrast`/`--saturation`/`--sharpness`/`--ev`, `--exposure` enum (`normal`/`sport`/`long`/`custom`), `--denoise` enum (`auto`/`off`/`cdn_off`/`cdn_fast`/`cdn_hq`), `--mode`, `--buffer-count`, `--tuning-file`); **`_rpicam_still`** (15 StillOptions extensions used by jpeg + detect — `-q`/`--quality` (default 93), repeatable `*-x`/`*--exif KEY=VALUE`, `--timelapse`, `--framestart`, `--datetime`, `--timestamp`, `--restart`, `-k`/`--keypress`, `-s`/`--signal`, `--thumb` `width:height:quality` spec, `-e`/`--encoding` enum (`jpg`/`png`/`rgb`/`rgb24`/`rgb48`/`bmp`/`yuv420`), `-r`/`--raw` (also save DNG), `--latest`, `--immediate`, `--autofocus-on-capture`). **Per-binary specifics**: **rpicam-jpeg/libcamera-jpeg** = `_rpicam_base` + `_rpicam_still`. **rpicam-raw/libcamera-raw** = `_rpicam_base` + 3 raw-specific flags (`--framerate`, `--save-pts`, `--segment`) NOT the still helper since raw uses a null-encoder direct path. **rpicam-detect/libcamera-detect** = `_rpicam_base` + `_rpicam_still` + 3 detect-specific flags from `DetectOptions`: `--object NAME` (matches the TFLite label), `--gap FRAMES` (smallest gap between captures, default 30), `--timeformat FMT` (strftime() format, default `%m%d%H%M%S`). The upstream's literal example command in source comment is preserved verbatim: `rpicam-detect --post-process-file object_detect_tf.json --lores-width 400 --lores-height 300 -t 0 --object cat -o cat%03d.jpg`. **Hunt-skip log this round**: `picamera2`/`pi-cam`/`rpi-camera` (Python libcamera bindings; library-not-binary precedent), `jupyterhub`/`spyder`/`qtconsole`/`streamlit-cli`/`gradio` and similar Python notebook/UI tools (most are already covered or are Python module entry points; deferred), `nbdev` (uses fastcore's `@call_parse` decorator instead of argparse — surface needs deeper drilling; deferred), `colly`/`twint-cli`/`scrapy-cli`/`photoprism-cli` (suffix-`-cli` variants of already-covered base binaries OR need canonical URL discovery), `cyclonedx-bom`/`cyclonedx-conan`/`scancode-toolkit`/`fossology` from R147 carryforward (still deferred), the broader carryforward queue (`forgejo-runner`, `gitea/act_runner`, `cuelang`, `proton`, `trpc`, `spiceai`, `zng`, `astro-cli`, AleoNet, `metamath-knife`, `metamath`, `esmtp`, `lbdb`, `nmh`, `barrier`, `peid`, `pestudio`, `elf-cli`, `nccmp`, `speedtest-go`, bioinformatics tail, batctl subcommand-internal flags). **Blacklist additions (6)**: 3 entries in `libcamera-*` range + 3 in `rpicam-*` range — all paired alias names per upstream meson.build canonical-name vs. RPi-OS-symlink-name distinction. LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 153rd consecutive clean round.
- **SBOM/license/vuln-DB sweep R147 — 3 files / 3 binaries** (`_grype-db` 1-stem + `_depscan` 1-stem + `_licensee` 1-stem). Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`. Pivot to supply-chain/SBOM/license tooling after wide-net dup-check sweeps across the ecosystem (grype/syft/cosign/trivy/snyk/ratchet/reuse/cyclonedx/spdx/cargo-deny/cargo-audit heavily covered; biome/rome/dprint/taplo/treefmt/yamlfmt covered; iceberg/dolt covered). Surface extracted source-direct via `gh api`. **File `_grype-db` (1-stem)** from [`anchore/grype-db/cmd/grype-db/cli/cli.go`](https://github.com/anchore/grype-db/blob/main/cmd/grype-db/cli/cli.go) `root.AddCommand(...)` chain + per-subcommand `cmd/grype-db/cli/commands/*.go` `Use:`/`Short:` fields. Anchore's backend tool that builds the Grype vulnerability database from upstream feeds (NVD/MSRC/OS vendor advisories) into the SQLite shape Grype consumes at scan-time — typically run in CI rather than at scan-time. Sister tool to the heavily-covered grype/syft/cosign supply-chain family. **6 top-level Cobra subcommands** decoded source-direct from `cli.go`: `build` (build SQLite DB from feeds for schema version), `cache` (with 4 nested subcommands `backup`/`delete`/`restore`/`status`), `list-providers`, `package` (tar and checksum a built DB for distribution), `pull` (pull and process all upstream vulnerability data), `version`. Plus 3 persistent root flags from `cmd/grype-db/cli/commands/root.go` `cmd.PersistentFlags()` declarations: `-c`/`--config`, `--dry-run` (parse config + flags then exit), `-q`/`--quiet`. Uses `_arguments -C` + state-handler dispatch with a nested `cache` state handler for the 4 cache subcommands. **File `_depscan` (1-stem)** from [`owasp-dep-scan/dep-scan/depscan/cli_options.py`](https://github.com/owasp-dep-scan/dep-scan/blob/master/depscan/cli_options.py) argparse `parser.add_argument(...)` calls including the `parser.add_mutually_exclusive_group(required=False)` block for the `--bom-engine` xor `--vulnerability-analyzer` mutex pair (caught source-direct by reading the `engine_group.add_argument(...)` pair under the mutually-exclusive-group declaration). OWASP open-source security audit tool for project dependencies; sister to the heavily-covered supply-chain family (snyk/trivy/grype/syft/cosign/cyclonedx/cargo-deny/cargo-audit). **~38 flags** decoded source-direct including: 4 short-form flags (`-i` input, `-o` output, `-t` type, `-q` quiet, `-v` version); rich enum types — `-t`/`--type` 13-entry project-type enum (`java`/`python`/`nodejs`/`go`/`rust`/`ruby`/`php`/`csharp`/`swift`/`dart`/`cpp`/`dockerfile`/`yaml-manifest`/`binary`), `--profile` 6-entry profile enum (`appsec`/`research`/`generic`/`standard`/`mlsec`/`licence`), `--lifecycle` 4-entry stage enum, `--technique` 3-entry enum (`precise`/`approximate`/`llm`), `--explanation-mode` 3-entry enum (`none`/`normal`/`verbose`); **2-way mutex `--bom-engine` xor `--vulnerability-analyzer`** (decoded from `engine_group = parser.add_mutually_exclusive_group(required=False)` block in upstream); `--reachability-analyzer` 3-entry enum (`SemanticReachability`/`FrameworkReachability`/`off`); 5 server-mode flags (`--server`/`--server-host`/`--server-port`/`--server-allowed-hosts`/`--server-allowed-paths`); 4 BOM-input flags (`--bom`/`--bom-dir`/`--cdxgen-args`/`--cdxgen-server`); per-report flags (`--report-name`/`--report-template`/`--reports-dir`); standalone toggles (`--csaf` for CSAF v2.0 VEX output, `--deep` semantic-reachability scan, `--explain`, `--fuzzy-search`, `--no-banner`, `--no-suggest`, `--no-universal`, `--no-vuln-table`, `--risk-audit`, `--annotate`). **File `_licensee` (1-stem)** from [`licensee/licensee/bin/licensee`](https://github.com/licensee/licensee/blob/master/bin/licensee) Thor entrypoint + the 4 command implementations in [`lib/licensee/commands/{detect,diff,license_path,version}.rb`](https://github.com/licensee/licensee/tree/master/lib/licensee/commands) (each is a Thor subclass with `desc <name>` + `option <flag>, type: ..., desc: ...` declarations). **Critical extraction quirk caught**: licensee uses Ruby's Thor CLI framework (NOT optparse), so `option :foo` becomes `--foo` and **the `--no-foo` negation form is auto-generated by Thor for boolean options** — explicitly captured both `--packages`/`--no-packages` and `--readme`/`--no-readme` pairs in the completion. GitHub's Ruby gem used to detect the open-source license of a repository (it's what powers the license badge on github.com); also runs standalone as the `licensee` CLI. **4 subcommands** decoded source-direct: `detect [PATH]` (the default task; 10 flags including `--json`, `--confidence` percent default 98, `--license SPDX_ID`, `--diff`, `--ref` for github.com-only commit/branch/tag, `--filesystem` to ignore git data), `diff [PATH]` (1 flag `--license SPDX_ID`), `license-path PATH` (no flags), `version` (no flags). Plus the global `--remote` flag (declared via `class_option :remote, type: :boolean` at the LicenseeCLI Thor class level — applies to all 4 subcommands). **Hunt-skip log this round**: `scancode`/`scancode-toolkit`/`fossology`/`fossology-cli` (canonical FOSS license tools; large/complex flag sets that need dedicated rounds — deferred), `cyclonedx-bom`/`cyclonedx-conan` (CycloneDX language-specific generators; deferred for separate audit), `spdx-tools`/`spdx-sbom-generator` (need canonical upstream URL discovery), `snyk-cli`/`snyk-iac` (Snyk variants where the canonical `snyk` binary is already covered; deferred until snyk-cli vs snyk distinction confirmed), `dependency-track-cli`/`pasta-cli`/`sigma2yara`/`shellcheck-sarif`/`scancode-py`/`taplo-cli`/`dprint-cli`/`biome-cli`/`syft-cli`/`iceberg-cli`/`duckdb-cli`/`dolt-cli` (all -cli suffix variants of already-covered base binaries OR need canonical URL discovery), `basedmypy`/`basedrust` (community forks of mypy/cargo — defer until pypi/crates.io presence confirmed), `sno`/`openchami`/`fly-cli`/`bjam`/`bjam-cli` (need canonical upstream URLs), the broader carryforward queue (`forgejo-runner`, `gitea/act_runner`, `cuelang`, `proton`, `trpc`, `spiceai`, `zng`, `astro-cli`, AleoNet, `metamath-knife`, `metamath`, `esmtp`, `lbdb`, `nmh`, `barrier`, `peid`, `pestudio`, `elf-cli`, `nccmp`, `speedtest-go`, bioinformatics tail, batctl subcommand-internal flags). **Blacklist additions (3)**: 1 entry in `d*` (depscan) + 1 in `g*` (grype-db) + 1 in `l*` (licensee). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 152nd consecutive clean round.
- **B.A.T.M.A.N. batctl dedicated round R146 — 1 file / 1 binary** (`_batctl` 1-stem). Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`. Carryforward from R145 hunt-skip log where the macro-table extraction was deferred for a dedicated round. Surface extracted source-direct from upstream [`open-mesh-mirror/batctl`](https://github.com/open-mesh-mirror/batctl) via `gh api`. **Extraction technique**: walked all **58 .c files** in the source root and grepped for the `COMMAND(_type, _handler, _abbr, _flags, _arg, _usage)` and `COMMAND_NAMED(...)` macros — each .c file registers a subcommand via one of these macros, so the canonical subcommand list lives across the file tree rather than in a central table. The subcommand TYPE field (first macro arg) determines whether it is `SUBCOMMAND` (general), `SUBCOMMAND_MIF` (mesh-interface-scoped), `SUBCOMMAND_VID` (vlan-scoped), `SUBCOMMAND_HIF` (hard-interface-scoped), `DEBUGTABLE` (debug table query), or `JSON_MIF`/`JSON_HIF`/`JSON_VID` (netlink query as JSON). **B.A.T.M.A.N.-advanced** (batctl) is the control + diagnostics utility for the B.A.T.M.A.N.-advanced Layer-2 mesh routing protocol; sister to the heavily-covered mesh-networking family (babeld/yggdrasil/yggdrasilctl/tinc/alfred). **Global flags from main.c's `getopt(argc, argv, "+hm:v")` invocation**: only 3 globals — `-h` help, `-m MESHIF` mesh interface (default `bat0`), `-v` version. **46-entry subcommand enum** decoded source-direct, organized by macro TYPE: **2 SUBCOMMAND general** (`event`/`e`, `routing_algo`/`ra`); **20 SUBCOMMAND_MIF mesh-interface scoped** (`aggregation`/`ag`, `ap_isolation`/`ap`, `bonding`/`b`, `bridge_loop_avoidance`/`bl`, `distributed_arp_table`/`dat`, `fragmentation`/`f`, `gw_mode`/`gw`, `hop_penalty`/`hp`, `interface`/`if`, `isolation_mark`/`mark`, `loglevel`/`ll`, `multicast_fanout`/`mo`, `multicast_forceflood`/`mff`, `multicast_mode`/`mm`, `orig_interval`/`it`, `ping`/`p`, `statistics`/`s`, `throughputmeter`/`tp`, `traceroute`/`tr`, `translate`/`t`); **2 SUBCOMMAND_HIF hard-interface scoped** (`elp_interval`/`et`, `throughput_override`/`to`); **8 DEBUGTABLE debug-table queries** (`backbonetable`/`bbt`, `claimtable`/`cl`, `dat_cache`/`dc`, `gateways`/`gwl`, `mcast_flags`/`mf`, `originators`/`o`, `transglobal`/`tg`, `translocal`/`tl`); **11 JSON_MIF JSON queries on mesh-interface** (`bla_backbone_json`/`bbj`, `bla_claim_json`/`clj`, `dat_cache_json`/`dcj`, `gateways_json`/`gwj`, `hardifs_json`, `mcast_flags_json`/`mfj`, `mesh_json`/`mj`, `neighbors_json`/`nj`, `originators_json`/`oj`, `transtable_global_json`/`tgj`, `transtable_local_json`/`tlj`); **2 JSON_HIF + JSON_VID JSON queries** (`hardif_json`/`hj`, `vlan_json`/`vj`). Plus 3 interface selectors offered (`meshif`/`hardif`/`vid`) which prefix scope-requiring subcommands. **Notable extraction quirk caught**: the short alias `hj` is registered twice in the upstream source — once for `hardif_json` (JSON_HIF type) and once for `hardifs_json` (JSON_MIF type) — flagged in the completion description text since this is a documented upstream collision and the actual dispatched subcommand depends on the active interface scope. The 1 binary `ap_isolation` itself is registered TWICE (under both SUBCOMMAND_MIF and SUBCOMMAND_VID via two separate `COMMAND_NAMED(...)` macros in the same file) — handled identically with one description per binary in the completion. **Hunt-skip log this round**: subcommand-internal flags (each subcommand has its own per-command flag handling beyond just the global getopt — e.g., `ping` accepts target MAC + `-c COUNT`, `traceroute` accepts target MAC + `-c COUNT`, `throughputmeter` accepts target MAC + duration; these per-subcommand flag tables would each require reading the corresponding `parse_*` function in the matching .c file — deferred for an even deeper batctl-internals round). **Blacklist additions (1)**: 1 entry in `b*` (batctl). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 151st consecutive clean round.
- **CoAP + Rust MQTT-broker sweep R145 — 2 files / 2 binaries** (`_coap-cli` 1-stem + `_rumqttd` 1-stem). Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`. Pivot to IoT protocols (CoAP + MQTT broker) after wide-net dup-check sweeps across time-series DBs + MQTT family + mesh networking + VPN (most heavily covered: mosquitto/mosquitto_pub/mosquitto_sub/mqtt-cli/emqx/influxd/wg/wg-quick/boringtun/tinc/yggdrasil/yggdrasilctl/babeld/alfred all in corpus). Surface extracted source-direct via `gh api`. **File `_coap-cli` (1-stem)** from [`absmach/coap-cli/main.go`](https://github.com/absmach/coap-cli/blob/main/main.go) cobra `rootCmd` + 4 method subcommands (`get`/`put`/`post`/`delete`) + their `Flags().<TypeVarP>(...)` / `PersistentFlags().<TypeVarP>(...)` declarations. **Crucial disambiguation caught**: there are TWO distinct projects called `coap-cli` in the wild — `coapjs/coap-cli` is a Node.js implementation while `absmach/coap-cli` is the Go implementation; this completion targets the Go implementation specifically per the canonical Cobra structure + entries verified at the absmach/ URL. CoAP (Constrained Application Protocol; RFC 7252) is the UDP-based REST counterpart to HTTP designed for constrained IoT devices; sister to the heavily-covered MQTT family. **13 persistent flags shared by all 4 method subcommands** factored into `_coap_persistent` helper: `-H`/`--host`, `-p`/`--port`, `-a`/`--auth`, `-c`/`--content-format` (default 50=application/json), repeatable `*-O`/`*--options NUM=TEXT` (hex prefix `0x` for binary; per upstream "If the text begins with 0x, then the hex text (two [0-9a-f] per byte) is converted to binary data"), `-k`/`--keep-alive`, `-m`/`--max-retries`, `-v`/`--verbose`, `-C`/`--cert-file`, `-K`/`--key-file`, `-A`/`--ca-file`, and `-s`/`--use DTLS`. **Notable upstream-quirk caught**: the `--use DTLS` long flag literally **contains a space** in its name per upstream `PersistentFlags().BoolVarP(&req.useDTLS, "use DTLS", "s", false, "Use DTLS")` declaration — this would be unusable from a typical shell because Cobra wouldn't parse `--use DTLS` as a single token without quoting (most users would invoke `-s` instead). Per-subcommand: **get** adds `-o`/`--observe` (subscribe-to-changes per CoAP observe semantics from RFC 7641); **put**/**post**/**delete** all add `-d`/`--data` for request payload. **File `_rumqttd` (1-stem)** from [`bytebeamio/rumqtt/rumqttd/src/main.rs`](https://github.com/bytebeamio/rumqtt/blob/main/rumqttd/src/main.rs) clap-derive `CommandLine` struct + `Command::GenerateConfig` Subcommand variant. High-performance MQTT broker in Rust from the bytebeamio team; embeddable + lightweight; built on the rumqtt async MQTT client/broker family. Sister broker to the heavily-covered emqx/mosquitto family. Minimal 4-flag + 1-subcommand surface: `-c`/`--config FILE` (default: built-in `rumqttd.toml`), **stackable** `*-v`/`--verbose` (`-v` info, `-vv` debug, `-vvv` trace — implemented as clap `ArgAction::Count` per upstream), `-q`/`--quiet` (skip banner), `--version`; single `generate-config` subcommand that writes the default `rumqttd.toml` to stdout. **Hunt-skip log this round**: `batctl` (B.A.T.M.A.N. mesh networking control — has rich macro-based subcommand registration via `COMMAND(_type, _handler, _abbr, _flags, _arg, _usage)` macros scattered across 40+ .c files in [`open-mesh-mirror/batctl`](https://github.com/open-mesh-mirror/batctl); confirmed 7-10 visible subcommands (`event`/`gw_mode`/`interface`/`loglevel`/`ping`/`routing_algo`/`statistics`/`throughputmeter`/`traceroute` + likely more) but the macro-table extraction would need a dedicated round to harvest all subcommand registrations across the source tree systematically; deferred), `coapjs/coap-cli` (Node.js alternative implementation; both projects ship a binary named `coap-cli` — the absmach/Go version is the one this round covers, deferred the Node.js variant), `amneziawg` (multi-repo project across openwrt/android/windows/mikrotik with no obvious canonical CLI; deferred), `horaedb` (Apache Horae Time-Series DB — exists but needs canonical CLI URL discovery; deferred), `softether`/`ovpncfg`/`openvpn-cli` (VPN CLIs need careful per-tool repo discovery; deferred), `paho-mqtt-c/cpp/python` (Eclipse Paho client LIBRARIES not standalone CLIs; deferred per library-not-binary precedent), the broader carryforward queue (`forgejo-runner`, `gitea/act_runner`, `cuelang`, `proton`, `trpc`, `spiceai`, `zng`, `astro-cli`, AleoNet, `metamath-knife`, `metamath`, `esmtp`, `lbdb`, `nmh`, `barrier`, `peid`, `pestudio`, `elf-cli`, `nccmp`, `speedtest-go`, bioinformatics tail). **Blacklist additions (2)**: 1 entry in `c*` (coap-cli) + 1 in `r*` (rumqttd). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 150th consecutive clean round. **R145 hits the 150-clean-round milestone.**
- **Subdomain-takeover + Linux-kernel-extractor sweep R144 — 2 files / 4 binaries** (`_subjack` 1-stem + `_kallsyms-finder` 3-stem). Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`. Pivot to offensive-security + kernel-RE tooling after wide-net dup-check sweeps across OpenAPI/GitOps (most heavily covered: openapi-generator-cli/oasdiff/oapi-codegen/swagger-codegen/redocly/buf/protoc-gen-go/protoc-gen-ts/argocd/flux/kustomize/crossplane/timoni/kpt/ko/porter all in corpus), embedded toolchains (probe-rs/probe-run/cargo-embed/cargo-flash/openocd/avrdude/esptool/idf.py/micropython/mpy-cross/mpremote covered), CTF/recon tooling (msfvenom/msfconsole/hashcat/amass/subfinder/assetfinder/httpx/katana/waybackurls/ropper/ropgadget/checksec covered). Surface extracted source-direct via `gh api`. **File `_subjack` (1-stem)** from [`haccer/subjack/main.go`](https://github.com/haccer/subjack/blob/master/main.go) Go stdlib `flag.StringVar/.IntVar/.BoolVar(...)` declarations. Subdomain takeover detection tool for offensive security / bug-bounty workflows; sister to the heavily-covered amass/subfinder/assetfinder/waybackurls/katana/httpx recon family. **14 flags** decoded source-direct including: 4 single-char single-dash flags `-d DOMAIN`/`-w WORDLIST`/`-t THREADS`/`-o OUTPUT`; 4 boolean toggles `-ssl`/`-a` (all-URLs)/`-v`/`-m` (manual dead-CNAME flag); 4 specialized check toggles `-ns`/`-ar`/`-axfr`/`-mail` (nameserver-takeover, stale-A-record, zone-transfer/AXFR-bruteforce, SPF+MX takeover). **File `_kallsyms-finder` (3-stem `#compdef kallsyms-finder vmlinux-to-elf vmlinuz-decompressor`)** from [`marin-m/vmlinux-to-elf/pyproject.toml`](https://github.com/marin-m/vmlinux-to-elf/blob/master/pyproject.toml) `[project.scripts]` entry-point mapping (4 entries: `kallsyms-finder` → `kallsyms_finder:main`, `vmlinux-to-elf` → `vmlinux_to_elf:main`, `vmlinuz-decompressor` → `vmlinuz_decompressor:main`, `vmlinux-to-elf-gui` → `gtk_app:main`) + each script's argparse `args.add_argument(...)` calls. **The 4th entry-point `vmlinux-to-elf-gui` is a Gtk app intentionally omitted** from this `#compdef` line since it takes no CLI args (it just spawns a GTK GUI). vmlinux-to-elf is an analysis-friendly extractor for Linux kernel binaries (vmlinux/vmlinuz/zImage) that decompresses and produces an ELF + symbol table consumable by Ghidra/IDA/radare2; sister to the heavily-covered reverse-engineering family (ghidra/cutter/rizin/binwalk/binsider). **Per-binary surface**: **kallsyms-finder** (6 flags + 1 positional; smaller-surface subset of vmlinux-to-elf for symbol-only extraction): `--base-address HEX`, `--bit-size` enum `32`/`64`, `--extra-info`, `--output FILE`, `--use-absolute` (absolute addresses instead of base-relative), positional vmlinux/vmlinuz/zImage input. **vmlinux-to-elf** (7 flags + 2 positionals; main extractor): `--e-machine`, `--bit-size` enum `32`/`64`, `--file-offset BYTES`, `--base-address HEX`, `--bss-size BYTES`, `--extra-info`, `--use-absolute`, positionals input file + output ELF. **vmlinuz-decompressor** (no flags + 2 positionals; standalone decompressor for vmlinuz/zImage that emits the raw vmlinux blob without ELF wrapping or symbol extraction). **Notable per-file extraction quirk**: the 3 scripts intentionally share `--base-address`/`--bit-size`/`--extra-info`/`--use-absolute` because they reuse the same argparse-fragment functions under-the-hood in `vmlinux_to_elf.utils` — kept the description text similar in each subcommand arm rather than factoring into a shared helper, since the 3 surfaces differ enough in other flags that a shared helper would be more verbose than inline. **Hunt-skip log this round**: `espflash` (initially flagged fresh but pre-write dup-check showed `_espflash` already covers the esp-rs/espflash Rust port of esptool; the `[[bin]] name = "espflash"` Cargo declaration confirms the canonical binary name), `pwntools`/`pwn-cli`/`pwndbg`/`gef-cli`/`metasploit-cli`/`angr-cli`/`manticore-cli` (most are Python REPL frameworks loaded via `from pwn import *` etc, not standalone CLIs with `--flag` surfaces; deferred), `uki-tool` (3 separate projects came back from `gh search` with that name — `voloko/uki-tools`, `SamD2021/lowell`, `trevortomesh/ukiTool` — none have a clearly canonical UKI-tooling CLI; deferred until upstream consolidation), `vmlinux-to-elf-gui` (Gtk GUI app with no CLI args; intentionally omitted from the #compdef line above), `nbtscan`/`reaver`/`onesixtyone`/`oclhashcat`/`hashview`/`hcxtools`/`hashcat-utils` (older offensive-security tools mostly already covered or with unclear upstream URLs; deferred), the broader carryforward queue (`forgejo-runner`, `gitea/act_runner`, `cuelang`, `proton`, `trpc`, `spiceai`, `zng`, `astro-cli`, AleoNet, `metamath-knife`, `metamath`, `esmtp`, `lbdb`, `nmh`, `barrier`, `peid`, `pestudio`, `elf-cli`, `nccmp`, `speedtest-go`, `megahit`/`unicycler`/`shovill`/`quast`/`busco`/`prokka`/`bakta`/`sourmash`/`trimal`/`polypolish`/`medaka`/`racon`/`nanopolish`). **Blacklist additions (4)**: 1 entry in `k*` (kallsyms-finder) + 1 in `s*` (subjack) + 2 in `v*` (vmlinux-to-elf, vmlinuz-decompressor). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 149th consecutive clean round.
- **htslib utility tail + Sanger assembly-stats R143 — 2 files / 4 binaries** (`_annot-tsv` 3-stem + `_assembly-stats` 1-stem). Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`. Pivot to bioinformatics utility tail after wide-net audits across bioinformatics tooling (seqkit/fastp/cutadapt/trim_galore/minimap2/hifiasm/spades/iqtree/mafft/clustalw/clustalo/mash/flye/canu/samtools/bcftools/tabix all heavily covered already). Surface extracted source-direct via `gh api`. **File `_annot-tsv` (3-stem `#compdef annot-tsv bgzip htsfile`)** from [`samtools/htslib`](https://github.com/samtools/htslib) [`annot-tsv.c`](https://github.com/samtools/htslib/blob/develop/annot-tsv.c) + [`bgzip.c`](https://github.com/samtools/htslib/blob/develop/bgzip.c) + [`htsfile.c`](https://github.com/samtools/htslib/blob/develop/htsfile.c) `Usage()` fprintf bodies + `getopt_long` option-string + long-option tables. Sister tools to the heavily-covered samtools/bcftools/tabix htslib family. The 3 htslib utility binaries not previously covered. All 3 use `getopt_long` with explicit long-option tables. **annot-tsv** (18 flags; annotates a TSV/BED file with overlapping records from another TSV/BED source — getopt string `c:C:f:m:o:s:t:a:HO:rxh:Id:` decoded source-direct from upstream `main()`): pairs of `SRC:TGT`-style options (`-c`/`-f`/`-m`/`-C`/`-d`/`-h` all take `SRC:TGT` strings); `-a`/`--annotate` enum `cnt`/`frac`/`nbp` (number-of-overlaps, fraction-of-target-overlapped, number-of-source-base-pairs); `-r` reciprocal overlap; `-x` exclude-no-overlap targets; `-I` case-insensitive; `-O` overlap-percent requirement; `--allow-dups`, `--max-annots`, `--help`, `--version` long-only. **bgzip** (16 flags + variadic positional; block-gzip compress/decompress with random-access index support via BGZF index): `-d`/`--decompress`, `-c`/`--stdout`, `-f`/`--force`, `-k`/`--keep`, `-i`/`--index` (create BGZF index), `-I FILE`/`--index-name` (default `file.gz.gzi`), `-l LEVEL`/`--compress-level` enum `-1..9`, `-r`/`--reindex`, `-s BYTES`/`--size` (partial decompress), `-t`/`--test` integrity check, `-g`/`--rebgzip`, `-b OFFSET`/`--offset`, `-@ N`/`--threads`, `--binary` long-only, `-o FILE`/`--output`. **htsfile** (7 flags + variadic positional;
