identifies and views file types supported by htslib — BAM/CRAM/SAM/VCF/BCF/FASTA/etc.; getopt string `cChHv` + long-option table for `--copy`/`--header-only`/`--no-header`/`--view`/`--verbose`/`--help`/`--version`): **2-way action mutex** `-c`/`--view` (write textual form to stdout) xor `-C`/`--copy` (exact copy to DESTFILE); **2-way header mutex** `-h`/`--header-only` xor `-H`/`--no-header`; `-v`/`--verbose`. **File `_assembly-stats` (1-stem)** from [`sanger-pathogens/assembly-stats/assembly-stats.cpp`](https://github.com/sanger-pathogens/assembly-stats/blob/master/assembly-stats.cpp) `usage = "usage: stats [options] ..."` body + hand-rolled `strcmp(argv[ops.infileStartIndex], "-X") == 0` argv-walking loop (NOT getopt). **Notable upstream-quirk caught**: the embedded usage prefix says `usage: stats [options]` but [`CMakeLists.txt`](https://github.com/sanger-pathogens/assembly-stats/blob/master/CMakeLists.txt) `add_executable(assembly-stats assembly-stats.cpp)` + `install(TARGETS assembly-stats ...)` confirm the **installed binary name is `assembly-stats`** (the `stats` prefix in the help text is a vestigial naming artifact, not the actual invocation name). 5 flags + variadic positional: `-l INT` minimum length cutoff (default 1), **3-way output-format mutex** `-s` (grep-friendly) xor `-t` (tab-delimited with header) xor `-u` (tab-delimited without header), `-v` print version. Wellcome Sanger Institute's reporter of sequence-length statistics from FASTA and FASTQ files. **Hunt-skip log this round**: `megahit`/`unicycler`/`shovill`/`quast`/`busco`/`prokka`/`bakta`/`sourmash`/`trimal`/`hmmer3`/`polypolish`/`medaka`/`racon`/`nanopolish` (broader bioinformatics audit returned many fresh candidates but most have large/complex flag sets that need dedicated rounds; deferred for later batches), `trimal` (specifically saw it uses hand-rolled `strcmp(argv[i], "-X")` argv-walking with ~40+ output format flags and threshold-parameter flags — deferred for a dedicated round), the broader carryforward queue (`forgejo-runner`, `gitea/act_runner`, `cuelang`, `proton`, `trpc`, `spiceai`, `zng`, `astro-cli`, AleoNet, `metamath-knife`, `metamath`, `esmtp`, `lbdb`, `nmh`, `barrier`, `peid`, `pestudio`, `elf-cli`, `nccmp`, `speedtest-go`). **Blacklist additions (4)**: 1 entry in `a*` (annot-tsv) + 1 in `a*` (assembly-stats) + 1 in `b*` (bgzip) + 1 in `h*` (htsfile). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 148th consecutive clean round.
- **Unidata netCDF + WebP utilities tail R142 — 2 files / 4 binaries** (`_nccopy` 2-stem + `_webpinfo` 2-stem). Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`. Pivot to scientific data formats + image-codec utilities after wide-net audits across netCDF + NCO operators (`ncdump`/`ncgen`/`ncks`/`ncrcat`/`ncbo`/`ncea`/`ncatted`/`ncrename`/`ncwa`/`ncap2`/`ncpdq` all already covered) + GDAL (last round) + GMT + GRASS + osmium family + WebP toolchain (`cwebp`/`dwebp`/`gif2webp` already covered). Surface extracted source-direct via `gh api`. **File `_nccopy` (2-stem `#compdef nccopy ncgen3`)** from [`Unidata/netcdf-c/ncdump/nccopy.c`](https://github.com/Unidata/netcdf-c/blob/main/ncdump/nccopy.c) `#define USAGE "...."` block + [`ncgen3/main.c`](https://github.com/Unidata/netcdf-c/blob/main/ncgen3/main.c) `static void usage(void)` body. Both binaries use **hand-rolled `case c:` getopt() loops (NOT getopt_long)**, so each flag set was cross-verified by reading the USAGE/usage() string AND the getopt argument-string + switch dispatch in `main()`. The 2 utility binaries not previously covered (ncdump and ncgen are already in the corpus). **nccopy** (24 flags + 2 positionals; copies a netCDF file with optional format/compression/variable-selection/chunking transformations): **5-way output-format mutex** `-3`/`-4`/`-5`/`-6`/`-7` (all aliases for `-k <kind>` decoded from upstream Usage comments `same as -k 'classic'` etc.); **5-entry `-k` enum** with named labels (`classic`/`64-bit offset`/`cdf5`/`netCDF-4`/`netCDF-4 classic model`); deflate level `-d 0-9` (0=none, 9=max); chunk control `-c CHUNKSPEC`/`-h CHUNK_CACHE_BYTES`/`-e CHUNK_CACHE_ELEMENTS`/`-M MINCHUNK`; **4-way variable-selection mutex** `-v`/`-V`/`-g`/`-G` (data-only-for-vars xor defs+data-for-vars xor data-only-for-groups xor defs+data-for-groups); `-s` shuffle filter, `-u` unlimited→fixed, `-w` diskless-write, `-r` diskless-read, `-m COPYBUFFER` (default 5MB), repeatable `*-F` filterspec, `-L` log-level. **ncgen3** (6 flags + variadic positional; converts CDL text into netCDF-3 classic-format binary — the legacy 3-format-only ncgen): `-b` binary output (default), `-c` C program output, `-f` Fortran program output, `-k KIND` (only `classic`/`64-bit offset` supported by ncgen3), `-x` skip fill-value padding, `-o FILE` output path. **File `_webpinfo` (2-stem `#compdef webpinfo webpmux`)** from [`webmproject/libwebp/examples/webpinfo.c`](https://github.com/webmproject/libwebp/blob/main/examples/webpinfo.c) + [`examples/webpmux.c`](https://github.com/webmproject/libwebp/blob/main/examples/webpmux.c). **Both binaries use hand-rolled `strcmp(argv[c], "-flag")` argv-walking loops** (NOT getopt/getopt_long), so the flag set was extracted by reading BOTH the `Help()` printf strings AND the strcmp dispatch in `main()` to ensure no documented flag was unimplemented or vice-versa. The 2 utility binaries not previously covered (the encoder/decoder/transcoder trio `cwebp`/`dwebp`/`gif2webp` are already in the corpus). **webpinfo** (9 flags + variadic positional; prints chunk-level structure and parsing diagnostics for WebP files): 4-way help-alias group `-h`/`-help`/`-H`/`-longhelp` (case-arm decoded — all 4 trigger the same `Help()` call upstream), `-version`, `-quiet`, `-diag`, `-summary`, `-bitstream_info`. **webpmux** (multi-form CLI; the most complex binary in the round): **6-way top-level mode mutex** `-get`/`-set`/`-duration`/`-strip`/`-frame`/`-info` driving different argument shapes downstream (decoded from upstream's multi-form Usage block: `webpmux -get GET_OPTIONS INPUT -o OUTPUT` xor `webpmux -set SET_OPTIONS ...` xor `webpmux -duration ...` etc.); `-get` thing enum (`icc`/`exif`/`xmp`/`frame`); `-set` thing enum (`loop`/`bgcolor`/`icc`/`exif`/`xmp`); `-strip` thing enum (`icc`/`exif`/`xmp`); repeatable `*-duration` and `*-frame` for animation building; `-loop` + `-bgcolor` (used with `-frame`); `-o OUTPUT`. **Hunt-skip log this round**: `nccmp` (NetCDF comparison utility — upstream `Unidata/nccmp` returns 404 on contents endpoint; the actual upstream is on SourceForge at `sourceforge.net/p/nccmp` per netCDF community; deferred until canonical GitHub mirror is identified), `ncdiff` (alias for `ncbo --op_typ=sbt` per NCO docs — not a separate binary; deferred per "alias-not-binary" precedent), `ncl_convert2nc` (NCL ecosystem in maintenance-only mode upstream; deferred), `speedtest-go` (`showwin/speedtest-go` repo exists but flag set extraction needs deeper drill; deferred), `goldendict-ng`/`goldendict-rust`/`magick-mogrify`/`svgtopng` (carryforward — pending canonical upstream URL discovery), the broader carryforward queue (`forgejo-runner`, `gitea/act_runner`, `cuelang`, `proton`, `trpc`, `spiceai`, `zng`, `astro-cli`, AleoNet, `metamath-knife`, `metamath`, `esmtp`, `lbdb`, `nmh`, `barrier`, `peid`, `pestudio`, `elf-cli`). **Blacklist additions (4)**: 2 entries in `n*` (nccopy, ncgen3) + 2 in `w*` (webpinfo, webpmux). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 147th consecutive clean round.
- **SDR + reverse-engineering + ADS-B sweep R141 — 3 files / 5 binaries** (`_SoapySDRUtil` 1-stem + `_dump978-fa` 3-stem + `_peframe` 1-stem). Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`. Pivot to security / SDR / ADS-B after wide-net audits across reverse-engineering (`ghidra`/`cutter`/`rizin`/`binwalk`/`yara`/`binsider`/`hexyl`/`bingrep`/`readpe` all heavily covered), SDR (`rtl_sdr`/`gqrx`/`gnuradio`/`gnuradio-companion`/`dump1090`/`inspectrum` covered), and PE/malware analysis tooling. Surface extracted source-direct from 3 distinct upstream repos via `gh api`. **File `_SoapySDRUtil` (1-stem)** from [`pothosware/SoapySDR/apps/SoapySDRUtil.cpp`](https://github.com/pothosware/SoapySDR/blob/master/apps/SoapySDRUtil.cpp) `static struct option long_options[]` table + `getopt_long_only(argc, argv, "", long_options, ...)` invocation. **Crucial extraction insight**: `getopt_long_only` with **empty short-option string `""`** means **ALL 15 options are long-only** despite each having a single-char enum tag (`'h'`/`'f'`/`'m'`/etc) in the upstream `long_options[]` table — those single-char tags are used only for the case-arm dispatch in `main()` and do NOT shadow as `-h`/`-f`/`-m` short aliases per upstream's `getopt_long_only` semantics with `""` short-string. Filename preserves upstream camelCase `SoapySDRUtil` exactly (zsh `#compdef` is case-sensitive). Utility tool that ships with the SoapySDR cross-vendor SDR API to enumerate, probe, and benchmark SDR devices across hardware vendors via the Soapy abstraction layer. Notable enums extracted from upstream behavior: `--format` (`CS8`/`CS12`/`CS16`/`CF32`/`CF64` — Soapy sample-format identifiers), `--direction` (`RX`/`TX` — Soapy stream direction). **File `_dump978-fa` (3-stem `#compdef dump978-fa faup978 skyaware978`)** from [`flightaware/dump978`](https://github.com/flightaware/dump978) [`dump978_main.cc`](https://github.com/flightaware/dump978/blob/master/dump978_main.cc) + [`faup978_main.cc`](https://github.com/flightaware/dump978/blob/master/faup978_main.cc) + [`skyaware978_main.cc`](https://github.com/flightaware/dump978/blob/master/skyaware978_main.cc) `po::options_description desc("Allowed options"); desc.add_options()(...)` blocks. **3 binaries all use Boost.Program_options** (NOT getopt or argparse) and ship from the same monorepo. **Binary names confirmed via Makefile targets** `all: dump978-fa skyaware978` + `faup978: faup978_main.o ...` (so `dump978-fa` is the FlightAware-suffixed binary name; the base `dump978` name does NOT ship as a separate binary here). 978 MHz UAT (Universal Access Transceiver) ADS-B decoder family from FlightAware — sister to the 1090 MHz `dump1090` decoder (already covered in the corpus). All 3 binaries support Boost.Program_options' standard `--help` and `--version` from upstream. **dump978-fa** (18 flags; main demodulator that reads UAT samples from an SDR/file/stdin and writes decoded messages to stdout or network listeners): repeatable `*--raw-port` + `*--raw-legacy-port` + `*--json-port` for network output (decoded via `po::value<std::vector<listen_option>>()` upstream); `--format` enum (`UC8`/`SC16`/`SC16Q11`); SDR control via `--sdr-auto-gain`/`--sdr-gain`/`--sdr-ppm`/`--sdr-antenna`/`--sdr-stream-settings`/`--sdr-device-settings`; alt input via `--stratuxv3` for Stratux v3 UAT serial dongle. **faup978** (3 flags; minimal protocol-bridge to a dump978 raw-message source): `--connect host:port` only. **skyaware978** (9 flags; SkyAware-style aggregator that writes rolling JSON history files for visualization): `--connect`, `--reconnect-interval` (default 0=disabled), `--json-dir`, `--history-count` (default 120), `--history-interval` (default 30s), `--lat`/`--lon` for receiver position. **File `_peframe` (1-stem)** from [`guelfoweb/peframe`](https://github.com/guelfoweb/peframe) [`setup.py`](https://github.com/guelfoweb/peframe/blob/master/setup.py) `entry_points.console_scripts` entry `peframe=peframe.peframecli` + [`peframe/peframecli.py`](https://github.com/guelfoweb/peframe/blob/master/peframe/peframecli.py) argparse `parser.add_argument(...)` calls. Static analyzer for Portable Executable (PE) malware and malicious MS Office documents; wraps pefile + strings + virustotal + a YARA signatures database. Sister to the heavily-covered YARA/yara-x family. Minimal 5-flag surface + 1 positional: `-v`/`--version`, `-i`/`--interactive` (REPL mode), `-x`/`--xorsearch PATTERN`, `-j`/`--json`, `-s`/`--strings`, plus the required `file` positional. **Hunt-skip log this round**: `ghidra-server` (initially flagged as fresh but upstream NSA/ghidra's collaborative server is shipped as Java JAR launchers via `ghidraSvr` scripts not as a standalone CLI binary with a flag surface; deferred), `firmware-mod-kit` (older binwalk-era toolkit with hand-rolled shell scripts per-tool that vary heavily; deferred), `elf-cli` / `peid` / `pestudio` (need canonical upstream URL discovery), `rtl-sdr` (the canonical package name; the binary `rtl_sdr` is already covered separately — `rtl-sdr` is a metapackage alias not a separate binary), the broader carryforward queue (`forgejo-runner`, `gitea/act_runner`, `cuelang`, `proton`, `trpc`, `spiceai`, `zng`, `astro-cli`, AleoNet, `metamath-knife`, `metamath`, `esmtp`, `lbdb`, `nmh`, `maildir`, `barrier`). **Blacklist additions (5)**: 1 entry in `S*` (SoapySDRUtil — preserving upstream camelCase) + 2 in `d*`/`f*` (dump978-fa, faup978) + 1 in `p*` (peframe) + 1 in `s*` (skyaware978). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 146th consecutive clean round.
- **Email + container-cache + Wayland-input sweep R140 — 3 files / 3 binaries** (`_goimapnotify` 1-stem + `_kaniko-warmer` 1-stem + `_waynergy` 1-stem). Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`. Mixed-domain sweep after wide-net audits across email/MUA, container security/registry, image processing, and PDF-tooling spaces (most heavily covered already — `himalaya`/`aerc`/`neomutt`/`notmuch`/`muchsync`/`isync`/`mbsync`/`offlineimap`/`fdm`/`msmtp`/`alot`/`afew` etc. for email; `trivy`/`grype`/`syft`/`cosign`/`rekor-cli`/`oras`/`crane`/`skopeo`/`umoci`/`buildah`/`buildkitd`/`buildctl`/`img`/`kaniko`/`jib`/`pack` for container security; `mutool`/`qpdf`/`cpdf`/`pdftk` for PDFs). Surface extracted source-direct from 3 distinct upstream repos via `gh api`. **File `_goimapnotify` (1-stem)** from [`shackra/goimapnotify/main.go`](https://github.com/shackra/goimapnotify/blob/master/main.go) Go stdlib `flag.String/.Bool/.Int(...)` declarations (NOT cobra/urfave/viper). Go reimplementation of the original Perl `imapnotify` by Brandon Long that subscribes to IMAP IDLE on configured mailbox folders and runs a user-defined command on new-mail / message-deleted / message-moved events. Sister to the heavily-covered `fdm`/`msmtp`/`isync`/`offlineimap` mail-pipeline family. Minimal 4-flag surface: `-conf FILE` (JSON config; default `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/goimapnotify/goimapnotify.conf`), `-list` (list mailboxes and exit), `-log-level` enum (`error`/`warning`/`warn`/`info`/`information`/`debug` — duplicate alias pairs preserved verbatim from upstream help text), `-wait` (seconds between IDLE event and script execution; default 1). **File `_kaniko-warmer` (1-stem)** from [`GoogleContainerTools/kaniko/cmd/warmer/cmd/root.go`](https://github.com/GoogleContainerTools/kaniko/blob/main/cmd/warmer/cmd/root.go) cobra `RootCmd.PersistentFlags().<TypeVar>(...)` declarations. Cache-warmer sister binary to the main `kaniko` executor (already covered in the corpus) — pre-pulls image layers from a registry into a local cache directory so the subsequent `kaniko` build can find them without going back to the registry; useful for CI environments where each build runs in a fresh container but shares a persistent cache volume. **19 flags** decoded source-direct including: shared logging (`-v`/`--verbosity` enum `trace`/`debug`/`info`/`warn`/`error`/`fatal`/`panic`, `--log-format` enum `text`/`color`/`json`, `--log-timestamp`); repeatable `*-i`/`*--image` for multiple cache targets; cache control (`-c`/`--cache-dir` default `/cache`, `-f`/`--force`, `--cache-ttl` Go-duration default 336h=2weeks); 8 registry tuning flags (`--insecure-pull`, `--skip-tls-verify-pull`, repeatable `*--insecure-registry`/`*--skip-tls-verify-registry`/`*--registry-certificate URL=PATH`/`*--registry-client-cert URL=CERT,KEY`/`*--registry-map`/`*--registry-mirror`, `--skip-default-registry-fallback`); platform/Dockerfile (`--customPlatform`, `-d`/`--dockerfile` to warm cache for each stage's base image, repeatable `*--build-arg NAME=VALUE` for dynamic base-image replacement). **File `_waynergy` (1-stem)** from [`r-c-f/waynergy/src/main.c`](https://github.com/r-c-f/waynergy/blob/master/src/main.c) `static struct sopt optspec[]` table using the **libsopt option-parsing library** (NOT getopt/getopt_long directly — caught at extraction time because the entries use `SOPT_INITL`/`SOPT_INIT_ARGL`/`SOPT_INIT_END` macros which differ from the standard getopt approach). Wayland-native Synergy client that replaces the abandoned Barrier project for Wayland users; connects to a Synergy server on another machine and forwards keyboard/mouse events into the local Wayland compositor via wlroots, KDE fake-input, or uinput. **18 flags** decoded source-direct: standard `-h`/`--help` + `-v`/`--version`; **3-way input backend enum** `-b`/`--backend` (`wlr`/`kde`/`uinput`); server connection `-c`/`--host` + `-p`/`--port`; **paired screen dimensions** `-W`/`--width` + `-H`/`--height` (manual override that must be supplied together); client identity `-N`/`--name`; logging `-l`/`--logfile` + `-L`/`--loglevel` (12-entry enum: integer `0`-`6` literals OR named `none`/`error`/`warn`/`info`/`debug`/`debugsyn`); 4 toggle flags `-n`/`--no-clip` (don't sync clipboard), **2-way mutex `-e`/`--enable-crypto` xor `-E`/`--disable-crypto`** TLS encryption (decoded from clap-equivalent's typical mutex pattern caught in the optspec table where both touch the same `[tls/enable]` config key per upstream help text), `-t`/`--enable-tofu` for trust-on-first-use TLS pinning; **4 long-only fatal-error toggles** `--fatal-none`/`--fatal-ebad`/`--fatal-ebsy`/`--fatal-timeout` decoded from the upstream macro `SOPT_INITL(CHAR_MAX + USYNERGY_ERROR_<KIND>, ...)` chain (offsets the short-form into the high-char-value range so no short alias is possible — a libsopt idiom for "long-only without polluting short-form namespace"). **Hunt-skip log this round**: `fulcio-cli` (initially flagged fresh but pre-write dup-check showed `_fulcio` already covers the Sigstore Fulcio CA binary — the canonical Cobra binary is named `fulcio` not `fulcio-cli`, so the existing coverage suffices; no separate CLI binary upstream), `barrier` (the abandoned upstream-of-waynergy KVM software — repo `debauchee/barrier` is archived as of 2024-01-25 with no further releases; deferred indefinitely), `esmtp`/`lbdb`/`nmh`/`maildir` (carryforward — pending canonical upstream URL discovery), the broader carryforward queue (`forgejo-runner`, `gitea/act_runner`, `cuelang`, `proton`, `trpc`, `spiceai`, `zng`, `astro-cli`, AleoNet, `metamath-knife`, `metamath`). **Blacklist additions (3)**: 1 entry in `g*` (goimapnotify) + 1 in `k*` (kaniko-warmer) + 1 in `w*` (waynergy). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 145th consecutive clean round. **First round logged in `HARVEST_LOG.md` directly** (previous rounds were logged in `README.md` before the bulk move into `HARVEST_LOG.md`).
- **Formal-methods sweep R139 — 2 files / 3 binaries** (`_apalache` 2-stem + `_alectryon` 1-stem). Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`. Pivot to formal methods + theorem-prover tooling after wide-net dup-check sweep across Coq/Lean/Z3/CVC5/Yices/Dafny/F*/Agda/MLton/Isabelle/PVS/Why3/TLA+/Spin/Alloy/Twelf (most heavily covered — only 7 formal-methods names came back fresh). Surface extracted source-direct via `gh api`. **File `_apalache` (2-stem `#compdef apalache apalache-mc`)** from [`apalache-mc/apalache/mod-tool/src/main/scala/at/forsyte/apalache/tla/tooling/opt/*.scala`](https://github.com/apalache-mc/apalache/tree/main/mod-tool/src/main/scala/at/forsyte/apalache/tla/tooling/opt). Apalache is the TLA+ model checker (symbolic + bounded) from Informal Systems — companion to TLC which is already covered as `tlc`. Ships as both `apalache-mc` (primary binary name; wrapper script) and `apalache` (older alias for the same binary). **Built on the `org.backuity.clist` Scala CLI parser** (NOT scopt or scallop) — `opt[T](name = "<long>", description = "<help>", default = ...)` declarations decoded source-direct. **2 reusable helpers**: **`_apalache_base_opts`** (8 base flags from `ApalacheCommand.scala` applied to ALL subcommands via Scala inheritance: `--config-file`, `--debug`, `--smtprof`, `--profiling`, `--out-dir`, `--run-dir`, `--write-intermediate`, `--features`); **`_apalache_checker_opts`** (7 model-checking flags from `AbstractCheckerCmd.scala` applied to the `check`/`simulate`/`test` subcommands that extend it: `--cinit`, `--config`, `--init`, `--inv`, `--length`, `--next`, `--temporal`). **9-subcommand dispatch** decoded from the 8 `*Cmd.scala` source files (AbstractCheckerCmd is a base class, not a subcommand): `check` (bounded symbolic model check; 11 subcommand-specific flags + base + checker helpers — including `--algo` 3-way enum `offline`/`incremental`/`remote`, `--smt-encoding` 3-way enum `oopsla19`/`arrays`/`funArrays`, `--smt-solver` 2-way enum `z3`/`cvc5`, `--discard-disabled`, `--no-deadlock`, `--max-error N`, `--view`, `--output-traces`, `--timeout-smt`, `--tuning-options`, `--tuning-options-file`); `simulate` (randomized symbolic simulation; `--max-run` + checker helpers); `test` (TLA+ test runner; `--action`/`--assertion`/`--before` + checker helpers); `parse` (parse-only; `--output`); `typecheck` (type-check-only; `--infer-poly` + `--output`); `transpile` (compile-to-intermediate); `tracee` (replay counter-example trace; `--expressions`); `server` (run as language server; no subcommand-specific flags); `config` (effective-config inspector; `--enable-stats` bool). Uses `_arguments -C` + state-handler dispatch for per-subcommand argument completion. **File `_alectryon` (1-stem)** from [`cpitclaudel/alectryon/alectryon/cli.py`](https://github.com/cpitclaudel/alectryon/blob/master/alectryon/cli.py) argparse `parser.add_argument(...)` calls grouped via `parser.add_argument_group(...)` blocks (Input config, Output config, Cache, HTML output, LaTeX output, Coq subprocess args, leanInk subprocess args, Warnings, Debug — 9 argument groups in upstream). Coq/Lean4 literate-programming and proof-state visualization tool that converts annotated Coq (Rocq) sources into HTML or LaTeX with interactive expandable proof-state goals at each step. **~30 flags** including: `--frontend` enum (11 entries: `coq`/`coq+rst`/`coqdoc`/`rst`/`lean3`/`lean3+rst`/`lean4`/`lean4+rst`/`dafny`/`dafny+rst`/`json` decoded from upstream `FRONTEND_CHOICES = sorted(PIPELINES.keys())`); `--backend` enum (12 entries: `html`/`latex`/`html-snippets`/etc decoded from `BACKEND_CHOICES = sorted(set(b for _, bs in PIPELINES.items() for b in bs))`); 3-way `--cache-compression` enum (`none`/`gz`/`xz`); 4-way `--webpage-style` enum (`centered`/`floating`/`windowed`/`plain`); `--html-dialect` enum (`html4`/`html5`); `--latex-dialect` enum (`pdflatex`/`xelatex`/`lualatex`); repeatable Coq-subprocess passthroughs `*--sertop-arg`, `*--rocq-arg` (with `--coqc-arg` synonym), and Coq load-path family `-I`/`--ml-include-path`, `-Q`/`--load-path`, `-R`/`--rec-load-path`. **Hunt-skip log this round**: `metamath-knife` (upstream `metamath/metamath-knife` repo exists per `gh search repos` but `gh api repos/metamath/metamath-knife/contents` returned 404 — likely a private/archived state; deferred until canonical URL stabilizes), `metamath` (the C-based `metamath/metamath-exe` repo exists with `src/metamath.c` entrypoint but uses a hand-rolled interactive REPL command parser via `cmdInput()`, not an argv-options flag set — would need significant subcommand-mode reverse engineering, deferred), `hol4`/`hol-light` (interactive REPL environments, no argv-flag surface), `formality`/`nuprl` (research languages with minimal CLIs), `tla2tools` (the canonical TLA+ tooling shows JAR/classpath invocation patterns; needs separate audit), `apalache` was previously misidentified as having existing coverage but actual `more_src/_apalache` was missing on disk per audit-time `more=0 stock=0 multi=0` check — that count was a pre-write false negative since the file we just wrote DID become `more=1`/`multi=1`. **Blacklist additions (3)**: 1 entry in `a*` (alectryon) + 2 in `a*` (apalache, apalache-mc). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 144th consecutive clean round.
- **OSGeo GDAL utility tail R138 — 5-stem multi-binary harvest** (`#compdef gdal_pansharpen gdal_retile gdalcompare gnmanalyse gnmmanage`). Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`; pre-write dup-check identified 22 GDAL utility binaries already in the corpus (`gdalinfo`, `gdal_translate`, `gdalwarp`, `ogr2ogr`, `gdal_grid`, `gdaladdo`, `gdalbuildvrt`, `gdaldem`, `gdal_contour`, `gdal_calc`, `gdal_merge`, `gdal_polygonize`, `gdal_proximity`, `gdal_sieve`, `gdal_viewshed`, `gdal_create`, `ogrinfo`, `ogrtindex`, `gdalsrsinfo`, `gdalmanage`, `gdaltransform`, `gdal_rasterize`), so this round fills the 5 GDAL utility binaries not previously covered. Surface extracted source-direct from upstream [`OSGeo/gdal`](https://github.com/OSGeo/gdal) via `gh api`. The 3 Python utilities (`gdal_pansharpen`, `gdal_retile`, `gdalcompare`) extracted from [`swig/python/gdal-utils/osgeo_utils/{gdal_pansharpen,gdal_retile,gdalcompare}.py`](https://github.com/OSGeo/gdal/tree/master/swig/python/gdal-utils/osgeo_utils) `def Usage(isError):` body `print(..., file=f)` blocks. The 2 C++ network utilities (`gnmanalyse`, `gnmmanage`) extracted from [`apps/gnmanalyse.cpp`](https://github.com/OSGeo/gdal/blob/master/apps/gnmanalyse.cpp) + [`apps/gnmmanage.cpp`](https://github.com/OSGeo/gdal/blob/master/apps/gnmmanage.cpp) `static void Usage(...)` C function `fprintf(..., "Usage: <prog> ...")` blocks. **Notable extraction technique**: `gdalcompare` uses a hand-rolled `for i in range(argc): if argv[i] == "-X"` loop (NOT argparse) for option parsing, so the 9-flag set was cross-verified by reading BOTH the `Usage()` output AND the argv-walking `elif argv[i] == "-X"` chain in `main()` to ensure no flag was documented but unimplemented (or vice-versa). **2 reusable helpers**: **`_gdal_common_help`** (`--help` + `--help-general` shared by all 5 binaries via GDAL's common args processing) and **`_gdal_resampling`** (the 5-entry `near`/`bilinear`/`cubic`/`cubicspline`/`lanczos` resampling enum shared by `gdal_retile` and `gdal_pansharpen`; pansharpen adds `average` as a 6th entry). **Notable per-binary surfaces**: **gdal_pansharpen** (14 flags + variadic positional; creates a pansharpened dataset by using a panchromatic band to enhance the spatial resolution of a multispectral dataset; repeatable `-b` band selection + `-w` per-spectral-band weight; `-spat_adjust` enum `union`/`intersection`/`none`/`nonewithoutwarning`; `-threads` enum `ALL_CPUS` literal + custom number). **gdal_retile** (18 flags + variadic positional; tiles a raster into a directory of fixed-size tiles with optional pyramid generation; **12-entry `-ot` data-type enum** `Byte`/`Int8`/`Int16`/`UInt16`/`UInt32`/`Int32`/`Float32`/`Float64`/`CInt16`/`CInt32`/`CFloat32`/`CFloat64`; `-pyramidOnly`/`-useDirForEachRow`/`-resume` 3-way independent toggles; `-levels` is REQUIRED per upstream Usage). **gdalcompare** (9 flags + 2 positionals; compares 2 GDAL datasets for regression-test diffing; **7 `-skip_*` independent skip-category toggles** `binary`/`overviews`/`geolocation`/`geotransform`/`metadata`/`rpc`/`srs` + `-dumpdiffs` + `-sds`). **gnmanalyse** (10 flags + variadic positional; Geographic Network Model network-analysis utility — shortest-path / k-shortest-paths / resource analysis; 3-mode operation enum `dijkstra`/`kpaths`/`resource`). **gnmmanage** (13 flags + variadic positional; GNM network management — create / import / connect / disconnect / rule / autoconnect / delete / change; 9-mode operation enum). The 2 C++ network utilities share **`-q`/`-quiet`** synonym pair (per upstream `Usage:` line `[-q][-quiet]`) plus `--long-usage` (full per-subcommand help). Both also share **`-dsco`/`-lco`** dataset/layer creation-option repeatables shared with the broader GDAL command family. **Hunt-skip log this round**: `gdalcubes` (initially flagged as fresh from my GDAL audit, but pre-write inspection showed it is an [R/Rcpp package](https://github.com/appelmar/gdalcubes/tree/master/src) rather than a standalone CLI binary — `Makevars.in`/`RcppExports.cpp` give it away; the actual user-facing interface is the `gdalcubes` R package loaded into the R interpreter, not a shell-invokable binary), the broader carryforward queue (`forgejo-runner`, `gitea/act_runner`, `cuelang`, `proton`, `trpc`, `spiceai`, `zng`, `astro-cli`, AleoNet — still pending canonical-URL discovery). **Blacklist additions (5)**: 3 entries in `gdal_*`/`gdalc*` range + 2 in `gnm*` range. LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 143rd consecutive clean round.
- **Mixed-domain sweep R137 — 3 files / 3 binaries** (`_signoz` + `_swift-doc` + `_presenterm-export`). Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`. Pivot to observability + Apple toolchain + tooling. Surface extracted source-direct from 3 distinct upstreams via `gh api`. **File `_signoz` (1-stem with nested 3-level Cobra dispatch)** from [`SigNoz/signoz`](https://github.com/SigNoz/signoz) [`cmd/root.go`](https://github.com/SigNoz/signoz/blob/main/cmd/root.go) (Cobra `RootCmd`) + [`cmd/community/main.go`](https://github.com/SigNoz/signoz/blob/main/cmd/community/main.go) (`registerServer(...)` + `cmd.RegisterGenerate(...)` + `cmd.RegisterMetastore(...)` calls) + each subcommand source file (`cmd/{server,genconfig,openapi,authz,metastore}.go`) `Use:` strings. SigNoz is an open-source observability platform built on OpenTelemetry for logs/metrics/traces in a single pane. **3-level subcommand tree** decoded source-direct: top-level `server | generate | metastore`, then `generate {config | web-settings | openapi | authz}` (4 generators), then `metastore migrate {sync | up | check}` (3 migration modes). Uses 3 nested state-handler dispatches in `_arguments -C` style: `state=subcmd → state=metasub` for the deepest level. **server** subcommand has `*--config=` repeatable flag (per upstream Cobra `StringArrayVar` + the help text "can be specified multiple times, later files override earlier ones"). **File `_swift-doc` (1-stem with 3-subcommand dispatch)** from [`SwiftDocOrg/swift-doc`](https://github.com/SwiftDocOrg/swift-doc) [`Sources/swift-doc/main.swift`](https://github.com/SwiftDocOrg/swift-doc/blob/master/Sources/swift-doc/main.swift) `SwiftDoc: ParsableCommand` with `subcommands: [Generate.self, Coverage.self, Diagram.self]` declaration + the 3 [`Subcommands/{Generate,Coverage,Diagram}.swift`](https://github.com/SwiftDocOrg/swift-doc/tree/master/Sources/swift-doc/Subcommands) source files (each is a swift-argument-parser `ParsableArguments` struct with `@Option`/`@Argument`/`@Flag` property wrappers). **Notable upstream-source quirk** caught: the `CommandConfiguration.commandName = "swift doc"` (with a space) per main.swift means the tool ALSO installs as a `swift package` subcommand invoked as `swift doc`, but the standalone `swift-doc` executable is what this completion file covers (zsh's completion system can't intercept multi-word commands like `swift doc` via standard `#compdef`). **Per-subcommand surface**: **generate** has `-n`/`--module-name`, `-o`/`--output`, `-f`/`--format html|commonmark`, `--base-url`, `--minimum-access-level private|fileprivate|internal|public|open`. **coverage** has `-o`/`--output` + the same `--minimum-access-level` enum. **diagram** has only `--minimum-access-level` + the variadic input directories positional. All 3 subcommands share the `inputs: [String]` variadic Swift-Argument-Parser `@Argument` for directories containing Swift files. **File `_presenterm-export` (1-stem)** from [`mfontanini/presenterm-export`](https://github.com/mfontanini/presenterm-export) [`pyproject.toml`](https://github.com/mfontanini/presenterm-export/blob/master/pyproject.toml) `[project.scripts] presenterm-export = "presenterm_export.cli:main"` entry-point declaration + [`presenterm_export/cli.py`](https://github.com/mfontanini/presenterm-export/blob/master/presenterm_export/cli.py) argparse `parser.add_argument(...)` calls. Companion tool to the already-covered `presenterm` terminal presentation viewer; captures the terminal output of a presenterm session via libtmux + tmux-driven capture and exports to PDF. Minimal surface: only 2 flags (`--emit-intermediate` for testing/debugging + `--version`) + a REMAINDER positional decoded from argparse `nargs=REMAINDER` for the wrapped presenterm command arguments. **Hunt-skip log this round**: `daft-cli` (the Cargo crate `name = "daft-cli"` exists, but its `python.rs` shows it's pyo3-wrapped and registered as a Python function — there is no standalone `daft-cli` binary that ships separately; the `daft` Python entry point is the actual user-facing CLI; deferred for separate audit since the `daft` Python tool also exists but needs its own dup check + interrogation), `mcp-grafana` (initially flagged as fresh from the Grafana MCP audit, but pre-write dup-check showed `more=1 multi=1` so it's already in the corpus; example of an audit-stage false positive caught before file creation), `forgejo-runner`/`gitea-runner` (carryforward — canonical URLs still 404), `signoz-cli` (no separate signoz-cli binary exists; the `signoz` Cobra binary is the canonical CLI). **Blacklist additions (3)**: 1 entry in `p*` + 2 entries in `s*` ranges. LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 142nd consecutive clean round.
- **Mixed-domain sweep R136 — 4 files / 7 binaries** (`_git-of-theseus-analyze` 4-stem + `_git-recall` 1-stem + `_passhole` 1-stem + `_rage-mount` 1-stem). Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`. Surface extracted source-direct from 4 distinct upstream repos via `gh api`. **File `_git-of-theseus-analyze` (4-stem `#compdef git-of-theseus-analyze git-of-theseus-line-plot git-of-theseus-stack-plot git-of-theseus-survival-plot`)** from [`erikbern/git-of-theseus/git_of_theseus/{analyze,line_plot,stack_plot,survival_plot}.py`](https://github.com/erikbern/git-of-theseus/tree/master/git_of_theseus) argparse `parser.add_argument(...)` calls + [`setup.py`](https://github.com/erikbern/git-of-theseus/blob/master/setup.py) `entry_points.console_scripts` mapping (4 entries: `git-of-theseus-analyze` → `analyze:analyze_cmdline`, then `git-of-theseus-{line,stack,survival}-plot` → `:{line,stack,survival}_plot_cmdline`). Erik Bernhardsson's code-survival analysis suite that measures how much of a repo's code remains from each cohort (year/quarter/etc.) of contributors, popularized by his "The half-life of code" blog post. Pipeline is `analyze → JSON files → plot`. Reusable helper `_theseus_plot_common` factors the 2-flag `--display` + `--outfile` pair shared by all 3 plotter binaries. **git-of-theseus-analyze** is the heaviest stage (11 flags + 1 positional): `--cohortfm` (Python datetime format), `--interval` (default 604800=1 week), repeatable `--ignore` and `--only` glob filters, `--outdir`, `--branch` (default `master`), `--ignore-whitespace`, `--all-filetypes`, `--quiet`, `--procs` (default 2), `--opt` (generate commit-graph; `~80KB/kCommit` disk overhead per upstream help text). The 3 plot variants have small per-binary additions: `--max-n` + `--normalize` on line/stack; `--exp-fit` + `--years` (default 5) on survival. **File `_git-recall` (1-stem)** from [`Fakerr/git-recall`](https://github.com/Fakerr/git-recall) — this is a single bash script (NOT a binary; decoded from `#!/usr/bin/env bash` shebang). Option set extracted from the `usage()` heredoc in the script body — 7 flags: `-d`/`--date`, `-a`/`--author` (use `"all"` for all users per upstream), `-b`/`--branch`, `-p`/`--path`, `-f`/`--fetch`, `-h`/`--help`, `-v`/`--version`. Invoked as a `git recall` subcommand for interactive recent-commit browsing. **File `_passhole` (1-stem with full subcommand state-dispatch)** from [`Evidlo/passhole/passhole/passhole.py`](https://github.com/Evidlo/passhole/blob/master/passhole/passhole.py) argparse `subparsers.add_parser(...)` calls. Python KeePass database manipulator with a `pass(1)`-like CLI but backed by a KeePass kdbx file instead of GPG-encrypted text files. **14 subcommands + 3 short aliases** decoded source-direct (`list`/`ls`, `add`, `remove`/`rm`, `move`/`mv`, `show`, `edit`, `type`, `init`, `grep`, `eval`, `dump`, `info`, `kill`, `restart`). **Crucial anti-dup catch**: the short alias `ph` ALSO ships from this same Python module per the original audit, but **intentionally omitted from the `#compdef` line** because `ph` is already covered as a different binary (the Pharo Smalltalk VM launcher) elsewhere in the corpus (`multi=2` per pre-write dup-check). The completion uses the standard `_arguments -C` + state-handler dispatch pattern with per-subcommand flag completion for all 14 subcommands (e.g. `add` has the 3-way mutex `-w`/`-a`/`-s` password-style flags with `length` defaults extracted from `nargs='?', const=N`). **File `_rage-mount` (1-stem)** from [`str4d/rage/rage/src/bin/rage-mount/cli.rs`](https://github.com/str4d/rage/blob/main/rage/src/bin/rage-mount/cli.rs) clap-derive `AgeMountOptions` struct. FUSE mount of an age-encrypted tar or zip archive as a read-only filesystem. Sister to `rage` + `rage-keygen` already in the corpus. **Notable surface quirks** caught from clap source: `-t`/`--types` archive-type enum (`tar`/`zip`); repeatable `-i`/`--identity` for multiple decryption keys (clap `Vec<String>`); `--max-work-factor WF` for scrypt DoS-mitigation on bad passphrases; clap's `#[command(disable_version_flag(true))]` shows the explicit `-V`/`--version` manual flag (vs clap's default `-V` auto-version). Help-text strings are internationalized via `fl!()` calls on the upstream side so descriptions in the completion summarize upstream behavior rather than echoing the source verbatim. **Hunt-skip log this round**: `forgejo-runner` (upstream `forgejo/runner` repo not accessible via `gh api`; only third-party packaging repos visible in `gh search repos`), `gitea-runner` / `gitea/act_runner` (404 at expected path; needs canonical URL discovery), `toxiproxy-client` (only 2 of 3 fresh-looking names were real — `toxiproxy-cli` + `toxiproxy-server` already in corpus, no separate `toxiproxy-client` binary in `Shopify/toxiproxy/cmd/`), `cuelang`/`proton`/`trpc`/`spiceai`/`zng` (carryforward — still no canonical repos identified), `astro-cli` / Astronomer Go CLI (deferred), AleoNet/snarkOS (404 carryforward). **Blacklist additions (7)**: 5 entries in `git-*` range + 1 in `p*` (passhole) + 1 in `r*` (rage-mount). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 141st consecutive clean round.
- **go-jsonnet aux + Bytecode Alliance wit-deps R135 — 2 files / 4 binaries** (`_dumpstdlibast` 3-stem + `_wit-deps` 1-stem). Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`. Surface extracted source-direct via `gh api`. **File `_dumpstdlibast` (3-stem `#compdef dumpstdlibast jsonnet-deps jsonnet-lint`)** from [`google/go-jsonnet/cmd/{dumpstdlibast,jsonnet-deps,jsonnet-lint}`](https://github.com/google/go-jsonnet/tree/master/cmd). Crucial disambiguation: these are NOT the C++ jsonnet binaries (the canonical `jsonnet`/`jsonnetfmt` from google/jsonnet are already covered in earlier rounds); these are the **Go port's auxiliary binaries** that ship alongside the Go ports of jsonnet/jsonnetfmt. Both `jsonnet-deps` and `jsonnet-lint` use a hand-rolled `processArgs(...)` loop walking `os.Args` directly (NOT `flag.Var` or cobra), so the option set was extracted by reading the `usage()` function bodies in each `cmd.go` and matching `arg == "-X" || arg == "--Y"` chains — yielding 3 shared flags `-h`/`--help`, `-v`/`--version`, `-J`/`--jpath` (repeatable, right-most wins per the help text), plus the `-o`/`--output-file` flag unique to `jsonnet-deps`. The `dumpstdlibast` binary takes a single positional file path with no flags, per source guard `if len(os.Args) != 2 { fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "usage: %s <file>\n", ...); os.Exit(2); }`. Reusable helper `_jsonnet_aux_common` factors the 3 shared flags between jsonnet-deps and jsonnet-lint. **File `_wit-deps` (1-stem)** from [`bytecodealliance/wit-deps/src/bin/wit-deps/main.rs`](https://github.com/bytecodealliance/wit-deps/blob/main/src/bin/wit-deps/main.rs) clap-derive `Cli` struct + 3 `#[derive(Subcommand)]` enum variants. WebAssembly WIT (Component-Model interface description) language dependency manager — the equivalent of cargo/npm/go-mod for WIT packages. **3 root-level flags** with hardcoded `default_value` extracted from clap attributes: `-d`/`--deps` (default `wit/deps`), `-m`/`--manifest` (default `wit/deps.toml`), `-l`/`--lock` (default `wit/deps.lock`). **3 subcommands** decoded from the `#[derive(Subcommand)] enum Command` body: **lock** (1 flag `-c`/`--check` — exits non-zero if dependencies were not already in-sync; CI-friendly drift detection), **update** (no flags; updates to latest versions allowed by manifest), **tar** (1 flag `-o`/`--output` to write to a file + 1 positional `package: Identifier` for the package to archive; writes a deterministic tar of the package's `wit` subdirectory to stdout otherwise). Uses the dispatch-via-state pattern with `_arguments -C` + `state` handler for per-subcommand argument completion. **Hunt-skip log this round**: `krelay` (canonical binary names are `kubectl-relay` for the client — already covered in the corpus — and a Docker-image-only `krelay-server` that runs in-cluster and is not typically invoked from a shell; the bare `krelay` name doesn't ship as a binary), `toolbox-create`/`toolbox-rm`/`toolbox-enter`/`toolbox-list`/`toolbox-run`/`toolbox-init` (these are NOT separate binaries — they are cobra subcommands of the `toolbox` binary which is already covered as multi=2; the dup-check false-positive was caught by reading [`containers/toolbox/src/cmd/`](https://github.com/containers/toolbox/tree/main/src/cmd) which shows them as `create.go`/`rm.go`/etc subcommand files under a single Cobra root), `bevy-cli` (the upstream `TheBevyFlock/bevy_cli/Cargo.toml` `[[bin]] name = "bevy"` shows the installed binary is `bevy` not `bevy-cli` — already covered as `bevy`), `astro-cli` / Astronomer (Go CLI; deferred for separate audit), `pkl-cli` (deferred), `cuelang`/`proton`/`trpc`/`spiceai`/`zng` (need per-tool repo discovery). **Blacklist additions (4)**: 1 entry in `d*` + 2 in `j*` + 1 in `w*` ranges. LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 140th consecutive clean round.
- **AWS mountpoint-s3 R134 — 3-stem multi-binary harvest** (`#compdef mock-mount-s3 mount-s3 mount-s3-log-analyzer`) (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`). Pivot to AWS infrastructure tooling. Surface extracted source-direct from upstream [`awslabs/mountpoint-s3`](https://github.com/awslabs/mountpoint-s3) via `gh api`: [`mountpoint-s3/src/cli.rs`](https://github.com/awslabs/mountpoint-s3/blob/main/mountpoint-s3/src/cli.rs) clap-derive `CliArgs` struct (used by BOTH `mount-s3` AND `mock-mount-s3` — the mock binary literally does `use mountpoint_s3::CliArgs; let cli_args = CliArgs::parse();` per upstream `mountpoint-s3/src/bin/mock-mount-s3.rs:23`, so its flag surface is identical to mount-s3 but the runtime uses an in-memory mock S3 backend with an `sthree-` bucket-name safety prefix) + [`mountpoint-s3/src/bin/mount-s3-log-analyzer.rs`](https://github.com/awslabs/mountpoint-s3/blob/main/mountpoint-s3/src/bin/mount-s3-log-analyzer.rs) separate small CliArgs. **Coverage**: 3-stem multi-binary harvest of the AWS mountpoint-s3 FUSE filesystem CLI family. mountpoint-s3 (Cargo `name = "mount-s3"`) mounts an S3 bucket as a POSIX filesystem with clap-derive options grouped into 7 help-headers (`BUCKET_OPTIONS`, `AWS_CREDENTIALS_OPTIONS`, `MOUNT_OPTIONS`, `CLIENT_OPTIONS`, `CACHING_OPTIONS`, `LOGGING_OPTIONS`, `ADVANCED_OPTIONS`). **`_ms3_shared_args` helper** factors the **40-flag CliArgs surface** shared between `mount-s3` + `mock-mount-s3` so the multi-stem stays maintainable. Notable mutex/dependency relations extracted source-direct from clap-derive `conflicts_with` and `requires` attributes: **2-way mutex `--allow-root`/`--allow-other`** (clap `conflicts_with = "allow_root"` on `--allow-other`); **3-way mutex `--part-size` xor (`--read-part-size` + `--write-part-size`)** (both read/write part-size variants have clap `conflicts_with = "part_size"`); **5-way clap_conflicts_with_all mutex `--no-log` against `--log-directory`/`--debug`/`--debug-crt`/`--log-metrics`** (decoded from `conflicts_with_all(["log_directory", "debug", "debug_crt", "log_metrics"])`); **2-way requires-pair `--otlp-export-interval` requires `--otlp-endpoint`** (decoded from `requires = "otlp_endpoint"`); cache flags have `requires = "cache"` / `requires = "cache_group"` chain. **Storage-class autocomplete enum** (8 entries: `STANDARD`/`STANDARD_IA`/`INTELLIGENT_TIERING`/`ONEZONE_IA`/`GLACIER_IR`/`GLACIER`/`DEEP_ARCHIVE`/`REDUCED_REDUNDANCY`); **bucket-type enum** (2 entries: `general-purpose`/`directory`); **SSE algorithm enum** (3 entries: `aws:kms`/`aws:kms:dsse`/`AES256`); **upload-checksums enum** (4 entries: `SHA1`/`SHA256`/`CRC32`/`CRC32C`). **mock-mount-s3** carries the same 40 flags + same positionals; only the bucket-name semantics differ (must begin with `sthree-` per upstream safety check). **mount-s3-log-analyzer** has its own 2-flag `requires`-pair CliArgs (`--mem-limit-mib` requires `--extra-metrics-out` and vice-versa) + 3 positionals (log_dir/out_file/test_name). **Hunt-skip log this round**: `zoekt-mirror-perforce` (initially flagged as fresh from a `sourcegraph/zoekt/contents/cmd` listing but the actual `cmd/zoekt-mirror-perforce/` directory returns 404 on direct lookup — the listing was a false positive; not a real binary in current zoekt master), Astronomer `astro-cli` (Go CLI; deferred for separate audit), Bytecode Alliance `wit-deps` v0.6.0 (single binary; deferred), Apple `pkl-cli` (separate from `pkl`; deferred), AleoNet/snarkOS (404 at all attempted endpoints; deferred until canonical URL identified), most modern frontend tools (`cuelang`/`proton`/`trpc`/`spiceai`/`zng` etc. — exist as separate names but need per-tool repo discovery before audit), various blockchain CLIs (`iota`/`celo-cli`/`mina-cli` — deferred). **Blacklist additions (3)**: 3 entries in `m*` range (mock-mount-s3, mount-s3, mount-s3-log-analyzer). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 139th consecutive clean round.
- **StarkWare Cairo + Starknet R133 — 11-stem multi-binary harvest** (`#compdef cairo-execute cairo-format cairo-run cairo-size-profiler cairo-test generate-syntax sierra-compile starknet-compile starknet-sierra-compile starknet-sierra-extract-code starknet-sierra-upgrade-validate`) (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`; `cairo-compile` was already covered in an earlier round and is deliberately omitted from this `#compdef` line). Pivot from init-system territory (R117-R132) to ZK-rollup / Layer-2 smart-contract tooling. Surface extracted source-direct from upstream [`starkware-libs/cairo/crates/bin/<name>/src/main.rs`](https://github.com/starkware-libs/cairo/tree/main/crates/bin) clap-derive `Args` (or `Cli`) struct definitions + `#[arg(...)]` macro attributes via `gh api`. Cairo is the Rust implementation of the Cairo language + Starknet smart-contract compiler stack maintained by StarkWare; the 11 binaries here ship from the same monorepo and use a consistent clap-derive `#[command(version, verbatim_doc_comment)]` style where doc-comments on each `#[arg]` field carry through as completion descriptions. **2 reusable helpers**: **`_cairo_common`** (the `(-s --single-file)` + `--allow-warnings` pair shared by 6 of the `cairo-*` binaries — extracted from their identical `Args` struct definitions); **`_starknet_libfuncs`** (the `--allowed-libfuncs-list-name` xor `--allowed-libfuncs-list-file` mutex pair shared by 3 of the `starknet-*` binaries — clap-derive enforces the mutex via `conflicts_with = "allowed_libfuncs_list_file"` on one side). **Notable per-binary surfaces**: **cairo-execute** (15 flags; the newer prover-friendly compile-and-run path; `--build-only` + `--output-path` group with extensive `conflicts_with = "prebuilt"` mutex enforcement on 7 other flags decoded from clap attributes; `--layout` enum autocomplete `plain`/`small`/`dex`/`recursive`/`starknet`/`starknet_with_keccak`/`recursive_large_output`/`all_cairo`/`all_cairo_stwo`/`all_solidity`/`dynamic`; `-r`/`--run-profiler` enum `none`/`cairo`/`sierra`). **cairo-run** (the older interpreter path; 5 flags + 1 positional; `--available-gas` + `--print-full-memory`). **cairo-test** (8 flags; `-f`/`--filter` filter-by-name + `--include-ignored` and **mutex** `--include-ignored` xor `--ignored` for ignored-test handling + `--starknet` to load the Starknet plugin so tests can use Starknet syscalls). **cairo-format** (10 flags; check + recursive + verbose + 5 long-only formatting-style flags with autocomplete enums `line-by-line`/`same-line`/`single-break-point` decoded from the multiline doc-comments in the `Args` struct). **cairo-size-profiler** (4 flags + 1 positional; **2-way mutex `--contract`/`--executable`** for which entity to size-analyze, decoded from `conflicts_with = "executable"` / `conflicts_with = "contract"` clap attributes). **sierra-compile** (2 positionals + no flags; minimal Sierra IR → CASM converter). **starknet-compile** (8 flags + 2 positionals; emits Starknet ContractClass JSON from a Cairo contract crate). **starknet-sierra-compile** (4 flags + 2 positionals; further compiles the Sierra ContractClass JSON to CASM ContractClass; `--add-pythonic-hints` for the Python-runner integration + `--max-bytecode-size 180000` default). **starknet-sierra-extract-code** (2 positionals; extract Sierra program from a ContractClass JSON; no flags besides version). **starknet-sierra-upgrade-validate** (9 flags + variadic positional; validates that Sierra contracts remain compilable after a Sierra version bump; **2-way input-source mutex** input class-info files (variadic positional) xor `--fullnode-url` RPC-fetch mode, decoded from clap-derive `required_unless_present = "fullnode_url"` + `conflicts_with_all`; the RPC-mode further has its own clap-derive `ArgGroup::new("range").multiple(true).conflicts_with("last_n_blocks")` for block-range vs last-N-blocks selection). **generate-syntax** (no CLI args — internal codegen tool that regenerates Cairo's syntax-tree node definitions at build time using `cairo_lang_syntax_codegen::generator::get_codes()`). **Hunt-skip log this round**: AleoNet (Aleo zero-knowledge CLI — repo path 404 at `AleoHQ/aleo` + `AleoNet/snarkOS` releases endpoint also 404; needs source URL discovery), Astronomer Astro CLI (`astronomer/astro-cli` has releases at `v1.42.1`; large Go CLI — deferred for separate audit), `wit-deps` from Bytecode Alliance (single binary; deferred), `pkl-cli` (Apple's Pkl language has separate CLI binary distinct from `pkl` — deferred), `iota`/`celo-cli`/`mina-cli`/`starknet` master binary (different from `starknet-compile`; these blockchain CLIs need per-project repo discovery first). **Blacklist additions (11)**: 6 entries in `cairo-*` range + 1 in `g*` (generate-syntax) + 1 in `s*` (sierra-compile) + 4 in `starknet-*` range. LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 138th consecutive clean round.
- **jdebp nosh master CLIs R132 — 5 files / 8 binaries** (`_system-control` 2-stem + `_console-ncurses-realizer` 2-stem + `_ptybandage` 2-stem + `_dbus-daemon-launch-helper` 1-stem + `_emergency-login` 1-stem). Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`; per-binary audit identified `exec` (stock zsh) + `getty` (covered) + `telinit` (covered) as the previously-covered nosh main commands, leaving 8 fresh. Surface extracted source-direct from upstream [`jdebp/nosh/source/*.cpp`](https://github.com/jdebp/nosh/tree/master/source) via `gh api` — popt::definition declaration tables + builtins-*.cpp forward-declaration lists + shell-script body inspection. **About the upstream**: nosh is Jonathan de Boyne Pollard's clean-room reimplementation of an entire init/supervision/service-management stack across Linux+BSDs; the source repo has **668 .cpp files producing ~200 binaries** (most as multicall aliases to a single `exec` binary listed in 194 `package/aliases1` entries). This round targets only the **11 master commands** from `package/commands1` (`exec`, `system-control`, `console-ncurses-realizer`, `console-terminal-emulator`, `emergency-login`, `getty`, `ptybandage`, `ptyrun`, `dbus-daemon-launch-helper`) + `package/commands8` (`telinit`, `system-manager`); the remaining 194 multicall aliases — including names like `find`/`chroot`/`false`/`enable`/`disable`/`chdir`/`foreground` that clash with shell builtins, system commands, or stock zsh — are deferred indefinitely as the audit overhead outweighs the per-name completion value. **File `_system-control` (2-stem `#compdef system-control system-manager`)** with reusable helper `_nosh_systemctl_compat` factoring the 5 `--full`/`--no-legend`/`--no-pager`/`--no-reload`/`--quite` (sic — typo preserved per upstream `popt::bool_definition quiet_option('\0', "quite", ...)`) systemctl-compatibility no-op flags. **system-control** offers a **76-subcommand autocomplete enum** extracted source-direct from `builtins-system-control.cpp` `extern void <subcmd> ( ... )` forward-declaration table (literally: every subcommand from `activate`/`builtins`/`cat`/`chkservice`/... through `write-volume-service-bundles` — covering systemctl-style verbs, daemontools `sv*` aliases, sysvinit runlevel verbs, and nosh-specific service-control verbs in a single autocomplete list). **system-manager** (the nosh pid 1 binary) uses argv[0] multicall dispatch into the SAME compiled binary as system-control but a DIFFERENT 17-subcommand `builtins-system-manager.cpp` table (no enable/disable, primary verbs are `init`/`activate`/`isolate`/`start` + runlevel aliases `emergency`/`rescue`/`normal`/`systemd`). **File `_console-ncurses-realizer` (2-stem `#compdef console-ncurses-realizer console-terminal-emulator`)** — nosh user-mode console framework alternative to the kernel virtual-terminal driver. **console-terminal-emulator** parses ECMA-48 escape sequences from a child program into a memory buffer (2 flags: `--vcsa` Linux vcsa-compatible buffer + `--inverted` initial inverted mode). **console-ncurses-realizer** renders that buffer to ncurses in an existing terminal (2 flags: `--wrong-way-up` bottom-to-top display + `--display-only` no-input-forwarding). Both binaries use jdebp's popt-style C++ framework where ALL flags are long-only — extracted from `popt::bool_definition <var>_option('\0', "<long>", ...)` declarations (note the `'\0'` short-form slot = no short alias). **File `_ptybandage` (2-stem `#compdef ptybandage ptyrun`)** — caught at extraction time that these are NOT C++ binaries but **5-line execline shell scripts** (`#!/command/execlineb -S0` shebang). Body: `pty-allocate / pty-run [-t] / setsid / open-controlling-tty --exclusive -- / $@`. ptybandage adds `-t` to its inner `pty-run` (tty allocation marker), ptyrun does not. Both wrap a program in a fresh pty pair where the program thinks it has a controlling tty — useful for nosh service definitions for programs that misbehave when not attached to a tty. **File `_dbus-daemon-launch-helper` (1-stem)** — caught as `#!/bin/sh -e` script (NOT a binary; NOT setuid — per upstream source comment `# Note: This script does NOT need to be set-UID/set-GID to anything`). Dispatches on `$DBUS_STARTER_BUS_TYPE` between system and session bus startup with no CLI flags or positionals. **File `_emergency-login` (1-stem)** — statically-linked stripped-down system-control variant with only 3 builtin subcommands (`builtins`, `emergency-login`, `version`) extracted from `builtins-emergency-login.cpp` forward decls. Spawned by system-manager as a last-resort recovery shell when normal getty + login fail. **Hunt-skip log this round**: the 194 nosh multicall aliases not in the 11 master command list (would require careful per-name shell-builtin/stock-zsh clash audit before adding — deferred indefinitely), nosh's `package/aliases7` and `package/aliases8` (more domain-specific subsets — needs separate audit), the remaining alt-init-system targets from R131 hunt-skip log (perp, daemontools-encore, minit — still unsourced). **Blacklist additions (8)**: 8 entries distributed across `c*`/`d*`/`e*`/`p*`/`s*` ranges. LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 137th consecutive clean round.
- **Alt-init-system sweep R131 — 4 files / 8 binaries** (`_dinit-check` 2-stem + `_fghack` 3-stem + `_finit` 1-stem + `_runit-init` 2-stem). Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`; per-binary audits identified the previously-covered binaries and excluded them. Surface extracted source-direct from 4 distinct upstream repos via `gh api`. **File `_dinit-check` (2-stem `#compdef dinit-check dinit-monitor`)** from [`davmac314/dinit/src/dinit-check.cc`](https://github.com/davmac314/dinit/blob/master/src/dinit-check.cc) + [`dinit-monitor.cc`](https://github.com/davmac314/dinit/blob/master/src/dinit-monitor.cc) `strcmp()` argv-walking option chains (dinit uses a custom argv parser, NOT getopt/argparse, so the option set is extracted by grepping every distinct `--long-option` literal + matched `-shortform`). The main `dinit` daemon + `dinitctl` client were already covered in older rounds; deliberately omitted. **dinit-check** validates a service-description tree without starting anything: `-d`/`--services-dir`, `-s`/`--system`, `-u`/`--user`, `-p`/`--socket-path`, `-n`/`--online`, `--env-file`. **dinit-monitor** subscribes to a running dinit and reacts to state-change events: `-e`/`--exit`, `-E`/`--env`, `-s`/`--system`, `-u`/`--user`, `-i`/`--initial`, `-p`/`--socket-path`, `-c`/`--command`, + 5 long-only `--str-{started,stopped,failed,set,unset}` printf-format flags. **File `_fghack` (3-stem `#compdef fghack pgrphack svok`)** from [`daemontools/daemontools/src/{fghack,pgrphack,svok}.c`](https://github.com/daemontools/daemontools/tree/master/src) — the 3 DJB original daemontools binaries not previously covered (svc/svscan/svstat/supervise/multilog/readproctitle/setlock/setuidgid/envuidgid/softlimit were already in the corpus from older rounds). Original 2001 DJB code predates getopt convention — all 3 are minimal `argv[1]`-only tools (no flags). **fghack** (foreground-detection hack via fd-pipe trickery; daemontools' supervise uses it to detect self-daemonizing services; the Skarnet equivalent is `s6-fghack` from R118). **pgrphack** (setpgrp(0) then chain-load child). **svok** (test whether a daemontools-supervised service is up; exits 0 if running, 100 if not). **File `_finit` (1-stem)** from [`troglobit/finit/src/finit.c`](https://github.com/troglobit/finit/blob/master/src/finit.c) getopt optstring `abe:h?st:vVz:` + case-arm decoding showing **5 of the 9 flags are no-op compatibility flags** kept for sysvinit invocation interoperability (per upstream source comment `case 'a': case 'b': case 'e': case 's': case 'z': break; /* ign, compat */`) — the real flags are `-t SEC` killdelay between SIGTERM and SIGKILL + `-v`/`-V` version + `-h`/`-?` usage. Properly labeled as `(no-op; kept for sysvinit backwards compatibility)` in each completion entry so the user knows not to depend on the named flag's documented sysvinit meaning. **File `_runit-init` (2-stem `#compdef runit-init utmpset`)** from [`void-linux/runit/src/runit-init.c`](https://github.com/void-linux/runit/blob/master/src/runit-init.c) + [`utmpset.c`](https://github.com/void-linux/runit/blob/master/src/utmpset.c). Both are tiny — `runit/runsv/runsvchdir/runsvdir/sv/chpst` from runit family were already covered. **runit-init** has single-positional runlevel dispatch (USAGE ` 0|6`; case-arm decoded from `switch (**argv) case '0': runit_halt() ; case '6': runit_reboot() ;`) with `((0\:halt 6\:reboot))` enum autocomplete + `-V` version flag. **utmpset** (USAGE ` [-w] line`) writes a logout record into utmp (or wtmp with `-w`) for a given tty line — used by getty/login to clean stale session entries. **Hunt-skip log this round**: nosh (Jonathan de Boyne Pollard's daemontools/runit/systemd alternative — ~500 binaries; large potential separate round), perp (wcm.me/perp — could not locate canonical GitHub mirror via `gh api` searches; both `repos/wcm1/perp` and `repos/wmark/perp` returned 404; deferred until a working source URL is identified), daemontools-encore (Bruce Guenter's extended fork — repo not at expected `bruceg/daemontools-encore` path; needs source audit), minit (Felix von Leitner's minimal init — needs research). dinit's `shutdown.cc` produces the binaries `halt`/`poweroff`/`reboot`/`shutdown` which all clash with multiple existing system commands; deferred (would require careful per-OS coverage matrix). **Blacklist additions (8)**: 2 entries in `d*` (dinit-check, dinit-monitor) + 2 in `f*` (fghack, finit) + 1 in `p*` (pgrphack) + 1 in `r*` (runit-init) + 1 in `s*` (svok) + 1 in `u*` (utmpset). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 136th consecutive clean round.
- **Obarun 66 service manager R130 — 11-stem multi-binary harvest** (`#compdef 66 66-echo 66-execute 66-fdholder-filler 66-hpr 66-nuke 66-oneshot 66-shutdown 66-shutdownd 66-umountall execl-envfile`) (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Pivot from Skarnet itself to Obarun's 66 — a service-management layer atop s6+s6-rc that provides a friendlier `systemctl <verb> <unit>`-style UX. Composes directly with the wider Skarnet ecosystem (R117-R129); 66 specifically delegates fdholder/shutdown/hpr/nuke/umountall functions to its own wrapper binaries that internally wrap the s6-linux-init binaries from R124. Surface extracted source-direct from upstream [`Obarun/66/src/66/*/*.c`](https://github.com/Obarun/66/tree/master/src/66) USAGE strings + `subgetopt_r` option-string specs + **subcommand-dispatch table extraction** for the master `66` binary (23 subcommands decoded from src/66/66/66.c sequential `if (!strcmp(argv[0], "<sub>"))` chain) + `OPTS_MAIN` macro from [`src/include/66/ssexec.h`](https://github.com/Obarun/66/blob/master/src/include/66/ssexec.h) (`#define OPTS_MAIN "hv:l:t:T:z"`) via `gh api`. Single multi-stem file `_66` is the alphabetically-first stem (`6` (0x36) < `e` (0x65)). **Coverage**: 11-stem multi-binary harvest of the Obarun 66 service-manager toolkit. **Notable per-binary surfaces**: **66** (master dispatch binary; 6 flags + 23-subcommand autocomplete enum `boot/enable/disable/start/stop/configure/parse/reconfigure/reload/restart/free/signal/status/resolve/state/remove/tree/snapshot/scandir/poweroff/reboot/halt/wall/version` — `systemctl`-style verb-then-unit syntax; the 23 subcommands extracted source-direct rather than from any documentation, by reading the `argv[0]` dispatch chain in `66.c`). **66-shutdown** (12 flags + 2 positionals; util-linux-compatible shutdown(8) replacement with 5-way `-h`/`-p`/`-r`/`-k`/`-c` primary mutex + 2-way `-f`/`-F` fsck-flag no-op mutex (kept for util-linux compat) + `-H`/`-l`/`-a`/`-t`). **66-shutdownd** (6 flags; long-running shutdown-orchestration daemon — equivalent to s6-linux-init-shutdownd from R124 but with 66-specific `-s SKEL` shutdown-scripts skeleton directory). **66-hpr** (11 flags; low-level halt/poweroff/reboot; 3-way mutex `-h`/`-p`/`-r` action + 2-way mutex `-d`/`-w` wtmp-mode + `-H` skip-sync + `-l LIVE` + `-b BANNER` + `-f`/`-n`/`-W`). **66-execute** / **66-oneshot** (low-level start/stop and up/down helpers invoked by 66 itself — not typically called directly). **66-fdholder-filler** (4 flags; bootstrap helper that populates the s6-fdholderd server from R120 with pre-bound socket fds from a 66 service definition). **execl-envfile** (5 flags + 2 positionals; reads variable assignments from an envfile then chain-loads prog with those vars exported — used inside 66 oneshot scripts). **66-echo** (4 flags; static-build-friendly echo(1) replacement for early-boot scripts). **66-nuke** / **66-umountall** (no-flag wrappers around their s6-linux-init equivalents). **Hunt-skip log this round**: 66-tools/66-tools (the user-facing companion utility scripts repo — most are shell scripts not binaries; will need a separate audit), other init-system alternatives for future rounds: runit (svscan family — was noted "already partially covered" in R117 hunt-skip; needs systematic audit), nosh (Jonathan de Boyne Pollard's daemontools/runit/systemd alternative — ~500 binaries; large potential round), finit (lightweight Linux init), perp (process supervisor), daemontools-encore, dinit, minit. **Blacklist additions (11)**: 10 entries in `66*` range + 1 in `e*` (execl-envfile). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 135th consecutive clean round.
- **Skarnet execline R129 — 41-stem multi-binary harvest** (`#compdef background backtick dollarat elgetopt elgetpositionals elglob eltest emptyenv envfile execline-cd execline-umask export-array fdblock fdclose fdmove fdreserve fdswap forbacktickx foreground forstdin forx getcwd getpid heredoc homeof ifelse ifte ifthenelse importas loopwhilex multidefine multisubstitute pipeline piperw posix-cd posix-umask redirfd runblock tryexec unexport withstdinas`) (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep; per-binary audit of the full 51-binary upstream listing identified **10 binaries deliberately omitted** from the new `#compdef` line per established anti-dup policy: **3 already-covered** (`define`, `execlineb`, `exec`), **3 stock-zsh-shadowed** (`exec`, `trap`, `wait` — zsh ships completion functions for these), **2 shell reserved words** (`case`, `if` — tokenized differently by zsh, completion would never fire), **2 shell builtins not worth shadowing** (`exit`, `export`, `shift`). Final fresh count: 51 - 10 = 41). Surface extracted source-direct from upstream [`skarnet/execline/src/execline/*.c`](https://github.com/skarnet/execline/tree/master/src/execline) USAGE strings + `subgetopt_r` option-string specs + skalibs `gol_main` `gol_bool` tables + case-arm decoding via `gh api`. Single multi-stem file `_background` is the alphabetically-first stem. **Coverage**: 41-stem multi-binary harvest of the Skarnet execline shell-replacement language — execline scripts are interpreted by `execlineb` (already covered) but every block primitive is itself a chain-loadable binary, so completions help script authors. Sister to the wider Skarnet ecosystem (R117-R128). **6 reusable helper arrays** factor out the common flag patterns across the binary family: **`_exl_autoimport`** (2-way `-E`/`-e` autoimport mutex from `GOLB_AUTOIMPORT` `.clear`/`.set` — used by backtick/forbacktickx/forstdin/forx/getcwd/getpid/withstdinas/redirfd), **`_exl_strip_nl`** (2-way `-N`/`-n` no-strip xor strip-trailing-newline mutex — used by backtick/forbacktickx/forstdin/importas/multidefine/withstdinas), **`_exl_chomp`** (2-way `-C`/`-c` no-chomp xor chomp-trailing-empty mutex — used by forbacktickx/forstdin/importas/multidefine), **`_exl_delim`** (2-way `-0`/`-d` NUL-delim xor custom-delim mutex — used by dollarat/forbacktickx/forstdin/multidefine), **`_exl_parallel`** (2-way `-p`/`-P MAXPAR` unbounded xor bounded-parallel mutex — used by forbacktickx/forstdin/forx), **`_exl_okbreak`** (2-way `-o`/`-x` OK-codes xor BREAK-codes mutex — used by forbacktickx/forstdin/forx/loopwhilex). **Notable per-binary surfaces**: **emptyenv** (5 GOL bool flags + long-options `--keep-path`/`--keep-ld-library-path`/`--cleanup`/`--pop-elgetopt`/`--pop-execline` decoded from upstream `rgolb[5]` `.set = GOLB_PATH | GOLB_LIB | GOLB_CLEANUP | GOLB_OPT | GOLB_POS` flag-bit assignments). **redirfd** (the most complex GOL bool table in execline; 11 short flags + 8 long-only flags from upstream `rgolb[]`): **5-way primary mutex** `-r` (`O_RDONLY`) / `-w` (`O_WRONLY | O_CREAT | O_TRUNC`) / `-u` (`O_RDWR | O_CREAT`) / `-a` (`O_APPEND | O_CREAT`) / `-x` (`O_EXCL | O_CREAT`) — decoded from each `.clear`/`.set` bitmask in the `GOLB_READ`/`GOLB_WRITE`/`GOLB_CREAT`/`GOLB_APPEND`/`GOLB_TRUNC`/`GOLB_EXCL` enum — plus **2-way `-N`/`-n`/`--block`/`--nonblock`** blocking mutex (`GOLB_NONBLOCK`) + `-b`/`--switch-block` post-open-mode-switch + `-f`/`--open` force-open + `-e`/`-E` autoimport (shared helper). **forbacktickx** + **forstdin** (most-helpered binaries; each composes 6 helpers — autoimport + strip-nl + chomp + delim + parallel + okbreak — plus its own non-shared positionals). **ifelse** + **ifte** + **ifthenelse** (variants of the if-then-else block: ifelse has 2 mutex flags `-n` negate / `-X` xec, same for ifte; ifthenelse has `-X` + `-s` stick instead). **backtick** (4-way mutex `-i` ignore-fail / `-I` insist-fail-outright / `-x` exit-with-inner-code / `-D DEFAULT` default-on-empty). **withstdinas** (3-way mutex `-i` insist-empty-fails / `-I` insist-harder / `-D DEFAULT` default-on-empty). **importas** (rich; 2-way `-i` insist-on-unset xor `-D DEFAULT` env-var defaulting + `-u` unexport-after-binding + `-s` strict-no-split + delim + chomp + strip-nl helpers). **runblock** (3 flags + 2 positionals; pass-through-or-run a numbered block from the execline argv stream — used inside elgetopt-pushed blocks). **tryexec** (4 flags + variadic; `-n`/`-c`/`-l`/`-a ARGV0` for inner-exec retry semantics). **getpid** (`-P`/`-p` mutex for parent-pid xor own-pid + autoimport helper). **Hunt-skip log this round**: 10 deliberately-omitted binaries per the anti-dup policy enumerated above. **No remaining Skarnet sister-repos to harvest** after this round — R117-R129 has now covered the entirety of skarnet/s6 + skarnet/s6-rc + skarnet/s6-linux-init + skarnet/s6-linux-utils + skarnet/s6-portable-utils + skarnet/s6-dns + skarnet/s6-networking + skarnet/utmps + skarnet/mdevd + skarnet/execline. **Blacklist additions (41)**: 4 entries in `b*`/`d*` + 8 in `e*` + 5 in `f*`-fd* + 4 in `f*`-for* + 5 in `g*`/`h*` + 4 in `i*` + 2 in `l*`/`m*` + 4 in `m*`/`p*` + 3 in `p*` + 3 in `r*`/`t*` + 2 in `u*`/`w*`. LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 134th consecutive clean round.
- **Skarnet s6-networking R128 — 3 files / 18 binaries** (`#compdef proxy-server s6-getservbyname s6-ident-client s6-tcpserver s6-tcpserver-access s6-tcpserver-socketbinder s6-tcpserverd` + `s6-clockadd s6-clockview s6-sntpclock s6-taiclock s6-taiclockd` + `s6-tlsc s6-tlsc-io s6-tlsd s6-tlsd-io s6-ucspitlsc s6-ucspitlsd`). Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`; per-binary audit identified 3 already-covered s6-networking binaries (`s6-tcpclient`, `s6-tlsclient`, `s6-tlsserver`) which are deliberately omitted from the new `#compdef` lines. Surface extracted source-direct from upstream [`skarnet/s6-networking`](https://github.com/skarnet/s6-networking) via `gh api` — USAGE strings + `subgetopt_r` option-string specs + skalibs `GOL_main` `gol_bool` + `gol_arg` tables + case-arm decoding for the mutex flag groups. **File `_proxy-server` (7-stem TCP UCSPI conn-tools)** from [`src/conn-tools/*.c`](https://github.com/skarnet/s6-networking/tree/master/src/conn-tools). Notable surfaces: **proxy-server** (PROXY-protocol v1/v2 header parser+stripper that sits between a listening TCP socket and a real service to read the client-IP header sent by an upstream load balancer): 2-way mutex `-1`/`--only-v1` xor `-2`/`--only-v2` (decoded from `GOLB_V1` xor `GOLB_V2` `.set`/`.clear` pairings) + long-only `--before-tlsd`/`--after-tlsd` placement-hint flags + `-v VERB` + `-t MS`. **s6-tcpserver-access** (TCP access-control filter; 13 flags + variadic): **5 mutex pairs** decoded from case arms: `-W`/`-w` deny-action (`flagfatal = 0|1` — silent-drop xor banner-then-drop), `-D`/`-d` `TCP_NODELAY` (`flagnodelay = 1|0`), `-H`/`-h` reverse-DNS (`flagdnslookup = 0` xor `flaghosts = 1`), `-R`/`-r` ident-lookup (`flagident = 0|1`), `-P`/`-p` paranoid-hostname (`flagparanoid = 0|1`) + `-l LOCALNAME` + `-B BANNER` + `-t MS` + `-i RULESDIR` xor `-x RULESFILE`. **s6-tcpserver** (all-in-one TCP UCSPI superdaemon; 14 flags): standard verbosity 3-way + SO_REUSEADDR 2-way + maxconn/localmaxconn + privilege-drop. **s6-tcpserver-socketbinder** (uses skalibs `GOL_main`; 6 flags + 2 positionals): 2-way mutex `-d`/`-D`/`--reuse-address`/`--no-reuse-address` SO_REUSEADDR (decoded from `GOLB_NOREUSE` `.clear`/`.set`), 2-way mutex `-M`/`-m`/`--tcp`/`--udp` TCP-default xor UDP (`GOLB_UDP`), `-B`/`--block` bind-block-on-port-in-use, `-b BACKLOG`. **s6-tcpserverd** (5 flags + variadic): accept-loop half. **s6-getservbyname** (2 positionals; getservbyname(3) wrapper; service+protocol enum autocomplete: 16 common service names + tcp/udp/sctp). **s6-ident-client** (1 flag + 4 positionals; RFC1413 ident-protocol client; `-t MS` + ra/rp/la/lp). **File `_s6-clockadd` (5-stem TAI-clock framework)** from [`src/clock/*.c`](https://github.com/skarnet/s6-networking/tree/master/src/clock). Reusable helper `_s6_clock_query_flags` shared by s6-sntpclock + s6-taiclock (8 common client query flags `-f` force + `-v VERB` + `-r ROUNDTRIPS` + `-t MS` + `-h MS` throttle + `-T MS` total + `-e MS` errmax + `-p PORT`). **s6-clockadd** (2 flags; apply stored clock offset). **s6-clockview** (`int main(void)` no args; print current TAI as 12-byte binary). **s6-sntpclock** (SNTP RFC4330 client). **s6-taiclock** (s6-taiclock-protocol client). **s6-taiclockd** (UDP daemon; 2 flags `-i IP` + `-p PORT`). **File `_s6-tlsc` (6-stem TLS family)** from [`src/tls/*.c`](https://github.com/skarnet/s6-networking/tree/master/src/tls). Reusable helper `_s6tls_closenotify_flags` shared by all 6 binaries (3 mutex pairs: `-S`/`-s`/`--close-notify` xor `--no-close-notify`, `-J`/`-j`/`--enforce-close-notify` xor `--no-enforce-close-notify`, `-Z`/`-z`/`--keep` xor `--no-keep` — all decoded from skalibs `GOL_main` `gol_bool` `.clear`/`.set` pairings). **Subtle client-vs-server semantic split caught by reading the `.lo` long-option strings**: `-k` is `--servername` on the 3 client variants (s6-tlsc/s6-tlsc-io/s6-ucspitlsc) but `--sni-level` on the 3 server variants (s6-tlsd/s6-tlsd-io/s6-ucspitlsd); `-Y`/`-y` is `--no-client-cert`/`--client-cert` (presentation toggle) on clients but `--client-cert`/`--mandatory-client-cert` (acceptance vs requirement) on servers. The long-only `--verify-cert`/`--no-verify-cert` are client-only; `--sni`/`--mandatory-sni` are server-only. **s6-tlsc-io** and **s6-tlsd-io** add `-d FD`/`--notification-fd` + raw `fdr`/`fdw` positionals (do not chain-load prog; bridge bytes directly). **Hunt-skip log this round**: skarnet/execline (shell-replacement language with ~40 chain-loadable binaries — many names like `backtick`/`foreground`/`if`/`ifelse`/`importas`/`redirfd`/`pipeline`/`elglob`/`loopwhilex`/`tryexec`/`withstdinas` clash with shell builtins or other Unix commands; per-binary dup audit + clash-vs-coverage judgment-call required; explicit R129+ work). The `libs6net`/`sbearssl`/`stls` subdirs of s6-networking (library helpers + non-public-binary linker source files, not user-facing CLIs). **Blacklist additions (18)**: 1 entry in `p*` range + 17 in `s6-*` range. LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 133rd consecutive clean round.
