zoekt-mirror-gitiles zoekt-repo-index zoekt-sourcegraph-indexserver zoekt-test`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`zoekt-dynamic-indexserver`** (4 flags): dynamic indexserver with HTTP control — `-repo_dir`, `-index_dir`, `-index_timeout` (default 1h), `-listen` (default `:6060`). **`zoekt-git-clone`** (3 flags + URL positional): `-dest`, `-name`, `-repoid`. **`zoekt-indexserver`** (9 flags): long-running periodic mirror+index — `-index_timeout`/`-max_log_age`/`-fetch_interval`/`-mirror_config`/`-mirror_duration`/`-cpu_fraction` (default 0.25)/`-git_index_flags`/`-data_dir` (default `$HOME/zoekt-serving`)/`-index_dir`. **`zoekt-mirror-bitbucket-server`** (9 flags + 2-value type enum): Bitbucket Server — `-dest`, `-url`, `-disable-tls`, `-credentials` (default `.bitbucket-credentials`), `-project`, `-delete`, `-name REGEX`, `-exclude REGEX`, `-type` (`NORMAL`/`PERSONAL`). **`zoekt-mirror-gerrit`** (8 flags + URL positional): Gerrit — `-dest`, `-name REGEX`, `-repo-name-format`, `-exclude REGEX`, `-delete`, `-fetch-meta-config`, `-http-credentials FILE`, `-active`. **`zoekt-mirror-gitea`** (12 flags incl. 2 repeatable): Gitea — `-dest`, `-url` (default `https://gitea.com`), `-org`, `-user`, `-token` (default `$HOME/.gitea-token`), `-forks`, `-delete`, `-name REGEX`, `-exclude REGEX`, repeatable `*-topic`, repeatable `*-exclude_topic`, `-no_archived`. **`zoekt-mirror-gitiles`** (4 flags + URL positional + 2-value type enum): gitiles/cgit web frontend — `-dest`, `-name REGEX`, `-exclude REGEX`, `-type` (`gitiles`/`cgit`). **`zoekt-repo-index`** (13 flags + manifest positional): Android-style multi-project repo-tool indexer — `-file_limit`, `-shard_limit`, `-parallelism`, `-rev_prefix` (default `refs/remotes/origin/`), `-base_url`, `-repo_cache`, `-index`, `-manifest_repo_url`, `-manifest_rev_prefix`, `-name`, `-url`, `-max_sub_projects` (debug), `-incremental` (default true). **`zoekt-sourcegraph-indexserver`** (14 flags): Sourcegraph-managed indexer — `-sourcegraph_url`, `-index`, `-listen`, `-hostname`, `-interval`, `-backoff_duration`, `-max_backoff_duration`, `-vacuum_interval`, `-merge_interval`, `-index_concurrency`, `-merge_target_size`, `-merge_min_size`, `-cpu_fraction`, `-shard_merging`, `-merge_min_age`. **`zoekt-test`** (5 flags + query positional): `-mem_profile FILE`, `-cpu_profile FILE`, `-repo`, `-indexDir`, `-case`. **Blacklist additions (10) across 1 insertion block**: all 10 entries inserted into the contiguous `zoekt-*` zone between `zoekt-archive-index` (29908) and `zoekt-webserver` (was 29914, now shifted) in canonical alphabetical positions. Single multi-stem file `_zoekt-archive-index` is `#compdef zoekt-archive-index zoekt-index zoekt-merge-index zoekt-mirror-github zoekt-mirror-gitlab zoekt-webserver`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`zoekt-archive-index`** (7 flags + archive positional): index archive downloads — `-incremental` (default true), `-name`, `-url`, `-branch`, `-commit`, `-strip_components N`, `-download-limit-mbps N`. **`zoekt-index`** (3 flags + directory positional): index local directory — `-cpu_profile FILE`, `-ignore_dirs DIRS` (default `.git,.hg,.svn`), `-meta FILE` (.meta JSON). **`zoekt-merge-index`** (state-machine with 2 sub-actions): `merge` (combine shards into compound) and `explode` (split compound into parts). **`zoekt-mirror-github`** (13 flags incl. 3-value visibility enum): mirror GitHub repos — `-dest DIR`, `-url URL` (GitHub Enterprise; default github.com), `-org`, `-user`, `-token FILE` (default `$HOME/.github-token`), `-forks`, `-delete`, `-name REGEX`, `-exclude REGEX`, repeatable `*-topic`, repeatable `*-exclude_topic`, `-no_archived`, repeatable `*-visibility` (`public`/`private`/`internal`). **`zoekt-mirror-gitlab`** (11 flags): mirror GitLab repos — `-dest`, `-url` (default GitLab.com API v4), `-token FILE`, `-membership`, `-public`, `-delete`, `-exclude_user`, `-name REGEX`, `-exclude REGEX`, `-last_activity_after DATE`, `-no_archived`. **`zoekt-webserver`** (16 flags): HTTP + RPC frontend — `-log_dir`, `-log_refresh` (default 24h), `-listen` (default `:6070`), `-index`, `-html` (default true), `-rpc`, `-indexserver_proxy`, `-print`, `-pprof`, `-ssl_cert`, `-ssl_key`, `-host_customization HOST1=QUERY,HOST2=QUERY`, `-template_dir`, `-dump_templates`, `-cors_origin`, `-version`. **Hunt-skip log this round**: 9 additional zoekt binaries deferred for later rounds — `zoekt-dynamic-indexserver`, `zoekt-git-clone`, `zoekt-indexserver`, `zoekt-mirror-bitbucket-server`, `zoekt-mirror-gerrit`, `zoekt-mirror-gitea`, `zoekt-mirror-gitiles`, `zoekt-repo-index`, `zoekt-sourcegraph-indexserver`, `zoekt-test` (similar surface patterns; Single-mode invocation `oats [flags] [test YAML files or directories]`. 13 flags: `-timeout DUR` (test-case timeout, default 30s), `-absent-timeout DUR` (absence-assertion timeout, default 10s), `-lgtm-version VER` (docker-otel-lgtm version, default `latest`), `-manual-debug` (keep containers running for debugging), `-lgtm-log-all` (logging for all LGTM containers), `-lgtm-log-grafana`/`-lgtm-log-loki`/`-lgtm-log-tempo`/`-lgtm-log-prometheus`/`-lgtm-log-pyroscope`/`-lgtm-log-collector` (per-container logging toggles), `-host HOST` (request-host override, default `localhost`), `-log-limit N` (max log output length per entry). Single multi-stem file `_event-generator` is `#compdef event-generator opkssh`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`event-generator`** (state-machine with 4 subcommands + nested `suite` 3-action enum): falcosecurity/event-generator generates 100+ suspect actions for Falco-rule testing (sister-binary to existing `_falco`/`_falcoctl` coverage). Subcommands: `run` (with `--loop`, `--sleep` default 100ms, `--all` + action-regexp positional), `test` (with `--falco-server` default `localhost:8080`), `bench` (with `--loop` for half-sleep-each-round, `--sleep` for EPS control, `--pid` to monitor specific Falco process), `suite` (v0.13+ YAML-declarative test scenarios — sub-actions `run`/`test`/`explain` with YAML file positional). **`opkssh`** (state-machine with 6 subcommands + nested `permissions` 3-action enum): openpubkey/opkssh — SSH access via OpenID Connect identities (eliminates long-lived SSH keys by integrating with OpenID Providers like Google/Microsoft/GitLab). Subcommands: `login` (with `--provider issuer,client_id[,client_secret,scopes]`, `-i KEY` custom key name, `--create-config` for `~/.opk/config.yml`), `logout` (with `-i KEY` to remove specific SSH key pair), `inspect PATH` (display SSH key/certificate details), `audit` (validate `/etc/opk/auth_id` + `/etc/opk/providers` consistency), `add <user> <identity> <issuer>` (with shorthand issuers `google`/`azure`/etc), `permissions` (3-action enum `check`/`fix`/`install`). **Blacklist additions (2) across 2 insertion blocks**: 1 between `evemu-record` (7586) and `event_rpcgen.py` (7587) for `event-generator` (within ev* at index 3 `m` (109, mu-record) < `n` (110, nt*); within event* at index 5 `-` (45, -generator) < `_` (95, _rpcgen.py)); 1 between `opkg` (18648) and `opl2ofm` (18649) for `opkssh` (within op* at index 2 `j` (106, j_dump) < `k` (107, k*) < `l` (108, l2ofm); within opk* at index 3 `g` (103, g) < `s` (115, ssh)). Persistent flag `--home DIR` (default `$HOME/.horcrux`). **Hunt-skip log this round**: `aws-lambda-rie` (env-var driven, no CLI flag surface), `amazon-cloudwatch-agent` (config-file driven), `copilot` already covered, `cozystack` no CLI binary, `letsencrypt boulder` no end-user CLI, `qiskit`/`pennylane`/`qsc` quantum libraries not CLIs. **Blacklist additions (1)**: 1 between `hopsworks-mcp` (11194) and `hornet` (11195) for `horcrux` (within ho* at index 2 `p` (112, p*) < `r` (114, r*); within hor* at index 3 `c` (99, crux) < `n` (110, net)). Primary subcommands `run` (execute EVM bytecode), `blocktest`, `statetest`, `transition` (alias `t8n` — full state transition), `transaction` (alias `t9n` — transaction validation), `block-builder` (alias `b11r`), `verkle` (alias `vkt` — binary-trie helpers). Verkle sub-actions: `tree-keys` (alias `v`), `single-key` (alias `vk`), `code-chunk-key` (alias `vck`), `chunkify-code` (alias `vcc`), `state-root` (alias `vsr`). Flags `--bench`, `--fuzz`, `--witness.cross-check`, `--dump`, `--human-readable`, `--statdump`, `--trace`, `--trace.format`, `--trace.nomemory`, `--trace.nostack`, `--trace.nostorage`, `--trace.noreturndata`. **`fetchpayload`** (4 flags): ethereum/go-ethereum fetch block + execution witness from an Ethereum node via RPC, writes combined payload to disk — `-rpc URL` (default `http://localhost:8545`), `-block BLOCK` (decimal/hex/`latest`), `-format FORMATS` (3-value enum `rlp`/`hex`/`json`, comma-separated), `-out DIR`. **Hunt-skip log this round**: `keeper` (single-mode stateless block validation tool, no flags or subcommands; just `getInput()` + `core.ExecuteStateless()` with exit codes 10/11/12/13). **Blacklist additions (2) across 2 insertion blocks**: 1 between `evince-thumbnailer` (7595) and `evmctl` (7596) for `evm` (within ev* at index 2 `i` (105, ince*) < `m` (109, m*); within evm* at index 3 end-of-string < `c` (99, ctl)); 1 between `fetchmail` (8005) and `fez` (8006) for `fetchpayload` (within fetch* at index 5 `m` (109, mail) < `p` (112, payload); within fe* at index 2 `t` (116, tch*) < `z` (122, z)). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — forty-seventh consecutive clean round.

Single multi-stem file `_blsync` is `#compdef blsync era`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`blsync`** (15 flags, no subcommands): ethereum/go-ethereum beacon light sync client (connects to a CL beacon-API + EL engine-API, drives the EL head from optimistically-synced beacon chain). 9 beacon flags (`--beacon.api`/`--beacon.api.header`/`--beacon.threshold`/`--beacon.nofilter`/`--beacon.config`/`--beacon.genesis.gvroot`/`--beacon.genesis.time`/`--beacon.checkpoint`/`--beacon.checkpoint.file`), 2 blsync flags (`--blsync.engine.api`/`--blsync.jwtsecret`), 4 network selector flags (`--mainnet`/`--sepolia`/`--holesky`/`--hoodi`). **`era`** (state-machine with 3 subcommands + 4 flags): ethereum/go-ethereum era1 file-format tools (rollup-style historical chain segments for trustless archival access) — subcommands `block <number>` (with `-txs` for full transaction values), `info <epoch>`, `verify <expected>`. Global flags `-dir DIR` (default `eras`), `-network NAME` (4-value enum mainnet/sepolia/holesky/hoodi, default `mainnet`), `-size N` (blocks per era, default `era.MaxSize`). **Blacklist additions (2) across 2 insertion blocks**: 1 between `bls` (2331) and `bltool` (2332) for `blsync` (within bls* at index 3 end-of-string < `y` (121, ync); within bl* at index 2 `s` (115, s*) < `t` (116, tool)); 1 between `equivs-control` (7427) and `era_check` (7428) for `era` (within e* at index 1 `q` (113, quivs) < `r` (114, ra); within era* at index 3 end-of-string < `_` (95, _check)). Single multi-stem file `_ethkey` is `#compdef ethkey rlpdump`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`ethkey`** (state-machine with 5 subcommands + 2 flags): ethereum/go-ethereum key management utility — subcommands `generate`/`inspect`/`changepassphrase`/`signmessage`/`verifymessage`; flags `--passwordfile FILE` and `--json` (output JSON instead of human-readable format). **`rlpdump`** (5 flags + positional): ethereum/go-ethereum RLP encoding/decoding tool — `-hex DATA` (dump given hex data), `-reverse` (convert ASCII to RLP), `-noascii` (do not print ASCII strings readably), `-single` (print only first element), `-pos` (display element byte positions). **Hunt-skip log this round**: `puppeth` (discontinued/removed from go-ethereum January 2023; users should consult private-networks docs for Ethash/Clique consensus), `bootnode` (docs page returned 404), `abidump`/`blsync`/`era`/`fetchpayload`/`keeper`/`workload` (no documented CLI surface in upstream docs site), Solana `agave-validator` / `solana-validator` / `solana-test-validator` (Agave fork from solana-labs/solana — minimal upstream flag enumeration). **Blacklist additions (2) across 2 insertion blocks**: 1 between `etherwake` (7539) and `ethminer` (7540) for `ethkey` (within eth* at index 3 `e` (101, er*) < `k` (107, key) < `m` (109, miner)); 1 between `rlogind` (22573) and `rls` (22574) for `rlpdump` (within rl* at index 2 `o` (111, ogind) < `p` (112, pdump) < `s` (115, s)). Global flags include `--loglevel` (default 4), `--keystore`, `--configdir`, `--chainid` (default 1), `--lightkdf`, `--nousb`, `--pcscdpath`, HTTP-RPC (`--http`/`--http.addr`/`--http.port` default 8550/`--http.vhosts`), IPC (`--ipcdisable`/`--ipcpath`), `--signersecret`, `--4bytedb-custom`, `--auditlog` (default `audit.log`), `--rules`, `--stdio-ui`/`--stdio-ui-test`, `--advanced`, `--suppress-bootwarn`. **`devp2p`** (state-machine with 7 subcommands + nested sub-actions): ethereum/go-ethereum networking + discovery utilities — `enrdump <base64>` (decode Ethereum Node Records), `key` (generate/to-enode), `dns` (sign/sync/to-cloudflare/to-route53), `nodeset` (info/filter with `-limit`/`-ip`/`-min-age`/`-eth-network`/`-les-server`/`-snap` filters), `discv4` (ping/resolve/crawl), `discv5` (ping/resolve/listen/crawl/test with `-listen1`/`-listen2`), `rlpx` (eth-test/eth66-test protocol tests). **Blacklist additions (2) across 2 insertion blocks**: 1 between `clearml-task` (3942) and `clementine` (3943) for `clef` (within cle* at index 3 `a` (97, an*/ar*) < `f` (102, f) < `m` (109, mentine)); 1 between `devorch` (6078) and `devpod` (6079) for `devp2p` (within dev* at index 3 `o` (111, orch) < `p` (112, p*); within devp* at index 4 `2` (50, 2p) < `o` (111, od)). Supports semantic types (`SerializableDate`/`SerializableTime`), nested schema refs across files, conditional + boolean subschemas, comprehensive numeric/string/array/object constraint validation. 6 documented flags + JSON positional: `-p`/`--package PACKAGE` (Go package name for generated code), repeatable `--schema-package ID=PATH` (map schema-ID to Go import path), repeatable `--schema-output ID=PATH` (output file for a specific schema), `-o`/`--output FILE`, `-h`/`--help`, `--version`. **Blacklist additions (1)**: 1 between `go-jsonnet` (9920) and `go-langserver` (9921) for `go-jsonschema` (within go-json* at index 7 `n` (110, net) < `s` (115, schema); within go-* at index 3 `j` (106, j*) < `l` (108, langserver)). State-machine dispatch with **85 top-level subcommands** transcribed from `^class cmd_` declarations: working-tree ops (`add`/`rm`/`mv`/`status`/`checkout`/`switch`/`restore`/`reset`), history (`log`/`diff`/`show`/`blame`/`annotate`/`shortlog`/`rev-list`), branching (`branch`/`tag`/`merge`/`merge-base`/`merge-tree`/`cherry`/`cherry-pick`/`rebase`/`revert`), remote (`fetch`/`fetch-pack`/`pull`/`push`/`clone`/`ls-remote`/`remote`/`receive-pack`/`upload-pack`/`daemon`/`web-daemon`), low-level (`commit-tree`/`write-tree`/`ls-tree`/`ls-files`/`for-each-ref`/`symbolic-ref`/`show-ref`/`show-branch`/`pack-objects`/`pack-refs`/`unpack-objects`/`dump-pack`/`dump-index`), maintenance (`gc`/`fsck`/`prune`/`repack`/`count-objects`/`maintenance`/`reflog`), inspection (`describe`/`diagnose`/`var`/`config`/`column`), bundle/archive (`archive`/`bundle`/`format-patch`/`mailinfo`/`mailsplit`/`interpret-trailers`/`stripspace`), filters (`filter-branch`/`grep`/`check-ignore`/`check-mailmap`), signing (`verify-commit`/`verify-tag`), advanced (`bisect`/`rerere`/`lfs`/`help`). Plus 6 grouped sub-actions: `notes` (4-value enum `add`/`list`/`remove`/`show`), `replace` (`delete`/`list`), `stash` (`list`/`pop`/`push`), `submodule` (`add`/`init`/`list`/`update`), `worktree` (8-value enum `add`/`list`/`lock`/`move`/`prune`/`remove`/`repair`/`unlock`), `remote` (`add`). Total: 85 top-level verbs + 6 grouped sub-action sets covering ~100+ CLI entry points. **Blacklist additions (1)**: 1 between `duk` (6871) and `dum` (6872) for `dulwich` (within du* at index 2 `k` (107, k) < `l` (108, lwich) < `m` (109, m*)). All share the nihui common dialect: `-i`/`-o`/`-g`/`-j`/`-m`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`cain-ncnn-vulkan`** (8 flags): CAIN (Channel Attention Is All You Need) video frame interpolation — two modes (`-0 FRAME0 -1 FRAME1 -o OUT` 2-frame OR `-i INDIR -o OUTDIR` batch). Flags `-0`/`-1`/`-i`/`-o`/`-m MODEL_DIR`/`-g GPU_ID`/`-j LOAD:PROC:SAVE`/`-f PATTERN`. **`dain-ncnn-vulkan`** (11 flags): DAIN (Depth-Aware Video Frame Interpolation) — same dual-mode interface, adds `-n FRAME_COUNT` (default 2x input), `-s STEP` (0-1, default 0.5), `-t TILE_SIZE` (≥128, default 256). **`rife-ncnn-vulkan`** (12 flags): RIFE (Real-Time Intermediate Flow Estimation) supporting v1.2–v4.6 models incl. anime variants — same dual-mode, adds `-n FRAME_COUNT`, `-s STEP`, `-x` (spatial TTA), `-z` (temporal TTA), `-u` (UHD mode). **`srmd-ncnn-vulkan`** (9 flags + 3-scale enum): SRMD super-resolution + denoiser — single-image mode with `-i IMG -o OUT -n DENOISE_LEVEL` (-1..10, default 3) `-s SCALE` (`2`/`3`/`4`) `-t TILE_SIZE` `-x` (TTA). **Blacklist additions (4) across 4 insertion blocks**: 1 between `cai` (2878) and `cairo-compile` (2879) for `cain-ncnn-vulkan` (within cai* at index 3 end-of-string < `n` (110, n*); within cai* at index 3 `n` (110, in-ncnn-vulkan) < `r` (114, ro-compile)); 1 between `dagu` (5610) and `dak` (5611) for `dain-ncnn-vulkan` (within da* at index 2 `g` (103, gu) < `i` (105, in-ncnn-vulkan) < `k` (107, k)); 1 between `rider` (22505) and `riff` (22506) for `rife-ncnn-vulkan` (within ri* at index 2 `d` (100, der) < `f` (102, fe/ff); within rif* at index 3 `e` (101, e-ncnn-vulkan) < `f` (102, f)); 1 between `srm` (24723) and `srptool` (24724) for `srmd-ncnn-vulkan` (within srm* at index 3 end-of-string < `d` (100, d-ncnn-vulkan); within sr* at index 2 `m` (109, m*) < `p` (112, ptool)). Surface transcribed directly from upstream docs at [nim-lang.org/docs/koch.html](https://nim-lang.org/docs/koch.html). Single-stem file `_koch` is `#compdef koch`. **Coverage**: `koch` is Nim's build orchestrator for the compiler itself (separate from `nim`/`nimble`/`nimsuggest`/`nimpretty` which are already covered). State-machine dispatches on **7 subcommands** (one alias): `boot` (bootstrap), `csource` (build C sources for install), `temp` (build compiler as `nim_temp` for testing), `test` / `tests` (alias; compile + run test suite), `web` (RST→HTML docs), `pdf` (PDF docs). Per-verb flags: `boot`/`csource` accept 3 `-d:` define flags (`-d:release` for optimized vs debug, `-d:nimUseLinenoise` for linenoise interactive mode on non-Windows, `-d:leanCompiler` to omit JS codegen + docs generator); `web` accepts `--parallelBuild=N` (1 = serial). **Blacklist additions (1)**: 1 between `koboldcpp` (13219) and `kodak2ti3` (13220) for `koch` (within ko* at index 2 `b` (98, boldcpp) < `c` (99, ch) < `d` (100, dak/de/di)). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — thirty-sixth consecutive clean round.
- **Mixed-theme R29 — node-llama-cpp + safe-cli + safe-creator (3 stems via single multi-stem `_node-llama-cpp` file)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream README + docs at [github.com/withcatai/node-llama-cpp](https://github.com/withcatai/node-llama-cpp) (+ [node-llama-cpp.withcat.ai/cli](https://node-llama-cpp.withcat.ai/cli)) and [github.com/safe-global/safe-cli](https://github.com/safe-global/safe-cli). Single multi-stem file `_node-llama-cpp` is `#compdef node-llama-cpp safe-cli safe-creator`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`node-llama-cpp`** (state-machine with 7 subcommands; typically invoked via `npx --no node-llama-cpp <cmd>`): withcatai/node-llama-cpp is Node.js bindings for llama.cpp with GPU acceleration support (Metal/CUDA/Vulkan), JSON-schema output enforcement, function calling, embedding/reranking — subcommands `pull` (download from URL), `chat` (interactive), `init` (template scaffold), `complete`, `infill` (prefix/suffix-bracketed completion), `inspect` (GPU/GGUF/perf), `source` (manage underlying llama.cpp codebase). Global flags `-h`/`--help`, `-v`/`--version`. **`safe-cli`** (state-machine with 6 unattended subcommands + 2 positional + 5 boolean toggles): safe-global/safe-cli manages Ethereum Safe smart-contract accounts independently of the Safe{Core} API — positional 1 = Safe checksum address (or owner address with `--get-safes-from-owner`), positional 2 = Ethereum node URL. Flags `--history`/`--no-history` (default off for security), `--get-safes-from-owner`/`--no-get-safes-from-owner`, `--non-interactive` (unattended), `--help`. Unattended commands: `send-ether`, `send-erc20`, `send-erc721`, `send-custom` (custom tx data), `tx-builder` (JSON file), `version`. **`safe-creator`** (9 flags + 2 positionals): safe-global/safe-creator deploys new Safe contracts — positional 1 = node URL, positional 2 = deployer private key. Flags `--threshold N` (default deployer only), `--owners ADDRESSES`, `--safe-contract ADDR` (custom master copy), `--proxy-factory ADDR`, `--callback-handler ADDR`, `--salt-nonce NONCE` (deterministic addresses), `--without-events` (gas saving), `--generate-vanity-addresses` (preview without deploy), `--no-confirm`, `-v`/`--version`. **Blacklist additions (3) across 2 insertion blocks** (safe-cli + safe-creator share one block): 1 between `node-gyp` (17812) and `node-red` (17813) for `node-llama-cpp` (within node-* at index 5 `g` (103, gyp) < `l` (108, llama) < `r` (114, red)); **2 between `safe-bundle` (23154) and `safe-rm` (23155)** for `safe-cli` + `safe-creator` (within safe-* at index 5 `b` (98, bundle) < `c` (99, cli/creator); within safe-c* at index 6 `l` (108, cli) < `r` (114, creator); back to safe-* at index 5 `c` (99) < `r` (114, rm)). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — thirty-fifth consecutive clean round.
- **Anchore sister-CLI harvest R28 — binny + chronicle + grant (3 stems via single multi-stem `_binny` file)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Continuation of the Anchore Go/clio-based CLI family already represented by previously-covered `_grype-cli`/`_syft-cli`/`_cocogitto`/`_clio`/`_quill`. Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream READMEs at [github.com/anchore/binny](https://github.com/anchore/binny), [github.com/anchore/chronicle](https://github.com/anchore/chronicle), and [github.com/anchore/grant](https://github.com/anchore/grant). Single multi-stem file `_binny` is `#compdef binny chronicle grant`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`binny`** (state-machine with 6 subcommands): anchore/binny manages a directory of binaries via `.binny.yaml` without a traditional package manager — `install` (deploy all), `check`, `update` (refresh pinned versions within constraints), `list`, `add`, `remove`. Acquisition methods: github-release, go-install, go-build, hosted-shell. Includes supply-chain cooldown for malicious-version detection. **`chronicle`** (5 flag pairs + positional + 4-format enum): anchore/chronicle is a GitHub-aware changelog generator — `-o/--output FORMAT` (`md`/`md-pretty`/`json`/`version`), `-s/--since-tag TAG` (default: last release), `-u/--until-tag TAG` (default: HEAD), `-n/--speculate-next-version` (guess next release), `-q` (suppress logging); positional 1 = repository path. **`grant`** (state-machine with 2 subcommands + repeatable target): anchore/grant is a license scanner for container images, SBOMs, and filesystems — `list` (enumerate licenses), `check` (validate against policy). Targets accept container image refs, `sbom:FILE`, `dir:PATH`, or filesystem paths. **Blacklist additions (3) across 3 insertion blocks**: 1 between `binmiscctl` (2204) and `binocle` (2205) for `binny` (within bin* at index 3 `m` (109, miscctl) < `n` (110, ny) < `o` (111, ocle)); 1 between `chronic` (3659) and `chronoctl` (3660) for `chronicle` (within chronic* at index 7 end-of-string < `l` (108, le); within chron* at index 5 `i` (105, ic*) < `o` (111, octl)); 1 between `granian` (line 10268, post-shift) and `granted` (10269) for `grant` (within gran* at index 4 `i` (105, ian) < `t` (116, t/ted); within grant* at index 5 end-of-string < `e` (101, ed)). Mid-edit slot-misordering caught + fixed: initial placement assumed `grant < granatier` but actual order is `granatier < granian < grant < granted` because at index 4 `granatier`'s `a` (97) precedes `grant`'s `t` (116) — `LC_ALL=C sort -c` caught it; one re-edit + re-verify. LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — thirty-fourth consecutive clean round.
- **Mixed-theme R27 — comfy + sloglint (2 stems; ComfyUI manager + Go log/slog linter)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Pivot out of pure Go-linter territory after R21-R26. Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream READMEs at [github.com/Comfy-Org/comfy-cli](https://github.com/Comfy-Org/comfy-cli) and [github.com/go-simpler/sloglint](https://github.com/go-simpler/sloglint). Single multi-stem file `_comfy` is `#compdef comfy sloglint`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`comfy`** (state-machine with **12 top-level subcommands**, nested state-machines for `node`/`model`/`generate`/`manager`/`pr-cache`): Comfy-Org/comfy-cli is a Python-based ComfyUI manager (binary `comfy`, installed via `pip install comfy-cli`). Top-level: `install` (with `--skip-manager`/`--fast-deps`/`--workspace`/`--pr`), `launch` (with `--background`/`--frontend-pr`/`-- ARGS` passthrough), `env`, `run`, `set-default PATH`, `which`, `pr-cache` (list/clean sub-actions), `node` (13 sub-actions incl. show/install/update/reinstall/fix/uninstall/install-deps/deps-in-workflow/uv-sync/save-snapshot/restore-snapshot/bisect with show taking 7-value enum installed/enabled/all/disabled/not-installed/snapshot/snapshot-list and bisect taking 4-value action start/good/bad/reset), `model` (download with `--url`/`--set-civitai-api-token`/`--set-hf-api-token`, list, remove with `--model-names`), `generate` (3 utility sub-actions list/schema/resume plus **16 hosted partner models** Flux/Flux-Pro/Ideogram/DALL·E/Recraft/Stability/Runway/Kling/Luma/Pika/Vidu/Hailuo/Seedance/Gemini-Flash-Image; generate flags `--prompt`/`--download`/`--async`/repeatable `--image`/`--api-key`), `manager` (7 sub-actions disable/enable-gui/disable-gui/enable-legacy-gui/clear/migrate-legacy/uv-compile-default), `tracking disable`. Plus core workspace-selector flags `--workspace=PATH`/`--recent`/`--here` available on most subcommands, plus `--install-completion` for typer-style shell-completion install. **`sloglint`** (positional only): go-simpler/sloglint is a Go `log/slog` style linter (enforces global-logger detection, context requirement, static message validation, key-value vs. attribute consistency, key naming conventions snake_case/kebab-case/camelCase/PascalCase); standalone surface is minimal because configuration is golangci-lint primary. **Blacklist additions (2) across 2 insertion blocks**: 1 between `cometbft` (4261) and `comm` (4262) for `comfy` (within com* at index 3 `c` (99, cast/cetbft? no — `cometbft` index 3='e') < `e` (101, et/etbft) < `f` (102, fy) < `m` (109, m)); 1 between `slogkey` (24121) and `slogverify` (24122) for `sloglint` (within slog* at index 4 `k` (107, key) < `l` (108, lint) < `v` (118, verify)). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — thirty-third consecutive clean round.
- **Go linter sweep R26 — bidichk + inamedparam + mnd + tagalign + tagliatelle + usetesting (6 stems via single multi-stem `_bidichk` file)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Continues the R21-R25 sweep with 6 more single-purpose analysis/passes-framework linters. Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream READMEs at [github.com/breml/bidichk](https://github.com/breml/bidichk), [github.com/macabu/inamedparam](https://github.com/macabu/inamedparam), [github.com/tommy-muehle/go-mnd](https://github.com/tommy-muehle/go-mnd), [github.com/4meepo/tagalign](https://github.com/4meepo/tagalign), [github.com/ldez/tagliatelle](https://github.com/ldez/tagliatelle), and [github.com/ldez/usetesting](https://github.com/ldez/usetesting). Single multi-stem file `_bidichk` is `#compdef bidichk inamedparam mnd tagalign tagliatelle usetesting`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`bidichk`** (1 flag + 9-rune enum + positional): breml/bidichk detects Trojan-Source Unicode bidirectional formatting characters (U+202A-U+202E + U+2066-U+2069) — `--disallowed-runes RUNES` (comma-separated; 9-value enum LEFT-TO-RIGHT-EMBEDDING/RIGHT-TO-LEFT-EMBEDDING/POP-DIRECTIONAL-FORMATTING/LEFT-TO-RIGHT-OVERRIDE/RIGHT-TO-LEFT-OVERRIDE/LEFT-TO-RIGHT-ISOLATE/RIGHT-TO-LEFT-ISOLATE/FIRST-STRONG-ISOLATE/POP-DIRECTIONAL-ISOLATE). **`inamedparam`** (1 flag + positional): macabu/inamedparam flags interface methods with unnamed parameters — `-skip-single-param` (exempt single-unnamed-param interfaces). **`mnd`** (4 flags + 6-check enum + positional): tommy-muehle/go-mnd is a magic-number detector — `-checks CHECKS` (6-value enum: `argument`/`assign`/`case`/`condition`/`operation`/`return`, comma-separated), `-ignored-numbers`, `-ignored-functions PATTERNS`, `-ignored-files PATTERNS`. Defaults exclude 0/1, test files, and `time.Date()`. **`tagalign`** (4 flags + positional): 4meepo/tagalign aligns and optionally sorts struct tags — `-fix`, `-sort`, `-order ORDER` (e.g. `json,xml`), `-strict` (dictionary-order grouped-names alignment). **`tagliatelle`** (positional only): ldez/tagliatelle is a struct-tag naming-convention linter; configuration is golangci-lint-primary (camelCase/PascalCase/kebab-case/snake_case/UPPER_SNAKE_CASE/Go-aware variants/header-case/upper/lower per tag), standalone surface is minimal. **`usetesting`** (positional only): ldez/usetesting suggests `t.TempDir`/`t.Setenv`/`t.Chdir`/`t.Context` replacements for `os.*` and `context.Background`/`context.TODO` calls in tests (Go 1.24+ for some); per-detection enable flags configured via golangci-lint primarily. **Blacklist additions (6) across 5 insertion blocks** (tagalign + tagliatelle share one block): 1 between `bicep` (2184) and `biff` (2185) for `bidichk` (within bi* at index 2 `c` (99, cep) < `d` (100, dichk) < `f` (102, ff)); 1 between `inadyn` (11885) and `inbucket` (11886) for `inamedparam` (within ina* at index 3 `d` (100, dyn) < `m` (109, medparam); within in* at index 2 `a` (97, a*) < `b` (98, bucket)); 1 between `mn` (16511) and `mneme` (16512) for `mnd` (within mn* at index 2 end-of-string < `d` (100, d) < `e` (101, eme)); **2 between `taffybar` (25501) and `taglib-config` (25502)** for `tagalign` + `tagliatelle` (within ta* at index 2 `f` (102, ffybar) < `g` (103, g*); within tag* at index 3 `a` (97, align) < `l` (108, liatelle/lib); within tagl* at index 4 `i` (105, both), at index 5 `a` (97, atelle) < `b` (98, b-config)); 1 between `usestdlibvars` (27358) and `usleep` (27359) for `usetesting` (within us* at index 2 `e` (101, e*) < `l` (108, leep); within use* at index 3 `s` (115, stdlibvars) < `t` (116, testing)). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — thirty-second consecutive clean round.
- **Go linter sweep R25 — copyloopvar + dupword + exhaustivestruct + fatcontext + funcorder + promlinter + testableexamples (7 stems via single multi-stem `_copyloopvar` file)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Continues the R21+R22+R23+R24 sweep with 7 more single-purpose analysis/passes-framework linters. Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream READMEs at [github.com/karamaru-alpha/copyloopvar](https://github.com/karamaru-alpha/copyloopvar), [github.com/Abirdcfly/dupword](https://github.com/Abirdcfly/dupword), [github.com/mbilski/exhaustivestruct](https://github.com/mbilski/exhaustivestruct), [github.com/Crocmagnon/fatcontext](https://github.com/Crocmagnon/fatcontext), [github.com/manuelarte/funcorder](https://github.com/manuelarte/funcorder), [github.com/yeya24/promlinter](https://github.com/yeya24/promlinter), and [github.com/maratori/testableexamples](https://github.com/maratori/testableexamples). Single multi-stem file `_copyloopvar` is `#compdef copyloopvar dupword exhaustivestruct fatcontext funcorder promlinter testableexamples`, dispatching on `$service`. **Hunt-note**: `exhaustivestruct` is archived 2022-12-06 but preserved here for historical-codebase use via golangci-lint v1.x. **Per-stem coverage**: **`copyloopvar`** (positional only): karamaru-alpha/copyloopvar flags unnecessary loop-variable copies (`v := v`, `i := i`) addressable since Go 1.22 loop-variable semantics. **`dupword`** (3 flags + positional): Abirdcfly/dupword detects duplicate words in comments + strings — `-fix`, `-keyword WORDS` (target words comma-separated), `-test=true|false` (analyze test files). **`exhaustivestruct`** (1 flag + positional): mbilski/exhaustivestruct flags struct literals with uninitialized fields — `-struct_patterns PATTERNS` (comma-separated package+name matchers). *ARCHIVED 2022-12-06.* **`fatcontext`** (positional only): Crocmagnon/fatcontext detects nested contexts in loops or function literals (e.g. `ctx = context.WithValue(ctx, ...)` inside a loop without `:=` rebinding). **`funcorder`** (4 bool flags + positional): manuelarte/funcorder enforces function/method/constructor ordering per Uber style guide — `-constructor=true|false`, `-struct-method=true|false`, `-alphabetical=true|false`, `-function=true|false`. **`promlinter`** (state-machine with 2 subcommands): yeya24/promlinter is a Prometheus metrics-naming linter built on promlint — `list` (display discovered metrics) and `lint` with `--disable CHECKS` + `--strict`. **`testableexamples`** (positional only): maratori/testableexamples flags Example functions missing `// Output:` comments (without them, `go test` compiles but doesn't execute them — silent rot). **Blacklist additions (7) across 7 insertion blocks**: 1 between `copybara` (4491) and `copyparty` (4492) for `copyloopvar` (within copy* at index 4 `b` (98, bara) < `l` (108, loopvar) < `p` (112, party)); 1 between `duply` (6912) and `dura` (6913) for `dupword` (within dup* at index 3 `l` (108, ly) < `w` (119, word); within du* at index 2 `p` (112, p*) < `r` (114, ra)); 1 between `exhaustive` (7631) and `exif` (7632) for `exhaustivestruct` (within exhaustive* at index 10 end-of-string < `s` (115, struct); within ex* at index 2 `h` (104, haustive*) < `i` (105, if)); 1 between `fatbinary` (7860) and `fatlabel` (7861) for `fatcontext` (within fat* at index 3 `b` (98, binary) < `c` (99, context) < `l` (108, label)); 1 between `funclatency-bpfcc` (8601) and `funcslower` (8602) for `funcorder` (within func* at index 4 `l` (108, latency) < `o` (111, order) < `s` (115, slower)); 1 between `prometheus-config-reloader` (20804) and `prompt` (20805) for `promlinter` (within prom* at index 4 `e` (101, etheus) < `l` (108, linter) < `p` (112, pt)); 1 between `test_be` (25893) and `testament` (25894) for `testableexamples` (within test* at index 4 `_` (95, _be) < `a` (97, a*); within testa* at index 5 `b` (98, bleex) < `m` (109, ment)). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — thirty-first consecutive clean round.
- **Go linter sweep R24 — forcetypeassert + gomoddirectives + interfacebloat + loggercheck + mirror + nestif + nilnil + rowserrcheck (8 stems via single multi-stem `_forcetypeassert` file)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Continues the R21+R22+R23 sweep with 8 more single-purpose analysis/passes-framework linters. Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream READMEs at [github.com/gostaticanalysis/forcetypeassert](https://github.com/gostaticanalysis/forcetypeassert), [github.com/ldez/gomoddirectives](https://github.com/ldez/gomoddirectives), [github.com/sashamelentyev/interfacebloat](https://github.com/sashamelentyev/interfacebloat), [github.com/timonwong/loggercheck](https://github.com/timonwong/loggercheck), [github.com/butuzov/mirror](https://github.com/butuzov/mirror), [github.com/nakabonne/nestif](https://github.com/nakabonne/nestif), [github.com/Antonboom/nilnil](https://github.com/Antonboom/nilnil), and [github.com/jingyugao/rowserrcheck](https://github.com/jingyugao/rowserrcheck). Single multi-stem file `_forcetypeassert` is `#compdef forcetypeassert gomoddirectives interfacebloat loggercheck mirror nestif nilnil rowserrcheck`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`forcetypeassert`** (positional only): gostaticanalysis/forcetypeassert flags unchecked type assertions, enforcing the 2-return form `value, ok := a.(int)`. **`gomoddirectives`** (3 flags + positional): ldez/gomoddirectives enforces policies on `go.mod` directives (retract/replace/exclude/ignore/tool/toolchain/godebug/go/module) — `-local` (allow local replacements), `-exclude` (forbid exclude directives), `-goversion PATTERN` (Go version regex). **`interfacebloat`** (positional only): sashamelentyev/interfacebloat flags interfaces with too many methods per Interface Segregation Principle. **`loggercheck`** (4 flags + 5-logger enum + positional): timonwong/loggercheck detects odd-arity key-value pairs in structured logging (`kitlog`/`klog`/`logr`/`slog`/`zap`) — `-requirestringkey`, `-disable LOGGERS` (5-value enum, comma-separated), `-fix`, `-rulefile FILE`. **`mirror`** (1 flag + positional): butuzov/mirror suggests mirror stdlib functions to avoid `[]byte`/`string` conversions (e.g. `MatchString` instead of `Match` after string→[]byte) — `--with-tests`. **`nestif`** (4 flags + positional): nakabonne/nestif is a cognitive-complexity-of-nested-if linter — `--min N` (threshold, default 1), `--top N` (default 10), `--json`, `--exclude-dirs DIRS`. **`nilnil`** (3 flags + 7-type enum + positional): Antonboom/nilnil flags `return nil, nil` patterns — `--checked-types TYPES` (7-value enum: `chan`/`func`/`iface`/`map`/`ptr`/`uintptr`/`unsafeptr`, comma-separated), `--detect-opposite` (also flag non-nil errors with valid values), `--only-two=true|false` (restrict to 2-return functions). **`rowserrcheck`** (positional only): jingyugao/rowserrcheck checks `sql.Rows.Err()` is called after iteration (forked from bodyclose, addresses silent data-integrity issues). **Blacklist additions (8) across 8 insertion blocks**: 1 between `force` (8382) and `forego` (8383) for `forcetypeassert` (within force* at index 5 end-of-string < `t` (116, type); within for* at index 3 `c` (99, c*) < `e` (101, ego)); 1 between `gomock` (9992) and `gomodifytags` (9993) for `gomoddirectives` (within gomod* at index 5 `d` (100, directives) < `i` (105, ifytags); within gom* at index 4 `c` (99, ock) < `d` (100, od*)); 1 between `interdiff` (12007) and `interlace` (12008) for `interfacebloat` (within inter* at index 5 `d` (100, diff) < `f` (102, facebloat) < `l` (108, lace)); 1 between `logger` (14966) and `login` (14967) for `loggercheck` (within logger* at index 6 end-of-string < `c` (99, check); within log* at index 5 `e` (101, ger*) < `i` (105, in)); 1 between `miri` (16261) and `mirrord` (16262) for `mirror` (within mir* at index 3 `i` (105, miri) < `r` (114, ror); within mirror* at index 6 end-of-string < `d` (100, rrord)); 1 between `nest-cli` (17433) and `nestjs` (17434) for `nestif` (within nest* at index 4 `-` (45, -cli) < `i` (105, if) < `j` (106, js)); 1 between `nilfs_cleanerd` (17663) and `nim` (17664) for `nilnil` (within nil* at index 3 `f` (102, fs*) < `n` (110, nil); within ni* at index 2 `l` (108, l*) < `m` (109, m)); 1 between `rover` (22674) and `rox-filer` (22675) for `rowserrcheck` (within ro* at index 2 `v` (118, ver) < `w` (119, ws*) < `x` (120, x*)). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — thirtieth consecutive clean round (one slot misordering caught + fixed mid-edit; interfacebloat alphabetizes between interdiff and interlace, not between interpreter and interrogate, because `f` (102) precedes `l` (108) at index 5).
- **Go linter sweep R23 — canonicalheader + durationcheck + errchkjson + errname + exhaustive + gochecknoglobals + intrange + ireturn + spancheck (9 stems via single multi-stem `_canonicalheader` file)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Continues the R21+R22 sweep with 9 more single-purpose analysis/passes-framework linters. Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream READMEs at [github.com/lasiar/canonicalheader](https://github.com/lasiar/canonicalheader), [github.com/charithe/durationcheck](https://github.com/charithe/durationcheck), [github.com/breml/errchkjson](https://github.com/breml/errchkjson), [github.com/Antonboom/errname](https://github.com/Antonboom/errname), [github.com/nishanths/exhaustive](https://github.com/nishanths/exhaustive), [github.com/leighmcculloch/gochecknoglobals](https://github.com/leighmcculloch/gochecknoglobals), [github.com/ckaznocha/intrange](https://github.com/ckaznocha/intrange), [github.com/butuzov/ireturn](https://github.com/butuzov/ireturn), and [github.com/jjti/go-spancheck](https://github.com/jjti/go-spancheck). Single multi-stem file `_canonicalheader` is `#compdef canonicalheader durationcheck errchkjson errname exhaustive gochecknoglobals intrange ireturn spancheck`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`canonicalheader`** (positional only): lasiar/canonicalheader checks HTTP header names use canonical capitalization on `http.Header` Get/Set/Add/Del/Values methods. **`durationcheck`** (positional only): charithe/durationcheck detects erroneous `time.Duration` multiplications (e.g. `waitForSeconds(5 * time.Second)` producing ~1388 hours). **`errchkjson`** (1 flag + positional): breml/errchkjson analyzes types passed to `json.Marshal` for safe (basic types) / unsafe (float, interface{}) / forbidden (complex, chan, func, unsafe.Pointer) classification — `-omit-safe`. **`errname`** (positional only): Antonboom/errname enforces sentinel `ErrXxx`/`errXxx` + `XxxError` type naming conventions per Go stdlib. **`exhaustive`** (1 flag + positional): nishanths/exhaustive switch + map exhaustiveness checker for enum-like constant groups — `-check switch|map|switch,map`. **`gochecknoglobals`** (1 flag + positional): leighmcculloch/gochecknoglobals flags global variables (smart-ignores err vars, regex.Compile, embedded files, `_`) — `-test=true|false` (analyze test files). **`intrange`** (positional only): ckaznocha/intrange suggests Go 1.22 integer-range syntax for `for i := 0; i < n; i++ {}` patterns. **`ireturn`** (2 flags + positional + 5 shorthand keywords): butuzov/ireturn enforces "accept interfaces, return concrete types" — `--accept INTERFACES` / `--reject INTERFACES` (comma-separated; shorthand keywords `empty`/`anon`/`error`/`stdlib`/`generic`). **`spancheck`** (3 flags + positional): jjti/go-spancheck is an OpenTelemetry/OpenCensus span linter — `-checks end,set-status,record-error` (3-value enum), `-extra-start-span-signatures SIGS`, `-ignore-check-signatures SIGS`. **Blacklist additions (9) across 9 insertion blocks**: 1 between `canlogserver` (2956) and `canplayer` (2957) for `canonicalheader` (within can* at index 3 `l` (108, logserver) < `o` (111, onicalheader) < `p` (112, player)); 1 between `dura` (6912) and `durdraw` (6913) for `durationcheck` (within dura* at index 4 end-of-string < `t` (116, tion); within dur* at index 3 `a` (97, a*) < `d` (100, draw)); 1 between `errcheck` (7437) and `errinfo` (7438) for `errchkjson` (within errch* at index 5 `e` (101, check) < `k` (107, chkjson); within err* at index 4 `c` (99, check) < `i` (105, info)); 1 between `errinfo` (7438 → line shifted) and `errno` (7439) for `errname` (within err* at index 4 `i` (105, info) < `n` (110, name/no); within errn* at index 5 `a` (97, name) < `o` (111, no)); 1 between `exfatlabel` (7626) and `exif` (7627) for `exhaustive` (within ex* at index 2 `f` (102, fat) < `h` (104, haustive) < `i` (105, if)); 1 between `gocc` (9929) and `gochopchop` (9930) for `gochecknoglobals` (within goc* at index 4 end-of-string < `h` (104, check/hop); within goch* at index 5 `e` (101, check) < `o` (111, hop)); 1 between `intltoolize` (12007) and `inv` (12008) for `intrange` (within in* at index 3 `l` (108, ltool) < `r` (114, range) < `v` (118, v*)); 1 between `iredis` (12230) and `irexec` (12231) for `ireturn` (within ire* at index 3 `d` (100, dis) < `t` (116, turn) < `x` (120, xec)); 1 between `spamprobe` (24452) and `spark-beeline` (24453) for `spancheck` (within spa* at index 3 `m` (109, m*) < `n` (110, ncheck) < `r` (114, rk-*)). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — twenty-ninth consecutive clean round.
- **Go linter sweep R22 — bodyclose + go-errorlint + maintidx + nonamedreturns + testpackage + usestdlibvars (6 stems via single multi-stem `_bodyclose` file)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Continues the R21 sweep with 6 more single-purpose analysis/passes-framework linters. Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream READMEs at [github.com/timakin/bodyclose](https://github.com/timakin/bodyclose), [github.com/polyfloyd/go-errorlint](https://github.com/polyfloyd/go-errorlint), [github.com/yagipy/maintidx](https://github.com/yagipy/maintidx), [github.com/firefart/nonamedreturns](https://github.com/firefart/nonamedreturns), [github.com/maratori/testpackage](https://github.com/maratori/testpackage), and [github.com/sashamelentyev/usestdlibvars](https://github.com/sashamelentyev/usestdlibvars). Single multi-stem file `_bodyclose` is `#compdef bodyclose go-errorlint maintidx nonamedreturns testpackage usestdlibvars`, dispatching on `$service`. Each tool is also runnable via `go vet -vettool=$(which TOOL) PKG/...` and through golangci-lint integration. **Per-stem coverage**: **`bodyclose`** (1 flag + positional): timakin/bodyclose verifies HTTP response bodies are properly closed for TCP connection reuse — `-check-consumption` (also verify body is consumed via io.Copy/io.ReadAll/JSON decoders/buffered readers). **`go-errorlint`** (1 flag + positional): polyfloyd/go-errorlint enforces Go 1.13 error-wrapping patterns (flags `%v` for errors → `%w`; `err == ErrFoo` → `errors.Is`; type assertions → `errors.As`) — `-fix` (apply autofixes). *NOTE: upstream archived; moved to Codeberg.* **`maintidx`** (1 flag + positional): yagipy/maintidx computes function maintainability index (cyclomatic + Halstead volume + LOC composite, Microsoft rebased scale) — `-under N` (show functions below threshold; default 20). **`nonamedreturns`** (1 flag + positional): firefart/nonamedreturns flags named-return values (variable shadowing / accidental modifications / unintended scope expansion concerns) — `-report-error-in-defer` (also report named errors used in defer; default false). **`testpackage`** (4 flags + positional): maratori/testpackage enforces separate `_test` packages for blackbox testing — `-skip-regexp PATTERN` (default `(export|internal)_test\.go`), `-allow-packages NAMES` (default `main`), `-test`, `-fix`. **`usestdlibvars`** (22 flags + positional): sashamelentyev/usestdlibvars suggests stdlib constants for hardcoded values across HTTP/time/crypto/OS/RPC/SQL/TLS domains — general `-V` (version), `-h` (help), `-flags` (JSON flags), `-json`, `-fix`, `-test`; 11 detection toggles `-http-method`, `-http-status-code`, `-time-layout`, `-time-month`, `-time-weekday`, `-os-dev-null`, `-crypto-hash`, `-constant-kind`, `-sql-isolation-level`, `-tls-signature-scheme`, `-rpc-default-path`; profiling/debug `-cpuprofile`, `-memprofile`, `-trace`, `-debug` (subset of `fpstv`), `-c N` (context lines, default -1). **Blacklist additions (6) across 6 insertion blocks**: 1 between `bochs` (2374) and `bodyfile.pl` (2375) for `bodyclose` (within bod* at index 4 `c` (99, close) < `f` (102, file)); 1 between `go-enum` (9896) and `go-feature-flag-relay-proxy` (9897) for `go-errorlint` (within go-e* at index 4 `n` (110, enum) < `r` (114, errorlint); within go-* at index 3 `e` (101, e*) < `f` (102, feature)); 1 between `maim` (15507) and `make` (15508) for `maintidx` (within m-a-* at index 2 `i` (105, maim/maintidx) < `k` (107, make); within mai* at index 3 `m` (109, maim) < `n` (110, maintidx)); 1 between `nona` (17815) and `nono` (17816) for `nonamedreturns` (within nona* at index 4 end-of-string < `m` (109, named); within non* at index 3 `a` (97, nona*) < `o` (111, nono)); 1 between `testkube` (25879) and `testparm` (25880) for `testpackage` (within test* at index 4 `k` (107, kube) < `p` (112, package/parm); within testp* at index 5 `a` (97, both); at index 6 `c` (99, package) < `r` (114, parm)); 1 between `users` (27328) and `usleep` (27329) for `usestdlibvars` (within us* at index 2 `e` (101, users/usestdlibvars) < `l` (108, usleep); within use* at index 3 `r` (114, users) < `s` (115, usestdlibvars)). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — twenty-eighth consecutive clean round.
- **Go linter/analyzer sweep R21 — go-consistent + go-critic + perfguard + ruleguard + structlayout-optimize + structlayout-pretty + testifylint (7 stems via single multi-stem `_go-consistent` file)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream READMEs + main.go source files at [github.com/quasilyte/go-consistent](https://github.com/quasilyte/go-consistent), [github.com/go-critic/go-critic](https://github.com/go-critic/go-critic), [github.com/quasilyte/go-perfguard](https://github.com/quasilyte/go-perfguard), [github.com/quasilyte/go-ruleguard](https://github.com/quasilyte/go-ruleguard), [github.com/dominikh/go-tools/tree/master/cmd/structlayout-optimize](https://github.com/dominikh/go-tools/tree/master/cmd/structlayout-optimize) and [structlayout-pretty](https://github.com/dominikh/go-tools/tree/master/cmd/structlayout-pretty), and [github.com/Antonboom/testifylint](https://github.com/Antonboom/testifylint). Single multi-stem file `_go-consistent` is `#compdef go-consistent go-critic perfguard ruleguard structlayout-optimize structlayout-pretty testifylint`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`go-consistent`** (1 flag + positional): quasilyte/go-consistent identifies inconsistent code patterns where multiple equivalent expressions exist (e.g. `make(map[K]V)` vs `map[K]V{}`) — `-v` (verbose), `--help`, conventional Go-target positional (`std`, `./...`, files, packages). **`go-critic`** (state-machine with 3 subcommands): go-critic/go-critic is a Go linter with ~100 diagnostics — `check <targets>` with `-v` + `-enable CHECKERS_OR_TAGS` (filter via `#diagnostic`/`#style`/`#performance`/`#experimental`/`#opinionated`/`#security` tags) + `-disable`, `version`, `doc <checker>`. **`perfguard`** (state-machine with 2 subcommands): quasilyte/go-perfguard is a Go performance analyzer — `lint` (traditional static analysis) with `--fix`, `optimize` (profile-guided) with `--heatmap CPU_PROFILE` + `--fix`; both take Go-target positional. **`ruleguard`** (5 flags + positional): quasilyte/go-ruleguard is a pattern-based dynamic Go linter — `-rules FILES` (comma-separated ruleguard file paths), `-e RULE` (execute a single rule from a string), `-fix` (apply autofixes), `-c` (display offending lines with context), `-json` (output JSON). **`structlayout-optimize`** (3 flags): dominikh/go-tools cmd/structlayout-optimize reorders struct fields to reduce padding — `-json` (format as JSON), `-r` (break up structs + reorder fields freely), `-version`. **`structlayout-pretty`** (2 flags): dominikh/go-tools cmd/structlayout-pretty pretty-prints structlayout output with ASCII art — `-v` (do not compact consecutive bytes), `-version`. **`testifylint`** (4 flags + positional): Antonboom/testifylint is a testify usage-pattern linter with 25+ checkers — `--enable-all`, `--enable CHECKERS`, `--disable CHECKERS`, `--fix` (autofix). **Blacklist additions (7) across 5 insertion blocks** (go-consistent + go-critic share one block, structlayout-optimize + structlayout-pretty share another): **2 between `go-callvis` (9893) and `go-enum` (9894)** for `go-consistent` + `go-critic` (within go-c* at index 4 `a` (97, callvis) < `o` (111, consistent) < `r` (114, critic); within go-* at index 3 `c` (99, c*) < `e` (101, enum)); 1 between `perf` (19517 → line shifted) and `perfquery` (19518) for `perfguard` (within perf* at index 4 end-of-string < `g` (103, guard) < `q` (113, query)); 1 between `rulegen` (22882 → line shifted) and `rulesync` (22883) for `ruleguard` (within rule* at index 4 `g` (103, both gen/guard); at index 5 `e` (101, gen) < `u` (117, guard); within rul* at index 4 `g` (103, guard) < `s` (115, sync)); **2 between `structlayout` (24987 → line shifted) and `structurizr-cli` (24988)** for `structlayout-optimize` + `structlayout-pretty` (within structlayout* at index 12 end-of-string < `-` (45, layout-*); within structlayout-* at index 13 `o` (111, optimize) < `p` (112, pretty); within struct* at index 6 `l` (108, layout) < `u` (117, urizr)); 1 between `testgdbm` (25870 → line shifted) and `testj1939` (25871) for `testifylint` (within test* at index 4 `g` (103, gdbm) < `i` (105, ifylint) < `j` (106, j1939)). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — twenty-seventh consecutive clean round.
- **Microsoft SARIF tooling R20 — sarif-multitool (1 stem; 19 verbs documented)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surface transcribed directly from upstream [github.com/microsoft/sarif-sdk docs/multitool-usage.md](https://github.com/microsoft/sarif-sdk). Continues the Microsoft security/analysis CLI sweep started in [R19](#) (`_appinspector`). Single-stem file `_sarif-multitool` is `#compdef sarif-multitool`. **Coverage**: `sarif-multitool` is a .NET-Global-Tool installed via `dotnet tool install --global Sarif.Multitool`, invoked as `sarif-multitool`. State-machine dispatches on **19 verbs** grouped into three families: **Primary log operations** (14 verbs): `absoluteuri` (relative→absolute URIs), `apply-policy`, `convert` (with `--tool` 13-value enum: AndroidStudio/ClangAnalyzer/ClangTidy/CppCheck/ContrastSecurity/Fortify/FortifyFpr/FxCop/PREfast/Pylint/SemmleQL/StaticDriverVerifier/TSLint), `export-validation-config`, `export-validation-rules`, `file-work-items`, `match-results-forward` (New/Absent/Unchanged result identification), `merge`, `page` (subset extraction), `query`, `rebaseuri`, `rewrite` (SARIF reformatter), `suppress`, `validate` (schema + correctness rules). **Event-log append flow** (5 verbs): `emit-init-run` (seed event log with SARIF run JSON), `add-result`, `add-notification`, `add-invocation`, `emit-finalize` (replay staged events into final SARIF). **Utility verbs** (2): `help`, `version`. Common `--log` parameter enum across many verbs: ForceOverwrite/Inline/PrettyPrint/Minify/Optimize. Per-verb flag enumeration is sparse upstream; primary verb-list + the convert tool enum + the log options are the well-documented surfaces. **Blacklist additions (1)**: 1 between `sarif` (23222) and `sash` (23223) for `sarif-multitool` (within sarif* at index 5 end-of-string < `-` (45, multitool); within sar* at index 4 `i` (105, sarif) < `m` (no — wait); at index 4 sarif/sarif-multitool both `i`; at index 5 end-of-string < `-`; at index 3 sarif/sarif-multitool/sash all `i` then `f` then end-vs-`-` vs `h`; sar* family ordering: sar < sar.sysstat < sarien < sarif < sarif-multitool < sash). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — twenty-sixth consecutive clean round.
- **Microsoft security/analysis CLI suite R19 — appinspector + devskim + 10x OSSGadget oss-* tools (12 stems via single multi-stem `_appinspector` file)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream READMEs + wiki pages at [github.com/microsoft/ApplicationInspector](https://github.com/microsoft/ApplicationInspector), [github.com/microsoft/DevSkim](https://github.com/microsoft/DevSkim) (Analyze Command wiki), and per-tool [github.com/microsoft/OSSGadget wiki pages](https://github.com/microsoft/OSSGadget/wiki). **Hunt-defer log this round**: `oss-characteristic` (wiki page exists but enumerates no flags; defer per the gh-grep precedent), `oss-find-domain-squats` (wiki page not enumerated; defer per the gh-grep precedent), `oss-reproducible` (wiki page not enumerated; defer per the gh-grep precedent). Single multi-stem file `_appinspector` is `#compdef appinspector devskim oss-defog oss-detect-backdoor oss-detect-cryptography oss-diff oss-download oss-find-source oss-find-squats oss-health oss-metadata oss-risk-calculator`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`appinspector`** (state-machine with 7 subcommands; `analyze` carries 4 short flags from README + many additional flags deferred to wiki page 1): microsoft/ApplicationInspector is a .NET-Global-Tool static analyzer with 400+ rule patterns — subcommands `analyze`/`tagdiff`/`exporttags`/`verifyrules`/`packrules`/`help`/`version`; `analyze` carries `-s` (source, required), `-f` (format html/json/sarif/markdown), `-o` (output file), `-g` (glob exclude). **`devskim`** (state-machine with `analyze` subcommand carrying **~30 flags**): microsoft/DevSkim is a .NET-Global-Tool security analyzer with inline IDE integration — required `-I/--source-code PATH`, `-O/--output-file FILE`; rules `--options-json`, `-r`, `--rule-ids`, `--ignore-rule-ids`, `--languages`, `--comments`; output `-o/--output-format`, `-f/--file-format` (text/sarif), `-s/--severity`, `--confidence`, `-g/--ignore-globs`; processing `-d/--disable-supression`, `--disable-parallel`, `-i/--ignore-default-rules`, `-c/--crawl-archives`, `--skip-git-ignored-files`; paths `--base-path`, `--absolute-path`, `--skip-excerpts`; logging `-x/--console-verbosity`, `--disable-console`, `-v/--log-file-level`, `-l/--log-file-path`; utility `-E` (exit code = issue count). **`oss-defog`** (10 flags + PURL): microsoft/OSSGadget oss-defog finds Base64/hex-encoded obfuscated text — `--download-directory`, `--report-blobs`, `--minimum-hex-length N` (default 8), `--minimum-base64-length N` (default 1), three `--save-found-binaries-to`/`--save-archives-to`/`--save-blobs-to` directory flags, `--use-cache`, `--help`, `--version`. **`oss-detect-backdoor`** (6 flag pairs + PURL): oss-detect-backdoor flags potential malicious code (high false-positive rate per upstream) — `-d/--download-directory`, `-f/--format` (text/sarifv1/sarifv2), `-o/--output-file`, `-c/--use-cache`. **`oss-detect-cryptography`** (9 flags + PURL): oss-detect-cryptography finds cryptographic implementations — `--verbose`, `--custom-rule-directory`, `--disable-default-rules`, `--download-directory`, `--use-cache`, `--format`, `--output-file`. **`oss-diff`** (12 flag pairs + PURL): oss-diff compares two package versions — `-d/--download-directory`, `-c/--use-cache`, `-w/--crawl-archives` (default true), `-B/--context-before N`, `-A/--context-after N`, `-C/--context N` (-1 = all, overrides B/A), `-a/--added-only`, `-r/--removed-only`, `-f/--format`, `-o/--output-location`. **`oss-download`** (6 flag pairs + PURL): oss-download retrieves and optionally extracts packages — `-x/--download-directory` (default cwd), `-m/--download-metadata-only`, `-e/--extract`, `-c/--use-cache`. **`oss-find-source`** (5 flag pairs + PURL): oss-find-source locates source repositories — `-f/--format`, `-o/--output-file`, `-S/--single` (top possibility only). **`oss-find-squats`** (6 flag pairs + PURL): oss-find-squats detects typo-squatting candidates — `-o/--output-file`, `-f/--format`, `-q/--quiet`, `-s/--sleep-delay N` (ms between checks). **`oss-health`** (4 flag pairs + PURL): oss-health collects project health metrics — `-f/--format`, `-o/--output-file`. **`oss-metadata`** (3 flag pairs + PURL): oss-metadata fetches package metadata from deps.dev or libraries.io — `-o/--output-file`. **`oss-risk-calculator`** (9 flag pairs + PURL): oss-risk-calculator generates risk assessments — required `-d/--download-directory`, optional `-r/--external-risk N`, `-f/--format`, `-o/--output-file`, `-n/--no-health`, `--verbose`, `-c/--use-cache`. **Blacklist additions (12) across 3 insertion blocks**: 1 between `appimagetool` (1138) and `appium` (1139) for `appinspector` (within app* at index 3 'i' (105, both) consistent; at index 4 'm' (109, appimage*) < 'n' (110, appinspector) < 'u' (117, appium)); 1 between `devs` (6070 → line shifted) and `devspace` (6071) for `devskim` (within dev* at index 4 's' (115, all) consistent; at index 5 end-of-string < 'k' (107, devskim) < 'p' (112, devspace)); **10 between `osqueryi` (18725 → line shifted) and `ossec-agent` (18726)** for the entire oss-* family (within os* at index 2 'q' (113, osqueryi) < 's' (115, oss-*); within oss* at index 3 '-' (45, all oss-*) < 'e' (101, ossec); within oss-* the 10 stems alphabetize as `defog < detect-backdoor < detect-cryptography < diff < download < find-source < find-squats < health < metadata < risk-calculator`). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — twenty-fifth consecutive clean round.
- **Mixed-theme R18 — fetch-master-6000/fm6000 + gh-download + gh-setup (4 stems via single multi-stem `_fetch-master-6000` file; Perl neofetch-style system info + gh-extension release-asset/download tooling)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream READMEs at [github.com/anhsirk0/fetch-master-6000](https://github.com/anhsirk0/fetch-master-6000), [github.com/yuler/gh-download](https://github.com/yuler/gh-download), and [github.com/k1LoW/gh-setup](https://github.com/k1LoW/gh-setup). Single multi-stem file `_fetch-master-6000` is `#compdef fetch-master-6000 fm6000 gh-download gh-setup`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`fetch-master-6000`/`fm6000`** (single dispatch arm — `fm6000` is the actual binary name, `fetch-master-6000` is the project name and an occasional packaging alias; 25+ flags with a **9-way character-mutex** spanning short + long aliases): anhsirk0/fetch-master-6000 is a Perl-based neofetch-style system info display for Linux/BSD with selectable Dilbert characters — character mutex covers `-w/--wally`, `-dog/--dogbert`, `-al/--alice`, `-phb`, `-as/--asok`, `-r/--random`, `-f/--file` (load from file), `-rd/--random-dir DIR` (random from directory); custom-display flags `-o/--os`, `-k/--kernel`, `-d/--de`, `-sh/--shell`, `-u/--uptime`, `-p/--package`, `-nd/--not_de` (WM instead of DE), `-v/--vnstat`; styling `-c/--color` (17-value enum: 8 base + 8 bright + `random`), `-s/--say`, `-sf/--say-file`; layout `-m/--margin`, `-g/--gap` (default 10), `-l/--length` (default 13). **`gh-download`** (positionals only): yuler/gh-download downloads folders/files from a GitHub repo via the Git tree API — positionals accept `OWNER/REPO PATH` or a GitHub URL; paths ending with `/` are treated as folders. **`gh-setup`** (11 flags): k1LoW/gh-setup installs GitHub release assets (works as gh-extension, standalone CLI, and GitHub Action) — required `--repo OWNER/REPO`, `--version` (default latest), `--os` (linux/darwin/windows/freebsd enum), `--arch` (amd64/arm64/386/arm enum), `--bin-match PATTERN`, `--checksum`, `--force`, `--strict`, `--verify-attestation` (uses `gh attestation verify`), `--attestation-flags`, `--gh-setup-version`. **Blacklist additions (4) across 4 insertion blocks**: 1 between `fetch-ebook-metadata` (7976) and `fetch_file` (7977) for `fetch-master-6000` (within fetch-* at index 6 `e` (101, ebook) < `m` (109, master); within fetch* at index 5 `-` (45, both `-`) < `_` (95, _file)); 1 between `fm` (8284) and `fmadm` (8285) for `fm6000` (within fm* at index 2 end-of-string < `6` (54, fm6000) < `a` (97, fmadm)); 1 between `gh-difftool` (9234) and `gh-drop` (9235) for `gh-download` (within gh-d* at index 4 `i` (105, difftool) < `o` (111, download) < `r` (114, drop)); Single multi-stem file `_gh-org-clone` is `#compdef gh-org-clone gh-pr-repl gh-provision-envs gh-start gh-submodule-add`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`gh-org-clone`** (10 short/long flag pairs + `--default`): crguezl/gh-org-clone batch-clones all repos in an org via Node + optional fzf interactive selection — `-s/--search`, `-r/--regexp`, `-c/--csr` (comma-separated repo list), `-f/--file` (load repo list from file), `-k/--fork` 3-value enum (`true|only|false`), `-n/--dryrun`, `-o/--org`, `--default` (set org as default for future use), `-D/--depth` (shallow clone), `-p/--parallel`. **`gh-pr-repl`** (interactive REPL with positionals): nobe4/gh-pr-repl is a Ruby-based interactive REPL for managing PRs — bare invocation uses PR from current branch, positionals accept `OWNER/REPO/BRANCH` or PR URLs (one or many for multi-PR sessions). **`gh-provision-envs`** (2 flags): AyogoHealth/gh-provision-envs provisions environment variables + secrets from a JSON config — `-c CONFIG.json` (required) + `-R OWNER/REPO`; upstream documents the JSON shape uses an `environments` object with `variables` + `secrets` arrays of `{name,value}` pairs and will **overwrite** existing values. **`gh-start`** (1 positional): balvig/gh-start (Ruby Cookpad-derived) creates a branch and opens a draft PR — `gh start "description"` for free-text or `gh start [issue-url]` to auto-populate from a GitHub issue. **`gh-submodule-add`** (7 short/long flag pairs): crguezl/gh-submodule-add batch-adds repos as submodules via Node + optional fzf — `-C/--clone` (clone only, skip submodule setup), `-o/--org`, `-s/--search`, `-r/--regexp`, `-k/--fork`, `-p/--parallel` (default 2), `-D/--depth`. **Blacklist additions (5) across 5 insertion blocks**: 1 between `gh-notify` (9253) and `gh-ost` (9254) for `gh-org-clone` (within gh-* at index 3 `n` (110, notify) < `o` (111, org/ost); positional 1 = file path. **`gh-view-md`** (1 flag + 1 positional): joshbeckman/gh-view-md renders issues/PRs as markdown for LLM consumption — `--max-diff LINES` (default 800), positional 1 = issue or PR URL. **`gh-xplr`** (1 positional): sayanarijit/gh-xplr is an interactive repo browser using the xplr TUI — single `<username>/<repository>` positional. **Blacklist additions (4) across 3 insertion blocks** (gh-bbc2gh + gh-blame-pr share one block): **2 between `gh` (9214) and `gh-board` (9215)** for `gh-bbc2gh` + `gh-blame-pr` (within gh-* at index 2 end-of-string for `gh` < `-` (45); 4 token flags `--source-pat`/`--target-pat`/`--github-source-pat`/`--github-target-pat` for gh-gei-style auth; behavior toggles `--verbose`/`--skip-envs`/`--skip-overwrite`/`--org-to-org`; custom endpoints `--source-endpoint`/`--target-endpoint`. **Blacklist additions (5) across 5 insertion blocks**: 1 between `gh-drop` (9232) and `gh-f` (9233) for `gh-ec2` (within gh-* at index 3 `d` (100, drop) < `e` (101, ec2) < `f` (102, f)); Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream READMEs at [github.com/Nick2bad4u/gh-secret-sync](https://github.com/Nick2bad4u/gh-secret-sync), [github.com/Nick2bad4u/gh-runs-cleanup](https://github.com/Nick2bad4u/gh-runs-cleanup), and [github.com/mona-actions/gh-repo-stats-plus](https://github.com/mona-actions/gh-repo-stats-plus). **Hunt-defer log this round**: `gh-contribution-mate` (trunklabs/gh-contribution-mate — README describes features but doesn't name CLI flags), `gh-polyrepos` (YanZisuka/gh-polyrepos — README describes purpose but doesn't enumerate subcommand/flag names), `gh-edu` (gh-cli-for-education/gh-edu — README references plugin-developer conventions `-f/--force`, `-s/--silence` but doesn't document the extension's own complete CLI surface) — all 3 deferred per the gh-grep precedent. Single multi-stem file `_gh-secret-sync` is `#compdef gh-secret-sync gh-runs-cleanup gh-repo-stats-plus`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`gh-secret-sync`** (11 flags with **2 mutex groups**): Nick2bad4u/gh-secret-sync safely syncs secrets across repos/envs/orgs; default is dry-run, **`--confirm` ↔ `--yes` mutex** unlocks application; **4-way `--secret-value-{env|file|prompt|stdin}` mutex** encodes mutually exclusive secret-source channels (env-var, file, interactive prompt, stdin pipe); `--secret-name`, repeatable `*--set NAME=VALUE` + `*--set-env NAME=VALUE`, `--plan-file JSON_OR_CSV`, `--json` machine-readable output. **`gh-runs-cleanup`** (15 flags with **2 mutex groups**): Nick2bad4u/gh-runs-cleanup bulk-deletes GitHub Actions workflow runs; default is dry-run, **`--confirm` ↔ `--yes` mutex** unlocks deletion; **3-way `--json` ↔ `--verbose` ↔ `--quiet` mutex** for output verbosity; repeatable filters (`*--status` with 11-value enum, `*--workflow`), single filters (`--branch`/`--event` with 5-value enum/`--user`/`--commit`), safety controls (`--repo`, `--max-delete N`, `--fail-fast`). **`gh-repo-stats-plus`** (state-machine with **8 subcommands** + no per-subcommand flags surfaced): mona-actions/gh-repo-stats-plus is a comprehensive repo-stats collector — `repo-stats`/`project-stats`/`org-repos`/`combine-stats`/`post-process`/`rows-to-columns`/`package-stats`/`codespace-stats`. Per-subcommand flags are deliberately not surfaced because upstream README only lists subcommand names and purposes without enumerating flags — surfacing them would be vibe-coding. **Blacklist additions (3) across 2 insertion blocks** (gh-repo-stats-plus + gh-runs-cleanup share one block): **2 between `gh-repo-stats` (9256) and `gh-s` (9257)** for `gh-repo-stats-plus` + `gh-runs-cleanup` (within gh-repo-stats* at index 13 end-of-string < `-` (45, plus); within gh-r* at index 4 `e` (101, repo) < `u` (117, runs); within gh-r* at index 3 `r` (114, both) consistent and at index 4 `u` (117, runs) < `s` (115, s) — actually no, `s` (115) is at index 3 of `gh-s` not index 4; at index 3 `r` (114) < `s` (115) so gh-r* < gh-s); 1 between `gh-screensaver` (9259 — line shifted by this round's 2-block) and `gh-signoff` (9260) for `gh-secret-sync` (within gh-s* at index 4 `c` (99, screensaver) < `e` (101, secret) < `i` (105, signoff)). **all 6 flags share one mutex group** because `-c`/`--classic` and `-m`/`--modern` and `-t`/`--type` are 3 different ways of expressing the same 2-value enum (`classic|modern`); upstream documents short-flag precedence over `--type` value and an `EXCUSE_TYPE` env-var fallback. **`gh-drop`** (state-machine with `auth` subcommand + 3 flags): drakulavich/gh-drop uploads images to GitHub issues/PRs without a browser — top-level repeatable `*--image FILE` + `--issue NUMBER` + `--body TEXT` (uses `<!-- gh-drop:IMAGE:N -->` placeholders for image positioning); `auth` subcommand with `login`/`logout` positional sub-action. **`gh-feed`** (2 flags): jo-es/gh-feed is a TUI for PR discussion reading and replying — `--pr NUMBER` (preselect) + `--repo OWNER/REPO`. **`gh-ghq-cd`** (5 flags): cappyzawa/gh-ghq-cd is a ghq-managed repo navigator with tmux/zellij integration — `-w` (new window/tab), `-p` (pane split), **`-H` ↔ `-V` mutex** for split direction, `-c COMMAND` for command-to-run-in-new-pane. **`gh-ranking`** (2 flags + 2 positionals): yukukotani/gh-ranking displays org-wide activity rankings — positional 1 = organization, positional 2 = action enum (`open-pr|review-pr`), `--team TEAM` filter, `--query QUERY` for additional GitHub search filters. **`gh-sparkle`** (5 flags): dyxushuai/gh-sparkle is an AI-driven commit-message generator via GitHub Models — `--language LANG` (description language; header stays English), `--examples N` (1-20, default 3), `--model MODEL` (defaults to `openai/gpt-4o-mini` per upstream), `--dry-run`, `--summary-only` (skip diff details for sensitive code). **Blacklist additions (6) across 6 insertion blocks**: 1 between `gh-board` (9215) and `gh-changelog` (9216) for `gh-bofh` (within gh-b* at index 4 `o` (111, both); Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream READMEs at [github.com/uzimaru0000/gh-board](https://github.com/uzimaru0000/gh-board), [github.com/daido1976/gh-default-branch](https://github.com/daido1976/gh-default-branch), [github.com/MH4GF/gh-prj](https://github.com/MH4GF/gh-prj), [github.com/balfons/gh-prpeek](https://github.com/balfons/gh-prpeek), and [github.com/Kesin11/gh-workflow-ls](https://github.com/Kesin11/gh-workflow-ls). **Hunt-skip log this round**: `gh-traffic` (yskszk63/gh-traffic — bare `gh traffic` only, no flags documented; skip per the bare-TUI-no-surface precedent), `gh-activity` (nogtk/gh-activity — interactive wrapper around `gh pr list` with no documented CLI flags; skip per the same precedent). Single multi-stem file `_gh-board` is `#compdef gh-board gh-default-branch gh-prj gh-prpeek gh-workflow-ls`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`gh-board`** (2 flags + 1 positional): uzimaru0000/gh-board is a Rust TUI kanban board for GitHub Projects V2 — `--owner LOGIN` (organization/user; `@me` for current account), `--no-cache`, optional `<project-number>` positional (omitting both runs the interactive project picker). **`gh-default-branch`** (state-machine with 2 subcommands): daido1976/gh-default-branch shows or renames a repo's default branch — `show` (with `-n/--name-only` for just-the-name output), `rename <new-name>`. Upstream README explicitly warns rename does **not** re-target open PRs, update draft releases, move branch protection rules, redirect web requests, or notify contributors — surface it as plain CLI, no special warnings. **`gh-prj`** (state-machine with 2 subcommands + 2 global flags): MH4GF/gh-prj browses and inspects GitHub Projects — `list` / `view` subcommands + global `-h` (help) + `-V` (version). **`gh-prpeek`** (6 flags): balfons/gh-prpeek is a live PR-status monitor — `--repos OWNER/REPO[,...]`, `--interval SECS` (default 60), `--labels L1[,L2,...]`, `--notify` (enabled by default), `--hide-checks`, `--show-labels`. **`gh-workflow-ls`** (1 flag + 1 positional): Kesin11/gh-workflow-ls expands reusable-workflow and composite-action calls in a GitHub Actions run — `-t TOKEN` (overrides `$GITHUB_TOKEN`) + positional run URL of the shape `https://github.com/OWNER/REPO/actions/runs/RUN_ID`. **Blacklist additions (5) across 4 insertion blocks** (gh-prj + gh-prpeek share one block): 1 between `gh` (9214) and `gh-changelog` (9215) for `gh-board` (within gh-* at index 3 `b` (98, board) < `c` (99, changelog); at index 2 end-of-string for `gh` < `-` (45)); 1 between `gh-dash` (9226 — line shifted by round 165's `gh-difftool` insert) and `gh-difftool` (9227) for `gh-default-branch` (within gh-d* at index 4 `a` (97, dash) < `e` (101, default) < `i` (105, difftool)); **2 between `gh-prism` (9244 — line shifted) and `gh-pv` (9245)** for `gh-prj` + `gh-prpeek` (within gh-pr* at index 5 `i` (105, prism) < `j` (106, prj) < `p` (112, prpeek); within gh-p* at index 4 `r` (114, pr*) < `v` (118, pv)); 1 between `gh-user-stars` (9260 — line shifted) and `gh-worktree` (9261) for `gh-workflow-ls` (within gh-w* at index 7 `f` (102, workflow) < `t` (116, worktree); within gh-* at index 3 `u` (117, user) < `w` (119, w*)). Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream READMEs at [github.com/speedyleion/gh-difftool](https://github.com/speedyleion/gh-difftool), [github.com/kawarimidoll/gh-prism](https://github.com/kawarimidoll/gh-prism), [github.com/carlsberg/gh-releaser](https://github.com/carlsberg/gh-releaser), [github.com/timrogers/gh-sizer](https://github.com/timrogers/gh-sizer), and [github.com/KOBA789/gh-sql](https://github.com/KOBA789/gh-sql). **Hunt-defer log this round**: `gh-describe` (proudust/gh-describe — README documents GitHub Actions input names but not CLI flag names for the binary invocation; defer per the gh-grep precedent), `gh-news` (chmouel/gh-news — interactive TUI with only bare `gh news` and a config file documented, no CLI flags surface; skip per bare-TUI-no-surface precedent). Single multi-stem file `_gh-difftool` is `#compdef gh-difftool gh-prism gh-releaser gh-sizer gh-sql`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`gh-difftool`** (3 named flags + 2 positionals): speedyleion/gh-difftool launches a visual difftool over PR changes — `-t/--tool TOOL` (override) + `--name-only` + `--skip-to FILE` + `--rotate-to FILE`; positional 1 = PR (number/URL/default current-branch's PR); files after `--` limit comparison scope. Also reads `GH_DIFFTOOL` env var and falls back to `git config diff.tool`/`merge.tool`. **`gh-prism`** (4 flags + 1 positional): kawarimidoll/gh-prism is a Rust+ratatui TUI for PR review — `--repo OWNER/REPO`, `--no-cache`, **`--light` ↔ `--dark` mutex** for theme override, `<PR_NUMBER>` positional. **`gh-releaser`** (state-machine with 4 subcommands): carlsberg/gh-releaser is a GitFlow release helper that creates branches + drafts releases + opens PRs — `start <tag>` (initiates the cycle), `close` (merges and publishes), `update <new tag>` (rename + update docs), `pr` (print current open release PR number). **`gh-sizer`** (state-machine with 2 subcommands): timrogers/gh-sizer wraps `git-sizer` over remote repos — `repo <owner/repo>` (clone+size+remove) and `generate-script <user-or-org>` (batch automation); both carry `--output-format text|json`. **`gh-sql`** (3 flag pairs + 2 positionals): KOBA789/gh-sql queries GitHub Projects via SQL (SELECT/DELETE/UPDATE with restrictions on Title/Assignees/Labels/Milestone/Repository) — `-e/--execute SQL` (non-interactive), `-o/--output table|json|initial` (3-value enum), `-oj` (shortcut for `--output json`), positional 1 = owner, positional 2 = project number. **Blacklist additions (5) across 5 insertion blocks**: 1 between `gh-dash` (9226) and `gh-f` (9227) for `gh-difftool` (within gh-d* at index 4 `a` (97, dash) < `i` (105, difftool); at index 3 `d` (100, dash/difftool) < `f` (102, f)); 1 between `gh-poi` (9241) and `gh-pv` (9242) for `gh-prism` (within gh-p* at index 4 `o` (111, poi) < `r` (114, prism) < `v` (118, pv)); 1 between `gh-pv` (9242) and `gh-repo-list` (9243) for `gh-releaser` (at index 3 `p` (112, pv) < `r` (114, releaser/repo); within gh-re* at index 5 `l` (108, releaser) < `p` (112, repo-list/repo-stats)); 1 between `gh-signoff` (9248) and `gh-skyline` (9249) for `gh-sizer` (within gh-s* at index 4 `i` (105, signoff/sizer) consistent at 4; within gh-si* at index 5 `g` (103, signoff) < `z` (122, sizer); at index 4 within gh-s* `i` (105, sizer) < `k` (107, skyline)); 1 between `gh-slack` (9250) and `gh-stack` (9251) for `gh-sql` (within gh-s* at index 4 `l` (108, slack) < `q` (113, sql) < `t` (116, stack)).
