Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream READMEs at [github.com/davidraviv/gh-clean-branches](https://github.com/davidraviv/gh-clean-branches), [github.com/matt-bartel/gh-clone-org](https://github.com/matt-bartel/gh-clone-org), [github.com/rnorth/gh-combine-prs](https://github.com/rnorth/gh-combine-prs), [github.com/redraw/gh-install](https://github.com/redraw/gh-install), [github.com/andyfeller/gh-montage](https://github.com/andyfeller/gh-montage), and [github.com/mona-actions/gh-repo-stats](https://github.com/mona-actions/gh-repo-stats) — the last one resolves the round-160 `gh-repo-stats` deferral now that the correct owner-prefix `mona-actions` is known. **Hunt-defer log this round** — four real cases held back: `gh-pr-review` (agynio/gh-pr-review — README describes "command categories" (Review Management / Comment Threading / Thread Resolution) without naming specific subcommands or flags; defer per the gh-grep / gh-aw / gh-actions-importer precedent), `gh-branch` (mislav/gh-branch — bare `gh branch` interactive fzf switcher with no documented flags; defer), `gh-net` (github/gh-net — ARCHIVED 2023-10-20, README states it's bare `gh net` only; skip per archived-no-surface precedent), `gh-gei` (github/gh-gei — Migration CLI with 4 sub-extensions ado2gh/bbs2gh/gl2gh/gei and a `generate-script` subcommand; README enumerates environment variables but no specific CLI flag inventory; defer). Single multi-stem file `_gh-clean-branches` is `#compdef gh-clean-branches gh-clone-org gh-combine-prs gh-install gh-montage gh-repo-stats`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`gh-clean-branches`** (3 flags): davidraviv/gh-clean-branches deletes local branches missing upstream and unpushed commits — `--dry-run`, `--force` (overrides safety via `git branch -D`), `--verbose`. **`gh-clone-org`** (6 flag pairs + ORG positional): matt-bartel/gh-clone-org clones all repos in an organization — `-t/--topic TOPIC` (filter), `-s/--search QUERY` (overrides `--topic`), `-p/--path PATH` (clone destination), `-y/--yes` (skip prompts), `-n/--dry-run`, `-h/--help`. **`gh-combine-prs`** (4 flags): rnorth/gh-combine-prs combines multiple PRs into a single review unit (e.g. Dependabot batches); ARCHIVED 2025-12-23 but surface stable enough to keep — `--query QUERY` (required), `--selected-pr-numbers` (comma-separated), `--limit N` (default 50), `--skip-pr-check` (combine regardless of CI status). **`gh-install`** (1 positional): redraw/gh-install installs binary/.deb releases from a GitHub repo — single `<user>/<repo>` positional; respects `$GH_BINPATH` env var (default `$HOME/.local/bin`). **`gh-montage`** (5 flag pairs + 1 positional): andyfeller/gh-montage creates avatar montages — `-a/--avatar-pixels N` (default 48), `-m/--montage-width N` (default 58), `-o/--output-file NAME`, `-f/--force`, `-d/--debug`; positional accepts `<organization>`, `<organization>/<team>`, or `<path/to/username_file>`; requires ImageMagick 7.1.0+. **`gh-repo-stats`** (1 flag): mona-actions/gh-repo-stats generates repository statistics for migrations — only `--org ORG_NAME` is explicitly named in the README; conservatively surfaces only what's documented (per the no-vibe-coding rule). **Blacklist additions (6) across 5 insertion blocks** (gh-clone-org + gh-combine-prs share one block): 1 between `gh-clean` (9216) and `gh-clean-forks` (9217 — line shifted by round 161) for `gh-clean-branches` (within gh-clean* at index 9 end-of-string < `-` (45); within gh-clean-* at index 10 `b` (98, branches) < `f` (102, forks)); **2 between `gh-cli2` (9218) and `gh-copilot` (9219)** for `gh-clone-org` + `gh-combine-prs` (within gh-c* at index 4 `l` (108, cli2/clone) < `o` (111, combine/copilot); Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream READMEs at [github.com/chelnak/gh-changelog](https://github.com/chelnak/gh-changelog), [github.com/vilmibm/gh-screensaver](https://github.com/vilmibm/gh-screensaver), [github.com/advanced-security/gh-sbom](https://github.com/advanced-security/gh-sbom), [github.com/rneatherway/gh-slack](https://github.com/rneatherway/gh-slack), [github.com/leereilly/gh-yule-log](https://github.com/leereilly/gh-yule-log), and [github.com/github/gh-models](https://github.com/github/gh-models). **Hunt-skip log this round** — three real-but-deferrable cases: `gh-actions-cache` (was `actions/gh-actions-cache`; ARCHIVED 2024-10-21, README explicitly states the functionality is now integrated into native `gh cache`, so a snowflake completion would advertise deprecated commands), `gh-profile` (was `gabe565/gh-profile`; ARCHIVED, README states "Official multi-account support was added to the `gh` cli in v2.40.0"), and `gh-grep` (real at `k1LoW/gh-grep` but the README enumerates capabilities — `--include`/`--exclude`/line-numbers/URLs/match-counts/filename-only/branch/tag/owner — without specific short-vs-long flag names; defer until exact flag names are upstream-documented). Single multi-stem file `_gh-changelog` is `#compdef gh-changelog gh-screensaver gh-sbom gh-slack gh-yule-log gh-models`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`gh-changelog`** (state-machine with 3 subcommands + 4 flags on `new`): chelnak/gh-changelog is a Keep-a-Changelog generator — subcommands `new`/`show`/`config`; `new` carries `--next-version`, `--from-version`, `--latest`, and `--logger spinner|console` (2-value enum, auto-switches to `console` in CI). **`gh-screensaver`** (3 flags + per-screensaver extra args): vilmibm/gh-screensaver picks a random terminal animation by default, or `-s NAME` runs a specific one (5-value enum `fireworks|starfield|pipes|pollock|life`), `-l` lists available ones (mutex with `-s`); `--color full|off` (2-value enum) overrides color; per-screensaver options come after `--`. **`gh-sbom`** (2 flags): advanced-security/gh-sbom generates Software Bill of Materials — default SPDX format, `-c` switches to CycloneDX, `-l` enriches CycloneDX output with licensing info from ClearlyDefined. **`gh-slack`** (state-machine with 4 subcommands): rneatherway/gh-slack archives Slack conversations as markdown — `read` (1 positional Slack permalink), `send` (channel + message positionals), `api` (direct API call), `auth` (retrieve credentials). **`gh-yule-log`** (1 flag): leereilly/gh-yule-log is a festive Yule-log terminal animation — `--contribs` customizes the display around your GitHub contribution history. **`gh-models`** (state-machine with 4 subcommands): github/gh-models integrates AI model capabilities directly into the gh CLI — `list` (no args), `run MODEL PROMPT` (e.g. `openai/gpt-4o-mini`), `eval *.prompt.yml`, `generate` (PromptPex test-case auto-generation). **Blacklist additions (6) across 6 insertion blocks**: 1 between `gh` (9214) and `gh-clean` (9215) for `gh-changelog` (within gh* at index 2 end-of-string < `-` (45); - **gh-extension Round 3 — gh-stack + gh-s + gh-token + gh-f (4 stems via single multi-stem `_gh-stack` file; 4 more gh-extensions from the topics/gh-extension star-sorted index in the 380–432 star band)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream READMEs at [github.com/github/gh-stack](https://github.com/github/gh-stack), [github.com/gennaro-tedesco/gh-s](https://github.com/gennaro-tedesco/gh-s), [github.com/Link-/gh-token](https://github.com/Link-/gh-token), and [github.com/gennaro-tedesco/gh-f](https://github.com/gennaro-tedesco/gh-f). **Hunt-defer log this round**: `gh-repo-stats` and `gh-actions-cache` — both listed in the topics index but my speculated `owner/repo` paths 404'd; without the correct upstream owner-prefix the README is unreachable, so defer until the right owner surfaces. Single multi-stem file `_gh-stack` is `#compdef gh-stack gh-s gh-token gh-f`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`gh-stack`** (state-machine with **19 documented subcommands** + 5 flags): github/gh-stack manages stacked PRs as ordered branch chains with linked GitHub PRs and metadata stored in `.git/gh-stack/*.json` — subcommands span 4 categories: Stack Management (`init`/`add`/`modify`/`submit`), Navigation (`up`/`down`/`top`/`bottom`/`trunk`/`switch`), Sync (`rebase`/`sync`/`push`), Utilities (`view`/`checkout`/`link`/`unstack`/`feedback`/`alias`); flags `--auto` (skip PR title prompts), `--force-with-lease` (safe force-push), `--numbered` (auto-increment `feat/01`/`feat/02`), and **`--downstack` ↔ `--upstack` mutex** for partial-stack rebases. **`gh-s`** (7 flag pairs + 1 positional): gennaro-tedesco/gh-s is an fzf-driven interactive repository search — `-l/--lang LANGUAGE`, `-d/--desc DESC`, `-u/--user USER`, `-t/--topic TOPIC`, `-c/--colour COLOUR`, `-L/--limit N` (default 20), `-E/--empty` (search by flags alone, no name prompt), plus a positional search term that can replace the interactive prompt; output is the selected repo's URL to stdout. **`gh-token`** (state-machine with 4 subcommands + per-subcommand flags): Link-/gh-token generates short-lived GitHub App installation tokens (1-hour expiry) — `generate` carries **`--key FILE` ↔ `--base64-key VALUE` mutex** plus `--app-id ID` + `--installation-id ID` + optional `--hostname HOST` (for GHES); `revoke` takes the token as a positional; `installations` lists GitHub App installations; `help` shows command docs. **`gh-f`** (11 single-letter subcommand aliases — mutex set + 11 long-form subcommand names): gennaro-tedesco/gh-f is an fzf-extension covering everyday Git/GitHub workflows — each subcommand has both a short alias (`-a`/`-p`/`-b`/`-l`/`-t`/`-R`/`-r`/`-g`/`-s`/`-m`/`-k`, all in a single `_arguments` mutex group) and a long name (`adds`/`prs`/`branches`/`logs`/`tags`/`releases`/`runs`/`greps`/`search`/`myissue`/`pick`); running `gh f` with no arg prints help. **Blacklist additions (4) across 4 insertion blocks**: 1 between `gh-dash` (9219) and `gh-fzf` (9220) for `gh-f` (within gh-f* at index 4 end-of-string < `z` (122, fzf); at index 3 `d` (100, dash) < `f` (102, f*)); 1 between `gh-pv` (9228) and `gh-signoff` (9229) for `gh-s` (within gh-s* at index 4 end-of-string < `i` (105, signoff); at index 3 `p` (112, pv) < `s` (115, s*)); 1 between `gh-skyline` (9230) and `gh-sub-issue` (9231) for `gh-stack` (within gh-s* at index 4 `k` (107, skyline) < `t` (116, stack) < `u` (117, sub-issue)); 1 between `gh-todo` (9233) and `ghalint` (9234) for `gh-token` (within gh-to* at index 5 `d` (100, todo) < `k` (107, token); at index 2 within ghX `-` (45, gh-*) < `a` (97, ghalint)). Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream READMEs at [github.com/basecamp/gh-signoff](https://github.com/basecamp/gh-signoff), [github.com/yusukebe/gh-markdown-preview](https://github.com/yusukebe/gh-markdown-preview), and [github.com/boostsecurityio/poutine](https://github.com/boostsecurityio/poutine). Candidates were sourced from the `github.com/topics/gh-extension` index (898 total public extensions at the time of the round); hunting against that index avoids the speculative-name failure mode of round 158's `gh-cm`/`gh-projects`/`gh-rollup`/`gh-prompt` deferrals. **Hunt-defer log this round**: `gh-aw` (github/gh-aw — Agentic Workflows, real 4.5k-star extension but README documents the workflow-markdown surface rather than CLI flags), `gh-actions-importer` (github/gh-actions-importer — real 1.2k-star CI/CD migrator but README only mentions `configure`/`update` invocations without flag details), `gh-eco` (jrnxf/gh-eco — real 475-star TUI extension but launches into Bubble Tea with no documented CLI flags) — all 3 deferred because their README surfaces are too vague to commit to without vibe-coding the specifics. Single multi-stem file `_gh-signoff` is `#compdef gh-signoff gh-markdown-preview poutine`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`gh-signoff`** (state-machine with 2 subcommands + step positionals): basecamp/gh-signoff is a local-CI sign-off helper — bare invocation signs off the current PR; `status` shows completed signoffs; `install` enforces signoff requirements on PR merges; bare positionals are signoff step names (`gh signoff tests lint security`). Encoded as `_describe` over {status, install} with a fall-through to positional step names. **`gh-markdown-preview`** (7 flags + 1 optional file positional): yusukebe/gh-markdown-preview renders markdown with GitHub's native styling — **`--dark-mode` ↔ `--light-mode` mutex**, `-p/--port N` (default 3333), `--host HOSTNAME` (default localhost), `--disable-auto-open`, `--disable-reload`, `--markdown-mode` (plain markdown vs the default GFM), optional positional markdown file (auto-detects README if omitted; also accepts stdin). **`poutine`** (5 global flags + 3 `analyze_*` subcommands): supply-chain vulnerability scanner for build pipelines — global `--token TOKEN`, `--format pretty|json|sarif` (3-value enum), `--threads N`, repeatable `*--skip RULE`, `--fail-on-violation` (exit 10 on findings); subcommands `analyze_local` (1 positional dir), `analyze_repo` (1 positional `owner/repo` or URL), `analyze_org` (1 positional organization). Encoded with state-based dispatch from the global-flag layer into per-subcommand `_arguments` blocks. **Blacklist additions (3) across 3 insertion blocks**: 1 between `gh-fzf` (9220) and `gh-mcp` (9221) for `gh-markdown-preview` (within gh-m* at index 4 `a` (97, markdown) < `c` (99, mcp); at index 3 `f` (102, fzf) < `m` (109, m*)); 1 between `gh-pv` (9227 — line shifted by round 158's inserts) and `gh-skyline` (9228) for `gh-signoff` (within gh-s* at index 4 `i` (105, signoff) < `k` (107, skyline); at index 3 `p` (112, pv) < `s` (115, signoff)); 1 between `pound` (20399) and `povray` (20400) for `poutine` (within pou* at index 3 `n` (110, pound) < `t` (116, poutine); at index 2 `u` (117, poutine) < `v` (118, povray)). - **gh-extension Round 1 — gh-skyline + gh-sub-issue + gh-not + gh-tidy (4 stems via single multi-stem `_gh-skyline` file; companions to the pre-existing `_gh-poi`/`_gh-notify`/`_gh-dash`/etc gh-extension files)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream READMEs at [github.com/github/gh-skyline](https://github.com/github/gh-skyline), [github.com/yahsan2/gh-sub-issue](https://github.com/yahsan2/gh-sub-issue), [github.com/nobe4/gh-not](https://github.com/nobe4/gh-not), and [github.com/HaywardMorihara/gh-tidy](https://github.com/HaywardMorihara/gh-tidy). **Already-covered skip log**: `gh-poi` (at `more_src/_gh-poi`), `gh-notify` (at `more_src/_gh-notify`), `gh-dash` (at `more_src/_gh-dash`) — all 3 source-verified this round but pre-existing. **Source-404 deferrals**: `gh-eco`, `gh-cm`, `gh-projects`, `gh-screensaver`, `gh-asana`, `gh-rollup`, `gh-prompt` — all 404 on direct README fetch from their candidate GitHub repos; treated as likely-misremembered names (the gh-extension namespace has thousands of variants and many of my hunt-list candidates were speculative templates rather than real binaries). `gh-act` exists (verified at `github.com/nektos/gh-act`) but is just a wrapper around `act` (which is already covered), so its CLI surface is identical to act's — deliberately skipped to avoid a snowflake completion that would duplicate the act surface. Single multi-stem file `_gh-skyline` is `#compdef gh-skyline gh-sub-issue gh-not gh-tidy`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`gh-skyline`** (8 flag pairs, no positionals): generate a 3D-printable STL of a GitHub contribution graph — `-h/--help`, `-d/--debug`, `-f/--full` (from join year), `-o/--output FILE`, `-u/--user USERNAME`, `-y/--year YEAR_OR_RANGE` (2008..current), `-w/--web`, `-a/--art-only` (ASCII preview only). **`gh-sub-issue`** (state-machine with 4 subcommands, each with its own flag set): `add` links existing issue to parent (`-R/--repo` + 1+ issue positional); `create` builds new linked issue (`-p/--parent` required + `-t/--title` required + `-b/--body` + repeatable `*-l/--label` + repeatable `*-a/--assignee` + `-m/--milestone` + `--project` + `-R/--repo`); `list` shows sub-issues (`-s/--state open|closed|all` + `-L/--limit N` + `--json FIELDS` + `-w/--web` + `-R/--repo` + parent positional); `remove` unlinks without deletion (`-f/--force` + `-R/--repo` + sub-issue positional). **`gh-not`** (1 flag): rule-based GitHub notification manager — uses YAML config + jq-syntax rules so most behavior is config-file driven; CLI surface is minimal: `-h/--help` and `--config FILE`. Upstream notes the project is "mostly done" so the surface is stable. **`gh-tidy`** (2 flags): checkout main + gc + prune merged branches — `--auto-delete-merged` for non-interactive automation, `--trunk BRANCH` to override the default `main`/`master`. **Blacklist additions (4) across 2 insertion blocks** (gh-skyline + gh-sub-issue + gh-tidy share one block): 1 between `gh-md-toc` (9222) and `gh-notify` (9223) for `gh-not` (within gh-* at index 3 `m` (109, md) < `n` (110, not/notify); within gh-not* at index 6 end-of-string < `i` (105, notify)); **3 between `gh-pv` (9226) and `gh-todo` (9227)** for `gh-skyline` + `gh-sub-issue` + `gh-tidy` (within gh-* at index 3 `p` (112, pv) < `s` (115, skyline/sub-issue) < `t` (116, tidy/todo); within gh-s* at index 4 `k` (107, skyline) < `u` (117, sub-issue); within gh-t* at index 4 `i` (105, tidy) < `o` (111, todo)). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — seventh consecutive clean round.
- **Post-git-extras pivot — tfnotify (1 stem standalone `_tfnotify` file; deliberate single-stem round after the tj/git-extras campaign closed at round 156)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). **Hunt-rate observation**: broad-scope hunts across Terraform/IaC extras (`tfsec-language-server`/`tflint-language-server`/`spacectl`/`tfctl` etc), observability extras (`promxy`/`vmctl`/`thanos-cli`/`perses-cli` etc), JS-tooling extras (`rolldown-vite`/`rspress`/`webcontainer` etc), and AI-CLI tools all turned up ~50 FRESH-tagged candidates but **none survived direct source verification** — most were either names I invented from category-knowledge templates rather than actual binary names, or already covered under their canonical names (e.g. `opa` covers `open-policy-agent`, `pulumi` covers `pulumi-cli`). Settling for a single solid stem is the correct outcome over force-padding the round with vibe-coded completions. The campaign-end state from round 156 + this single addition is sustainable: tj/git-extras is exhausted at 72 stems, and the harvest rate falls off naturally when the long tail consists of speculative-name guesses rather than verifiable upstream sources. Surface transcribed directly from upstream README at [github.com/mercari/tfnotify](https://github.com/mercari/tfnotify). Single standalone file `_tfnotify` is `#compdef tfnotify`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`tfnotify`** (3 global flags + 3 subcommands, stdin-driven): Mercari's Go-based wrapper that pipes Terraform output through template processing and posts to GitHub/GitLab/Bitbucket as PR comments. Global flags `--config FILE` (default `./.tfnotify.y{,a}ml`), `--message TEXT` (custom template message), `--help`. Subcommands `plan` / `apply` / `fmt` correspond to the upstream Terraform operation type — each consumes stdin (pipe-driven via `terraform plan | tfnotify plan`) and accepts only the global flags (no per-subcommand flag space documented). Completion encodes subcommand `_describe` dispatch with state-based progression to subcommand-args state for `--config`/`--message` re-completion. **Blacklist addition (1)**: 1 between `tfmv` (25881) and `tfplugindocs` (25882) for `tfnotify` (within tf* at index 2 `m` (109, mv) < `n` (110, notify) < `p` (112, plugindocs)). The canonical 74-name list was finally obtained this round from upstream `tj/git-extras/master/Commands.md` — cross-referencing against rounds 150–155 plus pre-existing files showed exactly 8 still-uncovered subcommands; 6 added in this round, the remaining 2 deferred. **Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream manpages**: `man/git-browse-ci.1` / `man/git-paste.1` / `man/git-reauthor.1` / `man/git-repl.1` / `man/git-reset-file.1` / `man/git-unlock.1`. **Source-404 deferral**: `man/git-rscp.1` — listed in `Commands.md` but 404s in master, deferred per the source-404 precedent. **Already-covered skip log**: `git-sync` (at `more_src/_git-sync`) — source-verified this round but pre-existing. **Cumulative deferrals across the campaign**: `git-rscp` + 8 other 404s from round 155 + 6 404s from round 154 (`git-mailmap`/`git-merges`/`git-promote`/`git-psykick`/`git-line-summary`/`git-jira` — these names appeared in informal hunt lists but never had `.1` files in master; likely never existed) + `git-mv` (API-listing artifact). Single multi-stem file `_git-browse-ci` is `#compdef git-browse-ci git-paste git-reauthor git-repl git-reset-file git-unlock`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`git-browse-ci`** (1 optional positional): open the CI page for a remote in the browser; defaults to first remote. **`git-paste`** (0 flags, passthrough): all args passed directly to `git format-patch`; with no args defaults to `@{u}` (upstream tracking branch). **`git-reauthor`** (6 flag pairs): rewrite author/committer identities — `-a/--all`, `-c/--use-config`, `-e/--correct-email EMAIL` (empty string allowed), `-n/--correct-name NAME` (empty NOT allowed per upstream), `-o/--old-email EMAIL`, `-t/--type` with 3-value enum `author|committer|both` (default `both`). **`git-repl`** (no flags): interactive git REPL — recognises bare commands (`git <cmd>`), `!`-prefixed shell commands, `ls` shortcut for `git ls-files`, `exit`/`quit` to terminate; the surface is entirely stdin-driven so no CLI completion content. **`git-reset-file`** (2 positionals): `[<filename>] commit-hash` — reset a single file to a given commit (default `HEAD`). **`git-unlock`** (1 positional): undo `git lock` (re-enables tracking of the assume-unchanged file). **Blacklist additions (6) across 5 insertion blocks** (git-repl + git-reset-file share one block): 1 between `git-browse` (9331) and `git-brv` (9332) for `git-browse-ci` (within git-browse* at index 9 end-of-string < `-` (45, ci); at index 6 `o` (111, browse) < `v` (118, brv)); 1 between `git-pages-cli` (9433) and `git-pkgs` (9434) for `git-paste` (within git-pa* at index 6 `g` (103, pages) < `s` (115, paste); at index 5 `a` (97, pa*) < `k` (107, pkgs)); 1 between `git-quickfix` (9439) and `git-rebase-patch` (9440 was-9440-after-r155) for `git-reauthor` (at index 4 `q` (113, quickfix) < `r` (114, reauthor/rebase); within git-re* at index 6 `a` (97, reauthor) < `b` (98, rebase)); **2 between `git-rename-tag` (9452) and `git-review` (9453)** for `git-repl` + `git-reset-file` (within git-re* at index 6 `n` (110, rename) < `p` (112, repl) < `s` (115, reset) < `v` (118, review); within git-re-p/s entries at index 7 `l` (108, repl) — no further branching needed); 1 between `git-trim` (9489) and `git-upload-archive` (9490) for `git-unlock` (at index 4 `t` (116, trim) < `u` (117, unlock); within git-u* at index 5 `n` (110, unlock) < `p` (112, upload)). - **git-extras Round 7 — git-psykorebase + git-rebase-patch + git-rename-branch + git-rename-remote + git-sed + git-squash (6 more tj/git-extras subcommands via single multi-stem `_git-psykorebase` file; cumulative git-extras coverage now 58 stems across rounds 150–155 + the pre-existing `_git-changelog`/`_git-info`/`_git-ignore`/`_git-get`/`_git-extras`/`_git-standup`/`_git-undo` via `_ugit`)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream manpages at [github.com/tj/git-extras](https://github.com/tj/git-extras) — `man/git-psykorebase.1` / `man/git-rebase-patch.1` / `man/git-rename-branch.1` / `man/git-rename-remote.1` / `man/git-sed.1` / `man/git-squash.1`. **Already-covered skip log** (caught by `^#compdef` recheck before write): `git-extras` (at `more_src/_git-extras`) and `git-standup` (at `more_src/_git-standup`) — both source-verified this round but excluded from the multi-stem; `git-fork` also re-verified but was already added in round 151. **Source-404 deferrals**: `man/git-rscp.1`, `man/git-back.1`, `man/git-blame-someone-else.1`, `man/git-bug.1`, `man/git-chore.1`, `man/git-archive-status.1`, `man/git-archive-now.1`, `man/git-refactor.1` — all return HTTP 404 on direct fetch from `tj/git-extras` master despite some appearing in API listings. **API-listing-artifact deferral**: `man/git-mv.1` appears in the GitHub `contents` API page-2 response but the raw file URL 404s; treated as a listing-side artifact (and stock `git mv` covers the user-facing surface anyway). Single multi-stem file `_git-psykorebase` is `#compdef git-psykorebase git-rebase-patch git-rename-branch git-rename-remote git-sed git-squash`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`git-psykorebase`** (2 flags + 2 positionals): "psyko rebase" workflow — `--no-ff` forces a merge commit message even when conflicts are absent; `--continue` resumes after manual conflict resolution and is a `(- *)` mutex against everything else; positional 1 = target branch, positional 2 = optional feature branch (default current). **`git-rebase-patch`** (1 positional): apply a single patch file as a rebase. **`git-rename-branch`** (2 positionals): `[<old-branch>] <new-branch>` — with 1 arg the current branch is renamed to that arg, with 2 args the named branch is renamed. **`git-rename-remote`** (2 positionals): `<old-remote-name> <new-remote-name>`. **`git-sed`** (2 flags + variable positionals): bulk sed across tracked files — `-c` commits with a standard auto-message (fails on unstaged changes), `-f FLAGS` passes sed regex flags (e.g. `-f g` enables global-per-line); positionals are `<search> <replacement> [-- <pathspec>]`. **`git-squash`** (1-flag mutex + 2 positionals): `[--squash-msg] <source-branch|commit-ref> [<commit-message>]` — `--squash-msg` (concatenated squashed messages as the commit message) is explicitly mutually exclusive with a custom commit-message positional, encoded as `(--squash-msg 2)` in `_arguments` syntax so a 2nd-positional candidate disables `--squash-msg` and vice-versa. **Blacklist additions (6) across 5 insertion blocks** (git-rename-branch + git-rename-remote share one block): 1 between `git-pr` (9435) and `git-pull-request` (9436) for `git-psykorebase` (within git-p* at index 5 `r` (114, pr) < `s` (115, psykorebase) < `u` (117, pull)); 1 between `git-quickfix` (9439) and `git-rec` (9440) for `git-rebase-patch` (at index 4 `q` (113, quickfix) < `r` (114, rebase/rec); within git-re* at index 6 `b` (98, rebase) < `c` (99, rec)); **2 between `git-remote-hg` (9447) and `git-rename-tag` (9448)** for `git-rename-branch` + `git-rename-remote` (within git-re* at index 6 `m` (109, remote) < `n` (110, rename); within git-rename-* at index 11 `b` (98, branch) < `r` (114, remote) < `t` (116, tag)); 1 between `git-secrets` (9455) and `git-series` (9456) for `git-sed` (within git-se* at index 6 `c` (99, secret/secrets) < `d` (100, sed) < `r` (114, series)); 1 between `git-split-diffs` (9465) and `git-ssh` (9466) for `git-squash` (within git-s* at index 5 `p` (112, spice/split) < `q` (113, squash) < `s` (115, ssh)). Single multi-stem file `_git-brv` is `#compdef git-brv git-clear git-clear-soft git-contrib git-cp git-create-branch git-delete-branch git-delete-squashed-branches git-delete-submodule git-delete-tag git-force-clone git-pull-request`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`git-brv`** (no flags): list branches sorted by last commit date. **`git-clear`** (2 flag pairs): combined hard reset + clean — `-f/--force` (no warning) + `-h/--help/?`. **`git-clear-soft`** (no flags): `git-clear` that respects `.gitignore`. **`git-contrib`** (1 optional positional): show commits by username or email. **`git-cp`** (2 positionals): copy a file preserving git history. **`git-create-branch`** (3 flags + branchname): `-r/--remote [name]` for remote tracking (defaults to `origin`) + `--from START` (defaults to current branch) + variable branch names. **`git-delete-branch`** (1+ positionals): delete one or more local + remote branches. **`git-delete-squashed-branches`** (1 flag pair + 1 optional positional): `-p/--proceed` to continue on errors + target branch (default current). **`git-delete-submodule`** (1 positional): full submodule removal by path. **`git-delete-tag`** (1+ positionals): delete one or more tags locally + remotely. **`git-force-clone`** (2 flags + 2 positionals): `--help` + `--branch=NAME` (post-clone checkout) + remote URL + destination path (replaces destination if it exists). **`git-pull-request`** (1 optional positional): file a PR against an optional target branch. **Blacklist additions (12) across 8 insertion blocks**: 1 between `git-browse` (9331) and `git-bug` (9332) for `git-brv` (within git-br* at index 6 `a` (97, branch) < `o` (111, browse) < `v` (118, brv)); 1 between `git-conflict` (9347) and `git-count` (9348) for `git-contrib` (within git-con* at index 7 `f` (102, conflict) < `t` (116, contrib); at index 6 `n` (110, contrib) < `u` (117, count)); **2 between `git-count` (9348) and `git-credential-gcloud` (9349)** for `git-cp` + `git-create-branch` (at index 4 `o` (111, count) < `p` (112, cp/create) < `r` (114, credential); within git-cp* at index 5 end-of-string < `r` (114, create); at index 6 `a` (97, create) < `d` (100, credential)); 1 between `git-dag` (9356) and `git-delete-merged-branches` (9357) for `git-delete-branch` (within git-d* at index 5 `a` (97, dag) < `e` (101, delete); within git-delete-* at index 11 `b` (98, branch) < `m` (109, merged)); **3 between `git-delete-merged-branches` (9357) and `git-delta` (9358)** for `git-delete-squashed-branches` + `git-delete-submodule` + `git-delete-tag` (within git-delete-* at index 11 `m` (109, merged) < `s` (115, squashed/submodule) < `t` (116, tag); within git-delete-s* at index 12 `q` (113, squashed) < `u` (117, submodule); within git-d* at index 5 `e` (101, delete) < `e` consistent then at index 6 `l` (108, delete) < `l` consistent then at index 7 `e` (101, delete) < `t` (116, delta) — so `git-delete-*` < `git-delta`); - **git-extras Round 5 — git-gh-pages + git-ignore-io + git-lock + git-locked + git-local-commits + git-show-merged-branches + git-show-unmerged-branches + git-show-tree + git-stamp + git-utimes (10 more tj/git-extras subcommands via single multi-stem `_git-gh-pages` file; cumulative git-extras coverage now 40 stems across rounds 150–153 + the pre-existing `_git-changelog`/`_git-info`/`_git-ignore`)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream manpages at [github.com/tj/git-extras](https://github.com/tj/git-extras) — `man/git-gh-pages.1` / `man/git-ignore-io.1` / `man/git-lock.1` / `man/git-locked.1` / `man/git-local-commits.1` / `man/git-show-merged-branches.1` / `man/git-show-unmerged-branches.1` / `man/git-show-tree.1` / `man/git-stamp.1` / `man/git-utimes.1`. Single multi-stem file `_git-gh-pages` is `#compdef git-gh-pages git-ignore-io git-lock git-locked git-local-commits git-show-merged-branches git-show-unmerged-branches git-show-tree git-stamp git-utimes`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`git-gh-pages`** (no flags): creates the gh-pages branch with a default initial index.html. **`git-ignore-io`** (7 flag pairs with 4 mutex groups): manages `.gitignore` via gitignore.io types — repeatable mutex `--append=TYPES` ↔ `--replace=TYPES` (both with short forms `-a`/`-r`), mutex `--list-in-table` ↔ `--list-alphabetically` (`-l`/`-L`), `-s/--search WORD`, `-t/--show-update-time`, `-u/--update-list` (each long-form ↔ short-form pair encoded as its own `_arguments` mutex). **`git-lock`** (1 positional): mark a file as locked via the assume-unchanged bit. **`git-locked`** (no flags): list all locked files. **`git-local-commits`** (no flags, all args passthrough): show commits not yet pushed; all args passed directly to `git log`. **`git-show-merged-branches`** / **`git-show-unmerged-branches`** / **`git-show-tree`** (each: no flags, no positionals): display merged/unmerged branch lists and the decorated commit-history graph respectively. **`git-stamp`** (1 flag + id + variable messages): append issue references to the last commit message — `git stamp [-r|--replace] <id> [<messages>]`; `-r` eliminates previous stamps sharing the same case-insensitive identifier. **`git-utimes`** (1 flag): restore file mtimes to last-commit dates; `--newer` only touches files newer than their last commit. **Blacklist additions (10) across 6 insertion blocks** (git-local-commits + git-lock + git-locked share one block; git-show-merged-branches + git-show-tree + git-show-unmerged-branches share another): 1 between `git-get` (9374) and `git-gone` (9375) for `git-gh-pages` (within git-g* at index 5 `e` (101, get) < `h` (104, gh) < `o` (111, gone)); 1 between `git-ignore` (9385) and `git-imerge` (9386) for `git-ignore-io` (within git-ignore* at index 10 end-of-string < `-` (45, io); at index 5 `g` (103, ignore) < `m` (109, imerge)); **3 between `git-lfs` (9396) and `git-machete` (9397)** for `git-local-commits` + `git-lock` + `git-locked` (within git-loc* at index 7 `a` (97, local) < `k` (107, lock/locked); within git-lock* at index 8 end-of-string < `e` (101, locked); at index 4 `l` (108, l*) < `m` (109, machete)); **3 between `git-shell` (9441) and `git-sim` (9442)** for `git-show-merged-branches` + `git-show-tree` + `git-show-unmerged-branches` (within git-sh* at index 6 `e` (101, shell) < `o` (111, show); within git-show-* at index 9 `m` (109, merged) < `t` (116, tree) < `u` (117, unmerged); within git-s* at index 5 `h` (104, shell/show) < `i` (105, sim)); 1 between `git-stack` (9447) and `git-standup` (9448) for `git-stamp` (within git-sta* at index 7 `c` (99, stack) < `m` (109, stamp) < `n` (110, standup)); 1 between `git-url-sub` (9465) and `git-vendor` (9466) for `git-utimes` (within git-u* at index 5 `r` (114, url) < `t` (116, utimes); at index 4 `u` (117, utimes) < `v` (118, vendor)). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly.
- **git-extras Round 4 — git-magic + git-merge-repo + git-missing + git-obliterate + git-pr + git-rename-tag + git-root + git-scp + git-setup (9 more tj/git-extras subcommands via single multi-stem `_git-magic` file; cumulative git-extras coverage now 30 stems across rounds 150–152 + the pre-existing `_git-changelog`/`_git-info`/`_git-ignore`)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream manpages at [github.com/tj/git-extras](https://github.com/tj/git-extras) — `man/git-magic.1` / `man/git-merge-repo.1` / `man/git-missing.1` / `man/git-obliterate.1` / `man/git-pr.1` / `man/git-rename-tag.1` / `man/git-root.1` / `man/git-scp.1` / `man/git-setup.1`. **Hunt-skip log this round**: `git-undo` shows `existing=more_src/_ugit` on the `^#compdef` recheck — the pre-existing `_ugit` completion (for the `ugit` interactive git tool) carries `git-undo` in its `#compdef` line, so the round-152 multi-stem excludes `git-undo` to avoid collision; the user already has `git-undo` completions via `_ugit` even though the upstream `tj/git-extras` shipping path is distinct. Single multi-stem file `_git-magic` is `#compdef git-magic git-merge-repo git-missing git-obliterate git-pr git-rename-tag git-root git-scp git-setup`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`git-magic`** (5 flags + `-h`): quick commit-and-push helper — `-a` adds untracked, repeatable `*-m msg` (concatenated as paragraphs per upstream), `-e` for editor-edit of the auto-message, `-p` for push, `-f` adds `-f` to the push. **`git-merge-repo`** (1 flag + 3 positionals): external-repo subtree merge — `<repo> <branch> <directory> [--squash]`; `--squash` collapses history to a single merge commit. **`git-missing`** (0 flags + 2 positionals + trailing `git log` opts): commit diff between branches — `[<first branch>] <second branch> [<git log options>]`; first defaults to current. **`git-obliterate`** (0 flags + files + trailing rev-list opts): erase files from entire history — `<files...> [-- <rev-list options...>]`; rev-list options after `--` per upstream's `git help rev-list`. **`git-pr`** (1-flag mutex + variable positional shapes): GitHub PR checkout — 4 synopsis shapes (number+remote, [remote]:number..., url..., `clean`) all dispatched via the same `*:` positional; `-m/--merge` switches between branch-tip vs merge-commit checkout. **`git-rename-tag`** (0 flags + 2 positionals): `<old-tag-name> <new-tag-name>` — local + remote tag rename. **`git-root`** (1 flag): `-r/--relative` for relative-from-root output, otherwise absolute root path. **`git-scp`** (1 flag + 2 positionals): secure-copy git changes via remote — `-h` for help; positional 1 = remote, positional 2+ = commits or paths; per upstream `git rscp` is the reverse-direction sibling that swaps the transfer direction (deliberately not stemmed by this completion since it's a separate command). **`git-setup`** (1 optional positional): `[<directory>]` — initialize a new repo in DIR (default cwd). **Blacklist additions (9) across 8 insertion blocks** (git-merge-repo + git-missing share one block): 1 between `git-machete` (9397) and `git-mediate` (9398) for `git-magic` (within git-ma* at index 5 `a` (97, both); at index 6 `c` (99, machete) < `g` (103, magic)); **2 between `git-merge-into` (9399) and `git-mob` (9400)** for `git-merge-repo` + `git-missing` (within git-merge-* at index 10 `i` (105, into) < `r` (114, repo); within git-m* at index 5 `e` (101, merge) < `i` (105, missing) < `o` (111, mob)); 1 between `git-number` (9408) and `git-octopus` (9409) for `git-obliterate` (at index 4 `n` (110, number) < `o` (111, both); within git-o* at index 5 `b` (98, obliterate) < `c` (99, octopus)); 1 between `git-pkgs` (9414) and `git-pulls` (9415) for `git-pr` (within git-p* at index 5 `k` (107, pkgs) < `r` (114, pr) < `u` (117, pulls)); 1 between `git-remote-hg` (9425) and `git-review` (9426) for `git-rename-tag` (within git-re* at index 6 `m` (109, remote) < `n` (110, rename) < `v` (118, review)); 1 between `git-revise` (9427) and `git-router` (9428) for `git-root` (within git-r* at index 5 `e` (101, revise) < `o` (111, root/router); within git-ro* at index 6 `o` (111, root) < `u` (117, router)); 1 between `git-router` (9428) and `git-secret` (9429) for `git-scp` (at index 4 `r` (114, router) < `s` (115, both scp/secret); within git-s* at index 5 `c` (99, scp) < `e` (101, secret)); 1 between `git-series` (9431) and `git-shell` (9432) for `git-setup` (within git-se* at index 6 `r` (114, series) < `t` (116, setup); within git-s* at index 5 `e` (101, setup) < `h` (104, shell)). - **git-extras Round 3 — git-abort + git-alias + git-authors + git-browse + git-bulk + git-coauthor + git-commits-since + git-count + git-fork + git-fresh-branch + git-graft + git-guilt + git-merge-into (13 more tj/git-extras subcommands via single multi-stem `_git-abort` file; cumulative git-extras coverage now 21 stems across rounds 150–151 + the pre-existing `_git-changelog`/`_git-info`/`_git-ignore`)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep; `git-info` and `git-ignore` are both pre-existing at `more_src/_git-info` and `more_src/_git-ignore` and were excluded from this round to avoid `#compdef` collisions). Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream manpages at [github.com/tj/git-extras](https://github.com/tj/git-extras) — `man/git-abort.1` / `man/git-alias.1` / `man/git-authors.1` / `man/git-browse.1` / `man/git-bulk.1` / `man/git-coauthor.1` / `man/git-commits-since.1` / `man/git-count.1` / `man/git-fork.1` / `man/git-fresh-branch.1` / `man/git-graft.1` / `man/git-guilt.1` / `man/git-merge-into.1`. **GitHub API `man/` directory listing now used as the canonical source-of-truth** (the listing for `tj/git-extras/contents/man?ref=master` returns reliable file presence in 130+-entry pages; this round verified by cross-checking each candidate against that listing before fetching). Single multi-stem file `_git-abort` is `#compdef git-abort git-alias git-authors git-browse git-bulk git-coauthor git-commits-since git-count git-fork git-fresh-branch git-graft git-guilt git-merge-into`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`git-abort`** (no flags, no positionals): aborts the current operation per upstream "There are no options, it just aborts current operation." **`git-alias`** (2-flag mutex + variable positionals): `--global` ↔ `--local` mutex; 9 synopsis shapes collapse to (optional flag) + (search-pattern or alias-name + command). **`git-authors`** (3 flags): `-l/--list` and `--no-email`. **`git-browse`** (4 positionals): `[remote_name] [file_name] [line_1] [line_2]` — remote defaults to first remote when omitted; line_2 forms a range with line_1. **`git-bulk`** (10 flags + variable positionals — the most-complex surface of this round): 6 distinct synopsis shapes encoded as a single 7-way mutex `(-a -w --addworkspace --removeworkspace --addcurrent --purge --listall)` with the modifier flag `-g` (confirmation prompt) usable across all execute-modes and the auxiliary flag `--from URL_OR_FILE` only meaningful with `--addworkspace`. **`git-coauthor`** (2 positionals): `<co-author> <co-author-email>`. **`git-commits-since`** (1 optional positional): date string (defaults to "last week" when omitted). **`git-count`** (1 flag): `--all` switches between total-commit-count and per-author breakdown. **`git-fork`** (1 optional positional): GitHub repo URL — works without args inside an existing GitHub repo directory. **`git-fresh-branch`** (1 positional): branch name for the new orphan branch. **`git-graft`** (2 positionals): `<src-branch> <dest-branch>` — merge src into dest. **`git-guilt`** (4 flags + 2 optional positionals): `-h` for usage, `-e/--email`, `-w/--ignore-whitespace`, `-d/--debug`; positional `<since> [<until>]` revisions. **`git-merge-into`** (1 flag + 2 positionals): `--ff-only` + `[src] <dest>` — src defaults to current branch when omitted. **Blacklist additions (13) across 13 insertion blocks** — **with one mid-round sort fix** caught by `LC_ALL=C sort -c` for `git-count`: 1 between `git` (9316) and `git-absorb` (9317) for `git-abort` (within git-* at index 5 `b` (98, both abort/absorb) consistent; at index 6 `o` (111, abort) < `s` (115, absorb)); 1 between `git-ai` (9318) and `git-annex` (9319) for `git-alias` (within git-a* at index 5 `i` (105, ai) < `l` (108, alias) < `n` (110, annex)); 1 between `git-archive-file` (9324) and `git-autofixup` (9325) for `git-authors` (within git-a* at index 5 `r` (114, archive) < `u` (117, both authors/autofixup); within git-au* at index 6 `t` (116, both); at index 7 `h` (104, authors) < `o` (111, autofixup)); 1 between `git-branchless` (9327) and `git-bug` (9328) for `git-browse` (within git-b* at index 5 `r` (114, both branchless/browse); at index 6 `a` (97, branch) < `o` (111, browse) < `u` (117, bug from index 5)); 1 between `git-buildpackage` (9329) and `git-cal` (9330) for `git-bulk` (within git-bu* at index 6 `i` (105, build) < `l` (108, bulk); at index 4 `b` (98, bu*) < `c` (99, cal)); 1 between `git-clone` (9337) and `git-codereview` (9338) for `git-coauthor` (within git-c* at index 5 `l` (108, clone) < `o` (111, coauthor/codereview); within git-co* at index 6 `a` (97, coauthor) < `d` (100, codereview)); 1 between `git-cola` (9339) and `git-conflict` (9340) for `git-commits-since` (within git-co* at index 6 `l` (108, cola) < `m` (109, commits) < `n` (110, conflict)); **1 between `git-conflict` (9340) and `git-credential-gcloud` (9341)** for `git-count` (**initially incorrectly placed AFTER `git-credential-oauth`; the `sort -c` recheck reported "disorder: git-count at 9352"**; within git-co* at index 5 `o` (111, both); at index 6 `n` (110, conflict) < `u` (117, count) and `o` (111, count) < `r` (114, credential) — so `git-conflict` < `git-count` < `git-credential-*` — the original placement was off by ~10 entries); 1 between `git-foresta` (9358) and `git-format-staged` (9359) for `git-fork` (within git-fo* at index 6 `r` (114, both foresta/fork/format); at index 7 `e` (101, foresta) < `k` (107, fork) < `m` (109, format)); - **git-extras Round 2 — git-effort + git-summary + git-feature + git-release + git-mr + git-archive-file + git-touch (7 tj/git-extras subcommands via single multi-stem `_git-effort` file; extends the pre-existing `more_src/_git-changelog` to an 8-stem git-extras corpus, with Round 149's `_git-foresta`/`_git-stein` standalone files for adjacent git plugin coverage)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep; the existing `_git-changelog` covers the eighth main git-extras subcommand and was specifically excluded from this round to avoid `#compdef` collision). Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream manpages at [github.com/tj/git-extras](https://github.com/tj/git-extras) — `man/git-effort.1` / `man/git-summary.1` / `man/git-feature.1` / `man/git-release.1` / `man/git-mr.1` / `man/git-archive-file.1` / `man/git-touch.1`. **Source-404 deferrals**: `man/git-cleanup.1`, `man/git-hotfix.1`, `man/git-publish.1`, `man/git-unpublish.1`, `man/git-recall.1`, `man/git-since.1` — all 404 on direct fetch from the tj/git-extras `master` branch despite the subcommands being listed in the project README; defer per the source-404 precedent, will revisit if/when the man pages land. Single multi-stem file `_git-effort` is `#compdef git-effort git-summary git-feature git-release git-mr git-archive-file git-touch`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage** (each transcribed from the exact SYNOPSIS line in the corresponding upstream `.1` manpage): **`git-effort`** (1 flag + path positionals + post-`--` git log opts): `git-effort [--above <value>] [<path>...] [-- [<log options>...]]` — `--above N` ignores files with commits ≤ N; the `--` separator marks the boundary between effort options and `git log` options (the "Commit Limiting" section of `git-log` is the relevant subset per upstream). **`git-summary`** (4 flags + optional committish/path): two synopsis shapes — `git-summary [--dedup-by-email] [--no-merges] [<committish>]` and `git-summary --line [<path>]` — encoded as a 3-way mutex: `--line` cannot combine with `--dedup-by-email` or `--no-merges`; `--output-style tabular|oneline` is a 2-value enum applied across both shapes. **`git-feature`** (6 flags + NAME or `finish`): two synopsis shapes — create form `git-feature [-a PREFIX] [-s SEP] [-r [REMOTE]] [--from START] NAME...` and finish form `git-feature [-a PREFIX] [-s SEP] finish [--squash] NAME...`; `-a/--alias` defaults to `feature`, `-s/--separator` defaults to `/`, `-r/--remote` defaults to `origin` when its optarg is omitted, `--from` defaults to current branch when omitted; the `finish` literal positional triggers `--squash` availability. **`git-release`** (9 flags + tagname or hook args): semver-aware release with pre/post-release hooks; `--semver major|minor|patch` (3-value enum) drives auto-increment; `--prefix STRING` prepends to semver tags (e.g. `r4.4.0` → `r4.5.0` with `--prefix r`); `-r REMOTE` push destination; `-m MESSAGE` custom commit message; `--no-empty-commit`; `-c` generates/updates changelog; `-s` creates signed annotated tag; `-u KEY-ID` GPG key for signed tag; any arguments after the `--` are passed to pre/post-release hooks. **`git-mr`** (0 flags, 3 shapes): GitLab merge-request checkout — `git-mr <number> [<remote>]` (remote defaults to `origin`), `git-mr <url>` (parses full GitLab MR URL), `git-mr clean` (removes local MR-checkout branches). **`git-archive-file`** (no options): creates a ZIP of the current HEAD; upstream manpage explicitly states "This command does not take any options." **`git-touch`** (1 positional): creates + stages a new file in one operation. **Blacklist additions (7) across 7 insertion blocks**: 1 between `git-archive-all` (9323) and `git-autofixup` (9324) for `git-archive-file` (within git-archive-* at index 12 `a` (97, all) < `f` (102, file); within git-a* at index 5 `r` (114, archive) < `u` (117, autofixup)); 1 between `git-delta` (9349) and `git-extras` (9350) for `git-effort` (at index 4 `d` (100, delta) < `e` (101, effort) and `e` < `e` then at index 5 within git-e* `f` (102, effort) < `x` (120, extras)); 1 between `git-fame` (9351) and `git-filter-repo` (9352) for `git-feature` (within git-fa* at index 5 `a` (97, fame) consistent; within git-f* at index 5 `a` (97, fame) < `e` (101, feature) < `i` (105, filter)); 1 between `git-modified-branches.sh` (9386) and `git-multi` (9387) for `git-mr` (within git-m* at index 5 `o` (111, modified) < `r` (114, mr) < `u` (117, multi)); 1 between `git-relation` (9404) and `git-remote-codecommit` (9405) for `git-release` (within git-rel* at index 7 `a` (97, relation) < `e` (101, release); within git-re* at index 6 `l` (108, release) < `m` (109, remote)); 1 between `git-suggest-coauthors` (9428) and `git-svn` (9429) for `git-summary` (within git-su* at index 6 `g` (103, suggest) < `m` (109, summary); within git-s* at index 5 `u` (117, su*) < `v` (118, svn)); 1 between `git-sync` (9430) and `git-town` (9431) for `git-touch` (within git-t* at index 5 `o` (111, both); within git-to* at index 6 `u` (117, touch) < `w` (119, town); within git-s* at index 5 `u` (117, sync) < git-t* `t` (116) — wait, **'s' (115) < 't' (116)** so git-sync < git-touch). All position-by-position; sort verified directly via `LC_ALL=C sort -c` (no mid-round corrections needed this round, in contrast to round 149). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **JtR final mop-up Round 6 + git-* extras Round 1 — telegram2john + ios7tojohn + itunes_backup2john (3 last JtR helpers in `_telegram2john` multi-stem) + git-foresta + git-stein (2 fresh standalone git extensions, each in its own file)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). **JtR-side**: 3 stems verified via direct fetch of `run/telegram2john.py` / `run/ios7tojohn.pl` / `run/itunes_backup2john.pl`. This round flushes the long-running JtR campaign — the broader run/ directory listing on GitHub appears to have **substantial hallucinated entries** (the api/tree listings claim files like `openvpn2john`/`pgp2john`/`putty2john`/`raw2john`/`vdi2john`/`wallet2john`/`whirlpool2john`/`wincreds2john`/`sxc2john`/`dominosec2john`/`o5logon2john`/`odf2john` but every one of these 404s on direct raw fetch — the source-of-truth is the raw URL, not the listing API). **Source-404 deferrals**: all 12 of the above plus `racf2john` (deferred since round 145). **Already-covered skip log**: `truecrypt2john` (at `src/_truecrypt2john`) and `pwsafe2john` (at `src/_pwsafe2john`) — both source-verified this round but pre-existing in `src/`. **git-extras side**: 2 standalone tools picked from a broader hunt where most names 404 on upstream fetch (`git-bonsai`/`git-codeowners`/`git-friendly`/`git-tidy`/`git-tools`/`git-trial`/`git-clone-quick`/`git-lab` are all in the FRESH hit set but the first two 404 their READMEs on direct fetch; git-friendly's surface is 5 git-subcommands rather than a single binary so the JtR-style positional-file completion doesn't fit). Both verified surfaces transcribed from upstream READMEs: [github.com/takaaki-kasai/git-foresta](https://github.com/takaaki-kasai/git-foresta) and [github.com/sh5i/git-stein](https://github.com/sh5i/git-stein). **Per-stem coverage**: **`_telegram2john` multi-stem** `#compdef telegram2john ios7tojohn itunes_backup2john`. **`telegram2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positional files-or-dirs): Telegram Desktop hash extractor accepting either a Telegram data dir (recursively searched) or individual `userconfig.xml`/`map0`/`map1`/`key_datas` files; upstream strips trailing quote characters. Linux: `~/.local/share/TelegramDesktop`; Windows: `C:/Users/<Name>/AppData/Roaming/Telegram Desktop`. **`ios7tojohn`** (0 flags, 1+ `.plist` positionals): Apple iOS 7 restrictions-password plist parser; outputs PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA1 hashes; handles both single-line and multi-line XML `<data>` elements. **`itunes_backup2john`** (0 flags, 1+ `Manifest.plist` positionals): iTunes backup parser with two output formats by iOS version — pre-iOS 10 `$itunes_backup$*9*<wpky>*<iter>*<salt>**`, iOS 10.x+ `$itunes_backup$*10*<wpky>*<iter>*<salt>*<dpic>*<dpsl>`; WPKY/SALT/DPSL field lengths validated upstream (40/20/20 bytes). **`_git-foresta` single-stem**: 11 named flags — `--help`/`--version`, `--style 1|2|10|15` (4-value enum), `--svdepth=N` (default 2), `--graph-margin-left=N` (default 2), `--graph-margin-right=N` (default 1), 5 symbol-customization flags (`--graph-symbol-commit/merge/overpass/root/tip` with their respective `●`/`◎`/`═`/`■`/`○` defaults), `--no-status` — plus a final `*:git log argument:` catch-all because the script passes through to `git log` (and explicitly rejects `--pretty`/`--format` per upstream). **`_git-stein` single-stem**: 19 named flags + 1 source positional — covers all 3 documented modes (Overwrite = source only; Transform = source + `-o target`; Duplicate = source + `-o target` + `-d`); the completion encodes the `-o/--output`/`-d/--duplicate`/`-j/--jobs`/`-n/--dry-run`/`-q/--quiet`/`-v/--verbose` long-form ↔ short-form mutex pairs explicitly; `--log` carries a 9-value enum drawn from `java.util.logging.Level` (`OFF`/`SEVERE`/`WARNING`/`INFO`/`CONFIG`/`FINE`/`FINER`/`FINEST`/`ALL`). **Blacklist additions (5) across 5 insertion blocks** — and **one mid-round sort fix** caught by `LC_ALL=C sort -c` during the verification step: 1 between `git-flow` (9354) and `git-format-staged` (9355) for `git-foresta` (initially incorrectly placed AFTER `git-format-staged`; the `sort -c` recheck reported "disorder: git-foresta at 9356"; within git-f* at index 5 `l` (108, flow) < `o` (111, format/foresta); within git-fo* at index 7 `e` (101, foresta) < `m` (109, format) — so `git-foresta` < `git-format-staged`); 1 between `git-stats` (9421) and `git-submodule-move` (9422) for `git-stein` (also initially incorrectly placed BETWEEN `git-stack` and `git-standup`; `sort -c` reported "disorder: git-standup at 9422"; within git-st* at index 6 `a` (97, stack/standup/stats) < `e` (101, stein); within git-sta* at index 7 `c` (99, stack) < `n` (110, standup) < `t` (116, stats) so `git-stack` < `git-standup` < `git-stats` < `git-stein`; within git-s* at index 5 `t` (116, st*) < `u` (117, submodule)); 1 between `ios-deploy` (11890) and `ios_webkit_debug_proxy` (11891) for `ios7tojohn` (within ios* at index 3 `-` (45, deploy) < `7` (55, ios7tojohn) < `_` (95, webkit)); 1 between `itunes` (12159) and `itweb-settings` (12160) for `itunes_backup2john` (within it* at index 2 `u` (117, itunes) < `w` (119, itweb); within itunes* at index 6 end-of-string < `_` (95, backup)); 1 between `telegram-cli` (25556) and `telemetrygen` (25557) for `telegram2john` (within telegram* at index 8 `-` (45, telegram-cli) < `2` (50, john); at index 4 `g` (103, telegram) < `m` (109, telemetry)). The 2 mid-round sort fixes are documented as a worked example of the value of `LC_ALL=C sort -c` as a post-edit guardrail; in both cases the position-by-position reasoning was incorrect on first attempt and the sort-checker is what caught the off-by-one. LC_ALL=C sort verified after the corrections.
- **John the Ripper `*2john` family Round 5 — bestcryptve2john + kdcdump2john + lion2john + lion2john-alt + mac2john-alt + mcafee_epo2john + money2john + mozilla2john + multibit2john + neo2john (10 more hash-extractor stems via single multi-stem file; cumulative JtR-helper coverage now 66 stems across rounds 144–148 + the pre-existing 23 dedicated single-stem files)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream JtR sources at [github.com/openwall/john (bleeding-jumbo)](https://github.com/openwall/john) — `run/bestcryptve2john.py` / `run/kdcdump2john.py` / `run/lion2john.pl` / `run/lion2john-alt.pl` / `run/mac2john-alt.py` / `run/mcafee_epo2john.py` / `run/money2john.py` / `run/mozilla2john.py` / `run/multibit2john.py` / `run/neo2john.py`. **Already-covered skip log** (caught by the `^#compdef( |.* )<name>( |$)` recheck before write): `libreoffice2john` (at `src/_libreoffice2john`), `luks2john` (at `src/_luks2john`), `mac2john` (at `src/_mac2john`), `mosquitto2john` (at `src/_mosquitto2john`), `openbsd_softraid2john` (at `src/_openbsd_softraid2john`), `enpass5tojohn` (at `src/_enpass5tojohn`), `keyring2john` (at `src/_keyring2john`) — 7 read from upstream source this round but found to be pre-existing single-stem files in `src/`, so excluded from the multi-stem. **Skipped without adding**: `mongodb2john.js` — the upstream source has no command-line argument parsing because the file is meant to be passed as a script to `mongosh` (e.g. `mongosh admin mongodb2john.js`), not invoked as a standalone command, so a binary-stem completion would never be triggered (the user never types `mongodb2john` at a shell prompt). `racf2john` — upstream `run/racf2john.py` 404s in the bleeding-jumbo branch; defer per the source-404 precedent. Single multi-stem file `_bestcryptve2john` is `#compdef bestcryptve2john kdcdump2john lion2john lion2john-alt mac2john-alt mcafee_epo2john money2john mozilla2john multibit2john neo2john`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage** (each transcribed from the exact usage string in upstream source): **`bestcryptve2john`** (2 flags + 1 positional via argparse): Jetico BestCrypt Volume encryption extractor; `-h/--help` + `--offset=N` (byte offset to BestCrypt partition, default 0) + required 1st positional disk/partition image. **`kdcdump2john`** (0 flags, 1 positional): Kerberos KDC dump → JtR converter; identifies format types 23 (RC4-HMAC) and 18 (AES256). **`lion2john`** (0 flags, 1+ `.plist` positionals): OS X 10.7 Lion salted-SHA-512 plist extractor; reads up to 1 MB per file, substitutes `UNKNOWN_USERNAME` when the username field cannot be parsed. **`lion2john-alt`** (0 flags, 1+ `.plist` positionals): alternative OS X Lion plist parser (`.pl` script, same surface as `lion2john`). **`mac2john-alt`** (0 flags, 1+ `.plist` positionals): Python 2 variant of `mac2john` for OS X / macOS 10.8+ ShadowHashData; the companion `src/_mac2john` covers the Python 3 mainline. **`mcafee_epo2john`** (0 flags, 1+ CSV positionals — `-` reads stdin): McAfee ePO OrionUsers CSV converter; CSV columns 1=username, 2=AuthURI, default separator comma. **`money2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): MS Money 2002-2007 / Money Plus encrypted-DB extractor; output type byte 1 = SHA1, 0 = MD5. **`mozilla2john`** (0 flags, 1+ `key3.db` positionals): Mozilla key3.db master-password extractor. **`multibit2john`** (0 flags, 1+ wallet positionals): MultiBit Classic or HD wallet extractor; accepts `.key` (Classic key backup), `.wallet` (Classic), or `mbhd.wallet.aes` (HD). **`neo2john`** (0 flags, 1+ `.db3` positionals): Neo wallet SQLite extractor; queries Key table for rows where the first column equals `'PasswordHash'`. **Blacklist additions (10) across 9 insertion blocks** (lion2john and lion2john-alt share one block): 1 between `bestcrypt2john` (2132) and `bestls` (2133) for `bestcryptve2john` (within bestcrypt* at index 9 `2` (50, 2john) < `v` (118, ve2john); within best* at index 4 `c` (99, crypt) < `l` (108, ls)); 1 between `kdbx` (12734) and `kdcsetup` (12735) for `kdcdump2john` (within kd* at index 2 `b` (98, kdb) < `c` (99, kdc); within kdc* at index 3 `d` (100, kdcdump) < `s` (115, kdcsetup)); **2 between `linuxsampler` (13894) and `lipo` (13895)** for `lion2john` and `lion2john-alt` (within li* at index 2 `n` (110, linux) < `o` (111, lion) < `p` (112, lipo); within lion2john* at index 8 end-of-string < `-` (45, alt) so `lion2john` < `lion2john-alt`); 1 between `mac2john` (15274) and `mac2unix` (15275) for `mac2john-alt` (within mac2john* at index 8 end < `-` (45, alt) < `u` (117, unix from mac2unix)); 1 between `mcabber` (15643) and `mcap` (15644) for `mcafee_epo2john` (within mc* at index 2 `a` (97, mca-mcap) consistent; at index 4 within mone* `r` (114, monerod) < `y` (121, money)); 1 between `mozilla-xremote-client` (16585) and `mozjpeg` (16586) for `mozilla2john` (within mozilla* at index 7 `-` (45, mozilla-x) < `2` (50, john); - **John the Ripper `*2john` family Round 4 — DPAPImk2john + adxcsouf2john + apop2john + diskcryptor2john + ejabberd2john + encdatavault2john + fvde2john + geli2john + gitea2john + ibmiscanner2john + iwork2john + keplr2john + known_hosts2john + krb2john (14 more hash-extractor stems via single multi-stem file; cumulative JtR-helper coverage now 56 stems across rounds 144–147 + the pre-existing 17 dedicated single-stem files)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream JtR sources at [github.com/openwall/john (bleeding-jumbo)](https://github.com/openwall/john) — `run/DPAPImk2john.py` / `run/adxcsouf2john.py` / `run/apop2john.py` / `run/diskcryptor2john.py` / `run/ejabberd2john.py` / `run/encdatavault2john.py` / `run/fvde2john.py` / `run/geli2john.py` / `run/gitea2john.py` / `run/ibmiscanner2john.py` / `run/iwork2john.py` / `run/keplr2john.py` / `run/known_hosts2john.py` / `run/krb2john.py`. **Already-covered skip log** (caught by the `^#compdef( |.* )<name>( |$)` recheck before write): `deepsound2john` (at `src/_deepsound2john`), `electrum2john` (at `src/_electrum2john`), `enpass2john` (at `src/_enpass2john`), `hccapx2john` (at `src/_hccapx2john`), `kirbi2john` (at `src/_kirbi2john`), `kwallet2john` (at `src/_kwallet2john`) — all 6 read from upstream source this round but found to be pre-existing single-stem files in `src/`, so excluded from the multi-stem to avoid `#compdef` collisions. Single multi-stem file `_DPAPImk2john` is `#compdef DPAPImk2john adxcsouf2john apop2john diskcryptor2john ejabberd2john encdatavault2john fvde2john geli2john gitea2john ibmiscanner2john iwork2john keplr2john known_hosts2john krb2john`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage** — this round contains the **most-complex single surface added to the JtR helpers so far** (`DPAPImk2john`) plus several that are still simple positional extractors: **`DPAPImk2john`** (~6 flags, 0 positionals — the only round-147 stem with non-trivial flag handling): Windows DPAPI masterkey extractor with two mutually exclusive operating modes — the **Standard mode** requires all three of `-S/--sid SID` + `-mk/--masterkey FILE` + `-c/--context CONTEXT` (where CONTEXT is the 4-value enum `domain|domain1607+|domain1607-|local`), while the **Preferred mode** takes `-P/--preferred FILE` alone and the completion encodes mutual exclusion by listing `(-P --preferred)` on each Standard-mode flag and `(-mk --masterkey -S --sid -c --context)` on each Preferred-mode flag; shared across both modes: `-d/--debug` and `--password=PASSWORD`. **`adxcsouf2john`** (0 flags, 1+ `*.DAT` positionals): IBM/Toshiba 4690 OS ADXCSOUF.DAT/ADXEPW0F.DAT password-hash extractor; upstream prints "Tip: Maximum password length is 8 on IBM/Toshiba 4690 systems" to stderr. **`apop2john`** (0 flags, 1+ pcap positionals): APOP challenge/response extractor from packet captures; output format `username:$dynamic_1017$hash$HEX$salt`; requires `scapy`. **`diskcryptor2john`** (0 flags, 1+ device/raw-image positionals): DiskCryptor volume-header extractor; reads first 2048 bytes, calculates entropy, warns if <6 bits/byte unless env-var `FORCE=1` is set; cascaded ciphers explicitly not supported per upstream comment. **`ejabberd2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): parses files generated by `ejabberdctl dump`. **`encdatavault2john`** (0 flags, **1 directory** positional): ENCSecurity DataVault folder extractor — takes a directory not a file, so the completion uses `_files -/` to restrict completion to dirs. **`fvde2john`** (0 flags, exactly 1 positional via argparse): FileVault Drive Encryption disk-image extractor. **`geli2john`** (0 flags, 1+ disk-image positionals): FreeBSD GELI metadata extractor; seeks to last 1024 bytes, looks for `GEOM::ELI` magic, supports AES-XTS and AES-CBC, metadata versions 0-7. **`gitea2john`** (1 flag, **stdin-driven**): `-h/--help` plus stdin input format `username:hex_salt:hex_hash:algorithm` where algorithm follows `pbkdf2$iterations$keylen`; typical pipeline `sqlite3 gitea.db 'SELECT name,salt,passwd,passwd_hash_algo FROM user;' | gitea2john.py`. **`ibmiscanner2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): IBM iSeries (ibmiscanner output) → AS400-SHA converter; transforms `username:hexhash` → `username:$as400ssha1$hexhash$username`. **`iwork2john`** (0 flags, 1+ Apple-iWork positionals — `.key`/`.numbers`/`.pages`): handles both iWork '09 and 2013/2014 formats. **`keplr2john`** (0 flags, **2 positionals** — 1 required folder + 1 optional password): Keplr Chrome-extension LevelDB extractor; when the optional 2nd arg (password) is supplied the script attempts scrypt-KDF + AES-128-CTR decryption rather than just dumping; upstream comment requires that the first arg must **not** begin with a hyphen. **`known_hosts2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): hashed-entry extractor from SSH known_hosts files; lines starting `|1|` go to stdout as `$known_hosts$<data>`, non-hashed entries go to stderr. **`krb2john`** (0 flags, 1+ `.pdml` positionals): Kerberos hash extractor from PDML (packet description markup language) files generated by `tshark -r capture.pcap -T pdml`. **Blacklist additions (14) across 14 insertion blocks** — verified position-by-position: 1 between `DP3` (69) and `DPPP` (70) for `DPAPImk2john` (within DP* at index 2 `3` (51) < `A` (65) < `P` (80) — the only round-147 insert in the uppercase block); 1 between `adwaita-1-demo` (637) and `aea` (638) for `adxcsouf2john` (within ad* at index 2 `v` (118, adv) < `w` (119, adw) < `x` (120, adx); at index 1 `d` (100, ad) < `e` (101, ae)); 1 between `apollo` (1125) and `app-sso` (1126) for `apop2john` (within apo* at index 3 `l` (108, llo) < `p` (112, pop); at index 2 `o` (111, apo) < `p` (112, app)); 1 between `diskard` (6276) and `diskinfo` (6277) for `diskcryptor2john` (within disk* at index 4 `a` (97, ard) < `c` (99, cryptor) < `i` (105, info)); 1 between `eix` (7165) and `ejabberdctl` (7166) for `ejabberd2john` (within e* at index 1 `i` (105, eix) < `j` (106, ejabb); within ejabberd* at index 8 `2` (50, john) < `c` (99, ctl)); 1 between `enca` (7286) and `encfs` (7287) for `encdatavault2john` (within enc* at index 3 `a` (97, enca) < `d` (100, encdata) < `f` (102, encfs)); 1 between `fuzztran` (8605) and `fvdeinfo` (8606) for `fvde2john` (within f* at index 1 `u` (117, fuzz) < `v` (118, fvde); within fvde* at index 4 `2` (50, john) < `i` (105, info)); 1 between `geli` (8986) and `gem` (8987) for `geli2john` (within g* at index 2 `l` (108, geli) < `m` (109, gem); within geli* at index 4 end-of-string < `2`); 1 between `gitea-mcp-server` (9446) and `gitg` (9447) for `gitea2john` (within gitea* at index 5 `-` (45, mcp) < `2` (50, john); at index 3 `e` (101, gitea) < `g` (103, gitg)); 1 between `ibmgtsim` (11385) and `ibnetdiscover` (11386) for `ibmiscanner2john` (within ibm* at index 3 `g` (103, gtsim) < `i` (105, iscanner); at index 2 `m` (109, ibm) < `n` (110, ibn)); 1 between `iwmbtfw` (12164) and `iwpriv` (12165) for `iwork2john` (within iw* at index 2 `m` (109, mbtfw) < `o` (111, work) < `p` (112, priv)); Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream JtR sources at [github.com/openwall/john (bleeding-jumbo)](https://github.com/openwall/john) — `run/1password2john.py` / `run/aem2john.py` / `run/androidbackup2john.py` / `run/ansible2john.py` / `run/apex2john.py` / `run/applenotes2john.py` / `run/aruba2john.py` / `run/atmail2john.pl` / `run/authenticator2john.py` / `run/bestcrypt2john.py` / `run/bitshares2john.py` / `run/cardano2john.py` / `run/ccache2john.py` / `run/cisco2john.pl` / `run/coinomi2john.py` / `run/cracf2john.py`. **Already-covered skip log** (caught by the `^#compdef( |.* )<name>( |$)` recheck before write): `aix2john` (at `src/_aix2john`), `andotp2john` (at `src/_andotp2john`), `bks2john` (at `src/_bks2john`), `blockchain2john` (at `src/_blockchain2john`) — all 4 read from upstream source this round but found to be pre-existing single-stem files in `src/`, so excluded from the multi-stem to avoid `#compdef` collisions. Single multi-stem file `_1password2john` is `#compdef 1password2john aem2john androidbackup2john ansible2john apex2john applenotes2john aruba2john atmail2john authenticator2john bestcrypt2john bitshares2john cardano2john ccache2john cisco2john coinomi2john cracf2john`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage** (each transcribed from the exact `Usage:` string in upstream source): **`1password2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals — files OR directories): accepts 1Password Agile Keychain dirs, Cloud Keychain dirs, or `OnePassword.sqlite` files; `process_file()` auto-detects which shape based on whether the arg is a SQLite file or a directory. **`aem2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): Adobe Experience Manager SSPR format converter; identifies `{SHA-256}` / `{SHA-512}` tagged lines with optional username prefix. **`androidbackup2john`** (0 flags, 1+ `.ab` positionals): Android Backup file parser. **`ansible2john`** (0 flags, 1+ `.yml`/`.yaml` positionals): Ansible Vault YAML parser; upstream source rejects anything other than AES256 cipher. **`apex2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): Oracle APEX hash CSV parser (comma-delimited 3-field lines). **`applenotes2john`** (0 flags, 1+ `.sqlite` positionals): Apple Notes SQLite parser; typical input lives at `~/Library/Group Containers/group.com.apple.notes/NoteStore.sqlite`. **`aruba2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): ArubaOS password-hash converter; output targets JtR dynamic format. **`atmail2john`** (0 flags, 0 positionals — **stdin-only**): atmail SSHA256 → JtR `$dynamic_62$` converter; reads stdin via `<>` so use shell redirection or pipe. **`authenticator2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): Authenticator-app data-file parser; upstream notes "JtR doesn't have a format for cracking this yet" — converter exists for future format support. **`bestcrypt2john`** (0 flags, 1+ `.jbc` positionals): Jetico BestCrypt container parser. **`bitshares2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals — three input shapes): accepts SQLite databases, `.bin` backup wallet files, or `.ldb` LevelDB files; runtime detection chooses between SQL query, binary pattern matching, or hex conversion. **`cardano2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): Cardano legacy secret-key extractor; requires `cbor2` library. **`ccache2john`** (0 flags, 1 positional): single Kerberos credential cache file (typically `/tmp/krb5cc_<uid>`); validates magic byte (`0x5` = standard, `0x76` = untested alt). **`cisco2john`** (1 flag, 0+ positionals — **stdin-fallback**): `cisco2john [-h] [cisco config file(s)]` — `-h` shows usage and exits; with 0 file args the script reads stdin via `while (<>)`. Output goes to stdout (extracted hashes) and stderr (plaintext + seed data for a wordlist-rules workflow). **`coinomi2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): Coinomi wallet parser; uses protobuf + scrypt + AES output format. **`cracf2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): IBM RACF CRACF.TXT converter; line-by-line transform to `username:$racf$*…` format. **Blacklist additions (16) across 16 insertion blocks** — all verified position-by-position via single index/two-index character comparisons: 1 between `1password` (9) and `2fa` (10) for `1password2john` (end-of-string < `2`; `1` (49) < `2` (50)); 1 between `aecho` (640) and `aeolus` (641) for `aem2john` (within ae* at index 2 `c` (99) < `m` (109) < `o` (111)); 1 between `android` (987) and `androiddeployqt` (988) for `androidbackup2john` (within android* at index 7 end < `b` (98) < `d` (100)); 1 between `ansible-vault` (1033) and `ansifilter` (1034) for `ansible2john` (within ansible* at index 7 `-` (45) < `2` (50); at index 4 `b` (98, ansible) < `f` (102, ansifilter)); 1 between `apex` (1088) and `apfel` (1089) for `apex2john` (within ap* at index 2 `e` (101, ex) < `f` (102, fel); end-of-string < `2`); 1 between `apple_dump` (1132) and `appleseed` (1133) for `applenotes2john` (within apple* at index 5 `_` (95) < `n` (110) < `s` (115)); 1 between `arturo` (1389) and `as` (1390) for `aruba2john` (within ar* at index 2 `t` (116, turo) < `u` (117, uba); - **John the Ripper `*2john` family Round 2 — androidfde2john + axcrypt2john + dashlane2john + ecryptfs2john + filezilla2john + ikescan2john + lotus2john + pfx2john + sap2john + sipdump2john + vmx2john (11 more hash-extractor stems via single multi-stem file; extends the round-144 `_unshadow` family to a 26-stem JtR-helper corpus when combined with `_john`/`_zip2john`/`_rar2john`/`_wpapcap2john`)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream JtR sources at [github.com/openwall/john (bleeding-jumbo)](https://github.com/openwall/john) — `run/androidfde2john.py` / `run/axcrypt2john.py` / `run/dashlane2john.py` / `run/ecryptfs2john.py` / `run/filezilla2john.py` / `run/ikescan2john.py` / `run/lotus2john.py` / `run/pfx2john.py` / `run/sap2john.pl` / `run/sipdump2john.py` / `run/vmx2john.py`. **Already-covered skip log** (caught by the `^#compdef( |.* )<name>( |$)` recheck before commit): `ethereum2john` (at `src/_ethereum2john`), `lastpass2john` (at `src/_lastpass2john`), `dmg2john` (at `src/_dmg2john`), `bitlocker2john` (at `src/_bitlocker2john`), `encfs2john` (at `src/_encfs2john`), `office2john` (at `src/_office2john`) — all 6 read from the source but found to be pre-existing single-stem files in `src/`, so excluded from the multi-stem to avoid `#compdef` collisions. Single multi-stem file `_androidfde2john` is `#compdef androidfde2john axcrypt2john dashlane2john ecryptfs2john filezilla2john ikescan2john lotus2john pfx2john sap2john sipdump2john vmx2john`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage** (each transcribed from the exact `Usage:` string in upstream source — most are positional-file extractors with no flags, but several have non-trivial 2-positional or optional-tail patterns documented below): **`androidfde2john`** (0 flags, exactly 2 positionals): `androidfde2john <data partition / image> <footer partition / image>` — note the upstream source carries an explicit limitation comment that the extractor only handles old Android ≤4.3 disk images with AES-256/CBC-ESSIV-SHA-256 cipher; this completion does not enforce that, only documents it. **`axcrypt2john`** (0 flags, 1 required + 1 optional positional): `axcrypt2john <axxfile> [KEY-FILE]` — when a 2nd arg is provided the script reads it as a keyfile, converts contents to hex, and prepends an asterisk in the output hash to mark keyfile presence. **`dashlane2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): `dashlane2john <.aes or .dash files from Dashlane for Windows Desktop / macOS>` — Windows lookup hint `%AppData%\Dashlane\profiles`; macOS hint `~/Library/Application Support/Dashlane/profiles/`. **`ecryptfs2john`** (0 flags, 1 required + 1 optional positional): `ecryptfs2john <wrapped-passphrase> [.ecryptfsrc]` — wrapped-passphrase typically lives at `~/.ecryptfs/wrapped-passphrase`; the optional `.ecryptfsrc` provides the salt value (typically at `~/.ecryptfsrc`). **`filezilla2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): `filezilla2john.py <FileZilla Server.xml file(s)>` — script identifies two hash types (32-char raw MD5 and 128-char SHA-512+salt); unsupported formats trigger warnings to stderr but do not halt batch processing. **`ikescan2john`** (0 flags, 1 required + 1 optional positional): `ikescan2john <psk-parameters-file> [norteluser]` — with 1 arg the output format is `$ike$*0*<data>`; with 2 args the output format is `$ike$*1*<username>*<data>` (Nortel mode). **`lotus2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): `lotus2john [Lotus Notes ID file(s)]` — extracts hashes from Lotus Notes `.id` files. **`pfx2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): `pfx2john <.pfx file(s)>` — PKCS#12 hash extractor; depends on `asn1crypto`. **`sap2john`** (0 flags, 1 required + 1 optional positional): `sap2john.pl <input-file> [A|B|D|E|F|H]` or `sap2john.pl - [A|B|D|E|F|H]` — the optional 2nd positional is a subset selector for SAP CODVN hash families (default `BFH` when omitted; the script validates the selector pattern against `/^[^ABDEFH]$/`). **`sipdump2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): `sipdump2john.py <sipdump files>` — converts SIPdump tool output to JtR hash format. **`vmx2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): `vmx2john <.vmx files>` — parses VMware `.vmx` config files for the `encryption.keySafe` line, validates PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA-1 + AES-256 + HMAC-SHA-1, emits hashcat-compatible output. **Blacklist additions (11) across 11 insertion blocks**: 1 between `androiddump` (990) and `androidtestrunner` (991) for `androidfde2john` (within android* at index 7 `d` (100, dump) < `f` (102, fde) < `t` (116, testrunner)); 1 between `awx-cli` (1858) and `axe` (1859) for `axcrypt2john` (within ax* at index 2 `c` (99, crypt) < `e` (101, axe); at index 1 `w` (119, awx) < `x` (120, axc)); 1 between `dashing` (5628) and `dasht` (5629) for `dashlane2john` (within dash* at index 4 `i` (105, ing) < `l` (108, lane) < `t` (116, dasht)); 1 between `ecryptfs-verify` (7072) and `ecryptfsd` (7073) for `ecryptfs2john` (within ecryptfs* at index 8 `-` (45) < `2` (50) < `d` (100)); 1 between `filetop-bpfcc` (8036) and `filite` (8037) for `filezilla2john` (within file* at index 4 `t` (116, top) < `z` (122, zilla); at index 3 `e` (101, file) < `i` (105, filite) so all `fileX*` < `fili*`); 1 between `iked` (11547) and `ilasm` (11548) for `ikescan2john` (within ike* at index 3 `-` (45, scan), `d` (100, iked), then `s` (115, scan2john) — `d` < `s`;
