- **John the Ripper `*2john` family Round 1 — unshadow + ssh2john + keepass2john + 7z2john + bitcoin2john + pdf2john + keychain2john + keystore2john + bitwarden2john + htdigest2john + monero2john (11 hash-extractor stems via single multi-stem file; extends the existing `_john` + `_zip2john` + `_rar2john` + `_wpapcap2john` coverage to a 15-stem JtR-helper corpus)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep; the pre-existing `_john` is also dup'd by stock zsh at `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh/site-functions/_john` but the 11 stems in this round are all stock-clean). Flag inventory transcribed directly from upstream JtR sources at [github.com/openwall/john (bleeding-jumbo)](https://github.com/openwall/john) — `src/*.c` for compiled helpers (`unshadow.c`/`keepass2john.c`) and `run/*.py`/`run/*.pl` for script helpers (`ssh2john.py`/`7z2john.pl`/`bitcoin2john.py`/`pdf2john.pl`/`keychain2john.py`/`keystore2john.py`/`bitwarden2john.py`/`htdigest2john.py`/`monero2john.py`). **The Debian-shipped manpages.debian.org pages all 302-redirect to a `dyn.manpages.debian.org` host that 404s the same content**, so this round abandoned man-page-based verification in favor of direct source-tree reading — the JtR sources display canonical usage strings (`fprintf(stderr, "Usage: %s …")`) that yield reliable surfaces. **Conflict-avoidance during writing**: initially the multi-stem `#compdef` line included `rar2john` and `wpapcap2john`, but the `^#compdef( |.* )<name>( |$)` recheck caught that both already have dedicated single-stem files at `src/_rar2john` and `src/_wpapcap2john`; both were removed from the new file before commit (would have produced double-#compdef registrations and broken at compinit time). **Hunt-dup-skip log this round**: ~25 candidates from a broader hunt of `*2john` helpers in `openwall/john/run/` — `ethereum2john`/`lastpass2john`/`pcap2john`/`gpg2john`/`dmg2john`/`bitlocker2john`/`androidfde2john`/`axcrypt2john`/`dashlane2john`/`ecryptfs2john`/`encfs2john`/`filezilla2john`/`ikescan2john`/`lotus2john`/`mongodb2john`/`office2john`/`pfx2john`/`pgpdisk2john`/`pwsafe2john`/`racf2john`/`sap2john`/`sipdump2john`/`sshng2john`/`uaf2john`/`vmx2john`/`zed2john` — these are deferred not because they don't exist (the GitHub `run/` directory listing confirms many do) but because their source files were not individually read this round to verify surface; per the no-vibe-coding rule the harvest claims only what's verified, and a future round can extend the multi-stem after reading each `.py`/`.pl`/`.c` source. Single multi-stem file `_unshadow` is `#compdef unshadow ssh2john keepass2john 7z2john bitcoin2john pdf2john keychain2john keystore2john bitwarden2john htdigest2john monero2john`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage** (each transcribed from the exact `Usage:` string in upstream source): **`unshadow`** (0 flags, exactly 2 positionals): `unshadow PASSWORD-FILE SHADOW-FILE` — the JtR helper that merges a Unix `/etc/passwd` and `/etc/shadow` into the JtR-cracking-friendly combined format. **`ssh2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): `ssh2john <RSA/DSA/EC/OpenSSH private key file(s)>` — extracts crackable hashes from SSH private keys; the `.py` script iterates `sys.argv[1:]` with no option parsing. **`keepass2john`** (1 flag, 1+ positionals): `keepass2john [-k <keyfile>] <.kdbx database(s)>` — `-k` registers an optional KeePass keyfile for two-factor crack inputs. **`7z2john`** (1 flag, 1+ positionals): `7z2john.pl [--skip-sensitive-data-warning] <7-Zip file>...` — the single flag suppresses the default "hashes may contain sensitive encrypted data" warning printed to stderr. **`bitcoin2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): `bitcoin2john [Bitcoin/Litecoin/PRiVCY wallet (.dat) files]` — the same `.py` extracts wallet hashes from Bitcoin, Litecoin, and PRiVCY-format `.dat` files. **`pdf2john`** (treated as 0 flags + 1+ positionals): the upstream `pdf2john.pl` is wired through ExifTool which exposes ExifTool's full flag surface, but the canonical contract documented in the synopsis is `pdf2john.pl <.pdf file(s)>` — keeping the completion to the documented contract avoids advertising ExifTool's incidental flags as if they were `pdf2john`'s own. **`keychain2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): `keychain2john [keychain files]` — extracts hashes from macOS `*.keychain*` files. **`keystore2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): `keystore2john.py <.keystore / .jks file(s)>` — extracts hashes from Java KeyStore files. **`bitwarden2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positional files-or-dirs): `bitwarden_extract.py <Bitwarden storage.js / com.x8bit.bitwarden_preferences.xml / Google Chrome's 'nngceckbap…' path>` — accepts any of three input shapes (Firefox extension storage, Android preferences XML, or a Chrome LevelDB directory path). **`htdigest2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): `htdigest2john.py <htdigest file(s)>` — Apache htdigest hash extractor. **`monero2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): `monero2john.py [Monero .keys file(s)]` — extracts hashes from Monero wallet `.keys` files. **Blacklist additions (11) across 11 insertion blocks**: 1 between `7z` (36) and `7za` (37) for `7z2john` (within 7z* at index 2 digit `2` (50) < lowercase `a` (97)); 1 between `bitcoin-wallet` (2224) and `bitcoind` (2225) for `bitcoin2john` (within bitcoin* at index 7 `-` (45) < `2` (50) < `d` (100)); 1 between `bitwarden-cli` (2234) and `bitwise` (2235) for `bitwarden2john` (within bitwarden* at index 9 `-` (45) < `2` (50); at index 4 within bitw* `a` (97, warden) < `i` (105, wise)); 1 between `htdigest2` (11078) and `htlatex` (11079) for `htdigest2john` (within htdigest2* at index 9 end-of-string < `j` (106); at index 2 within ht* `d` (100, digest) < `l` (108, latex)); 1 between `keepass2` (12751) and `keepassxc` (12752) for `keepass2john` (within keepass* at index 7 `2` (50) but within keepass2* at index 8 end < `j` (106); at index 7 within keepass* `2` (50) < `x` (120)); 1 between `keychain` (12789) and `keycloak` (12790) for `keychain2john` (within keychain* at index 8 end < `2` (50); at index 4 within keyc* `h` (104, chain) < `l` (108, cloak)); 1 between `keystone-wsgi-public` (12811) and `keytool` (12812) for `keystore2john` (within keysto* at index 6 `n` (110, stone) < `r` (114, store); at index 3 within key* `s` (115, store) < `t` (116, tool)); 1 between `monero-wallet-rpc` (16375) and `monerod` (16376) for `monero2john` (within monero* at index 6 `-` (45) < `2` (50) < `d` (100)); 1 between `pdf2htmlex` (19166) and `pdf2ps` (19167) for `pdf2john` (within pdf2* at index 4 `h` (104, htmlex) < `j` (106, john) < `p` (112, ps)); 1 between `ssh-vault` (24493) and `ssh3` (24494) for `ssh2john` (within ssh* at index 3 `-` (45) < `2` (50) < `3` (51)); 1 between `unsetpassword` (26893) and `unshar` (26894) for `unshadow` (within uns* at index 3 `e` (101, set) < `h` (104, shadow/shar); at index 5 within unsha* `d` (100, shadow) < `r` (114, shar)). - **BIND 9 small utils + Zeek package manager — arpaname + named-nzd2nzf + nsec3hash + zkg (4 stems across 2 files; pivots the network-tooling streak from the SiLK/YAF/Argus flow-record axis to the DNS-server + Zeek-package-manager axis)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep; existing BIND coverage `_dnssec-keygen`/`_dnssec-signzone`/`_dnssec-verify`/`_dnssec-revoke`/`_dnssec-importkey`/`_dnssec-cds`/`_rndc`/`_rndc-confgen`/`_named-checkconf`/`_named-checkzone` (also covers `named-compilezone`)/`_named-rrchecker`/`_named-journalprint`/`_ddns-confgen`/`_tsig-keygen`/`_genrandom`/`_dig`/`_delv`/`_dnstap-read`/`_mdig`/`_nsupdate` — none overlap with this round's 3 BIND additions; existing Zeek coverage `_bro` (covers both `bro` and `zeek` stems)/`_zeekctl`/`_zeek-cut`/`_zeek-archiver`/`_zeek-client`/`_zeek-config` does not include `zkg`). Flag inventory transcribed directly from upstream ISC docs at [bind9.readthedocs.io/en/latest/manpages.html](https://bind9.readthedocs.io/en/latest/manpages.html) + the canonical .rst sources at [gitlab.isc.org/isc-projects/bind9/-/raw/main/bin/tools/named-nzd2nzf.rst](https://gitlab.isc.org/isc-projects/bind9/-/raw/main/bin/tools/named-nzd2nzf.rst) + `bin/tools/nsec3hash.rst`, plus the Zeek package manager docs at [docs.zeek.org/projects/package-manager/en/stable/zkg.html](https://docs.zeek.org/projects/package-manager/en/stable/zkg.html). **Deferred per the source-404 precedent**: `isc-hmac-fixup` — the tool was removed from BIND ≥ 9.16; the v9_16 source-tree URL also 404s, so there is no canonical reference to transcribe; defer indefinitely unless a Debian-archive copy resurfaces. **Hunt-dup-skip log this round**: `broctl` + `bro-cut` (legacy Zeek aliases — broctl is renamed `zeekctl`, bro-cut is renamed `zeek-cut`; both shipped as compatibility shims pointing to the modern names already covered by `_zeekctl` + `_zeek-cut`; not worth a snowflake completion); `nfreader` (developer test-harness for libnfdump, no public man page); `ipset-save`/`ipset-restore` (these are subcommand forms of `ipset save`/`ipset restore` rather than separate binaries — and the base `ipset` ships with stock zsh anyway at `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh/functions/_ipset`). **Per-stem coverage**: **File 1 — `_arpaname` multi-stem `#compdef arpaname named-nzd2nzf nsec3hash` (3 BIND small utilities)**. **`arpaname`** (0 flags, 1 repeatable positional): variadic IPv4/IPv6 addresses to convert into `IN-ADDR.ARPA` / `IP6.ARPA` names. **`named-nzd2nzf`** (0 flags, 1 required positional): single `.nzd` database filename to dump as NZF text on stdout. **`nsec3hash`** (1 flag, ≥4 positional args): `-r` toggles between two invocation forms — standard `nsec3hash SALT ALGORITHM ITERATIONS DOMAIN` vs record-format `nsec3hash -r ALGORITHM FLAGS ITERATIONS SALT DOMAIN` (where the `-r` form mirrors NSEC3 record-presentation field order so users can paste record data directly). The `ALGORITHM` value is currently always `1` (SHA-1, the only NSEC3-defined algorithm). **File 2 — `_zkg` single-stem state-machine completion for the Zeek package manager**. Top level: **6 global flags** (`--version`/`--configfile=FILE`/`--user`/repeatable `*-v`/`*--verbose`/repeatable `*--extra-source NAME=URL`) + **20-subcommand `_describe` menu**. Per-subcommand coverage (only flags with explicit per-subcommand semantics are listed; `pin`/`unpin`/`config`/`env`/`search` have only positional package args): **`test`** (`--version`); **`install`** (`--skiptests`/`--nodeps`/`--nosuggestions`/`--version`/`--force`/repeatable `*--user-var NAME=VAL`); **`remove`/`uninstall`** (`--force`/`--nodeps`); **`purge`** (`--force`); **`bundle`** (`--force`/`--nodeps`/`--nosuggestions`/repeatable `*--manifest FILE`, positional bundle output file + packages); **`unbundle`** (`--replace`/`--force`/repeatable `*--user-var`, positional bundle file); **`refresh`** (`--aggregate`/`--fail-on-aggregate-problems`/`--push`/repeatable `*--sources`); **`upgrade`** (same 6 install-style flags); **`load`** (`--nodeps`); **`unload`** (`--force`/`--nodeps`); **`list`** (`--nodesc`/`--include-builtin`, positional category enum `installed|not_installed|loaded|unloaded|outdated|all`); **`info`** (`--version`/`--nolocal`/`--include-builtin`/`--json`/`--jsonpretty SPACES`/`--allvers`); **`autoconfig`** (`--force`); **`create`** (`--packagedir DIR` required/`--version`/repeatable `*--features`/`--template URL`/`--force`/repeatable `*--user-var`); **`template`** has its own nested state machine with a single sub-subcommand `template info` (`--json`/`--jsonpretty SPACES`/`--version`). **Blacklist additions (4) across 4 insertion blocks**: 1 between `arpalert` (1363) and `arpd` (1364) for `arpaname` (within arpa* at index 4 `l` (108, alert) < `n` (110, name); within arp* at index 3 `a` (97) < `d` (100, arpd) so `arpaname` < `arpd`); 1 between `named-journalprint` (17038) and `named-rrchecker` (17039) for `named-nzd2nzf` (within named-* at index 6 `j` (106, journal) < `n` (110, nzd) < `r` (114, rrchecker)); 1 between `nsd-zonec` (17709) and `nsenter` (17710) for `nsec3hash` (within ns* at index 2 `d` (100) < `e` (101) for nsd-* < nse*; within nse* at index 3 `c` (99, nsec3hash) < `n` (110, nsenter)); 1 between `zkTxnLogToolkit` (29563) and `zkserver` (29564) for `zkg` (within zk* at index 2 `T` (84 uppercase) < `g` (103 lowercase) < `s` (115 lowercase) — the uppercase block ends, zkg lands at the start of the lowercase block, and `g` (103) < `s` (115) so `zkg` precedes `zkserver`). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **Argus ra* Round 2 — racluster + radump + rahisto + ranonymize + rapath + rastream + rastrip + rasqlinsert (8 QoSient argus-client stems via single multi-stem file, extends the existing `_ra` multi-stem + `_rabins` to a 16-stem argus-client coverage)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep; existing coverage `_ra` covers `ra`/`ratop`/`ralabel`/`rasort`/`racount`/`rasplit` and `_rabins` covers `rabins`/`rabins2`, none of which overlap). Flag inventory transcribed directly from upstream Debian-shipped manpages at [manpages.debian.org/testing/argus-client/racluster.1.en.html](https://manpages.debian.org/testing/argus-client/racluster.1.en.html) + `radump.1`/`rahisto.1`/`ranonymize.1`/`rapath.1`/`rastream.1`/`rastrip.1`/`rasqlinsert.1`. **Deferred per the source-404 precedent**: `radium`, `ragator`, `raservices`, `ratimerange` (manpages.debian.org dynamic-redirect URLs all return 404 — the static path 302-redirects to a `dyn.manpages.debian.org` host that 404s the same content; will revisit when upstream restores). Single multi-stem file `_racluster` is `#compdef racluster radump rahisto ranonymize rapath rastream rastrip rasqlinsert`, dispatching on `$service`. Shared `_ra_common` (26 flags, mirrors the canonical inventory in `more_src/_ra` but kept local-scoped so the two files don't collide when both load) carries the base ra(1) surface every client inherits: `-h`/`-A`/`-b`, `-c` column delimiter, `-D 1-8` debug level, `-d` daemon toggle, `-e` regex match, `-E` rejected-record file, `-F .conf` config, `-H` human-readable, `-L` header frequency, `-n`/`-N`/`-p`/`-q`, **repeatable** `*-r FILE`, `-R DIR` recursive, **repeatable** `*-s FIELD`, `-S URI` remote source, `-t` time range, `-T` remote seconds, `-u` Unix time, `-w FILE` append, `-X` reset, `-z`/`-Z s|d|b` TCP state. Three shared field/object/dsr arrays carry per-tool enums: **`_agg` (28 values)** — aggregation objects used by `racluster -m` and `rasqlinsert -m` (`none`/`all`/`srcid`/`smac`/`dmac`/`soui`/`doui`/`smpls`/`dmpls`/`svlan`/`dvlan`/`saddr`/`daddr`/`matrix`/`proto`/`sport`/`dport`/`stos`/`dtos`/`sttl`/`dttl`/`stcpb`/`dtcpb`/`inode`/`sco`/`dco`/`sas`/`das`/`ias`); **`_hist_metrics` (24 values)** — `rahisto -H` metrics (`dur`/`avgdur`/`proto`/`sport`/`dport`/`stos`/`dtos`/`sttl`/`dttl`/`sbytes`/`dbytes`/`sappbytes`/`dappbytes`/`spkts`/`dpkts`/`smeansz`/`dmeansz`/`sload`/`dload`/`sloss`/`dloss`/`sploss`/`dploss`/`srate`/`drate`); **`_strip_dsr` (13 values)** — `rastrip -M` Data Source Record names (`flow`/`time`/`metric`/`agr`/`net`/`vlan`/`mpls`/`jitter`/`ipattr`/`suser`/`duser`/`mac`/`icmp`/`encaps`). **Per-stem coverage**: **`racluster`** (5 flags beyond shared): `-f racluster.conf`, repeatable `*-m OBJECT` (from `_agg`), repeatable **`*-M MODE`** 6-value enum (`correct`/`nocorrect`/`norep`/`rmon`/`ind`/`replace`), `-P N` (parallel processors, default 1), `-V` verbose; trailing filter expression. **`radump`** (shared only): tcpdump-style payload printer — inherits the full ra surface, no tool-specific flags in the man page; trailing filter expression. **`rahisto`** (3 flags beyond shared): repeatable **`*-M MODE`** 3-value enum (`nozero`/`outlayer`/`perflow`), repeatable **`*-H SPEC`** taking `[abs] metric bin[L]:range|size` (where `metric` completes from `_hist_metrics`), repeatable `*-m OBJECT` (from `_agg`); trailing filter expression. **`ranonymize`** (1 flag beyond shared): `-f anonymization.conf`; trailing filter expression. **`rapath`** (5 flags beyond shared): `-A` (draw path description with legend), repeatable **`*-M MODE`** 4-value enum (`node`/`addr`/`aspath`/`asnode`), repeatable `*-m FIELD` 3-value enum (`srcid`/`saddr`/`daddr`), `-q` (suppress per-node record output), `-f ralabel.conf` (ASN labeling); trailing filter expression. **`rastream`** (4 flags beyond shared): `-a N` (append suffix length, default 2), `-B N` (buffer hold time before processing in sec), `-f program` (post-processing program), repeatable **`*-M MODE`** 6-value enum (`count`/`size`/`time`/`flow`/`lock`/`lock=nonblock`); trailing filter expression. **`rastrip`** (1 flag beyond shared): repeatable **`*-M DSR`** with `_strip_dsr` 13-value enum plus `replace` (prefix with `+` to add or `-` to remove, per upstream); trailing filter expression. **`rasqlinsert`** (3 flags beyond shared): repeatable **`*-M MODE`** 4-value enum (`cache`/`drop`/`rewrite`/`time`), repeatable `*-m FIELDS` (DB schema flow keys — `none` or values from `_agg`), `-S` (read from argus data stream); trailing filter expression. **Blacklist additions (8) across 6 insertion blocks**: 1 between `rackup` (21548) and `raco` (21549) for `racluster` (within rac* at index 3 `k` (107) < `l` (108) < `o` (111)); 1 between `radtest` (21574) and `radvd` (21575) for `radump` (within rad* at index 3 `t` (116) < `u` (117) < `v` (118)); 1 between `rahash2` (21589) and `raid-check` (21590) for `rahisto` (within rah* at index 3 `a` (97) < `i` (105), and at index 2 `h` (104) < `i` (105) so `rahisto` < `raid-check`); 2 between `ranlib` (21632) and `rapper` (21633) for `ranonymize` + `rapath` (within ran* at index 3 `l` (108) < `o` (111) so `ranlib` < `ranonymize`; at index 2 `n` (110) < `p` (112) so `ranonymize` < `rapath` < `rapper`); 1 between `rasqal-config` (21657) and `raster2pgsql` (21658) for `rasqlinsert` (within rasq* at index 4 `a` (97) < `l` (108)); 2 between `rasterizer` (21660) and `rasttopnm` (21661) for `rastream` + `rastrip` (within rast* at index 4 `e` (101) < `r` (114) < `t` (116), and within rastr* at index 5 `e` (101) < `i` (105) so `rastream` < `rastrip`). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **YAF + super_mediator Round 1 — yaf + yafscii + getFlowKeyHash + yafMeta2Pcap + super_mediator (5 stems via single multi-stem file, pivot from SiLK to the adjacent CERT NetSA flow-meter family)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Flag inventory transcribed directly from upstream CERT NetSA per-tool docs at [tools.netsa.cert.org/yaf2/yaf.html](https://tools.netsa.cert.org/yaf2/yaf.html) + `yafscii.html`/`getFlowKeyHash.html`/`yafMeta2Pcap.html` plus [tools.netsa.cert.org/super_mediator/super_mediator.html](https://tools.netsa.cert.org/super_mediator/super_mediator.html). **Deferred per the source-404 precedent**: `filterv6` and `yafzcballast` (both upstream 404). Single multi-stem file `_yaf` is `#compdef yaf yafscii getFlowKeyHash yafMeta2Pcap super_mediator`, dispatching on `$service`. Shared `_log` (4 flags) carries the canonical NetSA log cluster (`--log` dest spec / `--loglevel quiet|error|critical|warning|message|info|debug` / `--verbose` / `--version`) — applied to the 2 tools whose docs explicitly list it; the other 3 get explicit help/version flags matching upstream. **Per-stem coverage**: **`yaf`** (the IPFIX flow meter — 70+ flags, the largest single-tool surface of the round): Lua `--config`, input cluster (`--in`, **`--live` 6-value enum** `pcap|pfring|dag|napatech|netronome|zc`, `--caplist`, `--noerror`, `--filter BPF`, `--filter-file`, `--decompress`, `--export-interface`), output cluster (`--out`, **`--ipfix` 4-value enum** `tcp|udp|sctp|spread`, `--ipfix-port` default 4739, `--rotate`, `--lock`, `--no-output`, `--stats` default 300s, `--no-stats`, `--no-tombstone`, `--tombstone-configured-id`), decoder cluster (`--no-frag`, `--max-frags`, **2-flag mutex** `--ip4-only` ↔ `--ip6-only`, `--gre-decode`, `--vxlan-decode` + `--vxlan-decode-ports`, `--geneve-decode` + `--geneve-decode-ports`, `--force-read-all`, `--no-vlan-in-key`), flow-table cluster (`--idle-timeout`/`--active-timeout`/`--udp-temp-timeout`/`--max-flows`/`--max-payload`/`--protocol-payload`/`--udp-payload`/`--silk`/`--udp-uniflow`), export cluster (`--export-payload`, `--payload-applabel-select`, `--max-export`, `--uniflow`, `--mac`, `--force-ip6-export`, `--observation-domain`, `--flow-stats`, `--delta`, `--ingress`, `--egress`, `--metadata-export`, `--time-elements` ms/μs/ns), app-label cluster (`--applabel` + `--applabel-rules` + `--applabel-check-early` + `--applabel-max-payload`), nDPI (`--ndpi` + `--ndpi-protocol-file`), `--entropy`, TLS cluster (`--tls`, `--tls-ca`, `--tls-cert`, `--tls-key`), Spread (repeatable `*--group`, **`--groupby` 5-value enum** `port|vlan|applabel|protocol|version`), privilege drop (`--become-user`/`--become-group`), PCAP-export cluster (`--pcap`, `--pcap-per-flow`, `--max-pcap` default 25MB, `--pcap-timer`, `--pcap-meta-file`, `--index-pcap`, `--hash`, `--stime`), plugin cluster (repeatable `*--plugin-name` + `--plugin-opts` + repeatable `*--plugin-conf`), p0f passive-OS-fingerprinting cluster (`--p0fprint` + `--p0f-fingerprints` + `--fpexport`). **`yafscii`** (IPFIX → text printer, 11 flags beyond shared `_log`): `--in`, `--out`, `--tabular`, `--mac` (with `--tabular`), `--print-header` (with `--tabular`), **2-flag mutex** `--daemon` ↔ `--foreground`, `--lock`, `--poll` default 60s, `--nextdir` (or `delete`), `--faildir` (or `delete`). **`getFlowKeyHash`** (compute flow-key hash, 14 flags + 1 help/version): `--in`/`--out`, IP+port+protocol+vlan inputs (`--sip4`/`--dip4` dotted IPv4, `--sip6`/`--dip6` IPv6, `--sport`/`--dport` 0-65535, `--protocol` 6=TCP/17=UDP, `--vlan`), `--ipfix` output mode, time controls (`--date YYYY-MM-DD` UTC, `--time HH:MM:SS[.sss]`), `--reverse` (swap IPs+ports), `--snmp` (ignore SNMP iface). **`yafMeta2Pcap`** (extract per-flow PCAP from yaf metadata, 12 flags): `--pcap-meta-file` (glob pattern), `--pcap` (glob pattern), `--caplist FILE` / `--metalist FILE`, `--out`, `--hash` (flow key hash), `--time`/`--etime` ms-since-epoch, **`-w+`/`--window` ms** (short + long form), `--packets` (total packet count for bi-flow), `--yaf-program` (path to yaf executable), `--verbose`. **`super_mediator`** (IPFIX collector/aggregator that ingests yaf output, 27 flags): `--config`, `--test-config` (parse + exit), input cluster (**`--ipfix-input` 2-value enum** `tcp|udp`, `--ipfix-port` default 18000, `--polling-interval`, `--move-dir`, `--no-locked-files`), output cluster (`--out`, **`--output-mode` 4-value enum** `tcp|udp|text|json`, `--export-port` default 18001, `--rotate`, `--fields`, `--print-headers`, `--no-stats`, `--preserve-obdomain`, `--rewrite-ssl-certs`, `--disable-metadata-export`), `--ipsetfile`, `--sleep` μs, `--udp-temp-timeout`, log cluster (`--log` dest, `--log-dir`, `--verbose`, `--quiet`), daemon cluster (`--daemonize`, `--pidfile`, `--become-user`/`--become-group`). **Blacklist - **SiLK Round 6 — rwappend + rwcombine + rwdedupe + rwmatch + rwnetmask + rwrandomizeip + rwgeoip2ccmap + rwpackchecker (8 single-purpose utility stems via single multi-stem file, extends cumulative SiLK coverage to 43)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Flag inventory transcribed directly from upstream CERT NetSA per-tool docs at [tools.netsa.cert.org/silk/rwappend.html](https://tools.netsa.cert.org/silk/rwappend.html) + `rwcombine.html`/`rwdedupe.html`/`rwmatch.html`/`rwnetmask.html`/`rwrandomizeip.html`/`rwgeoip2ccmap.html`/`rwpackchecker.html`. **Deferred per the source-404 precedent**: `rwconvert` and `rwallroute` — both pages return HTTP 404 upstream; will revisit when archives expose them. Single multi-stem file `_rwappend` is `#compdef rwappend rwcombine rwdedupe rwmatch rwnetmask rwrandomizeip rwgeoip2ccmap rwpackchecker`, dispatching on `$service`. Shared `_meta` (6 flags) carries the standard CERT NetSA header-annotation + compression + help/version cluster — applied only where the per-tool doc explicitly lists those flags, since `rwgeoip2ccmap` and `rwpackchecker` have stripped-down surfaces. **Per-stem coverage**: **`rwappend`** (3 flags beyond shared): `--create=-` (optional TEMPLATE_FILE; format/version/byte-order is inherited from template if given), `--print-statistics` (stderr report of per-source record counts + total appended), positional 1 = target SiLK file + repeatable positional sources. **`rwcombine`** (11 flags beyond shared): `--actions all|timeout` (combination strategy), **`--ignore-fields`** 12-value `_values -s ,` enum (`sIP`/`dIP`/`sPort`/`dPort`/`protocol`/`sensor`/`in`/`out`/`nhIP`/`class`/`type`/`application`), `--max-idle-time N` (seconds), `--print-statistics=-` (optional FILE), `--temp-directory`, `--buffer-size` (K/M/G suffixes, default ~2GB), `--print-filenames`, `--output-path`, repeatable `*--xargs`, `--help-fields`. **`rwdedupe`** (12 flags beyond shared): **`--ignore-fields`** 20-value `_values -s ,` enum (the dedupe surface extends combine's 12 with `packets`/`bytes`/`flags`/`sTime`/`duration`/`initialFlags`/`sessionFlags`/`attributes`), per-field delta tolerances (`--packets-delta N`/`--bytes-delta N`/`--stime-delta FLOAT_ms`/`--duration-delta FLOAT_ms`), `--temp-directory`, `--buffer-size`, `--print-filenames`, `--output-path`, repeatable `*--xargs`, `--help-fields`. **`rwmatch`** (10 flags beyond shared, 3 positionals): **repeatable `*--relate FIELD_PAIR`** (required ≥1), `--time-delta` (seconds), **3-flag time-mode mutex** `--absolute-delta` (default) ↔ `--relative-delta` ↔ `--infinite-delta`, `--symmetric-delta` (allow initial flow in response), `--unmatched q|r|b` (3-value enum), `--ipv6-policy ignore|asv4|mix|force|only`, `--help-relate`, positional 1 = query / 2 = response / 3 = output SiLK file. **`rwnetmask`** (13 flags beyond shared): per-axis IPv4/IPv6 prefix-length controls — `--4sip-prefix-length`/`--4dip-prefix-length`/`--4nhip-prefix-length` + their IPv6 counterparts `--6sip-`/`--6dip-`/`--6nhip-prefix-length` + 3 deprecated aliases (`--sip-prefix-length` = `--4sip-prefix-length`, etc), `--output-path`, `--print-filenames`, `--ipv6-policy`, repeatable `*--xargs`. **`rwrandomizeip`** (7 flags beyond shared, 2 positionals): `--seed N`, IPset filtering (`--only-change-set IPSET` ↔ `--dont-change-set IPSET`), `--consistent` (deterministic input → output IP mapping), state persistence (`--save-table FILE` / `--load-table FILE`), positional 1 = input / 2 = output. **`rwgeoip2ccmap`** (10 flags + 5 deprecated, **no shared `_meta`** — distinct annotation/help cluster matching upstream): **`--mode`** 7-value enum `auto|ipv4|ipv6|csv|binary|geoip2|legacy`, `--input-path`, `--output-path` (binary CC prefix-map), `--dry-run`, **`--fields`** 5-value `_values -s ,` enum (`location`/`registered`/`represented`/`flags`/`continent` — GeoName field priority), `--note-add`, `--note-file-add`, `--invocation-strip`, plus 5 deprecated legacy flags (`--csv-input`/`--v6-csv-input`/`--encoded-input`/`--input-file`/`--output-file`). **`rwpackchecker`** (5 flags + help/version — **stripped-down surface, no shared `_meta`**): repeatable `*--value TEST=VALUE`, repeatable `*--allowable-count TEST=ALLOWED`, `--print-all`, repeatable `*--xargs`, positional input SiLK files. **Blacklist additions (8)**: 1 inserted between `rwall` (22761) and `rwbagbuild` (22762) for `rwappend`; 1 between `rwcat` (22767) and `rwcount` (22768) for `rwcombine`; 1 between `rwcount` (22768) and `rwfileinfo` (22769) for `rwdedupe`; 1 between `rwflowpack` (22772) and `rwgroup` (22773) for `rwgeoip2ccmap`; 3 between `rwipfix2silk` (22776) and `rwpmapbuild` (22777) for `rwmatch` + `rwnetmask` + `rwpackchecker`; 1 between `rwpollexec` (22780) and `rwreceiver` (22781) for `rwrandomizeip`. - **SiLK Round 5 — rwflowpack + rwflowappend + rwpollexec + rwsender + rwreceiver (5 daemon binary stems via single multi-stem file, extends cumulative SiLK coverage to 35)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Flag inventory transcribed directly from upstream CERT NetSA per-tool docs at [tools.netsa.cert.org/silk/rwflowpack.html](https://tools.netsa.cert.org/silk/rwflowpack.html) + `rwflowappend.html`/`rwpollexec.html`/`rwsender.html`/`rwreceiver.html`. Single multi-stem file `_rwflowpack` is `#compdef rwflowpack rwflowappend rwpollexec rwsender rwreceiver`, dispatching on `$service` — covers the SiLK daemon pipeline (probe-side packing → hourly-file appending → directory-scan exec → encrypted file transfer). Three shared sub-arrays collapse the repetitive surfaces: **`_log` (7 flags)** carries the canonical 3-flag log-destination mutex (`--log-destination none|stdout|stderr|syslog|both` ↔ `--log-pathname FILE` ↔ `--log-directory DIR`) plus rotation/severity config (`--log-basename`, `--log-level emerg|alert|crit|err|warning|notice|info|debug`, `--log-sysfacility`, `--log-post-rotate`); **`_daemon` (5 flags)** carries `--pidfile`, `--no-chdir`, `--no-daemon`, `--help`, `--version`; **`_tls` (8 flags)** carries the GnuTLS cluster (`--tls-ca`/`--tls-cert`/`--tls-key`/`--tls-pkcs12`/`--tls-priority`/`--tls-security low|medium|high|ultra`/`--tls-crl`/`--tls-debug-level`). **Per-stem coverage**: **`rwflowpack`** (24 flags beyond shared): **`--input-mode` 4-value enum** `stream|fcfiles|pdufile|respool` driving four conditional flag groups, **`--output-mode` 3-value enum** `local-storage|incremental-files|sending`, `--sensor-configuration`, `--packing-logic`, `--sensor-name`, `--polling-interval`, `--incoming-directory`, `--netflow-file` (pdufile mode), `--root-directory`, `--incremental-directory`, `--sender-directory`, `--no-file-locking`, `--flush-timeout` (default 120s), `--file-cache-size` (default 128 open files), `--pack-interfaces`, `--byte-order native|big|little`, `--compression-method none|zlib|lzo1x|snappy|best`, `--site-config-file`, `--error-directory`, `--archive-directory`, `--flat-archive`, `--post-archive-command`, `--verify-sensor-config`. **`rwflowappend`** (15 flags beyond shared): `--incoming-directory` + `--root-directory` + `--error-directory` (all required), `--archive-directory`, `--flat-archive`, `--post-command`, `--hour-file-command`, `--threads` (default 1), `--reject-hours-past`/`--reject-hours-future`, `--no-file-locking`, `--polling-interval` (default 15s), **`--byte-order` 4-value enum** `as-is|native|big|little` (the `as-is` extra value distinguishes it from rwflowpack's 3-value enum), `--compression-method`, `--site-config-file`. **`rwpollexec`** (8 flags beyond shared): `--incoming-directory` + `--command` (with `%s` file substitution) + `--error-directory` (all required), `--archive-directory`, `--flat-archive`, `--simultaneous` (1-50, default 1), **repeatable `*--timeout=SIGNAL,SECS`** (send signal after timeout), `--polling-interval`. **`rwsender`** (17 flags beyond shared+tls, 7 of them repeatable): `--identifier`, **2-flag mutex** `--mode server|client`, `--incoming-directory`, `--processing-directory`, `--error-directory`, `--server-port` (server mode), repeatable `*--client-ident` (server mode), repeatable `*--server-address IDENT:HOST:PORT` (client mode), repeatable `*--local-directory [IDENT:]DIR` (duplicate copies), `--unique-local-copies`, repeatable `*--filter IDENT:REGEXP` (routing), repeatable `*--priority NUM:REGEXP` (0-100, default 50), `--polling-interval`, `--send-attempts` (0=unlimited), `--block-size` (256-65535, default 8192). **`rwreceiver`** (12 flags beyond shared+tls): `--identifier`, `--mode server|client`, `--destination-directory`, server flags (`--server-port` + repeatable `*--client-ident`), client flag (repeatable `*--server-address`), `--post-command` (supports `%s` for path, `%I` for sender ID, `%%` for literal %), repeatable `*--duplicate-destination`, `--unique-duplicates`, `--freespace-minimum` (supports K/M/G/T suffixes), `--space-maximum-percent` (default 100). **Blacklist additions (5)**: 2 inserted between `rwfilter` (22770) and `rwgroup` (22771) for the `rwflow*` block (`rwflowappend` → `rwflowpack`); 3 between `rwpmaplookup` (22777) and `rwsetbuild` (22778) for the `rwpollexec`+`rwreceiver`+`rwsender` block. Sort verified position-by-position: at index 3 within rwf* 'i' (filter, 105) < 'l' (flow, 108) so `rwflow*` > `rwfilter`; at index 6 within rwflow* 'a' (append, 97) < 'p' (pack, 112) so `rwflowappend` < `rwflowpack`; at index 2 'p' (pollexec) < 'r' (receiver) < 's' (sender), at index 3 within rws* `rwsender` 'e' then index 4 'n' (110) < 't' (rwsetbuild, 116) so `rwsender` < `rwsetbuild`. Single multi-stem file `_rwaggbag` is `#compdef rwaggbag rwaggbagbuild rwaggbagcat rwaggbagtool`, dispatching on `$service` — covers the Aggregate Bag suite, distinct from the single-key/single-counter Bag tools in round 136. Shared `_meta` (7 flags) array carries the standard CERT NetSA header annotation + compression + help/version cluster (`*--note-add`/`*--note-file-add`/`--invocation-strip`/`--compression-method none|zlib|lzo1x|snappy|best`/`--site-config-file`/`--help`/`--version`). **Per-stem coverage**: **`rwaggbag`** (build aggregate bag from flow records): `--keys` (key field list) + `--counters` (counter field list) — both required, `--output-path`, `--copy-input`, `--note-strip`, repeatable `*--xargs`, `--print-filenames`, `--ipv6-policy ignore|asv4|mix|force|only`, `--help-fields`. **`rwaggbagbuild`** (text → aggregate bag): `--fields` (combined key+counter list), repeatable `*--constant-field FIELD=VALUE`, `--column-separator` (default `|`), `--no-titles`, `--bad-input-lines FILE`, `--verbose`, `--stop-on-error`, `--output-path`, repeatable `*--xargs`, `--help-fields`. **`rwaggbagcat`** (print aggregate bag contents): `--fields` (output column order), repeatable `*--missing-field FIELD=STRING` (placeholder for missing-field rows), `--output-path`, `--pager`, `--timestamp-format`, **`--ip-format`** 8-value enum (`canonical`/`no-mixed`/`decimal`/`hexadecimal`/`zero-padded`/`map-v4`/`unmap-v6`/`force-ipv6`), `--no-titles`/`--no-columns`/`--column-separator`/`--no-final-delimiter`/`--delimited`, `--integer-sensors` (sensor IDs not names), `--integer-tcp-flags`, `--help-fields`. **`rwaggbagtool`** (aggregate bag arithmetic + filtering, 23 flags + 3 mutex groups): **2-flag field-selection mutex** `--remove-fields` ↔ `--select-fields`, repeatable `*--insert-field FIELD=VALUE`, **3-flag operation mutex** `--add` (default) ↔ `--subtract` ↔ `--divide`, `--zero-divisor-result error|remove|nochange|maximum`, repeatable `*--scalar-multiply` (either `N` or `FIELD=N`), repeatable per-field range filters (`*--min-field`/`*--max-field`), IPset masking (`*--set-intersect FIELD=FILE`/`*--set-complement FIELD=FILE`), **2-flag output-format mutex** `--to-bag BAG_KEY,BAG_COUNTER` ↔ `--to-ipset FIELD`, `--ipset-record-version 0|2|3|4|5`, output (`--output-path`, `--modify-inplace`, `--backup-path`), `--note-strip`, `--help-fields`. **Blacklist additions (4)**: all inserted between `rw` (22756) and `rwall` (22757) as a contiguous `rwaggbag*` block (`rwaggbag` → `rwaggbagbuild` → `rwaggbagcat` → `rwaggbagtool`). - **SiLK Round 3 — rwipfix2silk + rwsilk2ipfix + rwpmapbuild + rwpmapcat + rwpmaplookup + rwsiteinfo + rwswapbytes + rwtuc + rwsplit (9 binary stems via single multi-stem file, extends cumulative SiLK coverage to 26)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Flag inventory transcribed directly from upstream CERT NetSA per-tool docs at [tools.netsa.cert.org/silk/rwipfix2silk.html](https://tools.netsa.cert.org/silk/rwipfix2silk.html) + `rwsilk2ipfix.html`/`rwpmapbuild.html`/`rwpmapcat.html`/`rwpmaplookup.html`/`rwsiteinfo.html`/`rwswapbytes.html`/`rwtuc.html`/`rwsplit.html`. Single multi-stem file `_rwipfix2silk` is `#compdef rwipfix2silk rwsilk2ipfix rwpmapbuild rwpmapcat rwpmaplookup rwsiteinfo rwswapbytes rwtuc rwsplit`, dispatching on `$service` — covers the conversion + site-config + file-utility half of the SiLK suite. Combined with round-135 (`_rwfilter`, 8 stems) and round-136 (`_rwsetbuild`, 9 stems), **the repo now provides 26-stem SiLK coverage** which is essentially the full active toolkit. Shared `_meta` (5 flags) + `_disp` (8 flags) arrays carry over from round-136. **Per-stem coverage**: **`rwipfix2silk`** (IPFIX → SiLK binary): `--silk-output FILE`, `--interface-values snmp|vlan` (input/output interface meaning), `--log-destination none|stdout|stderr|PATH`, `--log-flags` (`sampling`/`show-templates`/`record-timestamps`), `--print-statistics`, repeatable `*--xargs FILE`. **`rwsilk2ipfix`** (SiLK → IPFIX): `--ipfix-output FILE`, `--single-template` (legacy single-template mode), `--no-site-name-elements` (exclude flowtype/class/type/sensor name elements), `--print-statistics`, `--site-config-file`. **`rwpmapbuild`** (text → binary prefix map): `--input-path FILE`/`--output-path *.pmap`, `--mode ipv4|ipv6|proto-port`, `--dry-run`, `--ignore-errors`, `--invocation-strip`. **`rwpmapcat`** (print prefix map contents): inherits `_disp` + **3-flag map-source mutex** `--map-file *.pmap` ↔ `--address-types[=FILE]` ↔ `--country-codes[=FILE]`, `--output-types` (comma-separated `_values -s ,` over `mapname`/`type`/`ranges`/`labels`), `--ignore-label`, `--ip-label-to-ignore IP`, `--no-cidr-blocks`, `--left-justify-labels`, deprecated `--integer-ips`/`--zero-pad-ips`. **`rwpmaplookup`** (lookup IPs against prefix maps): inherits `_disp` + same **3-flag map-source mutex** + **input-mode mutex** `--no-files` ↔ `--ipset-files`, repeatable `*--xargs`, `--fields` (comma-separated `_values -s ,` over `key`/`value`/`block`), `--no-errors`, deprecated IP-format flags. **`rwsiteinfo`** (silk.conf introspection): inherits `_disp` + `--fields` with a **17-value `_values -s ,` enum** seeded from the upstream field reference (`class`/`type`/`flowtype`/`sensor`/`sensor-id`/`sensor-description`/`default-class`/`default-type`/`repo-start-date`/`repo-end-date`/`file-size`/`group`/`default-class-type-list`/`class-type-list`/`class-list`/`sensor-list`/`flowtypes`), per-axis filters (`--classes`/`--types`/`--flowtypes`/`--sensors`/`--groups` — comma-separated), `--data-rootdir`/`--site-config-file`, `--timestamp-format`, `--list-delimiter` (for `:list` fields), `--help-fields`. **`rwswapbytes`** (byte-order conversion): **4-flag mutex** `--big-endian` ↔ `--little-endian` ↔ `--native-endian` ↔ `--swap-endian` (unconditional), 2 positionals (`infile`/`outfile`). **`rwtuc`** (text → SiLK uniform records): `--fields` (column parse spec), `--column-separator`, `--output-path`, `--bad-input-lines FILE` (capture unparseable lines), `*--xargs`, `--verbose`, `--stop-on-error`, `--no-titles`; **23 fixed-value override flags** for any field absent from text input (`--saddress`/`--daddress`/`--sport`/`--dport`/`--protocol`/`--packets`/`--bytes`/`--flags-all`/`--flags-initial`/`--flags-session`/`--stime`/`--etime`/`--duration`/`--sensor`/`--input-index`/`--output-index`/`--application`/`--class`/`--type`/`--icmp-type`/`--icmp-code`/`--attributes`/`--next-hop-ip` — the canonical pattern when text logs lack a field). **`rwsplit`** (split SiLK files by criteria): **4-flag split-criterion mutex** `--ip-limit N` ↔ `--flow-limit N` ↔ `--packet-limit N` ↔ `--byte-limit N`, `--basename PREFIX` (output filename prefix), random-sampling `--seed`/`--sample-ratio`/`--file-ratio`, `--max-outputs N`, `--print-filenames`, `--site-config-file`, repeatable `*--xargs`. **Blacklist additions (9)**: 4 inserted between `rwhod` (22769) and `rwsetbuild` (22770) for the `rwipfix2silk`/`rwpmap*` block; 2 between `rwsetunion` (22775) and `rwsnoop` (22776) for `rwsilk2ipfix`/`rwsiteinfo`; 1 between `rwsort` and `rwstats` for `rwsplit`; 2 between `rwstats` and `rwuniq` for `rwswapbytes`/`rwtuc`.
