- **lziprecover (Antonio Diaz Diaz's data-recovery tool for the lzip format)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-116 `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Flags transcribed directly from upstream [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/lziprecover/lziprecover.1](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/lziprecover/lziprecover.1.en.html). **Candidate-hunt dup-skip log this round (6 already-covered)**: `lzip`/`plzip` already at `more_src/_lzip` + `more_src/_plzip` (and `lzip-decompressor` covered as multi-stem via `src/_lzip-decompressor` `#compdef` line — caught by the round-116 grep upgrade), `pax` caught at stock zsh, `gron`/`huniq`/`fselect`/`sponge` (via multi-stem `more_src/_moreutils`) all already covered. `clzip`/`pdlzip` (C-only / public-domain lzip variants) truly fresh but share `lzip`'s flag set verbatim — could be added as multi-stem to `_lzip` in a future cleanup round; not a separate write. Single-file completion `_lziprecover` covers the data-recovery tool for the lzip format — repairs single-byte errors, splits multi-member archives, dumps/strips/removes corrupt members, and reproduces zeroed sectors using a known-good reference copy. ~26 flags grouped with 2 mutex sets: meta `-h/--help`, `-V/--version`. Core ops (each with `(...)` mutex against `-q` exclusion via `-v` selection): `-a/--trailing-error` (exit non-zero on trailing data), `-A/--alone-to-lz` (lzma-alone format conversion), `-c/--stdout`, `-d/--decompress`, `-D/--range-decompress N-M` (byte-range stdout dump), `-l/--list` (sizes), `-s/--split` (multi-member explode), `-t/--test` (integrity), `-f/--force`, `-k/--keep`. Repair-specific operations: `-e/--reproduce` (zeroed-sector reproduction via reference copy) + 3-flag tuning sub-group `--lzip-level N\|a\|m[N]` (single level / all / match length) / `--lzip-name NAME` (lzip executable, completes via `_command_names -e`) / `--reference-file FILE` (known-good `.lz` via glob); `-R/--repair` (single small-byte error repair); `-m/--merge` (correct errors using multiple corrupted copies of the same file). Output routing: `-o/--output FILE`, `-i/--ignore-errors`. Member-list operations (each takes a `[list]:d:t` spec — list of member ranges, `d` for damaged, `t` for trailing-data): `--dump SPEC` (dump to stdout), `--remove SPEC` (in-place removal), `--strip SPEC` (copy minus listed members). Verbosity mutex `-q/--quiet` ↔ repeatable `*-v/--verbose` (per upstream: `--quiet` cancels out `--verbose` and `-v -v` gives more detail than a single `-v`). Tolerance: `--loose-trailing` (allow trailing data that looks like a corrupt header). Repeatable positional `*:lzip file:_files -g "*.(lz|tlz|lzma)"` covers the 3 lzip-family extensions (.lz canonical / .tlz tar-bundled / .lzma legacy). `lziprecover` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 15184 between `lzip.lzip` and `lzless` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 4 `lzip.lzip`'s '.' (46) < `lziprecover`'s 'r' (114), and at index 2 `lziprecover`'s 'i' (105) < `lzless`'s 'l' (108)). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **zoo (Rahul Dhesi's classic .zoo archiver — Lempel-Ziv based, mid-80s era)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-116 `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Command + modifier inventory transcribed from [manpages.org/zoo](https://manpages.org/zoo). **Candidate-hunt dup-skip log this round** (5 already-covered + 1 deferred): `hcxdumptool` already at `src/_hcxdumptool`, `hcxpcapngtool`/`hcxhashtool` covered via multi-stem `more_src/_hcxtools` `#compdef` line, `airbase-ng`/`aireplay-ng` already at `src/`; `unarj` (ARJ extraction-only sibling) truly fresh but every man-page mirror returned 404 (`manpages.debian.org/bookworm/arj/unarj.1` dyn-redirect, `manpages.ubuntu.com/manpages/jammy/man1/unarj.1`, `manpages.org/unarj` no-results) — deferred per the round-106 source-404 precedent. Single-file completion `_zoo` covers the classic .zoo archive format (still used for legacy PD shareware archives) — dual-mode CLI: a 17-letter "expert" single-character command verb or a hyphenated 10-verb "novice" form (`-add`/`-extract`/etc) that mirrors the same operations. 17 expert commands enumerated via `_expert` describe: `a` (add — overwrites if newer), `u` (update — newer-only), `c` (comment), `f` (filter — compress/decompress with M/u/h modifiers), `D` (delete inside archive), `e` (extract basename only), `g` (generate sub-archive — `A`/`q`/`+`/`-`/`=` modifiers), `h`/`H` (short/verbose help), `l`/`L` (short/long list), `P` (pack/squeeze), `T` (fix timestamp), `U` (undelete), `v`/`V` (verbose/full-verbose list), `x` (extract preserving paths). 10 novice commands enumerated via `_novice` describe: `-add`/`-freshen`/`-delete`/`-update`/`-extract`/`-move`/`-print`/`-list`/`-test`/`-comment`. Positional 1 uses `_alternative` to multiplex the two command surfaces — users get both expert and novice candidates in a single tab, with type tags distinguishing them. Positional 2 is the archive file (`_files -g "*.(zoo|ZOO)"` for case-insensitive matching since old DOS-era zoo files often used `.ZOO`). Trailing `*:file inside archive:_files` for the file-list arguments. The ~50 sub-command modifiers (e.g. extract modifiers `N`/`O`/`S`/`o`/`p`/`/`/`d`; list modifiers `a`/`A`/`c`/`C`/`d`/`f`/`g`/`m`/`q`/`v`/`V`/`@`/`/`/`1`/`+`/`-`; etc) are documented in the man page but not modeled here — zoo's modifier syntax concatenates them onto the command letter (e.g. `zoo vC archive` for list with column-aligned output) which doesn't tab-complete cleanly; left for manual entry. `zoo` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 29519 between `zoneminder` and `zookeeper-server` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 2 `zoneminder`'s 'n' (110) < `zoo`'s 'o' (111); within the `zoo*` block, `zoo` (length 3) < `zookeeper-server` (length 16) by shorter-string-wins on shared `zoo` prefix). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **arj (Robert K Jung's ARJ Archiver — open-source maintenance fork)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the new `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep introduced this round). Command + switch inventory transcribed directly from upstream [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/arj/arj.1](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/arj/arj.1.en.html). **Tooling upgrade**: round-115's multi-stem false-positive (`aunpack` etc. flagged FRESH while really covered by `_atool`'s `#compdef` line) triggered an upgraded dup-check helper that now greps `^#compdef.*\<${c}\>` across `src/_*` + `more_src/_*` + `override_src/_*` before declaring a candidate fresh. **Candidate-hunt dup-skip log this round**: `xar` caught at multi-stem (`src/_xar` covers `#compdef xar` already), `lha` caught at stock zsh (`/opt/homebrew/share/zsh/functions/_lha`), `wpscan` already at `man_src/_wpscan`; `hashpump` (Brian Wallace's hash-length-extension attack tool) truly fresh but every man-page mirror returned 404 (`manpages.debian.org` dyn-redirect, `manpages.ubuntu.com/manpages/jammy/man1/hashpump.1`, GitHub repo also 404 since `bwall/HashPump` archived) — deferred per the round-106/108/109/111/113/114 source-404 precedent. Single-file completion `_arj` covers the open-source ARJ archiver — command-letter dispatch (`a`/`x`/`e`/`l`/`t`/etc) with a notorious 100+ switch tail organized in `-h*`/`-j*`/`-2*` prefix blocks. 27 commands enumerated via `_commands` describe: classic archive ops (`a`/`x`/`e`/`l`/`v`/`t`/`p`/`s`), modification (`d`/`f`/`u`/`m`/`n`/`o`/`r`/`g`/`c`/`y`), integrity/recovery (`i`/`q`/`w`), batch (`b`), housekeeping (`k`), join (`j`), plus 3 chapter-mode commands (`ac`/`cc`/`dc`) for chapter-archive workflow. ~25 most-used switches seeded (the upstream 100+ obscure `-hf4`/`-j#3`/`-2x1` long-tail left for power-users to type manually): meta `-help`; basic switches `-c` (skip time-stamp check), repeatable `*-e` (exclude paths), `-f` (freshen existing), repeatable `*-g PASSWORD` (garble password), `-i` (hide progress bar), `-m 0..4` (compression method enum), `-n` (only new files), `-r` (recurse), `-u` (update new + newer), `-v SIZE` (multi-volume), `-w DIR` (work directory), repeatable `*-x PATTERN` (exclude pattern), `-y` (assume yes); shifted `-h*` block — `-hk` (ARJ-PROTECT damage protection), `-hz` (ARJ-SECURITY envelope); shifted `-j*` block — `-jm` (max compression), `-jp` (paged), `-ji` (index file), `-je` (self-extracting), `-jf` (full paths), `-jt` (test temp archive), repeatable `*-jy` (suppress queries everywhere); shifted `-2*` block — `-2d` (DOS compat mode), `-2s` (preserve symlinks — UNIX), repeatable `*-2o` (store/restore ownership — UNIX). Positional 1 is the command letter (via `_commands` describe), positional 2 is the archive file (`_files -g "*.arj"`), and trailing `*: file:_files` for the file-list arguments. `arj` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 1292 between `arize-phoenix` and `arj-register` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 2 `arize-phoenix`'s 'i' (105) < `arj`'s 'j' (106); within the `arj*` block, `arj` (length 3) < `arj-register` (length 12) by shorter-string-wins on shared `arj` prefix). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **vnu (W3C Nu HTML/CSS/SVG validator — Java)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname`). Flags transcribed directly from upstream [validator.github.io/validator/docs/vnu.1.html](https://validator.github.io/validator/docs/vnu.1.html) OPTIONS section. **Candidate-hunt dup-skip log this round (substantial multi-stem catch)**: hit a beautifully misleading 6-stem false-positive — `aunpack`/`apack`/`als`/`acat`/`adiff`/`arepack` all flagged FRESH because `find -iname "_aunpack"` etc. returned nothing, but the existing `more_src/_atool` is multi-stem `#compdef atool aunpack apack als acat adiff arepack` so all 6 are already covered. **Reinforces the round-104 case-insensitivity finding**: a name-based `find` (even with `-iname`) misses multi-stem coverage entirely — must check the `#compdef` line of existing files to detect this class of dup. Also already-covered: `atool`/`dtrx`/`cmark`/`cmark-gfm`/`htmlhint`. Single-file completion `_vnu` covers the Nu HTML Checker (Java-based — invoked as `java -jar vnu.jar` or the `vnu` wrapper script) — validates URLs/files/directories against W3C schemas for HTML5+/CSS/SVG/XHTML. Sister binaries `vnu-server`/`vnu-client` expose the same checker over HTTP and are out of scope for this completion. ~27 flags grouped with 3 mutex sets: meta `--help`, `--version`. Message filtering: `--asciiquotes` (smart-quote substitution in messages); mutex `--skip-info-messages` ↔ `--errors-only`; `--Werror` (any warning → non-zero exit), `--exit-zero-always` (always 0 even with errors), `--stdout` (errors to stdout not stderr); `--filterfile FILE` (regex pattern file) + repeatable `*--filterpattern REGEX` for selective message suppression. Output format: `--format gnu\|xml\|json\|text` (full upstream enum). **Document-type dispatch mutex** (7-flag exclusion group — only one can be active at a time): `--skip-non-css`/`--css` for CSS-only mode, `--skip-non-svg`/`--svg` for SVG-only mode, `--skip-non-html`/`--html`/`--xml` for parser selection on HTML/XHTML files. Cross-type opt-ins (not in the mutex group since they're additive): `--also-check-css` and `--also-check-svg` for checking CSS+SVG alongside HTML. HTTP fetch: `--user-agent UA-STRING`, repeatable `*--additional-request-header "HEADER: VALUE"` for custom request headers when validating remote URLs. Parser behavior: `--no-langdetect` (skip language detection + `html[lang]` check), `--no-stream` (force buffered instead of streaming parse). Diagnostic: `--verbose` (print file names being checked). Synopsis-only flags reproduced with best-guess arg types: repeatable `*--entities` (extra entity defs) and `--schema URL-OR-FILE` (non-default schema override). Repeatable positional `*:URL/file/directory:_files` covers the three input forms vnu accepts. `vnu` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 27544 between `vnstati` and `void` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 1 both share 'n' (110), so resolves at index 2 — `vnstati`'s 's' (115) < `vnu`'s 'u' (117); and at index 1 `vnu`'s 'n' (110) < `void`'s 'o' (111)). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **when (Leon Brocard's tiny personal calendar utility)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname`). Flags + command codes transcribed from upstream [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/when/when.1](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/when/when.1.en.html). **Candidate-hunt dup-skip log this round (substantial — 12 caught + 2 deferred)**: 10 already-covered candidates `tdl`/`icdiff`/`pcre2grep`/`deheader`/`cppman`/`calcurse`/`remind`/`pueue`/`autorestic` plus the round-113-promised `strongswan` — `swanctl` man page sourced cleanly but the **stock zsh** at `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh/functions/_swanctl` already ships a `_swanctl` (and similarly `_ipsec`), so neither can be added; the `strongswan` umbrella binary's own man page returned 404 across `manpages.debian.org` dyn-redirect, `man.archlinux.org/man/strongswan.8.en`, and `wiki.strongswan.org/projects/strongswan/wiki/Strongswanctl` (Anubis access-denied) — deferred per the round-106/108/109/111/113 source-404 precedent. `ts` (task spooler) explored as fresh + in blacklist but skipped because the `ts` binary name collides with the unrelated `moreutils ts` timestamp filter — writing a single `_ts` would mismatch one of the two tools depending on which the user installed; deferred for proper multi-tool detection in a future round. Single-file completion `_when` covers the single-file calendar (default `~/.when/calendar`, one line per event) — long-form-only flags drive rendering and an 8-letter positional command code that can be **combined** (e.g. `when cw` = calendars then weekly items, `when iy` = items then year view). 8 command codes via `_verbs` describe: `i` (default — upcoming items), `c` (last/this/next month calendar grids without items), `e` (open `$EDITOR` on the calendar file), `w`/`m`/`y` (coming week/month/year items), `j` (modified Julian day for date arithmetic), `d` (current date only). ~36 long-form flags grouped with 9 mutex pairs encoded in zsh exclusion sets: meta `--help`/`--version`; window control `--language LANG`, `--future DAYS` (default 14), `--past DAYS` (default -1), `--calendar FILE`, `--editor COMMAND` (default `emacs -nw` — completes via `_command_names -e`); wrap mutex `--wrap COLS` / `--wrap_auto` ↔ `--noowrap_auto` / `--wrap_max COLS`; rows mutex `--rows ROWS` (default 40) / `--rows_auto` ↔ `--norows_auto`; header mutex `--header` ↔ `--noheader`; pager mutex `--paging` ↔ `--nopaging` + `--paging_less_options OPTS`; styling `--filter_accents_on_output`, `--styled_output`, `--styled_output_if_not_tty`, `--calendar_today_style STYLE`, `--items_today_style STYLE`; pre-processing `--prefilter PROGRAM` (`_command_names -e`); date control `--now "Y M D"` (override current date); month rendering mutex `--neighboring_months` ↔ `--noneighboring_months`; week-start mutex `--monday_first` ↔ `--nomonday_first`; Easter `--orthodox_easter`; time format mutex `--ampm` ↔ `--noampm` + `--auto_pm HOUR` (assume PM for hours below X); entry expansion mutex `--literal_only` ↔ `--noliteral_only`. `when` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 27913 between `wheel3.9` and `whence` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 3 `wheel3.9`'s 'e' (101) < `when`'s 'n' (110), and at index 4 `when` ends < `whence`'s 'c' (99) — wait, 'c' is 99 not nothing; correct comparison: `when` length-4 vs `whence` length-6, shared prefix `when`, so `when` < `whence` by shorter-string-wins). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **wg-quick (Jason Donenfeld's WireGuard setup wrapper)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname`). Subcommands transcribed from upstream [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/wireguard-tools/wg-quick.8](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/wireguard-tools/wg-quick.8.en.html). **Candidate-hunt dup-skip log this round** (8 already-covered): `libreswan`, `headscale`, `tailscale`, `tailscaled`, `openconnect`, `openvpn`, `smbtree` — all already at `more_src/`/`src/`. `strongswan` truly fresh + in blacklist line 24732 but defers to next round to do justice (the upstream strongswan CLI has 12+ subcommands and `--child=` connection lookups across `swanctl`/`ipsec` interactions worth source-verifying carefully). Single-file completion `_wg-quick` covers Jason Donenfeld's `wg(8)` setup wrapper — reads `/etc/wireguard/<INTERFACE>.conf` for Address/DNS/MTU/Table/PreUp/PostUp/PreDown/PostDown/SaveConfig directives, then drives `wg` + `ip(8)` accordingly. 4 verbs via `_verbs` describe: `up` (add and set up interface — Address/MTU/routes/PreUp/PostUp), `down` (tear down — PreDown/PostDown), `save` (save current state into config), `strip` (dump config with wg-quick-only directives stripped — pipe-friendly for `wg setconf`). 1 GNU-style flag: `-h/--help`. **Live-interface helper `_wg_quick_interfaces`** walks `/etc/wireguard/*.conf` with zsh nullglob `(N)`, strips the leading directory and trailing `.conf` via `:t:r` modifier (`/etc/wireguard/wg0.conf` → `wg0`), so the user-visible candidate is the bare interface name — matching how `wg-quick up wg0` is invoked in practice. Positional 2 uses `_alternative` to multiplex two surfaces: config-file path completion (`_files -g "*.conf"`) for absolute paths, plus the live-interface describe for the bare-name shortcut. `wg-quick` already in `blacklist.txt` at line 27890; no add needed.
- **djvumake (Yann Le Cun & Léon Bottou's DjVu chunk assembler)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname`). Chunk inventory transcribed from upstream [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/djvulibre-bin/djvumake.1](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/djvulibre-bin/djvumake.1.en.html). **Candidate-hunt dup-skip log this round** (5 already-covered + 4 fresh-but-no-completion-worthy-surface): `djvused`/`djview`/`djvuextract`/`djvups`/`bcal`/`dasel`/`sshs`/`hck` all caught as already covered. `djvumake` is the only fresh DjVu-family binary with a real CLI surface — the others (`djvm`/`djvmcvt`/`djvudigital`/`djvutoxml`) have known but already-covered completions in `man_src/`/`src/`. Single-file completion `_djvumake` covers the DjVu chunk assembler — builds a `.djvu` file from raw chunk fragments via a non-standard `CHUNKID=value` argument syntax (not GNU-style flags). 13 chunk types in the `_chunks` describe array (with key=value suffix-suppression for tab-completion): `INFO=` (initial info chunk W,H,DPI default 300), `Sjbz=` (JB2 foreground mask), `Smmr=` (MMR/G4 mask), `BG44=` (IW44 background — `file[:N]` for multiple chunks), `BGjp=` (JPEG background), `BG2k=` (JPEG-2000 background), `FGbz=` (foreground colour — file or `{#color[:x,y,w,h]}`), `FG44=`/`FGjp=`/`FG2k=` (foreground colour in IW44/JPEG/JP2 respectively), `INCL=` (DjVu3 include chunk for multipage), `Djbz=` (JB2 shape-dictionary), `PPM=` (emit IW44 BG + FG colour chunks from a PPM). 1 GNU-style flag: `-h`. Positional 1 is the output `.djvu` file (via `_files -g "*.djvu"`), repeatable positional 2+ accept chunk specs. The `_describe -S ''` suffix-suppression keeps zsh from auto-inserting a space after the `=` so the user can immediately continue typing the chunk argument. `djvumake` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 6318 between `djvuextract` and `djvups` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 4 `djvuextract`'s 'e' (101) < `djvumake`'s 'm' (109) < `djvups`'s 'p' (112)). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **pngnq (Stuart Coyle's NeuQuant-based PNG palette quantizer)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname`). Flags transcribed directly from [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/pngnq/pngnq.1](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/pngnq/pngnq.1.en.html). **Candidate-hunt dup-skip log this round** (4 caught + 1 deferred): `apngasm` (animated PNG assembler) explored as fresh but uses non-standard Windows-style slash flags (`/kp` keep-palette, `/kc` keep-color, `/f` invisible-frame) plus undocumented numeric frame-delay positionals — encoded as ad-hoc zsh _arguments couldn't model the slash syntax cleanly, deferred; `apngdis` (animated PNG disassembler) confirmed via man page to have **zero options** (just `filename.png [name-prefix]` positionals) — no completion-worthy surface, deferred; `gif2png` truly fresh but every man-page mirror returned cert/404 errors (`catb.org` TLS-cert-altname-invalid, `gitlab.com/esr/gif2png/raw/master/gif2png.c` 404, `manpages.ubuntu.com/manpages/jammy/man1/gif2png.1` 404) — deferred per round-106/108 source-404 precedent; `rsvg-convert`/`svgo`/`pup`/`hq` all caught as already covered. Single-file completion `_pngnq` covers the NeuQuant-based PNG palette reducer — maps full-color PNG → 8-bit palette PNG for smaller file size, optional Floyd-Steinberg dithering, default output suffix `-nq8.png`. 10 flags + repeatable positional: meta `-h`, `-V` (version + library versions — the uppercase `-V` distinguishes from the lowercase `-v` verbosity flag, an unusual convention worth honoring); diagnostic `-v` (verbose status messages); safety `-f` (force overwrite existing output); algorithm tuning `-s FACTOR` (NeuQuant sample factor — default 3, higher = faster + less accurate), `-n COLORS` (target palette size — 2..256, default 256), `-Q n\|f` (dither method enum — `n` = no dither/default, `f` = Floyd-Steinberg), `-g GAMMA` (image gamma override — defaults to reading the source PNG's gAMA chunk, falls back 1.0); output routing `-e SUFFIX` (filename suffix override, default `-nq8.png`), `-d DIR` (output directory override — useful for keeping originals untouched); repeatable positional `*:input PNG (stdin if omitted):_files -g "*.png"`. The `-Q n|f` dither enum is encoded as a 2-value choice list so users can't mistype it; sample-factor, color-count, and gamma are left as bare-numeric prompts (no enum) since the upstream-documented "good default" (3) is just a starting hint. `pngnq` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 19993 between `pngfix` and `pngquant` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 3 `pngfix`'s 'f' (102) < `pngnq`'s 'n' (110) < `pngquant`'s 'q' (113)). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **jpeginfo (Timo Kokkonen's libjpeg-based info dumper)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname`). Flags transcribed directly from upstream [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/jpeginfo/jpeginfo.1](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/jpeginfo/jpeginfo.1.en.html). Single-file completion `_jpeginfo` covers the libjpeg-based info/integrity tool — dimensions, JPEG coding, optional MD5/SHA-256/SHA-512 checksums, and `-d`-delete-on-error workflow. 18 flags + repeatable positional: meta `-h/--help`, `--version`; integrity `-c/--check` (scan beyond headers), `-C/--comments` (dump COM markers), `-d/--delete` (delete files with errors), `-m/--mode all\|erronly` (delete-mode selector, only meaningful with `-d`); input source `-f/--file LIST-FILE` (read filenames from file, `-` = stdin), `--stdin` and bare `-` (read image data from stdin); checksums `-2/--sha256`, `--sha512`, `-5/--md5` (three independent hash flags — can stack); detail `-i/--info` (extended: coding/density/CCIR601), `-H/--header` (column header row), `-v/--verbose`; **output-format mutex group** (4 flags, only one valid at a time) `-l/--lsstyle` ↔ `-j/--json` ↔ `-s/--csv` ↔ `-q/--quiet` — encoded as a single `(... ... ... ...)` exclusion list so zsh suppresses the dominated format flags once the user picks one. Repeatable positional `*:jpeg file:_files -g "*.(jpg|jpeg|jpe|jfif|jif)"` covers the 5 historical JPEG file extensions (`.jpg`/`.jpeg` modern + `.jpe` short DOS + `.jfif`/`.jif` raw JFIF). `jpeginfo` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 12368 between `jpegexiforient` and `jpegoptim` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 4 `jpegexiforient`'s 'e' (101) < `jpeginfo`'s 'i' (105) < `jpegoptim`'s 'o' (111)). LC_ALL=C sort verified.

Single-file completion `_probe-bcache` covers the bcache-tools superblock-probe binary used by udev rules to autostart bcache once both cache and backing devices show up in `/dev/`. 1 flag + repeatable positional: `-o FORMAT` (only `udev` is recognized — emits `ID_FS_UUID`/`ID_FS_UUID_ENC`/`ID_FS_TYPE` for udev consumption; without `-o` defaults to a human-readable UUID/TYPE one-liner); positional `*: block device:_files -g "/dev/*"` (repeatable — multiple devices probed in one invocation). The single-format enum is encoded as `(udev)` value table so users get tab-completion of the only valid spelling rather than mistakenly typing variants like `udev-style` or `ID_FS_UUID`. `probe-bcache` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 20503 between `prm` and `probe-rs` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 2 `prm`'s 'm' (109) < `probe-bcache`'s 'o' (111); within the `probe-*` block, index 6 `probe-bcache`'s 'b' (98) < `probe-rs`'s 'r' (114)). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **binbloom (Quarkslab's raw-firmware base-address + endianness finder)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname`). Flags transcribed directly from upstream [github.com/quarkslab/binbloom](https://github.com/quarkslab/binbloom) README usage table. **Candidate-hunt dup-skip log this round (substantial)**: hit a 7-tool wall of already-covered candidates (toot, bottles, spotdl, beets, gallery-dl, yt-dlp, calibre — all at `more_src/_$tool`) plus 8 stems-already-tracked-as-aliases (rad, erd, erdtree, tre, fossil, pijul, ty, pyrefly), plus `aursync`/`aursearch` were truly fresh but their canonical man-page sources returned 404 across `man.archlinux.org`/`manpages.debian.org`/`raw.githubusercontent.com/aurutils/aurutils/master/man1/aursync.1` (different repo path than the README suggested) — deferred rather than guessed per the round-106 "frida-compile" precedent. Single-file completion `_binbloom` covers Quarkslab's raw-firmware analyzer — auto-detects endianness and the loading base address of an unstructured firmware blob (no ELF/PE headers present), used in embedded-firmware RE before loading into Ghidra/IDA. 7 flags + 1 positional: meta `-h`; arch-detection overrides `-a 32|64` (word-size enum, default 32) and `-e le|be` (force endianness when auto-detect is unreliable); `-b HEX-ADDRESS` (supplied base address, skip auto-detect); performance `-t N` (thread count for parallel address search), `-d` (deep-search mode — slower, finds addresses in rare cases); diagnostic `*-v` (stackable verbosity); positional `1:firmware blob:_files`. All numeric/hex flag positions are uppercase short — encoded as `-a+`/`-b+`/`-e+`/`-t+` (next-word argument) to match the GNU short-arg style binbloom uses. `binbloom` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 2171 between `bin2obj` and `bind9-config` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 3 `bin2obj`'s '2' (50) < `binbloom`'s 'b' (98) < `bind9-config`'s 'd' (100)). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **Joern family — joern + joern-cli + joern-parse + joern-export + joern-scan + joern-slice + joern-flow + joern-vectors + joern-stats (9 binary stems via single multi-stem file)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname`). Flags transcribed directly from upstream Scala scopt argument parsers under [joernio/joern](https://github.com/joernio/joern) `joern-cli/src/main/scala/io/joern/joerncli/` — `JoernParse.scala`, `JoernExport.scala`, `JoernScan.scala`, `JoernSlice.scala`, `JoernFlow.scala`, `JoernVectors.scala`. Single multi-stem file `_joern` is `#compdef joern joern-cli joern-parse joern-export joern-scan joern-slice joern-flow joern-vectors joern-stats`, dispatching on `$service` — covers Whitespace Engineering's static code-analysis platform built around CPGs (Code Property Graphs). **`joern`/`joern-cli`** (interactive Scala REPL launcher): `--help`, `--script FILE` (run script instead of REPL, `.sc`/`.scala` glob), `*--param key=value`, `--import-script FILE`, `--server`/`--server-host HOST`/`--server-port PORT` (JSON-RPC server mode), `--nocolors`. **`joern-parse`** (source → CPG): `-o/--output FILE`, `--language` with 14-frontend enum (`c`/`cpp`/`java`/`jvm`/`javasrc`/`go`/`python`/`php`/`csharp`/`kotlin`/`js`/`javascript`/`ruby`/`swift`/`ghidra`), `--list-languages`, `--namespaces NS,NS`, mutex `--nooverlays` ↔ `--overlaysonly`, `--max-num-def N` (data-flow definition limit), trailing frontend-args after `--`. **`joern-export`** (CPG → graph format): `-o/--out DIR`, `--repr` (7-value: ast/cfg/ddg/cdg/pdg/cpg14/cpg/all, default cpg14), `--format` (4-value: dot/neo4jcsv/graphml/graphson). **`joern-scan`** (run query scans): `--overwrite`, `--store`, `--dump`/`--dump-to FILE`, `--list-query-names`, `--updatedb`/`--dbversion`, `--names NAMES,NAMES` and `--tags TAGS,TAGS` query-set filters, `--depth N` interprocedural call depth, `--language`/`--list-languages` (12-frontend enum). **`joern-slice`** (CPG slicing — state-machine sub-tree with 2 modes): common flags `-o/--out`, `--dummy-types`, `--file-filter`/`--method-name-filter`/`--method-parameter-filter`/`--method-annotation-filter` regex filters, `-p/--parallelism N`; **`data-flow` mode** adds `--slice-depth N` (default 20), `--sink-filter REGEX`, `--end-at-external-method`; **`usages` mode** adds `--min-num-calls N` (default 1), `--exclude-operators`, `--exclude-source`. **`joern-flow`** (data-flow query): 3 positionals `src-regex`/`dst-regex`/`cpg-file`, plus `--src-param N`/`--dst-param N`/`--depth N`/`--verbose`. **`joern-vectors`** (CPG → vectors): `-o/--out DIR`, `--features` flag, positional CPG. **`joern-stats`** (minimal flag set — scopt config not exposed): `--help` + CPG positional. **Blacklist additions (9)**: `joern`, `joern-cli`, `joern-export`, `joern-flow`, `joern-parse`, `joern-scan`, `joern-slice`, `joern-stats`, `joern-vectors` inserted between `joe` and `john` — sort verified position-by-position: at index 2 `joe`'s 'e' (101) = `joern`'s 'e' (101), so `joe` < `joern` by shorter-string-wins (length 3 < 5); within the `joern-*` block, suffix order resolves as `cli` (c=99) < `export` (e=101) < `flow` (f=102) < `parse` (p=112) < `scan` (sc=115,99) < `slice` (sl=115,108) < `stats` (st=115,116) < `vectors` (v=118); finally at index 2 `joern`'s 'e' (101) < `john`'s 'h' (104). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **apkleaks (dwisiswant0's APK secret scanner)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname`). Flags transcribed from upstream [github.com/dwisiswant0/apkleaks](https://github.com/dwisiswant0/apkleaks) README usage table. **Candidate-hunt dup-skip log this round**: `entr` (Eric Radman's fs-watcher — ships in stock zsh at `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh/functions/_entr`) and `capa` (Mandiant capability matcher — already at `more_src/_capa`) both caught at dup-check before reaching write; `frida-compile` truly fresh but upstream `bin/compile.ts` returned 404 across multiple URL forms so flag inventory couldn't be source-verified — deferred rather than guessed. Single-file completion `_apkleaks` covers the jadx-backed APK static scanner that surfaces URIs, endpoints, and embedded credentials via a JSON-defined regex set. 7 flags: meta `-h/--help`, `-v/--version`; input `-f/--file APK` (completes via `_files -g "*.(apk|xapk|aab)"` covering standard APK + Aurora Store split-APK bundles + Android App Bundles); output `-o/--output FILE` (random tmp file if omitted), `--json` (JSON format instead of plain text); customization `-p/--pattern FILE` (custom regex pattern JSON via `_files -g "*.json"`), `-a/--args STRING` (extra args forwarded to jadx — e.g. `--deobf --log-level DEBUG`). `apkleaks` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 1110 between `apkeep` and `apko` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 3 `apkeep`'s 'e' (101) < `apkleaks`'s 'l' (108) < `apko`'s 'o' (111)). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **shellcraft (pwntools' shellcode generator with hierarchical shellcode catalog)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` — used the upgraded `-iname` (case-insensitive) dup-check this round following last round's macOS HFS+/APFS case-insensitivity gotcha). Flags transcribed directly from upstream [docs.pwntools.com/en/stable/commandline.html](https://docs.pwntools.com/en/stable/commandline.html) options table plus the `pwn` global-flag table. Single-file completion `_shellcraft` covers Gallopsled's standalone `shellcraft` binary (also reachable as `pwn shellcraft <name>`); generates pre-built shellcodes from a hierarchical catalog (architecture `.` os `.` payload — e.g. `amd64.linux.sh`, `i386.linux.dupsh`, `arm.linux.execve`, `aarch64.linux.sh`, `mips.linux.sh`, `thumb.linux.sh`) with per-name argument lists, plus encoding/output transformations applied after generation. ~20 flags grouped: meta `-h/--help`, `-?/--show` (per-name doc), `-l/--list` (catalog browse, optional filter), `--syscalls` (list all syscalls for selected arch+os, exit); output `-o/--out FILE`, `-f/--format` with the full 18-value upstream enum table (`r`/`raw`, `s`/`str`/`string`, `c`, `h`/`hex`, `a`/`asm`/`assembly`, `p`, `i`/`hexii`, `e`/`elf`, `d`/`escaped`, `default`); debug `-d/--debug` (auto-launch GDB), `-b/--before` and `-a/--after` (insert debug-trap markers around emitted code), `--delim` (multi-shellcode separator); bad-byte encoding `-v/--avoid HEX-BYTES`, `-n/--newline`, `-z/--zero` (NULL-byte avoid — the three classic shellcode-encoding constraints); execution `-r/--run` (run the generated shellcode in-process for quick smoke-test), `--address HEX` (load-address override); ELF emission `-s/--shared` (build shared-library ELF instead of raw blob); pwn-global overrides `--arch` (11-value enum `i386`/`amd64`/`arm`/`aarch64`/`thumb`/`mips`/`mipsel`/`mips64`/`mips64el`/`powerpc`/`sparc`), `--os` (5-value enum `linux`/`freebsd`/`windows`/`baremetal`/`android`); color mutex `--color` ↔ `--no-color`. **`_names` catalog** seeds the positional argument with 21 of the most common CTF picks (amd64+i386+arm+aarch64+mips+thumb x sh/execve/dupsh/read/write/open/connect/listen/cat) plus common synthetic `common.{nop,infloop,debugbreak}` — users still tab through arbitrary names since the upstream catalog is much larger than this seed. Positional argument 2+ is the shellcode-specific argument list (e.g. `i386.linux.read 3 0x8048000 100` for read(fd, buf, len)) — left as free-form `*:shellcode argument:` since arg semantics depend entirely on which shellcode is selected. `shellcraft` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 23534 between `shellcheck` and `shellharden` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 5 both `shellcheck` and `shellcraft` share 'c' (99), so resolves at index 6 — `shellcheck`'s 'h' (104) < `shellcraft`'s 'r' (114); and `shellcraft`'s index-5 'c' (99) < `shellharden`'s index-5 'h' (104)). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **one_gadget (David Wang's libc "one gadget RCE" finder)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — flags transcribed directly from upstream [github.com/david942j/one_gadget](https://github.com/david942j/one_gadget) README options table. Single-file completion `_one_gadget` covers the Ruby tool that locates the single-instruction `execve("/bin/sh", NULL, NULL)` shortcut inside a libc image — the bread-and-butter primitive for libc-leak exploits in pwn CTF. **Candidate-hunt dup-skip log**: round 104 fresh-check turned up `ROPgadget` (Jonathan Salwan's ROP gadget finder) which was correctly skipped — existing `more_src/_ROPgadget` (canonical filename `_ropgadget` on disk; macOS HFS+/APFS case-insensitive resolves both spellings) already declares `#compdef ROPgadget ropgadget` with comprehensive flag coverage including `--rawArch`/`--rawMode`/`--rawEndian` capstone enums and `--mipsrop` MIPS-specific dispatch. Note for future fresh-checks: case-insensitive macOS filesystems make `find -name` (case-sensitive) lie about what's already on disk — the file walker resolved the file as `_ropgadget` even though the dup-check name was `_ROPgadget`. 12 flags: meta `-h/--help`, `--version`, `--info BuildID` (show version info for a specific libc BuildID without lookup). Input source: `-b/--build-id BuildID` (SHA1 BuildID lookup against bundled db) versus libc-file positional via `_files -g "*libc*"` — repeatable; mutex `-f/--force-file` ↔ `--no-force-file` controls precedence between file-first vs BuildID-first lookup. Output: `-l/--level 0|1|2|3` (verbosity — higher reveals more conditional/marginal gadgets), `-o/--output-format pretty|raw|json` and `-r/--raw` shorthand (both wired into the same mutex group so zsh suppresses the dominated flag); `-n/--near FUNCTIONS-OR-FILE` orders results by proximity to specified ELF functions or GOT entries (completes via `_files`). Exploit-integration: `-s/--script EXPLOIT-SCRIPT` (execute script per-gadget with the offset as `$1` — heavily used in CTF auto-pwn pipelines); `--base HEX-ADDRESS` (libc base override, default 0). `one_gadget` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 18150 between `ondir` and `oneagent` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 2 `ondir`'s 'd' (100) < `one_gadget`/`oneagent` shared 'e' (101), then at index 3 `one_gadget`'s '_' (95) < `oneagent`'s 'a' (97) — underscore precedes lowercase letters in ASCII so `one_gadget` slots ahead of the `onea*` block). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **pwninit (Andrew Otto's CTF binary-exploit challenge initializer)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — flags extracted directly from upstream [github.com/io12/pwninit](https://github.com/io12/pwninit) `src/opts.rs` (structopt definitions that generate clap args). Single-file completion `_pwninit` covers the auto-detection + patchelf + pwntools-template-emission flow used at the start of every pwn CTF challenge. 11 flags: meta `-h/--help`, `-V/--version`. Auto-detect overrides: `--bin FILE` (challenge binary), `--libc FILE` (challenge libc, completes via `_files -g "*libc*"`), `--ld FILE` (linker for libc preload, completes via `_files -g "*ld*"`). Template customization: `--template-path FILE` (custom pwntools solve-script template, completes via `_files -g "*.py"`), `--template-bin-name NAME` (binary-variable name in emitted template, default `exe`), `--template-libc-name NAME` (default `libc`), `--template-ld-name NAME` (default `ld`). Disable switches: `--no-patch-bin` (skip patchelf), `--no-template` (skip solve-script emission). All file-completion specs use targeted globs — `*libc*` matches the typical CTF naming convention (`libc.so.6`, `libc-2.31.so`, `libc6_2.27-3ubuntu1_amd64.deb`-extracted), `*ld*` matches `ld-linux-x86-64.so.2`/`ld-2.31.so`/etc, `*.py` matches the pwntools template. `pwninit` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 20894 between `pwned` and `pwpolicy` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 3 `pwned`'s 'e' (101) < `pwninit`'s 'i' (105), and at index 2 `pwninit`'s 'n' (110) < `pwpolicy`'s 'p' (112) so it remains at the end of the `pwn*` block before `pwp*` begins). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **chartmuseum (Helm chart-repository server — Go binary)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — flags transcribed directly from upstream [chartmuseum.com/docs/](https://chartmuseum.com/docs/) ("Cache, Multitenancy, Storage Configuration" tables) plus chartmuseum/chartmuseum README. The candidate hunt also turned up `curl-impersonate` as fresh — correctly skipped as a drop-in curl replacement whose flag surface is dominated by 100+ overlapping curl flags (the impersonation-profile binaries `curl_chrome116`/`curl_ff117` etc. expose only the profile name, not new flags). Single-file completion `_chartmuseum` covers ~70 flags (every flag has an equivalent UPPERCASE env var — this completion covers the flag side only). Meta `--help`/`--version`/`--config FILE` (yaml/yml/json/toml glob). Core server: `--port`, `--listen-host` (via `_hosts`), `--debug`, `--log-json` (structured logging), `--log-health`, `--log-latency-integer`, `--read-timeout SECONDS`, `--write-timeout SECONDS`. Auth/security: `--basic-auth-user`/`--basic-auth-pass`, `--auth-anonymous-get` (allow anon GET even with auth set), `--bearer-auth` + `--auth-realm URL` + `--auth-service NAME` + `--auth-cert-path *.pem` + `--auth-actions-search-path JMESPath`, TLS trio `--tls-cert`/`--tls-key`/`--tls-ca-cert`, `--cors-allow-origin`. **`--storage BACKEND` with 10-value enum** (`local`/`amazon`/`google`/`microsoft`/`oracle`/`alibaba`/`baidu`/`tencent`/`openstack`/`etcd`) — covers every cloud-provider object store ChartMuseum supports. Per-backend flag families: local (`--storage-local-rootdir DIR`); AWS S3 (`--storage-amazon-bucket`/`-prefix`/`-region` with 17-region enum aws-east/west/eu/asia/etc/`-endpoint`/`-sse AES256\|aws:kms`); GCS (`-bucket`/`-prefix`); Azure (`-microsoft-container`/`-prefix`); Oracle (`-bucket`/`-prefix`/`-region`/`-compartmentid`); Alibaba OSS (`-bucket`/`-prefix`/`-endpoint`/`-sse AES256\|KMS`); Baidu BOS, Tencent COS (each `-bucket`/`-prefix`/`-endpoint`); OpenStack (`-container`/`-prefix`/`-region`/`-auth v1\|auto`/`-cacert`); etcd (`-endpoint`/`-prefix`/`-cafile`/`-certfile`/`-keyfile`); plus `--storage-timestamp-tolerance DURATION`. Chart management: `--chart-url`, `--chart-post-form-field-name`, `--prov-post-form-field-name`, `--max-upload-size` (default 20971520 = 20 MiB), `--max-storage-objects`, `--allow-overwrite`, `--disable-force-overwrite`, `--disable-delete`, `--disable-api` (read-only mode). Indexing/caching: `--gen-index` (emit-and-exit), `--disable-statefiles`, `--index-limit`, `--cache redis` + `--cache-redis-addr host:port`/`-db`/`-password`, `--cache-interval`. Multitenancy/routing: `--depth`/`--depth-dynamic`, `--context-path`. Monitoring: `--enable-metrics` (Prometheus). `chartmuseum` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 3479 between `chartex` and `chartread` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 5 `chartex`'s 'e' (101) < `chartmuseum`'s 'm' (109) < `chartread`'s 'r' (114)). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **kots + kubectl-kots (Replicated kOTS — Kubernetes-Off-The-Shelf, 2 binary stems via single multi-stem file)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — subcommand tree + per-leaf flag detail extracted from upstream [docs.replicated.com/reference/kots-cli-getting-started](https://docs.replicated.com/reference/kots-cli-getting-started) navigation menu plus per-command pages (`kots-cli-install`, `kots-cli-admin-console-index`, `kots-cli-get-index`). Single multi-stem file `_kots` is `#compdef kots kubectl-kots` because Replicated ships kots as both a standalone binary and a kubectl plugin (the `kubectl-kots` convention auto-attaches it under `kubectl kots …`). 20 top-level subcommands: `install`, `admin-console`, `backup`, `docker`, `download`, `enable-ha`, `get`, `identity-service`, `pull`, `remove`, `reset-password`, `reset-tls`, `restore`, `set`, `upload`, `upstream`, `velero`, `version`, `completion`, `help`. 4 shared persistent flags via `_global`: `-h/--help`, `-n/--namespace NS` (Admin Console namespace), `--kubeconfig FILE`, `--context CONTEXT`. **`install`** carries 35 flags (largest single subcommand in the repo): air-gap (`--airgap`, `--airgap-bundle` with `.tar`/`.tgz` glob), labels/annotations (`--additional-annotations`, `--additional-labels`), config (`--app-version-label`, `--config-values *.yaml`, `--license-file *.yaml`, `--local-path` for local-render test, `--name`), networking (`--copy-proxy-env`, `--http-proxy`, `--https-proxy`, `--no-proxy`, `--port` default 8800, `--no-port-forward`), registry (`--disable-image-push`, `--kotsadm-namespace`, `--kotsadm-registry host/ns`, `--registry-username`, `--registry-password`, `--repo helm-uri`), security (`--ensure-rbac`, `--private-ca-configmap`, `--shared-password`, `--strict-security-context`, `--use-minimal-rbac`, `--skip-rbac-check`, `--skip-registry-check`, `--skip-compatibility-check`, `--skip-preflights`), storage (`--with-minio`, `--storage-class`), timeouts (`--preflights-wait-duration`, `--wait-duration`). **`admin-console` state machine** (5 sub-sub: `garbage-collect-images`/`generate-manifests`/`push-images`/`upgrade` plus the implicit port-forward variant), with `push-images` carrying `--kotsadm-registry`+`--registry-username`/`--registry-password`+ images-bundle positional, `generate-manifests` carrying `--rootdir`/`--shared-password`/`--with-minio`. **`get` state machine** (5 leaves: `apps`/`backups`/`config`/`restores`/`versions`), each with `-o/--output json`; `config`/`versions` add `--decrypt` and require an app-slug positional. **`set` state machine** (`config`/`license`) targeting an app-slug positional. **`pull`/`upload`/`download`** triplet for source-bundle round-tripping with `--rootdir`/`--source`/`--dest` and `--airgap-bundle` air-gap variant. **`backup`/`restore`** with `--wait` flags and Velero/`--velero-namespace` integration. **`upstream` state machine** (`upgrade`/`download`) with airgap-bundle + `--deploy` flags. **`velero` state machine** (8 store-config variants: `configure-aws-s3`/`configure-other-s3`/`configure-azure`/`configure-gcs`/`configure-nfs`/`configure-internal` plus `ensure-permissions` and `print-fs-instructions`). **`identity-service` state machine** (`configure`/`enable-shared-password`). **`docker ensure-secret`** with `--dockerhub-username`/`--dockerhub-password`. **Live-resource helper `_kots_apps`** runs `kots get apps 2>/dev/null | tail -n +2 | awk '{print $1}'` for installed app-slug positionals across `remove`/`set config`/`set license`/`download`/`upload`/`upstream upgrade`/`upstream download`/`get config`/`get versions`. **Blacklist additions (2)**: `kots` between `kotlinc-jvm` (13024) and `koyeb` (13025) — sort verified at index 3 `kotlinc-jvm`'s 'l' (108) < `kots`'s 's' (115), and at index 2 `kots`'s 't' (116) < `koyeb`'s 'y' (121); `kubectl-kots` between `kubectl-karmada` (13261) and `kubectl-krew` (13262) — sort verified at index 9 `kubectl-karmada`'s 'a' (97) < `kubectl-kots`'s 'o' (111) < `kubectl-krew`'s 'r' (114). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **kp (kpack-cli — K8s-native OCI image builder using Cloud Native Buildpacks)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — subcommand inventory + per-leaf flag detail extracted from upstream [buildpacks-community/kpack-cli](https://github.com/buildpacks-community/kpack-cli) `docs/kp.md` plus per-subcommand pages (`docs/kp_image.md`, `docs/kp_build.md`, `docs/kp_secret.md`, `docs/kp_config.md`) generated from the live cobra command tree by `go run cmd/docs/main.go`. The repo also turned up a `cr` candidate (Helm chart-releaser) in candidate hunt — correctly skipped because the existing `more_src/_cr` already covers it; `chart-releaser` itself isn't shipped as a separate binary by upstream. Single-file completion `_kp` covers VMware Tanzu's kpack CLI — a K8s-native image builder driven by Cloud Native Buildpacks specs. 14 top-level subcommands via `_subcmds`: `build`, `builder`, `buildpack`, `clusterbuilder`, `clusterbuildpack`, `clusterlifecycle`, `clusterstack`, `clusterstore`, `completion`, `config`, `image`, `import`, `secret`, `version`, `help`. 6 shared persistent flags via `_global` re-applied per leaf: `-h/--help`, `--kubeconfig FILE`, `--context CONTEXT`, `-n/--namespace NS`, `--registry-ca-cert-path FILE`, `--registry-verify-certs`. **Shared 6-action CRD pattern** `_crd_actions` (create/delete/list/patch/save/status) reused across builder/buildpack/clusterbuilder/clusterbuildpack/clusterlifecycle/clusterstack/clusterstore — single shared array means a future kpack API addition (e.g. a new clusterlifecycle action) cascades to all CRD-style subtrees. **Per-subtree specialization**: `image` adds a 7th action `trigger` (force a new build), with `create`/`save` carrying 16 flags (source-trio mutex via `--git`/`--blob`/`--local-path` plus `--git-revision`, `--registry`, `--cluster-builder`/`--builder`, `--service-account`, `--sub-path`, repeatable `*--env key=value` + `*--delete-env`, repeatable `*--cache-size`, `--additional-tag`, `--wait`, `--dry-run`, `--output yaml|json`); `build` collapses to 3 actions (list/logs/status) and `logs` adds `--build N`, `-f/--follow`, `--timestamps`; `secret create` carries auth mixins for DockerHub (`--dockerhub user`), GCR (`--gcr file`), generic registry (`--registry url`/`--registry-user user`), git SSH (`--git-ssh-key file`) + git HTTPS (`--git-user user`), with `--service-account` attachment; `config` is a 2-key K/V tree (`default-repository` and `default-service-account`); `import` covers `-f/--filename FILE`, `--show-changes` (dry-run-style preview), `--force` (overwrite); `completion` takes bash/fish/powershell/zsh. **Live-resource helper `_kp_resources`** runs `kubectl get <CRD-fully-qualified-kind> -o name` per CRD family — `images.kpack.io`, `builders.kpack.io`, `clusterbuilders.kpack.io`, `clusterbuildpacks.kpack.io`, `clusterlifecycles.kpack.io`, `clusterstacks.kpack.io`, `clusterstores.kpack.io`, `buildpacks.kpack.io` — and strips the `<kind>/` prefix so resource-name positionals auto-complete from the live cluster. **Blacklist additions (2)**: `kp` and `kpack` inserted between `koyeb` and `kpackagetool5` (sort verified: at index 1 'o' < 'p', then shorter-string-wins gives `kp` (length 2) < `kpack` (length 5) < `kpackagetool5` (length 13)). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **Two TUI/GTK file managers in one round — clex (Vlado Potisk's ncurses FM) + spacefm (Ignorantguru's PCManFM fork)** (both dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — **clex** flags transcribed from [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/clex/clex.1](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/clex/clex.1.en.html); **spacefm** flags from [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/spacefm/spacefm.1](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/spacefm/spacefm.1.en.html). **`_clex`** (3 flags, behaviour entirely keymap-driven inside the TUI): `--help` and `--version` (both marked `(- *)` mutex so zsh suppresses further suggestions after either is typed), `--log=LOGFILE` (append session log; completes via `_files`). **`_spacefm`** (19 flags + repeatable folder positional): tab/window-target mutex group `-t/--new-tab` ↔ `-r/--reuse-tab` ↔ `-w/--new-window` (default `-t`), `-n/--no-saved-tabs`, `-p/--panel N` (N=1..4 enum) for SpaceFM's multi-panel feature; desktop manager `--no-desktop` (compat no-op), `--desktop` (launch daemon), `--desktop-pref [N]` (preferences dialog, optional tab number); daemon + config `-d/--daemon-mode`, `-c/--config-dir DIR` (via `_files -/`); features `-f/--find-files` (search dialog), `--set-wallpaper FILE` (completes via image glob `_files -g "*.(jpg|jpeg|png|gif|bmp|svg|webp|tif|tiff)"`); IPC `-g/--dialog` (custom dialog spec, see help), `-s/--socket-cmd` (send socket command); compat `--profile PROFILE` (no-op); X11 `--display`; meta `-h/--help`, `--version`. The man-page has overlapping `-p` listings for `--panel` and `--profile` — encoded `-p` as the active `--panel` meaning since profile is documented as compat-only. Trailing repeatable positional `*:folder:_files -/` for paths to open. **Blacklist additions (2)**: `clex` inserted at line 3922 between `clevis-luks-unlock` and `cli` (sort verified: at index 3 `clevis`'s 'v' (118) < `clex`'s 'x' (120), and at index 2 `clex`'s 'e' (101) < `cli`'s 'i' (105)); `spacefm` inserted at line 24110 between `spacectl` and `spacelift` (sort verified: at index 5 `spacectl`'s 'c' (99) < `spacefm`'s 'f' (102) < `spacelift`'s 'l' (108)). LC_ALL=C sort verified. **Corpus crossed 28,300** milestone this round (28,299 → 28,301 in a single commit). A round-99 fresh-check turn-up of `clojure` was correctly skipped because the existing `src/_clj` already declares `#compdef clj clojure` — multi-stem coverage is invisible to a name-based grep for `_clojure`.
- **ptw (pytest-watch — fs-watch-and-rerun harness for pytest)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — flags transcribed directly from upstream [github.com/joeyespo/pytest-watch](https://github.com/joeyespo/pytest-watch) README options table. Single-file completion `_ptw` covers Joe Esposito's pytest-watch — watches the filesystem and re-runs pytest on change. 19 flags total; unknown extra args pass through to pytest. Meta `-h/--help`, `-V/--version`. Watcher filtering: repeatable `*--ignore DIR` (file-set walker excludes), `--ext exts` (comma-separated `.py`/`*.pyx`/etc that trigger re-runs — default `.py`, `*` allows any), `--config FILE` (override implicit config-file lookup). Trigger control: `-c/--clear` (clear screen before each run), `-n/--nobeep`, `-w/--wait` (let a run finish before re-triggering — opposed to default-interrupt-on-fs-event), `--spool ms` (re-run delay default 200ms — lets fs events queue up), `-p/--poll` (polling instead of inotify-style events — useful in VMs/Docker bind mounts). Hook commands (each completes via `_command_names -e`): `--beforerun` (pre-run), `--afterrun` (post-run; gets pytest exit code as `$1`), `--onpass`, `--onfail`, `--onexit`, `--runner` (override the default `pytest` command). Debugger: `--pdb` (start pdb on errors, also implies `--wait` to prevent pdb interruption). Verbosity mutex `-q/--quiet` ↔ `-v/--verbose` (with `--quiet` taking precedence per upstream — encoded as a single mutex group so zsh suppresses the dominated flag once the user has selected). Trailing `*:pytest path or pytest arg:_files` passes through to pytest's own argument parser. `ptw` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 20750 between `ptunnel` and `ptweens` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 2 `ptunnel`'s 'u' (117) < `ptw`'s 'w' (119), and at index 3 `ptw` ends while `ptweens`'s 'e' (101) is present — shorter-string-wins gives `ptw` < `ptweens`). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **hiawatha (Hugo Leisink's secure-by-default web server)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — flags extracted directly from upstream `hiawatha.c` `main()` argument parser on [gitlab.com/hsleisink/hiawatha](https://gitlab.com/hsleisink/hiawatha/-/raw/master/src/hiawatha.c) (no kingpin/getopt — hand-rolled C argv scanner). Single-file completion `_hiawatha` covers the secure-by-default forking web server. Defaults: daemonize + read from compiled `$CONFIG_DIR` (usually `/etc/hiawatha`). 7 flags total — almost all server behavior is config-driven (see virtual-host + reverse-proxy directives in `hiawatha.conf`), so the CLI surface is intentionally minimal: meta `-h` (help), `-v` (version + copyright), `-m` (list enabled modules and exit); validation/inspection `-k` (config-validate-and-exit — does not start the server), `-s` (sandbox info, only built when compiled with `ENABLE_SANDBOX`); config dir `-c DIR` (overrides compiled `$CONFIG_DIR`, completes via `_files -/`); foreground `-d` (do not daemonize — useful for systemd unit `Type=simple` or container PID 1). All quit-after-printing flags (`-h`/`-v`/`-m`/`-s`/`-k`) marked with `(- *)` mutex so zsh suppresses follow-up flag suggestions. `hiawatha` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 10861 between `hh` and `hid2hci` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 1 `hh`'s 'h' (104) < `hiawatha`'s 'i' (105), and at index 2 `hiawatha`'s 'a' (97) < `hid2hci`'s 'd' (100) — so `hiawatha` is the lexicographically-first `hi*` entry, preceded only by the `hh*` block). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **byzanz family — byzanz-record + byzanz-playback (2 binary stems via single multi-stem file)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — flags transcribed directly from [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/byzanz/byzanz-record.1](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/byzanz/byzanz-record.1.en.html) + sibling `byzanz-playback.1`. Single multi-stem file `_byzanz-record` is `#compdef byzanz-record byzanz-playback`, dispatching on `$service` — covers Benjamin Otte's GNOME-based X11 screencast recorder + the debug-recording-to-final-format converter. **byzanz-record** (14 flags + 1 positional): GTK help trio `-?/--help`, `--help-all`, `--help-gtk`; capture pipeline `-a/--audio` (only honored when output format supports audio), `-c/--cursor` (capture mouse pointer); timing `-d/--duration SECONDS` (default 10), `--delay SECONDS` (default 1); subprocess `-e/--exec COMMAND` (record until command exits — completes via `_command_names -e`); X11 `--display DISPLAY`; recording rectangle `-x/--x`, `-y/--y`, `-w/--width`, `-h/--height` (all pixels); output `-v/--verbose`; positional output file via `_files -g "*.(gif|byzanz|flv|ogv|ogg|webm)"` (format inferred by extension — `.gif` for the canonical animated-GIF case, `.byzanz` for the debug-recording format consumed by `byzanz-playback`, `.flv`/`.ogv`/`.ogg`/`.webm` for muxed audio+video). **byzanz-playback** (1 flag + 2 positionals): minimal — `-h/--help`, positional 1 byzanz debug recording (`_files -g "*.byzanz"`), positional 2 output file (format inferred — `_files -g "*.(gif|flv|ogv|ogg|webm)"`). Multi-stem keeps both binaries in one file because they share the same package and the playback side has too few flags to justify its own file. Blacklist additions (2): `byzanz-playback`, `byzanz-record` inserted between `bytebase` and `byzanz-window` (sort verified: at index 2 't' < 'z', then suffix order `playback < record < window` by index-7 'p' < 'r' < 'w'). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **ssh-vault (Nicolas Bigaouette's SSH-key asymmetric encryption tool)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — flags transcribed directly from upstream [github.com/ssh-vault/ssh-vault](https://github.com/ssh-vault/ssh-vault) `cmd/ssh-vault/main.go` (Go std `flag` package — no kingpin/cobra here). Verified `ssh-vault.com` for subcommand naming. Single-file completion `_ssh-vault` covers the small-secret asymmetric-encryption flow: encrypt to a SSH public key (file path or live GitHub-user lookup) and decrypt with the corresponding private key. 5 flags + 2 positionals: `-h` (help), `-v` (version), `-f` (boolean — create using key matching fingerprint, OR print fingerprint when fingerprint subcommand selected), `-k KEY-OR-INDEX` (public ssh key file path or numerical index when `-u` returns multiple keys; default `~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub`; completes via `_files -g "*.pub"`), `-o FILE` (output file — only honored by `view` subcommand), `-u USER` (GitHub username or URL — fetches all that user's published pubkeys). 8 subcommand aliases via `_ops` describe (4 long + 4 single-letter short): `create`/`c`, `edit`/`e`, `view`/`v`, `fingerprint`/`f`. Positional 1 = operation (via `_ops`), positional 2 = vault file (`_files -g "*.(vault|sshv)"` for the common vault extensions). `ssh-vault` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 24388 between `ssh-mitm` and `ssh3` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 3 '-' = '-', then index 4 'm' (109) < 'v' (118), and ssh-vault's index-3 '-' (45) < ssh3's index-3 '3' (51) preserves the `ssh-*` block before the `ssh3`-onward block). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **in-toto family completion — verify + sign + mock + match-products (4 binary stems via single multi-stem file)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — flag inventory extracted directly from upstream [in-toto/in-toto](https://github.com/in-toto/in-toto) Python argparse sources: `in_toto/in_toto_verify.py`, `in_toto/in_toto_sign.py`, `in_toto/in_toto_mock.py`, `in_toto/in_toto_match_products.py`, plus `in_toto/common_args.py` (shared `VERBOSE_ARGS`/`QUIET_ARGS`/`GPG_HOME_ARGS`/`DSSE_ARGS`/`LSTRIP_PATHS_ARGS`) and `pyproject.toml` `[project.scripts]` entry-points table (which confirmed `in-toto-keygen` is no longer an upstream binary — Python source removed from develop branch — so the candidate-hunt fresh hit for `in-toto-keygen` was correctly skipped). Single multi-stem file `_in-toto-verify` is `#compdef in-toto-verify in-toto-sign in-toto-mock in-toto-match-products`, dispatching on `$service`. Complements the pre-existing `_in-toto-run` and `_in-toto-record` files (the other 2 upstream entry-points). Shared `_shared` array (4 flags re-applied in every stem branch): `--version`, mutex `-v/--verbose` ↔ `-q/--quiet`, `-h/--help`. Per-stem flag coverage: **in-toto-verify** (`-l/--layout LAYOUT` root layout, repeatable `*--verification-keys FILE` for rsa/ed25519/ecdsa key formats, repeatable `*-g/--gpg KEYID` for GPG keyring lookups, `--gpg-home DIR`, `--link-dir DIR` for step link metadata, default `.`, `--inspection-timeout SECONDS` max subprocess timeout); **in-toto-sign** (`-f/--file FILE` link/layout to operate on, repeatable `*-k/--key FILE` for PEM/PKCS8, `-p/--prompt` for decryption-password TTY prompt, repeatable `*-g/--gpg KEYID`, `--gpg-home DIR`, `-o/--output FILE`, `-a/--append` to add signatures instead of replacing, `--verify` mode-switch to verify-using-key-or-gpg instead of sign); **in-toto-mock** (`-n/--name NAME` for `<name>.link` output filename, `--use-dsse` experimental DSSE metadata generation, trailing `*::command and args:_command_names -e` for the wrapped subprocess); **in-toto-match-products** (`-l/--link FILE` link metadata to match against, repeatable `*-p/--paths PATH` local artifact paths defaulting to cwd, repeatable `*-e/--exclude PATTERN` gitignore-style filters, repeatable `*--lstrip-paths PREFIX` prefix-strip controls). Blacklist additions (4): `in-toto-match-products`, `in-toto-mock`, `in-toto-sign`, `in-toto-verify` inserted between `in-cli` and `in.tftpd` to preserve sort (LC_ALL=C verified position-by-position: at index 2 '-' < '.', then suffix order match < mock < record < run < sign < verify). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **witness (in-toto / TestifySec supply-chain attestation collector)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — subcommand + flag inventory extracted directly from upstream [in-toto/witness](https://github.com/in-toto/witness) Cobra command tree: `cmd/root.go` (subcommand registry + `ro.AddFlags`), `options/root.go` (4 persistent flags), `options/run.go` (15 flags), `options/sign.go` (4 + signer/KMS-provider mixins), `cmd/verify.go` (17 verifier flags). Single-file completion `_witness` covers the modern Cobra CLI used in CI/CD pipelines to attest builds (SLSA Provenance v1, sigstore artifact metadata, SBOM/VEX/SARIF, GitHub/GitLab/Jenkins runtime claims, AWS IID + GCP IIT, lockfile + system-packages snapshots) with sigstore/fulcio signing, RFC 3161 timestamping, and Archivista storage. 8 top-level subcommands via `_subcmds`: `run` (root operation — exec subprocess + collect attestations + sign envelope), `sign` (sign arbitrary policy/data file), `verify` (verify a witness-policy-signed artifact), `attestors` (list/info/schema/subjects/run-type sub-tree), `policy` (generate/validate/sign/verify/check sub-tree), `completion` (bash/fish/powershell/zsh), `version`, `help`. 5 persistent root flags via `_global`: `-c/--config FILE` (witness config yaml/json), `-l/--log-level debug|info|warn|error`, `--debug-cpu-profile-file FILE`, `--debug-mem-profile-file FILE`, `-h/--help`. **Shared signer-flag array `_signer`** reused across `sign` and `run` (13 flags): key+cert (`--signer-key-path`, `--signer-cert-path`, `--signer-intermediate-paths`), Fulcio (`--signer-fulcio-url`, `--signer-fulcio-token`, `--signer-fulcio-token-issuer-url`, `--signer-fulcio-token-client-id`, `--signer-fulcio-token-grant-flow device-code|authcode`), KMS (`--signer-kms-ref awskms:/...|gcpkms:/...`, `--signer-kms-region`, `--signer-kms-hash-type SHA256|SHA384|SHA512`), TUF (`--signer-tuf-mirror`, `--signer-tuf-root`). **Attestor catalog `_attestors`** (21 attestor names + one-line descriptions, sourced from upstream `attestation/` package): `aws-iid`, `commandrun`, `environment`, `gcp-iit`, `git`, `github`, `gitlab`, `jenkins`, `jwt`, `lockfile`, `material` (auto), `maven`, `oci`, `product` (auto), `sarif`, `sbom`, `secretscan`, `slsa`, `sigstore`, `system-packages`, `vex`. **Per-subcommand handling**: `run` covers `-d/--workingdir DIR`, repeatable `*-a/--attestations attestor` (using `_attestors` describe), repeatable `*--dirhash-glob`, repeatable `*--hashes sha256|sha384|sha512|sha1|md5`, `-o/--outfile`, `-s/--step`, `-r/--trace`, repeatable `*-t/--timestamp-servers URL`, env-sensitive controls (`--env-filter-sensitive-vars` for filter-vs-obfuscate, `--env-disable-default-sensitive-vars`, repeatable `*--env-add-sensitive-key key|glob`, repeatable `*--env-allow-sensitive-key key` no-globs), Archivista trio (`--enable-archivista`, `--archivista-server URL` default `https://archivista.testifysec.io`, repeatable `*--archivista-headers`), and trailing `*::command and args:_command_names -e` so the wrapped subprocess autocompletes against `$PATH`. `sign` covers `-t/--datatype URI` (default `https://witness.testifysec.com/policy/v0.1`), `-o/--outfile`, `-f/--infile`, repeatable `*--timestamp-servers`. `verify` covers `--policy-ca` (deprecated) + `*--policy-ca-root`/`*--policy-ca-intermediate`, `-p/--policy FILE`, `-k/--publickey FILE`, repeatable `*-a/--attestations FILE`, `-f/--artifactfile FILE`, `-d/--artifactdirectory DIR`, repeatable `*--subjects DIGEST`, repeatable `*--policy-timestamp-server`, cert constraint group (`--policy-common-name CN`, repeatable `*--policy-dns-name`, `*--policy-email`, `*--policy-organization`, `*--policy-uri`, `*--policy-fulcio-cert-extension OID=value`), Archivista pair. `attestors` state machine: `list`/`info`/`schema`/`subjects`/`run-type` with attestor positional from `_attestors`. `policy` state machine: `generate`/`validate`/`sign`/`verify`/`check` with `-o/--outfile`/`-f/--infile`. `completion` takes `bash`/`fish`/`powershell`/`zsh`. `witness` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 27989 between `withsctp` and `witnessme` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 3 'h' < 'n' and at index 7 end-of-string < 'm').
- **vnstat (Teemu Toivola's network statistics monitor)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — flags transcribed directly from [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/vnstat/vnstat.1](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/vnstat/vnstat.1.en.html). Single-file completion `_vnstat` for the per-interface byte/packet counter that persists to a local sqlite-style db. 36 flags grouped: meta `-?/--help`, `--longhelp` (full options list), `-v/--version`, `-D/--debug`; selection `-i/--iface IFACE` (with live `_net_interfaces`), `-q/--query` (force query mode), `-s/--short`; time-resolution stats `-5/--fiveminutes [N]` (5-min stats for last N hours), `-h/--hours [N]`, `-hg/--hoursgraph` (24-hour bar graph), `-d/--days [N]`, `-m/--months [N]`, `-y/--years [N]`, `-t/--top [N]` (all-time top traffic days); range filtering `-b/--begin DATE`, `-e/--end DATE`; live mode `-l/--live [mode 0\|1\|2]`; on-demand calc `-tr/--traffic [SECONDS]` (measure traffic over next N sec); units `-ru/--rateunit [mode 0\|1]` (swap configured rate unit); db management `--add`, `--remove`, `--rename NAME`, `--setalias ALIAS`; listing `--dbiflist [mode 0\|1]`, `--iflist [mode 0\|1]`; config `--config FILE`, `--dbdir DIR`, `--showconfig`, `--style 0..5`, `--locale LOC` (e.g. C, en_US.UTF-8), `--limit N` (max list entries); machine-readable `--oneline [mode b]`, `--json [mode a\|d\|f\|h\|m\|s\|t\|y]`, `--xml [mode a\|d\|f\|h\|m\|s\|t\|y]`; alerts `--alert spec` (output exit type condition limit unit — free-form). 2-letter short flag handling (`-hg`, `-ru`, `-tr`) encoded as separate `_arguments` entries with explicit mutex parentheses against their long equivalents so zsh's option parser handles both the fused (`-hg`) and split (`--hoursgraph`) forms correctly. `vnstat` already in blacklist line 27507 (sibling `vnstatd` daemon at 27508 and `vnstati` image variant at 27509 are separate binaries with their own flag sets, not covered by this completion); no add needed.
- **cppcheck (Daniel Marjamäki's C/C++ static analyzer)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — flags transcribed directly from [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/cppcheck/cppcheck.1](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/cppcheck/cppcheck.1.en.html) plus the cppcheck-2.10 reference. Single-file completion `_cppcheck` covers the largest open-source C/C++ static analyzer. ~50 flags grouped: meta `-h/--help`, `--version`, `--errorlist` (dump every possible diagnostic id as XML); analysis modes `--check-config` (config-only run), `--check-library` (incomplete-library reports), `--check-level reduced|normal|exhaustive`, `--inconclusive`; **repeatable** `*--enable=` / `*--disable=` over check groups `all`/`warning`/`style`/`performance`/`portability`/`information`/`unusedFunction`/`missingInclude`; preprocessor repeatable `*-D macro` / `*-U macro`; language `--std c89|c99|c11|c17|c23|c++03|c++11|c++14|c++17|c++20|c++23`, `--language c|c++`, `--platform unix32|unix64|win32A|win32W|win64|native|unspecified`; includes repeatable `*-I dir`, `--includes-file FILE`, repeatable `*--include FILE`, repeatable `*-i path`; libraries `*--library` with full upstream value table (`std`/`posix`/`gnu`/`microsoft_sal`/`microsoft_atl`/`microsoft_unittest`/`boost`/`qt`/`python`/`sdl`/`gtk`/`wxwidgets`/`opengl`/`openssl`/`windows`/`zlib`/`bsd`/`libcerror`/`libcurl`/`libstdcpp`/`libsigc++`/`ruby`/`tinyxml2`/`emscripten`/`googletest`/`catch2`); output `--template` (simple/gcc/vs/edit/daca2/cppcheck1), `--template-location`, `--xml`, `--xml-version 2`, `--output-file FILE`, `-q/--quiet`, `-v/--verbose`, `--report-progress`, `--error-exitcode N`; suppressions repeatable `*--suppress id:file:line`, `--suppressions-list FILE`, `--suppress-xml FILE`, `--inline-suppr`, `--exitcode-suppressions FILE`; parallelism `-j N`, `-l LOAD`, `--max-configs N` (default 12), `--max-ctu-depth N` (default 2), `--max-template-recursion N`; input `--file-list FILE`, `--force`; config exclusion `--config-exclude PATH`, `--config-excludes-file FILE`; rules repeatable `*--rule REGEX`, repeatable `*--rule-file XMLFILE`; addons repeatable `*--addon misra|threadsafety|y2038|cert|naming`, repeatable `*--addon-python` (interpreter completed via `_command_names -e`); Clang interop `--plist-output DIR`, `--cppcheck-build-dir DIR` (whole-program cache); project `*--project FILE` (Visual Studio `.sln`/`.vcxproj` or CMake `compile_commands.json` via `_files -g "*.(sln|vcxproj|json|cppcheck)"`), `--project-configuration NAME`; debug `--debug-warnings`, `--debug-simplified`, `--debug-symdb`, `--showtime file|file-total|summary|top5|top5_file|top5_summary`; analysis modes `--bug-hunting` (premium), `--safety`; positional `*:source file or directory:_files`. `cppcheck` already in blacklist line 4582 (between `cpp-9` and `cppcheck-htmlreport` neighborhood); This round hit two stock-zsh dup hits — `_debchange` and `_dpkg_source` both ship in `/usr/share/zsh/5.9/functions/` and `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh/functions/` with `#compdef debchange dch=debchange` and `#compdef dpkg-source` respectively — so candidates `dch`/`debchange`/`dpkg-source` were correctly rejected as dups before reaching write. Single-file `_vainfo` covers libva's driver enumeration tool that prints supported entrypoints/profiles/rendering attributes. 4 flags: `-h/--help`, `-a/--all` (extended attribute dump), `--display BACKEND` (live-enumerated against `g_display_hooks_available[]` static array — `x11`/`wayland`/`drm`/`win32` plus magic `help` value that prints the available-display list), `--device DEV` (only meaningful for `drm` and `win32` backends — completes via `_files -g "/dev/dri/*"` for the common DRM render-node case `/dev/dri/renderD128`). `vainfo` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 27053 between `vagrant-box-search` and `val` (LC_ALL=C sort verified position-by-position: at index 2 `g` < `i` < `l`). - **flutter (Google's cross-platform UI toolkit CLI)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — subcommand tree + per-command flag inventory extracted from upstream [docs.flutter.dev/reference/flutter-cli](https://docs.flutter.dev/reference/flutter-cli) (top-level reference + per-command pages). Single-file completion `_flutter` covers the largest mobile-dev CLI in the repo — 30 top-level subcommands, full build-target dispatch, pub sub-dispatch, custom-devices sub-dispatch, and live device discovery. 30 top-level subcommands: `analyze`, `assemble`, `attach`, `bash-completion`, `build`, `channel`, `clean`, `config`, `create`, `custom-devices`, `daemon`, `debug-adapter`, `devices`, `doctor`, `downgrade`, `drive`, `emulators`, `gen-l10n`, `generate`, `ide-config`, `install`, `logs`, `packages` (deprecated), `precache`, `pub`, `run`, `screenshot`, `symbolize`, `test`, `upgrade`. 14 shared global flags in `_global` array re-applied at every state machine layer: `-v/--verbose`, `--version`, `-h/--help`, `-d/--device-id DEV` (with `_flutter_devices` helper), `--suppress-analytics`, `--disable-analytics`/`--enable-analytics`, `--machine`, `--color`/`--no-color` (mutex pair), `--packages FILE`, `--device-timeout SEC`, `--version-check`/`--no-version-check` (mutex pair). `build` state machine: 12 build targets (`apk`/`appbundle`/`aar`/`ios`/`ios-framework`/`ipa`/`macos`/`macos-framework`/`linux`/`windows`/`web`/`bundle`) → `_build_common` (19 flags: mutex `--release`/`--debug`/`--profile`, `-t/--target FILE`, `--target-platform` android-arm/arm64/x64, `--build-name`, `--build-number`, `--analyze-size`, `--tree-shake-icons`, repeatable `*--dart-define key=val`, repeatable `*--dart-define-from-file JSON`, `--obfuscate`, `--split-debug-info DIR`, `--source-maps`, `--no-pub`, `--web-renderer` auto/html/canvaskit/skwasm, `--csp`, `--flavor`). `run` covers same mode mutex + `--hot`/`--no-hot`, `--enable-software-rendering`, `--trace-skia`, `--start-paused`, `--use-application-binary`, `--web-port`, `--web-hostname` (via `_hosts`). `test` covers `--coverage`/`--coverage-path`, repeatable `*--name PATTERN`, `*--plain-name SUBSTR`, `*--tags`/`*--exclude-tags`, `--update-goldens`, `--reporter` (compact/expanded/json/silent/github), `--concurrency`, `--start-paused`. `create` covers `--org`, `-t/--template` (app/module/package/plugin/plugin_ffi), `--project-name`, `--description`, `--ios-language` objc/swift, `--android-language` java/kotlin, repeatable `*--platforms` (android/ios/linux/macos/web/windows), `--overwrite`, `--sample`, `--empty`. `pub` state machine: 15 pub subcommands (`get`/`add`/`remove`/`upgrade`/`downgrade`/`outdated`/`deps`/`cache`/`publish`/`run`/`global`/`token`/`login`/`logout`/`pub`) — `add` accepts `--dev` + package, `remove` accepts repeatable packages, `run` takes executable + script args, `global` takes action (activate/deactivate/list/run), `cache` takes action (add/clean/repair/preload/list), `deps` takes `--style` compact/tree/list. `custom-devices` state machine: list/add/delete/reset — `add` accepts `--json FILE` + `--check`, `delete` takes device-id. `emulators` covers `--launch ID`, `--cold`, `--create`, `--name`. `doctor` covers `--android-licenses`, `--check-for-remote-artifacts`. `channel` takes `stable`/`beta`/`dev`/`master`/`main`. `analyze` covers `--write FILE`, `--congratulate`/`--no-congratulate`, `--preamble`/`--no-preamble`. `screenshot` covers `-o/--out`, `--type` device/rasterizer/skia, `--vm-service-url`. `symbolize` covers `-i/--input`, `-o/--output`, `-d/--debug-info`. `gen-l10n` covers `--arb-dir`, `--template-arb-file`, `--output-localization-file`, `--output-class`, `--header-file`, `--use-deferred-loading`. `precache` covers per-platform flags (`--all-platforms`/`--android`/`--ios`/`--web`/`--linux`/`--windows`/`--macos`/`--fuchsia`/`--universal`/`--force`). `upgrade`/`downgrade` cover `-f/--force` + `--continue`. `config` covers 14 persistent-setting flags including `--build-dir`, `--enable-{web,android,ios,linux-desktop,macos-desktop,windows-desktop}`, `--clear-features`, `--android-sdk`, `--android-studio-dir`, `--jdk-dir`. Live device discovery `_flutter_devices` runs `flutter devices --machine 2>/dev/null` and extracts JSON `"id":"..."` fields via `grep -oE` + `sed` for portable parsing without jq dependency. `flutter` already in blacklist line 8220; no add needed.
- **tmuxinator + mux (Ruby YAML-driven tmux session manager, 2 binary stems)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` — verified on this host that no shipped `_tmuxinator` is present, even though upstream documents installing one at `/usr/local/share/zsh/site-functions/_tmuxinator` from the gem) — subcommand inventory and config-file priority list extracted from the upstream [tmuxinator/tmuxinator README](https://github.com/tmuxinator/tmuxinator). Single-file completion `_tmuxinator` is multi-stem `#compdef tmuxinator mux` (the `mux` binary is upstream's short alias, same Thor app behind both names; `mux` was already at blacklist line 16805 with no file). 14 verbs covered with all aliases enumerated as separate `_describe` entries: `new`/`n`, `open`/`o`, `edit`/`e`, `start`/`s`, `copy`/`c`/`cp`, `list`/`l`/`ls`, `delete`/`rm`, `stop`, `stop-all`, `implode`/`i`, `doctor`, `debug`, `help`/`h`, `version` → 25 dispatch keys. Per-verb argument handling: `new`/`open` accept `-l/--local` (creates `.tmuxinator.yml` in cwd) plus project name; `edit` takes project name; `start`/`s` covers `-p/--project-config FILE` (explicit YAML via `_files -g "*.(yml|yaml)"`), `-n/--name` (session-name override), `--suppress-tmux-version-warning`, project positional, plus repeatable `*:override key:value` for Thor template substitution at launch; `copy`/`c`/`cp` takes 2 positionals (source project, new name); `delete`/`rm` accepts repeatable `*:project name`; `stop` takes one project; `debug` adds mutex `--cmd`/`--no-cmd` plus project + overrides; `doctor`/`implode`/`list`/`stop-all`/`version` take no positionals. Live-project helper `_tmuxinator_projects` walks all 4 documented config roots in upstream priority order — `$TMUXINATOR_CONFIG` (custom env), `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/tmuxinator`, `$HOME/.config/tmuxinator`, `$HOME/.tmuxinator` — globs `*.yml`/`*.yaml` (zsh nullglob `(N)`), strips `:t:r` to bare project name, deduplicates with `${(u)…}`, and appends a synthetic `[local: ./.tmuxinator.yml]` marker entry when a local-project YAML is present. `tmuxinator` already at blacklist line 25890 and `mux` already at line 16805; no add needed.
- **ponymix (Sean Pringle's PulseAudio command-line mixer)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — verb/flag inventory extracted directly from upstream [github.com/falconindy/ponymix/blob/master/bash-completion](https://github.com/falconindy/ponymix/blob/master/bash-completion) source script (authoritative for the action surface).
