minimal mutex `-4/--ipv4`/`-6/--ipv6` only), `tcptracer` + `tcptracer-bpfcc` (tools/tcptracer.py — trace TCP IPv4/IPv6 connect()/accept()/close() events with PID, comm, source/dest; 9 flags `-t/--timestamp`, `-p/--pid` (default 0=all), `-N/--netns` Network Namespace filter (default 0=all), `--cgroupmap`/`--mntnsmap`, mutex IPv4/IPv6, `-v/--verbose` include netns in output, `--ebpf`), `bindsnoop` + `bindsnoop-bpfcc` (tools/bindsnoop.py — trace bind() syscalls with src IP/port and return code; 12 flags `-t/--timestamp`, `-w/--wide` IPv6 cols, `-p/--pid`, `-P/--port` CSV ports, `-E/--errors`, `-U/--print-uid`, `-u/--uid`, `--count` aggregate, `--cgroupmap`/`--mntnsmap`, `--ebpf`, `--debug-source`), `sslsniff-bpfcc` (tools/sslsniff.py — sniff OpenSSL/GnuTLS/NSS plaintext payloads via SSL_read/SSL_write uprobes; 14 flags `-p/--pid`, `-u/--uid`, `-x/--extra` UID+TID cols, `-c/--comm`, `-o/--no-openssl`, `-g/--no-gnutls`, `-n/--no-nss`, repeatable `*-d/--debug` (count-action verbosity stacker), `--hexdump`, `--max-buffer-size` (default 8192), `-l/--latency`, `--handshake` (requires `-l`), repeatable `*--extra-lib lib_type:lib_path`. Renamed `sslsniff-bpfcc` to avoid collision with Moxie Marlinspike's `sslsniff` SSL MITM tool already covered by src/_sslsniff), `swapin` + `swapin-bpfcc` (tools/swapin.py — count swap-in events by process to detect swap thrashing; 3 flags `-T/--notime`, `--ebpf` + interval (1)/count positionals), `gethostlatency` + `gethostlatency-bpfcc` (tools/gethostlatency.py — trace getaddrinfo/gethostbyname[2]() libc DNS resolution latency; minimal `-p/--pid` (default -1=all) + `--ebpf`).
- **bcc/eBPF wakeup/off-wake tracers + stackcount + kernel-lock stats + slab top + TCP states/drops + bash readline tracer** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — `wakeuptime` + `wakeuptime-bpfcc` (iovisor/bcc tools/wakeuptime.py — sample task WAKER stacks during block time; 9 flags `-u/--useronly`, `-p/--pid`, `-v/--verbose` raw addrs, `-f/--folded` flame-graph format, `--stack-storage-size` default 16384, `-m/--min-block-time us` (default 1), `-M/--max-block-time us` (default U64_MAX), `--ebpf`, duration positional), `offwaketime` + `offwaketime-bpfcc` (tools/offwaketime.py — off-CPU time with waker stacks, flame-graph-ready; 14 flags incl. CSV `-p/--pid` (dest=tgid)/`-t/--tid` (dest=pid), mutex `-u/--user-threads-only`/`-k/--kernel-threads-only`, mutex `-U/--user-stacks-only`/`-K/--kernel-stacks-only`, `-d/--delimited`, `-f/--folded`, `--stack-storage-size`, `-m`/`-M` min/max-block-time, `--state` TASK_state bitmask, duration positional), `stackcount` + `stackcount-bpfcc` (tools/stackcount.py — count function calls and their callers via kernel/user stacks (kprobes/uprobes/USDT/tracepoints); 15 flags incl. `-p/--pid`, `-c/--cpu`, `-i/--interval`, `-D/--duration` (note CAPITAL D), `-T/--timestamp`, `-r/--regexp` (regex-not-glob), `-s/--offset` addr offsets, `-P/--perpid` stacks-per-process, mutex `-K/--kernel-stacks-only`/`-U/--user-stacks-only`, `-v/--verbose` raw addrs, `-d/--delimited`, `-f/--folded`, `--debug` print-BPF, `pattern` positional), `klockstat` + `klockstat-bpfcc` (tools/klockstat.py — trace kernel mutex/spinlock acquire/hold stats with stacks; 9 flags `-d/--duration`, `-i/--interval`, `-n/--locks N`, `-s/--stacks N` (default 1), `-c/--caller` filter, `-S/--sort acq_max/acq_total/acq_count/hld_max/hld_total/hld_count`, `-p/--pid`, `-t/--tid`, `--stack-storage-size`), `slabratetop` + `slabratetop-bpfcc` (tools/slabratetop.py — top-like kernel SLAB/SLUB allocation rate per cache; minimal `-C/--noclear`, `-r/--maxrows` default 20, `--ebpf` + interval/count positionals), `tcpstates` + `tcpstates-bpfcc` (tools/tcpstates.py — trace TCP session state transitions and durations; 10 flags incl. mutex IPv4/IPv6, `-T/--time` HH:MM:SS, `-w/--wide` IPv6-fitting cols, `-s/--csv`, `-L/--localport`/`-D/--remoteport` CSV port filters, `-Y/--journal` log state changes to systemd journal), `tcpdrop` + `tcpdrop-bpfcc` (tools/tcpdrop.py — trace kernel TCP packet drops with stack traces; 5 flags mutex IPv4/IPv6, `--netns-id` netns ID filter (default 0), `--pid-netns` PID-whose-netns-to-filter (default 0), `--ebpf`), `bashreadline` + `bashreadline-bpfcc` (tools/bashreadline.py — print bash commands system-wide via readline() uprobe; minimal single flag `-s/--shared` libreadline.so path (default `/lib/libreadline.so`)).
- **bcc/eBPF dcache + page-cache top + mount/kill/direct-reclaim + file/dir top tracers** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — `dcsnoop` + `dcsnoop-bpfcc` (iovisor/bcc tools/dcsnoop.py — trace directory entry (dcache) lookups; minimal 2-flag `-a/--all` (default is fails-only), `--ebpf`), `dcstat` + `dcstat-bpfcc` (tools/dcstat.py — dcache REFS/SLOW/MISS/HIT% statistics by interval; NO argparse — manual sys.argv parsing — `interval` (default 1)/`count` positionals only), `cachetop` + `cachetop-bpfcc` (tools/cachetop.py — top-like page cache HITS/MISSES/DIRTIES per process; 2 args `-p/--pid` (typed=int) + interval positional default 5s), `mountsnoop` + `mountsnoop-bpfcc` (tools/mountsnoop.py — trace mount() and umount() syscalls system-wide; 4 flags `-P/--parent_process`, `--cgroupmap`/`--mntnsmap` BPF map filters, `--ebpf`), `killsnoop` + `killsnoop-bpfcc` (tools/killsnoop.py — trace kill() signal calls; 5 flags `-x/--failed` failures-only, `-p/--pid` sender filter, `-T/--tpid` target/receiver filter, `-s/--signal` signal or comma-list (TERM/KILL/HUP/INT/USR1/USR2/STOP/CONT/etc.), `--ebpf`), `drsnoop` + `drsnoop-bpfcc` (tools/drsnoop.py — trace direct memory reclaim slowpath events (alloc_pages reclaim); 9 flags `-T/--timestamp`, `-U/--print-uid`, `-p/--pid`, `-t/--tid`, `-u/--uid`, `-d/--duration`, `-n/--name` (substring match on comm), `-v/--verbose` show system memory state, `--ebpf`), `filetop` + `filetop-bpfcc` (tools/filetop.py — top-like display of files by reads/writes/bytes; 11 flags `-a/--all-files` include non-regular, `-C/--noclear`, `-r/--maxrows` default 20, `-s/--sort all/reads/writes/rbytes/wbytes`, `-p/--pid` (dest=tgid), mutex `--read-only`/`--write-only`, `-d/--directory` single-dir filter, `--ebpf`, interval/count positionals (default 1/unlimited)), `dirtop` + `dirtop-bpfcc` (tools/dirtop.py — top-like display of file reads/writes BY DIRECTORY; 8 flags incl. REQUIRED `-d/--root-directories` (comma-separated dirs to observe), same `-C/-r/-s/-p` shape as filetop but no `-a` / `--read-only` / `--write-only`).
- **bcc/eBPF filesystem-slow tracers + arg-distribution + scheduler + cap audit + stat() tracer** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — `ext4slower` + `ext4slower-bpfcc` + `xfsslower` + `xfsslower-bpfcc` + `zfsslower` + `zfsslower-bpfcc` (iovisor/bcc tools/{ext4,xfs,zfs}slower.py — multi-stem compdef; all three share identical 4-flag shape `-j/--csv`, `-p/--pid`, `--ebpf`, positional `min_ms` default 10), `btrfsslower` + `btrfsslower-bpfcc` (tools/btrfsslower.py — same shape + extra `-d/--duration` total-trace-seconds), `fileslower` + `fileslower-bpfcc` (tools/fileslower.py — trace slow synchronous VFS read/write; `-p/--pid` (dest=tgid), `-a/--all-files` include non-regular (sockets, FIFOs), `--ebpf`, `min_ms` default 10), `argdist` + `argdist-bpfcc` (tools/argdist.py — display function-parameter values as histogram or freq count; probe specifier `P:LIB:FUNC(SIG)[:EXPR[:FILTER]][#LABEL]` where P=p/r/u/t (kprobe/kretprobe/uprobe/tracepoint); 13 flags incl. `-p/--pid`, `-t/--tid`, `-z/--string-size` char* max (default 80), `-i/--interval` default 1, `-d/--duration`, `-n/--number` (dest=count), `-v/--verbose`, `-c/--cumulative` no-clear-at-interval, `-T/--top N`, repeatable `*-H/--histogram` + `*-C/--count` probe specifiers, repeatable `*-I/--include` header files, `--ebpf`), `cpudist` + `cpudist-bpfcc` (tools/cpudist.py — task on-CPU (or off-CPU with `-O/--offcpu`) time histogram; 10 flags incl. `-O/--offcpu`, `-T/--timestamp`, `-m/--milliseconds`, `-P/--pids` per-PID histograms, `-L/--tids` per-TID, `-p/--pid` filter, `-I/--include-idle`, `-e/--extension` avg/total/count summary, interval/count positionals), `runqlen` + `runqlen-bpfcc` (tools/runqlen.py — CPU run queue LENGTH histogram (samples queue length at intervals — distinct from runqlat's wait-time); 6 flags `-T/--timestamp`, `-O/--runqocc` queue occupancy report, `-C/--cpus` per-CPU output, `--ebpf`, interval/count), `capable` + `capable-bpfcc` (tools/capable.py — trace kernel capability(7) checks via `cap_capable()`; 9 flags `-v/--verbose` include non-audit checks, `-p/--pid`, `-K/--kernel-stack`, `-U/--user-stack`, `-x/--extra` extra TID+INSETID columns, `--cgroupmap`/`--mntnsmap` BPF map filters, `--unique` dedupe stacks per pid/cgroup), `statsnoop` + `statsnoop-bpfcc` (tools/statsnoop.py — trace stat() syscalls; 5 flags `-t/--timestamp`, `-s/--sysname` include syscall name, `-x/--failed` failures-only, `-p/--pid`, `--ebpf`).
- **bcc/eBPF profiling, syscall counting, leak detection, IRQ timing** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — `tcpretrans` + `tcpretrans-bpfcc` (iovisor/bcc tools/tcpretrans.py — trace TCP retransmits incl. tail-loss probe attempts; 6 flags `-s/--sequence` show TCP seq numbers, `-l/--lossprobe` include TLP, `-c/--count` per-flow counts, mutex `-4/--ipv4`/`-6/--ipv6`, `--ebpf`), `tcptop` + `tcptop-bpfcc` (tools/tcptop.py — top-like display of TCP send/recv throughput per session; 10 flags incl. `-C/--noclear`, `-S/--nosummary`, `-p/--pid`, `--cgroupmap`/`--mntnsmap`, mutex IPv4/IPv6, interval (default 1s)/count positionals), `syscount` + `syscount-bpfcc` (tools/syscount.py — count syscalls aggregated by latency or count; 15 flags incl. `-p/--pid`/`-t/--tid`/`-c/--ppid` filters, `-T/--top N` (default 10), `-x/--failures` only failures, `-e/--errno` (numeric or EPERM/EACCES/... symbolic), `-L/--latency`, `-m/--milliseconds`, `-P/--process` count-by-proc-not-syscall, `-l/--list` enumerate syscalls, `--syscall` single syscall filter, repeatable `*--exec cmd...` to run+trace a command), `profile` + `profile-bpfcc` (tools/profile.py — sample on-CPU stack traces at frequency, flame-graph-ready folded output; 18 flags incl. CSV `-p/--pid`/`-L/--tid`, mutex `-U/--user-stacks-only`/`-K/--kernel-stacks-only`, mutex `-F/--frequency Hz`/`-c/--count` events, `-d/--delimited`, `-a/--annotations` add `_[k]` to kernel frames, `-I/--include-idle`, `-f/--folded`, `--hash-storage-size` default 40960, `--stack-storage-size` default 16384, `-C/--cpu` default -1, `-A/--address` raw addrs, duration positional), `memleak` + `memleak-bpfcc` (tools/memleak.py — trace+aggregate outstanding memory allocations to detect leaks; 17 flags incl. `-p/--pid` (kernel-allocs if absent), `-t/--trace` log every alloc/free, `-a/--show-allocs` addresses+sizes, `-o/--older` prune-younger-than-ms (default 500), `-c/--command`, `--combined-only`, `--wa-missing-free`, `-s/--sample-rate` (default 1), `-T/--top` (default 10), `-z/--min-size`/`-Z/--max-size` byte filters, `-O/--obj` (default `c`) allocator-library, `--percpu` percpu allocs, `--sort size/count`, `--symbols-prefix`; interval (default 5s)/count positionals), `hardirqs` + `hardirqs-bpfcc` (tools/hardirqs.py — time hard IRQs as histogram or summary; 8 flags `-T/--timestamp`, `-N/--nanoseconds`, `-C/--count` events-not-timing, `-d/--dist` histograms, `-c/--cpu` filter, interval/outputs positionals), `softirqs` + `softirqs-bpfcc` (tools/softirqs.py — time soft IRQs as histogram or summary; same 8-flag shape with `-C/--events` event-count synonym + interval/count positionals).
- **bcc/eBPF observability deep-dive (block I/O + TCP + scheduler + page-cache)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — `biolatency` + `biolatency-bpfcc` (iovisor/bcc tools/biolatency.py — block I/O latency histogram; 11 flags `-T/--timestamp`, `-Q/--queued` include-OS-queue-time, `-m/--milliseconds`, `-D/--disks` per-device histogram, `-F/--flags` per-flag histogram, `-e/--extension` avg/total summary, `-j/--json`, `-d/--disk` trace single device, `--ebpf`; plus interval/count positionals), `biosnoop` + `biosnoop-bpfcc` (tools/biosnoop.py — trace block I/O with PID and latency; 4 flags `-Q/--queue`, `-d/--disk`, `-P/--pattern` sequential-or-random, `--ebpf`), `tcpconnect` + `tcpconnect-bpfcc` (tools/tcpconnect.py — trace TCP active (outbound) connect()s; 13 flags incl. mutex `-4/--ipv4`/`-6/--ipv6`, `-P/--port` dest-ports CSV, `-L/--lport` LPORT, `-U/--print-uid`, `-u/--uid` UID filter, `-c/--count` aggregate counts, `--cgroupmap`/`--mntnsmap` BPF map filters, `-d/--dns` DNS-query correlation), `tcpaccept` + `tcpaccept-bpfcc` (tools/tcpaccept.py — trace TCP passive (inbound) accept()s; 10 flags incl. mutex IPv4/IPv6, `-w/--wide` IPv6-fitting column output, `-s/--csv`, `-L/--localport`/`-D/--remoteport` CSV port filters), `offcputime` + `offcputime-bpfcc` (tools/offcputime.py — sample off-CPU time with kernel/user stacks, flame-graph-ready; minimal flag set `-T/--timestamp` + interval (default 1s) + count (default unlimited)).
- **bcc/eBPF func-tracing tools + perf-tools funcgraph + RUA AUR helper** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — `funccount` + `funccount-bpfcc` (iovisor/bcc tools/funccount.py argparse — count kernel/user/library function calls via kprobes/uprobes/USDT/tracepoints; 8 flags `-p/--pid`, `-i/--interval` sec, `-d/--duration` sec, `-T/--timestamp`, `-r/--regexp` regex-not-glob, `-D/--debug` print-BPF, `-c/--cpu`, positional `pattern` for events e.g. `vfs_*` or `p:do_sys_open`), `funclatency` + `funclatency-bpfcc` (tools/funclatency.py — latency histogram per function; 12 flags incl. mutually-exclusive `-u/--microseconds`/`-m/--milliseconds` histogram unit, `-F/--function` per-function histograms, `-l/--level` for nested/recursive depth, `--ebpf` internal debug; `pattern` positional), `funcslower` + `funcslower-bpfcc` (tools/funcslower.py — trace kernel funcs slower than threshold; 13 flags incl. mutex `-m/--min-ms`/`-u/--min-us` threshold, `-a/--arguments N` print-as-hex, `-T/--time` HH:MM:SS vs `-t/--timestamp` seconds-at-us, `-f/--folded` flame-graph format, `-U/--user-stack`, `-K/--kernel-stack`, `*: function` nargs=`+`), `funcinterval` + `funcinterval-bpfcc` (tools/funcinterval.py — time between same kernel function as histogram; 9 flags same shape as funclatency but single-function), `funcgraph` (brendangregg/perf-tools kernel/funcgraph bash ftrace-function_graph wrapper — 13 getopt flags `-a` all-info, `-C` on-CPU-only, `-d sec` duration+buffers, `-D` no-duration, `-H` headers, `-m maxdepth` stack depth, `-p PID`/`-L TID` on-CPU filter, `-P` process-names, `-t` timestamps, `-T` tail-comments; positional `funcstring`), `rua` (vn971/rua bubblewrap-jailed AUR helper from src/cli_args.rs clap derive — 7 subcommands `builddir`/`info`/`install`/`search`/`shellcheck`/`tarcheck`/`upgrade`; root `--color auto/never/always`; deep per-subcommand maps: `builddir` (`-o/--offline` no-internet, `-f/--force` makepkg-force, target dir positional), `install` (`--asdeps` install-as-dep, `--offline`, repeatable AUR-package positional), `tarcheck` (positional `*.pkg.tar.{xz,gz,zst}` archive), `shellcheck` (PKGBUILD or directory, defaults to `./PKGBUILD`), `upgrade` (`-d/--devel` rebuild git/hg/svn/bzr/cvs/darcs devel packages, `--printonly` outdated-list-only, repeatable `*--ignore` package).
- **Litecoin Core daemon family + squashfuse FUSE archive tools** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — `litecoind` + `litecoin-qt` + `litecoin-node` (litecoin-project/litecoin Bitcoin Core fork — bootstrap flags from src/init.cpp `SetupServerArgs` covering chain selection (`-testnet`/`-signet`/`-regtest`/`-chain main/test/signet/regtest`), datadir/conf/blocksdir/settings/pid, `-daemon`/`-daemonwait` background mode, debug logging (`-debug` category, `-debuglogfile`, `-printtoconsole`, `-shrinkdebugfile`, `-logtimestamps`, `-loglevel trace/debug/info/warning/error`, `-logips`), network (`-listen`/`-discover`/`-port`/`-bind`/`-whitebind`/`-proxy`/`-onion`/`-i2psam`/`-addnode`/`-connect`/`-maxconnections`/`-onlynet ipv4/ipv6/onion/i2p/cjdns`/`-dns`/`-dnsseed`), RPC (`-server`/`-rest`/`-rpcbind`/`-rpcallowip`/`-rpcauth`/`-rpccookiefile`/`-rpcuser`/`-rpcpassword`/`-rpcport`/`-rpcworkqueue`/`-rpcthreads`/`-rpcservertimeout`), wallet (`-disablewallet`/`-wallet`/`-walletdir`), ZMQ pubs (`-zmqpubhashblock`/`-zmqpubhashtx`/`-zmqpubrawblock`/`-zmqpubrawtx`), and storage tuning (`-prune`/`-txindex`/`-reindex`/`-reindex-chainstate`/`-assumevalid`/`-dbcache`/`-par`/`-maxmempool`/`-mempoolexpiry`/`-checkmempool`); litecoin-node is the multiprocess variant, litecoin-qt is the Qt GUI), `litecoin-cli` (src/bitcoin-cli.cpp `SetupCliArgs` — 18 flags incl. `-generate` blocks-immediately with optional nblocks/maxtries, `-getinfo`, `-netinfo` 0-4 verbosity, `-named` named-args mode, `-rpcconnect`/`-rpcport` default 9332 mainnet / 19332 testnet / 38332 signet / 19443 regtest, `-rpccookiefile`/`-rpcuser`/`-rpcpassword`/`-rpcwallet` for non-default wallets, `-rpcwait`/`-rpcwaittimeout`, `-stdin`/`-stdinrpcpass`/`-stdinwalletpassphrase`), `litecoin-tx` (src/bitcoin-tx.cpp `SetupBitcoinTxArgs` — `-create`/`-json`/`-txid` + chain selection + positional commands `delin=N`/`delout=N`/`in=TXID:VOUT[:SEQUENCE]`/`outaddr=VALUE:ADDRESS`/`outpubkey=VALUE:PUBKEY[:FLAGS]`/`outmultisig=VALUE:REQUIRED:PUBKEYS:KEY1:KEY2...[:FLAGS]`/`outscript=VALUE:SCRIPT[:FLAGS]`/`outdata=[VALUE:]DATA`/`locktime=N`/`nversion=N`/`replaceable(=N)`/`sign=SIGHASH-FLAGS`/`load=NAME:FILENAME`/`set=NAME:JSON-STRING`), `litecoin-wallet` (src/bitcoin-wallet.cpp — `-datadir`/`-wallet`/`-debug`/`-printtoconsole` + chain selection + 3 subcommands `info`/`create`/`salvage`; note Litecoin Core does NOT yet have Bitcoin Core's newer `dump`/`createfromdump` subcommands). `squashfuse` + `squashfuse_ll` + `squashfuse_ls` + `squashfuse_extract` (vasi/squashfuse SquashFS FUSE driver — flags from fuseprivate.c `sqfs_usage()` + hl.c `fuse_opts[]` + ll_main.c + ls.c + extract.c; high-level driver `squashfuse` supports `-o offset=N`/`-o subdir=PATH`/`-o notify_pipe=PATH` plus FUSE pass-through (`-o ro`/`-o allow_other`/`-o allow_root`/`-o default_permissions`/`-o direct_io`/`-o kernel_cache`/`-o auto_cache`/`-o uid=N`/`-o gid=N`/`-o umask=M`); low-level `squashfuse_ll` adds `-o timeout=N` idle-auto-unmount + `-o uid=N`/`-o gid=N` file-owner mapping; `squashfuse_ls` takes a single ARCHIVE positional with no flags; `squashfuse_extract` takes `ARCHIVE PATH_TO_EXTRACT` or `ARCHIVE -a` for whole-archive extraction).
- **nvchecker version-checker family** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — `nvchecker` (lilydjwg/nvchecker async TOML-config new-version checker from nvchecker/__main__.py + nvchecker/core.py `add_common_arguments` — 9 flags: tool-specific `-k/--keyfile` (overrides config keyfile), `-t/--tries` retry count, `--failures` exit-3-on-error, `-e/--entry` single-entry debug mode, plus common `-l/--logging debug/info/warning/error`, `--logger pretty/json/both`, `--json-log-fd` numeric fd, `-c/--file` TOML config, `-V/--version`), `nvtake` (nvchecker/tools.py `take()` — promote newver entries into oldver: 5 common flags + `--all` (take all updates), `--ignore-nonexistent`, plus repeatable positional `NAME` or `NAME=VERSION` (pin a specific version instead of newver); critical: writes a `~`-suffixed backup of oldver via shutil.copy/rename), `nvcmp` (nvchecker/tools.py `cmp()` — diff oldver vs newver: 5 common flags + `-j/--json` array-of-dicts output (or array of names with `--quiet`), `-q/--quiet` names-only, `-a/--all` include unchanged, `-s/--sort parse_version/vercmp/awesomeversion/none` version comparison strategy, `-n/--newer` only newer-version diffs, `--exit-status` exit-4 if updates exist). All three share `core.add_common_arguments` from nvchecker/core.py.

- **RISC Zero toolchain installer + gnark zk-SNARK benchmarker** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — `rzup` (risc0/risc0 toolchain installer/version manager — 8 subcommands from rzup/src/cli/mod.rs clap derive: `install`/`update` alias, `check`, `default`, `show`, `uninstall`, `build`, `publish` (nested), plus root flags `-v/--verbose`/`-q/--quiet`; per-subcommand maps from rzup/src/cli/commands.rs for install (`-f/--force`, component name + version positionals, with `rust`/`risc0-groth16`/`cargo-risczero`/`r0vm`/`cpp`/`risc0-zkr` enum), default/uninstall (component+version), build (mutually-exclusive `-t/--tag-or-commit` vs `-p/--path`), publish→{upload, set-latest, create-artifact} where `upload` takes component+version+payload positionals with `--target-agnostic`/`--target-triple` mutex (`x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu`/`x86_64-apple-darwin`/`aarch64-apple-darwin`/`aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu`) and `--force`; `create-artifact` with `--input`/`--output`/`--compression-level` 0-9), `gnark-bench` (Consensys/gnark-bench — 2 subcommands `groth16`/`plonk` from cmd/{groth16,plonk}.go sharing 6 persistent flags from cmd/root.go: `--circuit` expo/cubic/mimc/sha256/ecdsa/eddsa, `--size`, `--count`, `--algo` compile/setup/prove/verify, `--profile` none/trace/cpu/mem, `--curve` bn254/bls12-377/bls12-381/bls24-315/bls24-317/bw6-633/bw6-756/bw6-761).
- **Firmware-extraction (Unblob) + web screenshot recon (gowitness)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — `unblob` (onekey-sec/unblob firmware extraction suite — 20 flags from python/unblob/cli.py click decorators incl. `-e/--extract-dir`, `-f/--force`, `-d/--depth` recursion, `-n/--randomness-depth` entropy depth for unknown files, `-P/--plugins-path` (repeatable), `-S/--skip-magic` (repeatable) + `--skip-extension` (repeatable) + `--clear-skip-magics`, `-p/--process-num` parallel workers, `--report` JSON metadata file, `--log` text log file, `-s/--skip-extraction` carve-only mode, `--no-sandbox` (disable Landlock for debugging), `-k/--keep-extracted-chunks`, `--delete-extracted-files`, `--carve-suffix`/`--extract-suffix` naming, `--show-external-dependencies`/`--build-handlers-doc`, `-v` verbosity stackable), `gowitness` (sensepost/gowitness web screenshot + info-gathering — 2 top-level subcommands `scan`/`report` plus `version`/`completion` from cmd/{root,scan,scan_*,report,report_*}.go; `scan` nested into 5 sub-subcommands `single`/`file`/`cidr`/`nmap`/`nessus` with shared 27-flag scan-persistent map covering driver (`--driver` gorod/chromedp), threading (`-t/--threads`, `-T/--timeout`, `--delay`), screenshots (`-s/--screenshot-path`, `--screenshot-format` jpeg/png, `--screenshot-jpeg-quality`, `--screenshot-fullpage`, `--screenshot-skip-save`, `--write-screenshots`), content (`--save-content`, `--skip-html`, `--skip-network-logs`, `--javascript`, `--javascript-file`), data outputs (`--write-db`/`--write-db-uri`, `--write-csv`/`--write-csv-file`, `--write-jsonl`/`--write-jsonl-file`, `--write-stdout`), Chrome browser (`--chrome-path`, `--chrome-proxy`, `--chrome-user-agent`, `--chrome-window-x`/`-y`, `--chrome-header` repeatable); `report` nested into 6 sub-subcommands `list`/`merge`/`migrate`/`convert`/`generate`/`server` with `server`-specific `-a/--address`+`--db`+`--screenshot-path`, generate/convert `-f/--file`+`--format` html/csv/jsonl, merge multi-source positional; plus global `-D/--debug-log`, `-q/--quiet`, `--cpu-profile`, `--mem-profile`).
- **Bisq decentralized exchange + Mythril symbolic-execution security** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — `bisq` + `bisq-cli` (bisq-network/bisq P2P Bitcoin exchange gRPC client — 60+ methods enumerated from cli/src/main/java/bisq/cli/CliMain.java covering wallets (getbalance, getaddressbalance, getfundingaddresses, getunusedbsqaddress), transactions (gettransactions, sendbsq, sendbtc, gettxfeerate, settxfeerate, unsettxfeerate, verifybsqsenttoaddress), offers (createoffer, editoffer, canceloffer, getoffer, getoffers, getmyoffers, takeoffer), trades (gettrade, gettrades, confirmpaymentstarted/xmr, confirmpaymentreceived, closetrade, withdrawfunds, failtrade, unfailtrade), payment accounts (getpaymentmethods, getpaymentacctform, createpaymentacct, createcryptopaymentacct, getpaymentaccts), wallet security (lockwallet, unlockwallet, setwalletpassword, removewalletpassword), DAO governance (16 proposal/voting methods), network (getversion, getnetwork, getdaostatus, getavgbsqprice, getbtcprice, registerdisputeagent, stop); plus per-method maps for getbalance (--currency-code BTC/BSQ), gettrades (--category open/closed/failed), createoffer/editoffer (--direction BUY/SELL, --currency-code, --amount, --min-amount, --use-market-based-price, --fixed-price, --market-price-margin, --security-deposit, --payment-account, --maker-fee-currency-code BTC/BSQ), takeoffer (--offer-id, --payment-account, --taker-fee-currency-code), sendbtc/sendbsq (--address, --amount, --tx-fee-rate, --memo), plus `--password`/`--host`/`--port` for gRPC daemon connection), `mythril` + `myth` (Consensys/mythril Ethereum smart-contract symbolic-execution security tool — 11 subcommands from mythril/interfaces/cli.py: `analyze`/`a`, `disassemble`/`d`, `foundry`/`f`, `concolic`/`c`, `safe-functions`, `read-storage`, `function-to-hash`, `hash-to-address`, `list-detectors`, `version`, `help`; with input flags (`-c/--code` bytecode, `-f/--codefile` file, `-a/--address` on-chain pull, `--bin-runtime`, `--solv`), RPC flags (`--rpc` infura-mainnet default, `--infura-id`, `--rpctls`), global flags (`-v` log-level 0-5, `--epic`, `-o/--outform` text/markdown/json/jsonv2), and a 16-flag `analyze` subcommand map (`--execution-timeout`, `--solver-timeout`, `--create-timeout`, `--max-depth`, `--call-depth-limit`, `--transaction-count`, `--strategy` dfs/bfs/naive-random/weighted-random/pending (repeatable), `--loop-bound`, `--enable-iprof`, `--disable-dependency-pruning`, `--enable-coverage-strategy`, `--enable-state-merging`, `--phrack`, `--no-onchain-storage-access`, `--enable-summaries`, `--modules`)).
- **QMK Firmware + Trakt.tv CLI + AWS Smithy IDL** — every new addition was first dup-checked against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` (after the round-11 stock-zsh collision audit caught 8 dupes that had to be reverted). `qmk` (qmk/qmk_firmware mechanical-keyboard firmware CLI — 49 subcommands from lib/python/qmk/cli/ + docs.qmk.fm/cli_commands organized into compile/flash + new-keyboard/new-keymap + list-keyboards/list-keymaps + info/find/lint + json2c/c2json/via2json/kle2json/format-json + import-keyboard/import-keymap/import-kbfirmware + config/console/doctor/env/migrate/clean/cd/docs + generate-{api,compilation-database,config-h,rules-mk,info-json,keyboard-h,keyboard-c,keymap-h,keymap-c,dfu-header,develop-pr-list,version-h} + license-check/mass-compile/painter-make-font/painter-make-image/painter-convert-graphics + pytest/test-c/hello + userspace-add/userspace-remove/userspace-list/userspace-compile/userspace-doctor + ci, plus root flags (`-v` verbose, `--no-color`/`--color`, `--log-level`/`--log-file-level` critical/error/warning/info/debug/notset, `--log-file`/`--config-file`/`--datetime-fmt`/`--log-fmt`) and per-subcommand maps for compile/flash (`-kb/--keyboard`, `-km/--keymap`, `-bl/--bootloader`, `-j/--parallel`, `-e/--env`, `-c/--clean`/`--no-clean`, `-n/--dry-run`, `--target`), new-keyboard (`-l/--layout`, `-t/--type` MCU, `-u/--username`, `-n/--realname`), info (`-l/--layouts`, `-m/--matrix`, `-f/--format` friendly/text/json, `--ascii`), lint (`--strict`), console (`-b/--bootloaders-only`, `-d/--device`, `-l/--list`, `-n/--numeric`, `-t/--timestamp`, `-w/--wait`), doctor (`-y/--yes`/`-n/--no`), mass-compile (`-c/--clean`, `-t/--no-temp`, `-j/--parallel`, `-f/--filter`), docs (`-b/--browser`, `-p/--port`)), `trakt-cli` (angristan/trakt-cli — 3 real subcommands `auth`/`history`/`search` from cmd/{auth,history,search}.go cobra with `--client-id`/`--client-secret` for auth device-code flow, `--type` movies/shows/seasons/episodes filter + `--limit`/`--start-at`/`--end-at` for history, `--type` movie/show/episode/person/list for search; config defaults to `~/.trakt.yaml`), `smithy` (smithy-lang/smithy AWS service-modeling IDL CLI — 12 subcommands `ast`/`build`/`clean`/`diff`/`format`/`init`/`lock`/`migrate`/`select`/`validate`/`version`/`warmup` from smithy-cli/src/main/java/.../commands/*.java with shared `ConfigOptions`/`DiscoveryOptions`/`ValidatorOptions` flag groups: `-c/--config` smithy-build.json + `--no-config`, `--discover`/`--discover-classpath`, `--severity` NOTE/WARNING/DANGER/ERROR + `--no-validate` + `--hide-validators`/`--show-validators` + `--quiet-validation-summary`, plus standard `-q/--quiet`, `--debug`, `--stacktrace`, `--logging` OFF/SEVERE/WARNING/INFO/CONFIG/FINE/FINER/FINEST/ALL, `--no-color`/`--force-color`; per-subcommand maps for build (`--projection`/`--plugin`/`--output`), diff (`--mode` arbitrary/project/git + `--old`/`--new` + `--old-format`/`--new-format`/`--format` text/csv/json + `--events-format`), init (`-l/--list`/`-o/--output`/`-t/--template`/`-u/--url`/`--include-archive`), migrate (`-d/--dry-run`), select (`-e/--expression` selector + `--show-traits` + `--vars`)).
